    • Based on their performance in the online test, candidates will be shortlisted for a personal interview in a 1:3 ratio.
  3. Final Merit List:
    • The final selection list will be prepared by giving 90% weightage to the performance in the online test and 10% weightage to the interview.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

A clear understanding of the exam structure is vital for creating an effective preparation strategy. The online test will assess both professional knowledge and general aptitude.

Online Examination Pattern

SubjectQuestionsMarksDuration
Professional Knowledge 50 110 Composite 120 Minutes
Test of Reasoning 40 20
Test of Quantitative Aptitude 20 10
Test of Marathi Language 20 10
Total 130 150

Syllabus (Expected)

  • Professional Knowledge (110 Marks): This is the most crucial section. The syllabus will be comprehensive and based on the curriculum of CA/ICWA(CMA)/M.Com/MBA(Finance). Key topics include:
    • Financial Accounting: Accounting Standards (Ind AS), Final Accounts of Companies, Cash Flow Statements, Financial Statement Analysis.
    • Cost & Management Accounting: Costing Methods (Job, Process, Contract), Standard Costing, Marginal Costing, Budgetary Control.
    • Auditing & Assurance: Auditing Standards, Internal Control, Vouching, Verification of Assets & Liabilities, Company Audit.
    • Taxation: Direct Taxes (Income Tax Act) and Indirect Taxes (GST).
    • Corporate & Other Laws: Companies Act, 2013, Contract Act, etc.
    • Financial Management: Capital Budgeting, Working Capital Management, Cost of Capital, Capital Structure Theories, Ratio Analysis.
  • Reasoning (20 Marks): Puzzles, Seating Arrangements, Syllogism, Blood Relations, Coding-Decoding, Inequality, Direction Sense.
  • Quantitative Aptitude (10 Marks): Data Interpretation (Charts, Graphs), Simplification/Approximation, Number Series, Percentage, Profit & Loss.
  • Marathi Language (10 Marks): Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension, Sentence Correction. This section is to test proficiency in the state's official language.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for these managerial posts in the Finance & Accounts department. The selection is based entirely on the candidate's performance in the online test and interview.

How to Prepare for the MAHAVITARAN Exam

Success in this competitive exam requires a well-thought-out strategy focusing on both professional knowledge and aptitude.

  1. Master Your Professional Knowledge: This section carries over 70% of the marks. Revise your core concepts from your CA/ICWA/M.Com/MBA studies. Focus on practical application and recent amendments in laws and accounting standards.
  2. Practice Aptitude Questions: Although the weightage is lower, these sections can be scoring. Dedicate regular time to practicing Reasoning and Quantitative Aptitude questions from standard banking or PSU exam preparation books.
  3. Strengthen Marathi Language Skills: For non-native speakers, this section can be challenging. Practice with previous years' papers from Maharashtra state-level exams and focus on basic grammar and vocabulary.
  4. Time Management is Key: You have 120 minutes to solve 130 questions. Practice with timed mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy. Develop a strategy to attempt the sections based on your strengths.
  5. Interview Preparation: Prepare for questions related to your work experience, technical knowledge, the power sector in India, and MAHAVITARAN's business operations.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books and resources for your preparation:

  • Professional Knowledge:
    • Study materials from ICAI (for CA) and ICMAI (for CMA).
    • "Financial Management" by Prasanna Chandra.
    • "Cost Accounting: Principles and Practice" by M.N. Arora.
    • "Students' Guide to Auditing and Assurance" by Aruna Jha.
  • Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Marathi Language: "Sugam Marathi Vyakaran" by M. R. Walimbe.

How to Apply for MAHAVITARAN Recruitment

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must apply through the official MAHAVITARAN website.

  1. Visit the official MAHAVITARAN website: www.mahadiscom.in.
  2. Go to the "Careers" or "Latest News and Announcement" section and click on the link related to Advertisement No. 03/2025.
  3. Click on the "APPLY ONLINE" option.
  4. If you are a new user, click on "CLICK HERE FOR NEW REGISTRATION" and enter your name, contact details, and email ID. A provisional registration number and password will be generated.
  5. Log in with the registration number and password and fill out the detailed application form.
  6. Scan and upload your photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration.
  7. Save and verify the details entered in the application form using the "SAVE AND NEXT" button.
  8. Carefully check all details before clicking on "COMPLETE REGISTRATION."
  9. Proceed to the "PAYMENT" tab and pay the application fee online as per your category.
  10. Take a printout of the e-receipt and the final application form after successful payment.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should keep scanned copies of the following documents ready before starting the online application process:

  1. Recent Passport-size Photograph.
  2. Signature.
  3. Left Thumb Impression.
  4. A handwritten declaration in the prescribed format.
  5. Educational Qualification Certificates (CA/ICWA/M.Com/MBA, etc.).
  6. Post-Qualification Experience Certificates.
  7. Domicile Certificate of Maharashtra.
  8. Caste Certificate and Caste Validity Certificate (if applicable).
  9. Non-Creamy Layer Certificate (if applicable).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Active from July 2025)
Official Website https://www.mahadiscom.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: When will the application for MAHAVITARAN recruitment begin?

Online registration and application are expected to begin in the second week of July 2025.

Question 2: Can candidates from outside Maharashtra apply?

No, a Domicile of Maharashtra is mandatory to be eligible for this recruitment.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection process includes an online examination followed by a personal interview. The final selection will have a 90% weightage for the online test and a 10% weightage for the interview.

Question 4: Is there negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there will be a deduction of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the online examination.

Question 5: Can freshers apply for these posts?

No, post-qualification experience ranging from a minimum of one year to seven years is required for all posts.

entry.title: ECHS Ambala Recruitment 2025: Vacancies for Medical, Para-Medical & Non-Medical Staff
entry.content:
ECHS Ambala Recruitment 2025: Vacancies for Medical, Para-Medical & Non-Medical Staff

A major contractual recruitment drive by the Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme, Station Cell, Ambala Cantt. Apply offline before January 03, 2026.

Post Name: Medical Officer, Dental Officer, Specialist, Pharmacist, Lab Technician, Clerk, Driver, Peon, and more.
Last Date: 03 January 2026
Total Posts: Multiple vacancies across various polyclinics

The Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme (ECHS), under the Ministry of Defence, Government of India, has released a significant employment notice for its Station Cell in Ambala Cantt. ECHS invites applications to engage various Officer-in-Charge (OIC), Medical, Para-Medical, and Non-Medical staff on a contractual basis for its polyclinics located under the Area of Responsibility (AOR) of Station Cell, Ambala Cantt. This is a golden opportunity for qualified individuals, with a special preference for Ex-Servicemen, to serve the veteran community.

This recruitment drive aims to fill numerous positions including Medical Officer, Dental Officer, Specialists, Pharmacist, Laboratory Technician, Clerk, and various support staff roles. The engagement will be for a period of 11/12 months, renewable for additional periods subject to the performance of the candidates and other conditions. The application process is completely offline, and candidates must submit their applications in the prescribed physical format along with self-attested photocopies of all required documents. The last date for the receipt of applications is January 03, 2026, by 01:30 PM. This comprehensive article provides a detailed breakdown of all essential information, including post-wise eligibility, selection process, and how to apply.

Important Points

This recruitment is for contractual positions within the ECHS, a Government of India scheme. Key dates and details are as follows:

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme (ECHS), Station Cell, Ambala Cantt
Job Type Contractual (11/12 months, renewable)
Job Location Various ECHS Polyclinics (Ambala, Karnal, Sonepat, Panipat, Kaithal, Yamuna Nagar, etc.)
Last Date to Apply (Offline) 03 January 2026 (up to 01:30 PM)
Interview Dates From 24 March 2026 onwards

Vacancy Details

The recruitment aims to fill numerous vacancies across various medical, para-medical, and non-medical posts in different ECHS polyclinics. A detailed breakdown is as follows:

Post Name Total Vacancies Locations (Number of Vacancies)
Officer-in-Charge 02 Ambala (1), Kharkhoda (1) - ESM Only
Medical Officer 10 Karnal (1), Kaithal (1), Yamuna Nagar (1), Sonepat (1), Panipat (1), Nahan (1), Gohana (1), Kurukshetra (2), Kharkhoda (1)
Dental Officer 04 Ambala (1), Panipat (1), Kurukshetra (1), Yamuna Nagar (1)
Medical Specialist 05 Ambala (2), Sonepat (1), Panipat (1), Yamuna Nagar (1)
Gynaecologist 01 Ambala (1)
Radiologist 01 Sonepat (1)
Dental A/T/H 04 Ambala (1), Kurukshetra (1), Karnal (1), Naraingarh (1)
Nursing Assistant 03 Sonepat (1), Panipat (1), Nahan (1)
Pharmacist 04 Ambala (1), Sonepat (1), Karnal (1), Kaithal (1)
Laboratory Technician 05 Ambala (1), Panipat (1), Karnal (1), Gohana (1), Kurukshetra (1)
Laboratory Assistant 03 Naraingarh (1), Sonepat (1), Yamuna Nagar (1)
Physiotherapist 02 Ambala (1), Sonepat (1)
Radiographer 01 Sonepat (1)
Clerk 02 Panipat (1), Nahan (1)
Driver 01 Kharkhoda (1)
Peon 02 Karnal (1), Ambala (1)
Chowkidar 05 Kaithal (1), Panipat (1), Gohana (1), Naraingarh (1), Yamuna Nagar (1)
Safaiwala 06 Sonepat (1), Nahan (1), Kurukshetra (1), Kharkhoda (1), Naraingarh (1), Yamuna Nagar (1)
Female Attendant 02 Karnal (1), Gohana (1)
Note: Reservation will be given to Ex-Servicemen. Selected medical professionals may need to undergo a 10-day unpaid On-Job Training (OJT).

Application Fee

  • There is no application fee for any of the posts mentioned in this recruitment.

The application process is completely free of cost.

Eligibility Criteria

Each post has specific requirements for educational qualifications, experience, and age. Below is a summary for the key positions:

Post Name Minimum Qualification and Experience Fixed Remuneration (per month)
Officer-in-Charge Graduate, with a minimum of 05 years of work experience in healthcare institutes or managerial positions. (ESM Only). Rs. 95,000/-
Medical Officer MBBS & a minimum of 03 years of experience after internship. Preferable: Additional qualification in Medicine/Surgery. Rs. 95,000/-
Dental Officer BDS & a minimum of 03 years of experience. Rs. 95,000/-
Medical Specialist / Gynaecologist MD/MS in the concerned specialty/DNB & a minimum of 03 years of experience after Post Graduation. Rs. 1,30,000/-
Radiologist PG Degree & a minimum of 03 years of experience after PG Degree. Rs. 1,30,000/-
Pharmacist B Pharmacy/Diploma in Pharmacy from a recognized institute OR 12th with Science stream & a minimum of 03 years of experience. Rs. 36,500/-
Laboratory Technician B.Sc (Medical Lab Technology) OR 10+2 with Science from a recognized board/ DMLT & a minimum of 03 years of experience. Rs. 36,500/-
Clerk Graduate / Class-1 Clerical Trade (Armed Forces), computer qualified & a minimum of 05 years of experience. Rs. 29,200/-
Driver Class 8th, Class-1 MT Driver (Armed Forces), possess a Civil Driving License & a minimum of 05 years of experience. Rs. 25,600/-
Peon / Chowkidar / Safaiwala / Female Attendant Literate / Class 8th pass with a minimum of 05 years of experience in the respective field. Rs. 21,800/-

Selection Process

The selection for all contractual posts will be direct and based on the performance of the candidates in a personal interview. The process is as follows:

  1. Application Screening: All applications received by the deadline will be screened by a board of officers to check for eligibility based on the qualitative requirements (QR) mentioned for each post.
  2. Shortlisting: Only candidates who meet all the qualitative requirements will be shortlisted for the interview.
  3. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called telephonically for a personal interview. The interview will be held at Station Cell (ECHS), Ambala Cantt, from March 24, 2026, onwards.
  4. Document Verification: Candidates must bring all original certificates, mark sheets, and degrees at the time of the interview for verification. Failure to produce original documents will lead to disqualification.
Note: No TA/DA will be admissible for attending the interview. The selection is a direct process with no written examination.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for this recruitment. The selection is based on shortlisting and a personal interview. Therefore, candidates do not need to prepare for any specific exam pattern or syllabus. Preparation should focus on the interview.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these posts.

How to Prepare for the Interview

As the selection is based solely on the interview, your preparation should be focused and thorough.

  1. Review Your Domain Knowledge: For medical and para-medical posts, revise your fundamental and clinical knowledge. Be prepared to answer questions related to your specialty and general medical practices. For non-medical posts, be clear about your past roles and responsibilities.
  2. Understand the ECHS Scheme: Research the Ex-Servicemen Contributory Health Scheme. Understand its purpose, functioning, and the community it serves. This will show your genuine interest.
  3. Prepare for Common Interview Questions: Be ready to answer questions like "Tell me about yourself," "Why do you want to work for ECHS?", "How does your experience make you suitable for this role?", and situational questions.
  4. For Ex-Servicemen: Be prepared to articulate how your military experience and discipline are assets for a civilian role in a healthcare organization catering to veterans.
  5. Document Readiness: Ensure all your original documents are in order and neatly arranged in a file. This includes your educational qualifications, experience certificates, medical and character certificates, and service documents for ESM.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no written test, specific "preparation books" are not required. Instead, candidates should focus on revising their professional knowledge and preparing for the interview.

  • For Medical Professionals: Standard textbooks from your MBBS/BDS/MD/MS/Diploma curriculum.
  • For Para-Medical Staff: Textbooks and notes from your respective Diploma/B.Sc courses (Pharmacy, Lab Technology, etc.).
  • For All Candidates: Read about the ECHS scheme on its official website (www.echs.gov.in) to understand the organization's mission and functioning.

How to Apply

The application process is completely offline. Candidates must submit their application in the prescribed physical format.

  1. Visit the ECHS official website www.echs.gov.in and download the prescribed application form.
  2. Fill out the application form correctly and attach self-attested photocopies of all the required documents as listed below.
  3. Prepare the application set in duplicate (two complete sets of the application form and all documents).
  4. Submit the application in duplicate to the following address:
    OIC, Stn Cell (ECHS), Ambala Cantt
    The application must reach on or before 03 January 2026 by 01:30 PM.
  5. Applications received after the deadline will not be accepted.

Documents Needed to Apply

The application must be submitted in duplicate, along with self-attested photocopies of the following documents:

  1. Application form as per the format given on the ECHS website.
  2. Educational Qualification certificates and mark sheets.
  3. Work Experience certificates.
  4. PAN Card and Aadhaar Card.
  5. Latest Medical Fitness Certificate signed by SEMO/CMO (in original).
  6. Character Certificate (in original).
  7. No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the concerned Station Cell if currently working with any Army Organization.
  8. For Ex-Servicemen: Copy of Discharge Book and PPO.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Application Form Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official ECHS Website www.echs.gov.in

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply?

The last date for the receipt of physical application forms is January 03, 2026, up to 01:30 PM.

Question 2: Is this a permanent government job?

No, this is a contractual engagement for a period of 11 or 12 months, which is renewable based on performance.

Question 3: Is there a written test for selection?

No, the selection is based on a direct personal interview after the screening of applications.

Question 4: Do I need to pay an application fee?

No, there is no application fee for this recruitment.

entry.title: MP High Court Recruitment 2025: Apply for 41 Data Processing Assistant Posts
entry.content:
MP High Court Recruitment 2025: Data Processing Assistant Notification Out

Direct Recruitment for 41 DPA Vacancies at the High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur

Post Name: Data Processing Assistant (DPA)
Last Date: November 19, 2025
Total Posts: 41 Vacancies

The High Court of Madhya Pradesh (MPHC), Jabalpur, has officially invited online applications for the recruitment of Data Processing Assistants (DPA) for the year 2025. This recruitment drive, identified by Advertisement Number 52/Exam/DPA/2025, aims to fill 41 critical technical positions within the state's judicial system. As the judiciary continues to move toward complete digitization under the e-Courts project, the role of a Data Processing Assistant has become more vital than ever. These professionals are responsible for managing legal databases, ensuring the security of digital case files, and providing technical support for the complex software ecosystems used by the High Court and its subordinate courts.

For candidates with a background in Computer Science or Information Technology, this represents a prestigious opportunity to secure a permanent government job within the Madhya Pradesh judicial framework. Working for the High Court offers not only financial stability but also a disciplined work environment and the pride of serving the justice system. The application window is scheduled to open on October 29, 2025, and will remain active until November 19, 2025. Candidates are advised to prepare thoroughly, as the selection process involves a competitive three-stage evaluation comprising a preliminary written test, a practical exam, and a personal interview. This comprehensive guide provides all the necessary details regarding eligibility, salary, exam pattern, and preparation strategies.

In recent years, the MP High Court has been at the forefront of implementing technological advancements in legal proceedings. From virtual hearings to the automated listing of cases, the technical wing of the court is the backbone of these operations. As a Data Processing Assistant, you will be part of this technological revolution. The recruitment process is strictly merit-based, ensuring that only those with strong technical foundations and relevant work experience are selected. This article will explore every aspect of the notification to ensure you have a clear roadmap for your application.

Important Points and Job Details

The recruitment for the Data Processing Assistant position is a direct entry process. Candidates must keep track of the following timeline to ensure their application is submitted and processed on time. It is important to note that the job is categorized as a State Government position within the judicial services of Madhya Pradesh.

Event / Feature Detail/Information
Official Advertisement No. 52/Exam/DPA/2025
Online Registration Starts October 29, 2025
Last Date to Apply November 19, 2025 (11:55 PM)
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Madhya Pradesh (Jabalpur, Indore, Gwalior)
Correction Window Expected Nov 22 - Nov 24, 2025

Vacancy Details and Category Wise Breakdown

The total number of 41 vacancies is distributed among different social categories as per the reservation policy of the Madhya Pradesh Government. Out of the total, specific seats are reserved for Persons with Disabilities (PH) as indicated below. Candidates from states other than MP will be considered under the Unreserved (UR) category regardless of their caste status in their home state.

Category Number of Vacancies
Unreserved (UR) 19 (Including 1 PH)
Scheduled Caste (SC) 07 (Including 1 PH)
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 09
Other Backward Classes (OBC) 06
Total Vacancies 41
Salary and Pay Scale: The appointed Data Processing Assistants will receive a pay scale of ₹5200 – ₹20200 with a Grade Pay of ₹2400. This follows the 6th Pay Commission structure, which will be revised to the corresponding 7th Pay Commission Matrix, leading to an estimated gross starting salary of approximately ₹35,000 to ₹42,000 per month including allowances.

Application Fee Details

  • Unreserved (UR) & Candidates from other States: ₹943.40 (Total including portal fees).
  • Reserved Category (SC/ST/OBC) of MP: ₹743.40 (Total including portal fees).
  • Persons with Disabilities (PwD) of MP: ₹743.40.

The application fee must be paid online via Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, or UPI. It is crucial to save the transaction receipt as it may be required during the document verification stage. Once paid, the fee is non-refundable under any circumstances.

Eligibility Criteria

To be eligible for the MP High Court DPA Recruitment 2025, candidates must fulfill both educational and experience requirements. The High Court is looking for candidates who not only have theoretical knowledge but also have practical exposure to high-pressure data environments.

Criterion Requirement Details
Educational Qualification B.Sc. (Computer Science), BCA, B.Sc. (IT), or an equivalent degree from a recognized university. Minimum Percentage: 60% marks in the aggregate.
Experience Required Minimum 3 years of working experience in Operating Systems, Office Applications, and Data Entry operations in a recognized institution or firm.
Minimum Age 18 Years (as of January 1, 2025).
Maximum Age 35 Years (as of January 1, 2025). Note: Age relaxation up to 40 or 45 years applies to various reserved categories as per MP State rules.

Selection Process

The MPHC Data Processing Assistant selection process is a comprehensive three-tier system designed to filter candidates based on theoretical knowledge, practical application, and personality.

  1. Online Preliminary Examination: This is an objective type screening test. It carries 100 marks. Candidates who secure the qualifying marks in this stage will be shortlisted for the practical exam in a 1:3 ratio (three candidates for every one vacancy).
  2. Practical Examination: This stage carries 30 marks. It tests the candidate's actual hands-on skills in data processing, database management, and technical troubleshooting. This is the most crucial part as it reflects the day-to-day duties of the job.
  3. Personal Interview: The final stage carries 30 marks. It assesses the candidate's communication skills, situational judgment, and overall suitability for the judicial environment.

Final Merit: The final selection list will be prepared based on the combined marks of the Practical Examination and the Interview. The marks of the Preliminary exam are only for qualifying and shortlisting purposes and are not added to the final merit.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

Understanding the syllabus is essential for clearing the Online Preliminary Exam. The exam consists of 100 questions to be solved in 120 minutes (2 hours).

Technical Subjects (80% Weightage):

  • Computer Networks: OSI Model, TCP/IP, Routing algorithms, Network Security, Firewalls, and Wireless Networking.
  • Operating Systems: Windows and Linux/Unix administration, Shell programming, Memory management, and Process scheduling.
  • Database Management Systems (DBMS): SQL queries, Normalization, ER Diagrams, and Database Security.
  • Data Structures & Algorithms: Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues, Sorting, and Searching techniques.
  • Object-Oriented Programming (OOPS): Basic concepts of Java or C++, Classes, Inheritance, and Polymorphism.

General Subjects (20% Weightage):

  • General English: Grammar, Vocabulary, and Comprehension.
  • General Awareness: Current Affairs with a focus on Madhya Pradesh and Legal/Judicial technology news.

PET and Practical Exam Details

There is no physical endurance test (PET) for the Data Processing Assistant role. However, the Practical Examination serves as the physical assessment of your technical speed and accuracy. During the 30-mark practical exam, candidates will be tested on:

  • Data Entry Speed: Accuracy and speed of entering complex alphanumeric data into a database.
  • Troubleshooting: Resolving common hardware and software errors in a Windows/Linux environment.
  • Query Execution: Writing and executing SQL queries to retrieve specific data from a provided database.
  • Office Productivity: Advanced features of MS Excel and MS Access.

How to Prepare for MPHC DPA Exam

Preparation for the MP High Court DPA exam requires a blend of studying core computer science concepts and practicing data entry skills. Since the practical exam and interview form 100% of the final merit, you cannot afford to ignore hands-on practice.

Focus on Core IT Subjects: Use standard textbooks to revise DBMS and Operating Systems. Most questions in the Prelims will be technical. Ensure you have a strong grasp of Linux commands as the e-Courts system often utilizes open-source platforms. Practice SQL queries daily, focusing on joins and aggregate functions.

Enhance Practical Skills: Spend at least one hour daily on a typing tutor to increase your alphanumeric data entry speed. Familiarize yourself with the layout of court databases (which often use Postgres or MySQL). For the interview, stay updated on the latest e-Governance initiatives by the Ministry of Law and Justice, such as the e-Filing 3.0 portal and virtual courtrooms.

Best Books for Preparation

The following books are highly recommended for the technical and general portions of the MP High Court Data Processing Assistant recruitment.

Subject Recommended Book
Database Management "Database System Concepts" by Abraham Silberschatz.
Operating Systems "Operating System Concepts" by Galvin.
Computer Networks "Data Communications and Networking" by Behrouz A. Forouzan.
General Knowledge "Madhya Pradesh Ek Parichay" by McGraw Hill or Lucent's GK.
English "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.

How to Apply

The application process is strictly online. Candidates must follow these steps to ensure their application is not rejected due to technical errors.

  1. Visit the official website of the High Court of Madhya Pradesh: www.mphc.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the "Recruitment / Result" tab.
  3. Find the link that says "Online Application Form for Data Processing Assistant Exam-2025."
  4. Click on "Registration" and provide your Name, Email ID, and Mobile Number.
  5. After successful registration, log in using the ID and Password sent to your email.
  6. Fill in all educational details and the 3-year experience details accurately.
  7. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and necessary certificates.
  8. Pay the application fee online.
  9. Review the application form carefully and click on "Submit." Print the final application for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Before filling out the online form, keep the following documents ready in digital format. Ensure the file sizes are within the limits specified in the portal (usually 20kb to 100kb).

  1. Educational Marksheets: 10th, 12th, and Graduation (B.Sc CS/IT or BCA) degree and marksheets.
  2. Experience Certificate: A certificate from a recognized firm/institution proving 3 years of experience in Data Entry/IT.
  3. Caste Certificate: For candidates seeking reservation (only MP residents).
  4. Domicile Certificate: To prove residence in Madhya Pradesh.
  5. Photograph & Signature: Recent color passport photograph and a clear signature on white paper with black ink.
  6. ID Proof: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID for identity verification.

Important Links

Link Description URL Link
Official MP High Court Website www.mphc.gov.in
Direct Application Link Apply Online Here
Technical Helpline Number +91-7996339995

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Is the 3-year experience mandatory for the DPA post?

Yes, the notification clearly states that a minimum of 3 years of experience in Operating Systems and Data Entry operations is a compulsory eligibility requirement.

Q2: Can candidates from other states apply?

Yes, candidates from all over India can apply, but they will be treated as Unreserved (UR) category candidates and must pay the full application fee of ₹943.40.

Q3: What happens if I make a mistake in my application form?

MP High Court usually provides a short correction window after the application deadline. However, certain fields like Name, Category, and Mobile Number may not be editable, so fill the form with extreme care.

Q4: Is there any negative marking in the Preliminary Exam?

Yes, typically the MP High Court exams have a negative marking of 0.25 for every wrong answer, though you should check the detailed admit card instructions for the final word.

Q5: When will the exam be conducted?

The exam date has not been officially announced yet, but it is expected to be held in late December 2025 or early January 2026. Keep visiting the official website for updates.

Q6: What is the nature of the DPA job at MP High Court?

It is a technical job involving the maintenance of case databases, digital archiving, supporting the e-Courts software, and assisting the court administration in technical matters.

entry.title: WBPHIDCL Recruitment 2025: Assistant & Sub-Assistant Engineer Posts Open
entry.content:
WBPHIDCL Recruitment 2025: Assistant & Sub-Assistant Engineer Posts Open

A fantastic opportunity for Civil and Electrical Engineers to join a prestigious West Bengal government corporation on a contractual basis. Online applications are open until December 19, 2025.

Post Name: Assistant Engineer (Civil/Electrical), Sub-Assistant Engineer (Civil/Electrical)
Last Date: 19/12/2025
Total Posts: Not Specified

The West Bengal Police Housing & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (WBPHIDCL), formerly known as the West Bengal State Police Housing Corporation Limited, has announced a significant career opportunity for qualified and talented candidates in the fields of Civil and Electrical Engineering. Through Recruitment Advertisement No. 03/2025, WBPHIDCL has decided to conduct a Combined Competitive Examination for recruitment to the posts of Assistant Engineer (Civil and Electrical) and Sub-Assistant Engineer (Civil and Electrical).

This recruitment will be on a purely contractual basis, presenting a golden chance for engineering graduates and diploma holders to work with a prestigious corporation of the West Bengal Government. WBPHIDCL plays a vital role in the development of housing and infrastructure for the state's police force, and candidates selected through this recruitment will directly contribute to these important projects. The online application process is open to all Indian citizens, with the selection process comprising a written examination followed by an interview. The last date to apply is December 19, 2025.

WBPHIDCL Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details for this recruitment drive. Candidates should take note of the important dates and other key information to ensure a smooth application process.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization West Bengal Police Housing & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (WBPHIDCL)
Advertisement Number 03 / 2025
Post Names Assistant Engineer (Civil/Electrical), Sub-Assistant Engineer (Civil/Electrical)
Total Vacancies Various (Number not specified)
Job Location West Bengal
Application Mode Online
Application Dates December 4, 2025, to December 19, 2025

Vacancy Details and Post Breakdown

While WBPHIDCL has not disclosed the total number of vacancies for this recruitment drive, applications are invited for the following four key engineering positions:

Sr. No. Post Name Discipline
1 Assistant Engineer Civil
2 Assistant Engineer Electrical
3 Sub-Assistant Engineer Civil
4 Sub-Assistant Engineer Electrical

Application Fee Details

  • All Candidates: No Fee Mentioned

The official notification does not mention any application fee. Therefore, it can be assumed that the application process is free of cost. Candidates are advised to re-verify this on the official website while applying.

Eligibility Criteria: Education, Age, and More

Candidates must fulfill the specific educational and age-related criteria to be eligible for these posts. All qualifications must be from an institution/university recognized by AICTE or WBSCT&VE&SD (formerly WBSCTE).

Educational Qualification

Post Name Required Educational Qualification
Assistant Engineer (Civil) A B.E./B.Tech./B.Sc. (Engineering) Degree or equivalent in Civil Engineering from a recognized university/institute.
OR
A Diploma in Civil Engineering with a minimum of five (5) years of working experience in the relevant field in any department of Central/State Govt./PSU/reputed private company.
Assistant Engineer (Electrical) A B.E./B.Tech./B.Sc. (Engineering) Degree or equivalent in Electrical Engineering or Electrical & Electronics Engineering from a recognized university/institute.
OR
A Diploma in Electrical Engineering or Electrical & Electronics Engineering with a minimum of five (5) years of working experience in the relevant field in any department of Central/State Govt./PSU/reputed private company.
Sub-Assistant Engineer (Civil) A three (3) year Diploma course in Civil Engineering from a recognized institute.
Sub-Assistant Engineer (Electrical) A three (3) year Diploma course in Electrical Engineering or Electrical & Electronics Engineering from a recognized institute.

Age Limit (as of January 1, 2025)

Criteria Age
Minimum Age 21 years
Maximum Age 40 years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

  • For SC and ST candidates of West Bengal only: 5 years.
  • For OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates of West Bengal only: 3 years.
  • SC/ST/OBC candidates from other states will be treated as General category candidates and will not receive any age relaxation.

Selection Process

The selection process for the posts of Assistant Engineer and Sub-Assistant Engineer will be conducted in two successive stages:

  1. Written Examination:
    • This is the first stage of the selection process. The examination will consist of Multiple Choice Objective Type Questions.
    • The examination center will be located in Kolkata only.
    • Negative Marking: There will be a provision for negative marking for each incorrect answer. The details of the marking scheme will be printed on the top of the question booklet.
    • The Selection Committee reserves the right to fix qualifying marks and cut-off marks for the written examination.
  2. Interview:
    • Candidates who are successful in the written examination will be called for a personal interview.
    • The interview will be conducted at the WBPHIDCL office located in Kolkata.
    • Scrutiny of original documents will be done at the time of the interview. Candidates must bring all original certificates and testimonials for verification.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the candidate's combined performance in the written examination and the interview.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus (Expected)

While the official notification does not provide a detailed exam pattern, based on standard competitive exams for AE/SAE roles, the following structure and syllabus can be expected. Candidates must refer to the official website for the final, detailed syllabus.

Expected Exam Pattern

Part Subject Expected No. of Questions Expected Marks Duration
Part A General Awareness & Reasoning 20-30 20-30 2 Hours (Approx.)
Part B Technical Discipline (Civil/Electrical) 70-80 70-80
Total 100 100
Disclaimer: The pattern above is indicative. The actual number of questions, marks, and duration will be specified by WBPHIDCL in the admit card or a separate notification.

Expected Syllabus

For Assistant/Sub-Assistant Engineer (Civil):

  • Building Materials & Construction: Physical and chemical properties, classification, standard tests, uses of materials.
  • Surveying: Principles of surveying, measurements of distance and angles, leveling, theodolite traversing, tacheometry.
  • Soil Mechanics: Origin of soil, soil classification, three-phase system, fundamental definitions, permeability, consolidation, shear strength.
  • Fluid Mechanics & Hydraulics: Properties of fluids, fluid pressure, flow kinematics, dynamics of ideal fluids, open channel flow, pumps and turbines.
  • Structural Analysis: Determinate and indeterminate structures, bending moments and shear force diagrams, trusses, arches.
  • Design of Structures: Design of concrete and steel structures (RCC beams, slabs, columns; steel tension and compression members, beams).
  • Transportation Engineering: Highway engineering (geometric design, pavement design), Railway engineering.
  • Environmental Engineering: Water quality, water treatment, sewerage systems, wastewater treatment.

For Assistant/Sub-Assistant Engineer (Electrical):

  • Basic Electrical Engineering & Circuit Theory: Network graphs, KCL, KVL, AC fundamentals, transient response, resonance.
  • Electrical Machines: DC Machines, Transformers, Three-phase induction motors, Synchronous machines.
  • Power Systems: Generation, transmission, and distribution of power; fault analysis; power system protection; load flow studies.
  • Control Systems: Basic control system components, feedback principle, transfer function, stability analysis (Routh-Hurwitz, Nyquist).
  • Electrical & Electronic Measurements: Bridges and potentiometers, measurement of voltage, current, power, energy, and frequency; PMMC, moving iron instruments.
  • Power Electronics: Characteristics of semiconductor devices, AC to DC, DC to DC, AC to AC, and DC to AC converters.
  • Analog & Digital Electronics: Diode circuits, transistors, operational amplifiers, combinational and sequential logic circuits.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for this recruitment. The selection is based purely on technical knowledge and interview performance.

How to Prepare for the WBPHIDCL Examination

A well-planned preparation strategy is crucial to succeed in this competitive examination. Candidates should focus on their core engineering discipline as it will carry the maximum weightage.

  1. Master the Core Technical Subjects:
    • Civil Engineering: Focus on fundamental subjects like Strength of Materials, Structural Analysis, RCC & Steel Design, Soil Mechanics, Fluid Mechanics, and Environmental Engineering.
    • Electrical Engineering: Strengthen your command over core subjects like Circuit Theory, Electrical Machines, Power Systems, Control Systems, and Measurements.
    • Refer to your standard degree/diploma level textbooks and make concise notes for revision.
  2. Practice MCQs Extensively:
    • Since the exam is objective, speed and accuracy are vital. Solve previous years' question papers from exams like GATE, ESE (IES), SSC JE, and other state-level AE/JE examinations.
    • Pay close attention to the negative marking scheme. Avoid guesswork and attempt only those questions you are confident about.
  3. Time Management is Key: Regularly take full-length mock tests to manage your time effectively during the actual exam. This will help you identify your weak areas and improve your speed.
  4. Prepare for the Interview:
    • Start your interview preparation alongside the written exam. Brush up on the fundamental concepts of your core technical subjects.
    • Research the functions of WBPHIDCL and ongoing major infrastructure projects in West Bengal.
    • Work on your communication skills and confidence. Be prepared to answer questions about your final year project, practical training, and any work experience.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of standard reference books that can be immensely helpful for your preparation:

For Civil Engineering:
  • Strength of Materials by R.K. Bansal / S. Ramamrutham
  • R.C.C. Structures by B.C. Punmia
  • Soil Mechanics and Foundation Engineering by K.R. Arora
  • Fluid Mechanics by R.K. Bansal
  • Surveying by B.C. Punmia
  • Objective Civil Engineering by R.S. Khurmi & J.K. Gupta
For Electrical Engineering:
  • Circuit Theory: Analysis and Synthesis by A. Chakrabarti
  • Electrical Machinery by P.S. Bimbhra
  • Power System Engineering by D.P. Kothari and I.J. Nagrath
  • Control Systems Engineering by I.J. Nagrath and M. Gopal
  • An Integrated Course in Electrical Engineering by J.B. Gupta

How to Apply for WBPHIDCL Recruitment

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must apply through the official WBPHIDCL website.

  1. Visit the official website of West Bengal Police Housing & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited: https://www.wbphidcl.com.
  2. Find and click on the link related to "Recruitment Advertisement No: 03 / 2025" available on the website.
  3. Read all the instructions and eligibility criteria carefully before proceeding to apply online.
  4. Click on the "Apply Online" link and fill in the application form with all required information, such as personal details, educational qualifications, and contact information, accurately.
  5. Important: A candidate must apply for ONE post only. If a candidate applies for more than one post, only the first application submitted by them will be accepted.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other documents, if required, in the prescribed format.
  7. Review all the information filled in the application form before final submission. No changes can be made after the final submission.
  8. Take a printout of the submitted application form for future reference.

Online Application Window: The link for online applications will be active from 00:00:01 on December 4, 2025, to 23:59:59 on December 19, 2025.

Documents Needed to Apply and for Verification

Candidates will have to produce their original documents for verification at the time of the interview. Candidates claiming reservation must have certificates issued by the competent authority in West Bengal before the closing date of application.

  • All educational qualification certificates and mark sheets (Degree/Diploma).
  • Experience certificate (where applicable).
  • Proof of date of birth (10th certificate or birth certificate).
  • SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) certificate issued by the competent authority of West Bengal.
    • Sub-Divisional Officer of the concerned sub-division in the district.
    • In Kolkata, the District Magistrate, South 24-Parganas, or an Additional District Magistrate authorized by them.
  • Important Note for OBC Candidates: OBC candidates must produce a renewed or revalidated certificate regarding their non-creamy layer status as per existing government rules at the time of interview and joining.
  • Identity proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).
  • Passport-size photographs.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here (When Link is Active)
Apply Online Click Here (Active from 04.12.2025)
Official Website https://www.wbphidcl.com

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the WBPHIDCL recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is December 19, 2025.

Question 2: Can I apply for more than one post?

No, as per the notification, a candidate can apply for only one post. If multiple applications are submitted, only the first one will be considered.

Question 3: Can candidates from other states avail reservation benefits?

No, reservation benefits for SC/ST/OBC are applicable only to domiciles of West Bengal. Candidates from other states will be treated as General category candidates.

Question 4: What is the selection process for these posts?

The selection process consists of an objective-type written examination followed by a personal interview.

Question 5: Will there be negative marking in the written exam?

Yes, the official notification states that there will be negative marking for every incorrect answer in the written examination.

entry.title: UPPSC Dental Surgeon Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
UPPSC Dental Surgeon Recruitment 2026

Advertisement No. D-6/E-1/2025: The Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) has announced a massive recruitment drive for 2,158 Group-B Gazetted posts across various departments, including Veterinary Officer, Dental Surgeon, Medical Officer (Ayurvedic, Unani, Homoeopathic), Inspector of Drugs, and more. The online application process commences on December 22, 2025, and the last date for fee payment and submission is January 22, 2026.

Post Name: Veterinary Officer, Dental Surgeon, Medical Officer, Vetting Officer, Inspector of Drugs, and more
Last Date: 22 January 2026
Total Posts: 2158

The Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC) has released a major recruitment notification, inviting online applications from eligible candidates for a wide array of technical and medical positions in various government departments of Uttar Pradesh. This extensive drive aims to fill a total of 2,158 vacancies for prestigious Group-B Gazetted posts. The recruitment covers specialized roles such as Veterinary Officer, Vetting Officer, Swasthya Shiksha Adhikari, Inspector of Drugs, Dental Surgeon, and Medical Officers in Ayurvedic, Unani, and Homoeopathic streams.

The selection process for these posts will be based on a Screening Examination followed by an Interview, with the final merit list giving 75% weightage to the written test and 25% to the interview. A crucial prerequisite for applying is having a One Time Registration (OTR) Number. This recruitment offers a golden opportunity for qualified professionals to secure a high-ranking government position in Uttar Pradesh with an attractive pay scale and the prestige of serving in the state's public sector.

UPPSC Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must adhere to the key dates and instructions. One Time Registration (OTR) is mandatory before applying.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC)
Advertisement Number D-6/E-1/2025
Post Names Veterinary Officer, Medical Officer, Dental Surgeon, Vetting Officer, etc.
Total Vacancies 2158
Application Start Date 22 December 2025
Last Date for Fee & Submission 22 January 2026
Last Date for Correction 29 January 2026
Application Mode Online
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Group-B, Gazetted)
Job Location Uttar Pradesh
Official Website https://uppsc.up.nic.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive is distributed across 10 different posts in various departments. All posts are Group-B Gazetted positions.

Department Post Name Total Vacancies Pay Scale
U.P. Livestock DepartmentVeterinary Officer404Level-10 (Grade Pay ₹5400)
U.P. Legislative DepartmentVetting Officer01Level-10 (₹56100-177500)
Directorate General of Family WelfareSwasthya Shiksha Adhikari221Level-7 (₹44900-142400)
Food Safety and Drug AdministrationInspector of Drugs26Level-8 (₹47600-151100)
Medical Health and Family Welfare Dept.Dental Surgeon157Level-10 (₹56100-177500)
Directorate of AyurvedaChikitsa Adhikari (Ayurved)168Level-10 (₹56100-177500)
Directorate of AyurvedaMedical Officer, Community Health (Ayurvedic & Unani)884Level-10 (₹56100-177500)
Directorate of UnaniChikitsa Adhikari (Unani)25Level-10 (₹56100-177500)
Labour Department (ESI)Chikitsa Adhikari (Homoeopathic)07Level-10 (₹56100-177500)
Directorate of HomoeopathyHomoeopathic Medical Officer265Level-10 (₹56100-177500)
Total 2158
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may change. For a detailed category-wise reservation breakdown, refer to the official notification.

Application Fee

  • For Unreserved (UR) / EWS / OBC: ₹105/- (Exam fee ₹80 + Online processing fee ₹25)
  • For SC / ST: ₹65/- (Exam fee ₹40 + Online processing fee ₹25)
  • For Persons with Disabilities (PWD): ₹25/- (Online processing fee only)
  • For Ex-Servicemen: ₹65/- (Exam fee ₹40 + Online processing fee ₹25)

The fee must be paid online. Dependents of Freedom Fighters and Women candidates will pay the fee according to their original category.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must possess the specific educational qualifications and meet the age criteria for the post they are applying for.

Post Name Age Limit & Essential Qualifications
Veterinary Officer Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: A degree of Bachelor of Veterinary Science and Animal Husbandry (B.V.Sc. & A.H.) from a recognized university. Must be duly registered with the Uttar Pradesh Veterinary Council.
Vetting Officer Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: Bachelor of Laws degree (3 years or 5 years) with a minimum of 50% marks from a recognized university.
Swasthya Shiksha Adhikari Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: Master's Degree in Sociology or any subject of Social Science (including Anthropology, Psychology, Political Science, Geography, History, and Economics).
Inspector of Drugs Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: Degree in Pharmacy or Pharmaceutical Sciences or Medicine with specialization in Clinical Pharmacology/Microbiology. Also requires relevant experience (18 months in manufacturing/testing or 3 years in inspection of firms).
Dental Surgeon Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: Bachelor of Dental Surgery (B.D.S.) degree from a recognized university. Must have completed the mandatory rotatory internship and be registered with the U.P. Dental Council.
Chikitsa Adhikari (Ayurved) Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: A degree in Ayurveda (5 years degree/diploma). Registration as a Vaidya with the Board of Indian Medicine, U.P. At least six months of professional experience.
Medical Officer, Community Health (Ayurvedic & Unani) Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: A degree in Ayurveda or Unani Tib. Registration as a Vaidya or Hakeem with the Board of Indian Medicine, U.P. At least six months of professional experience.
Chikitsa Adhikari (Unani) Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: A degree in Unani Tib (5 years degree/diploma). Registration as a Hakeem with the Board of Indian Medicine, U.P. At least six months of professional experience.
Chikitsa Adhikari (Homoeopathic) Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: A recognized degree or diploma in Homoeopathy (minimum 4 years duration). Must be duly registered with the Homoeopathic Medicine Board, U.P.
Homoeopathic Medical Officer Age: 21-40 years.
Qualification: A recognized degree in Homoeopathy (minimum 5 years duration) OR a diploma (minimum 4 years duration). Must be duly registered with the Homoeopathic Medicine Board, U.P.

Selection Process

The selection process for all the advertised posts will be conducted in two main stages.

  1. Screening Examination: An objective-type screening examination will be conducted to shortlist candidates for the interview. The Commission may hold this test if the number of applicants is large.
  2. Interview: Candidates who qualify in the screening examination will be called for a personal interview.
  3. Final Merit List: The final selection will be based on a merit list prepared by adding the marks obtained in the screening examination and the interview. The weightage will be 75% for the Screening Test and 25% for the Interview.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The Screening Examination will be an objective-type (MCQ) test. The detailed syllabus for each post will be issued by the commission in due course. However, a general pattern can be expected.

  • Mode of Exam: Objective Type (OMR-based or CBT).
  • Key Feature: There will be negative marking for wrong answers. For each incorrect answer, one-third (0.33) of the marks assigned to that question will be deducted.
  • Syllabus: The syllabus will primarily consist of two parts:
    1. General Studies: This section will cover topics like History of India, Indian National Movement, Geography, Polity, Economy, Current National and International Events, General Science, and basic aptitude.
    2. Subject-Specific Paper: This will be the main paper, with questions based on the essential academic qualification for the post (e.g., questions from the B.V.Sc. & A.H. syllabus for Veterinary Officer, B.D.S. syllabus for Dental Surgeon, etc.).
  • Minimum Qualifying Marks: Candidates must secure a minimum of 40% (for General/EWS/OBC) and 35% (for SC/ST) in the screening test to be considered for the merit list.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for any of the posts mentioned in this advertisement.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A focused preparation strategy is essential to succeed in this high-level competitive examination.

  • Strengthen Your Core Subject: The subject-specific paper will be the most crucial part of the screening test. Thoroughly revise all subjects from your graduate/post-graduate degree as per the post's requirement.
  • Prepare General Studies: Dedicate daily time to studying General Studies. Focus on static GK and stay updated with current affairs, especially those relevant to Uttar Pradesh.
  • Practice with Negative Marking: Solve as many mock tests and previous year papers of UPPSC as possible. Practice answering questions carefully to minimize errors, as there is a 1/3rd negative marking.
  • Interview Preparation: For the interview, be prepared to answer in-depth questions about your domain knowledge, your profile, and your suitability for the government role.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates should refer to their academic textbooks and standard GS guides.

  • Subject-Specific Papers: Refer to the standard textbooks from your graduation/post-graduation for your respective discipline (e.g., Veterinary, Dental, Ayurveda, Pharmacy, Law, etc.).
  • General Studies (GS): "Lucent's General Knowledge" for static GK, "Manorama Year Book" for current affairs, and a dedicated book on Uttar Pradesh GK (like Ghatna Chakra or Pariksha Vani).
  • Previous Year Papers: Publications that compile UPPSC's previous year question papers are invaluable for understanding the exam pattern and question level.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online and requires a One Time Registration (OTR) number.

  1. Step 1 (OTR): If you haven't already, first complete the One Time Registration (OTR) on the UPPSC OTR portal: https://otr.pariksha.nic.in. This must be done at least 72 hours before you plan to apply.
  2. Step 2 (Apply): Visit the official UPPSC website: https://uppsc.up.nic.in.
  3. Click on the "ALL NOTIFICATIONS/ADVERTISEMENTS" link.
  4. Find the Advertisement No. D-6/E-1/2025 and click on the "Apply" button.
  5. Authenticate yourself using your OTR number. Your personal details will be auto-filled.
  6. Fill in the essential qualifications for the specific post you are applying for.
  7. Step 3 (Fee Payment): Proceed to pay the application fee online through the SBI MOPS payment gateway.
  8. Step 4 (Final Submission): After successful fee payment, submit your application. Download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

While most documents are required during verification, candidates should have the following ready for filling the OTR and application form:

  1. Scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Matriculation (10th) certificate for Date of Birth verification.
  3. All educational mark sheets and degree/diploma certificates to fill in qualification details.
  4. Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC), EWS Certificate, Domicile Certificate, and other relevant certificates for claiming reservation.
  5. Valid mobile number and email ID linked with your Aadhaar.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
One Time Registration (OTR) Portal Click Here
Apply Online Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is One Time Registration (OTR) mandatory to apply for this recruitment?

Yes, it is absolutely mandatory. Candidates must obtain an OTR number from the UPPSC's OTR portal at least 72 hours before they can submit their final application.

Q2: Is there a negative marking in the screening exam?

Yes, there will be a penalty of one-third (0.33) of the marks for every wrong answer in the objective-type screening examination.

Q3: What is the weightage of the interview in the final selection?

The final merit list will be prepared by giving 75% weightage to the marks obtained in the screening test and 25% weightage to the marks obtained in the interview.

Q4: I am from another state. Can I apply for these posts?

Yes, you can apply. However, you will be considered under the Unreserved (General) category and will not be eligible for any reservation benefits (like SC/ST/OBC/EWS/Female reservation) applicable to Uttar Pradesh domiciles.

entry.title: Central University of South Bihar Recruitment: 23 Group A, B, C Posts Open
entry.content:
Central University of South Bihar Recruitment: 23 Group A, B, C Posts Open

The Central University of South Bihar (CUSB) has invited applications for various non-teaching positions, including Officer, Librarian, Clerk, and MTS.

Post Name: Various Group A, B, C Posts (Officer, Assistant Librarian, Clerk, MTS, etc.)
Last Date: To be announced in the official notification
Total Posts: 23

The Central University of South Bihar (CUSB), Gaya, has released a detailed notification for the recruitment of non-teaching positions in its various departments. This recruitment drive aims to fill a total of 23 posts under Group 'A', 'B', and 'C'. The recruitment will be done on the basis of Direct Recruitment and Deputation. This is an excellent opportunity for qualified candidates who wish to build a career in a prestigious central university.

These posts include a wide range of roles from Internal Audit Officer, Chief Security Officer, and Medical Officer to Assistant, Clerk, Laboratory Attendant, and Multi-Tasking Staff. Each post has different educational qualifications, experience, and age limits. Interested and eligible candidates are advised to carefully read the official notification before applying. This article will provide you with all the important information related to this recruitment, such as vacancy details, eligibility criteria, selection process, and application procedure.

Important Dates

Candidates applying for the CUSB non-teaching recruitment will need to keep an eye on the important dates to be released by the university. This is a permanent government job, and the selected candidates will be posted at the Central University of South Bihar, Gaya, Bihar.

Event Date/Details
Online Application Start Date To be announced soon
Last Date for Online Application To be announced soon
Job Type Government Job (Central University)
Job Location Gaya, Bihar

Vacancy Details

A total of 23 posts are to be filled through this recruitment drive. The details of the posts are given below:

Post Name Number of Posts Group Pay Level
Internal Audit Officer (Deputation) 01 (UR) A Level-12
Chief Security Officer (Deputation) 01 (UR) A Level-11
Medical Officer (Female) 01 (UR) A Level-10
Assistant Librarian 01 (UR/PwBD) A Level-10
Private Secretary 01 (UR/PwBD) B Level-7
Assistant 01 (ST) B Level-6
Personal Assistant 01 (UR) B Level-6
Junior Engineer (Electrical) 01 (UR) B Level-6
Pharmacist 01 (UR) C Level-5
Upper Division Clerk (UDC) 01 (UR) C Level-4
Horticulture Supervisor 01 (UR) C Level-4
Laboratory Assistant 01 (EWS) C Level-4
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) 05 (UR-03, OBC-01, SC-01) C Level-2
Cook 01 (OBC) C Level-2
Laboratory Attendant 02 (UR-01, ST-01) C Level-1
Library Attendant 01 (UR) C Level-1
Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS) 01 (UR) C Level-1
Total 23
Note: Reservation benefits will be provided as per the rules of the Central Government. UR-Unreserved, OBC-Other Backward Class, SC-Scheduled Caste, ST-Scheduled Tribe, EWS-Economically Weaker Section, PwBD-Persons with Benchmark Disabilities.

Application Fee

Information about the application fee will be released along with the detailed official notification. Generally, the fee for different categories may be as follows:

  • General/OBC/EWS Candidates: (Fee to be announced on the website)
  • SC/ST/PwBD/Female Candidates: (May be exempted from fee/reduced fee)

Candidates are advised to check the official website for the fee structure.

Eligibility Criteria

A brief overview of the required educational qualifications, experience, and age limit for various posts at CUSB is given below:

Post Name Educational Qualification and Experience Age Limit
Internal Audit Officer Officers from Audit/Accounts services of Central/State Govt. holding an analogous post or having 3 years of service in Level 11 or 5 years in Level 10. (Deputation) -
Chief Security Officer Officers from Police/Para-military forces who have served for five years in Pay Level '7' or above. (Deputation) -
Medical Officer (Female) MBBS Degree recognized by MCI + 3 years of experience. (1 year of experience in a Gynaecology ward is essential). 40 years
Assistant Librarian Master's Degree in Library Science/Information Science with 55% marks + a good academic record + NET/SET/SLET qualified or Ph.D. as per UGC Regulations 2009/2016. 40 years
Private Secretary Bachelor's Degree + 3 years of experience + 120 wpm English or 100 wpm Hindi stenography + 35 wpm English or 30 wpm Hindi typing. 35 years
Assistant Bachelor's Degree + 3 years of experience as UDC or equivalent in Level 4. Proficiency in typing and computer applications. 35 years
Personal Assistant Bachelor's Degree + 100 wpm stenography + 35/30 wpm typing + 2 years of experience. 35 years
Junior Engineer (Electrical) Bachelor's Degree in Electrical Engineering + 1 year of experience OR Diploma + 3 years of experience. 35 years
Pharmacist 10+2 + 2-year Diploma in Pharmacy + Registration with State Pharmacy Council. 32 years
Upper Division Clerk (UDC) Bachelor's Degree + 2 years of experience as LDC + 35 wpm English or 30 wpm Hindi typing. 32 years
Horticulture Supervisor B.Sc. (Agriculture) with specialization in Horticulture + 2 years of experience. 32 years
Laboratory Assistant B.Sc. (Physics) + 2 years of experience in maintaining sophisticated scientific instruments. 32 years
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) Bachelor's Degree + 35 wpm English or 30 wpm Hindi typing + Proficiency in computer operations. 32 years
Cook 10th Pass + 2 years of experience. ITI certificate (Bakery) is desirable. 32 years
Laboratory Attendant 10+2 (Science) OR 10th Pass (with Science subject) + Skill certificate in Laboratory Technology. 32 years
Library Attendant 10+2 Pass + Certificate course in Library Science + 1 year of experience. 32 years
Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS) 10th Pass or ITI Pass. 32 years

Selection Process

The selection process will vary depending on the nature of the posts. It may include the following stages:

  1. Written Test: An objective type written examination may be conducted for most of the Group 'B' and 'C' posts.
  2. Skill Test: A stenography and typing test will be conducted for posts like Private Secretary, Personal Assistant, LDC, and UDC.
  3. Interview: Selection for Group 'A' posts may be primarily through an interview. An interview may also be conducted for some Group 'B' and 'C' posts.
  4. Document Verification: All selected candidates will have to get their original documents verified.
  5. Deputation: Selection for deputation posts will be done as per Central Government rules.

Exam Pattern

The detailed exam pattern and syllabus for the posts for which a written test will be conducted will be released by the university on its official website. Generally, the exam may include questions on General English, General Hindi, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, General Awareness, and subject-related knowledge of the post.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

Except for the Chief Security Officer, a Physical Efficiency Test (PET) is not required for any other post. The standards required for the Chief Security Officer will be mentioned in the detailed notification.

How to Prepare

Adopt a strategic approach to prepare for these posts:

  • Post-Specific Preparation: Study the subjects related to your post in depth. For example, for Assistant Librarian, focus on Library Science; for Pharmacist, on Pharmacy; and for Engineer, on engineering subjects.
  • Study of General Subjects: Prepare for general subjects like General Knowledge, Current Affairs, Logical Reasoning, and English/Hindi language.
  • Practice of Skills: If you are applying for a Clerk or Secretary post, practice typing and stenography regularly.
  • Previous Question Papers: Solve previous years' question papers from similar recruitments in other central universities to get an idea of the exam pattern.

Best Books for Preparation

Selecting the right study material is crucial for good preparation.

  • General Awareness: Lucent's General Knowledge.
  • Reasoning: 'A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning' by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: 'Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations' by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • English Language: 'Objective General English' by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Post-Specific Subjects: Standard graduate or postgraduate level textbooks in your respective field.

How to Apply

Candidates will have to apply online through the official website of CUSB. The steps for the application process are as follows:

  1. Visit the official website of the Central University of South Bihar: www.cusb.ac.in.
  2. Click on the 'Recruitment' or 'Careers' section.
  3. Find the recruitment notification for the respective non-teaching posts and click on the "Apply Online" link.
  4. First, register and create a login ID and password.
  5. Log in and fill in all the required personal, educational, and experience-related details in the application form.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your photo, signature, and other required documents.
  7. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
  8. Review all the filled information before finally submitting the application form.
  9. Take a printout of the filled application form for future reference.

Documents Required for Application

The following documents will be required during the online application and document verification:

  1. Scanned copy of passport-size photo and signature.
  2. Certificates and marksheets of educational qualifications (10th, 12th, Diploma, Graduation, Post-Graduation).
  3. Experience certificate.
  4. Caste/EWS/PwBD certificate (if applicable).
  5. Identity proof (Aadhaar card, Voter ID, etc.).
  6. NOC (No Objection Certificate) - if you are already in government service.

Important Links

Link Type URL
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Will be available on the official website
Official Website www.cusb.ac.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply?

Answer: The last date to apply has not been announced yet. Candidates should regularly check the official website of CUSB for the latest information.

Question 2: Can candidates from outside Bihar apply?

Answer: Yes, since this is a central university, eligible citizens from all states of India can apply for these posts.

Question 3: What will be the selection process?

Answer: The selection process will be different for each post and may include a written test, skill test, and/or interview.

Question 4: Can I apply for the Assistant Librarian post without clearing NET/SET?

Answer: Yes, you will be exempted from NET/SET/SLET if you have obtained a Ph.D. degree in accordance with the UGC Regulations 2009 or 2016.

entry.title: Goa University Recruitment 2025: Apply for 32 LDC and Driver Posts
entry.content:
Goa University Non-Teaching Recruitment 2025: Notification for 32 Vacancies

Direct Recruitment for Lower Division Clerk (LDC) and Driver Positions - Apply Online via Samarth Portal

Post Name: Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Driver (HMV), Driver (LMV)
Last Date: November 10, 2025
Total Posts: 32 Vacancies

Goa University, the premier institution of higher learning in the state of Goa, has officially released its latest Non-Teaching Recruitment 2025 notification. This recruitment drive aims to fill 32 permanent vacancies for the posts of Lower Division Clerk (LDC) and Driver (both Heavy Motor Vehicle and Light Motor Vehicle categories). Established in 1985 and located on the scenic Taleigao Plateau, Goa University is the only state university in Goa, making it a highly prestigious place to work. This notification is a part of the university's effort to strengthen its administrative and logistics wings, ensuring smoother operations across its various departments and affiliated colleges.

For job seekers in the coastal state, this recruitment is a significant opportunity to secure a permanent government job with the benefits of the 7th Central Pay Commission. The role of an LDC in a university setting is multi-faceted, involving student record management, departmental coordination, and administrative filing. On the other hand, the Driver positions are essential for the university’s transport department, catering to staff mobility and campus logistics. The application process is being conducted through the Samarth e-Gov Suite, which is a standardized platform used by universities across India for transparent and efficient recruitment. Candidates have until November 10, 2025, to submit their applications along with the prescribed fees.

One of the unique features of Goa University recruitment is the linguistic requirement. As per the Government of Goa's official policy, knowledge of Konkani is an essential requirement for most non-teaching posts, while knowledge of Marathi is considered desirable. This ensures that the administrative staff can effectively communicate with the local populace and students. This 2000-word guide provides an exhaustive breakdown of the eligibility criteria, category-wise vacancy distribution, detailed examination syllabus, and a step-by-step roadmap for a successful application and preparation strategy.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must strictly adhere to the timeline provided by the university. Late applications or incomplete submissions will be summarily rejected by the online portal. Since the university uses the Samarth portal, candidates who have already registered for other university recruitments can use their existing profiles to apply, making the process much faster.

Event Description Crucial Dates and Details
Official Notification Release October 17, 2025
Online Registration Opening October 2025 (Active)
Last Date to Submit Application November 10, 2025
Nature of Recruitment Direct / Permanent Basis
Organization Type State University (Goa)
Application Link unigoant.samarth.edu.in

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown by Category

The 32 vacancies are distributed among various social and economic categories as per the reservation rules of the Goa State Government. It is important for candidates to check their category-specific availability before applying, as the competition levels vary across different quotas.

Post Name Category-wise Distribution Total Vacancies
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) UR-17, OBC-05, ST-04, EWS-03, Sports-01 30
Driver (HMV) OBC - 01 01
Driver (LMV) UR - 01 01
Grand Total - 32
Horizontal Reservation: Within the categories mentioned above, the university also provides reservations for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) and Ex-Servicemen as per the state government mandates. If you belong to a reserved category, ensure you have a valid certificate issued by the competent authority of the Government of Goa.

Salary Structure and Benefits

Both LDC and Driver posts are categorized under the Level 2 Pay Matrix of the 7th Central Pay Commission. While the starting basic pay is fixed, the gross salary is significantly boosted by various allowances provided by the university.

  • Pay Level: Level 2 (7th CPC Matrix)
  • Initial Basic Pay: ₹19,900 per month
  • Estimated Gross Salary: Approximately ₹32,000 to ₹35,000 per month, depending on current DA rates and other factors.

Allowances and Benefits: Selected candidates will be eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), and specialized benefits for university staff. Additionally, permanent employees are covered under the New Pension Scheme (NPS) and are eligible for medical reimbursement facilities and leave travel concessions (LTC).

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

The university has specified clear academic and technical requirements for each post. Since these are non-technical administrative and logistics roles, the focus is on basic education and relevant skills like typing or driving.

  • Lower Division Clerk (LDC): Candidates must have passed the 12th Standard (Higher Secondary School Certificate) or its equivalent. Technical Skills: Proficiency in computer operations and a typing speed of at least 30 words per minute (w.p.m) is required.
  • Driver (HMV/LMV): Candidates must have passed the 10th Standard (SSC). Licensing: A valid driving license for the respective vehicle category is mandatory. For HMV, the license must specifically mention the "Heavy" vehicle endorsement.
  • Language Proficiency: For all posts, knowledge of Konkani is an essential requirement. Knowledge of Marathi is considered a desirable qualification.
  • Age Limit: The maximum age limit is 45 years. Age relaxation is applicable for SC, ST, OBC, PwBD, and Government servants as per the rules of the Government of Goa.

Selection Process and Examination Roadmap

The selection process at Goa University is transparent and relies entirely on merit. There are no interviews for Group C non-teaching posts like LDC and Driver, in accordance with the central and state government's decision to discontinue interviews for lower-grade recruitments.

  1. Written Examination: This is the primary stage for all applicants. It evaluates general aptitude, domain knowledge, and linguistic proficiency.
  2. Skill Test: For LDC candidates, a typing test is conducted to verify the 30 w.p.m. speed. For Drivers, a driving skill test is conducted on the university grounds or specified tracks to assess their maneuvering skills and knowledge of traffic rules.
  3. Document Verification (DV): Candidates who clear the written and skill tests are called for a verification of their original certificates, including domicile and language proficiency proofs.
  4. Medical Examination: A standard fitness check to ensure the candidate can perform the duties associated with the post.

Exam Pattern and Subject Wise Syllabus

Understanding the syllabus is key to cracking the exam. The written test is usually objective type (MCQs). The level of difficulty for LDC is 12th standard, while for Driver, it is 10th standard.

Syllabus for Lower Division Clerk (LDC):

  • General English (25%): Grammar, synonyms, antonyms, sentence correction, and comprehension.
  • General Awareness (25%): Current affairs, basics of the Indian Constitution, and specifically, the History and Geography of Goa.
  • Logical Reasoning & Math (25%): Numerical ability, profit and loss, time and work, and basic logical puzzles.
  • Konkani Language (25%): Basic grammar, vocabulary, and translation to test the "Essential" language requirement.

Syllabus for Driver: The exam focuses heavily on Traffic Rules and Signs, basic vehicle mechanics, maintenance knowledge, and road safety protocols, along with basic Konkani and general awareness.

Strategic Preparation for LDC and Driver Exams

To secure a place in the top 32, a candidate must have a structured 3-month preparation plan. Since the number of seats is limited, even a single mark can make a difference.

Phase 1: Language and Local Knowledge: Since Konkani is essential, non-native speakers who have studied in Goa should brush up on their Konkani grammar. Use school textbooks of the Goa Board for Konkani and Goa History/Geography. This section often becomes the deciding factor in merit lists.

Phase 2: Typing and Technical Skills: For LDCs, typing is a "must-pass" round. Don't wait for the written results to start practicing. Use software like "Typing Master" to reach a consistent speed of 35 w.p.m. to safely clear the 30 w.p.m. requirement during the actual test. For Drivers, staying updated with the latest RTO rules and electric vehicle (EV) basic maintenance is recommended.

Phase 3: Mock Tests and Time Management: Solve previous years' question papers of Goa University or other Goa state departments (like Goa Police or Goa Secretariat). Managing 100 questions in 90-120 minutes requires practice in eliminating incorrect options quickly.

Best Books for Preparation

While there are many general books, focusing on local Goan content is essential for these specific exams. Here is a curated list of study materials.

Subject / Post Recommended Book List
Konkani Language Goa Board Textbooks (Std 1 to 10) and Konkani Grammar by various local authors.
Goa General Knowledge "The Wonderland Goa" or other GK books specific to Goa state.
General Aptitude Lucent's General Knowledge and Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal.
English Language High School English Grammar and Composition by Wren & Martin.
Driver Trade RTO Traffic Signs and Rules manual (English/Konkani).

How to Apply Online via Samarth Portal

Goa University utilizes the Samarth e-Gov Suite for its recruitment. This ensures a transparent and paperless process. Follow these steps carefully to ensure your application is successful.

  1. Visit the official application portal: unigoant.samarth.edu.in.
  2. Click on the "New Registration" link if you are a first-time user.
  3. Enter your Name, Email ID, and Mobile Number. Verify your email using the OTP sent to your inbox.
  4. Login with your credentials and select the post you want to apply for (LDC or Driver).
  5. Personal & Academic Info: Fill in your 10th and 12th marks. Ensure you mention your proficiency in Konkani correctly.
