  6. Document Upload: Upload your passport-size photo, signature, and relevant certificates. The file size should be within the limits specified (usually 10kb to 100kb).
  7. Application Fee: Pay the fee of ₹500 (General) or ₹250 (SC/ST) using the integrated online payment gateway.
  8. Final Submission: Review your application for any spelling errors. Click on "Final Submit" and download the PDF for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Having your documents ready in digital format will save you from last-minute stress. All documents should be scanned from the original certificates.

  1. Educational Proof: 10th and 12th Standard mark sheets and passing certificates.
  2. Identity & Residence: Aadhaar Card and 15-year Domicile Certificate of Goa (Essential for reservation and local preference).
  3. Language Proof: Certificates proving knowledge of Konkani (Usually covered by 10th/12th mark sheets if studied locally).
  4. Technical Proof: Typing certificate (for LDC) and a valid Driving License (for Driver).
  5. Caste Certificate: Valid SC/ST/OBC certificate issued by the Government of Goa.
  6. Category Proof: EWS or PwBD certificates if applicable.

Important Links and Official Resources

Resource Description Official Link
Samarth Online Application Portal Apply Now
Goa University Official Website unigoa.ac.in
Recruitment & Vacancy Section View Notifications

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Is knowledge of Marathi mandatory for this recruitment?

No. Knowledge of Konkani is the "Essential" requirement, meaning you must know it to be eligible. Knowledge of Marathi is "Desirable," meaning it gives you an advantage but you can still apply without it.

Q2: Can I apply for both LDC and Driver posts?

Yes, but you must submit separate applications and pay the prescribed fees for each post. Ensure that you meet the technical requirements (typing for LDC and license for Driver) for both.

Q3: What is the last date to apply for Goa University LDC?

The final date for submitting the online application via the Samarth portal is November 10, 2025. Fees must also be paid by this date.

Q4: Is there an interview for the Lower Division Clerk post?

No. As per the latest government guidelines for Group C posts, selection will be based entirely on the Written Test and the Qualifying Skill Test (Typing). There is no personal interview.

Q5: What happens if I am unable to produce a 15-year residence certificate?

Preference and reservations in Goa University are heavily dependent on the 15-year residence certificate. If you are applying under any reserved category or seeking local preference, this document is mandatory.

Q6: Are candidates from other states eligible to apply?

While the recruitment is open, the "Essential" requirement of Konkani knowledge and the 15-year residence criteria for local benefits make it primarily accessible to those residing in or belonging to Goa.

entry.title: Customs Marine Wing Recruitment 2025: Apply for 19 Seaman, Greaser, and Tradesman Posts
entry.content:
Customs Marine Wing Recruitment 2025: Apply for 19 Seaman, Greaser, and Tradesman Posts

Direct Entry for 10th Pass and ITI Candidates in the Office of the Commissioner of Customs (Preventive), Cochin

Post Name: Tradesman, Seaman, Greaser, Senior Store Keeper
Last Date: December 15, 2025
Total Posts: 19 Vacancies

The Office of the Commissioner of Customs (Preventive), Cochin, functioning under the Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC), Ministry of Finance, has officially released its recruitment notification for the year 2025. This specialized recruitment drive is aimed at strengthening the Marine Wing of the Customs Department in the coastal region of Kochi. The Customs Marine Wing plays a vital role in safeguarding the nation's maritime borders, preventing smuggling activities at sea, and ensuring the technical maintenance of surveillance vessels. This notification invites applications for various Group C non-gazetted and non-ministerial posts including Tradesman, Seaman, Greaser, and Senior Store Keeper.

For candidates who have completed their 10th standard (SSC) or possess ITI certifications and have a background in maritime operations, this is an exceptional opportunity to join a Central Government Department. Working with the Marine Wing offers a unique blend of technical work and sea-faring challenges. The roles involve the operation and maintenance of high-speed interceptor boats and other mechanized vessels used by the Preventive unit. With the application deadline set for December 15, 2025, eligible Indian citizens are encouraged to submit their applications through the prescribed offline mode. This 2000-word comprehensive guide provides all the necessary details, including salary structures, eligibility requirements, selection procedures, and expert preparation strategies for the upcoming exams.

Joining the Customs Department as part of the Marine Wing is highly prestigious. These positions are permanent and come with the stability of the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) pay levels. Beyond the salary, employees are part of a disciplined force dedicated to national security and revenue protection. Whether you are an experienced seaman or a skilled tradesman looking for a disciplined career path, this notification provides a gateway to a rewarding professional life in Cochin, Kerala. In the following sections, we will break down the academic, physical, and technical requirements needed to clear this competitive recruitment process.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Dates

As this is an offline recruitment process, candidates must be extremely careful with the timeline. Applications must reach the specified address in Cochin via Ordinary Post only. It is recommended to dispatch your application early to avoid potential postal delays, especially if sending from outside Kerala. Please note that courier services or applications delivered by hand will not be entertained.

Event / Feature Dates and Information
Official Notification Release November 2025
Application Mode Offline (By Ordinary Post)
Last Date for Receipt of Application December 15, 2025
Organization Customs (Preventive) Commissionerate, Kochi
Job Category Group C (Non-Gazetted/Non-Ministerial)
Job Location Cochin (Kerala)

Vacancy Details and Pay Matrix

The 19 vacancies are categorized according to the functional needs of the vessels and the storage units. The salary for these posts is structured as per the latest 7th CPC matrix, providing attractive entry pay levels along with central government allowances.

Post Name Vacancies & Category Breakdown Pay Level (7th CPC)
Seaman 11 (UR-4, EWS-1, OBC-1, SC-3, ST-2) Level 1 (₹18,000 - ₹56,900)
Greaser 04 (UR-1, EWS-1, SC-1, ST-1) Level 1 (₹18,000 - ₹56,900)
Tradesman 03 (UR-1, OBC-1, SC-1) Level 2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200)
Senior Store Keeper 01 (UR-1) Level 5 (₹29,200 - ₹92,300)
Total Combined Posts 19 Vacancies -
Special Note: Out of the 11 Seaman posts, 01 position is horizontally reserved for Ex-Servicemen (ESM). All selected candidates will be eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), and medical benefits as per Central Government rules.

Eligibility Criteria and Qualifications

The Marine Wing requires a specific set of academic qualifications and, more importantly, sea-going experience. The experience must be verified through certificates issued by the owners of registered mechanized vessels or recognized maritime organizations.

  • Tradesman: 10th Standard pass plus ITI Certificate in relevant trades like Mechanic Diesel, Fitter, Turner, Welder, or Electrician. Must have 2 years of experience in an engineering or ship repair organization. Age Limit: Max 25 Years.
  • Seaman: 10th Standard pass with 03 years of experience in sea-going mechanized vessels. This must include at least 2 years in Helmsmanship and Seamanship work. Desirable: Certificate of "Mate of Fishing Vessel." Age Limit: 18 to 25 Years.
  • Greaser: 10th Standard pass with 03 years of experience in sea-going mechanized vessels, specifically in the maintenance of main and auxiliary machinery. Desirable: Certificate of "Engine Driver of Fishing Vessel." Age Limit: 18 to 25 Years.
  • Senior Store Keeper: 10th Standard pass with a minimum of 08 years of experience in storekeeping, accounting for engineering/automobile parts, and procurement procedures. Age Limit: Max 30 Years.

Age Relaxation: Standard relaxations apply: 5 years for SC/ST and 3 years for OBC. For Central Government employees, the upper age limit is relaxed up to 35 years as per the latest government directives.

Selection Process

The selection process for the Cochin Customs Marine Wing is designed to assess the candidate's aptitude and physical readiness for sea duties. The process is transparent and strictly merit-based.

  1. Written Examination: An objective-type test focusing on general knowledge, mathematics, reasoning, and technical trade questions (where applicable).
  2. Physical Endurance Test (PET): Since these are maritime roles, candidates for Seaman, Greaser, and Tradesman may be required to pass a swimming test or other endurance drills to prove their fitness for duty at sea.
  3. Document Verification (DV): Shortlisted candidates must present their original certificates, especially their experience certificates from registered vessels.
  4. Medical Fitness Test: Final selection is subject to medical clearance by a government authorized medical board. Standard vision and hearing requirements for maritime roles apply.

Detailed Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The written exam is the most crucial part of the screening process. Candidates should focus their preparation on the following areas to ensure a high rank in the merit list.

General Awareness & Current Affairs: Indian History, Geography of coastal India, basic Science, and important events of 2024-25. Special focus on the Role of Customs, basics of Maritime Laws, and the Department of Revenue.

Mathematics & Reasoning: Profit and Loss, Ratio and Proportion, Number Systems, Time and Work. Logical puzzles, analogies, and coding-decoding questions. The difficulty level will be of the 10th standard.

Technical/Trade Section: For Tradesman roles, questions will be asked from the ITI syllabus (Diesel mechanics, electrical circuits, welding types, etc.). For Seaman/Greaser, questions on basic ship-handling, safety at sea, and engine maintenance components may be included.

Physical Endurance Test Details

The PET is generally qualifying in nature. For maritime posts, the Swimming Test is the most important component. Candidates may be asked to swim a specific distance (e.g., 50 meters) without assistance. Other drills may include rope climbing or lifting weights to test the physical strength required for deck operations and engine room maintenance.

Expert Preparation Strategy for Customs Marine Exam

Success in this recruitment requires a blend of general study and practical knowledge. Since there is no interview for Group C posts, your performance in the written test is the only factor for your selection.

Master the Domain Knowledge: For Seaman and Greaser roles, read about the basics of "Seamanship." Understand the names of different boat parts, knots, and safety signals used at sea. For Tradesmen, revise your ITI textbooks thoroughly, as technical questions usually carry high weightage.

Improve Calculation Speed: The math section is often the tie-breaker. Practice 10th-standard mathematics daily. Focus on accuracy to avoid losing marks in the competitive environment. For General Awareness, follow a monthly current affairs magazine or reliable news apps.

Prepare for the PET: If you are not a confident swimmer, start practicing at a local swimming pool. Sea duties require high stamina, so include cardio exercises like running and cycling in your daily routine to stay fit for the physical drills held in Cochin.

Best Books for Preparation

Using the right resources is essential for a targeted preparation. Below is a list of recommended books for the Group C Customs exam.

General Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
Reasoning Ability: "Modern Approach to Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
General Knowledge: Lucent's General Knowledge and Manorama Yearbook.
Maritime Basics: Basic Seamanship Handbooks or NCERT Vocational texts on maritime operations.
ITI Technical: Trade-specific guides by Arihant Publications for Diesel Mechanics, Fitter, and Electrician.

How to Apply Offline: Step-by-Step Guide

Since the application process is strictly offline via Ordinary Post, follow these steps to ensure your form is not rejected due to clerical errors.

  1. Download the Prescribed Application Format from the official departmental website: cenexcisekochi.gov.in.
  2. Fill out the form in BLOCK LETTERS using a blue or black ballpoint pen. Ensure your name and father's name match your 10th-standard certificate.
  3. Paste a recent self-attested passport-size color photograph in the designated box on the application form.
  4. Experience Certificates: Attach self-attested copies of your experience letters. Ensure the period of service and the nature of the vessel are clearly mentioned.
  5. Postal Address: On the main envelope (size 28 cm x 13 cm), clearly write in bold: "APPLICATION FOR MARINE WING POST – CUSTOMS (PREVENTIVE) COMMISSIONERATE, KOCHI" along with the Post Name and Category (e.g., Seaman - OBC).
  6. Include two self-addressed envelopes (size 25 cm x 12 cm) along with the application form. These are used to send the admit cards and interview letters.
  7. Send the completed set only through Ordinary Post to the address mentioned below.

Documents Needed to Apply

Ensure you have self-attested copies of the following documents ready before packing the envelope. Do not send original documents.

  1. Educational Proof: 10th Standard Marksheet and Passing Certificate.
  2. Technical Proof: ITI Certificate in the relevant trade (for Tradesman post).
  3. Experience Certificates: Proof of service on mechanized vessels or recognized engineering firms.
  4. Caste Certificate: Valid SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificate in the format prescribed by the Central Government.
  5. Identity & Address Proof: Photocopy of Aadhaar Card or Voter ID.
  6. Age Proof: Birth Certificate or 10th Standard Certificate.
  7. Photographs: Four additional passport-size photographs without signature or stamps.

Important Links and Address

Resource / Action Details / Link
Postal Address to Send Application The Additional Commissioner of Customs (Establishment), Office of the Commissioner of Customs (Preventive), 5th Floor, Catholic Centre, Broadway, Cochin – 682031
Official Kochi Customs Website Visit Site
Notification & Application Form Download PDF

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Is there an application fee for the Customs Marine Wing recruitment?

No. According to the official notification released by the Commissioner of Customs, Cochin, there is no application fee for any category. The process is completely free of charge.

Q2: Can I apply through Speed Post or Courier?

No. The notification explicitly mentions that applications should be sent through Ordinary Post only. Applications sent via Speed Post, Registered Post, or Courier services may be rejected by the department.

Q3: What is the last date to reach the application in Cochin?

The application must reach the Office of the Commissioner of Customs (Preventive) in Cochin by December 15, 2025. Applications received after this date will not be processed.

Q4: What kind of sea-going experience is required for the Seaman post?

Candidates must have at least 03 years of experience on mechanized vessels registered with the maritime authorities. This should include tasks like deck maintenance, mooring operations, and basic helmsman duties.

Q5: Is knowledge of Malayalam mandatory for this post?

While the job is located in Cochin, knowledge of Malayalam is not mentioned as a mandatory requirement in the recruitment rules. However, knowledge of English and Hindi is always beneficial for Central Government roles.

Q6: Are candidates from states other than Kerala eligible to apply?

Yes. This is a Central Government recruitment, and all Indian citizens meeting the eligibility criteria and experience requirements from any state are eligible to apply.

entry.title: NCERT Recruitment 2025: 173 Non-Academic Posts for Group A, B & C Open
entry.content:
NCERT Recruitment 2025: 173 Non-Academic Posts for Group A, B & C Open

A massive recruitment drive by the National Council of Educational Research and Training for various roles across India. Apply online from December 27, 2025.

Post Name: Superintending Engineer, Production Officer, Assistant Engineer, LDC, Technician, Lab Assistant, and many more.
Last Date: 16 January 2026
Total Posts: 173

The National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT), a premier autonomous organization under the Ministry of Education, Government of India, has released a major recruitment notification (Advertisement No - 01/2025/Non-Academic) for a wide range of non-academic positions. This is a golden opportunity for eligible Indian nationals to build a career in one of the most prestigious educational bodies in the country. The recruitment aims to fill up vacancies under Direct Recruitment through an Open Competitive Examination, Skill Tests, and Interviews, as applicable.

This massive drive will fill a total of 173 vacancies across Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' at its headquarters in New Delhi, the Central Institute of Educational Technology (CIET), Pandit Sunderlal Sharma Central Institute of Vocational Education (PSSCIVE) in Bhopal, various Regional Institutes of Education (RIEs), and Regional Production-cum-Distribution Centers (RPDCs) across India. The application process is entirely online, commencing on December 27, 2025, and closing on January 16, 2026. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all the essential details of this recruitment process, including post-wise eligibility, selection process, and how to apply.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government positions in a Central Government autonomous body with postings across India. Key dates and details are outlined below.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization National Council of Educational Research and Training (NCERT)
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Autonomous Body)
Job Location New Delhi, Ajmer, Bhopal, Bhubaneswar, Mysuru, Shillong, Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, etc.
Online Application Start Date 27 December 2025, 09:00 AM
Online Application Last Date 16 January 2026 (up to 11:55 PM)

Vacancy Details

The recruitment aims to fill 173 non-academic posts across Group A, B, and C. The group-wise summary is as follows:

Group Total Vacancies
Group A 09
Group B 26
Group C 138
Grand Total 173
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary. A detailed post-wise and category-wise breakdown is available in the official notification. Horizontal reservation for PwBD and ESM is applicable as per Government of India rules.

Application Fee

  • For Group A (Level 10-12): ₹1500/- for UR/OBC/EWS.
  • For Group B (Level 6-7): ₹1200/- for UR/OBC/EWS.
  • For Group C (Level 2-5): ₹1000/- for UR/OBC/EWS.
  • For SC/ST/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen: NIL (No Fee).

The fee must be paid online. Fee once paid shall neither be refunded nor be adjusted under any circumstances.

Eligibility Criteria

The eligibility criteria, including educational qualifications, experience, and age limit, are specifically defined for each of the 47 unique posts. A summary for some key positions is provided below:

Post Name Eligibility Requirements (Qualification, Experience, Age)
Superintending Engineer (Group A) M.Tech in Electronics/Communication/IT with 10 years of experience, OR B.Tech with 12 years of experience in reputed media organizations. Max Age: 50 years.
Production Officer (Group A) Bachelor's Degree in Printing Technology and 8 years of experience in print production in a reputed publishing/printing organization. Max Age: 40 years.
Assistant Engineer Grade-A (Group A) Bachelor's Degree in Electronics/Communication/IT or equivalent, OR Master's Degree in the same fields, with 5 years of relevant experience in broadcast equipment maintenance. Max Age: 35 years.
Senior Accountant (Group B) Graduate in Commerce/Economics. 3 years of regular service in Pay Band-I with GP 2400/-. Max Age: 30 years.
Technician Grade-I (Group C) 10th/12th pass with a three-year Diploma in Electronics/Communication or relevant area. Max Age: 27 years.
Store Keeper Grade-I (Group C) Bachelor's Degree in Arts/Science/Commerce and two (2) years' experience of stores in a well-established organization. Max Age: 27 years.
Lab Assistant (Group C) A Bachelor's Degree in the required field (Physics, Chemistry, Biology, etc.). Experience in handling instruments is required. Max Age: 27 years.
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) (Group C) Intermediate (10+2) or equivalent. A typing speed of 35 w.p.m. in English OR 30 w.p.m. in Hindi on a computer. Max Age: 27 years.

Selection Process

The mode of selection will be as specified for each post in Part-II of the advertisement. The Council reserves the right to conduct a written test for any or all posts. The general process is as follows:

  1. Written Test (CBT): For most posts, a Computer Based Test (CBT) will be conducted to adjudge the basic and domain-specific knowledge of the candidates.
  2. Skill / Proficiency / Trade Test: For posts requiring specific skills (e.g., LDC, Stenographer, Technician, Cameraman), a qualifying skill test will be conducted. This may include typing tests, trade tests, or practical demonstrations.
  3. Interview: For Group 'A' posts and some Group 'B' posts, candidates who qualify in the written/skill test will be called for a personal interview.
  4. Document Verification: All shortlisted candidates will have to undergo verification of their original testimonials and certificates.

The provisional merit list will be drawn based on the marks scored in the CBT/Written Test. The final selection will be based on the combined performance as per the scheme for each post.

Exam Pattern

While the specific exam pattern for each of the 47 posts will vary, a general structure for such recruitment typically involves objective-type questions (MCQs). The syllabus is generally divided into two parts:

  • Part 1: General Paper - This section usually covers subjects like General English, General Hindi, General Knowledge, Current Affairs, Reasoning Ability, Quantitative Aptitude, and Computer Literacy.
  • Part 2: Domain-Specific Paper - This is the most crucial section and will contain questions from the educational qualification required for the post. For example, for a Production Officer, questions will be from Printing Technology. For a Senior Accountant, questions will be from Commerce and Accountancy.

Candidates must refer to the NCERT website for the detailed syllabus for each specific post, which will be uploaded in due course.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these non-academic posts.

How to Prepare

A focused and post-specific preparation strategy is required to succeed in the NCERT recruitment exam.

  1. Analyze the Syllabus: Once NCERT releases the detailed syllabus for your post, analyze it thoroughly to understand the weightage of different topics.
  2. Strengthen Your Domain Knowledge: The majority of questions will be from your specific field (e.g., Engineering, Media Production, Accountancy, Library Science). Revise your graduation/post-graduation level subjects in depth.
  3. Prepare for General Papers: Do not neglect the general sections. Regularly practice reasoning and aptitude, stay updated with current affairs, and brush up on your English/Hindi grammar.
  4. Practice for Skill Tests: If your post requires a skill test (typing, stenography, trade test), start practicing immediately. Aim for speed and accuracy.
  5. Solve Mock Tests: Attempt mock tests and previous years' papers of similar organizations (like KVS, NVS, or other autonomous bodies) to get an idea of the exam pattern and to manage your time effectively.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended resources for your preparation:

  • For General Subjects (English, Reasoning, Aptitude): Standard competitive exam books by publishers like R.S. Aggarwal (for Aptitude & Reasoning) and S.P. Bakshi (for English).
  • For General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and regular reading of newspapers and magazines.
  • For Domain-Specific Subjects: Refer to the standard textbooks from your degree/diploma/master's curriculum. For objective questions, books for competitive exams like GATE, UGC-NET, or IES can be very helpful.

How to Apply

The application process is completely online. Interested and eligible persons are required to apply online only on the link available at www.ncert.nic.in.

  1. Go to the NCERT website: www.ncert.nic.in > Announcement > Vacancies > Non-Academic.
  2. Carefully read the detailed advertisement and instructions before filling up the form.
  3. Click on the "Apply Online" link for the desired post.
  4. Fill in the application form with all your personal and educational details. Fields marked with a star (*) are mandatory.
  5. Pay the application fee through the online payment gateway (Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking).
  6. Ensure that the photograph and signature uploaded are clear.
  7. After verifying all the entered data, click the "Final Submit" button to generate a Unique Registration Number (URN).
  8. Take a printout of the final confirmation page (Application Form) for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must upload self-attested copies of all documents at the time of submitting the application. No fresh documents will be considered at the time of Document Verification.

  1. Scanned copy of your recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Certificates and marksheets for all educational qualifications.
  3. Experience certificates from all previous employers, clearly mentioning the duration, duties, and pay scale.
  4. Caste/Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD) in the prescribed format.
  5. A valid Photo ID.
  6. No Objection Certificate (NOC) if you are a government employee.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website www.ncert.nic.in

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the NCERT recruitment?

The last date for filling the online application is January 16, 2026, until 11:55 PM.

Question 2: Can I apply for multiple posts?

The general instructions usually require a separate application and fee for each post. Please refer to the detailed instructions on the website for final confirmation.

Question 3: Is there an interview for all posts?

No, interviews are generally conducted for Group 'A' posts. The selection for Group 'B' and 'C' posts is typically based on a written test and/or a skill test.

Question 4: Where will the examination centers be located?

The council will allot exam centers in all major States/UTs of India, subject to receiving a sufficient number of applications. The final allotted center will be mentioned on the admit card.

entry.title: BSNL Recruitment 2025: Senior Executive Trainee की 120 वैकेंसी के लिए आवेदन करें
entry.content:
BSNL Recruitment 2025: Senior Executive Trainee की 120 वैकेंसी के लिए आवेदन करें

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) ने Senior Executive Trainee (SET) के पदों के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है। यह अवसर इंजीनियरिंग ग्रेजुएट्स के लिए सुनहरा मौका है।

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL) भारत की प्रमुख दूरसंचार सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र की कंपनी है। कंपनी ने Senior Executive Trainee (SET) के 120 पदों के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है। यह भर्ती Telecom और Finance दोनों स्ट्रीम्स में की जाएगी।

रिक्तियाँ और वेतनमान विवरण

पद का नामरिक्तियाँवेतनमान (IDA E-3 Grade)
Senior Executive Trainee - Telecom Stream95₹24,900 – ₹50,500/-
Senior Executive Trainee - Finance Stream25
वेतन के साथ लाभ: बेसिक वेतन के अलावा DA, HRA, मेडिकल और अन्य भत्ते BSNL नियमों के अनुसार दिए जाएंगे।

योग्यता मानदंड

  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता:
    • Telecom Stream: Electronics & Communication, Computer Science, Information Technology, Electrical या Instrumentation में B.E/B.Tech।
    • Finance Stream: Chartered Accountant (CA) या Cost & Management Accountancy (CMA)।
  • आयु सीमा:
    • न्यूनतम आयु – 21 वर्ष
    • अधिकतम आयु – 30 वर्ष (आरक्षित वर्ग को छूट लागू)

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. ऑनलाइन परीक्षा: इसमें Technical Knowledge, Aptitude, Reasoning और English शामिल होंगे।
  2. डॉक्यूमेंट वेरिफिकेशन और इंटरव्यू: परीक्षा पास करने वाले उम्मीदवारों को बुलाया जाएगा।
  3. मेडिकल टेस्ट: अंतिम चयन मेडिकल फिटनेस पर निर्भर करेगा।

फाइनल चयन Written Test और Interview के कुल अंकों पर आधारित होगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. BSNL की वेबसाइट www.bsnl.co.in पर जाएं।
  2. Career/Recruitment सेक्शन में “Senior Executive Trainee 2025” लिंक खोलें।
  3. सभी आवश्यक विवरण भरें और डॉक्यूमेंट अपलोड करें।
  4. फीस जमा करें और आवेदन सबमिट करें।
  5. भविष्य के लिए एक प्रिंटआउट सुरक्षित रखें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंकविवरण
Official Websiteयहाँ क्लिक करें
Notification Release Date27 अक्टूबर 2025
अंतिम तिथिजल्द घोषित होगी

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: कुल कितनी वैकेंसी हैं?

कुल 120 (Telecom – 95, Finance – 25) वैकेंसी हैं।

Q2: वेतन कितना मिलेगा?

IDA Pay Scale ₹24,900 – ₹50,500/- के अनुसार वेतन मिलेगा।

Q3: Training अवधि कितनी होगी?

2 वर्ष की Training दी जाएगी, जिसके दौरान पूरी Salary और भत्ते मिलेंगे।

Q4: क्या Final Year छात्र आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

इसकी जानकारी आधिकारिक अधिसूचना में दी जाएगी।

entry.title: APSC भर्ती 2025: असम सरकार में Adviser (Production) पद के लिए आवेदन करें!
entry.content:
APSC भर्ती 2025: असम सरकार में Adviser (Production) पद के लिए आवेदन करें!

असम लोक सेवा आयोग (APSC) ने Industries, Commerce & Public Enterprise विभाग में उच्च स्तरीय प्रबंधकीय पद के लिए ऑनलाइन आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं।

असम लोक सेवा आयोग (APSC) ने विज्ञापन संख्या 32/2025 के तहत अधिसूचना जारी की है। इस भर्ती के अंतर्गत आयोग, असम सरकार के Industries, Commerce & Public Enterprise विभाग में Adviser (Production) पद को भरने जा रहा है।

यह अवसर उन वरिष्ठ इंजीनियरिंग पेशेवरों के लिए है जिनके पास उत्पादन प्रबंधन (Production Management) में अनुभव है और जो राज्य के औद्योगिक विकास में योगदान देना चाहते हैं। आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह ऑनलाइन है और उम्मीदवार का असम का स्थायी निवासी होना अनिवार्य है। योग्य और इच्छुक उम्मीदवारों को अंतिम तिथि से पहले आवेदन करना होगा।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ24 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि23 नवंबर 2025
आवेदन शुल्क भुगतान की अंतिम तिथि25 नवंबर 2025

रिक्ति एवं वेतनमान विवरण

पद का नामविभागपदों की संख्यावेतनमान (PB-4)ग्रेड पे
Adviser (Production) Industries, Commerce & Public Enterprise 01 (Unreserved) ₹30,000 – ₹1,10,000 ₹16,900
नोट: यह पद सामान्य (Unreserved) श्रेणी के अंतर्गत है। SC/ST/OBC/PwBD उम्मीदवारों को इसमें कोई आरक्षण नहीं दिया गया है।

आवेदन शुल्क

श्रेणीApplication Fee (₹)Processing Fee (₹)कुल राशि (₹)
General25047.20297.20
OBC/MOBC15047.20197.20
SC/ST/BPL/PwBDNIL47.2047.20

पात्रता मानदंड

मानदंडआवश्यकता
शैक्षणिक योग्यताChemical / Civil / Mechanical / Electrical / Industrial Production में BE या B.Tech डिग्री।
अनुभवकम से कम 10 वर्ष का अनुभव किसी वरिष्ठ प्रबंधकीय या पर्यवेक्षी पद पर, किसी सरकारी / अर्ध-सरकारी / निगम / बोर्ड या निजी संगठन में।
आयु सीमा (01.01.2025 तक)उम्मीदवार की आयु 45 से 52 वर्ष के बीच होनी चाहिए। SC/ST को 5 वर्ष और OBC/MOBC को 3 वर्ष की छूट दी जाएगी।
निवास प्रमाणउम्मीदवार असम का स्थायी निवासी होना चाहिए और PRC या अन्य वैध दस्तावेज प्रस्तुत करना आवश्यक है।

चयन प्रक्रिया

इस पद के लिए अंतिम चयन प्रक्रिया APSC द्वारा बाद में अधिसूचित की जाएगी। इसमें निम्नलिखित चरण शामिल हो सकते हैं:

  • Screening Test या लिखित परीक्षा
  • Interview (Viva-Voce)
आवेदनों की संख्या के अनुसार आयोग चयन प्रक्रिया तय करेगा। उम्मीदवारों को सलाह दी जाती है कि वे नियमित रूप से APSC की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट पर अपडेट चेक करें।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट पर जाएँ – https://apscrecruitment.in
  2. ‘Register Here’ पर क्लिक कर One Time Registration (OTR) पूरा करें।
  3. रजिस्ट्रेशन के बाद लॉगिन कर आवेदन फॉर्म भरें।
  4. फॉर्म भरने से पहले OTR प्रोफाइल में सभी दस्तावेज़ अपडेट करें।
  5. Adviser (Production) पद के लिए आवेदन फॉर्म भरें और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें।
  6. आवेदन शुल्क ऑनलाइन भुगतान करें और आवेदन सबमिट करें।
  7. फाइनल सबमिशन के बाद आवेदन की प्रति डाउनलोड करें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदनयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या असम के बाहर के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, केवल असम के स्थायी निवासी ही इस पद के लिए पात्र हैं।
प्रश्न 2: क्या लिखित परीक्षा होगी?
आयोग आवेदन की संख्या के अनुसार Screening या Interview आयोजित कर सकता है।
प्रश्न 3: सामान्य वर्ग के लिए आयु सीमा क्या है?
सामान्य वर्ग के उम्मीदवार की आयु 45 से 52 वर्ष के बीच होनी चाहिए।
प्रश्न 4: क्या सरकारी कर्मचारी को NOC देना होगा?
हाँ, नियमित सरकारी कर्मचारियों को आवेदन के समय अपनी संस्था से NOC प्रस्तुत करना होगा।

entry.title: BWSSB Recruitment 2025: Bumper Recruitment for 224 AE, JE, Assistant & Other Posts in Bangalore
entry.content:
BWSSB Recruitment 2025: Bumper Recruitment for 224 AE, JE, Assistant & Other Posts in Bangalore

A golden opportunity by the Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA) for engineers and graduates to join the Bangalore Water Supply and Sewerage Board. Online applications are open from November 17 to November 25, 2025.

Post Name: Assistant Engineer, Junior Engineer, Assistant, Junior Assistant, Meter Reader, Storekeeper
Last Date: 25/11/2025
Total Posts: 224

The Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA), operating under the Government of Karnataka, has released a major recruitment notification for the Bangalore Water Supply and Sewerage Board (BWSSB). This recruitment drive is being conducted to fill various technical and non-technical posts under Group-B and Group-C. A total of 224 vacancies have been announced, including prestigious posts such as Assistant Engineer, Junior Engineer, Assistant, Junior Assistant, Meter Reader, and Second Division Storekeeper. This is a golden opportunity for the youth of Karnataka, especially engineering and other graduates, to secure a stable and respected government job.

This recruitment is being carried out for two different cadres: Kalyana-Karnataka (Local Cadre) and the Residual Parent Cadre (RPC). The recruitment process will be managed by KEA, which is renowned for its transparent and fair competitive examinations. BWSSB, a key organization responsible for water supply and wastewater management for the city of Bangalore, offers a career that not only provides job security but also an opportunity to contribute to the development of the city's essential infrastructure. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the official KEA website from November 17, 2025. The last date to apply is November 25, 2025. This article will provide you with detailed information on all important aspects of this recruitment.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details of this recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameBangalore Water Supply and Sewerage Board (BWSSB)
Exam Conducting AuthorityKarnataka Examinations Authority (KEA)
Job LocationBangalore, Karnataka
Application ModeOnline
Application Start DateNovember 17, 2025
Application Last DateNovember 25, 2025
Last Date to Pay FeeNovember 26, 2025

Vacancy Details and Cadre-wise Breakdown

BWSSB has announced a total of 224 posts under this recruitment drive. These vacancies are divided into two cadres: 165 posts for the Residual Parent Cadre (RPC) (120 Direct + 45 Backlog) and 59 posts for the Kalyana-Karnataka (KK) Cadre (50 Direct + 9 Backlog). A detailed post-wise breakdown is provided below:

BWSSB Recruitment 2025: Total Vacancy Details
Sr. No. Post Name Group RPC Cadre Kalyana-Karnataka Cadre Total Posts
1 Assistant Engineer (Civil) B 13 05 18
2 Assistant Engineer (Electrical) B 04 01 05
3 Assistant Engineer (Mechanical) B 02 01 03
4 Assistant Engineer (Computer Science) B 01 - 01
5 Junior Engineer (Civil) C 20 03 23
6 Junior Engineer (Electrical) C 21 (20+1 Backlog) 02 23
7 Junior Engineer (Mechanical) C 10 01 11
8 Assistant C 03 (Backlog) 05 (3+2 Backlog) 08
9 Junior Assistant C 50 15 (13+2 Backlog) 65
10 Meter Reader C 37 (Backlog) 26 (21+5 Backlog) 63
11 Second Division Storekeeper C 04 (Backlog) - 04
Grand Total 165 59 224
Note: A detailed category-wise classification of vacancies is available in the official notification. Candidates are advised to carefully check the notification for the exact number of posts in their respective categories.

Salary and Pay Scale

Selected candidates at BWSSB will be offered an attractive salary package as per the rules of the Government of Karnataka. The pay scale for each post is detailed below:

Post Name Group Pay Scale (per month)
Assistant Engineer (All Disciplines) B ₹ 53,250 - ₹ 1,15,460/-
Junior Engineer (All Disciplines) C ₹ 39,170 - ₹ 99,410/-
Assistant C ₹ 34,510 - ₹ 94,410/-
Junior Assistant C ₹ 27,750 - ₹ 86,910/-
Meter Reader C ₹ 27,750 - ₹ 86,910/-
Second Division Storekeeper C ₹ 27,750 - ₹ 86,910/-

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Specific educational qualifications have been prescribed for each post. Along with these qualifications, it is also mandatory for the candidate to have a certificate of passing a Computer Basics course of at least six months duration.

Educational Qualification

Post Name Minimum Educational Qualification
Assistant Engineer (Civil) A degree in Civil Engineering or Technology from a recognized university.
Assistant Engineer (Electrical) A degree in Electrical Engineering or Technology from a recognized university.
Assistant Engineer (Mechanical) A degree in Mechanical Engineering or Technology from a recognized university.
Assistant Engineer (Computer Science) A degree in Computer Science Engineering or Technology from a recognized university.
Junior Engineer (Civil) A 3-year Diploma in Civil Engineering or Technology.
Junior Engineer (Electrical) A 3-year Diploma in Electrical Engineering or Technology.
Junior Engineer (Mechanical) A 3-year Diploma in Mechanical Engineering or Technology.
Assistant A degree in Arts/Commerce/Science from a recognized university + a 1-year computer course.
Junior Assistant PUC or equivalent qualification + a 1-year computer course.
Meter Reader PUC or equivalent qualification + a minimum 6-month computer course.
Second Division Storekeeper PUC + a certificate course in Material Management from a recognized institute + a 1-year computer course.
Note: Candidates must have obtained a minimum of 50% aggregate marks in the qualifying examination.

Age Limit (as on 25.11.2025)

Candidates must have completed a minimum of 18 years of age as of the last date for receipt of applications. The maximum age limit varies for different categories, and a one-time relaxation of 3 years has also been provided as per government order.

Category Maximum Age Limit
General Merit 38 years
Category 2A, 2B, 3A, 3B 41 years
SC, ST, Category-1 43 years
Persons with Disabilities (PwD) / Widow Additional 10 years relaxation
Ex-Servicemen Relaxation of the number of years of service + 3 years

Selection Process

The selection process for the BWSSB recruitment will involve two main stages: a compulsory Kannada language test (for some candidates) and a competitive written examination.

  1. Compulsory Kannada Language Test: This test is mandatory for those candidates who have not studied Kannada as a first or second language in their SSLC or equivalent examination. The test will be for 150 marks, and it is necessary to score a minimum of 50 marks to qualify. The marks from this test will not be considered for the merit list; it is purely qualifying in nature.
  2. Competitive Written Examination (Offline-OMR Type): An offline OMR-based competitive examination will be conducted for all posts. The examination will consist of two papers. Negative Marking: For every wrong answer, 1/4th (one-fourth) of the marks assigned to that question will be deducted. Minimum Qualifying Marks: Candidates must score at least 35% aggregate marks in the competitive examination.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Examination Pattern for Assistant Engineer (All Disciplines)

PaperSubjectMarksDuration
Paper-1 General Paper (General Knowledge) 300 2 Hours
Paper-2 Specific Paper (Relevant Engineering Branch) 300 2 Hours

Examination Pattern for Junior Engineer (All Disciplines)

PaperSubjectMarksDuration
Paper-1 General Knowledge 100 2 Hours
Paper-2 Specific Paper (Relevant Diploma Branch) 100 2 Hours

Examination Pattern for Junior Assistant, Meter Reader, and Storekeeper

PaperSubjectMarksDuration
Paper-1 General Knowledge 100 2 Hours
Paper-2 General Kannada, General English, Computer Knowledge 100 2 Hours

Syllabus Outline

  • General Paper (Paper-1 for all posts): This paper will cover Current Affairs (national and international), General Science, Indian History (with special reference to Karnataka), Indian Geography, Social Sciences, and General Mental Ability (Reasoning and Numerical Ability).
  • Specific Paper (Paper-2 for AE/JE): The syllabus will be based on the curriculum of the respective engineering degree/diploma. For example, for Civil Engineering, topics will include Strength of Materials, Structural Analysis, Fluid Mechanics, Geotechnical Engineering, Transportation, and Environmental Engineering.
  • Paper-2 (for non-technical posts): This paper will test proficiency in General Kannada and General English (Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension) and fundamental knowledge of computer applications (MS Office, Internet, etc.).

How to Prepare for the BWSSB Exam by KEA

Success in the KEA-conducted BWSSB exam requires comprehensive and planned preparation.

  1. Understand the Syllabus: The first step is to thoroughly understand the prescribed syllabus for your post. Divide the subjects for both the General Paper and the Specific Paper.
  2. General Knowledge (Paper-1):
    • Current Affairs: Focus on the latest events of national and Karnataka state importance. Regularly read newspapers and follow news channels.
    • History and Geography of Karnataka: Pay special attention to the history, freedom struggle, geography, and culture of Karnataka.
    • General Science and Mental Ability: Practice basic science principles and topics like logical reasoning and numerical ability.
  3. Specific Subject (Paper-2):
    • This paper plays the most crucial role in determining your rank.
    • Engineering/Diploma Candidates: Study the core subjects of your respective branch (Civil, Electrical, Mechanical) in depth. Refer to standard textbooks and previous question papers of KEA.
    • Non-Technical Candidates: Focus on General Kannada, General English, and basic computer principles for Paper-2.
  4. Time Management and Mock Tests: Regularly solve mock tests and previous years' question papers. This will help you understand the exam pattern and improve your time management. Pay attention to accuracy to avoid negative marking.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books to aid your preparation:

  • General Knowledge (Paper-1): "Karnataka Year Book" by Spardha Vijetha, "Lucent's General Knowledge," and standard NCERT books.
  • Mental Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • For Engineering Subjects: Standard textbooks from your degree/diploma curriculum and objective books for competitive exams by publishers like Made Easy or ACE Academy.
  • For Non-Technical Paper-2: "General English" by S.P. Bakshi, a standard book on Kannada grammar, and a basic computer knowledge book like "Computer Awareness" by Arihant.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must apply through the official KEA website by following the steps below:

  1. First, visit the official KEA website: https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/.
  2. On the homepage, click on the link related to BWSSB Recruitment 2025.
  3. Read the detailed notification carefully and ensure you meet all the eligibility criteria.
  4. Click on the "Apply Online" link. If you wish to apply for more than one post, you can select them in a single application.
  5. Fill in all the required details in the application form, such as personal information, educational qualifications, and reservation details, accurately.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph (between 50KB and 200KB JPEG) and signature (between 50KB and 70KB JPEG).
  7. Review all the filled-in information before submitting the application form, as no changes can be made after submission.
  8. Pay the application fee as per your category through the online payment gateway.
  9. Keep a printout of the final submitted application form and the fee receipt for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

The following documents will be required during the online application and at the time of document verification:

  • Aadhaar Card.
  • SSLC/10th Class Marksheet (for proof of date of birth).
  • All certificates and marksheets of the educational qualifications prescribed for the respective post (Degree/Diploma/PUC).
  • Computer Literacy Certificate.
  • Caste Certificate (SC/ST/Cat-1/2A/2B/3A/3B) for claiming reservation.
  • Rural Candidate Certificate (if applicable).
  • Kannada Medium Certificate (if applicable).
  • Ex-Servicemen Certificate (if applicable).
  • Persons with Disabilities (PwD) Certificate (if applicable).
  • Certificate for belonging to the Kalyana-Karnataka region (Article 371(J)).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification (RPC Cadre) Click Here
Download Official Notification (KK Cadre) Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Active from 17.11.2025)
KEA Official Website https://cetonline.karnataka.gov.in/kea/

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the BWSSB recruitment?

The last date to apply online is November 25, 2025, and the last date for fee payment is November 26, 2025.

Question 2: Can final-year students apply?

No, as per the notification, the candidate must possess the prescribed educational qualification as of the last date of application.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 1/4th of a mark for every incorrect answer.

Question 4: For whom is the Kannada language test mandatory?

This test is mandatory for those candidates who have not studied Kannada as a first or second language in the 10th standard.

Question 5: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for more than one eligible post in the same online application form.

entry.title: CCL अपरेंटिस भर्ती 2025: 1180 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!
entry.content:
CCL अपरेंटिस भर्ती 2025: 1180 ITI, Diploma, Graduate पदों पर भर्ती!

Central Coalfields Limited में Apprentice बनने का सुनहरा मौका — NAPS/NATS पोर्टल पर करें आवेदन।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! यदि आपने ITI, Diploma या Graduation पूरी कर ली है तो यह आपके लिए शानदार अवसर है। Central Coalfields Limited (CCL), रांची (झारखंड) — जो Coal India Limited की सहायक कंपनी है — ने Apprenticeship Act के अंतर्गत एक बड़ी भर्ती अधिसूचना (Advertisement No. CCL/Apprenticeship/2025-26/229) जारी की है।

इस भर्ती के अंतर्गत Trade Apprentice, Technician Apprentice और Graduate Apprentice के कुल 1180 पदों पर प्रशिक्षण हेतु आवेदन आमंत्रित किए गए हैं। आवेदन प्रक्रिया NAPS (National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme) और NATS (National Apprenticeship Training Scheme) पोर्टलों के माध्यम से की जाएगी। आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 24 अक्टूबर 2025 है।

CCL Apprentice रिक्तियाँ 2025

नीचे दी गई तालिका में पद-वार रिक्तियों का पूरा विवरण दिया गया है:

Apprentice TypeTrade / Disciplineकुल पद
Trade Apprentice (ITI)Electrician300
Fitter150
Mechanic Diesel35
Welder15
Computer Operator & Programming Assistant10
Plumber5
Assistant Mines Surveyor5
Health Sanitary Inspector5
Fresher ApprenticeMedical Lab Technician, Wireman आदि52
Technician Apprentice (Diploma)Mining Engineering180
अन्य इंजीनियरिंग शाखाएँ200
Graduate Apprentice (Degree)Mining Engineering30
अन्य इंजीनियरिंग शाखाएँ100
B.Com / BBA / BCA50
B.Sc. Nursing28
कुल योग1180

CCL Apprentice स्टाइपेंड 2025

चयनित अभ्यर्थियों को Apprenticeship Rules के अनुसार मासिक स्टाइपेंड दिया जाएगा:

Apprentice Typeमासिक स्टाइपेंड
Trade Apprentice (ITI)₹7,000
Fresher Apprentice₹6,000 – ₹7,700
Technician Apprentice (Diploma)₹8,000
Graduate Apprentice (Degree)₹9,000

CCL Apprentice पात्रता मानदंड

Apprentice Typeआयु सीमा (01.08.2025 तक)शैक्षणिक योग्यता
Trade Apprentice18 – 32 वर्ष10वीं पास + ITI (NTC Certificate)
Fresher Apprentice18 – 27 वर्ष10वीं / 12वीं पास
Technician Apprentice2021 या उसके बाद पास आउटDiploma in relevant Engineering branch
Graduate ApprenticeB.E./B.Tech/B.Com/BBA/BCA/B.Sc. Nursing
आयु में छूट: OBC को 3 वर्ष और SC/ST को 5 वर्ष की छूट दी जाएगी।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

कार्यक्रमतिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ04 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि24 अक्टूबर 2025

CCL Apprentice चयन प्रक्रिया

इस भर्ती में कोई परीक्षा या इंटरव्यू नहीं होगा। चयन पूरी तरह शैक्षणिक अंकों के आधार पर बने मेरिट लिस्ट से होगा।

चरणविवरण
Phase 1NAPS/NATS पोर्टल पर ऑनलाइन आवेदन
Phase 2मेरिट सूची का निर्माण (शैक्षणिक अंकों के आधार पर)
Phase 3दस्तावेज़ सत्यापन

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

इस भर्ती के लिए आवेदन केवल NAPS और NATS पोर्टलों के माध्यम से ऑनलाइन किए जाएँगे।

  1. Step 1: ITI/Fresher उम्मीदवार NAPS Portal (www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) पर और Diploma/Graduate उम्मीदवार NATS Portal (https://nats.education.gov.in) पर पंजीकरण करें।
  2. Step 2: लॉगिन कर “Apprenticeship Opportunities” सेक्शन में जाएँ।
  3. Step 3: Establishment Name "CENTRAL COALFIELDS LIMITED (E02182000001)" सर्च करें।
  4. Step 4: संबंधित trade/disciplines के अनुसार “Apply” बटन पर क्लिक करें।

आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

  • 10वीं / High School Marksheet व प्रमाणपत्र
  • ITI / Diploma / Degree प्रमाणपत्र
  • जाति प्रमाणपत्र (यदि लागू हो)
  • Aadhaar कार्ड
  • बैंक पासबुक (स्टाइपेंड हेतु)
  • पासपोर्ट आकार का फोटो

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
NAPS Portal (ITI/Fresher)यहाँ क्लिक करें
NATS Portal (Diploma/Graduate)यहाँ क्लिक करें
Official Notification PDFयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: चयन कैसे होगा?

कोई परीक्षा नहीं होगी। चयन पूरी तरह से आपके ITI/Diploma/Degree अंकों के आधार पर बने मेरिट लिस्ट से किया जाएगा।

प्रश्न 2: क्या NAPS/NATS पंजीकरण अनिवार्य है?

हाँ, बिना NAPS या NATS पर पंजीकरण किए आप आवेदन नहीं कर सकते।

प्रश्न 3: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 24 अक्टूबर 2025 है।

entry.title: CWC Recruitment 2025: Junior Personal Assistant और Junior Executive की भर्ती
entry.content:
CWC Recruitment 2025: Junior Personal Assistant और Junior Executive की भर्ती

Central Warehousing Corporation (CWC) ने 22 पदों पर भर्ती की घोषणा की है। आवेदन प्रक्रिया 17 अक्टूबर 2025 से शुरू होकर 15 नवंबर 2025 तक चलेगी।

Central Warehousing Corporation (CWC) जो कि Ministry of Consumer Affairs, Food & Public Distribution के अंतर्गत आती है, ने योग्य उम्मीदवारों से ऑनलाइन आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं। यह भर्ती Junior Personal Assistant और Junior Executive (Rajbhasha) पदों के लिए है। कुल 22 पद भरे जाएंगे।

पदों का विवरण और वेतन

पद का नामकुल पदवेतनमान (IDA)
Junior Personal Assistant16₹29,000 – ₹93,000 (S-V)
Junior Executive (Rajbhasha)06₹29,000 – ₹93,000 (S-V)
नोट: चयनित उम्मीदवारों को Basic Pay के साथ CPF, Gratuity, LTC और अन्य लाभ भी मिलेंगे।

पात्रता मानदंड (Eligibility Criteria)

1. Junior Personal Assistant

  • किसी मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक (Graduate)।
  • Office Management या Secretarial Practice में कम से कम 1 वर्ष का कोर्स।
  • English Shorthand में 80 wpm और Typing में 40 wpm की गति आवश्यक।
  • अधिकतम आयु: 28 वर्ष (16.11.1997 से पहले और 15.11.2007 के बाद जन्म नहीं होना चाहिए)।

2. Junior Executive (Rajbhasha)

  • किसी मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक, जिसमें Hindi एक Elective Subject और English एक मुख्य विषय हो।
  • Hindi Software Applications का ज्ञान वांछनीय।
  • अधिकतम आयु सीमा: 28 वर्ष।

चयन प्रक्रिया (Selection Process)

Junior Personal Assistant के लिए:

  1. Online Test (Computer Based)।
  2. Typing/Stenography Skill Test (Qualifying Nature)।
  3. Document Verification।

Junior Executive (Rajbhasha) के लिए:

  1. Online Test (Objective + Descriptive)।
  2. Document Verification।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया और शुल्क

  1. Registration: CWC की वेबसाइट www.cewacor.nic.in पर “Career” सेक्शन में जाएँ।
  2. Form भरना: आवश्यक विवरण भरें और दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें (Photo, Signature, Thumb Impression)।
  3. Fee Payment: शुल्क ऑनलाइन जमा करें:
    • UR/EWS/OBC: ₹1350 (₹850 Fee + ₹500 Charges)
    • SC/ST/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen/Women: ₹500 (केवल Intimation Charges)

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक (Important Links)

लिंक शीर्षकलिंक
Official Websiteयहाँ क्लिक करें
Online Apply (17 Oct 2025 से सक्रिय)यहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 15 नवंबर 2025 है।

Q2: क्या Final Year के छात्र आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

नहीं, उम्मीदवार के पास 15.11.2025 तक डिग्री प्रमाणपत्र होना चाहिए।

Q3: क्या परीक्षा में Negative Marking होगी?

हाँ, प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर पर 1/4 अंक काटे जाएँगे।

Q4: क्या दोनों पदों के लिए अलग-अलग आवेदन करना होगा?

हाँ, दोनों पदों के लिए अलग-अलग आवेदन और शुल्क देना होगा।

entry.title: CID बिहार भर्ती 2025: 189 Assistant Director और Scientific Assistant पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
CID बिहार भर्ती 2025: 189 Assistant Director और Scientific Assistant पदों पर भर्ती!

M.Sc., M.Tech, B.Tech धारकों के लिए बिहार में सरकारी नौकरी — बिना परीक्षा मेरिट के आधार पर चयन।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! यदि आपने विज्ञान या इंजीनियरिंग के क्षेत्र में Postgraduate Degree प्राप्त की है, तो आपके लिए यह एक सुनहरा अवसर है। बिहार पुलिस के Crime Investigation Department (CID) ने Forensic Science Laboratory के लिए Assistant Director (सहायक निदेशक) और Senior Scientific Assistant (वरिष्ठ वैज्ञानिक सहायक) पदों पर भर्ती हेतु अधिसूचना जारी की है (विज्ञापन संख्या – 01/2025)।

यह भर्ती पूरी तरह से संविदा (Contractual) आधार पर 11 महीनों की अवधि के लिए की जाएगी। कुल 189 पदों पर नियुक्तियाँ होंगी। इस भर्ती की सबसे बड़ी विशेषता यह है कि इसमें कोई लिखित परीक्षा नहीं होगी — चयन पूरी तरह से शैक्षणिक योग्यता (Academic Merit) के आधार पर किया जाएगा।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया 06 अक्टूबर 2025 से प्रारंभ होकर 31 अक्टूबर 2025 तक चलेगी। नीचे आपको इस भर्ती से जुड़ी पूरी जानकारी दी गई है।

CID बिहार रिक्तियाँ 2025

नीचे दी गई तालिका में पदवार कुल रिक्तियों का विवरण दिया गया है:

पद का नामकुल पद
Assistant Director (सहायक निदेशक)89
Senior Scientific Assistant (वरिष्ठ वैज्ञानिक सहायक)100
कुल योग189

CID बिहार वेतनमान 2025 (मानदेय)

यह पद संविदा आधारित है, इसलिए चयनित उम्मीदवारों को एक निश्चित मासिक मानदेय (Fixed Monthly Honorarium) दिया जाएगा जो 11 महीनों की अवधि के लिए लागू होगा।

पद नाममासिक मानदेय
Assistant Director₹65,000 प्रति माह
Senior Scientific Assistant₹50,000 प्रति माह

CID बिहार पात्रता मापदंड 2025

आवेदन करने से पहले उम्मीदवारों को नीचे दी गई शैक्षणिक योग्यता और आयु सीमा को पूरा करना आवश्यक है:

विभागशैक्षणिक योग्यता
Chemistry Division (रसायन विभाग)M.Sc./M.Tech in Forensic Science, Chemistry, Biochemistry, Pharmacy, या Toxicology।
Physics Division (भौतिकी विभाग)M.Sc./M.Tech in Forensic Science, Physics, Mathematics या
B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science, Electronics, Instrumentation के साथ 1 वर्ष का स्पेशलाइजेशन।
Biology Division (जीव विज्ञान विभाग)M.Sc./M.Tech in Forensic Science, Biology, Botany, Zoology, Biotechnology, Microbiology, Genetics, या Molecular Biology।
Psychology Division (मनोविज्ञान विभाग)M.Sc./M.Tech/M.A. in Psychology या Clinical Psychology।
आयु सीमा (01.10.2025 तक):
• न्यूनतम आयु: 21 वर्ष
• सेवानिवृत्त कर्मियों के लिए अधिकतम आयु: 65 वर्ष
(सामान्य उम्मीदवारों की अधिकतम आयु सीमा हेतु आधिकारिक अधिसूचना देखें)

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

इस भर्ती प्रक्रिया से जुड़ी सभी प्रमुख तिथियाँ नीचे दी गई हैं:

कार्यक्रमतिथि व समय
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ06 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि31 अक्टूबर 2025 (रात 11:59 बजे)
आवेदन शुल्क: ₹100 (सभी उम्मीदवारों के लिए समान)। भुगतान ऑनलाइन माध्यम या Demand Draft द्वारा किया जा सकता है।

CID बिहार चयन प्रक्रिया 2025

इस भर्ती के लिए कोई लिखित परीक्षा नहीं होगी। चयन पूरी तरह से शैक्षणिक मेरिट के आधार पर किया जाएगा। मेरिट सूची केवल उम्मीदवार के Post-Graduate (PG) में प्राप्त अंकों के आधार पर तैयार की जाएगी।

चरणविवरण
चरण 1ऑनलाइन आवेदन जमा करना
चरण 2PG अंकों के आधार पर मेरिट सूची तैयार करना
चरण 3दस्तावेज़ सत्यापन (Document Verification)
चरण 4चिकित्सा परीक्षण (Medical Examination)

CID बिहार भर्ती 2025 के लिए आवेदन प्रक्रिया

इस भर्ती के लिए आवेदन केवल CID Bihar की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट के माध्यम से ऑनलाइन स्वीकार किए जाएँगे। नीचे चरणवार प्रक्रिया दी गई है:

  1. चरण 1: आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://cidfslrecruitment.bihar.gov.in/ पर जाएँ।
  2. चरण 2: “Apply Online” लिंक पर क्लिक करें।
  3. चरण 3: नया पंजीकरण करें और आवेदन फॉर्म में मांगी गई सभी जानकारी (व्यक्तिगत व शैक्षणिक विवरण) ध्यानपूर्वक भरें।
  4. चरण 4: अपना फोटो और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ (scanned copies) अपलोड करें।
  5. चरण 5: आवेदन शुल्क का भुगतान ऑनलाइन माध्यम या Demand Draft के जरिए करें।
  6. चरण 6: सबमिट करने से पहले अपने आवेदन का “Preview” देख लें और सभी विवरण जांचें।
  7. चरण 7: आवेदन को “Final Submit” करें और उसका प्रिंटआउट निकालकर सुरक्षित रखें।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन के लिए आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

ऑनलाइन आवेदन करते समय उम्मीदवारों को नीचे दिए गए दस्तावेज़ों की स्वयं-सत्यापित (self-attested) स्कैन कॉपी अपलोड करनी होगी:

  • पासपोर्ट आकार का नवीनतम फ़ोटोग्राफ़
  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता प्रमाणपत्र (Post-Graduate Marksheets व Degree)
  • अनुभव प्रमाणपत्र (यदि लागू हो)
  • जाति प्रमाणपत्र (आरक्षण हेतु)
  • निवास प्रमाणपत्र (आरक्षण हेतु)

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करें (06.10.2025 से सक्रिय)यहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक अधिसूचना PDFयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या इस भर्ती के लिए कोई परीक्षा होगी?

नहीं, इस भर्ती के लिए कोई परीक्षा नहीं होगी। चयन पूरी तरह से Post-Graduate (PG) अंकों के आधार पर बनाई गई मेरिट सूची से होगा।

प्रश्न 2: क्या यह नौकरी स्थायी (Permanent) है?

नहीं, यह एक अस्थायी (Temporary) नौकरी है जो 11 महीनों के संविदा (Contract) पर आधारित है।

प्रश्न 3: आवेदन शुल्क कितना है?

सभी उम्मीदवारों के लिए आवेदन शुल्क ₹100 निर्धारित है।

entry.title: Rajasthan Jamadar Recruitment 2025: 72 पदों पर शानदार सरकारी भर्ती
entry.content:
Rajasthan Jamadar Recruitment 2025: 72 पदों पर शानदार सरकारी भर्ती

Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB) ने Abkari Vibhag में Jamadar Grade-II के 72 पदों पर भर्ती का नोटिफिकेशन जारी किया है। केवल CET (Senior Secondary)-2025 पास उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB) द्वारा Abkari Vibhag में Jamadar Grade-II के कुल 72 पदों पर सीधी भर्ती निकाली गई है — जिनमें 64 पद Non-Scheduled Area और 8 पद Scheduled Area के लिए हैं। आवेदन 17 अक्टूबर 2025 से 15 नवंबर 2025 तक किए जा सकते हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

कार्यक्रमतिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ17 अक्टूबर 2025
आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि15 नवंबर 2025 (रात 11:59 बजे)
परीक्षा की संभावित तिथि27 दिसंबर 2025

पात्रता मानदंड (Eligibility Criteria)

शैक्षणिक योग्यता

  • Senior Secondary (12वीं) या समकक्ष परीक्षा पास।
  • Computer ज्ञान – “O” Level, NIELIT, COPA, DPCS, या RS-CIT कोर्स।
  • देवनागरी लिपि में हिंदी का ज्ञान तथा राजस्थान की संस्कृति की जानकारी।

शारीरिक मापदंड

मापदंडपुरुषमहिला
ऊँचाई168 सेमी152 सेमी
सीना81 सेमी (5 सेमी फुलाव आवश्यक)लागू नहीं

आयु सीमा

1 जनवरी 2026 को न्यूनतम 18 वर्ष और अधिकतम 40 वर्ष। आरक्षित वर्गों को नियमानुसार छूट दी जाएगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. लिखित परीक्षा (Offline OMR) – 100 प्रश्न, 1/3 नकारात्मक अंकन।
  2. शारीरिक दक्षता परीक्षण (PET):
    • पुरुष: 10 किमी दौड़ – 60 मिनट में।
    • महिला: 5 किमी दौड़ – 35 मिनट में।
  3. दस्तावेज़ सत्यापन (Document Verification)।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया और शुल्क

  1. SSO Portal (sso.rajasthan.gov.in) पर One Time Registration (OTR) पूरा करें।
  2. CET 2025 Application Number दर्ज कर भर्ती के लिए आवेदन करें।
  3. सभी विवरण सही भरें और Live Photo कैप्चर करें।

आवेदन शुल्क (OTR के माध्यम से)

  • General / OBC (Creamy Layer): ₹600/-
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)/EWS/SC/ST: ₹400/-
  • Divyang: ₹400/-

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंककार्यवाही
Official Websiteयहाँ क्लिक करें
Online Apply (SSO Portal)यहाँ आवेदन करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: क्या CET 2025 जरूरी है?

हाँ, केवल वही उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं जिन्होंने CET (Senior Secondary Level) 2025 परीक्षा पास की है।

Q2: क्या PwD उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

नहीं, इस पद के कार्य स्वरूप के कारण Divyang उम्मीदवारों के लिए पद आरक्षित नहीं हैं।

Q3: क्या अन्य राज्य के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

हाँ, लेकिन उन्हें General Category माना जाएगा और आरक्षण का लाभ नहीं मिलेगा।

Q4: क्या Live Photo आवश्यक है?

हाँ, आवेदन के दौरान Live Photo कैप्चर करना अनिवार्य है।

entry.title: PGIMER Recruitment 2026: 59 Non-Teaching Posts Open in Chandigarh & Sangrur
entry.content:
PGIMER Recruitment 2026: 59 Non-Teaching Posts Open in Chandigarh & Sangrur

A major recruitment drive by the Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education & Research for various Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' posts. Apply online from January 1, 2026.

Post Name: Blood Transfusion Officer, Store Keeper, Junior Engineer, Pharmacist, Technician, Security Guard, and more
Last Date: 16 February 2026
Total Posts: 59

The Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education & Research (PGIMER), Chandigarh, an autonomous institution of national importance, has released a major recruitment notification (Advertisement No. PGI/RC/050/2025/2455) for various non-teaching positions. The institute invites online applications from eligible Indian citizens for direct recruitment to fill a total of 59 posts across Group 'A', 'B', and 'C'. These vacancies are spread across PGIMER, Chandigarh (55 posts) and the PGI Satellite Centre, Sangrur, Punjab (04 posts).

This is a golden opportunity for candidates with diverse qualifications, ranging from medical degrees and engineering diplomas to matriculation and ITI certificates, to secure a stable and prestigious career in one of India's foremost medical and research institutions. The recruitment drive includes a wide array of posts such as Blood Transfusion Officer, Junior Engineer, Pharmacist, various Technicians, and Security Guard. The selection process will be primarily based on a Computer Based Test (CBT), with an interview for Group 'A' posts. The online application portal will be open from January 1, 2026, to February 16, 2026. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all essential details of this recruitment process.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government positions in a premier medical institute with postings in Chandigarh and Sangrur. Key dates and details are as follows:

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Postgraduate Institute of Medical Education & Research (PGIMER), Chandigarh
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Autonomous Institute)
Job Location Chandigarh and Sangrur, Punjab
Online Application Start Date 01 January 2026
Last Date for Online Application 16 February 2026

Vacancy Details

The recruitment aims to fill 55 posts at PGIMER, Chandigarh, and 04 posts at PGI Satellite Centre, Sangrur. The detailed breakdown is as follows:

For PGIMER, Chandigarh (55 Posts)

Post Name Group Total Posts
Blood Transfusion Officer A 01
Store Keeper B 06
Junior Engineer (Civil, Electrical, R&A, Horticulture) B 07
Pharmacist Grade-II C 02
Receptionist C 03
Sanitary Inspector Grade-II C 03
Technician Grade-IV (Manifold Room) C 02
Staff Car Driver C 01
Technician Grade-I (Laundry) C 11
Security Guard Grade-II C 06
Other C-Group Posts (Photographer, Mechanic, etc.) C 13

For PGI Satellite Centre, Sangrur (04 Posts)

Post Name Group Total Posts
Laboratory Attendant Grade-II C 02
CSR Assistant Grade-II C 01
Technician Grade-IV (Manifold Room/Plant) C 01
Note: Some vacancies are backlog vacancies. Detailed category-wise reservation is available in the official notification. Candidates who previously applied for the Blood Transfusion Officer post against a 2024 advertisement need not apply again.

Application Fee

  • For General/OBC/EWS category: Rs. 1500/- + Transaction Charges.
  • For SC/ST category: Rs. 800/- + Transaction Charges.
  • Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) are exempted from payment of the fee.

The fee must be paid online via Debit/Credit Card or Net Banking. The fee is non-refundable.

Eligibility Criteria

Each post has specific requirements for educational qualifications, experience, and age. The crucial date for determining eligibility is the last date of application submission (16.02.2026).

Post Name Eligibility Requirements (Qualification, Experience, Age)
Blood Transfusion Officer (Group A) A recognized medical qualification (MBBS). Experience: Five years of experience in a Blood Bank after registration as a Medical Graduate. Must be registered with a State Medical Council. Age: 18-35 years.
Store Keeper (Group B) Bachelor's degree in Maths/Economics/Commerce/Statistics AND MBA/Postgraduate in Maths/Economics/Commerce/Statistics/Finance with at least 50% marks. Age: 18-30 years.
Junior Engineer (Civil/Elec/R&A/Hort.) (Group B) Degree in the respective branch of Engineering OR a minimum of 3 years Diploma in the respective branch from a recognized Institute. Age: 18-30 years.
Pharmacist Grade-II (Group C) Matriculation or equivalent. Diploma in Pharmacy from a recognized Institution. Should be a registered Pharmacist. Age: 18-30 years.
Technician Grade-IV (Manifold Room/Plant) (Group C) Matric/10th Standard with ITI Certificate in the respective trade OR Matric with trade certificate and 5 years of experience. Age: 18-30 years.
Security Guard Grade-II (Group C) Matriculation from a recognized Board. Physical Standards: Height-167 Cms, Chest-80 Cms for males. Ex-servicemen with excellent records are preferred. Must qualify in a Physical Efficiency Test. Age: 18-30 years.

Selection Process

The selection process is a multi-stage procedure designed to ensure a fair and comprehensive assessment of candidates.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT): All candidates who successfully submit their online application will be allowed to appear in the online CBT. This is the primary stage of selection.
  2. Shortlisting: On the basis of the CBT, candidates who meet the minimum qualifying criteria (40% for General/EWS, 35% for SC/ST/OBC) will be shortlisted for the next stage. The number of candidates shortlisted will be 3 times the number of posts (for posts without a skill test) or 5 times the number of posts (for posts with a skill test).
  3. Skill Test / Physical Test (Qualifying): For posts like Staff Car Driver and Security Guard Grade-II, a mandatory qualifying Trade Test or Physical Efficiency Test will be conducted.
  4. Interview (for Group 'A' Posts Only): For Group 'A' posts (Blood Transfusion Officer), there will be an interview for 15 marks. For Group 'B' and 'C' posts, there is no interview.
  5. Document Verification: All shortlisted candidates will have to undergo verification of their original documents to confirm their eligibility.

Exam Pattern

The Computer Based Test (CBT) will be conducted in English and will consist of objective-type multiple-choice questions. There will be negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.

Pattern for Group 'A' Posts

  • Total Questions: 85 MCQs
  • Total Marks: 85
  • Duration: 85 minutes
  • Interview: 15 Marks
  • Grand Total: 100 Marks

Pattern for Group 'B' & 'C' Posts

  • Total Questions: 100 MCQs
  • Total Marks: 100
  • Duration: 100 minutes
  • Grand Total: 100 Marks

The syllabus for the online CBT will be in accordance with the prescribed educational qualification and experience for the respective post. The detailed syllabus is usually released on the PGIMER website.

PET

A Physical Efficiency Test (PET) is mandatory for the post of Security Guard Grade-II only. The test is qualifying in nature, and candidates must secure a minimum of 50% of the total marks (25 for males, 15 for females). The test includes:

  • For Males (Total 50 Marks): Long Jump (11 ft), High Jump (4 ft), 100m Sprint, 792m Race, Obstacles & Pull-ups.
  • For Females (Total 30 Marks): Long Jump (8'6"), High Jump (3'2"), 100m Sprint, 400m Race.

For all other posts, there is no PET.

How to Prepare

A structured preparation strategy is key to succeeding in this competitive exam.

  1. Understand the Syllabus: The syllabus will be based on your educational qualification. For a Junior Engineer, revise your diploma/degree subjects. For a Pharmacist, revise your pharmacy curriculum.
  2. Practice MCQs: Solve as many MCQs as possible from standard reference books and previous years' papers of similar recruitment exams (like AIIMS, JIPMER, etc.).
  3. Time Management: The exam has a strict time limit (approx. 1 minute per question). Practice with timed online mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy.
  4. Prepare for Physical Test (for Security Guard): If applying for Security Guard, start your physical training immediately. Focus on running, jumping, and pull-ups to meet the qualifying standards.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended resources for your preparation:

  • For Technical/Medical Posts: Standard textbooks from your respective degree/diploma curriculum. Objective question books for competitive exams in your field are highly recommended.
  • For General Aptitude (if asked): "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal and "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • For General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online. No offline application form will be accepted.

  1. Visit the official PGIMER website: https://pgimer.edu.in and go to the "Recruitment" link on the Home Page.
  2. Find the relevant advertisement (No.PGI/RC/050/2025/2455) and click on the "Apply Online" link.
  3. First, complete the online registration by filling in your mandatory details to get a User ID and password.
  4. Log in and fill out the detailed application form step-by-step, including personal information, qualifications, and experience.
  5. Upload a scanned copy of your photograph (snapped on or after Oct 1, 2025), signature, and other required documents.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Debit/Credit Card or Net Banking.
  7. Review your application carefully before final submission. After submission, take a printout of the application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must upload legible scanned copies of their original certificates during the online application process.

  1. Scanned copy of your recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Proof of Date of Birth (10th certificate).
  3. All educational marksheets and certificates (from Matric onwards).
  4. Experience certificates, where applicable.
  5. Caste/Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD). The OBC/EWS certificate must be valid for the financial year 2025-26.
  6. No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the current employer if working in a government/autonomous body.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Click Here (Not Active)
Official Notification Click Here

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the PGIMER recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is February 16, 2026.

Question 2: Is there an interview for Group 'B' and 'C' posts?

No, the notification clearly states that for Group 'B' and 'C' (Non-Gazetted) posts, there will be no interview. Selection will be based on the merit of the CBT only.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the CBT?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the Computer Based Test.

entry.title: MP Apex Bank Recruitment 2026 for 1763 Posts
entry.content:
MP Apex Bank Recruitment 2026 for 1763 Posts

The M.P. Rajya Sahakari Bank Mytd (Apex Bank), Bhopal, has announced a massive recruitment drive for 1763 posts of Computer Operator and Society Manager. These appointments will be made in 38 District Central Cooperative Banks (DCCBs) across Madhya Pradesh. The online application process will be active from January 06, 2026, to February 05, 2026.

Post Name: Computer Operator, Society Manager
Last Date: 05 February 2026
Total Posts: 1763

The M.P. Rajya Sahakari Bank Mytd (Apex Bank) has released a significant employment notification, inviting online applications from eligible Indian citizens for a large-scale recruitment. This drive aims to fill a total of 1763 vacancies for the positions of Computer Operator and Society Manager. The selected candidates will be appointed in the 38 District Central Cooperative Banks (DCCBs) of Madhya Pradesh, playing a crucial role in the state's cooperative banking sector.

This is a golden opportunity for candidates seeking a career in the banking domain within Madhya Pradesh. The selection process will involve an online examination, for which call letters will be issued approximately 10 days prior to the exam date. The entire application process, including fee payment, is to be completed online through the official Apex Bank website. Aspirants are advised to read the instructions carefully and apply within the stipulated timeline.

MP Apex Bank Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take note of the crucial dates for this major recruitment drive. The entire process will be conducted online.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization M.P. Rajya Sahakari Bank Mytd (Apex Bank)
Recruiting for 38 District Central Cooperative Banks (DCCBs) of Madhya Pradesh
Post Names Computer Operator & Society Manager
Total Vacancies 1763
Application Start Date 06 January 2026
Application Last Date 05 February 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Cooperative Bank Job
Job Location Across Madhya Pradesh
Official Website www.apexbankmp.bank.in

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill a combined total of 1763 vacancies for two key positions in the DCCBs of Madhya Pradesh.

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Computer Operator 1763 (Combined)
Society Manager
Total 1763
Note: The detailed breakdown of vacancies for each post and for each of the 38 District Central Cooperative Banks will be available in the full notification on the official website.

Application Fee

The official notification summary confirms that an application fee/intimation charge is applicable and must be paid online. However, the exact amount for different categories has not been specified in the initial notice.

  • Candidates are advised to refer to the detailed advertisement on the official website, www.apexbankmp.bank.in, for the complete fee structure.

Eligibility Criteria

The detailed eligibility criteria regarding age and educational qualifications have not been mentioned in the initial advertisement. Candidates must refer to the full notification on the official Apex Bank website for this crucial information.

Criteria Expected Details (To be confirmed from full notification)
Educational Qualification
  • For Computer Operator: A Bachelor's Degree in any discipline, likely with a Diploma in Computer Application (DCA) or an equivalent computer qualification.
  • For Society Manager: A Bachelor's Degree, likely in Commerce/Agriculture/Management or a related field.
Age Limit The minimum and maximum age limits, along with relaxation for reserved categories as per M.P. Government norms, will be detailed in the full advertisement. A typical age range for such posts is 18 to 35 years for the general category.

Selection Process

Based on the information provided, the selection process will consist of the following stages:

  1. Online Written Examination: All eligible candidates will have to appear for an online examination. The call letters for this exam will be made available for download approximately 10 days before the exam date.
  2. Document Verification: Candidates who are shortlisted based on their performance in the online examination will be called for Document Verification.

A skill test might be included for the post of Computer Operator, the details of which will be available in the full notification.

Exam Pattern (Expected)

While the exact exam pattern will be detailed in the official notification, a typical pattern for such cooperative bank examinations includes the following subjects in an objective-type (MCQ) online test.

Subject Expected No. of Questions
Reasoning Ability40-50
Quantitative Aptitude40-50
English Language40-50
General Awareness (with special focus on Banking & Cooperatives)40-50
Computer Knowledge40-50

Candidates must check the official notification for the final and confirmed exam structure, including marks, duration, and details about negative marking.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for the posts of Computer Operator and Society Manager.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A focused and structured preparation strategy is essential to succeed in this large-scale recruitment.

  • Understand the Syllabus: Once the detailed notification is out, thoroughly analyze the syllabus for each subject.
  • Focus on Banking & Cooperatives: The General Awareness section will likely have a strong focus on the banking industry, cooperative movement, and financial awareness. Study these topics in depth.
  • Practice Mock Tests: The key to clearing banking exams is speed and accuracy. Solve as many online mock tests as possible to improve your time management and get accustomed to the exam interface.
  • Strengthen Basics: Build a strong foundation in Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English Grammar, as these sections are common in all banking exams.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for building a strong foundation for the online exam.

  • Reasoning Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • English Language: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Computer Knowledge: "Objective Computer Awareness" by Arihant Publications.
  • Banking Awareness: "Banking Awareness" by Arihant Experts or by Disha Publication.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the MP Apex Bank website.

  1. Visit the official website: www.apexbankmp.bank.in.
  2. On the homepage, find the link for "Recruitment for Computer Operator & Society Manager in DCCBs 2026".
  3. Read the detailed notification carefully and then click on the "Apply Online" link.
  4. Register on the portal by providing your basic details to generate a registration ID and password.
  5. Log in and fill out the online application form with all the required personal and educational details.
  6. Upload your scanned photograph, signature, and other required documents as per the specified format.
  7. Pay the application fee online via the integrated payment gateway.
  8. Review the application form thoroughly before final submission.
  9. Download and take a printout of the final submitted application and the fee receipt for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should keep the following documents scanned and ready before starting the online application:

  1. Recent passport-size photograph.
  2. Scanned copy of your signature.
  3. Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets.
  4. Computer diploma/certificate (if applicable).
  5. Caste/Category certificate (if claiming reservation).
    6. *
  6. Domicile certificate for M.P. residents.
  7. Valid mobile number and email ID.

Important Links

Action Link
