  1. First, visit the official BDL careers website: https://bdl-india.in.
  2. Go to the "Careers" or "Recruitment" section and click on the relevant advertisement "Advt No. BDL/C-HR (TA & CP) /2025-4".
  3. Read the detailed advertisement and instructions carefully before applying.
  4. Click on the "Apply Online" link and begin the registration process.
  5. Fill in the application form with your personal details, educational qualifications, and other information accurately.
    6. _
  6. Upload scanned copies of all the required documents in the prescribed format as listed below.
    7. _
  7. Pay the application fee as per your category through SBI e-pay (Debit Card / Credit Card / Net Banking / UPI).
    8. _
  8. Review all the filled-in information before finally submitting the application form.
    9. _
  9. Take a printout of the registration slip and fee receipt for future reference.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here (Link will be active when available)
Apply Online Click Here (Active from 03.12.2025)
Official Website https://bdl-india.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: When will the application for BDL MT Recruitment 2025 begin?

The online application process will start at 09:00 AM on December 3, 2025.

Question 2: What is the last date to apply?

The last date to apply online is 04:00 PM on December 29, 2025.

Question 3: Can final-year students apply?

As per the notification, the candidate must possess the required qualification by November 25, 2025. You can apply if your final result is declared by this date.

Question 4: Is there negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the Computer Based Online Test (CBoT).

Question 5: What will be the approximate CTC after confirmation as a Management Trainee?

After successful completion of training and confirmation as an Assistant Manager, the approximate average CTC will be around Rs. 15.55 Lakhs per annum.

entry.title: SSC GD Constable Recruitment 2026: Bumper Vacancy for 25,487 Posts in CAPFs, SSF, and Assam Rifles
entry.content:
SSC GD Constable Recruitment 2026: Bumper Vacancy for 25,487 Posts in CAPFs, SSF, and Assam Rifles

A golden opportunity for 10th-pass youth to join the armed forces and serve the nation. Online applications are open from December 1, 2025, to December 31, 2025.

Post Name: Constable (General Duty) and Rifleman (General Duty)
Last Date: 31/12/2025
Total Posts: 25,487

The Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has once again provided a golden opportunity for the youth of India to join the armed forces and serve the country. The SSC has released a detailed notification (F. No. HQ-C-3007/10/2025-C-3) for the recruitment of Constable (General Duty) in Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs), Secretariat Security Force (SSF), and Rifleman (General Duty) in Assam Rifles (AR). Through this massive recruitment drive, a total of 25,487 vacancies will be filled, presenting a huge opportunity for candidates who have passed the 10th grade.

This recruitment is being conducted to fill the crucial posts of Constables in various forces that play a vital role in the country's security, such as the Border Security Force (BSF), Central Industrial Security Force (CISF), Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF), Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP), Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB), SSF, and Assam Rifles (AR). It paves the way for a prestigious career for young individuals who dream of wearing the uniform and serving the nation with pride and dedication. The selection process will be through an open competitive examination, which includes a Computer Based Examination (CBE), Physical Standard Test (PST), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Medical Examination, and Document Verification. This comprehensive article will guide you through all the important details of this recruitment.

SSC GD Constable Recruitment 2026 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other key information to ensure a smooth application process.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Staff Selection Commission (SSC)
Examination Name Constable (GD) in CAPFs, SSF, and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles Examination, 2026
Application Start Date December 1, 2025
Last Date to Apply Online December 31, 2025 (up to 11:00 PM)
Job Type Permanent Central Government Job
Job Location All India

Vacancy Details and Force-wise Breakdown

A total of 25,487 vacancies have been announced under the SSC GD Constable Recruitment 2026. These vacancies are distributed among various forces for male and female candidates. Below is a detailed force-wise breakdown of the vacancies:

Force Name Male Female Total Posts
BSF (Border Security Force) 524 92 616
CISF (Central Industrial Security Force) 13135 1460 14595
CRPF (Central Reserve Police Force) 5366 124 5490
SSB (Sashastra Seema Bal) 1764 0 1764
ITBP (Indo-Tibetan Border Police) 1099 194 1293
AR (Assam Rifles) 1556 150 1706
SSF (Secretariat Security Force) 23 0 23
Grand Total 23,467 2,020 25,487
Note: A detailed state-wise and category-wise classification of vacancies is available on the SSC website. Candidates must check the vacancies for their respective states and categories before filling out the force preferences.

Salary and Pay Scale

  • Pay Level: Level-3 of the 7th Central Pay Commission Pay Matrix
  • Pay Scale: ₹ 21,700 - ₹ 69,100/-

Selected candidates will receive a handsome salary package which will include the basic pay along with Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), and other applicable benefits as per Central Government rules. The initial in-hand salary would be approximately ₹30,000 - ₹35,000 per month, depending on the place of posting.

Eligibility Criteria: Education, Age, and Physical Standards

Candidates must fulfill the following eligibility criteria to be considered for the recruitment process.

Educational Qualification (as on 01.01.2026)

Qualification Details
Minimum Educational Qualification The candidate must have passed Matriculation (10th Class) or an equivalent examination from a recognized Board or University.

Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026)

Criteria Age
Age Limit 18 - 23 years
Date of Birth Candidates should not have been born earlier than 02-01-2003 and later than 01-01-2008.

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

CategoryRelaxation
SC/ST 5 years
OBC 3 years
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) 3 years after deduction of the military service rendered from the actual age
Victims of 1984 riots 5/8/10 years (as per category)

Selection Process

The SSC GD Constable selection is a comprehensive multi-stage process designed to assess the mental, physical, and medical fitness of the candidates.

  1. Stage 1: Computer Based Examination (CBE): This is the first and most crucial stage of the selection process. It is an online objective test that evaluates a candidate's aptitude and general knowledge.
  2. Stage 2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Candidates who qualify in the CBE will be called for PET and PST. These tests are qualifying in nature and will be conducted by the CAPFs.
  3. Stage 3: Detailed Medical Examination (DME) and Document Verification (DV): Candidates who are successful in the PET/PST will be called for a medical examination and document verification.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

The Computer Based Examination (CBE) is the gateway to the next stages. A clear understanding of its pattern and syllabus is essential.

Computer Based Examination (CBE) Pattern

  • The exam will consist of 80 objective-type questions, with each question carrying 2 marks.
  • The total duration of the examination will be 60 minutes.
  • The question paper will be available in English, Hindi, and 13 regional languages.
  • Negative Marking: There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each incorrect answer.
PartSubjectQuestionsMaximum Marks
Part-A General Intelligence and Reasoning 20 40
Part-B General Knowledge and General Awareness 20 40
Part-C Elementary Mathematics 20 40
Part-D English / Hindi 20 40
Total 80 160

Detailed Syllabus for CBE

  • General Intelligence and Reasoning: Analogies, Similarities and Differences, Spatial Visualization, Spatial Orientation, Visual Memory, Discrimination, Observation, Relationship Concepts, Arithmetical Reasoning, Figural Classification, Arithmetic Number Series, Non-verbal Series, Coding and Decoding.
  • General Knowledge and General Awareness: India and its neighboring countries especially pertaining to Sports, History, Culture, Geography, Economic Scene, General Polity, Indian Constitution, and Scientific Research. These questions will be of such a nature that they do not require a special study of any discipline.
  • Elementary Mathematics: Number Systems, Computation of Whole Numbers, Decimals and Fractions, Relationship between Numbers, Fundamental arithmetical operations, Percentages, Ratio and Proportion, Averages, Interest, Profit and Loss, Discount, Mensuration, Time and Distance, Ratio and Time, Time and Work.
  • English / Hindi: This section will test the candidate's ability to understand basic English/Hindi, and their basic comprehension would be tested. Topics include Error Spotting, Fill in the Blanks, Vocabulary, Synonyms, Antonyms, Spellings, Idioms & Phrases, One word substitution, and Comprehension Passage.

PET and PST Details

Candidates who clear the CBE will be called for the Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Physical Standard Test (PST).

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

Candidates must complete the following race within the specified time limits:

CandidateRaceTime LimitRemarks
Male 5 km in 24 minutes For candidates other than those belonging to Ladakh Region
Female 1.6 km in 8 ½ minutes
Male (Ladakh Region) 1.6 km in 7 minutes For candidates of Ladakh Region only
Female (Ladakh Region) 800 metres in 5 minutes

Physical Standard Test (PST)

The minimum physical standards required are as follows:

StandardMaleFemale
Height (General, SC & OBC) 170 cm 157 cm
Height (ST) 162.5 cm 150 cm
Chest (Un-expanded) 80 cm (76 cm for ST) Not Applicable
Chest (Min. expansion) 5 cm Not Applicable
Note: Relaxations in height and chest measurements are available for candidates belonging to certain hill areas and regions. Please refer to the official notification for details.

How to Prepare for the SSC GD Exam

A disciplined and strategic preparation is key to cracking the SSC GD Constable exam.

  1. Understand the Exam Pattern: Thoroughly analyze the exam pattern, marking scheme, and negative marking.
  2. Create a Study Plan: Allocate time for each of the four subjects daily. Give more time to your weaker areas.
  3. Focus on Basics for Maths: The mathematics section is elementary. Strengthen your concepts from Class 8 to 10 NCERT books. Practice daily.
  4. Practice Reasoning Regularly: Reasoning is a high-scoring section. Solve previous years' papers and practice puzzles and series questions to improve your speed and logic.
  5. Stay Updated with GK: For General Knowledge, read newspapers daily and follow a monthly current affairs magazine. Focus on static GK topics like history, geography, and polity.
  6. Choose Your Language Wisely: Decide whether you will attempt the English or Hindi section and prepare accordingly. Focus on grammar and vocabulary.
  7. Physical Preparation: Start your physical training for PET well in advance. Begin with jogging and gradually build your stamina to complete the required race distance comfortably.
  8. Take Mock Tests: Regularly attempt online mock tests to simulate the exam environment. This will help you manage your time effectively and identify areas that need improvement.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of highly recommended books for your SSC GD preparation:

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Knowledge & Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • Elementary Mathematics: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • English Language: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Hindi Language: "Samanya Hindi" by Lucent Publications.
  • Practice Sets: A good set of practice papers and mock tests from a reputed publisher like Arihant or Kiran Prakashan is essential.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must apply through the new official SSC website, https://ssc.gov.in. A One-Time Registration (OTR) is mandatory before applying.

  1. One-Time Registration (OTR):
    • Visit the new SSC website https://ssc.gov.in and click on 'Register Now'.
    • Complete the OTR process using your personal details, mobile number, email ID, Aadhaar number, and 10th class details.
    • Important: OTR done on the old SSC website (ssc.nic.in) is no longer valid. Everyone must complete a new OTR on the new portal.
  2. Filling the Application Form:
    • After completing OTR, log in with your registration number and password.
    • In the 'Live Examination' section, click the 'Apply' link for "Constable (GD) ... Examination, 2026".
    • Most of your information will be auto-filled from your OTR profile.
    • Fill in your examination center preferences, and most importantly, the preferences for the forces (BSF, CISF, etc.).
    • Capture your Live Photograph using a webcam or smartphone. This is a new requirement.
    • Upload your signature and accept the declaration.
    • Pay the application fee if applicable and finally submit your application.
    • Save a printout of the filled application form for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

The following documents will be required during the online application and at the time of document verification:

  1. Mobile Number and Email ID (for OTP verification).
  2. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN Card, etc.).
  3. 10th Class Marksheet and Certificate.
  4. Domicile/Permanent Resident Certificate (PRC).
  5. Reservation Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) in the prescribed format.
  6. NCC Certificate (if applicable, for bonus marks).
  7. Webcam or Smartphone for capturing a live photograph.
  8. Scanned copy of signature (JPEG, 10-20 KB).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online (New SSC Website) Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the SSC GD Constable recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is December 31, 2025 (until 11:00 PM).

Question 2: What is the minimum educational qualification for this recruitment?

The candidate must have passed the 10th Class (Matriculation) from any recognized board.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the Computer Based Examination.

Question 4: Do I need to have a pre-clicked photograph for the application?

No, under the new SSC process, you will be required to capture a live photograph of yourself through a webcam or smartphone while filling out the application.

Question 5: Can I change my preference for the forces later?

No, you must fill in your order of preference for all seven forces (BSF, CISF, CRPF, SSB, ITBP, AR, SSF) very carefully while filling the online application form. No changes will be allowed after the final submission.

entry.title: IIMC Recruitment 2025: 51 Non-Teaching Vacancies, Become UDC, Clerk, Assistant - Full Details!
entry.content:
IIMC Recruitment 2025: 51 Non-Teaching Vacancies, Become UDC, Clerk, Assistant - Full Details!

Apply for Group A, B, and C posts at the Indian Institute of Mass Communication's New Delhi and regional centers.

Post Name: Assistant Registrar, Section Officer, UDC, Stenographer, Assistant, etc.
Last Date: 12 January 2026 (Online), 19 January 2026 (Hard Copy)
Total Posts: 51

The Indian Institute of Mass Communication (IIMC), India's premier institute for mass communication education, research, and training, has released a major recruitment notification (Advt. No. 03/2025). Established in 1965 under the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting, IIMC has invited applications from eligible candidates to fill various vacancies at its New Delhi campus and regional centers in Aizawl, Amravati, Dhenkanal, Jammu, and Kottayam.

Through this recruitment drive, a total of 51 non-teaching posts under Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' will be filled. These include prestigious positions such as Assistant Registrar, Section Officer, Upper Division Clerk (UDC), Stenographer, and Assistant. This is a fantastic opportunity for candidates who wish to build a stable and respected career in the country's top media education institute. The application process involves two stages: first, applying online, and then sending a hard copy of the online application with the required documents. Online applications will be open from December 13, 2025, to January 12, 2026.

Important Points

Details Information
Online Application Start Date 13 December 2025
Last Date for Online Application 12 January 2026
Last Date for Submission of Hard Copy 19 January 2026 (by 5:00 PM)
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location New Delhi, Dhenkanal, Aizawl, Amravati, Jammu, Kottayam

Vacancy Details

IIMC has announced a total of 51 posts in various groups, with the post-wise details as follows:

Post Name Pay Level Group Total Posts
Library and Information Officer Level-11 A 01
Assistant Registrar Level-10 A 05
Section Officer Level-7 B 04
Senior Research Assistant Level-6 B 01
Assistant Level-6 B 11
Professional Assistant Level-6 B 05
Junior Programmer Level-6 B 05
Upper Division Clerk (UDC) Level-4 C 12
Stenographer Level-4 C 06
Grand Total 51
Note: One post of Assistant Editor is on a deputation basis. For detailed category-wise reservation, please refer to the official notification.

Application Fee

  • Group A (UR/OBC): ₹1500/- | (SC/ST/Women/EWS/PwD): ₹750/-
  • Group B (UR/OBC): ₹1000/- | (SC/ST/Women/EWS/PwD): ₹500/-
  • Group C (UR/OBC): ₹500/- | (SC/ST/Women/EWS/PwD): ₹250/-

The fee is to be paid via Demand Draft in favor of the "Indian Institute of Mass Communication", payable at New Delhi.

Eligibility Criteria

Specific educational qualifications, experience, and age limits are prescribed for each post:

Post Name Eligibility (Qualification, Experience, and Age)
Assistant Registrar Master's Degree with at least 55% marks + 3 years of experience as a Section Officer. Age: 40 years.
Section Officer Bachelor's Degree + 3 years of experience as an Assistant or 8 years as a UDC. Knowledge of computer operations. Age: 35 years.
Assistant Bachelor's Degree + 3 years of experience as a UDC. Proficiency in typing, computer applications, and noting/drafting. Age: 35 years.
Upper Division Clerk (UDC) Bachelor's Degree + 2 years of experience as an LDC. Typing speed of 35 wpm in English or 30 wpm in Hindi. Knowledge of computers. Age: 32 years.
Stenographer Bachelor's Degree + Stenography speed of 80 wpm and typing speed of 35/30 wpm (Eng/Hin). Knowledge of computers. Age: 32 years.

Selection Process

The selection process will vary according to the group of the post:

  1. Group 'A' Posts: Selection will be based on a Written Test, Skill Test, and Interview.
  2. Group 'B' and 'C' Posts: Selection will be based on a Written Test and a Skill Test. There will be no interview for these posts.

Only candidates who qualify in the written test will be called for the skill test/interview.

How to Apply

The application process will be completed in two stages:

  1. Online Application:
    • First, visit the IIMC Samarth portal: https://iimcnt.samarth.edu.in/.
    • Select the post you wish to apply for and register yourself.
    • Fill out the online application form carefully with all the required information.
    • After final submission, take a printout of the filled application form.
  2. Sending the Hard Copy:
    • Take a printout of the online application form.
    • Attach self-attested photocopies of all the required documents as mentioned below.
    • Attach the original Demand Draft for the application fee.
    • Send all these in a sealed envelope via Speed Post/Registered Post to the following address:
      "The Deputy Registrar, Indian Institute of Mass Communication, Aruna Asaf Ali Marg, New JNU Campus, New Delhi – 110067"
    • The hard copy must reach by 5:00 PM on January 19, 2026.

Documents to Send with the Hard Copy

You need to send self-attested copies of the following documents with the printout of your online application:

  1. All educational certificates and marksheets (from 10th to the highest qualification).
  2. Experience certificates.
  3. Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD).
  4. Identity proof (Aadhaar Card, etc.).
  5. Original Demand Draft for the application fee.
  6. Any other relevant certificates.

How to Prepare

A good preparation is necessary for success in the IIMC recruitment exam:

  1. Check the Syllabus: IIMC will release a detailed syllabus and exam scheme for each post on its website.
  2. Focus on General Subjects: Subjects like General Knowledge, Current Affairs, Reasoning, English, and Hindi will be important for all posts.
  3. Practice Skills: If you are applying for posts like UDC or Stenographer, practice typing and stenography regularly.
  4. Post-Specific Preparation: For posts like Assistant Registrar and Section Officer, focus on government rules, administrative procedures (noting/drafting), and computer applications.
  5. Refer to Previous Papers: Analyze old question papers from non-teaching recruitments of other central universities or similar organizations.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right study material is crucial for good preparation. Here are some recommended books by subject:

Subject Recommended Books
General Knowledge and Current Affairs
  • "Lucent's Samanya Gyan"
  • Monthly current affairs magazines.
Reasoning
  • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" - R.S. Aggarwal
English and Hindi Language
  • "Objective General English" - S.P. Bakshi
  • "Lucent's Sampurna Hindi Vyakaran aur Rachna"
Administrative Aptitude (Noting/Drafting)
  • Books on Central Government office manuals and administrative procedures.

Important Links

Link Type Anchor Text
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Download Here
Official Website iimc.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Is the application process completely online?

No, you first have to apply online and then send a printout of that application form with all the necessary documents and the Demand Draft by post.

Question 2: Can I apply for multiple posts?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you are eligible, but you will have to submit separate online applications and send separate hard copies for each post.

Question 3: How do I pay the application fee?

The application fee is to be paid only through a Demand Draft (DD), which should be payable to the "Indian Institute of Mass Communication" in New Delhi.

entry.title: SJVN Workman Trainee Recruitment 2025: Apply for 87 Vacancies in Himachal Pradesh
entry.content:
SJVN Workman Trainee Recruitment 2025: Notification for 87 Vacancies

Exclusive Recruitment Opportunity for Himachal Pradesh Domicile Candidates in SJVN Limited

Post Name: Workman Trainee (Assistant, Driver, Electrician, Fitter, Welder, Surveyor)
Last Date: October 13, 2025
Total Posts: 87 Vacancies

SJVN Limited, a premier Mini-Ratna Category-I Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of Power, Government of India, has officially released its recruitment notification for the year 2025. Under Advertisement Number 124/2025, the organization is looking to fill 87 vacancies for various Workman Trainee positions. This recruitment drive is specifically targeted at the youth of Himachal Pradesh, as it is a mandatory requirement for applicants to be domiciles of the state. SJVN is a joint venture between the Government of India and the Government of Himachal Pradesh, and this recruitment represents its commitment to providing local employment and fostering economic growth in the Himalayan region.

The roles advertised cover a broad spectrum of technical and administrative functions, including Assistant (Accounts), General Assistant, Driver, and several ITI-based trades such as Electrician, Fitter, Welder, and Surveyor. For young professionals in Himachal Pradesh, SJVN offers not just a job, but a career in the clean energy sector. As the company expands its footprint in hydroelectric, solar, and wind power across India and neighboring countries, joining as a Workman Trainee provides a foundation in one of the most stable and respected PSUs in the country. The application window is set to open on September 22, 2025, and will close on October 13, 2025.

Working in the power sector, especially with a focus on sustainable energy, requires a high level of discipline and technical expertise. SJVN ensures that its trainees are provided with world-class training during their initial year, preparing them for the rigors of power plant operations and corporate administration. This 2000-word guide is designed to provide you with an in-depth analysis of the eligibility criteria, the detailed syllabus for the written exam, the salary structure during and after training, and a step-by-step strategy to secure a position in this competitive recruitment process.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Dates

Candidates must strictly adhere to the timeline provided by SJVN. Unlike larger national exams, PSU recruitments often have shorter application windows. It is vital to have all your Himachal Pradesh domicile documents and educational certificates ready before the portal opens. Late submissions or applications with incomplete documentation will not be considered under any circumstances.

Event / Feature Dates and Information
Advertisement Number 124/2025
Online Registration Opens September 22, 2025 (10:00 AM)
Last Date for Online Application October 13, 2025 (06:00 PM)
Last Date for Fee Payment October 13, 2025
Job Type Government PSU (Permanent after Training)
Domicile Requirement Himachal Pradesh Residents Only

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown by Trade

The 87 vacancies are distributed among technical trades and administrative roles. This ensures that candidates from both vocational and academic backgrounds have a chance to join the organization. Below is the categorical breakdown as per the initial notification data.

Post Designation Trade / Discipline Number of Vacancies
Workman Trainee (ITI) Electrician 30
Workman Trainee (ITI) Fitter 15
Workman Trainee (ITI) Welder 10
Workman Trainee (ITI) Surveyor 05
Assistant (Accounts) Commerce / Finance 12
Assistant (General) Any Graduate 10
Driver Heavy/Light Vehicle 05
Total Vacancies - 87
Special Note: The vacancies include reservations for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), EWS, and PwBD candidates as per the Government of India and SJVN guidelines. Only those candidates who belong to these categories in Himachal Pradesh can avail of the reservation benefits.

Salary and Career Benefits

One of the primary reasons why SJVN is a preferred employer is its transparent and lucrative pay structure. The company follows the Industrial Dearness Allowance (IDA) pattern of pay scales, which is revised quarterly based on the consumer price index.

  • Training Period (1 Year): Selected candidates will be placed as "Trainees" for a period of one year. During this time, they will receive a fixed consolidated stipend of ₹21,500 per month.
  • Regularization: Upon successful completion of the one-year training, candidates will be regularized in the W3 or W4 grade (depending on the post).
  • Regular Pay Scale: The basic pay starts at ₹21,500 with an annual increment of 3%.

Additional Allowances: Once regularized, employees are entitled to a comprehensive benefits package, including Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA) or Company Leased Accommodation, Site Allowance (if posted at project sites), Medical facilities for self and family, and the New Pension Scheme (NPS). SJVN also provides performance-related pay (PRP) based on company and individual achievements.

Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility is the most crucial part of the notification. Candidates must ensure they meet the specific educational requirements for their respective trades. The maximum age limit is 30 years as of the closing date of the application, with relaxations for reserved categories.

  • Assistant (Accounts): Full-time B.Com degree with a minimum of 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/PwBD). Candidates must also have a typing speed of 40 words per minute on a computer.
  • Assistant (General): Full-time Graduate degree in any discipline with a minimum of 55% marks. Additionally, a one-year Diploma in Computer Applications is mandatory.
  • ITI Trades (Electrician/Fitter/Welder): 10th Standard pass plus a full-time ITI course in the respective trade. Must have a minimum of 55% marks in ITI.
  • Driver: 8th Standard pass with a valid driving license for Light Motor Vehicles (LMV) and Heavy Motor Vehicles (HMV). Minimum 2 years of experience in driving is often preferred.
  • Domicile: Candidates must possess a Bonafide Himachali Certificate issued by the competent authority.

Selection Process

SJVN follows a merit-based selection process that emphasizes technical proficiency and general aptitude. There is no interview for Workman Trainee positions, making the written test the most critical stage of the recruitment.

Post Category Stage 1 Stage 2
ITI Trades Computer Based Test (CBT) Document Verification
Assistant / Accounts Computer Based Test (CBT) Typing Test (Qualifying)
Driver Computer Based Test (CBT) Trade Test / Driving Test

Qualifying Marks: To be considered for the merit list, candidates must score a minimum of 50% marks (General/OBC/EWS) or 40% marks (SC/ST/PwBD) in the CBT. Candidates will be shortlisted for the next stage (Typing or Trade test) in the ratio of 1:5 or as decided by the management.

Computer Based Test Exam Pattern

The Computer Based Test (CBT) is conducted in both Hindi and English. It is a 100-mark objective exam that tests two major areas. A significant advantage for candidates in the SJVN exam is that there is no negative marking.

  • Part 1: Subject Knowledge (70 Questions): This section is specific to the trade or degree. For Electricians, it covers electrical theory; for Accounts Assistants, it covers financial accounting and taxation.
  • Part 2: General Aptitude (30 Questions): This section is common for all and includes:
    • General Knowledge (Current Affairs, HP Geography, History).
    • Reasoning Ability (Analogies, Number Series).
    • Quantitative Aptitude (Basic Mathematics).
    • General English (Grammar and Vocabulary).

The duration of the exam is 90 minutes. Since there is no negative marking, candidates are encouraged to attempt all questions.

Detailed Technical Syllabus

To succeed in the 70-mark technical section, candidates must have a deep understanding of their vocational or academic subjects. Below is a guide on what to focus on for major posts.

ITI Electrician: Focus on DC Machines, Transformers, AC Motors, Wiring Systems, Electrical Safety, Circuit Breakers, and Basic Electronics. Study the NCVT/SCVT curriculum thoroughly.

Assistant (Accounts): Principles of Accounting, Corporate Accounting, Auditing, Income Tax laws, GST, Tally Prime features, and Financial Management basics. Familiarize yourself with the Companies Act 2013.

General Assistant: Proficiency in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), Internet Browsing, Basic Computer Hardware, and General Administrative procedures. Focus on the typing speed as it is the qualifying hurdle.

Driver: Road signs, traffic rules, basic engine maintenance, LMV/HMV mechanisms, and safety regulations as per the Motor Vehicles Act.

Preparation Strategy for SJVN Recruitment

Cracking a PSU exam like SJVN requires a specialized approach. Since the domicile is restricted to Himachal Pradesh, the competition is localized but still intense among top-tier candidates of the state.

Phase 1: Trade Mastery: Since 70% of the marks come from your trade, you must revisit your ITI or College textbooks. For ITI students, Arihant or Youth Competition Times books for technical trades are excellent. For Accounts, rely on your graduation notes and professional practice books.

Phase 2: Aptitude and HP GK: The 30 marks in the aptitude section can be the difference between selection and rejection. Focus specifically on Himachal Pradesh General Knowledge (Geography of rivers, Hydroelectric projects in HP, and State history). SJVN often asks questions related to its own projects like Nathpa Jhakri or Rampur Hydro Power Station.

Phase 3: Speed and Accuracy: Practice mock tests in a timed environment. Even though there is no negative marking, managing 100 questions in 90 minutes requires quick thinking. Use online portals to practice typing if you are applying for the Assistant posts.

Best Books for Preparation

Quality study material is essential for a focused preparation. Here is a list of recommended books for the SJVN Workman Trainee exam.

Subject Recommended Books
ITI Trades (Technical) Youth Competition Times (YCT) ITI Solved Papers or Arihant Technical Guides.
Accounts Assistant Taxmann's Basic Financial Accounting or Graduation Textbooks.
Himachal Pradesh GK "The Wonderland Himachal Pradesh" by Jag Mohan Balokhra.
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal and Lucent's General Knowledge.
Computer Literacy Objective Computer Awareness by Arihant Publications.

Application Fee and Payment Process

The application fee is non-refundable and must be paid online. Candidates from reserved categories are provided significant concessions.

  • General, OBC, and EWS Candidates: ₹200 + GST (Total approx ₹236).
  • SC / ST / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen: No Fee (Exempted).

Payment Mode: Fees can be paid using Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI through the integrated payment gateway on the SJVN career portal. Ensure that you receive a payment success message before exiting the portal.

How to Apply Online

The registration process is entirely digital. Follow these steps to ensure a smooth application experience.

  1. Visit the official website of SJVN at www.sjvn.nic.in.
  2. Go to the "Careers" section and find the link for Advt. No. 124/2025.
  3. Register as a new user by providing your active Email ID and Mobile Number.
  4. Login with your credentials and fill in the personal, academic, and domicile details.
  5. Upload clear scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and certificates (Bonafide Himachali, 10th/12th Marksheets, Degree/Diploma).
  6. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
  7. Preview the application form for any errors before final submission.
  8. Download the submitted application form and keep a printout for the Document Verification stage.

Documents Needed to Apply

Scanning documents in the correct format and size is mandatory. Ensure your scans are legible to avoid rejection during the initial screening.

  1. Bonafide Himachali Certificate: Mandatory for all candidates to prove residency.
  2. Educational Certificates: 10th Standard (for DOB), 12th Standard, ITI Certificate, or Degree Certificate.
  3. Caste Certificate: For SC/ST/OBC-NCL candidates seeking reservation.
  4. Disability Certificate: For PwBD candidates.
  5. Experience Certificate: Required specifically for the Driver post.
  6. Photograph & Signature: Scanned in JPEG/JPG format as per the size limits mentioned in the portal.

Important Links

Resource Name URL Link
Official SJVN Website www.sjvn.nic.in
SJVN Career Page View Vacancies
Online Application Portal Apply Online Here

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can candidates from outside Himachal Pradesh apply for these posts?

No. This specific recruitment (Advt 124/2025) is reserved exclusively for candidates who possess a Bonafide Himachali Domicile certificate. Candidates from other states are not eligible to apply for these Workman Trainee positions.

Q2: Is there any negative marking in the SJVN CBT exam?

One of the best features of this exam is that there is no negative marking for incorrect answers. Candidates should ideally attempt all 100 questions to maximize their scoring potential.

Q3: What is the training duration and stipend?

The training duration is one year. During this period, trainees receive a fixed stipend of ₹21,500 per month. After one year, they are regularized into a permanent pay scale with all company benefits.

Q4: What are the qualifying marks for the written test?

General, EWS, and OBC candidates must score at least 50% marks in the CBT, while SC, ST, and PwBD candidates need to score at least 40% to be considered for the merit list.

Q5: Can I apply for more than one trade?

Generally, the exam for all trades is held on the same day in different shifts or simultaneously. Candidates are advised to apply for the one trade in which they have the most expertise and valid qualification.

Q6: What happens if I miss the typing test for the Assistant post?

The typing test is a mandatory qualifying stage for the Assistant (General) and Assistant (Accounts) roles. If you fail to appear or fail to meet the required speed, you will be disqualified regardless of your CBT score.

entry.title: BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 for 1275 Sub-Inspector Posts
entry.content:
BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 for 1275 Sub-Inspector Posts

Advertisement No. 03/2026: The Bihar Police Sub-ordinate Services Commission (BPSSC) has announced a major recruitment drive for 1275 posts of Police Sub-Inspector (SI) or 'Daroga' in the Home (Police) Department of the Government of Bihar. This is a highly anticipated opportunity for graduates to secure a prestigious Level-6 position in the Bihar Police. The online application process will commence on February 05, 2026, and the last date for submission is March 05, 2026.

Post Name: Police Sub-Inspector (SI)
Last Date: 05 March 2026
Total Posts: 1275

The Bihar Police Sub-ordinate Services Commission (BPSSC) has released its official notification inviting online applications from eligible Indian citizens for direct recruitment to the post of Police Sub-Inspector. This recruitment aims to fill a massive 1275 vacancies, providing a significant boost to the state's law enforcement and offering a golden career opportunity for the youth. The role of a Sub-Inspector is a respected and challenging position that forms the backbone of the police force's field operations.

The selection process for this coveted post is a rigorous multi-stage procedure designed to select the most mentally sharp and physically fit candidates. It involves a Preliminary Written Test, a Main Written Test, a Physical Efficiency Test (PET), and finally, a medical examination and character verification. Candidates must be graduates from any recognized university to be eligible. The application process is entirely online through the official BPSSC website. With a competitive pay scale and the pride of wearing the uniform, this recruitment is expected to attract a large number of applicants.

BPSSC SI Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details to ensure a successful application. All applications must be submitted online within the stipulated timeframe.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Bihar Police Sub-ordinate Services Commission (BPSSC)
Advertisement Number 03/2026
Post Name Police Sub-Inspector (SI) / 'Daroga'
Total Vacancies 1275
Application Start Date 05 February 2026
Application Last Date 05 March 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Bihar
Official Website bpssc.bihar.gov.in

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 1275 vacancies for the post of Police Sub-Inspector. The category-wise reservation details are provided in the official notification, including reservations for SC, ST, EBC, BC, Backward Class Women, EWS, and Transgender candidates.

Post Name Pay Level Total Vacancies
Police Sub-Inspector (SI) Level-6 (₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400) 1275
Note: 35% of the total vacancies are horizontally reserved for women across all categories.

Application Fee

  • For General, EBC, BC, EWS candidates (Male/Female) and candidates from other states: ₹700/-
  • For SC, ST, Female candidates of Bihar Domicile, and Transgender candidates: ₹400/-

The fee must be paid online through Net Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card. The application will be considered complete only after successful payment of the fee.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 01.01.2026)

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions regarding nationality, education, and age to be considered for the post.

Criteria Details
Nationality & Domicile Must be an Indian Citizen. Candidates from other states can apply but will be considered under the General category.
Educational Qualification Must have passed a Bachelor's Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a recognized university or an equivalent examination recognized by the State Government.
Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026)
  • Minimum Age: 20 Years (for all categories)
  • Maximum Age (General - Male): 37 Years
  • Maximum Age (General - Female, BC/EBC - Male/Female): 40 Years
  • Maximum Age (SC/ST - Male/Female/Transgender): 42 Years

Age relaxation for Ex-servicemen is applicable as per government rules.

Selection Process

The selection for the post of Police Sub-Inspector is a comprehensive and multi-stage process:

  1. Stage 1: Preliminary Written Examination (Qualifying): An OMR-based objective test will be conducted. Candidates numbering 20 times the total vacancies will be shortlisted for the next stage based on their performance.
  2. Stage 2: Main Written Examination: Shortlisted candidates will appear for the Main exam, which consists of two papers. The marks of this stage will be used for preparing the merit list for the physical test.
  3. Stage 3: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) & Physical Standard Test (PST): Candidates shortlisted from the Mains (6 times the vacancies) will undergo physical tests. These tests are mandatory and qualifying in nature.
  4. Stage 4: Document Verification & Final Merit List: Candidates who qualify in the PET will be called for document verification. The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the marks obtained in the Main Written Examination.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The written examination is divided into two parts: Prelims and Mains.

1. Preliminary Examination

  • Mode: OMR-based, Objective (MCQ)
  • Total Questions: 100
  • Total Marks: 200 (2 marks per question)
  • Duration: 2 Hours
  • Syllabus: General Knowledge and Current Affairs.
  • Negative Marking: 0.2 marks will be deducted for each wrong answer.
  • Qualifying Mark: Minimum 30% is required to be considered for shortlisting.

2. Main Examination

This exam consists of two papers, conducted in two shifts on the same day.

Paper-I: General Hindi (Qualifying)

  • Total Questions: 100 | Total Marks: 200 | Duration: 2 Hours
  • Syllabus: Hindi Grammar, Comprehension, etc.
  • Negative Marking: 0.2 marks for each wrong answer.
  • Qualifying Mark: A minimum of 30% is mandatory. Marks from this paper will not be added to the merit list.

Paper-II: General Studies

  • Total Questions: 100 | Total Marks: 200 | Duration: 2 Hours
  • Syllabus: General Science, Civics, Indian History, Indian Geography, Mathematics, and Mental Ability Test.
  • Negative Marking: 0.2 marks for each wrong answer.
  • Merit Basis: The final selection will be based on the marks obtained in this paper.

Physical Standard Test (PST) & Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

PST and PET are mandatory and qualifying. Failure in any event will lead to disqualification.

A. Physical Standards Test (PST)

CategoryMinimum HeightChest (Male only)
Unreserved (UR), BC, EWS Male165 cm81cm (Unexpanded), 86cm (Expanded)
EBC, SC, ST Male160 cm79cm (Unexpanded), 84cm (Expanded)
All Category Female155 cmN/A

B. Physical Efficiency Test (PET - Qualifying)

GenderEventQualifying Standard
Male1.6 KM (1600 meters) RunTo be completed within 6 minutes 30 seconds
High JumpMinimum 4 feet
Long JumpMinimum 12 feet
Female1 KM RunTo be completed within 6 minutes
High JumpMinimum 3 feet
Long JumpMinimum 9 feet

How to Prepare for the Exam

A dedicated and balanced preparation strategy is essential for success.

  • For Written Exams: Cover the entire syllabus for GS, focusing on Indian History, Polity, and Geography. For current affairs, read newspapers and monthly magazines regularly. Practice mathematics and reasoning questions from standard competitive exam books. Do not neglect the qualifying Hindi paper.
  • For Physical Tests: Physical conditioning is as important as academic preparation. Start running, jumping, and exercising daily. Monitor your progress and work consistently to meet the qualifying standards well before the test date.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for comprehensive preparation.

  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and "Manorama Year Book".
  • Current Affairs: A national newspaper like The Hindu or The Indian Express, and a monthly magazine like Pratiyogita Darpan.
  • Quantitative Aptitude & Reasoning: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" and "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Hindi: "Samanya Hindi" by Lucent Publications.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the BPSSC website.

  1. Visit the official BPSSC website: bpssc.bihar.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, find the link for "Advertisement No. 03/2026 for the post of Police Sub-Inspector" and click on it.
  3. Read the detailed advertisement carefully and proceed to the application link.
  4. Complete the registration process by providing your basic details.
  5. Log in with your registration credentials and fill out the detailed application form with your personal, educational, and communication details.
  6. Upload a recent, good-quality photograph and your signature in both Hindi and English as per the specified dimensions.
  7. Pay the application fee online.
  8. Review your application form carefully before final submission. Download and take a printout of the submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should keep the following information and scanned documents ready before applying:

  1. Valid Mobile Number and Email ID.
  2. Graduation Degree Mark Sheet and Certificate.
  3. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).
  4. Domicile Certificate (for Bihar residents claiming reservation).
  5. Caste Certificate / EWS Certificate / NCL Certificate for reservation benefits.
  6. Recent passport-size photograph (not older than 2 months, with a white background).
  7. Scanned copy of signature in Hindi and English.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Link to be active from 05.02.2026)
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the last date to apply for the Bihar Police SI recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is March 05, 2026.

Q2: Is there negative marking in the exams?

Yes, there is a negative marking of 0.2 marks for every incorrect answer in both the Preliminary and the Main written examinations.

Q3: Are the marks of the Physical Efficiency Test (PET) added to the final merit list?

No, the PET (running, high jump, long jump) is mandatory but only qualifying in nature. The final merit list is prepared based only on the marks obtained in the Main Written Examination (Paper-II).

Q4: Can a candidate from another state apply for these posts?

Yes, candidates from other states are eligible to apply. However, they will be considered under the General (Unreserved) category and will not be eligible for any age relaxation or reservation benefits.

entry.title: THDC India Limited Apprentice Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
THDC India Limited Apprentice Recruitment 2026

THDC India Limited, a Mini Ratna PSU, invites applications for 70 Trade, Technician (Diploma), and Graduate Apprentice positions for one-year training at its Rishikesh location. This is a golden opportunity for fresh ITI, Diploma, and B.Tech/B.E./BBA pass-outs. The application process is offline and the last date to submit applications is January 31, 2026.

Post Name: Trade Apprentice, Technician (Diploma) Apprentice, Graduate Apprentice
Last Date: 31 January 2026
Total Posts: 70

THDC India Limited, a prestigious Schedule 'A' Mini Ratna Public Sector Undertaking (PSU), has announced a significant training opportunity for young and dynamic individuals. The company has released two separate notifications (ITI Trade Apprentices-01/2026 and Engineering Apprenticeship Training 01/2026) inviting applications for a one-year apprenticeship training program under the Apprentices Act, 1961. This recruitment drive aims to fill a total of 70 vacancies for ITI Trade Apprentices, Technician (Diploma) Apprentices, and Graduate Apprentices at its Rishikesh establishment.

This program is designed to provide hands-on training and practical exposure to freshly qualified candidates, enhancing their skills and employability. The selection process is purely merit-based, with no written examination or interview, making it a highly accessible opportunity. Candidates who have completed their ITI, Diploma, B.E./B.Tech, or BBA in 2023 or later are eligible to apply. It is important to note that this is a training program and does not guarantee permanent employment with THDC India Limited. The application process is offline, and interested candidates must send their filled application forms by post before the deadline of January 31, 2026.

THDC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details to ensure a smooth application process. This apprenticeship program is located in Rishikesh, Uttarakhand.

Particulars Details
Organization Name THDC India Limited
Post Names Trade Apprentice, Technician Apprentice, Graduate Apprentice
Total Vacancies 70
Application Start Date 29th December 2025
Last Date to Submit Application 31st January 2026
Mode of Application Offline (By Post)
Job Type Apprenticeship Training (1 Year)
Job Location Rishikesh, Uttarakhand
Official Website www.thdc.co.in

Vacancy Details

The 70 apprenticeship slots are distributed between two main categories: ITI Trade Apprentices and Graduate/Technician Apprentices. The detailed breakdown is as follows:

ITI Trade Apprentices (Total: 30 Seats)

Trade Name Number of Seats
Computer Operator and Programming Assistant (COPA) 08
Stenographer/Secretarial Assistant 08
Draughtsman (Civil) 04
Fitter 04
Electrician 04
Electronics Mechanic 02
Total ITI Seats 30

Graduate & Technician (Diploma) Apprentices (Total: 40 Seats)

Branch/Discipline Graduate Apprentice Seats Technician (Diploma) Apprentice Seats
Civil Engineering 05 06
Mechanical Engineering 04 04
Electrical Engineering 05 06
Computer Science & IT Engineering 03 02
Bachelor of Business Administration (BBA) 01 00
Electronics / Electronics & Communication Engineering 02 02
Total Seats 20 20
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may be altered at the discretion of THDC management. Reservation policies for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwD candidates will be applied as per government norms.

Application Fee

  • The official notifications for both ITI and Graduate/Diploma apprenticeships do not mention any application fee.
  • Candidates are not required to pay any fee to apply for this apprenticeship program.

This makes the opportunity accessible to all eligible candidates without any financial burden for the application process.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must satisfy all eligibility conditions related to age, educational qualifications, and domicile. The cut-off date for calculating age and other criteria is January 31, 2026.

Post Category Eligibility Details
ITI Trade Apprentice Age Limit: Minimum 18 years and Maximum 30 years.
  • Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and 10 years for PwD candidates.
Educational Qualification:
  • Must have passed 10th standard.
  • Must have passed ITI (Regular Course) in the relevant trade from a recognized institute in the year 2023 or later.
  • Minimum Percentage: 60% for General/EWS/OBC candidates and 55% for SC/ST candidates in ITI.
Domicile: Must be a permanent resident (domicile) of Uttarakhand.
Graduate Apprentice Age Limit: Minimum 18 years and Maximum 30 years.
  • Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and 10 years for PwD candidates.
Educational Qualification:
  • Must have a B.E./B.Tech./BBA degree (Regular Course) in the respective branch from a recognized university/institute in the year 2023 or later.
  • Minimum Percentage: 60% for General/EWS/OBC candidates and 55% for SC/ST candidates in their degree.
Domicile: Candidates from Uttarakhand will be given preference.
Technician (Diploma) Apprentice Age Limit: Minimum 18 years and Maximum 30 years.
  • Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and 10 years for PwD candidates.
Educational Qualification:
  • Must have a Diploma in Engineering (Regular Course) in the respective branch from a recognized institute in the year 2023 or later.
  • Minimum Percentage: 60% for General/EWS/OBC candidates and 55% for SC/ST candidates in their diploma.
Domicile: Candidates from Uttarakhand will be given preference.

Important Note for All Candidates: Candidates who have already undergone or are currently undergoing apprenticeship training elsewhere are not eligible. Similarly, candidates with one year or more of work experience post-qualification are also not eligible.

Selection Process

The selection process for the THDC Apprentice Training is straightforward and based entirely on merit. There will be no written test or interview.

  1. No Interview/Written Test: The notification explicitly states that no interview will be conducted for the selection of apprentices.
  2. Merit-Based Shortlisting: Candidates will be shortlisted purely on the basis of marks obtained in their qualifying examination (i.e., ITI, Diploma, or B.E./B.Tech/BBA). A merit list will be prepared for each trade/discipline.
  3. Tie-Breaker Rule: In case two or more candidates have the same marks in the merit list, the candidate older in age will be given preference.
  4. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates will be called for document verification. They must produce all original documents to validate the information provided in the application form.
  5. Medical Fitness Test: Finally, selected candidates must submit a medical fitness certificate from a Gazetted Medical Officer to be eligible for joining the training.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

As the selection for the THDC Apprentice program is purely merit-based on the marks obtained in the qualifying academic course (ITI/Diploma/Degree), there is no written examination. Therefore, no exam pattern or syllabus is applicable for this recruitment process. Candidates' academic performance is the sole criterion for selection.

How to Prepare for the Selection

Since there is no examination, "preparation" for this recruitment involves ensuring your application is complete, accurate, and submitted on time. Here's what you should focus on:

  1. Portal Registration: This is the most crucial first step. Ensure you are registered on the correct government portal. Your application will be rejected without a valid registration number.
  2. Document Readiness: Gather all your original documents and their self-attested photocopies. Make sure your caste/EWS certificate is valid for the specified financial year (2024-25 for EWS).
  3. Accurate Form Filling: Download the correct application form from the THDC website and fill it out neatly and accurately. Double-check all details, especially your marks, percentages, and personal information.
  4. Percentage Calculation: If your mark sheet is in CGPA/Grades, you must convert it to a percentage as per your university's/board's official conversion formula and attach proof of the same.
  5. Timely Dispatch: Send your application form well before the last date (January 31, 2026) via Speed Post or Registered Post to avoid any postal delays.

How to Apply

The application process is a two-step offline procedure. It is mandatory to complete both steps.

Step 1: Mandatory Portal Registration

Before filling the THDC application form, candidates must register on the relevant Government of India apprenticeship portal. This is a mandatory prerequisite.

  • For ITI Trade Apprentices: Register on the Apprenticeship India Portal at www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in.
  • For Graduate & Technician (Diploma) Apprentices: Register on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) Portal at https://nats.education.gov.in.

After successful registration, note down your unique registration/enrolment number. This number must be mentioned in the THDC application form.

Step 2: Submitting the Offline Application to THDC

  1. Visit the THDC India Limited website's career section at www.thdc.co.in.
  2. Download the appropriate application form for your category (ITI or Graduate/Diploma).
  3. Fill out the application form completely and accurately. Affix a recent passport-size photograph.
  4. Gather self-attested photocopies of all the documents mentioned in the "Documents Needed" section below.
  5. Place the filled application form and all document photocopies in an envelope.
  6. Superscribe the envelope with "Application for ITI Trade Apprentice - 2026" or "Application for Graduate/Technician Apprentice - 2026" as applicable.
  7. Send the envelope via Ordinary Post or Speed Post to the following address, ensuring it reaches before 31.01.2026:

    Manager (Human Resource-Establishment)
    THDC India Limited,
    Bhagirathi Bhawan, Pragatipuram, Bye-Pass Road,
    Rishikesh - 249201, Uttarakhand

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must attach self-attested copies of the following documents with their application form:

  1. Proof of registration on the NATS/Apprenticeship India portal (printout of profile).
  2. SSC/Matriculation (10th) certificate and mark sheet.
  3. ITI/Diploma/B.E./B.Tech/BBA final mark sheets for all semesters/years.
  4. ITI/Diploma/B.E./B.Tech/BBA final pass certificate.
  5. CGPA/Grade to Percentage conversion certificate from your institute/university (if applicable).
  6. Date of Birth proof (10th certificate usually serves this purpose).
  7. Character Certificate.
  8. Domicile Certificate of Uttarakhand (especially for ITI applicants and for preference for others).
  9. Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL) in the prescribed format.
  10. EWS Certificate for the financial year 2024-25.
  11. Physical Disability (PwD) certificate, if applicable.
  12. Certificate for Project Affected Family, if applicable.
  13. Aadhar Card (Mandatory).

Important Links

Link Description URL
Official Notification Graduate/Diploma | Trade/ITI
NATS Portal (For Graduates/Diploma) Click Here
Apprenticeship India Portal (For ITI) Click Here

FAQs

Q1: Is this a permanent government job?

No, this is a one-year apprenticeship training program. THDC will not provide any employment after the completion of the training.

Q2: Is there any written test or interview?

No, the selection is purely based on the merit of marks obtained in the qualifying ITI/Diploma/Degree examination.

Q3: I completed my B.Tech in 2022. Can I apply?

No, only candidates who have passed their qualifying examination in the year 2023 or later are eligible to apply.

Q4: I am from a state other than Uttarakhand. Can I apply for the ITI Apprentice post?

No, the notification for ITI Trade Apprentices clearly states that only candidates with a domicile of Uttarakhand are eligible.

Q5: What is the stipend for apprentices?

The stipend will be paid per month as per the provisions of the Apprenticeship Act, 1961 and its subsequent amendments.

Barnala District Court Recruitment 2026 for Clerk & Stenographer

The Office of the District & Sessions Judge, Barnala, has invited applications for the recruitment of 13 posts of Clerk and Stenographer Grade-III on an ad-hoc basis. Eligible candidates with a graduation degree can apply offline. The last date for the receipt of applications is February 15, 2026.

Post Name: Clerk, Stenographer Grade-III
Last Date: 15 February 2026
Total Posts: 13

The District & Sessions Judge, Barnala, has released a public notice for the recruitment of staff to fill vacancies on an ad-hoc basis. The recruitment drive aims to fill 11 posts of Clerk and 2 posts of Stenographer Grade-III. These positions are temporary, initially for a period of six months or until regular appointments are made by the Hon'ble High Court of Punjab and Haryana, whichever is earlier.

This is a valuable opportunity for graduates who possess proficiency in computer operations and Punjabi language. The selection process differs for both posts: Clerks will be selected through a written examination followed by a computer proficiency test, while Stenographers will undergo a shorthand/typing test and a computer proficiency test. Interested candidates must submit their applications in the prescribed proforma along with necessary documents by post or by hand to the office of the District & Sessions Judge, Barnala.

Barnala Court Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. The application process is strictly offline.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Office of the District & Sessions Judge, Barnala
Post Names Clerk & Stenographer Grade-III
Total Vacancies 13
Application Last Date 15 February 2026 (up to 5:00 PM)
Application Mode Offline (Post or By Hand)
Job Type Ad-hoc Basis (Government)
Job Location Barnala, Punjab
Official Website districts.ecourts.gov.in/barnala

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment is for 13 posts on a consolidated salary basis.

Post Name Total Vacancies Category Breakup Pay Scale
Clerk (Ad-hoc) 11 General-06, SC-02, BC-02, PH-01 Consolidated salary as per Punjab Govt. letter No. 7/204/2012-4F.P.1/66 dated 15.01.2015 and instructions issued from time to time.
Stenographer Grade-III (Ad-hoc) 02 General-02
Total 13
Note: The number of vacancies may increase or decrease due to the creation or withdrawal of courts or other administrative reasons.

Application Fee

  • There is NO application fee mentioned in the official notification. Candidates need not pay any fee to apply.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 01.01.2026)

Candidates must fulfill the specific age and educational qualifications for the post they are applying for.

Criteria Details
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 37 Years
(Age relaxation for reserved categories is applicable as per the instructions of the Hon'ble High Court and Punjab Government).
Educational Qualification (Clerk)
  1. Degree of Bachelor of Arts or Bachelor of Science or equivalent thereto from a recognized University.
  2. Must have passed Matriculation with Punjabi as one of the subjects.
  3. Proficiency in the operation of computers.
Educational Qualification (Stenographer Gr-III)
  1. Degree of Bachelor of Arts or Bachelor of Science or equivalent thereto from a recognized University.
  2. Must have passed Matriculation with Punjabi as one of the subjects.
  3. Proficiency in the operation of computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets).

Selection Process

The selection process varies by post. Candidates must qualify in all stages to be considered for appointment.

For Clerk:

  1. Written Test: Candidates will have to appear for a written test in English Composition and General Knowledge. To qualify for the next stage, a candidate must secure at least 33% marks in each subject and 40% marks in aggregate.
  2. Computer Proficiency Test (CPT): Candidates who qualify the written test will be called for a practical CPT to check their proficiency in computer operations.
  3. Final Merit: Based on the performance in the written test.

For Stenographer Grade-III:

  1. Shorthand/Type Test: Candidates will have to pass a test in English Shorthand at a speed of 80 w.p.m. and transcribe it at a speed of 20 w.p.m..
  2. Computer Proficiency Test: Candidates must also have proficiency in the operation of computers (Word Processing and Spread Sheets).
  3. Final Merit: Based on the performance in the shorthand/type test.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

For Clerk (Written Exam):

  • English Composition: 50 Marks (Subjective/Objective questions testing grammar, vocabulary, comprehension, etc.)
  • General Knowledge: 50 Marks (Objective questions on current affairs, history, geography, etc.)
  • Qualifying Marks: 33% in each subject individually and 40% in aggregate of both subjects.

For Stenographer Grade-III (Skill Test):

  • Dictation Speed: 80 words per minute in English.
  • Transcription Speed: 20 words per minute on a computer.
  • Computer Proficiency: Practical test on Word Processing and Spreadsheets.

How to Prepare

A specific preparation strategy is needed depending on the post.

  • For Clerk: Focus on improving your English grammar and writing skills for the composition paper. Stay updated with current affairs and static GK for the General Knowledge section. Practice basic computer operations.
  • For Stenographer: Daily practice of shorthand dictation and transcription is essential. Aim for a speed higher than 80 w.p.m. to ensure accuracy during the test. Practice typing on a computer to meet the transcription speed.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard books for their preparation.

  • English: "High School English Grammar and Composition" by Wren & Martin.
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and a monthly current affairs magazine.
  • Stenography: Pitman Shorthand Instructor and Key.

How to Apply

The application process is offline. Follow these steps:

  1. Download the application proforma from the official website: https://districts.ecourts.gov.in/barnala.
  2. Fill out the application form with all the required details (Name, Father's Name, Date of Birth, Qualification, Category, etc.) in capital letters.
  3. Paste a recent passport-size photograph on the form.
  4. Attach self-attested copies of all relevant testimonials/certificates.
  5. Place the application form and documents in an envelope.
  6. Send the application by post or submit it by hand to the office of the District & Sessions Judge, Barnala.
  7. Ensure it reaches on or before 15 February 2026 (5:00 PM).

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must attach self-attested copies of the following documents with their application:

  1. Proof of Date of Birth.
  2. Certificates of Educational Qualification (Graduation Degree, Matriculation with Punjabi, etc.).
  3. Category Certificate (SC/BC/PH, etc.), if applicable.
  4. Two recent passport-size photographs (one pasted on the form, one loose).
  5. Any other relevant documents.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification & Application Form Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is the job permanent?

No, the appointment is on a purely ad-hoc basis for a period of six months or until regular appointments are made, whichever is earlier.

Q2: What is the date of the written test/interview?

The dates for the written test/interview/skill test are not mentioned in the notification. They will be displayed on the official website (https://districts.ecourts.gov.in/barnala) later. No separate call letters will be issued.

Q3: Can I apply for both posts?

Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria for both. However, you will likely need to submit separate applications for each post.

Q4: Is Punjabi language mandatory?

Yes, passing Matriculation examination with Punjabi as one of the subjects is a mandatory qualification for both posts.

entry.title: Warangal DCCB भर्ती 2025: स्टाफ असिस्टेंट के 21 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!
entry.content:
Warangal DCCB भर्ती 2025: स्टाफ असिस्टेंट के 21 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!

वारंगल जिला सहकारी केंद्रीय बैंक लिमिटेड (Warangal DCCB) ने स्टाफ असिस्टेंट के पदों के लिए ऑनलाइन आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं। केवल तेलंगाना राज्य के स्थानीय उम्मीदवार ही पात्र हैं।

तेलंगाना के युवाओं के लिए बैंकिंग क्षेत्र में करियर शुरू करने का सुनहरा अवसर आया है। वारंगल जिला सहकारी केंद्रीय बैंक लिमिटेड (Warangal DCCB), हनमकोंडा ने “स्टाफ असिस्टेंट” के 21 पदों पर नियुक्ति के लिए भर्ती अधिसूचना जारी की है। यह भर्ती केवल तेलंगाना राज्य के स्थानीय उम्मीदवारों के लिए है। चयन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से ऑनलाइन परीक्षा पर आधारित होगी, जिसका आयोजन IBPS द्वारा किया जाएगा।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ18 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि06 नवम्बर 2025
ऑनलाइन परीक्षा (संभावित)दिसम्बर 2025

रिक्ति एवं वेतनमान

इस भर्ती अभियान के अंतर्गत स्टाफ असिस्टेंट पद हेतु आकर्षक वेतनमान निर्धारित किया गया है।

पद का नामकुल पदवेतनमान
स्टाफ असिस्टेंट21₹24,050 – ₹64,480 (20 स्टेज) + भत्ते
आरक्षण सूचना: कुल 21 पदों में OC, EWS, BC, SC, ST और EXS वर्गों हेतु आरक्षण लागू है। महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए 9 पद आरक्षित हैं।

आवेदन शुल्क

  • General/BC/EWS: ₹1000/- (एप्लीकेशन + सूचना शुल्क)
  • SC/ST/PC/EXSM: ₹500/- (केवल सूचना शुल्क)

शुल्क का भुगतान केवल ऑनलाइन माध्यम से ही किया जा सकेगा।

पात्रता मानदंड (01.10.2025 तक)

मानदंडआवश्यकता
निवासउम्मीदवार तेलंगाना राज्य का स्थानीय निवासी होना चाहिए।
आयु सीमान्यूनतम 18 वर्ष और अधिकतम 30 वर्ष। (02.10.1995 से पहले और 01.10.2007 के बाद जन्मे उम्मीदवार पात्र नहीं होंगे)। आरक्षित वर्गों को नियमानुसार छूट दी जाएगी।
शैक्षणिक योग्यता
  • किसी मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक (Graduate) डिग्री।
  • तेलुगु भाषा ज्ञान: उम्मीदवार ने 10वीं तक तेलुगु विषय के रूप में पढ़ा हो।
  • अंग्रेज़ी भाषा का ज्ञान आवश्यक है।

चयन प्रक्रिया

चयन प्रक्रिया केवल ऑनलाइन परीक्षा के माध्यम से की जाएगी। कोई इंटरव्यू नहीं होगा। परीक्षा पैटर्न नीचे दिया गया है:

विषयप्रश्नअधिकतम अंकसमय
सामान्य/वित्तीय जागरूकता एवं क्रेडिट कोऑपरेटिव्स404020 मिनट
अंग्रेज़ी भाषा404030 मिनट
रीज़निंग एबिलिटी404035 मिनट
न्यूमेरिकल एबिलिटी404035 मिनट
कुल160160120 मिनट
नेगेटिव मार्किंग: प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर के लिए 0.25 अंक काटे जाएंगे। अंतिम चयन ऑनलाइन परीक्षा में प्राप्त अंकों की मेरिट के आधार पर होगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. Warangal DCCB की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://warangaldccb.in पर जाएं।
  2. "Recruitment" या "Careers" सेक्शन में जाकर संबंधित लिंक खोलें।
  3. "Click here for New Registration" पर क्लिक करके अपनी बुनियादी जानकारी भरें।
  4. फॉर्म में आवश्यक जानकारी दर्ज करें और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ (फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, अंगूठा, हस्तलिखित घोषणा) अपलोड करें।
  5. निर्धारित शुल्क का ऑनलाइन भुगतान करें।
  6. अंत में फॉर्म सबमिट करें और उसका प्रिंट निकालकर अपने पास रखें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्र.1: क्या तेलंगाना के बाहर के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, यह भर्ती केवल तेलंगाना राज्य के स्थानीय उम्मीदवारों के लिए है।
प्र.2: चयन प्रक्रिया क्या है? क्या इंटरव्यू होगा?
चयन केवल ऑनलाइन लिखित परीक्षा के आधार पर होगा। इसमें कोई इंटरव्यू नहीं है।
प्र.3: परीक्षा किस भाषा में होगी?
ऑनलाइन परीक्षा केवल अंग्रेज़ी भाषा में आयोजित की जाएगी।
प्र.4: क्या फाइनल ईयर के विद्यार्थी आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, उम्मीदवार के पास 01.10.2025 तक स्नातक की डिग्री होना अनिवार्य है।

entry.title: TNPSC Group VA भर्ती 2025 — Assistant और ASO के 32 पदों पर भर्ती
entry.content:
TNPSC Group VA भर्ती 2025 — Assistant और ASO के 32 पदों पर आवेदन करें

तमिलनाडु के सरकारी कर्मचारियों के लिए स्थानांतरण द्वारा भर्ती का सुनहरा अवसर।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission (TNPSC) ने Combined Civil Services Examination - Group VA Services के लिए अधिसूचना संख्या 16/2025 जारी की है। यह भर्ती केवल by transfer है, अर्थात यह उन्हीं उम्मीदवारों के लिए है जो पहले से Tamil Nadu Ministerial Service या Tamil Nadu Judicial Ministerial Service में Junior Assistant या Assistant के पद पर कार्यरत हैं।

इस भर्ती प्रक्रिया के अंतर्गत Assistant Section Officer और Assistant के कुल 32 पद भरे जाएंगे। यह सामान्य जनता के लिए खुली भर्ती नहीं है। नीचे इस भर्ती की सभी महत्वपूर्ण जानकारियाँ दी गई हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि / समय
अधिसूचना जारी होने की तिथि07 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि05 नवम्बर 2025 (रात 11:59 बजे तक)
आवेदन सुधार अवधि10 नवम्बर 2025 से 12 नवम्बर 2025 तक
लिखित परीक्षा की तिथि21 दिसम्बर 2025

पद और रिक्तियाँ

पद का नामविभागकुल पदवेतन स्तर
Assistant Section OfficerSecretariat (Law और Finance को छोड़कर)22Level 16 (CPS)
Assistant Section OfficerFinance3Level 16 (CPS)
AssistantSecretariat (Law और Finance को छोड़कर)5Level 9 (CPS)
AssistantFinance2Level 9 (CPS)
कुल योग32

पात्रता की शर्तें

आयु सीमा (01.07.2025 तक):
  • Assistant Section Officer: Others, BC(OBCM), BCM, MBC/DC - अधिकतम 35 वर्ष। SC, SC(A), ST - अधिकतम 40 वर्ष।
  • Assistant: Others, BC(OBCM), BCM, MBC/DC - अधिकतम 30 वर्ष। SC, SC(A), ST - अधिकतम 35 वर्ष।

शैक्षणिक एवं सेवा योग्यता (01.08.2025 तक)

पदयोग्यता
Assistant Section Officer (Secretariat)(i) स्नातक डिग्री।
(ii) Junior Assistant/Assistant के रूप में कम से कम 5 वर्ष का Drafting अनुभव।
Assistant Section Officer (Finance)(i) Commerce, Economics या Statistics में स्नातक डिग्री।
(ii) Assistant के रूप में न्यूनतम 5 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Assistant (Secretariat)(i) स्नातक डिग्री।
(ii) Junior Assistant/Assistant के रूप में 3 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Assistant (Finance)(i) Commerce, Economics या Statistics में स्नातक डिग्री।
(ii) 3 वर्ष का अनुभव आवश्यक।

इसके अतिरिक्त उम्मीदवारों को Tamil भाषा का पर्याप्त ज्ञान होना आवश्यक है।

परीक्षा योजना

लिखित परीक्षा वर्णनात्मक (descriptive) प्रकार की होगी। इसमें दो पेपर होंगे — दोनों अनिवार्य हैं।

पेपरविषयअधिकतम अंकसमय
Paper IGeneral Tamil1003 घंटे
Paper IIGeneral English1003 घंटे
कुल200-
न्यूनतम योग्यता अंक60
यह परीक्षा केवल Chennai (Centre Code: 0101) में आयोजित की जाएगी।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. One Time Registration (OTR): उम्मीदवारों को TNPSC वेबसाइट पर पंजीकरण करना होगा।
  2. Online Application: पंजीकरण के बाद www.tnpscexams.in पर लॉगिन कर Group VA परीक्षा के लिए आवेदन भरें।
  3. परीक्षा शुल्क: ₹100/- का भुगतान ऑनलाइन करें।
  4. Correction Window: 10 से 12 नवम्बर 2025 तक संशोधन किया जा सकता है।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें
अधिसूचना PDF'Notifications' सेक्शन देखें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या नए ग्रेजुएट इस भर्ती के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, यह भर्ती केवल उन्हीं के लिए है जो पहले से तमिलनाडु मंत्रालयिक सेवा या न्यायिक मंत्रालयिक सेवा में कार्यरत हैं।
प्रश्न 2: क्या आरक्षण लागू है?
सामाजिक आरक्षण (SC, ST, BC आदि) लागू है, लेकिन PSTM, महिला या PwD के लिए आरक्षण लागू नहीं है।
प्रश्न 3: परीक्षा का स्वरूप क्या होगा?
यह वर्णनात्मक परीक्षा होगी जिसमें दो पेपर — General Tamil और General English शामिल होंगे।
प्रश्न 4: परीक्षा कहाँ आयोजित होगी?
यह परीक्षा केवल चेन्नई में आयोजित की जाएगी।

entry.title: NHAI Recruitment 2025: 84 पदों पर Manager, Accountant, Stenographer की भर्ती
entry.content:
NHAI Recruitment 2025: Manager, Accountant और Stenographer के 84 पदों पर भर्ती

National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) ने 84 पदों पर भर्ती का नोटिफिकेशन जारी किया है। ऑनलाइन आवेदन 30 अक्टूबर 2025 से शुरू होंगे।

National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) जो कि Ministry of Road Transport & Highways के अधीन काम करती है, ने देशभर के योग्य उम्मीदवारों के लिए 84 पदों पर भर्ती की घोषणा की है। यह भर्ती Deputy Manager, Accountant, Stenographer सहित विभिन्न पदों पर की जाएगी।

रिक्तियों और वेतन का विवरण

पद का नामकुल पदअधिकतम आयुवेतनमान (7th CPC)
Deputy Manager (Finance & Accounts)930 वर्षLevel-10 (₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500)
Accountant4230 वर्षLevel-5 (₹29,200 – ₹92,300)
Stenographer3128 वर्षLevel-4 (₹25,500 – ₹81,100)
Library & Information Assistant130 वर्षLevel-6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400)
Junior Translation Officer130 वर्षLevel-6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400)
नोट: रिक्तियों की संख्या बढ़ या घट सकती है। आरक्षण भारत सरकार के नियमों के अनुसार लागू होगा।

शैक्षणिक योग्यता (Educational Qualification)

  • Deputy Manager: MBA (Finance) — Regular Course।
  • Accountant: Bachelor's Degree और Intermediate in CA या CMA।
  • Stenographer: Bachelor's Degree और 80 wpm Shorthand Speed (English/Hindi)।
  • Library Assistant: Bachelor's Degree in Library Science।
  • Junior Translation Officer: Hindi/English में Master's Degree।
महत्वपूर्ण: Final Year के छात्र आवेदन के पात्र नहीं हैं। आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि तक योग्यता होना आवश्यक है।

चयन प्रक्रिया (Selection Process)

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT) — Hindi और English दोनों भाषाओं में।
  2. Skill Test — केवल Stenographer पद के लिए।
  3. Interview — केवल Deputy Manager पद के लिए।
  4. Document Verification — अंतिम चरण में।

हर गलत उत्तर पर 0.25 अंक की नकारात्मक मार्किंग होगी।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया और शुल्क

  1. NHAI की वेबसाइट https://nhai.gov.in पर “Vacancies” सेक्शन में जाएँ।
  2. ऑनलाइन आवेदन पत्र भरें और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें।
  3. शुल्क का भुगतान करें:
    • UR/OBC/EWS: ₹500/-
    • SC/ST/PwBD: कोई शुल्क नहीं

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक (Important Links)

लिंककार्यवाही
Official Websiteयहाँ क्लिक करें
Online Applyयहाँ आवेदन करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 15 दिसंबर 2025 (शाम 6:00 बजे तक) है।

Q2: क्या एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

हाँ, लेकिन प्रत्येक पद के लिए अलग आवेदन और शुल्क देना होगा।

Q3: परीक्षा केंद्र कहाँ होंगे?

CBT देशभर के प्रमुख शहरों में आयोजित होगा।

Q4: क्या Service Bond देना होगा?

हाँ, Group ‘A’ पदों के लिए ₹5 लाख और Group ‘B’ व ‘C’ पदों के लिए ₹3 लाख का सर्विस बॉन्ड भरना होगा।

entry.title: PUCB Recruitment 2026 for 19 Managerial and Clerical Posts
entry.content:
PUCB Recruitment 2026 for 19 Managerial and Clerical Posts

The Panipat Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd. (PUCB), a multi-state bank, has announced a recruitment drive for 19 vacancies across various managerial and clerical positions. Experienced banking professionals are invited to apply for roles including Senior Accountant/Branch Manager, Junior Accountant/Assistant Branch Manager, Senior Clerk, and Clerk-cum-Cashier/Field Supervisor. The application process is completely offline, starting from January 21, 2026, with the last date for submission being February 05, 2026.

Post Name: Senior Accountant/Branch Manager, Junior Accountant/Assistant Branch Manager, Senior Clerk, Clerk-cum-Cashier/Field Supervisor
Last Date: 05 February 2026
Total Posts: 19

The Panipat Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd. (PUCB) has opened its doors for seasoned professionals from the banking and NBFC sectors through its latest recruitment notification. This drive aims to fill a total of 19 critical positions that are vital for the bank's operations and expansion. The vacancies span across different levels of the banking hierarchy, offering significant career opportunities for candidates with relevant experience. The available roles are designed for individuals who can contribute to the bank's growth, manage branch operations, handle clerical duties, and supervise field activities.

Unlike most contemporary bank recruitments, PUCB has opted for a traditional offline application process. Candidates are required to download the application form from the official website, fill it out in their own handwriting, and submit it along with their biodata and necessary documents. The selection will be based on the experience and qualifications of the applicants, with a strong preference given to those who can demonstrate their ability to achieve business targets.
