This recruitment is an excellent opportunity for experienced individuals, including retired bankers, to join a growing multi-state co-operative bank.

PUCB Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take note of the crucial dates and other important details to ensure their application is considered. This recruitment is experience-based and requires offline submission.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization The Panipat Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd. (PUCB)
Post Names Senior Accountant/Branch Manager, Junior Accountant/Assistant Branch Manager, Senior Clerk, Clerk-cum-Cashier/Field Supervisor
Total Vacancies 19
Application Start Date 21 January 2026
Last Date for Application Submission 05 February 2026 (up to 05:00 PM)
Application Mode Offline (In-person or Post/Courier)
Job Type Co-operative Bank Job
Job Location Panipat, Haryana (or any branch of the bank)
Official Website pucb.bank.in

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 19 positions across four different posts. The detailed breakdown of vacancies is as follows:

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Senior Accountant / Branch Manager 03
Junior Accountant / Assistant Branch Manager 03
Senior Clerk 03
Clerk-cum-Cashier / Field Supervisor 10
Total 19
Note: Selected candidates may be posted in any branch of the Panipat Urban Co-operative Bank.

Application Fee

  • The official notification released by the Panipat Urban Co-operative Bank does not mention any application fee for this recruitment process.

Candidates are advised to download the application form and submit it as per the given instructions. No fee payment is required unless specified otherwise in the application form itself.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet the specific experience and qualification requirements for the post they wish to apply for. The eligibility criteria are stringent and experience-focused.

Post Name Eligibility Details
Senior Accountant / Branch Manager
  • Qualification: Graduate in any discipline.
  • Experience: Minimum Seven (7) years of experience in the Banking or NBFC sector.
  • OR
  • Must be a retired banker below the age of 62 years.
Junior Accountant / Assistant Branch Manager
  • Qualification: Graduate in any discipline.
  • Experience: Minimum Five (5) years of experience in the Banking or NBFC sector.
Senior Clerk
  • Qualification: Commerce Graduate.
  • Experience: Minimum two (2) years of experience in the Banking or NBFC sector.
  • Preferred: Candidates with an MBA in Finance & Marketing will be given preference.
Clerk-cum-Cashier / Field Supervisor
  • Qualification: Commerce Graduate with a computer training certificate of at least six months.
  • Preferred: Candidates with an MBA in Finance & Marketing will be given preference.

Selection Process

The official notification does not detail a written examination. Therefore, the selection process is expected to be based on the screening of applications followed by a personal interview.

  1. Application Scrutiny and Shortlisting: The bank will first scrutinize all the applications received. Candidates will be shortlisted based on their fulfillment of the eligibility criteria, including years of relevant experience, educational qualifications, and preferred qualifications (like MBA).
  2. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called for a personal interview. The interview will be designed to assess the candidate's domain knowledge, practical experience, problem-solving skills, and suitability for the specific role. For managerial positions, the interview will also focus on leadership qualities, business development strategies, and the ability to achieve targets.
  3. Document Verification: The final selection will be contingent upon the verification of original documents submitted by the candidates. Any discrepancy found can lead to disqualification.

Exam Pattern

The official advertisement for the PUCB recruitment 2026 does not mention any written examination. The selection process is focused on evaluating the practical experience and professional suitability of the candidates through an interview.

Candidates should therefore focus their preparation on the personal interview. The interview is likely to cover the following areas:

  • For Managerial Posts (Branch Manager/Asst. Branch Manager): Core banking operations, credit management, NPA recovery, business development, team leadership, customer relationship management, and RBI regulations.
  • For Clerical Posts (Senior Clerk/Clerk-cum-Cashier): General banking procedures, accounting principles (for Commerce Graduates), cash handling protocols, customer service skills, and knowledge of banking products.
  • For Field Supervisor: Knowledge of loan products, field verification processes, recovery strategies, and local area knowledge.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of the posts in this recruitment drive. The selection is based purely on professional qualifications and experience.

How to Prepare for the Selection

Since the selection is interview-centric, preparation should be tailored accordingly. Here’s a strategic approach:

  • Update Your Biodata/Resume: Create a comprehensive and professional biodata that clearly highlights your educational background, years and nature of experience, key responsibilities, and major achievements in your previous roles. Quantify your achievements where possible (e.g., "grew the loan book by X%" or "reduced NPAs by Y%").
  • Brush Up on Banking Knowledge: Revise core banking concepts, recent RBI circulars, and trends in the co-operative banking sector. Be prepared for technical questions related to your field (accounting, credit, operations).
  • Research PUCB: Learn about the Panipat Urban Co-operative Bank – its history, management, product portfolio, financial performance, and branch network. Understanding the bank's vision will help you align your answers during the interview.
  • Prepare for Situational Questions: Be ready to answer scenario-based questions. For instance, "How would you handle a difficult customer?" or "What strategy would you implement to increase the branch's business?" Use the STAR (Situation, Task, Action, Result) method to structure your answers.
  • Focus on Target Achievement: The advertisement explicitly states a preference for candidates who can achieve targets. Prepare specific examples from your past experience where you successfully met or exceeded targets.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no written exam, the focus should be on reference material for the interview.

  • "Banking Awareness" by Arihant Experts: For a quick revision of general banking concepts.
  • Publications from the Indian Institute of Banking and Finance (IIBF): Books on CAIIB/JAIIB subjects are excellent for in-depth technical knowledge.
  • "The Victory Project: Six Steps to Peak Potential" by Saurabh Mukherjea and Anupam Gupta: For managerial insights and strategic thinking.
  • Business Newspapers: Reading The Economic Times or Business Standard daily will keep you updated on the latest financial news and regulations.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely offline. Candidates must follow these steps meticulously:

  1. Visit the official PUCB website at pucb.bank.in.
  2. Navigate to the "Career" section from the top menu.
  3. Find the recruitment advertisement and click on the "Application Form" link to download the prescribed form.
  4. Print the application form and fill it out neatly and legibly in your own handwriting.
  5. Prepare a complete application package which includes: the filled-in application form, a detailed biodata, self-attested photocopies of all required certificates (educational, experience, computer training, etc.), and a latest passport-size coloured photograph affixed to the form.
  6. Place all documents in an envelope.
  7. Submit the application either in person at the bank's Head Office or send it via post/courier to the address below, ensuring it reaches before the deadline.

Submission Address:
The Managing Director,
The Panipat Urban Co-operative Bank Ltd.,
ॐ City Centre, #932-935/8, GT Road,
Panipat, Haryana - 132103

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must attach self-attested photocopies of the following documents along with their handwritten application form:

  1. Duly filled Application Form in the applicant's own handwriting.
  2. A detailed Biodata or Curriculum Vitae (CV).
  3. Certificates and mark sheets of all educational qualifications (Graduation, Post-Graduation, etc.).
  4. Certificate of six-month computer training (for Clerk-cum-Cashier post).
  5. Experience certificates clearly stating the duration and nature of work in the Banking/NBFC sector.
  6. Latest passport-size coloured photograph.
  7. Copy of ID proof and Address proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Voter ID).

Important Links

Action Link
Official Career Page Click Here
Application Form Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the last date to submit the application for PUCB recruitment?

The last date for the receipt of the offline application is February 05, 2026, up to 05:00 PM.

Q2: Is there a written test for this recruitment?

No, the official notification does not mention any written test. The selection is likely to be based on the shortlisting of applications and a personal interview.

Q3: Can a fresh Commerce Graduate apply for the Senior Clerk post?

No, the Senior Clerk post requires a minimum of two years of experience in the Banking/NBFC sector in addition to a Commerce degree.

Q4: I am a retired banker aged 60. Can I apply for the Branch Manager post?

Yes, retired bankers below the age of 62 are eligible to apply for the Senior Accountant / Branch Manager post.

entry.title: EME Srinagar Recruitment 2025: Apply Offline for Tin & Copper Smith and LDC Posts
entry.content:
EME Srinagar Recruitment 2025: Apply Offline for Tin & Copper Smith and LDC Posts

A golden opportunity for 10th/ITI/12th pass candidates to join the Indian Army's EME Corps as a civilian employee in Srinagar.

Post Name: Tin & Copper Smith (Skilled), Lower Division Clerk (LDC)
Last Date: Within 21 days from the date of publication (Approx. 13/12/2025)
Total Posts: 02

An important employment notification has been issued for direct recruitment to Group 'C' (Defence Civilian) posts by the 7015 EME Battalion, Badami Bagh Cantonment, Srinagar (Jammu & Kashmir), under the Directorate General of the Electronics and Mechanical Engineers (EME) Corps of the Indian Army. This is a golden opportunity for Indian citizens who wish to contribute to nation-building by serving in a stable and respected civilian post with the Indian Armed Forces. Through this recruitment drive, the posts of Tin & Copper Smith (Skilled) and Lower Division Clerk (LDC) will be filled.

This recruitment will be conducted entirely through an offline process, requiring candidates to send their neatly typed or handwritten application in the prescribed format via Ordinary Post only. It is an excellent chance for candidates with technical or clerical skills who wish to be a part of a defense establishment in a strategic location like Jammu and Kashmir. Selected candidates will be offered an attractive salary package as per the 7th Central Pay Commission, along with other government allowances. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all the important details related to this recruitment, ensuring you have all the information needed to apply correctly and prepare effectively.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Recruiting OrganizationCorps of Electronics and Mechanical Engineers (EME), Indian Army
Unit Name7015 EME Battalion, c/o 56 APO
Post NamesTin & Copper Smith (Skilled) and Lower Division Clerk (LDC)
Total Vacancies02
Job LocationBadami Bagh Cantonment, Srinagar, Jammu & Kashmir
Application ModeOffline (Only by Ordinary Post)
Application DatesWithin 21 days from the date of publication in Employment News (Estimated Last Date: December 13, 2025)

Vacancy Details and Reservation

A total of 02 posts will be filled through this recruitment drive. Both posts are for the Unreserved (UR) category, which means that candidates from any category (General, SC, ST, OBC) can apply for these positions based on merit.

Post Name Number of Vacancies Category
Tin & Copper Smith (Skilled) 01 UR (Unreserved)
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) 01 UR (Unreserved)

Salary and Pay Scale

Selected candidates will be placed in Level 2 of the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC) Pay Matrix. This is a stable and attractive salary package that includes Basic Pay along with other allowances like Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Transport Allowance (TA) as applicable from time to time.

Post Name Pay Matrix Level Pay Scale
Tin & Copper Smith (Skilled) and LDC Level 2 ₹ 19,900 - ₹ 63,200/-

The initial in-hand salary, including all allowances, would be approximately ₹28,000 - ₹32,000 per month, depending on the current DA rates and other factors.

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

The minimum required educational qualifications and skills for both posts are detailed below:

Educational Qualification

Post Name Required Qualification
Tin & Copper Smith (Skilled)
  • ITI certificate from a recognized Industrial Training Institute in the respective trade. OR
  • Armed Forces personnel or Ex-Servicemen from the appropriate trade and minimum grade I.
  • Desirable: Certificate from the National Council for Vocational Training (NCVT).
Lower Division Clerk (LDC)
  • 12th class pass from a recognized Board or University.
  • A typing speed of 35 words per minute in English on a computer OR a typing speed of 30 words per minute in Hindi on a computer. (This corresponds to 10500 KDPH / 9000 KDPH).

Age Limit

The age of the candidates will be calculated as of the last date of receipt of the application. The age limit for the General category is 18 to 25 years. Age relaxation for reserved categories will be applicable as per government rules, though these posts are unreserved.

CategoryAge LimitRelaxation Details
Unreserved (UR) 18 - 25 years -
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) After deducting the period of total military service from the actual age, the resultant age should not exceed the prescribed upper age limit by more than 3 years.
Departmental Candidates Up to 40 years of age (with a minimum of 3 years of continuous service).
Widows, Divorced Women Up to 35 years of age.
Note: Since both posts are unreserved (UR), SC/ST/OBC candidates who apply for these posts will not be given any age relaxation and will be treated as General candidates.

Selection Process

The selection process will be completed in multiple stages, primarily involving a written examination and a skill test. The selection of candidates will be based on the following steps:

  1. Shortlisting of Applications: Received applications will be shortlisted based on the percentage of marks obtained in the essential qualification. Only shortlisted candidates will be issued admit cards for the written examination. A higher percentage in 10th/ITI/12th will increase the chances of being called for the exam.
  2. Written Examination: The exam will be offline (OMR-based) and of an objective type. It will consist of 150 questions for 150 marks, with a duration of 2 hours. The question paper will be bilingual (English and Hindi). There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.
  3. Skill Test (Qualifying): Candidates who pass the written examination will be called for a skill test. This test will be qualifying in nature.
    • For LDC: A typing test on a computer to assess speed and accuracy.
    • For Tin & Copper Smith: A practical test in the relevant trade to assess hands-on skills.
  4. Document Verification and Biometrics: Biometric registration and verification of original documents will be conducted during the selection process.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Written Examination Pattern for Tin & Copper Smith (Skilled)

PartSubjectQuestionsMarks
Part-IGeneral Intelligence & Reasoning2525
Part-IIGeneral Awareness2525
Part-IIIGeneral English2525
Part-IVNumerical Aptitude2525
Part-VTrade Specific5050
Total150150

Written Examination Pattern for Lower Division Clerk (LDC)

PartSubjectQuestionsMarks
Part-IGeneral Intelligence & Reasoning2525
Part-IIGeneral Awareness2525
Part-IIIGeneral English5050
Part-IVNumerical Aptitude5050
Total150150

Detailed Syllabus

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: Questions will be of 10th/12th standard and will include topics on analogies, similarities and differences, spatial visualization, problem-solving, analysis, judgment, decision making, visual memory, discrimination, observation, relationship concepts, arithmetical reasoning, and figural classification.
  • General Awareness: Questions will be designed to test the candidate’s general awareness of the environment around them. Topics will include current events, India and its neighboring countries, sports, history, culture, geography, economic scene, general polity including the Indian Constitution, and scientific research.
  • General English: This section will test the candidate’s understanding of the basics of the English language, its vocabulary, grammar, sentence structure, synonyms, antonyms, and its correct usage. For the LDC post, this section is more significant.
  • Numerical Aptitude: This part will include questions on problems relating to Number Systems, Computation of Whole Numbers, Decimals and Fractions, relationship between numbers, Fundamental arithmetical operations, Percentages, Ratio and Proportion, Averages, Interest, Profit and Loss, Discount, use of Tables and Graphs, Mensuration, Time and Distance, Ratio and Time, Time and Work. For the LDC post, this section has a higher weightage.
  • Trade Specific (For Tin & Copper Smith): This is the most crucial section for this post. Questions will be based on the ITI syllabus for the Tin & Copper Smith trade. Topics will include knowledge of sheet metals (copper, tin, galvanized iron, aluminum), tools and equipment, marking, cutting, bending, forming operations, joints (seaming, riveting), soldering, brazing, and workshop safety.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for these Group 'C' civilian posts. The selection is based purely on merit in the written examination and qualifying the skill test.

How to Prepare for the EME Recruitment Exam

A structured preparation strategy is essential to succeed in this examination. Considering the syllabus, candidates should focus on the following areas:

  1. General Intelligence and Reasoning: This section tests logical and analytical reasoning. Practice topics like analogies, series, coding-decoding, and non-verbal reasoning from a standard reasoning book.
  2. General Awareness: This section will include questions on current events related to India and its neighbors, history, culture, geography, and scientific research. Regular newspaper reading is a must.
  3. General English (Crucial for LDC): This section tests basic English comprehension, vocabulary, grammar, and sentence structure. Since it carries 50 marks for the LDC post, give it special attention.
  4. Numerical Aptitude (Crucial for LDC): This section will have questions on basic mathematical topics like number systems, percentages, profit-loss, and averages. It also carries 50 marks for the LDC post, so practice is key.
  5. Trade Specific (Crucial for Tin & Copper Smith): This is the most important section with 50 marks. Thoroughly study the syllabus of your ITI trade. Focus on sheet metal work, soldering, brazing, and the tools involved.
  6. Typing Practice (for LDC): Along with preparing for the written exam, practice typing regularly on a computer to achieve the required speed with accuracy.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books to aid your preparation:

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Numerical Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Trade Specific (Tin & Copper Smith): ITI Trade Theory books for Sheet Metal Worker / Tinsmith trade by Nimmi Pattern or Arihant Publications.

How to Apply (Offline Process)

This recruitment is completely offline. Interested candidates must follow the steps below to send their application:

  1. Prepare the Application Form: Neatly type or handwrite the application on an A4 size paper as per the format given in the advertisement.
  2. Fill the Form: Fill in all the details (Name, Father's Name, Date of Birth, Address, etc.) in BLOCK LETTERS.
  3. Affix Photographs: Paste a recent passport-size photograph on the application form and the admit card/acknowledgment slip.
  4. Prepare Required Documents: Prepare self-attested photocopies of all the certificates listed in the "Documents Required" section below.
  5. Self-Addressed Envelope: Take a blank envelope (size 10.5 cm x 25 cm), write your complete postal address on it, and affix a postage stamp of ₹5. The admit card will be sent back to you in this envelope.
  6. Prepare the Main Envelope: Place your filled application form, all photocopies of documents, and the self-addressed envelope with the ₹5 stamp inside a larger envelope.
  7. Superscribe the Envelope: On top of the main envelope, clearly write "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF _________" (write the name of the post in the blank space).
  8. Send the Application: Send the filled envelope only through ORDINARY POST to the following address:

    Commanding Officer
    7015 EME Bn, PIN-907015
    c/o 56 APO
    (BB Cantt, Srinagar, J&K-190004)

  9. Last Date: Ensure your application reaches the above address before the last date. Applications sent through any other mode (Speed Post, Registered Post, or By Hand) will not be accepted.

Documents Required with the Application

It is mandatory to enclose self-attested photocopies of the following documents with the application form:

  1. Filled and signed Application Form.
  2. Filled Acknowledgment/Admit Card form.
  3. Two additional self-attested passport-size photographs (write your name and father's/mother's name on the back).
  4. Educational qualification certificates (10th, 12th Marksheet/Certificate).
  5. ITI Certificate (for the Tin & Copper Smith post).
  6. Date of Birth certificate (if not in the 10th certificate).
  7. Caste certificate (though the posts are unreserved, it is good practice to attach it).
  8. Copy of Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, etc.).
  9. Discharge Certificate for Ex-Servicemen.
  10. One self-addressed envelope with a ₹5 postage stamp affixed.

Important Links

Link Type Description
Download Official Notification Click Here
Employment News Website Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: How do I submit the application form?

The application form, either typed or handwritten on A4 paper, must be sent along with all required documents only through Ordinary Post to the address provided.

Question 2: Is there any application fee?

No, there is no application fee. However, you must send a self-addressed envelope with a ₹5 postage stamp affixed with your application.

Question 3: How will I receive the admit card?

If your application is shortlisted, the recruitment agency will use the self-addressed envelope you provided to send the admit card to you via ordinary post.

Question 4: Is there negative marking in this exam?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the written examination.

Question 5: What is the job location?

Selected candidates will be posted at Badami Bagh Cantonment, Srinagar, Jammu and Kashmir.

entry.title: Bihar BTSC Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancy for 3493 JE, Operator & Inspector Posts, Full Details!
entry.content:
Bihar BTSC Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancy for 3493 JE, Operator & Inspector Posts, Full Details!

A golden opportunity for 10th+ITI and Diploma holders in Bihar to secure a government job. Apply for Junior Engineer, Pump Operator, and Work Inspector posts.

Post Name: Junior Engineer (Civil, Mechanical, Electrical), Pump Operator, Work Inspector (Mechanical)
Last Date: 12 January 2026
Total Posts: 3493

Great news for technically qualified youth dreaming of a stable and prestigious government job in Bihar. The Bihar Technical Service Commission (BTSC) has released a series of advertisements (Advt. No. 28/2025 to 32/2025) for regular appointments to several technical posts in various major departments of the state. Through this mega recruitment drive, a total of 3493 vacant posts of Junior Engineer (Civil, Mechanical, Electrical), Pump Operator, and Work Inspector (Mechanical) will be filled.

This recruitment drive opens a huge gateway of opportunities for 10th+ITI and Engineering Diploma holders. Selected candidates will receive an attractive salary from Pay Level-2 to Pay Level-7, along with all the benefits applicable to Bihar Government employees. This not only offers a stable career but also a significant opportunity to contribute directly to the development of Bihar. The application process for all posts is entirely online and will be conducted on the commission's official website www.btsc.bihar.gov.in from December 12, 2025, to January 12, 2026. This detailed article will provide you with complete information about post-wise vacancies, eligibility, selection process, exam pattern, preparation strategy, and the application process.

Important Points

Details Information
Online Application Start Date 12 December 2025
Last Date for Online Application 12 January 2026
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Bihar Government)
Job Location Bihar

Post-wise Vacancy Details

BTSC has announced a total of 3493 vacancies for various technical posts:

Post Name Advertisement Number Total Vacant Posts Pay Level
Junior Engineer (Civil) 28/2025 2653 Level-7
Junior Engineer (Mechanical) 29/2025 70 Level-7
Junior Engineer (Electrical) 30/2025 86 Level-7
Work Inspector (Mechanical) 31/2025 493 Level-2
Pump Operator 32/2025 191 Level-2
Grand Total 3493
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and subject to change. Reservation will be applicable as per the rules of the Government of Bihar.

Application Fee

  • Examination fee for all posts and all categories of candidates: ₹100/- (One Hundred Rupees only) + Bank Charges.

The fee is to be paid through online mode only.

Post-wise Eligibility Criteria

Specific educational qualifications and age limits are prescribed for each post. It is mandatory for the candidate to be a citizen of India for all posts.

1. Junior Engineer (Civil/Mechanical/Electrical)

CriteriaRequirement
Educational QualificationA Diploma or equivalent qualification in the respective engineering branch (Civil, Mechanical, or Electrical) from an AICTE or UGC recognized institute/university through a regular course (Non-Distance Mode).
Age Limit (as on 01.08.2025)Minimum 18 years. Maximum: Unreserved (Male)-37 years, Unreserved (Female)/BC/EBC-40 years, SC/ST-42 years.

2. Work Inspector (Mechanical)

CriteriaRequirement
Educational Qualification(i) Passed Matriculation (10th) from a recognized board.
(ii) Passed a regular course from a recognized ITI institute in Machinist / Fitter / Wireman / Electrician trade.
Age Limit (as on 01.08.2025)Minimum 18 years. Maximum: Unreserved (Male)-37 years, Unreserved (Female)/BC/EBC-40 years, SC/ST-42 years.

3. Pump Operator

CriteriaRequirement
Educational Qualification(i) Passed Matriculation (10th) from a recognized board.
(ii) Passed a regular course from a recognized ITI institute in Machinist or Fitter trade.
Age Limit (as on 01.08.2025)Minimum 18 years. Maximum: Unreserved (Male)-37 years, Unreserved (Female)/BC/EBC-40 years, SC/ST-42 years.

Selection Process

The selection process varies according to the posts:

1. For Junior Engineer:

  1. Written Examination (CBT): The weightage for the marks of the written exam in the selection will be 75 marks. The exam will be of 100 marks, and the marks obtained will be multiplied by 0.75 for the final calculation.
  2. Work Experience: Candidates working as a Junior Engineer on a contract basis in the Government of Bihar or any of its undertakings/bodies will be given a weightage for experience. 05 marks will be given for each completed year of experience, with a maximum limit of 25 marks.

Final Merit List: Will be prepared out of a total of 100 marks by combining the 75% weightage of the written exam and the 25% weightage of experience.

2. For Work Inspector and Pump Operator:

  1. Written Examination (CBT): For these posts, the selection will be based entirely on the marks obtained in a 100-mark written examination.

Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the performance in the written examination.

Exam Pattern & Syllabus

A written examination (CBT) will be conducted for all posts, with negative marking (0.25 marks per wrong answer).

1. Junior Engineer (100 questions, 100 marks, 2 hours):

  • Domain Knowledge: 100 questions will be from the Diploma level syllabus prescribed by AICTE for the respective engineering branch (Civil/Mechanical/Electrical).

2. Work Inspector (Mechanical) (100 questions, 100 marks, 2 hours):

SubjectNumber of Questions
Machinist Trade15
Fitter Trade15
Wireman Trade15
Electrician Trade15
Mathematics (Matriculation Level)20
General Knowledge20

3. Pump Operator (100 questions, 100 marks, 2 hours):

SubjectNumber of Questions
Machinist Trade30
Fitter Trade30
Mathematics (Matriculation Level)20
General Knowledge20

How to Prepare for the Exam

A systematic and smart preparation strategy is necessary to succeed in these competitive examinations.

  1. Prioritize the Syllabus: Understand the syllabus well according to your qualification and post. JE candidates should completely focus on their diploma curriculum, and ITI candidates on their trade syllabus, as most of the questions will come from there.
  2. Strengthen Basic Concepts: Clarify all the basic concepts, formulas, and principles of your trade/branch. Emphasize understanding rather than rote learning.
  3. Practice Objective Questions: Solve previous years' question papers and objective question banks from relevant competitive exams (like SSC JE, State JE, ITI-based exams). This will familiarize you with the question types and difficulty level.
  4. Practice Time Management: Keeping the negative marking in mind, take regular online mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy. Create a strategy to solve 100 questions in 2 hours.
  5. General Knowledge and Mathematics: Candidates for Work Inspector and Pump Operator should also focus on 10th-level mathematics and general knowledge (especially related to Bihar).

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right study material is crucial for good preparation. Here are some recommended books by subject:

Post/BranchRecommended Books/Sources
Junior Engineer (All Branches)
  • Standard diploma-level textbooks of the respective engineering branch.
  • "Objective Engineering" books (like R.S. Khurmi, J.B. Gupta).
  • Handbooks from Made Easy or ACE Engineering Academy.
Work Inspector / Pump Operator
  • ITI trade theory books based on the NIMI pattern.
  • Objective books for ITI trades by Arihant or Upkar Prakashan.
  • "Lucent's Samanya Gyan" (for General Knowledge).
  • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" - R.S. Aggarwal (for Mathematics).

How to Apply

The application process is only through the BTSC's online portal:

  1. Go to the official website of the Bihar Technical Service Commission: www.btsc.bihar.gov.in.
  2. Click on the "Online Application" tab and select the "Apply" link for the respective advertisement.
  3. Click on "Register" and complete your registration with your basic information.
  4. Fill out the application form carefully and upload scanned copies of all the required documents as mentioned below.
  5. Pay the online application fee.
  6. Review your application before final submission and then submit it. Take a printout of the application form for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep scanned copies of the following documents ready while applying online:

  1. Latest passport-size color photograph and signature.
  2. Matriculation (10th) certificate (for proof of date of birth).
  3. Diploma / ITI certificate and marksheets of all years.
  4. Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/EBC/BC/EWS) (if applicable).
  5. Permanent Residence Certificate of Bihar (to avail reservation benefits).
  6. Work experience certificate (for JE post, if applicable) - in the prescribed format.
  7. Photo identity card (such as Aadhaar Card).

Important Links

Link Type Anchor Text
Apply Online Click Here
Junior Engineer (Civil) Notification Download Here
Junior Engineer (Mechanical) Notification Download Here
Junior Engineer (Electrical) Notification Download Here
Work Inspector (Mechanical) Notification Download Here
Pump Operator Notification Download Here
Official Website www.btsc.bihar.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Can candidates from outside Bihar avail reservation benefits?

No, reservation benefits are only for the original residents of Bihar. Additionally, 40% of the JE posts are reserved for those who have completed their diploma from institutions in Bihar.

Question 2: Is a diploma/ITI from distance learning valid?

No, only qualifications obtained through a regular course are valid for all posts.

Question 3: What is the final basis of selection?

For JE, the merit will be based on a combination of written test marks (75%) and experience (25%). For Work Inspector and Pump Operator, the merit will be based solely on the marks of the written test.

entry.title: HSSC Group D Recruitment 2026 for 10,500 Posts
entry.content:
HSSC Group D Recruitment 2026 for 10,500 Posts

Advertisement No. 03/2026: The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) has invited online applications from Common Eligibility Test (CET) qualified candidates for approximately 10,500 Group-D posts in various government departments, boards, and corporations of Haryana. This is the main recruitment phase following the CET exam. The online application portal for submitting preferences will be open from February 01, 2026, to February 21, 2026.

Post Name: Group-D Posts
Last Date: 21 February 2026
Total Posts: 10,500 (Approx.)

The Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC) has initiated the next crucial phase of the Group-D recruitment process. Following the declaration of the Common Eligibility Test (CET) results, the commission has now invited applications from all CET-qualified candidates to fill approximately 10,500 vacancies across various government bodies in Haryana. This is not a new exam notification but a call for qualified candidates to submit their consent and preferences for various departments and posts.

The selection will be made purely based on the merit of the CET score (with 95% weightage) and marks awarded for Socio-Economic criteria (5% weightage). There will be no further written examination. This step is mandatory for all qualified candidates who wish to be considered for a Group-D position. The application process is entirely online through the official HSSC website, and no application fee is required at this stage.

HSSC Group D Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and understand that this is an application process for CET-qualified candidates only.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Haryana Staff Selection Commission (HSSC)
Advertisement Number 03/2026
Post Name Group-D Posts
Total Vacancies 10,500 (Approximately)
Application Start Date 01 February 2026
Application Last Date 21 February 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Haryana
Official Website www.hssc.gov.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 10,500 Group-D vacancies in various Departments, Boards, Corporations, and other government bodies of Haryana.

Post Name Vacancies Pay Scale
Group-D Posts ~ 10,500 Pay Level DL (₹16,900 - ₹53,500) + special pay as applicable
Note: The detailed department-wise list of posts is available in the official advertisement. Candidates will be required to give their preferences for these departments during the online application process.

Application Fee

  • No Fee Required: No application fee is required to be deposited by any candidate for applying against this advertisement.

The necessary examination fee was already collected at the time of registration for the Common Eligibility Test (CET).

Eligibility Criteria

Only those candidates who have qualified the Common Eligibility Test (CET) for Group-D posts are eligible to apply.

Criteria Details
Mandatory Qualification Must have a valid Common Eligibility Test (CET) Group-D Score.
Educational Qualification Must have passed Matriculation (10th Class) from a recognized Board.
Must have studied Hindi or Sanskrit as one of the subjects in Matriculation or Higher Education.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 42 Years
(Age criteria as met during CET registration). Age relaxation for reserved categories (SC/ST/BC/PwD/ESM) is applicable as per Haryana Government rules.

Selection Process

The selection process does not involve any new written test. The selection will be based on the merit of the already conducted CET exam and socio-economic criteria.

  1. Application & Preference Submission: All CET-qualified candidates must apply online and submit their preferences for various departments and posts.
  2. Merit List Preparation: A final merit list will be prepared based on the following weightage:
    • Common Eligibility Test (CET) Score: 95% weightage
    • Socio-Economic Criteria & Experience: 5% weightage
  3. Document Verification: Candidates will be shortlisted based on the merit list and called for Document Verification. During DV, claims for Socio-Economic marks will be verified.
  4. Final Allotment: Based on the final merit list (after DV) and the preferences submitted by the candidates, departments and posts will be allocated.

Exam Pattern

There is no new written examination for this advertisement. The selection is based on the score of the Common Eligibility Test (CET) for Group-D, which has already been conducted. The final merit will be calculated out of 100 marks, with 95 marks from the CET score and a maximum of 5 marks for socio-economic criteria.

How to Prepare for This Stage

Since there is no new exam, the preparation for this stage is focused on the application process and document readiness.

  • Analyze Preferences: Carefully study the list of departments and posts available in the notification. Research the nature of work in different departments to make an informed decision while filling your preferences.
  • Gather Socio-Economic Documents: If you are eligible to claim marks under the socio-economic criteria (e.g., no government job in the family, widow, fatherless, etc.), ensure you have the required certificates and affidavits in the prescribed format, duly verified by the competent authority.
  • Keep CET Details Ready: Keep your CET Registration Number, Scorecard, and other details handy for logging into the application portal.

How to Apply Online

The application process is online through the official HSSC portal.

  1. Visit the official HSSC website: www.hssc.gov.in.
  2. Find the link for "Applying online for Group-D posts against Advt. No. 03/2026".
  3. Log in using your CET Registration Number and other required credentials.
  4. Your personal details will be auto-filled from your CET application. Verify them carefully.
  5. Proceed to the section for filling your preferences for various departments and posts. Fill these in order of your priority.
  6. If you are claiming marks under socio-economic criteria, fill in the required details and upload the supporting documents.
  7. Review the entire application form carefully before final submission.
  8. Download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply and for Verification

While applying online, you will primarily need your CET details. For Document Verification, you must have the following original documents ready:

  1. Printout of the final submitted application form.
  2. Matriculation (10th) Certificate.
  3. Caste Certificate (SC/BCA/BCB) and EWS Certificate, if applicable.
  4. Haryana Domicile Certificate.
  5. Certificates and Affidavits for claiming marks under Socio-Economic Criteria (e.g., No Job Certificate verified by Tehsildar).
  6. Aadhaar Card and other valid photo ID proofs.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there a new written exam for this HSSC Group D recruitment?

No, there is no new written examination. This is an invitation for candidates who have already qualified the Common Eligibility Test (CET) for Group-D to apply for posts and submit preferences.

Q2: Who is eligible to apply for these 10,500 posts?

Only those candidates who have a valid and qualifying score in the HSSC Common Eligibility Test (CET) for Group-D are eligible to apply against this advertisement.

Q3: Do I need to pay an application fee?

No, the official notification clearly states that no fee is required to be deposited by any candidate for applying against this advertisement, as the fee was already paid during the CET registration.

Q4: How will the final selection be made?

The final merit list and selection will be based on the candidate's CET score (95% weightage) and marks for socio-economic criteria and experience (5% weightage).

entry.title: India Exim Bank MT Recruitment 2026 for 40 Management Trainee Posts
entry.content:
India Exim Bank MT Recruitment 2026 for 40 Management Trainee Posts

Advertisement No. HRM/ MT/ 2025-26/ 05: The Export-Import Bank of India (India Exim Bank) has announced a direct recruitment drive for 40 Management Trainee (MT) positions in Banking Operations. This is a premier opportunity for dynamic post-graduates in Finance/IB or Chartered Accountants to join an all-India financial institution dedicated to promoting India's international trade. The online application process starts on January 17, 2026, and concludes on February 01, 2026. Selected candidates will undergo a one-year training program with a handsome stipend of ₹65,000 per month before absorption as Deputy Managers.

Post Name: Management Trainee (MT) (Banking Operations)
Last Date: 01 February 2026
Total Posts: 40

The Export-Import Bank of India, a key player in the nation's financial landscape, is seeking to induct a new batch of talented professionals as Management Trainees. This recruitment drive aims to fill 40 vacancies and is a gateway to a rewarding career in specialized banking functions like finance, international business, and foreign trade. The role promises not just a job but a comprehensive career path, starting with a structured one-year training period designed to equip the trainees with the necessary skills and knowledge to excel in the domain of export-import financing.

The selection process for this prestigious role is a two-tiered system, comprising a descriptive Written Examination followed by a Personal Interview. The Bank has outlined a detailed syllabus focusing on professional knowledge in finance, making it a targeted opportunity for candidates with strong academic credentials in this field. Upon successful completion of the training, the Management Trainees will be absorbed into the Junior Management (JM-I) grade as Deputy Managers, with an attractive pay scale and a wide array of allowances and benefits. This recruitment is an excellent chance for eligible Indian citizens to secure a high-profile position in a leading government financial institution.

India Exim Bank MT Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take careful note of the key dates and details to ensure a smooth application process. All applications must be submitted online within the stipulated timeframe.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Export-Import Bank of India (India Exim Bank)
Advertisement Number HRM/ MT/ 2025-26/ 05
Post Name Management Trainee (MT) (Banking Operations)
Total Vacancies 40
Application Start Date 17 January 2026
Application Last Date 01 February 2026
Tentative Written Exam Month February 2026
Application Mode Online
Job Type Permanent (Government Financial Institution)
Job Location Anywhere in India or Abroad
Official Website www.eximbankindia.in/careers

Vacancy Details

A total of 40 vacancies have been announced for the position of Management Trainee (Banking Operations), with reservations as per Government of India guidelines.

Position UR SC ST OBC (NCL) EWS Total
Management Trainee (MT) (Banking Operations) 19 05 03 10 03 40
Note: Reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) is horizontal. One vacancy is reserved for a PwBD candidate with Hearing Impairment. PwBD candidates will be placed in their respective social categories (UR/SC/ST/OBC/EWS).

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

  • For General and OBC candidates: ₹600/-
  • For SC, ST, PwBD, EWS, and Female candidates: ₹100/- (Intimation charges only)

The fee must be paid online. Candidates are advised to ensure their eligibility before paying the fee, as it will not be refunded under any circumstances.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 31.12.2025)

Candidates must meet all the eligibility requirements related to age and educational qualifications as on the specified cut-off dates.

Criteria Eligibility Details
Educational Qualification

Candidates must possess the following qualifications from a recognized University/Institute (full-time courses only):

  1. Graduation: Minimum 60% aggregate marks or equivalent CGPA in any discipline. The graduation course must be of a minimum 3-year duration.
  2. Post-Graduation:
    • MBA / PGDBA / PGDBM / MMS with a specialization in Finance / International Business / Foreign Trade. The course must be of a minimum 2-year duration with at least 60% aggregate marks or equivalent CGPA.
    • OR
    • Chartered Accountant (CA). For CA candidates, passing the professional examination is sufficient. Membership of ICAI is mandatory (can be submitted upon passing the examination).

Note for Final Year Students: Candidates who have appeared or are appearing for their final PG/CA examination in January 2026 are eligible to apply. However, they must produce documentary evidence of having passed the qualification by June 01, 2026.
Age Limit (as on 31.12.2025)
  • Minimum Age: 21 Years
  • Maximum Age (for UR/General): 28 Years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

CategoryMaximum Age
Scheduled Caste/ Scheduled Tribe (SC/ST)33 Years
Other Backward Classes (OBC - Non-Creamy Layer)31 Years
Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) - UR/EWS38 Years
Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) - OBC41 Years
Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) - SC/ST43 Years

An additional relaxation of 5 years is available for Ex-servicemen for Group A posts.

Selection Process

The selection for the Management Trainee position is a rigorous two-stage process designed to identify the most suitable candidates:

  1. Written Test: All eligible candidates will be called for a subjective (descriptive) written test. This test will assess their professional knowledge in the field of finance and banking. There is no sectional cut-off in the written examination.
  2. Personal Interview: Candidates who are shortlisted based on their performance in the Written Test will be invited for a Personal Interview. The interview will be conducted in Mumbai and/or New Delhi.
  3. Final Merit List: The final selection will be based on the combined performance in both stages. The weightage for the final merit list is as follows:
    • Written Test: 70% weightage
    • Personal Interview: 30% weightage

Written Exam Pattern

The written examination is a descriptive test designed to evaluate the candidate's in-depth knowledge and analytical skills in finance. The total duration of the test is 2 hours and 30 minutes.

Name of the Test Question Details Max. Marks Total Time
Professional Knowledge - Subjective PART - I: Financial Statement
(One Compulsory Question)		 40 02:30 hours
PART - II
(Attempt any 6 out of 8 Questions)		 60
Total 100 02:30 hours

Detailed Syllabus and Focus Areas:

  • Part I (Financial Statement Analysis): This compulsory question will likely involve a case study or a set of financial statements (Balance Sheet, Profit & Loss Account, Cash Flow Statement). Candidates will be expected to analyze the financial health of a company by calculating and interpreting various financial ratios (e.g., liquidity, solvency, profitability, efficiency ratios), commenting on its performance, and identifying key strengths and weaknesses. A strong understanding of accounting principles and standards (Ind AS) is crucial.
  • Part II (Finance & International Business): This section will offer choices, allowing candidates to play to their strengths. The questions will be drawn from the core MBA (Finance)/CA curriculum. Key topics to focus on include:
    • Corporate Finance: Capital Budgeting (NPV, IRR), Cost of Capital, Working Capital Management, Capital Structure theories.
    • International Finance & Trade: Foreign Exchange Markets, Exchange Rate Determination, Forex Risk Management (Hedging techniques like Forwards, Futures, Options), Modes of International Trade Finance (Letters of Credit, Bank Guarantees, Forfaiting, Factoring).
    • Financial Markets & Institutions: Knowledge of Indian financial system, money markets, capital markets, and regulatory bodies like RBI and SEBI.
    • Project Appraisal: Techniques for evaluating the feasibility of large-scale projects, including technical, financial, and economic analysis.
    • Banking & Credit: Principles of lending, credit appraisal techniques, NPA management.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for the recruitment of Management Trainees in India Exim Bank.

How to Prepare for the Exam and Interview

A structured preparation strategy is key to cracking this competitive examination. Candidates should focus on both the written test and the personal interview.

For the Written Examination:

  1. Master the Fundamentals: Revisit your post-graduation/CA textbooks. Ensure your core concepts in Financial Accounting, Corporate Finance, and International Finance are crystal clear.
  2. Practice Case Studies: For Part I, practice solving case studies based on company annual reports. Learn to quickly calculate ratios and, more importantly, write a coherent analysis of what they imply.
  3. Develop Writing Skills: Since it's a descriptive test, the ability to articulate your thoughts clearly, concisely, and professionally is vital. Practice writing answers to previous years' papers of similar banking exams (e.g., RBI Grade B, SEBI Grade A) to improve your speed and structure.
  4. Stay Updated: Follow business newspapers and financial magazines to stay abreast of current economic trends, changes in RBI policies, and developments in international trade. This will be useful for adding relevant points to your answers.

For the Personal Interview:

  1. Know the Bank: Thoroughly research India Exim Bank—its mission, vision, key functions, recent deals, and role in the Indian economy.
  2. Brush up on Technicals: Be prepared for in-depth questions on the topics mentioned in the written exam syllabus. The interview panel will test the depth of your knowledge.
  3. Prepare for HR Questions: Be ready to answer standard HR questions about your strengths, weaknesses, career goals, and why you want to join Exim Bank. Structure your answers with confidence.
  4. Communication is Key: Work on your communication and interpersonal skills. The bank is looking for candidates who are not only knowledgeable but also articulate and confident.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard academic and professional books to build a strong foundation for the written examination.

  • Financial Management: "Financial Management: Theory and Practice" by Prasanna Chandra; "Financial Management" by I. M. Pandey.
  • International Finance: "International Financial Management" by P.G. Apte; "International Financial Management" by Vyuptakesh Sharan.
  • Financial Accounting & Statement Analysis: "Advanced Accountancy" by S.P. Jain & K.L. Narang; Refer to materials from ICAI for CA Final (Financial Reporting).
  • Banking & Economics: "Indian Financial System" by M. Y. Khan; "Indian Economy" by Ramesh Singh.
  • Business Newspapers & Magazines: The Economic Times, Business Standard, The Wall Street Journal, and The Economist.

How to Apply Online

The application must be submitted through the online portal only. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official Exim Bank website and navigate to the 'Careers' section.
  2. Find the advertisement for "DIRECT RECRUITMENT DRIVE FOR MANAGEMENT TRAINEES" and click on the "Apply Online" link.
  3. Click on "Click here for New Registration" and enter your basic details (Name, Contact, Email) to generate a provisional Registration ID and Password.
  4. Log in and fill out the detailed application form. Use the "SAVE AND NEXT" feature to save your progress.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, left thumb impression, handwritten declaration, and other required academic documents as per the specifications.
  6. Verify all details in the application form using the 'Preview' tab before final submission.
  7. Proceed to the 'Payment' tab and pay the application fee using the available online payment modes.
  8. After successful payment, submit the application form. A registration confirmation will be sent via email/SMS.
  9. Download and print a copy of the final submitted application form and the e-receipt for your future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Scanning)

Candidates should keep clear, scanned copies of the following documents ready before starting the application process:

  1. Photograph: 4.5cm x 3.5cm, recent, colour passport-size (20kb-50kb).
  2. Signature: On white paper with black ink (10kb-20kb).
  3. Left Thumb Impression: On white paper with black or blue ink (20kb-50kb).
  4. Handwritten Declaration: In English on white paper with black ink. The text is: "I, [Name of the candidate], hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required." (50kb-100kb).
  5. Academic Documents (PDF): Consolidated Mark Sheets and Degree Certificates for both Graduation and Post-Graduation (20kb-500kb each).
  6. Caste Certificate (PDF): If applicable, in the format prescribed by the Govt. of India (20kb-500kb).

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Link will be active from 17.01.2026)
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the selection process for the India Exim Bank MT post?

The selection process consists of two stages: a descriptive Written Test assessing professional knowledge (worth 70% weightage) and a Personal Interview (worth 30% weightage).

Q2: Is there a service bond for this position?

Yes, selected candidates must execute a personal bond for ₹3 lakh to serve the Bank for a minimum of one year as a Management Trainee and five years as an employee after absorption.

Q3: Can students in the final year of their MBA/CA apply for this recruitment?

Yes, candidates appearing for their final examination in Post Graduation or Chartered Accountancy to be held during January 2026 are eligible to apply. However, they must provide proof of passing the qualification by June 01, 2026.

Q4: What is the salary of a Management Trainee after absorption?

After the one-year training period, MTs are absorbed as Deputy Managers (JM-I) in the pay scale of ₹48480-2000-62480-2340-67160-2680-85920, along with various allowances and benefits as per the Bank's rules.

entry.title: SECL आंतरिक भर्ती 2025: Assistant Foreman (Electrical) के 543 पदों पर आवेदन करें!
entry.content:
SECL आंतरिक भर्ती 2025: Assistant Foreman (Electrical) के 543 पदों पर आवेदन करें!

South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL) के विभागीय कर्मचारियों के लिए T&S Grade-C में प्रमोशन का सुनहरा अवसर।

South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL), जो Coal India Limited (CIL) की एक सहायक कंपनी है, ने अपने विभागीय कर्मचारियों के लिए एक आंतरिक अधिसूचना जारी की है (Ref. No. SECL/BSP/HR/NEE/2025/1218)। इस भर्ती के तहत कंपनी Assistant Foreman (Electrical), T&S Grade-C के 543 पदों को भरने जा रही है।

यह केवल SECL के नियमित और स्थायी कर्मचारियों के लिए है। चयन प्रक्रिया एक लिखित परीक्षा के आधार पर होगी, और आवेदन केवल कंपनी के आंतरिक ऑनलाइन पोर्टल के माध्यम से ही किए जा सकते हैं। पात्र कर्मचारी अंतिम तिथि से पहले आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि / समय
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ16 अक्टूबर 2025 (सुबह 10:00 बजे)
आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि9 नवम्बर 2025 (रात 11:59 बजे)
Objection फाइल करने की तिथि16 नवम्बर से 20 नवम्बर 2025 तक

रिक्तियों का विवरण

श्रेणीपदों की संख्या
UR (Unreserved)356
SC118
ST48
PwBD21
कुल योग543

कैरियर ग्रोथ का अवसर

यह भर्ती SECL कर्मचारियों के लिए एक प्रमोशनल मौका है। इस पद पर चयन होने से आपकी जिम्मेदारियाँ और कैरियर ग्रोथ दोनों बढ़ेंगे।

पात्रता मानदंड

केवल वे कर्मचारी जो 30.09.2025 तक नीचे दिए गए मानदंडों को पूरा करते हैं, आवेदन के पात्र हैं।

विकल्पयोग्यता एवं अनुभव
विकल्प A(I): Asst. Foreman (Trainee)
  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता: Electrical Engineering में Diploma (कम से कम 3 वर्ष) या Degree।
  • अनुभव: कंपनी में कम से कम 3 वर्ष का अनुभव आवश्यक।
  • नोट: चयनित उम्मीदवारों को 2 वर्ष के लिए Trainee के रूप में रखा जाएगा।
विकल्प A(II): Direct Placement
  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता: Diploma या Non-Diploma Holder जिनके पास Mines में कार्य हेतु वैध Supervisory Certificate है।
  • अनुभव: ऐसा प्रमाणपत्र रखने वाले कोई भी स्थायी कर्मचारी आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
  • नोट: चयनित उम्मीदवारों को सीधे Assistant Foreman (Elec.) T&S-C पद पर नियुक्त किया जाएगा।

चयन प्रक्रिया एवं परीक्षा योजना

चयन केवल लिखित परीक्षा के अंकों के आधार पर होगा। गलत उत्तरों के लिए कोई नकारात्मक अंकन नहीं होगा।

पैरामीटरविवरण
परीक्षा प्रकारOMR शीट आधारित Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ)
कुल प्रश्न100
कुल अंक100 (प्रत्येक प्रश्न = 1 अंक)
विषयवार वितरण
  • मानसिक / तर्कशक्ति एवं गणितीय क्षमता – 20 अंक
  • सामान्य जागरूकता (CIL/SECL संबंधित) – 20 अंक
  • विषय ज्ञान (Electrical) – 60 अंक
न्यूनतम उत्तीर्णांक:
  • सामान्य वर्ग – 35%
  • SC/ST वर्ग – 30%

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. यह आंतरिक अधिसूचना है – केवल SECL के वर्तमान स्थायी कर्मचारी ही आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
  2. ऑनलाइन आवेदन पोर्टल: https://portals.secl-cil.in/internal/index.php
  3. यह पोर्टल केवल LAN नेटवर्क से एक्सेस किया जा सकता है।
  4. आवेदन से पहले Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) ध्यान से पढ़ें।
  5. आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ जैसे Matriculation, Diploma/Degree, Supervisory Certificate आदि की Self-attested कॉपी अपलोड करें।
  6. आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 09 नवम्बर 2025 है।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन (Internal Portal)यहाँ क्लिक करें (केवल SECL कर्मचारियों के लिए)
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या SECL के बाहर के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, यह भर्ती केवल SECL के स्थायी विभागीय कर्मचारियों के लिए है।
प्रश्न 2: क्या परीक्षा में नकारात्मक अंकन होगा?
नहीं, किसी भी गलत उत्तर के लिए कोई नकारात्मक अंकन नहीं किया जाएगा।
प्रश्न 3: क्या Supervisory Certificate रखने वाले बिना Diploma वाले कर्मचारी पात्र हैं?
हाँ, यदि आपके पास वैध Supervisory Certificate है तो आप बिना Diploma के भी पात्र हैं।
प्रश्न 4: यदि दो उम्मीदवारों के अंक समान हों तो चयन कैसे होगा?
टाई की स्थिति में प्राथमिकता क्रम: 1️⃣ प्रारंभिक नियुक्ति तिथि 2️⃣ जन्मतिथि (उम्र में बड़े उम्मीदवार को प्राथमिकता) 3️⃣ नाम के Alphabetical क्रम के अनुसार तय की जाएगी।

entry.title: IIT Gandhinagar भर्ती 2025: नॉन-टीचिंग के कई पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
IIT Gandhinagar भर्ती 2025: नॉन-टीचिंग के कई पदों पर भर्ती!

Superintending Engineer, Deputy Librarian, Registrar, JE, Nurse और अन्य पदों के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar (IITGN) ने एक नई भर्ती अधिसूचना (No. IITGN/STAFF/RECT/01/2025-26) जारी की है। इस भर्ती के अंतर्गत संस्थान में Direct या Deputation आधार पर कई नॉन-टीचिंग पदों के लिए योग्य भारतीय नागरिकों से ऑनलाइन आवेदन मांगे गए हैं।

यह उन सभी उम्मीदवारों के लिए सुनहरा अवसर है जो IIT जैसे प्रतिष्ठित संस्थान में एक बेहतरीन करियर बनाना चाहते हैं। इस लेख में हम आपको पात्रता, वेतन, आयु सीमा, चयन प्रक्रिया और आवेदन प्रक्रिया की पूरी जानकारी देंगे।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि / समय
ऑनलाइन आवेदन शुरू होने की तिथि15 अक्टूबर 2025 (सुबह 10:00 बजे से)
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि17 नवम्बर 2025 (रात 11:59 बजे तक)

आवेदन शुल्क

कोई आवेदन शुल्क नहीं: अधिसूचना के अनुसार इन पदों पर आवेदन करने के लिए कोई भी शुल्क नहीं देना होगा।

पद, योग्यता और वेतन

नीचे दी गई तालिका में सभी पदों का विवरण दिया गया है:

पद का नामवेतन स्तरपद संख्याआयु सीमाअनिवार्य योग्यता / अनुभव
Superintending EngineerLevel 13 (₹1,23,100/- से शुरू)01 (UR)50 वर्षBE/B.Tech के साथ 12 वर्ष का अनुभव, जिसमें 7 वर्ष Executive Engineer (Level 11) के रूप में होना चाहिए।
Deputy LibrarianAcademic Level 12 (₹79,800/- से शुरू)01 (PwBD-HH)50 वर्षLibrary Science/Information Science में 55% अंकों के साथ परास्नातक और 8 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Deputy RegistrarLevel 12 (₹78,800/- से शुरू)01 (UR)50 वर्ष55% अंकों के साथ परास्नातक और 9 वर्ष का अनुभव या 5 वर्ष का अनुभव Assistant Registrar के रूप में।
Assistant RegistrarLevel 10 (₹56,100/- से शुरू)02 (OBC-1, EWS-1)45 वर्ष55% अंकों के साथ परास्नातक और 8 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Junior Engineer (Civil/Electrical)Level 6 (₹35,400/- से शुरू)02 (UR-1, SC-1)32 वर्षCivil/Electrical Engineering में 55% अंकों के साथ 3 वर्ष का Diploma और 3 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Junior SuperintendentLevel 604 (UR-3, ST-1)32 वर्ष55% अंकों के साथ Master’s Degree और 3 वर्ष का अनुभव या Bachelor’s Degree और 5 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Junior Accounts OfficerLevel 602 (OBC-1, SC-1)32 वर्ष55% अंकों के साथ Master’s Degree और 3 वर्ष का अनुभव या Bachelor’s Degree और 5 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Assistant Staff NurseLevel 501 (UR)27 वर्ष10+2 के बाद 3 वर्ष का General Nursing & Midwifery Course तथा Nursing Council में पंजीकरण आवश्यक।
Junior AssistantLevel 311 (UR-4, OBC-4, SC-2, EWS-1)27 वर्ष55% अंकों के साथ Bachelor’s Degree तथा कंप्यूटर अनुप्रयोगों में 2 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Junior Accounts AssistantLevel 304 (UR-3, OBC-1)27 वर्ष55% अंकों के साथ Bachelor’s Degree और अकाउंटिंग में 2 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Junior Laboratory AssistantLevel 307 (UR-6, OBC-1)27 वर्षBE/B.Tech/Diploma/B.Sc/ITI (55%) और संबंधित क्षेत्र में अनुभव।

चयन प्रक्रिया

सभी योग्य उम्मीदवारों को सीधे इंटरव्यू के लिए नहीं बुलाया जाएगा। संस्थान शैक्षणिक योग्यता और अनुभव के आधार पर उम्मीदवारों को शॉर्टलिस्ट करेगा।

  • शॉर्टलिस्ट उम्मीदवारों को लिखित परीक्षा / कौशल परीक्षा / साक्षात्कार के लिए बुलाया जाएगा।
  • चयन प्रक्रिया IIT Gandhinagar कैंपस में आयोजित होगी।
  • Deputation के लिए आवेदन करने वाले उम्मीदवारों का पिछले 5 वर्षों का APAR "Very Good" होना चाहिए।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. IIT Gandhinagar की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://iitgn.ac.in/careers/non-academic-staff पर जाएं।
  2. ऑनलाइन आवेदन फॉर्म को ध्यानपूर्वक भरें और सभी पात्रता मापदंड जांच लें।
  3. आवेदन करते समय अपने शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र, अनुभव प्रमाणपत्र और अन्य आवश्यक दस्तावेज अपलोड करें।
  4. अधूरे आवेदन अस्वीकार कर दिए जाएंगे।
  5. एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन करने पर प्रत्येक के लिए अलग फॉर्म भरना आवश्यक है।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें
अधिसूचना PDFडाउनलोड करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या इस भर्ती के लिए कोई आवेदन शुल्क है?
नहीं, इस भर्ती के लिए कोई आवेदन शुल्क नहीं लिया जाएगा।
प्रश्न 2: क्या ये नौकरियाँ स्थायी हैं?
हाँ, ये सभी नियमित (Regular) पद हैं जिन्हें Direct/Deputation आधार पर भरा जाएगा।
प्रश्न 3: यदि उम्मीदवार पहले से सरकारी नौकरी में है तो कैसे आवेदन करे?
ऐसे उम्मीदवारों को “Through Proper Channel” आवेदन करना होगा या NOC तथा Vigilance Clearance Certificate अपलोड करना होगा।
प्रश्न 4: चयन का आधार क्या होगा?
चयन केवल न्यूनतम पात्रता पर आधारित नहीं होगा; बल्कि उच्च योग्यता और संबंधित अनुभव वाले उम्मीदवारों को प्राथमिकता दी जाएगी।

entry.title: DAV ओडिशा शिक्षक भर्ती 2025-27: PGT, TGT, PRT और स्टाफ के पदों पर बड़ी भर्ती!
entry.content:
DAV ओडिशा शिक्षक भर्ती 2025-27: PGT, TGT, PRT और स्टाफ के पदों पर बड़ी भर्ती!

DAV Institutions, Odisha ने सत्र 2026-27 के लिए शिक्षकों और गैर-शिक्षण स्टाफ की भर्ती हेतु DAV Recruitment Test (DAV-CBT) की घोषणा की है। इच्छुक उम्मीदवार ऑनलाइन आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

शिक्षण क्षेत्र में करियर बनाने का सपना देखने वाले उम्मीदवारों के लिए बड़ी खबर! प्रतिष्ठित DAV Institutions, Odisha ने सत्र 2026-27 के लिए शिक्षकों और स्टाफ के पदों हेतु भर्ती विज्ञापन (No. 02/2025) जारी किया है। इस भर्ती का पहला चरण होगा DAV Recruitment Test (DAV-CBT), जो जनवरी 2026 में आयोजित किया जाएगा। इच्छुक उम्मीदवार ऑनलाइन आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन शुरू06 October 2025 (सुबह 8:00 बजे से)
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि31 October 2025 (रात 8:00 बजे तक)
प्रिंटआउट वेरिफिकेशन की अंतिम तिथि26 November 2025
DAV-CBT परीक्षाJanuary 2026

भर्ती किन पदों पर हो रही है?

  • PGT: English, Hindi, Maths, Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Commerce, Economics, History, Geography, Sociology, Computer Science।
  • TGT: English, Hindi, Odia, Sanskrit, Maths, Science, Social Science, Physical Education, Painting/Art, Computer Science।
  • PRT: English, Hindi, Odia, Maths, Science, Social Science, Art & Craft, Music, Dance, Librarian (Grade-II)।
  • अन्य शिक्षक पद: Nursery/Pre-Primary (केवल महिलाएं), Wellness Teacher, Counselor।
  • Ministerial पद: UDC, LDC (Accounts/Admin), Receptionist (महिलाएं), Library Assistant, Computer Lab Assistant, Nurse (महिलाएं)।

आवेदन शुल्क

ऑनलाइन आवेदन के दौरान शुल्क नहीं लिया जाएगा। लेकिन जिन उम्मीदवारों का चयन Demo-Interview के लिए होगा, उन्हें ₹300/- का हैंडलिंग शुल्क क्षेत्रीय स्तर पर देना होगा।

पात्रता मानदंड

सभी पदों के लिए योग्यता DAVCMC/State/CBSE के नियमों के अनुसार होगी। विस्तृत विषयवार योग्यता जानकारी के लिए उम्मीदवार आधिकारिक वेबसाइट देखें।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. DAV-CBT परीक्षा: जनवरी 2026 में कंप्यूटर आधारित परीक्षा।
  2. Provisional Eligibility Card (PEC): परीक्षा पास करने वालों को अगला चरण देने की पात्रता के रूप में मिलेगा।
  3. Demo-Interview: PEC धारक उम्मीदवार किसी भी DAV स्कूल में Demo Interview देंगे।
  4. Final Selection: Demo-Interview के प्रदर्शन के आधार पर अंतिम चयन किया जाएगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया (3 चरणों में)

  1. Step 1: Online Registration https://davrecruit.davcmc.in पर जाकर रजिस्टर करें और 31 October 2025 से पहले सबमिट करें।
  2. Step 2: Printout Verification प्रिंटआउट लेकर अपने दस्तावेजों के साथ किसी भी DAV School में जाकर 26 November 2025 से पहले वेरिफाई कराएं।
  3. Step 3: Final Submission स्कूल से वेरिफिकेशन के 7 दिनों के भीतर लॉगिन करके अंतिम सबमिशन करें।
महत्वपूर्ण: बिना स्कूल वेरिफिकेशन और फाइनल सबमिशन के आवेदन मान्य नहीं होगा।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकक्रिया
ऑनलाइन आवेदन पोर्टलयहाँ क्लिक करें
DAV Odisha आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: क्या यह सीधी भर्ती है?
नहीं, यह पहले DAV-CBT परीक्षा के लिए आवेदन है। पास करने के बाद Demo-Interview में भाग ले सकते हैं।
Q2: क्या आवेदन फॉर्म का वेरिफिकेशन जरूरी है?
हाँ, प्रिंटआउट को स्कूल जाकर वेरिफाई कराना आवश्यक है।
Q3: पोस्टिंग कहाँ होगी?
ओडिशा के किसी भी DAV Public School में।
Q4: क्या एक से ज़्यादा पोस्ट के लिए आवेदन संभव है?
हाँ, लेकिन हर पद के लिए अलग फॉर्म भरना होगा।

entry.title: TN Co-op Bank Recruitment 2025: 50 Assistant Posts in Tamil Nadu Apex Co-operative Bank
entry.content:
TN Co-op Bank Recruitment 2025: 50 Assistant Posts in Tamil Nadu Apex Co-operative Bank

The State Recruitment Bureau, Chennai, has issued a notification for the direct recruitment of Assistants in the Tamil Nadu State Apex Co-operative Bank. Only online applications will be accepted.

Post Name: Assistant
Last Date: 31-12-2025
Total Posts: 50

A fantastic opportunity has emerged for young individuals aspiring to build a career in the banking sector. The State Recruitment Bureau (SRB), Chennai, has released a detailed notification (No: 02/2025) for the direct recruitment of 50 vacant posts of Assistant in the Tamil Nadu State Apex Co-operative Bank. This recruitment drive is for Indian citizens who possess the necessary educational and cooperative training qualifications. This post offers a stable and respected career in a prestigious cooperative bank headquartered in Chennai.

Under this recruitment process, candidates will be selected based on a written examination and an interview. The application process is entirely online, and candidates must apply through the official website www.tncoopsrb.in. Applications will commence on December 14, 2025, and will close at 05:45 PM on December 31, 2025. A graduate degree along with Cooperative Training is a mandatory qualification for this recruitment, although certain specific degree holders are exempted. In this article, we will discuss in detail all the important aspects of this recruitment, such as important dates, vacancy details, eligibility criteria, selection process, examination scheme, and how to apply.

Important Dates

Candidates applying for the TN Co-op Bank Assistant Recruitment 2025 should be aware of all the important dates. This is a permanent government job in a cooperative bank, and the location will be Tamil Nadu.

Event Date
Date of Notification 11-12-2025
Commencement of Online Application 14-12-2025
Last Date for Submission of Online Application 31-12-2025 (by 05:45 PM)
Date and Time of Written Examination 24-01-2026 (10:00 AM to 01:00 PM)
Job Type Cooperative Bank (Government)
Job Location Chennai, Tamil Nadu

Vacancy Details

A total of 50 posts of Assistant will be recruited in the Tamil Nadu State Apex Co-operative Bank. The details of these posts are given in the table below:

Post Name Organization Name Number of Posts Pay Scale
Assistant Tamil Nadu State Apex Co-operative Bank 50 ₹ 32,020 - ₹ 96,210
Note: The number of advertised posts is tentative and may change before the final selection list is released. These 50 posts are reserved for various categories such as GT, BC, BCM, MBC/DC, SC, SC(A), as well as for women, destitute widows, ex-servicemen, persons with disabilities, and candidates who have studied in Tamil medium, as per the reservation rules of the Tamil Nadu government.

Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application and examination fee while applying online.

  • SC, SC(A), ST, Destitute Widows (DW), Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PWBD): ₹ 250/-
  • All Other Categories (BC, BCM, MBC/DC, General): ₹ 500/-

The fee can be paid only through online mode (Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI). The fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Assistant post, candidates must meet the following educational qualification and age limit criteria:

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification (as on the date of notification)

The candidate must possess any one of the following qualifications:

  • A Bachelor's Degree in any subject (recognized by UGC).
  • OR, a B.E./B.Tech degree in Computer Science/IT/Electronics and Communication.
  • OR, a Bachelor's Degree in Law.
  • OR, a Bachelor's Degree in Commerce.
  • OR, a B.E./B.Tech degree in Civil or Electrical.

Mandatory Qualification: For all the above degree holders, it is mandatory that they have completed or are currently undergoing Cooperative Training and have knowledge of computer applications.

Exemption from Cooperative Training: Candidates who have obtained degrees like B.Com (Co-op), M.Com (Co-op), M.A. (Co-op), B.A. (Co-op) are exempted from cooperative training, provided they have studied subjects like Book-keeping, Banking, Cooperation, and Auditing in their curriculum.
Age Limit (as on 01-07-2025)

Minimum Age: 18 years for all categories.

Maximum Age Limit:

  • General Category: 32 years. (42 years for PwBD, 50 years for Ex-Servicemen).
  • SC, SC(A), ST, MBC/DC, BC, BCM and Destitute Widows of all castes: No maximum age limit.

Note: "No maximum age limit" means the candidate should not have completed 60 years of age on the date of appointment.
Language Knowledge Candidates must have adequate knowledge of the Tamil language.

Selection Process

The selection process for the TN Co-op Bank Assistant post will be completed in two stages. The final selection of the candidates will be based on the merit list prepared from the total marks obtained in both stages.

  1. Written Examination: An objective-type written examination will be conducted for eligible candidates. This examination will be for a total of 170 marks.
  2. Interview: Candidates shortlisted based on their performance in the written examination will be called for an interview. The interview will be for 30 marks.
  3. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based on the total marks obtained in the written examination (170 marks) and the interview (30 marks), out of a total of 200 marks.

Written Exam Pattern

The written examination for the Assistant post will be objective type (OMR based). The detailed scheme of the examination is given below:

  • Medium of Examination: The question paper will be in both Tamil and English languages.
  • Total Questions: 200
  • Total Marks: 170
  • Duration: 3 hours (180 minutes).
  • Examination Centre: Chennai.
  • Negative Marking: The notification does not mention negative marking.

The subjects for the examination are as follows:

Subject
1. Cooperative Management
2. Cooperative Credit and Banking
3. Cooperative Accounting
4. MIS and Computer Application/Basics of Computer
5. General Knowledge
6. Mental Ability
7. Aptitude and Reasoning
8. Tamil

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

The post of Assistant is an office-based job, so there is no provision for any Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) in this recruitment process.

How to Prepare for the Exam

Success in this competitive examination requires comprehensive preparation, as it includes both general and subject-specific questions.

  1. Focus on Cooperative Subjects: A large part of the syllabus is related to Cooperation. Study the principles of cooperative management, banking, credit, and accounting in depth.
  2. General Studies: Study standard competitive examination books for general knowledge, current affairs, mental ability, and reasoning.
  3. Tamil Language: There will also be questions on the Tamil language and literature, so study Tamil grammar and literature.
  4. Computer Knowledge: Study the basics of MIS and computers, as this is essential for today's banking environment.
  5. Mock Tests: Regularly solve mock tests and previous years' question papers to improve time management and accuracy.

Best Books for Preparation

Selecting the right study material is crucial for good preparation. Here are some recommended books by subject:

  • Cooperative Management and Banking: Books published by the Tamil Nadu Cooperative Union or study material on cooperative management from IGNOU.
  • General Knowledge: Lucent's General Knowledge or Manorama Yearbook.
  • Aptitude and Reasoning: 'Quantitative Aptitude' and 'Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning' by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Tamil: Tamil textbooks from the Tamil Nadu State Board from 6th to 12th grade.
  • Computer Knowledge: A book on computer awareness from Arihant or Lucent publications.

How to Apply

Candidates can apply online only through the official website of the State Recruitment Bureau. Follow the steps below to apply:

  1. First, visit the official website www.tncoopsrb.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the notification for 'Assistant in Tamil Nadu State Apex Co-operative Bank' recruitment.
  3. Click on the "Apply Online" link. Ensure you have a valid email ID and mobile number before applying.
  4. Fill in all the required personal details, educational qualifications, and other information carefully in the application form.
  5. Upload a scanned copy of your recent passport-size photograph and signature as per the instructions.
  6. Upload all required documents (educational, caste, cooperative training certificate, etc.) in PDF format.
  7. Review all the filled-in information before finally submitting the application form.
  8. Finally, pay the application fee as per your category through the online payment gateway.
  9. After successful payment, take a printout of the filled application form and the e-receipt.

Documents Required for Application

The following documents must be scanned and uploaded while applying online:

  1. Passport-size photo and signature.
  2. 10th, 12th marksheets.
  3. Graduate degree certificate and consolidated marksheet.
  4. Cooperative training certificate (or relevant degree certificate for exemption).
  5. Caste certificate (if applicable).
  6. Certificate for studying in Tamil medium (PSTM) (if applicable).
  7. Identity proof (Aadhaar card, etc.).

Important Links

Link Type URL
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
Official Website www.tncoopsrb.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Who can apply for this recruitment?

Answer: Any Indian citizen who has a bachelor's degree in any discipline and has completed cooperative training can apply for this post.

Question 2: Is cooperative training mandatory?

Answer: Yes, cooperative training is mandatory for all graduate degree holders. However, candidates holding a degree in a cooperation subject (like B.Com Co-op, M.A. Co-op) are exempted.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

Answer: The selection process includes a written examination of 170 marks and an interview of 30 marks. Final selection will be based on the total marks obtained out of 200.

Question 4: What is the last date to apply?

Answer: The last date to apply online is December 31, 2025 (by 05:45 PM).

entry.title: Wildlife Institute of India (WII) भर्ती 2025: Technician, Cook और Lab Attendant के पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
Wildlife Institute of India (WII) भर्ती 2025: Technician, Cook और Lab Attendant के पदों पर भर्ती!

Wildlife Institute of India, देहरादून में 10वीं और 12वीं पास उम्मीदवारों के लिए सुनहरा अवसर।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! Wildlife Institute of India (WII), देहरादून — जो कि Ministry of Environment, Forests and Climate Change के अंतर्गत आता है, ने Technician, Lab Attendant और Cook के पदों पर भर्ती हेतु अधिसूचना (Advertisement No. WII/ADM/2025/60) जारी की है। इस भर्ती के तहत नियमित (Regular) और बैकलॉग (Backlog) दोनों प्रकार की रिक्तियाँ भरी जाएँगी।

यह उन उम्मीदवारों के लिए सुनहरा मौका है जो Wildlife Conservation और Research के क्षेत्र में करियर बनाना चाहते हैं। आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से ऑफ़लाइन है। नीचे दी गई जानकारी में पूरी भर्ती का विवरण दिया गया है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनाअंतिम तिथि
आवेदन प्राप्त होने की अंतिम तिथि18 नवम्बर 2025
दूरदराज़ क्षेत्रों के लिए अंतिम तिथि25 नवम्बर 2025

पदों का विवरण और वेतन

इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत कुल 06 पद भरे जाएँगे, जिनमें Regular और Backlog दोनों प्रकार की रिक्तियाँ शामिल हैं।

Regular Vacancies

पद का नामपदों की संख्यावेतन स्तर
Technician (Audio Visual)01 (SC-1)Level-2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200)
