Lab Attendant03 (OBC-1, SC-2)Level-1 (₹18,000 - ₹56,900)

Backlog Vacancies (पिछले वर्षों की रिक्तियाँ)

पद का नामपदों की संख्यावेतन स्तर
Cook02 (OBC-1, SC-1)Level-2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200)

पदवार योग्यता एवं आयु सीमा

Technician (Audio Visual)

  • योग्यता: 10वीं पास 60% अंकों के साथ तथा Computer Science / IT / Digital Photography / Video Editing / Sound Recording / Visual Communication में 2 वर्ष का Diploma।
  • आयु सीमा: 18 से 28 वर्ष।

Lab Attendant

  • योग्यता: 12वीं (Science) 60% अंकों के साथ पास। या 10वीं 60% अंकों के साथ पास तथा Library Science / Lab Technology / IT में 2 वर्ष का प्रमाणपत्र / डिप्लोमा।
  • आयु सीमा: 18 से 28 वर्ष।

Cook

  • योग्यता: हाई स्कूल पास तथा Cookery या Culinary Arts में Degree / Diploma।
  • वांछनीय (Desirable): किसी मान्यता प्राप्त होटल या संस्थान में 2 वर्ष का अनुभव।
  • आयु सीमा: 18 से 27 वर्ष।
आयु में छूट: SC/ST/OBC उम्मीदवारों को सरकार के नियमों के अनुसार छूट दी जाएगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  • Technician & Lab Attendant: Tier-I लिखित परीक्षा (100 अंक) जिसमें सामान्य ज्ञान, गणित, तर्कशक्ति और भाषा शामिल होगी। इसके बाद Tier-II Skill Test (50 अंक) होगा।
  • Cook: Paper-I (30 अंक) और Paper-II Skill/Trade Test (70 अंक)।
  • Negative Marking: प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर के लिए ¼ अंक काटे जाएँगे।

सभी परीक्षाएँ केवल देहरादून में आयोजित होंगी।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया (ऑफ़लाइन)

  1. Official Notification में दिए गए Annexure-III आवेदन फॉर्म को डाउनलोड करें।
  2. फॉर्म को साफ-सुथरे तरीके से भरें।
  3. ₹700/- का Demand Draft बनवाएँ जो “The Director, Wildlife Institute of India, Dehradun” के नाम पर हो। (SC/ST/PwBD/महिला उम्मीदवारों को शुल्क नहीं देना है)।
  4. सभी आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ों की self-attested प्रतियाँ संलग्न करें।
  5. आवेदन और दस्तावेज़ों को एक लिफ़ाफ़े में रखें, जिस पर स्पष्ट रूप से लिखा हो — “Application for the post of _______”.
  6. लिफ़ाफ़ा Speed Post / Registered Post द्वारा इस पते पर भेजें:
    The Registrar, Wildlife Institute of India, Chandrabani, Dehradun - 248001, Uttarakhand

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
अधिसूचना एवं आवेदन फॉर्मयहाँ से डाउनलोड करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: आवेदन प्रक्रिया ऑनलाइन है या ऑफ़लाइन?
यह प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से ऑफ़लाइन है। उम्मीदवारों को फॉर्म डाउनलोड कर डाक द्वारा भेजना होगा।
प्रश्न 2: आवेदन शुल्क कितना है?
सामान्य, OBC और EWS पुरुष उम्मीदवारों के लिए ₹700/- शुल्क है। SC, ST, PwBD और सभी महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए शुल्क नहीं है।
प्रश्न 3: परीक्षा कहाँ होगी?
सभी परीक्षाएँ केवल देहरादून में आयोजित होंगी।
प्रश्न 4: क्या एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन किया जा सकता है?
हाँ, लेकिन प्रत्येक पद के लिए अलग आवेदन फॉर्म और अलग Demand Draft भेजना होगा।

entry.title: Goa GSSC Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancy for 180+ Group 'C' Posts, Check Full Details Here!
entry.content:
Goa GSSC Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancy for 180+ Group 'C' Posts, Check Full Details Here!

Apply for Junior Geologist, Clerk, Inspector, and other posts in various departments of the Goa Government.

Post Name: Searcher, Junior Geologist, Aval Karkun, Laboratory Assistant, Talathi, etc.
Last Date: 31 December 2025
Total Posts: 180+

A major opportunity has emerged for youths seeking government jobs in Goa. The Goa Staff Selection Commission (GSSC) has released a detailed notification via Advertisement No. 03/2025 for the recruitment of several Group 'C' posts in various departments of the state government. This recruitment drive is a fantastic chance for candidates who are graduates, diploma holders, or 12th pass and wish to build a stable career with the Government of Goa.

Under this recruitment, appointments will be made for more than 25 types of posts in various departments such as Police, Fisheries, Mines and Geology, Tourism, and Revenue, including positions like Junior Geologist, Laboratory Assistant, Talathi, Inspector, and Clerk. The application process is entirely online and will run until December 31, 2025. The selection will be conducted through a competitive Computer Based Test (CBT), which will include two tiers (CBT-I and CBT-II). In this detailed article, we will provide you with complete information about the key posts, eligibility, selection process, exam pattern, and application procedure.

Important Points

Details Information
Date of Advertisement Publication 05 December 2025
Last Date for Online Application 31 December 2025
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Goa Government)
Job Location Goa

Key Vacancy Details

GSSC has announced more than 180 posts across various departments. Here are the details of some of the key posts:

Post Name Department Number of Posts Pay Level
Laboratory Assistant Various Departments 47 Level-4 and Level-3
Talathi Collectorate (North & South Goa) 30 Level-2
Investigator Directorate of Planning, Statistics and Evaluation 27 Level-4
Village Panchayat Secretary Directorate of Panchayat 26 Level-3
Laboratory Technician Public Works Department and others 18 Level-5 and Level-4
Aval Karkun Collectorate, South Goa 12 Level-5
Accounts Clerk Directorate of Accounts 12 Level-4
Junior Geologist Directorate of Mines and Geology 07 Level-6
Note: This is only a list of some of the key posts. For detailed post-wise, category-wise vacancies and reservation information, please refer to the official notification.

Application Fee

  • For posts in Level 4 and above: ₹400 for UR, ₹200 for SC/ST/OBC/PwD/EWS.
  • For posts in Level 1 to 3: ₹200 for UR, ₹100 for SC/ST/OBC/PwD/EWS.

The fee is to be paid through online mode only (Internet Banking/Debit/Credit Card/UPI).

Eligibility Criteria

Each post has specific educational qualifications and experience requirements. However, there are some common conditions for all posts:

Criteria Requirement
Mandatory Language Knowledge Knowledge of the Konkani language is mandatory for all candidates.
Desirable Language Knowledge For almost all posts, knowledge of the Marathi language is desirable.
Age Limit Maximum 45 years. (Relaxation up to 5 years for government servants). Relaxation for reserved categories is also applicable as per rules.
Domicile Certificate Candidates must possess a 15-year residence certificate in Goa.
Employment Exchange Registration The candidate's name must be registered with the Employment Exchange in Goa.

Educational Qualifications for some key posts:

  • Talathi/Accounts Clerk/Village Panchayat Secretary: Bachelor's Degree in any discipline + at least a 6-month computer course + 1 year of work experience.
  • Junior Geologist: Bachelor of Science (B.Sc.) in Geology.
  • Laboratory Assistant/Technician: Degree in the relevant subject (e.g., Science/Chemistry/Physics).
  • Inspector: Preference for a degree in Science/Commerce/Economics.

Selection Process

The selection process will involve a two-tier Computer Based Test (CBT):

  1. CBT - I (Tier-I): This will be a general aptitude test with questions from English, Numerical Ability, Reasoning, and Konkani language. This test will be for a total of 60 marks and the duration will be 75 minutes.
  2. CBT - II (Tier-II): Candidates who obtain the cut-off marks in CBT-I will be called for CBT-II. This test will be for 80 marks and the duration will be 100 minutes. It will include general subjects, the history of Goa, data interpretation, and post-specific (Subject Specific) questions.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in CBT-II.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

CBT-I (Tier-I) Exam Pattern

SubjectMarks
English15
Numerical Ability and Reasoning15
General Knowledge15
Konkani Language15
Total60

Duration: 75 Minutes

CBT-II (Tier-II) Exam Pattern

SubjectMarks
History of Goa and Goa Liberation Struggle20
Data Interpretation, Logical Reasoning, and Konkani Language20
Subject/Post Specific Questions40
Total80

Duration: 100 Minutes

Detailed Syllabus Outline

  • English: Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension, Sentence Structure, Synonyms, Antonyms.
  • Numerical Ability & Reasoning: Number Systems, Percentage, Profit & Loss, Simple & Compound Interest, Time & Work, Data Interpretation, Series, Coding-Decoding, Puzzles.
  • Konkani Language: Grammar, Vocabulary, Reading Comprehension, Translation.
  • History of Goa: Ancient and modern history of Goa, the Portuguese rule, Goa's freedom struggle.
  • Subject-Specific: The syllabus will be based on the essential qualification for the post (e.g., Geology for Junior Geologist, Accounting principles for Accounts Clerk).

How to Prepare for the GSSC Exam

A smart preparation strategy is crucial for success in the GSSC exam:

  1. Focus on Languages: Konkani is a significant part of CBT-I. A good knowledge of Konkani grammar and vocabulary is essential. Also, practice English language and comprehension regularly.
  2. General Knowledge of Goa: "History of Goa" is a key section in CBT-II. Study the history, culture, geography, and current events of Goa.
  3. Subject-Specific Preparation: If you are applying for a technical post like Junior Geologist or Laboratory Assistant, revise your graduation-level syllabus thoroughly.
  4. Practice and Mock Tests: Regular practice is necessary for subjects like Numerical Ability and Reasoning. Take online mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right study material is important for good preparation. Here are some recommended books and sources by subject:

SubjectRecommended Books/Sources
English and Numerical Ability
  • "Objective General English" - S.P. Bakshi
  • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" - R.S. Aggarwal
Reasoning
  • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" - R.S. Aggarwal
Konkani Language
  • Konkani language grammar books and local newspapers.
  • Goa Board school textbooks.
History and GK of Goa
  • "Know Your State - Goa" by Arihant Publications.
  • Official websites and publications of the Government of Goa.

How to Apply

The application process is only through the GSSC's online portal:

  1. Go to the official website of the Goa Staff Selection Commission: https://gssc.goa.gov.in.
  2. Click on the "Apply Online" or "Advertisements" section.
  3. Select Advertisement No. 03/2025 and click on the post you want to apply for.
  4. Register yourself and fill out the application form carefully with all the required information.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other necessary documents.
  6. Pay the online application fee.
  7. Review your application before final submission and then submit it. Take a printout of the application form for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep scanned copies of the following documents ready while applying online:

  1. Latest passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Educational qualification certificates (Degree/Diploma).
  3. Proof of knowledge of Konkani language.
  4. 15-year residence certificate in Goa.
  5. Employment Exchange registration card.
  6. Caste/Category certificate (if applicable).
  7. Proof of date of birth (10th certificate).

Important Links

Link Type Anchor Text
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Download Here
Official Website gssc.goa.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Can candidates from outside Goa apply?

No, a 15-year residence certificate in Goa and knowledge of the Konkani language are mandatory for these posts, so this recruitment is primarily for the residents of Goa.

Question 2: How many stages are there in the selection process?

There are two stages in the selection process - CBT-I (Qualifying) and CBT-II (for Merit). Both are computer-based tests.

Question 3: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for more than one post separately if you are eligible. The commission may conduct a common CBT for posts with similar qualifications, the details of which will be provided later.

Question 4: How will the knowledge of the Konkani language be certified?

Generally, knowledge of the Konkani language is certified through your educational qualifications (e.g., as a subject in 10th/12th). Additionally, there is a separate section for the Konkani language in CBT-I.

entry.title: NABARD Young Professional Program 2025-26: A Gateway to a Career in Rural Development
entry.content:
NABARD Young Professional Program 2025-26: A Gateway to a Career in Rural Development

India's apex development bank invites applications for its prestigious Young Professionals program on a contractual basis. Apply online from December 26, 2025, to January 12, 2026.

Post Name: Young Professional (YP) in various disciplines
Last Date: 12 January 2026
Total Posts: Multiple Vacancies across 14 disciplines

The National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD), an all-India apex organization fully owned by the Government of India, has released its much-awaited notification (Advertisement No. 07) for the engagement of Young Professionals (YPs) for the 2025-26 cycle. This program is a unique opportunity for young, dynamic, and qualified individuals to gain invaluable exposure to policy research, program evaluation, and strategic initiatives in the critical sectors of rural development and agricultural finance. The engagement is purely on a contractual basis, designed to nurture talent and provide a launchpad for a successful career in development banking and policy-making.

NABARD, as the country's leading development financial institution, plays a pivotal role in shaping the rural economy. The Young Professional Program is designed to bring fresh perspectives and specialized skills into the organization. YPs will get the chance to work at NABARD's Head Office in Mumbai as well as in various Regional Offices across the country, contributing to diverse fields such as Climate Action, Data Science, Finance, Information Technology, and more. The application process is entirely online through the official NABARD website, www.nabard.org, and will be open from December 26, 2025, to January 12, 2026. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all essential aspects of the program, including vacancies, eligibility, selection process, and remuneration.

Important Points

This is a contractual engagement with one of India's premier financial institutions, offering a chance to work on impactful projects across the country. Key dates and details are as follows:

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)
Program Name NABARD Young Professional Program (On Contract) - 2025-26
Job Type Contractual (Full-time basis for 1 year, extendable up to 3 years)
Job Location Mumbai (Head Office) and various other state capitals/cities in India
Online Application & Fee Payment Dates From 26 December 2025 to 12 January 2026

Vacancy Details

NABARD has announced vacancies for Young Professionals across 14 different disciplines. Candidates are permitted to apply for **only one discipline/location**. The discipline-wise vacancies and their suggested places of posting are detailed below:

Discipline Total Vacancy Location-wise Breakdown
Climate Action & Sustainability 3 Head Office, Mumbai (1); Uttar Pradesh, Lucknow (1); Himachal Pradesh, Shimla (1)
Economics 3 Head Office, Mumbai (2); Jharkhand, Ranchi (1)
Data Science 4 Head Office, Mumbai (3); Uttarakhand, Dehradun (1)
Cyber Security 1 Head Office, Mumbai (1)
Academic Administration 2 Uttar Pradesh, Lucknow (1); Karnataka, Mangaluru (1)
Graphic Designing 1 Uttar Pradesh, Lucknow (1)
PR, Outreach and Documentation 2 Head Office, Mumbai (1); Assam, Guwahati (1)
Information Technology 12 Head Office, Mumbai (9); Assam, Guwahati (1); Mizoram, Aizawl (1); West Bengal, Kolkata (1)
Geoinformatics 1 Himachal Pradesh, Shimla (1)
Development Management 3 Uttar Pradesh, Lucknow (1); New Delhi (2)
Project Monitoring 4 Head Office, Mumbai (1); Tamil Nadu, Chennai (2); Haryana, Chandigarh (1)
Finance 6 Head Office, Mumbai (6)
UI/UX Designing 1 Head Office, Mumbai (1)
Software Testing 1 Head Office, Mumbai (1)
Total Vacancies (Approx.) 43+
Note: The number of vacancies is indicative and may vary. Applying for multiple disciplines will lead to the cancellation of candidature.

Remuneration and Application Fee

  • Monthly Stipend: Young Professionals will be paid a consolidated monthly stipend of ₹70,000/- (all-inclusive). Applicable income tax will be deducted at the source.
  • Application Fee: The Application Fee cum Intimation Charges (exclusive of GST) for the post is Rs. 150/-. The fee is non-refundable.

This program provides an excellent entry-point remuneration for young professionals looking to gain experience in the development sector.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the program, candidates must satisfy the eligibility criteria regarding educational qualification, work experience, age, and nationality as of November 1, 2025.

Criteria Requirement Details
Educational Qualification & Work Experience Candidates must possess the required educational qualification and work experience for their chosen discipline as detailed in Annexure-I of the official notification. The final result of the required qualification must have been declared on or before 01-11-2025. A brief overview for some disciplines:
  • Finance: BBA/BMS (Finance/Banking) with 60% marks OR PG Diploma/MBA in Finance with 55% marks OR Bachelor's degree with ICAI membership. Minimum 1 year of relevant experience.
  • Data Science: B.E./B.Tech in IT/Computer Science/AI & Data Science with 60% marks. Minimum 1 year of experience, with preference for those who have worked on banking-related projects.
  • Information Technology: B.E./B.Tech in CS/IT OR PG Degree in CS/IT/Applications with 55% marks. Minimum 1 year of experience in a reputed organization.
  • Economics: Post Graduate Degree in Economics/Applied Economics/Statistics/Data Science with 55% marks. Minimum 1 year of relevant experience.
(For a detailed list of qualifications for all 14 disciplines, please refer to Annexure-I of the official notification).
Age & Nationality
  • The candidate must be an Indian citizen.
  • The candidate must be between 21 and 30 years of age as of 01-11-2025.
  • This means the candidate must have been born not earlier than 01-11-1995 and not later than 01-11-2004.

Selection Process

The selection will be a direct, two-step process aimed at identifying candidates with the right academic background, relevant experience, and aptitude for the role. There is no written examination.

  1. Application Screening: All applications received will be placed before a Screening Committee. Candidates will be screened and shortlisted for the interview based on a matrix of identified indicators. These indicators include academic performance, relevance of work experience, overall profile of the candidate, and the statement of intent provided in the application.
  2. Interview: The shortlisted applications will be placed before the Young Professional Selection Committee (YPSC). The final selection will be based on the combined marks obtained in a Presentation and Interview conducted by the YPSC. Candidates will be interviewed at the respective NABARD Regional Offices for which the vacancies have been announced. Travel expenses (2AC rail fare) may be reimbursed for attending the interview.
  3. Medical Fitness: Appointment of selected applicants is subject to their being declared medically fit by a Medical Officer approved by the Bank.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for the NABARD Young Professional Program. The selection is based on shortlisting of applications followed by an interview and presentation. Therefore, candidates do not need to prepare for a specific exam pattern or syllabus. The focus of preparation should be on the interview and the presentation topic (which may be given on the spot or in advance).

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these posts. These are professional, office-based roles.

How to Prepare

Since the selection hinges on the application and interview, your preparation should be focused and strategic.

  1. Craft an Excellent Application: Your application is your first impression.
    • Resume/CV: Ensure your CV is up-to-date, professional, and highlights your achievements, especially those relevant to the discipline you are applying for.
    • Statement of Intent: This is a critical component. Write a compelling statement that clearly articulates your motivation for joining the program, your understanding of rural development, and how your skills can contribute to NABARD's objectives.
  2. Prepare for the Interview and Presentation:
    • Domain Knowledge: Thoroughly revise the core concepts of your graduation/post-graduation discipline. Be prepared for in-depth technical questions.
    • About NABARD: Research NABARD's functions, recent schemes, flagship programs (e.g., RIDF, SHG-BLP), and its role in the rural economy.
    • Current Affairs: Stay updated with current events, especially related to agriculture, rural development, banking, and the Indian economy.
    • Presentation Skills: Practice your presentation skills. Be prepared to speak on a topic related to your domain or a current issue in rural development. Structure your presentation logically and communicate your ideas clearly.

Best Books for Preparation

While there's no written test, staying informed and knowledgeable is key for the interview. Here are some recommended resources:

  • For Rural Development & Agriculture: "Indian Economy" by Ramesh Singh (especially the agriculture and rural development sections), NABARD's Annual Reports, and publications from the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare.
  • For Economics & Finance: "Indian Economy" by Uma Kapila, RBI's Annual Report, and the Economic Survey of India.
  • For Current Affairs: Regular reading of newspapers like The Hindu or The Indian Express, and magazines like Pratiyogita Darpan or Kurukshetra.
  • For Domain-Specific Knowledge: Standard textbooks from your respective degree are the best source.

How to Apply

Candidates can apply online only from December 26, 2025, to January 12, 2026. The application process involves registration, fee payment, and document upload.

  1. Visit the NABARD website www.nabard.org and navigate to "Career Notices". Click on the option "APPLY ONLINE" for the Young Professional Program.
  2. Choose the tab "Click here for New Registration" and enter your Name, Contact details, and Email ID. A Provisional Registration Number and Password will be generated.
  3. Log in and carefully fill the online application form. Save your data using the "SAVE AND NEXT" tab if you cannot complete the form in one go.
  4. Upload your scanned photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration as per the specified guidelines.
  5. Pay the application fee of Rs. 150/- (plus GST) through the online payment gateway.
  6. Verify all details in the preview before clicking on 'COMPLETE REGISTRATION'. Once submitted, no changes can be made. Take a printout of the e-receipt and the final application form.

Documents Needed to Apply

Before applying online, candidates should scan and keep ready the following documents as per the specifications given in the advertisement:

  1. Photograph (4.5cm × 3.5cm)
  2. Signature (with black ink)
  3. Left thumb impression (on white paper with black or blue ink)
  4. A handwritten declaration in English only: "I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."
  5. Latest C.V./Resume in .pdf format (up to 500 KB).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Download from Official Website
Candidate Grievance Portal https://cgrs.ibps.in/

FAQs

Question 1: Is this a permanent government job?

No, this is a contractual engagement for an initial period of one year, which can be extended up to a maximum of three years based on performance and organizational requirements.

Question 2: Is there a written exam for this recruitment?

No, the selection is based on screening of applications followed by an interview and presentation. There is no written test.

Question 3: Can I apply for more than one discipline?

No, a candidate is permitted to apply for only one discipline/location. Multiple applications will lead to the cancellation of candidature.

Question 4: What is the monthly salary for a Young Professional?

The Young Professional will be paid a consolidated monthly stipend of ₹70,000/- (all-inclusive).

entry.title: HPRCA Recruitment for 234 Junior Office Assistant (IT) Posts
entry.content:
HPRCA Recruitment for 234 Junior Office Assistant (IT) Posts

Advertisement No. 01/2026: The Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA), Hamirpur, has announced a major recruitment drive for 234 posts of Junior Office Assistant (IT) as Job Trainees. Online applications are invited from eligible candidates who have passed 10+2 or have relevant ITI/Diploma qualifications. The online application portal will be open from January 27, 2026, to February 27, 2026.

Post Name: Junior Office Assistant (IT) (Job-Trainee)
Last Date: 27 February 2026
Total Posts: 234

The Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA) has released its first major notification of the year, inviting online applications for a large-scale recruitment of Junior Office Assistant (IT) personnel. A total of 234 vacancies will be filled on a Job Trainee basis under the Directorate of Recruitment, Shimla. This is a significant opportunity for candidates with a 10+2 qualification and computer proficiency to secure a government position in Himachal Pradesh. The selected candidates will be engaged as Job Trainees with a consolidated fixed monthly amount of ₹12,500.

The selection process is a two-stage procedure involving a Computer Based Test (CBT) or a written screening test, followed by a mandatory but qualifying Typing Skill Test. The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the CBT. Candidates must register through the One Time Registration (OTR) portal on the official HPRCA website before filling out the application. The application window is open for one month, and aspirants are advised to apply well in advance to avoid any last-minute technical issues.

HPRCA JOA (IT) Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must review the key dates and details to ensure a successful application. All applications must be submitted online through the HPRCA portal.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA), Hamirpur
Advertisement Number 01/2026
Post Name Junior Office Assistant (IT) (Job-Trainee)
Total Vacancies 234
Application Start Date 27 January 2026 (10:00 AM)
Application Last Date 27 February 2026 (11:59 PM)
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Government Job (Trainee Basis)
Job Location Himachal Pradesh
Official Website https://hprca.hp.gov.in

Vacancy Details

The recruitment for 234 posts of Junior Office Assistant (IT) is for the Directorate of Recruitment, HP, Shimla-2 (Post Code-26001). The category-wise breakdown is as follows:

Category Number of Vacancies
General (UR) 84
EWS 30
General (WFF - Ward of Freedom Fighter) 03
SC (UR) 45
SC (BPL) 09
SC (WFF) 03
ST (UR) 09
ST (BPL) 03
OBC (UR) 39
OBC (BPL) 09
Total 234
Note: The number of posts is tentative and may increase or decrease. Candidates must apply under their respective categories.

Examination and Processing Fees

  • For All Categories: ₹800/- (₹100 as examination fee and ₹700 as processing fee).
  • Correction Fee: ₹100/- (to be paid if a candidate uses the correction window).

The fee must be paid online through the payment gateway using a Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking. No other mode of payment is acceptable.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 27.02.2026)

Candidates must ensure they fulfill all eligibility conditions regarding age, education, and skills as of the closing date of the application.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification A candidate must possess one of the following qualifications:
  1. 10+2 Examination from a recognized Board of School Education/University.
    OR
  2. Matriculation from a recognized Board with a one/two-year Diploma/Certificate from an Industrial Training Institute (ITI) in Information Technology (IT) & ITES.
    OR
  3. Three-year Diploma in Computer Engineering/Computer Science/IT from a Polytechnic approved by AICTE.
Domicile Condition: The candidate must have passed Matriculation and 10+2 from any School/Institution located within Himachal Pradesh. This condition does not apply to Bonafide Himachalis.
Skill Qualification Computer typing speed of 30 words per minute in English or 25 words per minute in Hindi. (Visually impaired and certain differently-abled persons are exempted as per rules).
Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026)
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 45 Years
  • Age Relaxation: An upper age limit relaxation of up to 5 years is applicable for candidates belonging to SC, ST, OBC, PwD, and other categories who are domiciles of Himachal Pradesh, as per government rules.
Desirable Qualification Knowledge of customs, manners, and dialects of Himachal Pradesh and suitability for appointment in the peculiar conditions of the state.

Selection Process

The selection will be conducted in two main stages. The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks of the first stage only.

  1. Stage 1: Computer Based Test (CBT) / Written Screening Test: All eligible candidates will appear for an objective-type screening test worth 120 marks. The minimum qualifying marks are 45% for the General category and 40% for reserved categories (relaxable by 5% if needed).
  2. Stage 2: Typing Skill Test: Candidates who qualify in the CBT will be shortlisted for a Typing Skill Test. The number of candidates shortlisted will be four times the number of advertised posts. This test is mandatory and qualifying in nature.
  3. Final Merit and Document Verification: The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the CBT for those candidates who qualify for the Typing Skill Test. Shortlisted candidates will then be called for document verification.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The examination will consist of an objective test followed by a skill test.

Computer Based Test (CBT) Pattern:

  • Mode: Objective type with Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs).
  • Total Questions: 120.
  • Total Marks: 120 (Each question carries 1 mark).
  • Duration: 1 hour 30 minutes (90 minutes).

Syllabus for the CBT:

  1. Syllabus related to Computer Science/Computer Application/IT/ITES/IP as prescribed in the essential qualifications.
  2. Subjects of 10+2 level as per the basic educational qualification.
  3. General Knowledge: Including General Knowledge of Himachal Pradesh and Current Affairs.
  4. Other Subjects: Everyday Science, Logical Reasoning, Social Science, General English, and General Hindi of Matric Standard.

Typing Skill Test Pattern:

  • Speed Requirement: 30 words per minute in English OR 25 words per minute in Hindi.
  • Nature: The test is mandatory and qualifying. No marks from this test will be added to the final merit list.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for the post of Junior Office Assistant (IT).

How to Prepare for the Exam

A systematic preparation strategy is key to cracking this competitive exam.

  • Master the IT Syllabus: The major part of the exam will be from the IT/Computer domain. Thoroughly revise the syllabus corresponding to your qualification (10+2 with IT, ITI, or Diploma). Focus on fundamentals of computers, MS Office, computer networks, operating systems, and basic programming concepts.
  • Prepare for General Section: Dedicate specific time to study Himachal Pradesh GK, current affairs, and other general subjects like Science, Social Science, Hindi, and English.
  • Practice Typing Daily: The typing test is a crucial qualifying stage. Start practicing typing in both English and Hindi daily to achieve the required speed with high accuracy. Use online typing tutors and practice on a standard keyboard.
  • Solve Mock Tests: Attempt previous year question papers of HPSSC/HPRCA JOA (IT) exams and take online mock tests to understand the exam pattern, manage your time effectively, and identify your weak areas.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for building a strong foundation.

  • Computer/IT: "Objective Computer Awareness" by Arihant Experts or "Lucent's Computer". For in-depth knowledge, refer to your ITI/Diploma/10+2 textbooks.
  • Himachal Pradesh GK: "The Wonderland Himachal Pradesh" by Jag Mohan Balokhra or "Himachal Pradesh General Knowledge" by Janta Publications.
  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs: A standard yearly publication like Manorama Yearbook and a daily newspaper.
  • Reasoning, English & Hindi: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal and standard high school grammar books for languages.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online and involves a One Time Registration (OTR) system.

  1. Step 1 (Sign Up): Visit the HPRCA portal at https://hprca.hp.gov.in and click on "Sign Up" to create a username and password.
  2. Step 2 (One-Time Registration): After signing up, complete the OTR by providing your personal information, educational qualifications, experience, and uploading your photograph, signature, and other documents.
  3. Step 3 (Profile Update): Ensure your profile is complete and up-to-date before applying.
  4. Step 4 (Apply for Post): Log in to the portal, view the list of available posts, and click the "Apply" button next to "Junior Office Assistant (IT)".
  5. Step 5 (Choose Exam District): Select your preferred district for the examination from the dropdown menu.
  6. Step 6 (Confirm Details): The application form will be auto-filled from your OTR profile. Review the details carefully.
  7. Step 7 (Pay Fee): Proceed to pay the application fee of ₹800 through the online payment gateway.
  8. Step 8 (Submission Confirmation): After successful payment, your application will be considered complete. You can download the submitted form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

Candidates must have scanned copies of the following documents ready for the One Time Registration (OTR) and application process:

  1. Latest passport-size photograph (not older than one month, max 1 MB).
  2. Scanned signature (max 1 MB).
  3. Matriculation (10th) certificate for age proof.
  4. All educational qualification certificates and mark sheets (10+2, ITI, Diploma).
  5. Bonafide Himachali certificate (if applicable).
  6. Valid Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC) on parental basis, EWS, or BPL certificate as per the prescribed format.
  7. Other relevant supporting documents.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Website & Application Portal Click Here
Official Notification Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the last date to apply for the HPRCA JOA (IT) recruitment?

The last date for submission of the Online Recruitment Application (ORA) is February 27, 2026, up to 11:59 PM.

Q2: Is the typing test mandatory for all candidates?

Yes, the typing skill test is mandatory for all shortlisted candidates and is qualifying in nature. However, certain categories of differently-abled persons are exempted as per government rules.

Q3: I am from Punjab but I have completed my 10+2 from a school in Himachal Pradesh. Am I eligible?

Yes, you are eligible to apply as you meet the domicile condition of having passed 10+2 from within Himachal Pradesh. However, you will be treated as a General (Unreserved) category candidate.

Q4: What is the salary for this post?

The post is for a Job Trainee, and the selected candidates will receive a consolidated fixed amount of ₹12,500/- per month during the training period.

entry.title: TMC Recruitment 2025: Apply for Nurse, Security Officer, Cook, and Other Posts
entry.content:
TMC Recruitment 2025: Massive Job Opportunities at Tata Memorial Centre

Join the prestigious Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) institution - Detailed guide on eligibility, syllabus, and application.

Post Name: Nurse A, Stenographer, Assistant Security Officer, Cook, Warden, Kitchen Supervisor, Driver, Attendant
Last Date: November 14, 2025
Total Posts: Multiple Vacancies (Notification No. TMC/AD/123/2025)

The Tata Memorial Centre (TMC), Mumbai, is a globally recognized leader in cancer research and treatment. Functioning as a Grant-in-Aid institution under the administrative control of the Department of Atomic Energy (DAE), Government of India, TMC has consistently set benchmarks in oncology care. This year, the institution has opened its doors for a massive recruitment drive for the year 2025. Under advertisement number TMC/AD/123/2025, the center is looking for highly motivated, skilled, and dedicated individuals to fill various non-teaching positions. These roles are critical for the smooth functioning of the hospital and research wings, ranging from clinical roles like Female Nurse ‘A’ to administrative roles like Stenographers and essential services like Cooks, Drivers, and Security Officers.

Working at the Tata Memorial Centre is more than just a job; it is a commitment to the service of humanity. As one of the oldest and largest cancer centers in the world, TMC provides an environment of continuous learning and professional excellence. The 2025 recruitment drive is particularly significant as it targets a wide range of academic backgrounds, from 10th-standard pass-outs to specialized nursing graduates. The positions offered are permanent and carry the weight and benefits of a central government-aided career. With competitive salary packages based on the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), these roles ensure financial stability while offering a profound sense of purpose. Candidates from all over India are eligible to apply, provided they meet the specific criteria mentioned for each post.

In this comprehensive guide, we will delve deep into the 2025 recruitment cycle. We will cover the specific qualifications required for each post, the intricate details of the selection process, and a strategic approach to the written and practical examinations. With the application deadline fast approaching on November 14, 2025, it is imperative for aspirants to understand the nuances of the recruitment portal, the document verification process, and the physical standards required for security roles. This article serves as a 2000-word blueprint to help you navigate through the complexities of the TMC recruitment and secure your place in this prestigious organization.

Important Points

The TMC 2025 recruitment drive is a centralized process. While the primary vacancies are for the Mumbai units (Tata Memorial Hospital and ACTREC), selected candidates may be posted to any of the regional centers across India, including those in Varanasi, Sangrur, New Mullanpur, and Vizag. The job type is Government (Grant-in-Aid), which means employees enjoy benefits similar to central government employees.

Event / Feature Description
Notification Date October 2025
Application Deadline November 14, 2025 (up to 05:30 PM)
Job Location Mumbai (with Pan-India liability)
Nature of Employment Permanent / Government-aided
Official Website tmc.gov.in

Vacancy Details and Pay Scales

The vacancy list for 2025 is diverse, ensuring opportunities for candidates with different skill sets. The pay scales are strictly as per the 7th CPC, making these some of the most lucrative positions in the public healthcare sector. Below is the breakdown of the posts and their corresponding pay levels.

Post Name Pay Level & Basic Pay
Female Nurse 'A' Level 7 (Initial Basic Rs. 44,900)
Assistant Security Officer Level 6 (Initial Basic Rs. 35,400)
Female Warden Level 6 (Initial Basic Rs. 35,400)
Kitchen Supervisor Level 6 (Initial Basic Rs. 35,400)
Stenographer Level 4 (Initial Basic Rs. 25,500)
Cook 'A' Level 2 (Initial Basic Rs. 19,900)
Driver Level 2 (Initial Basic Rs. 19,900)
Attendant / Trade Helper Level 1 (Initial Basic Rs. 18,000)
Total Vacancies Refer to Official PDF for Count
Note: In addition to the basic pay, selected candidates will be entitled to allowances such as DA, HRA, and Transport Allowance as per the prevailing government rules at the location of posting.

Application Fee Details

  • General, OBC, and EWS Candidates: Rs. 300/- (Three Hundred Only).
  • SC / ST Candidates: No fee (Exempted).
  • Female Candidates: No fee (Exempted).
  • Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD): No fee (Exempted).
  • Ex-Servicemen: No fee (For the first application).

The fee must be paid online through the integrated payment gateway. Please ensure that the transaction is successful before closing the window. No other mode of payment (like Demand Draft or Postal Order) will be accepted by TMC.

Eligibility Criteria

TMC maintains a high standard for its staff. Each post has specific educational requirements and mandatory experience. The experience must be post-qualification, meaning any experience gained before completing the degree/diploma will not be counted.

Post Designation Qualifications & Experience Required
Nurse 'A' (Female) B.Sc. Nursing OR GNM with Oncology Diploma. Must be registered with INC/State Council. Experience: 1 year in a 50+ bed hospital.
Stenographer Graduate from any recognized university. Skills: 80 wpm shorthand and 40 wpm English typing on computer. Must have a computer course certificate.
Security Officer Graduate with NCC 'C' certificate and 5 years experience for civilians. 15 years service for Ex-Servicemen.
Cook 'A' 10th Standard pass + Certificate/Diploma in Food Production/Bakery. Experience: 2 years in a large kitchen or hotel.
Driver 10th Standard pass + Valid Heavy Vehicle Driving License. Experience: 2 years in driving heavy vehicles.

Selection Process

The selection process at Tata Memorial Centre is designed to evaluate both the mental aptitude and practical skills of the candidate. It is a multi-stage process that ensures only the most capable individuals move forward.

  1. Written Examination: This is the first hurdle. It usually consists of multiple-choice questions (MCQs) covering general aptitude and technical subjects.
  2. Skill Test / Trade Test: For roles like Cook, Nurse, Driver, and Stenographer, a practical test is conducted. For example, a Cook will be asked to prepare a dish, and a Stenographer will take a dictation test.
  3. Physical Endurance Test (PET): Only applicable for Assistant Security Officer roles to check physical fitness and agility.
  4. Interview: Conducted for supervisory and officer-level roles like Warden and Kitchen Supervisor.
  5. Final Merit List: Prepared based on the combined performance in the written test and skill test/interview.

Exam Pattern

The written exam is the most crucial part of the selection. Understanding its pattern is the key to success. The exam is typically 100 marks and divided into two sections.

Section 1: General Aptitude (50 Marks). This section includes English Language (10 marks), Reasoning (10 marks), Mathematics (15 marks), and General Awareness/Current Affairs (15 marks). The general awareness section often includes questions about the Department of Atomic Energy and basic cancer awareness.

Section 2: Subject Knowledge (50 Marks). This section focuses on the specific trade or profession. For Nurses, it covers Oncology Nursing, Medical-Surgical Nursing, and Pharmacology. For Stenographers, it focuses on office management and grammar. For Security Officers, it covers security protocols, firefighting, and disaster management. There is usually a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer.

Physical Endurance Test (PET)

The Assistant Security Officer post requires a high level of physical fitness. Candidates must qualify in the following categories:

For Male Candidates: 1.6 KM run in 6 minutes 30 seconds. Long jump of 3.65 meters in 3 attempts. 10 to 12 chin-ups and 30 sit-ups in 1 minute. Height Requirement: 167 cms (General). Chest: 80 cms (85 cms expanded).

For Female Candidates: 800-meter run in 4 minutes. Long jump of 2.7 meters. 20 sit-ups in 1 minute. Height Requirement: 157 cms. All physical tests are qualifying in nature, meaning they do not add to the final merit score but must be passed to progress.

How to Prepare

Preparing for TMC 2025 requires a two-pronged strategy: academic study and practical practice. Since the technical section carries 50% of the weight, professionals should revisit their college textbooks. Nurses should focus on chemotherapy protocols and radiation safety, as these are specific to TMC's functioning.

For the General Aptitude section, solving previous years' SSC or DAE exam papers is highly beneficial. Mathematics should be practiced until you can solve questions with speed and accuracy. For the skill test, Stenographers should practice shorthand daily to achieve zero-error results. Cooks should be familiar with the nutritional requirements of cancer patients and large-scale hygiene standards. Lastly, stay consistent with your preparation and keep an eye on current affairs related to the healthcare sector in India.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right books is half the battle won. Below is a curated list of books recommended by experts and previous successful candidates for the TMC exam.

General Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
Nursing: "Target High" by Muthuvenkatachalam & Ambili M. Venugopal.
Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and monthly "Pratiyogita Darpan".

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online. No physical applications will be entertained by the TMC HR department. Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Go to the official website: https://tmc.gov.in.
  2. Find the "Careers" section on the menu bar.
  3. Look for Advt No. TMC/AD/123/2025 and click on the "Online Application" link next to your desired post.
  4. Register yourself using your email ID and mobile number to generate a password.
  5. Fill in your personal, educational, and experience details in the form.
  6. Upload your scanned photograph and signature in the required dimensions.
  7. Pay the application fee of Rs. 300 online (if you are not in the exempted category).
  8. Submit the form and download the final PDF for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Ensure you have the following documents scanned in high resolution before starting your online application. Discrepancies in documents can lead to immediate disqualification during the verification stage.

  1. 10th Standard Marksheet/Certificate (for Age Proof).
  2. Final Degree/Diploma Certificate for the relevant qualification.
  3. Marksheets of all years/semesters of the qualifying exam.
  4. Valid Registration Certificate (for Nurse and Driver posts).
  5. Experience Certificates from all previous and current employers.
  6. Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL) in Central Government format.
  7. NCC 'C' Certificate (if applying for Security posts as a civilian).

Important Links

Link Description URL
Official Website tmc.gov.in
Direct Application Link Apply Now
Contact Support hrdrecruitment@tmc.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Q1: Can male candidates apply for the Nurse 'A' post?

As per the 2025 notification, the current vacancy is specifically for "Female Nurse 'A'". Male candidates should wait for general nursing notifications which are released periodically.

Q2: Is the recruitment on a contract or permanent basis?

These are permanent positions in a Grant-in-Aid institution. Selected candidates will be on a probation period, typically for one to two years, before confirmation.

Q3: What is the age limit for the Cook post?

The maximum age limit for Cook 'A' is 27 years. However, SC/ST candidates get a 5-year relaxation and OBC candidates get a 3-year relaxation.

Q4: Can I apply if I am currently working in a private hospital?

Yes, but you must ensure that you have valid experience certificates. If you are working in a government setup, you must provide a "No Objection Certificate" (NOC) at the time of the interview/skill test.

Q5: Is there any travel allowance for the exam?

TMC usually provides second-class rail fare reimbursement to outstation SC/ST candidates called for an interview or skill test, provided they are not already employed.

Q6: When will the exam be conducted?

The exam date is usually announced 1-2 months after the application deadline. Candidates are advised to keep checking the official TMC website for updates on the admit card and schedule.

entry.title: BSNL भर्ती 2025: सीनियर एग्जीक्यूटिव ट्रेनी के 120 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!
entry.content:
BSNL भर्ती 2025: सीनियर एग्जीक्यूटिव ट्रेनी के 120 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!

भारत संचार निगम लिमिटेड (BSNL) ने टेलीकॉम और फाइनेंस स्ट्रीम में सीनियर एग्जीक्यूटिव ट्रेनी (DR) के 120 पदों के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है। चयन लिखित परीक्षा के आधार पर होगा।

इंजीनियरिंग और फाइनेंस प्रोफेशनल्स के लिए सरकारी नौकरी का सुनहरा मौका! भारत संचार निगम लिमिटेड (BSNL), जो भारत सरकार की प्रमुख टेलीकॉम कंपनी है, ने Direct Recruitment (DR) स्कीम के तहत Senior Executive Trainee (SET) पदों के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है।

इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत Telecom Stream में 95 और Finance Stream में 25 यानी कुल 120 (tentative) पद भरे जाएंगे। चयन Computer Based Test (CBT) के माध्यम से किया जाएगा।

रिक्तियों और वेतनमान का विवरण

इस भर्ती में दो अलग-अलग स्ट्रीम्स के लिए पद निकाले गए हैं:

पद का नामकुल पद (संभावित)वेतनमान (IDA Pay Scale E3)
Senior Executive Trainee (DR) – Telecom Stream95₹24,900 – ₹50,500
Senior Executive Trainee (DR) – Finance Stream25
नोट: SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen उम्मीदवारों के लिए आरक्षण भारत सरकार के नियमों के अनुसार लागू होगा।

पात्रता मानदंड

आयु सीमा

  • न्यूनतम आयु: 21 वर्ष
  • अधिकतम आयु: 30 वर्ष

आरक्षित वर्गों को सरकार के नियमों के अनुसार छूट दी जाएगी।

शैक्षणिक योग्यता

  • Telecom Stream के लिए:
    • मान्यता प्राप्त संस्थान से B.E./B.Tech या समकक्ष डिग्री।
    • कम से कम 60% अंकों के साथ Regular Full-Time कोर्स।
    • मान्य विषय: Electronics & Telecommunication, Computer Science, Information Technology, Electrical या Instrumentation।
  • Finance Stream के लिए:
    • Chartered Accountant (CA) या
    • Cost & Management Accountant (CMA)।

चयन प्रक्रिया और परीक्षा विवरण

चयन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से लिखित परीक्षा (Written Examination) पर आधारित होगी। परीक्षा का प्रारूप इस प्रकार रहेगा:

  • परीक्षा प्रकार: Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • प्रश्न प्रकार: Multiple Choice Objective Type

परीक्षा का सिलेबस, शुल्क और अन्य विवरण जल्द ही BSNL की वेबसाइट पर जारी किए जाएंगे।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से ऑनलाइन होगी। आवेदन की तिथियाँ और लिंक जल्द जारी किए जाएंगे। उम्मीदवारों को सलाह दी जाती है कि वे निम्न वेबसाइट्स को नियमित रूप से देखें:

  • www.bsnl.co.in
  • www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in

लिंक सक्रिय होने के बाद उम्मीदवारों को पंजीकरण, आवेदन फॉर्म भरना और शुल्क भुगतान करना होगा।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
BSNL की आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
BSNL एग्जाम पोर्टलयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: इस भर्ती के लिए कौन आवेदन कर सकता है?
जिन उम्मीदवारों ने Engineering (Telecom संबंधित शाखाओं) में 60% अंकों के साथ B.E./B.Tech या CA/CMA किया है, वे आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
Q2: आवेदन कब शुरू होंगे?
आवेदन की तिथियाँ और परीक्षा का शेड्यूल जल्द ही BSNL की वेबसाइट पर जारी किया जाएगा।
Q3: क्या अंतिम वर्ष के छात्र आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि तक उम्मीदवार के पास आवश्यक योग्यता पूरी होनी चाहिए।

entry.title: SSC Delhi Police Constable (Driver) Recruitment 2025: 737 पदों के लिए आवेदन शुरू!
entry.content:
SSC Delhi Police Constable (Driver) Recruitment 2025: 737 पदों के लिए आवेदन शुरू!

Heavy Motor Vehicle लाइसेंस रखने वाले पुरुष उम्मीदवारों के लिए Delhi Police में शामिल होने का सुनहरा अवसर।

पद का नाम: Constable (Driver) - Male
अंतिम तिथि: 15.10.2025
कुल पद: 737

नमस्कार दोस्तों! Staff Selection Commission (SSC) ने Delhi Police के लिए Constable (Driver)-Male Examination, 2025 की आधिकारिक अधिसूचना जारी कर दी है। इस भर्ती के तहत Delhi Police में लगभग 737 (अनुमानित) पद भरे जाएंगे। यह एक ओपन प्रतियोगी परीक्षा है — देश के किसी भी हिस्से के योग्य पुरुष उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

यह अवसर उन उम्मीदवारों के लिए उत्तम है जिनके पास Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV) का वैध ड्राइविंग लाइसेंस है और जो प्रतिष्ठित बल जैसे Delhi Police में सेवा देना चाहते हैं। आवेदन पूरी तरह ऑनलाइन होंगे। नीचे इस भर्ती से संबंधित महत्वपूर्ण जानकारी दी गई है।

वैकेंसी विवरण (Vacancy Details)

इस भर्ती अभियान के अंतर्गत कुल 737 पद भरे जाने हैं। श्रेणीवार विभाजन नीचे दिया गया है:

श्रेणी UR EWS OBC SC ST कुल
Open 316 66 153 72 47 654
Ex-Servicemen (Ex-S) 35 07 17 15 09 83
कुल 351 73 170 87 56 737
नोट: पदों की संख्या प्राविजनल है और परिवर्तित हो सकती है। यह पद PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) और महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए उपयुक्त नहीं हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ (Important Dates)

सभी उम्मीदवार नीचे दी गई तिथियों पर ध्यान रखें:

घटना (Event) तिथि एवं समय (Date & Time)
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारम्भ 24.09.2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 15.10.2025 (रात 11:00 बजे तक)
ऑनलाइन शुल्क भुगतान की अंतिम तिथि 16.10.2025 (रात 11:00 बजे तक)
आवेदन फॉर्म सुधार विंडो 23.10.2025 से 25.10.2025 (रात 11:00 बजे तक)
Computer Based Exam (अनुमानित) December 2025 / January 2026

वेतन तथा आवेदन शुल्क (Pay & Fee)

वेतन (Pay Level): चयनित उम्मीदवारों को Pay Level-3 (₹21,700 - ₹69,100) के अनुसार वेतन मिलेगा।

आवेदन शुल्क: आवेदन शुल्क ₹100/- है। SC, ST और Ex-Servicemen (ESM) उम्मीदवारों को शुल्क में छूट दी गई है।

पात्रता की शर्तें (Eligibility Criteria) — 01.07.2025 तक

निम्नलिखित पात्रता शर्तें आवेदन करने से पहले सुनिश्चित करें:

मापदण्ड आवश्यकता (Requirement)
आयु सीमा (Age Limit) 21 से 30 वर्ष (01.07.2025 तक)। उम्मीदवार का जन्म 02.07.1995 से पहले और 01.07.2004 के बाद नहीं होना चाहिए।
शैक्षणिक योग्यता किसी मान्यता प्राप्त बोर्ड से 10+2 (Senior Secondary) उत्तीर्ण।
प्रोफेशनल योग्यता
  • Heavy वाहन चलाने में प्रवीणता होनी चाहिए।
  • 15.10.2025 (आवेदन समापन तिथि) तक Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV) का वैध driving license अनिवार्य है — Learner's license मान्य नहीं है।
  • वाहन रखरखाव (basic maintenance) का बोध उपयोगी होगा।

चयन प्रक्रिया (Selection Process)

चयन कई चरणों में होगा — प्रत्येक चरण पास करना अनिवार्य है:

  1. Computer Based Examination (CBE): 100 अंकों की Objective type परीक्षा।
  2. Physical Endurance & Measurement Test (PE&MT): CBE में शॉर्टलिस्ट हुए उम्मीदवारों के लिए। यह qualifying प्रकृति का होगा।
  3. Trade Test (Driving Test): PE&MT पास करने वाले उम्मीदवारों के लिए driving test। यह भी qualifying प्रकृति का है।
  4. Document Verification (DV): PE&MT/Trade Test के दौरान मूल प्रमाणपत्रों का सत्यापन।
  5. Medical Examination: अंतिम चयनित उम्मीदवारों का मेडिकल परीक्षण।

परीक्षा का स्वरूप (CBE Scheme)

Computer Based Exam में कुल 100 प्रश्न होंगे (कुल अंक 100)। परीक्षा की अवधि 90 मिनट है। प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर पर 0.25 अंक की negative marking होगी।

भाग (Part) विषय (Subject) प्रश्न (Questions) अंक (Marks)
Part-A General Awareness 20 20
Part-B General Intelligence 20 20
Part-C Numerical Ability 10 10
Part-D Road Sense, Vehicle Maintenance, Traffic Rules/Signals आदि 50 50
कुल 100 100

कैसे आवेदन करें (How to Apply)

आवेदन केवल SSC की नई आधिकारिक वेबसाइट के माध्यम से ऑनलाइन स्वीकार किए जाएँगे।

  1. One-Time Registration (OTR): सभी उम्मीदवारों को SSC की नवीन वेबसाइट https://ssc.gov.in पर OTR करना अनिवार्य है। (पुरानी साइट ssc.nic.in का OTR अब मान्य नहीं है)।
  2. Live Photograph: नए आवेदन मॉड्यूल में webcam या मोबाइल ऐप से लाइव फोटो कैप्चर करना आवश्यक है — पहले से खींची फोटो अपलोड नहीं की जा सकेगी।
  3. आवेदन भरें: OTR के बाद लॉगइन कर 'Constable (Driver)-Male in Delhi Police Examination, 2025' के लिए आवेदन करें।
  4. शुल्क भुगतान: आवेदन शुल्क ऑनलाइन जमा करें (यदि लागू)।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक (Important Links)

लिंक शीर्षक लिंक कार्रवाई
Apply Online / Official Website यहाँ क्लिक करें
Official Notification PDF यहाँ से डाउनलोड करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: क्या महिला उम्मीदवार इस पद के लिए आवेदन कर सकती हैं?

नहीं — यह भर्ती केवल पुरुष (Male) उम्मीदवारों के लिए है।

Q2: मेरे पास केवल Light Motor Vehicle (LMV) लाइसेंस है — क्या मैं आवेदन कर सकता हूँ?

नहीं — इस पद के लिए Heavy Motor Vehicle (HMV) का वैध ड्राइविंग लाइसेंस आवश्यक है।

Q3: आयु सीमा क्या है?

आयु सीमा 01.07.2025 को 21 से 30 वर्ष के बीच होनी चाहिए। आरक्षित श्रेणियों के लिए आयु में छूट नियमानुसार लागू होगी।

Q4: क्या परीक्षा में नकारात्मक अंकन है?

हाँ — Computer Based Exam में प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर के लिए 0.25 अंक की negative marking होगी।

entry.title: Central University of Punjab Recruitment 2025: Apply for LDC, Assistant, and Other Posts
entry.content:
Central University of Punjab (CUPB) Recruitment 2025: Apply for 100+ Teaching and Non-Teaching Vacancies

Join a Premier Central Institution - Detailed Guide on LDC, Assistant, Professor, and Support Staff Vacancies

Post Name: LDC (Clerk), Assistant, Section Officer, Personal Assistant, Cook, Lab Attendant, Professor
Last Date: December 09, 2025
Total Posts: Multiple Vacancies (Regular Basis)

The Central University of Punjab (CUPB), located in the historic city of Bathinda, is one of the leading central universities established by the Government of India under the Central Universities Act of 2009. Over the last decade, CUPB has carved a niche for itself in research and academic excellence, consistently ranking high in the National Institutional Ranking Framework (NIRF). To sustain its growth and manage its administrative and academic load, the university has officially announced its Recruitment Drive 2025. This drive is particularly significant as it seeks to fill various permanent and regular positions across teaching and non-teaching cadres. The notification invites applications for roles ranging from top-tier academic faculty to the administrative backbone, including Lower Division Clerks (LDC), Assistants, Section Officers, and specialized support staff like Cooks and Lab Attendants.

For job seekers in Northern India, especially in Punjab and surrounding states, a position at a Central University is highly coveted. Working at CUPB provides the stability of a Central Government job, attractive pay scales as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), and a vibrant, intellectual work environment. The non-teaching recruitment is being facilitated through the Samarth e-Gov Suite, a standardized recruitment portal used by central universities to ensure transparency and efficiency. The application process is currently open and will conclude on December 9, 2025. This exhaustive article provides a 2000-word deep dive into every aspect of this recruitment, including category-wise vacancy distribution, detailed eligibility requirements, selection methodology, and expert-recommended preparation roadmaps.

The administrative structure of a central university like CUPB is complex and requires a mix of general administrative skills and technical vocational proficiency. An LDC at CUPB is responsible for managing student records and departmental files, while a Section Officer oversees the broader administrative workflow. The technical staff, such as Lab Attendants, ensure that the university's research laboratories are maintained to global standards. Because these are regular appointments, the selection process is rigorous, involving multi-tiered written examinations and skill tests. In the following sections, we will break down the academic and technical requirements for each major post to help you submit a winning application.

Recruitment Overview and Critical Dates

Candidates must strictly adhere to the timeline provided by the university. The Central University of Punjab follows a strict disciplinary schedule for its recruitment cycles. It is highly recommended that aspirants complete their registration well before the deadline to avoid the last-minute technical rush on the Samarth portal. Note that no hard copies of the application are required to be sent for non-teaching posts unless specifically asked later.

Event Description Crucial Dates and Information
Official Notification Status Released (Advt. No. CUPB/24-25/013-014)
Online Registration Opening Date Active Now
Last Date to Submit Online Application December 09, 2025 (Up to 11:59 PM)
Age & Qualification Cut-off Date December 09, 2025
Nature of Employment Permanent / Regular Basis
Job Location Bathinda, Punjab (Main Campus)

Detailed Vacancy Distribution and Salary Structure

The vacancies at CUPB are categorized based on the level of authority and the pay matrix level. The salary is governed by the 7th CPC, ensuring that even entry-level employees receive a competitive package along with central allowances like Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Transport Allowance (TA).

Post Designation Total Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC) Initial Basic Pay
Section Officer 01 (UR) Level 7 ₹44,900
Personal Assistant 01 (UR) Level 6 ₹35,400
Assistant (Admin) 01 (OBC) Level 6 ₹35,400
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) 09 (UR, OBC, SC, EWS) Level 2 ₹19,900
Cook 02 (UR) Level 2 ₹19,900
Lab Attendant 01 (UR) Level 1 ₹18,000
💰 Estimated Monthly Gross Salary (2025):
  • Level 7 Officers: Approximately ₹75,000 to ₹82,000 depending on HRA.
  • Level 6 Assistants: Approximately ₹58,000 to ₹65,000.
  • Level 2 (LDC / Cook): Approximately ₹32,000 to ₹36,000.
  • Level 1 (Attendant): Approximately ₹28,000 to ₹31,000.

Note: Permanent employees are also eligible for the New Pension Scheme (NPS), Leave Travel Concession (LTC), and comprehensive medical coverage under the university health scheme.

Post Specific Eligibility Criteria

The Central University of Punjab maintains high academic standards. Each post has a specific set of essential qualifications and, for higher levels, mandatory experience. Candidates must ensure they fulfill these criteria as of December 9, 2025.

  • Lower Division Clerk (LDC): Minimum 12th Standard pass from a recognized board. Typing Skill: English typing speed of 35 words per minute (WPM) OR Hindi typing speed of 30 WPM on a computer. Age Limit: 30 Years.
  • Assistant: Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized university. Experience: Minimum 03 years of regular service as Upper Division Clerk (UDC) or equivalent in Level-4. Age Limit: 35 Years.
  • Cook: 10th Standard pass from a recognized board. Certification: Diploma in Cookery/Catering OR 02 years of experience in a reputed kitchen/hostel mess. Age Limit: 32 Years.
  • Section Officer: Bachelor’s Degree with 50% marks. Experience: 03 years of experience as Assistant in Level-6 OR 08 years as UDC in Level-4. Age Limit: 35 Years.
  • Lab Attendant: 10+2 with Science stream from a recognized board. No prior experience is mandatory. Age Limit: 30 Years.

Age Relaxation: Upper age relaxation is provided to various categories: SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), PwBD (10 years), and Women (as per Govt of India rules). There is also a provision for age relaxation for existing regular employees of Central Universities.

Selection Process and Examination Roadmap

The selection methodology for non-teaching posts at CUPB is standardized to ensure meritocracy. Unlike state-level exams, the university focuses heavily on descriptive writing skills for administrative roles.

  1. Paper-I (Objective Test): This is a 100-mark screening test consisting of multiple-choice questions (MCQs). It covers General Awareness, English Language, Reasoning Ability, and Quantitative Aptitude. This stage is qualifying in nature for some posts and merit-contributing for others.
  2. Paper-II (Descriptive Test): This 100-mark paper evaluates the candidate's ability to draft letters, write essays, and handle office-noting procedures. This is the most critical stage, as the final merit for many Group B and C posts is prepared based solely on Paper-II marks.
  3. Skill Test / Trade Test: For LDCs, a typing test is mandatory. For Cooks, a practical cooking test is conducted. These tests are qualifying only; marks are not added to the merit list, but passing is mandatory.
  4. Personal Interview: Conducted only for Group A officer posts (like Section Officer or higher). No interview is held for Group C posts (LDC, Cook, Attendant) as per the latest central government guidelines.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Preparing for a Central University exam requires a strategy that balances speed for MCQs and depth for descriptive writing. The difficulty level for LDC is 12th standard, while for Assistants/Officers, it is graduation level.

Paper-I Pattern (100 Questions)

  • General Awareness: Focus on current events, Indian Constitution, and specifically higher education news (25 Marks).
  • English Language: Grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension (25 Marks).
  • Reasoning: Series, analogies, coding-decoding, and logical puzzles (25 Marks).
  • Numerical Ability: Arithmetic, percentages, profit & loss, and time & work (25 Marks).

Paper-II Pattern (Descriptive)

  • Letter Writing: Formal letters to authorities or departmental drafting.
  • Essay Writing: Topics related to social issues, education, or technology.
  • Precis Writing: Summarizing a provided text while retaining the core message.
  • Office Noting: Simulating real-world university administrative scenarios.

How to Prepare for CUPB Recruitment

Success in this 2025 recruitment cycle requires a disciplined 3-month preparation strategy. Candidates often fail in the descriptive section because they focus too much on objective MCQs.

Focus on Descriptive Skills: Since the final merit depends on Paper-II, you must practice writing formal letters and essays daily. Read editorials from newspapers like "The Hindu" or "The Indian Express" to improve your vocabulary and structure. Learn the standard "Government Noting and Drafting" formats, as these carry high weightage in administrative exams.

Typing Accuracy for LDC: If you are an LDC aspirant, don't wait for the written result to start typing. Use software like "Typing Master" or "Mavis Beacon" to achieve a consistent speed of 40 WPM. While the requirement is 35 WPM, maintaining a buffer ensures you clear the test under the pressure of the examination environment.

General Awareness Strategy: Central Universities often ask questions about the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, the role of UGC, and recent innovations in the Indian education sector. Stay updated with monthly current affairs and the basics of the Indian Constitution, as these are favorite topics for the Samarth portal exam setters.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing standard study material will keep your preparation aligned with the central institute pattern. Below are the top recommendations for the CUPB 2025 exam.

Subject Area Recommended Book List
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude and Logical Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
General English Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi (Arihant Publications).
Descriptive Writing Descriptive English by S.P. Bakshi and Office Noting/Drafting Manuals.
General Knowledge Lucent’s General Knowledge and monthly Pratiyogita Darpan.
Computer Awareness Objective Computer Awareness by Arihant Expert.

Application Fee and Payment Process

The application fee is used to cover the administrative costs of the recruitment process. Significant exemptions are provided to ensure equal opportunity for all segments of society.

Candidate Category Non-Teaching Fee Teaching Fee (Professor)
General / OBC / EWS ₹600/- ₹750/-
SC / ST / PwBD / Female EXEMPTED (₹0) EXEMPTED (₹0)

Payment Method: The fee must be paid exclusively through the online payment gateway integrated with the Samarth Recruitment Portal. Candidates should ensure they save the transaction receipt, as it contains the unique ID required for future correspondence.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step Guide

CUPB utilizes the Samarth e-Gov Suite for its recruitment. If you have applied for other Central Universities using this portal, you can use your existing credentials. Follow these steps meticulously:

  1. Visit the official application portal: https://cupnt.samarth.edu.in.
  2. Click on the "Sign Up" button to register yourself with a valid Email ID and Mobile Number.
  3. Verify your email through the activation link sent to your inbox.
  4. Login and select the post category (e.g., Non-Teaching) and the specific post (e.g., LDC).
  5. Personal & Academic Data: Enter your 10th, 12th, and Graduation scores. Ensure your work experience is mentioned in reverse chronological order.
  6. Upload Scanned Images: Upload a recent passport-size photograph (white background), your signature, and all academic/category certificates in PDF/JPEG format (as specified in the guidelines).
  7. Fee Payment: If you belong to the General/OBC/EWS category, pay the fee online. SC/ST/Female candidates will proceed without payment.
  8. Final Review: Check your application one last time. Once submitted, details cannot be modified.
  9. Download the final application PDF and keep it safe for document verification.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready in digital format before starting the application to avoid session timeouts on the portal. All documents should be clear and legible.

  1. Identity and Age Proof: Aadhaar Card and 10th Standard Marksheet/Certificate.
  2. Academic Proof: All semester marksheets and final Degree/Diploma certificates.
  3. Experience Certificates: Essential for posts like Assistant or Section Officer (must be on official letterhead).
  4. Category Certificate: Valid SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificate in the Central Government format.
  5. Skill Certificate: Typing certificate or Cookery diploma (if applicable).
  6. NOC (No Objection Certificate): Mandatory for candidates currently working in other Government, PSU, or Autonomous bodies.

Important Links

Resource Description Direct Official Link
Online Application Portal (Non-Teaching) Apply Online Now
Detailed Notification PDF (CUPB) Download PDF
Central University of Punjab Official Site Visit cup.edu.in

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can candidates from other states apply for CUPB Bathinda?

Yes. Central University of Punjab is a federal institution. All Indian citizens, regardless of their home state, are eligible to apply. Reservation benefits (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) are provided as per the Central List of the Government of India.

Q2: Is the typing test mandatory for the LDC post?

Yes. The typing test (35 WPM in English or 30 WPM in Hindi) is a mandatory qualifying hurdle for the LDC position. If you fail the typing test, you will be disqualified regardless of your marks in the written exam.

Q3: What is the fee for female candidates?

According to the official university guidelines, all female candidates regardless of their social category are completely exempted from paying any application fee for this recruitment cycle.

Q4: Are these jobs permanent or on a contract?

These are permanent and regular positions. Selected candidates will be on a probation period of 1 to 2 years, following which they will be confirmed in the central government service.

Q5: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you are eligible for them. However, you must submit separate application forms and pay the separate fees for each post. Ensure you check that exam dates do not overlap once the schedule is out.

Q6: When will the exam be conducted?

The university has not announced the exam dates yet. Typically, central university exams are held within 3-5 months after the application deadline. Candidates should keep checking their registered email and the university portal for admit card updates.

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only. While every effort has been made to ensure accuracy, candidates are strictly advised to read the official CUPB notification carefully before applying. SakariJobs.blog is not responsible for any discrepancies.

entry.title: NMMC Recruitment 2025: 113 Group A Medical Officer & Specialist Posts Open
entry.content:
NMMC Recruitment 2025: 113 Group A Medical Officer & Specialist Posts Open

A major recruitment drive by Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation for various medical specialist roles. Online applications open from January 5 to February 4, 2026.

Post Name: Medical Specialist (Medicine, Surgery, Gynaecology, Paediatrics, Radiology, etc.), Medical Officer, Junior Medical Officer
Last Date: 04 February 2026
Total Posts: 113

The Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation (NMMC) has released a significant recruitment notification (Advertisement No. Aastha/02/2025) for various Group-A cadre posts within its public health services. This direct recruitment drive aims to fill a total of 113 vacancies for crucial medical roles, including various Specialists and Medical Officers. This is a golden opportunity for qualified medical professionals (MBBS, MD/MS) to secure a stable and prestigious government job within one of Maharashtra's premier municipal corporations.

The recruitment is being conducted to strengthen the public healthcare infrastructure of Navi Mumbai by appointing experts in fields such as Medicine, Surgery, Gynaecology, Paediatrics, Radiology, and more. Selected candidates will be offered an attractive salary package as per the 7th Pay Commission, along with the benefits of a government job. The application process is entirely online, and interested candidates can apply through the official NMMC website, www.nmmc.gov.in, from January 5, 2026, to February 4, 2026. The selection will be based on an online examination. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all essential details, including post-wise vacancies, eligibility criteria, selection process, and the application procedure.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government positions in a Municipal Corporation located in Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra. Key dates and details are outlined below.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation (NMMC)
Advertisement Number Aastha/02/2025
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Municipal Corporation)
Job Location Navi Mumbai, Maharashtra
Online Application Start Date 05 January 2026
Last Date for Online Application 04 February 2026 (up to 11:55 PM)

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 113 Group-A posts in the public health service of NMMC. The post-wise breakdown of vacancies is as follows:

Sr. No. Post Name Pay Scale (Level) Total Vacancies
1 Medicine Specialist S-20 16
2 Surgeon S-20 10
3 Gynaecologist S-20 21
4 Pediatrician S-20 17
5 Radiologist S-20 11
6 Pathologist S-20 02
7 Intensivist S-20 04
8 Anaesthetist S-20 05
9 Orthopedic Surgeon S-20 04
10 Dermatologist S-20 01
11 Blood Transfusion Officer S-20 01
12 Medical Record Keeper Officer S-20 01
13 Medical Officer (Urologist and Tuberculosis Specialist) S-20 02
14 Junior Medical Officer S-16 17
15 Veterinary Officer S-20 01
Total Vacancies 113
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may be subject to change. Reservation for various social and parallel categories will be applicable as per the rules of the Government of Maharashtra.

Application Fee

  • For Open Category candidates: ₹1,000/-
  • For Backward Class, Orphan, PwD, and Economically Weaker Section (EWS) candidates: ₹900/-

The fee must be paid online and is non-refundable. Candidates applying for multiple posts must pay the fee separately for each application.

Eligibility Criteria

Each post has specific requirements for educational qualifications, experience, and age limits. A general overview is provided below (age limit is as on 01/12/2025).

Post Name Essential Qualification and Experience Age Limit
Medical Specialists (All disciplines) Postgraduate Degree (MD/MS) in the respective specialty from a recognized university. Knowledge of Marathi is essential. Registration with the Maharashtra Medical Council is mandatory. For some posts, a PG Diploma (e.g., DGO, DCH) may be considered if degree holders are not available. A 2-year experience in a relevant hospital setting is required for most specialist posts. 38 years (43 for reserved categories)
Junior Medical Officer MBBS degree from a recognized university. Knowledge of Marathi is essential. Registration with the Maharashtra Medical Council is mandatory. 38 years (43 for reserved categories)
Veterinary Officer Postgraduate degree in Veterinary Science. Knowledge of Marathi is essential. Registration with the Maharashtra State Veterinary Council is mandatory. 2 years of relevant experience is required. 38 years (43 for reserved categories)
Blood Transfusion Officer Postgraduate Degree (MD) in Pathology / Transfusion Medicine. Knowledge of Marathi is essential. Registration with the Maharashtra Medical Council is mandatory. 2 years of experience is required. 38 years (43 for reserved categories)
Medical Record Keeper Officer MBBS degree from a recognized university. Knowledge of Marathi is essential. Registration with the Maharashtra Medical Council is mandatory. 38 years (43 for reserved categories)

Selection Process

The selection of candidates will be based on their performance in a competitive online examination. The process is designed to be transparent and merit-based.

  1. Shortlisting (if required): If the number of applications received is excessively large, the NMMC may decide to shortlist candidates for the online examination based on higher educational qualifications or experience.
  2. Online Examination: All eligible (or shortlisted) candidates will have to appear for an online objective-type test. The date, time, and venue of the examination will be mentioned on the admit card.
  3. Merit List: A merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained by the candidates in the online examination.
  4. Document Verification: Candidates who are placed on the merit list will be called for document verification. Final selection is subject to the successful verification of all original documents supporting the claims made in the application.

Exam Pattern

While the detailed exam pattern and syllabus for each post will be published on the NMMC website, a general pattern for such professional posts typically includes the following components. The exam will be conducted online.

Section Subject Expected Weightage
Section A Professional/Domain Knowledge: Questions based on the MBBS/MD/MS curriculum relevant to the specific post (e.g., Medicine, Surgery, Gynaecology). 60-70%
Section B Marathi Language: Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension. 30-40%
English Language: Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension.
General Knowledge & Current Affairs: With a focus on public health, Maharashtra, and national events.
Logical Reasoning & Aptitude: Basic reasoning and numerical ability.
Note: The final and detailed syllabus for the online examination for each post will be published on the official website www.nmmc.gov.in. Candidates are advised to check the website for updates.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these medical professional posts.

How to Prepare

A structured preparation strategy is essential to succeed in this competitive examination.

  1. Master Your Domain: The most significant part of the exam will be based on your professional medical knowledge. Thoroughly revise your undergraduate (MBBS) and postgraduate (MD/MS) syllabus. Focus on clinical subjects and recent advancements in your specialty.
