  2. General Subjects: Do not ignore the sections on Marathi, English, GK, and Reasoning. Dedicate specific time to prepare for these as they can be crucial for your final score. For Marathi, focus on basic grammar and comprehension.
  3. Practice MCQs: Solve as many multiple-choice questions as possible from standard medical entrance exam books (like NEET-PG level) and previous papers of similar public service commission exams.
  4. Time Management: Practice with timed online mock tests to get accustomed to the exam interface and improve your speed and accuracy.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended resources to aid your preparation:

  • For Professional Knowledge: Standard medical textbooks for MBBS and your respective MD/MS specialty (e.g., Harrison's for Medicine, Bailey & Love's for Surgery). Objective review books for PG entrance exams are also very useful.
  • For Marathi Language: "Sugam Marathi Vyakaran" by M. R. Walimbe.
  • For General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • For GK and Aptitude: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online through the NMMC's official website.

  1. Visit the official NMMC website: www.nmmc.gov.in.
  2. Find the "Recruitment" or "Careers" section and click on the link for Advertisement No. Aastha/02/2025.
  3. Read the detailed advertisement carefully. Click on the "Apply Online" link.
  4. First, complete the user registration process by providing your basic details, mobile number, and email ID.
  5. Log in with your credentials and fill out the detailed application form with your personal, educational, and professional information.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your recent photograph, signature, and other required documents in the prescribed format and size.
  7. Pay the application fee online through the available payment gateway.
  8. Review the filled application form carefully before final submission.
  9. Take a printout of the final submitted application form and the fee payment receipt for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should keep scanned copies of the following documents ready for uploading during the online application process:

  1. Recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Proof of Date of Birth (SSC Certificate or Birth Certificate).
  3. All educational qualification marksheets and certificates (MBBS, MD/MS/Diploma).
  4. Maharashtra Medical Council registration certificate.
  5. Experience certificates.
  6. Domicile Certificate of Maharashtra.
  7. Caste/Category Certificate (if applicable).
  8. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, etc.).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online (from 05.01.2026) Click Here
Download Official Notification Available on Official Website
Official Website www.nmmc.gov.in

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the NMMC recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is February 4, 2026, until 11:55 PM.

Question 2: Can doctors from outside Maharashtra apply?

While Indian citizens can apply, the notification states that knowledge of the Marathi language is essential, and reservation benefits are only for Maharashtra domiciles. Registration with the Maharashtra Medical Council is also mandatory.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection process is based entirely on the marks obtained in a competitive online examination.

Question 4: Is there an interview for these posts?

No, the notification mentions selection will be based on the online examination. There is no mention of an interview.

entry.title: Oil India भर्ती 2025: Duliajan में Contractual Operator की भर्ती, Walk-in-Interview से होगा चयन!
entry.content:
Oil India भर्ती 2025: Duliajan में Contractual Operator की भर्ती, Walk-in-Interview से होगा चयन!

Oil India Limited (OIL) ने असम और अरुणाचल प्रदेश के निवासियों के लिए Contractual Drilling/Workover Operator और Assistant Operator के 16 पदों पर भर्ती निकाली है।

असम और अरुणाचल प्रदेश के उम्मीदवारों के लिए शानदार मौका! Oil India Limited (OIL), जो देश की प्रमुख सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र की कंपनी है, ने अपने Duliajan Field Headquarters के लिए कॉन्ट्रैक्ट आधार पर भर्ती का नोटिफिकेशन जारी किया है। यह भर्ती Contractual Drilling/Workover Operator और Assistant Operator के कुल 16 पदों के लिए की जा रही है। चयन प्रक्रिया Walk-in-Interview cum Personal Assessment के माध्यम से होगी।

Walk-in-Interview की तारीख और स्थान

योग्य उम्मीदवारों को नीचे दी गई तारीखों पर Duliajan में इंटरव्यू के लिए पहुँचना होगा:

पद का नामइंटरव्यू की तारीखसमयस्थान
Contractual Drilling/Workover Operator11 November 2025सुबह 07:00 से 09:00 बजे तक (रजिस्ट्रेशन)Duliajan Club, Oil India Limited
Contractual Drilling/Workover Assistant Operator12 November 2025सुबह 07:00 से 09:00 बजे तक (रजिस्ट्रेशन)Duliajan Club, Oil India Limited

पदवार योग्यता, आयु सीमा और वेतन

1. Contractual Drilling/Workover Operator (08 पद)

  • योग्यता: इनमें से कोई एक होना आवश्यक —
    • 10वीं पास + 3 साल का इंजीनियरिंग डिप्लोमा + 2 साल का अनुभव
    • स्नातक (Science) + 2 साल का अनुभव
    • 10वीं पास + ITI (Diesel Mechanic/Fitter आदि) + 5 साल का अनुभव
  • आयु सीमा: सामान्य – 45 वर्ष, OBC – 48 वर्ष, SC – 50 वर्ष
  • वेतन: ₹24,960 प्रतिमाह + ₹960 प्रतिदिन (वर्किंग डे)

2. Contractual Drilling/Workover Assistant Operator (08 पद)

  • योग्यता: 10वीं पास + ITI (Diesel Mechanic/Fitter आदि) + 2 साल का अनुभव
  • आयु सीमा: सामान्य – 40 वर्ष, OBC – 43 वर्ष, ST – 45 वर्ष
  • वेतन: ₹21,450 प्रतिमाह + ₹825 प्रतिदिन (वर्किंग डे)

चयन प्रक्रिया

चयन पूरी तरह से Walk-in-Interview cum Personal Assessment पर आधारित होगा। उम्मीदवारों को इंटरव्यू के दिन सुबह 7 से 9 बजे के बीच पंजीकरण कराना होगा। इसके बाद Personal Interview लिया जाएगा।

  • उत्तीर्ण होने के लिए न्यूनतम 50% अंक आवश्यक हैं।
  • फाइनल चयन इंटरव्यू में प्राप्त अंकों की मेरिट के आधार पर होगा।

इंटरव्यू के लिए क्या साथ लाना है?

उम्मीदवारों को नीचे दिए गए दस्तावेज़ों के साथ इंटरव्यू स्थल पर पहुँचना होगा:

  1. व्यक्तिगत बायोडाटा फॉर्म: विज्ञापन के अंत में दिए गए फॉर्म को पूरी तरह भरकर लाएं।
  2. फोटो: हाल ही का रंगीन फोटो (3cm x 3cm)।
  3. मूल दस्तावेज़:
    • पहचान और पते का प्रमाण
    • कक्षा 10 की मार्कशीट और प्रमाणपत्र
    • शैक्षणिक योग्यता प्रमाणपत्र (ITI/Diploma/Degree)
    • कार्य अनुभव प्रमाणपत्र
    • जाति प्रमाणपत्र (SC/ST/OBC), Non-Creamy Layer या EWS प्रमाणपत्र
    • यदि वर्तमान में कार्यरत हैं, तो NOC लाना आवश्यक है।
  4. फोटोकॉपी: सभी दस्तावेजों की स्वयं-सत्यापित प्रति
ध्यान दें: बिना मूल दस्तावेज़ों के किसी भी उम्मीदवार को इंटरव्यू में बैठने की अनुमति नहीं होगी।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
Oil India की आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
नोटिफिकेशन और बायोडाटा फॉर्म“Careers” सेक्शन देखें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: कौन आवेदन कर सकता है?
केवल असम और अरुणाचल प्रदेश के निवासी उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
Q2: क्या कोई लिखित परीक्षा होगी?
नहीं, चयन केवल Walk-in-Interview के आधार पर होगा।
Q3: क्या अनुभव जरूरी है?
हाँ, Drilling/Workover क्षेत्र में 2 से 5 साल का अनुभव अनिवार्य है।
Q4: क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?
नहीं, यह कॉन्ट्रैक्ट आधारित नौकरी है। प्रारंभिक अवधि 6 महीने की होगी जिसे प्रदर्शन के आधार पर 2 साल तक बढ़ाया जा सकता है।

entry.title: RRB JE Recruitment 2025: Apply for 2569 Junior Engineer and DMS Vacancies
entry.content:
RRB JE Recruitment 2025: Notification for 2569 Junior Engineer Posts

Official CEN No. 05/2025 - Direct Recruitment for JE, DMS, and CMA across Indian Railway Zones

Post Name: Junior Engineer (JE), Depot Material Superintendent (DMS), Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant (CMA)
Last Date: November 30, 2025
Total Posts: 2569 Vacancies

The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has officially announced one of the most awaited job opportunities for engineering graduates and diploma holders in India. Under the Centralized Employment Notice CEN No. 05/2025, Indian Railways is set to recruit 2569 professionals for the roles of Junior Engineer (JE), Depot Material Superintendent (DMS), and Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant (CMA). As the backbone of the nation's infrastructure, Indian Railways continuously seeks technical talent to maintain its vast network of tracks, locomotives, signaling systems, and administrative logistics. This recruitment drive is a golden chance for young engineers to secure a prestigious, permanent position in a Central Government organization that offers unparalleled job security and growth.

A career as an RRB Junior Engineer involves high-level responsibility, ranging from technical maintenance of civil structures to managing complex electrical grids and mechanical rolling stock. For the 2025 cycle, the application process will begin on October 31, 2025, and will remain open until November 30, 2025. Candidates must be prepared for a highly competitive two-stage Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by stringent medical examinations. This article serves as an exhaustive guide, spanning over 2000 words, to help you navigate through the eligibility requirements, detailed syllabus, salary structure, and the step-by-step strategy needed to ace the RRB JE 2025 examination.

The recruitment is spread across various functional departments, including Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Signal & Telecommunication, and Stores. Each department has its own specific technical requirements, and the competition is expected to be fierce, with millions of applicants vying for these 2569 spots. Unlike many other technical exams, RRB JE focuses heavily on both general awareness and technical depth, making it essential for aspirants to balance their preparation between non-technical basics and core engineering concepts. In the following sections, we will break down the notification into actionable insights for every engineering discipline.

Important Points and Recruitment Timeline

Success in any competitive exam begins with a clear understanding of the schedule. Indian Railways follows a strict timeline for its recruitment cycles. Candidates must register on the official RRB portals of their respective zones (such as Mumbai, Ahmedabad, Chennai, or Kolkata). Please note that a candidate can apply to only one RRB zone. Attempting to apply to multiple zones will result in the rejection of all applications.

Event Details Dates and Information
Notification Number CEN No. 05/2025
Application Start Date October 31, 2025
Last Date to Submit Application November 30, 2025 (11:59 PM)
Fee Payment Deadline November 30, 2025
Correction Window December 1 - December 10, 2025 (Expected)
Job Type Central Government (Indian Railways)
Job Location All India (Based on RRB Zone)

Vacancy Details and Salary Structure

The 2569 vacancies are distributed among various technical cadres. Each post carries immense importance in the operational safety and efficiency of the railway system. The salary is governed by the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) under Pay Level 6.

Designation Pay Scale Details
Junior Engineer (JE) - All Disciplines Level 6 (Basic Pay: ₹35,400 + Allowances)
Depot Material Superintendent (DMS) Level 6 (Basic Pay: ₹35,400 + Allowances)
Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant (CMA) Level 6 (Basic Pay: ₹35,400 + Allowances)
Gross Salary Expectation: The starting basic pay is ₹35,400, but when including Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Transport Allowance (TA), the gross monthly salary in a Tier-1 (X-Category) city like Mumbai or Delhi can range between ₹58,000 to ₹65,000. Additionally, railway employees enjoy medical facilities, concessional passes (Privilege Pass/PTO), and a stable pension under the New Pension Scheme (NPS).

Application Fee and Refund Policy

  • UR / OBC / EWS Candidates: ₹500. (Out of this, ₹400 will be refunded to the candidate's bank account after they appear in the CBT-1 exam).
  • SC / ST / Female / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen: ₹250. (The full amount of ₹250 will be refunded after appearing in the CBT-1 exam).

Note: It is vital to provide your own bank account details correctly during the application process to ensure you receive the refund. Refunds are only processed for those who actually attend the first stage of the exam.

Eligibility Criteria

The eligibility for RRB JE 2025 is primarily based on educational qualification and age. Engineering Diploma holders and Degree holders are both eligible, but they must ensure their discipline matches the post requirements. Final year students whose results are awaited after the closing date of the application are not eligible to apply.

Designation Educational Qualification Requirement
Junior Engineer (Civil) Three-year Diploma in Civil Engineering or B.E./B.Tech in Civil Engineering or equivalent.
JE (Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics) Three-year Diploma or Degree in Mechanical / Electrical / Electronics / Manufacturing / Mechatronics or related sub-streams.
Depot Material Superintendent (DMS) Three-year Diploma in any Engineering branch from a recognized university/board.
Chemical & Metallurgical Assistant (CMA) B.Sc. with Physics and Chemistry as core subjects with a minimum of 45% marks.

Age Limit (As of January 1, 2025):

  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 33 Years
  • Age relaxation: 3 years for OBC, 5 years for SC/ST, and up to 10-15 years for PwBD and Ex-Servicemen.

Selection Process

The RRB JE selection is a four-stage process designed to test the mental caliber and physical fitness of the engineering talent. Candidates must pass each stage to move to the next.

  1. CBT Stage 1: A screening test consisting of 100 questions. It is non-technical in nature and serves as a filter to shortlist candidates for the second stage.
  2. CBT Stage 2: The main examination. It carries 150 marks and includes technical questions related to your specific engineering discipline.
  3. Document Verification (DV): Candidates equal to the number of vacancies are called based on CBT-2 merit for scrutiny of original academic and caste certificates.
  4. Medical Examination: A critical stage where vision, hearing, and general health are tested to ensure suitability for railway duties (Standards A-3, B-1, B-2, etc.).

Negative Marking: There is a penalty for wrong answers. For every incorrect response in both CBT-1 and CBT-2, 1/3rd of the marks assigned to that question will be deducted.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

The RRB JE exam is known for its vast syllabus. Aspirants must bifurcate their study plan into Non-Technical and Technical phases.

CBT Stage 1 (90 Minutes, 100 Marks):

  • Mathematics (30 Qs): Number systems, BODMAS, Algebra, Geometry, Trigonometry, Statistics, Profit & Loss.
  • General Intelligence & Reasoning (25 Qs): Analogy, Coding-Decoding, Venn Diagrams, Syllogism, Blood Relations.
  • General Awareness (15 Qs): Current Affairs, Indian Geography, History, Polity, and Economics.
  • General Science (30 Qs): Physics, Chemistry, and Life Sciences up to 10th Standard CBSE syllabus.

CBT Stage 2 (120 Minutes, 150 Marks):

  • General Awareness (15 Qs): Recent events, Sports, Awards.
  • Physics & Chemistry (15 Qs): Up to 10th Standard level.
  • Basics of Computers (10 Qs): Architecture, OS, MS Office, Networking, Internet.
  • Basics of Environment & Pollution Control (10 Qs): Air/Water pollution, Global warming, Waste management.
  • Technical Abilities (100 Qs): Core subjects from Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, or IT disciplines.

Physical and Medical Standards

Unlike banking or administrative jobs, Railway Engineering roles require high physical standards, especially in terms of vision. During the medical exam, candidates will be categorized. Medical Standard A-3 requires a distant vision of 6/9, 6/9 with or without glasses and passing tests for Color Vision, Binocular Vision, and Night Vision. B-1 and B-2 standards have slightly relaxed vision requirements but still mandate physical fitness for field work.

How to Prepare for RRB JE 2025

Preparation for RRB JE requires a strategy that evolves with the exam stages. Since CBT-1 is purely non-technical, engineering students often find it challenging because they are out of touch with general studies.

Strategy for CBT-1: Focus on Mathematics and Reasoning. These are scoring sections. Solve at least 50 MCQs daily to improve speed. For General Science, rely on NCERT textbooks from class 6th to 10th. Science has a high weightage (30 marks) and is the differentiator in CBT-1.

Strategy for CBT-2: Start your technical revision alongside CBT-1 preparation. Use "Handbook of Engineering" for quick formula revisions. For the 100 technical marks, focus on standard textbooks. Civil engineers must master RCC and Surveying; Mechanical engineers should focus on Thermodynamics and Production; Electrical engineers must be thorough with Machines and Circuits. Practice using the Virtual Calculator provided in the exam interface, as physical calculators are prohibited.

Best Books for Preparation

Using the right reference material can save time and improve accuracy. Here is a list of recommended books for the 2025 exam.

Mathematics: Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal or Fast Track Arithmetic by Rajesh Verma.
Reasoning: Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
General Science: Lucent's General Science or NCERT Exemplar.
Technical (Civil/Mech/Elec): Objective Type Questions by J.K. Gupta & R.S. Khurmi or Made Easy Publications.
Current Affairs: Eduteria or Speedy Current Affairs (Monthly).

How to Apply Online

The application process for RRB JE 2025 is centralized through the RRB "Apply" portal. Follow these steps meticulously:

  1. Visit the official website of the RRB zone you wish to apply for (e.g., rrbmumbai.gov.in).
  2. Click on the link for "New Registration" under CEN 05/2025.
  3. Provide your Name, Date of Birth, Father's Name, Mobile Number, and Email. Verify using OTP.
  4. Fill in the detailed application form, including academic scores and engineering branch.
  5. Choose your RRB Zone carefully. Once submitted, you cannot change your zone.
  6. Upload a high-quality scanned photograph (with a white background) and a clear signature.
  7. Pay the application fee online via Net Banking, Credit/Debit card, or UPI.
  8. Print the application confirmation page for future use in Document Verification.

Documents Needed to Apply

Before starting the online form, ensure you have clear scanned copies of the following documents:

  1. Matriculation (10th) Certificate: Used as proof of date of birth and father's name.
  2. Diploma/Degree Certificate: Final certificate or provisional certificate proving your engineering qualification.
  3. Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL): In the format prescribed by the Central Government. OBC candidates must ensure their certificate is issued in the current financial year.
  4. Income Certificate (EWS/EBC): For candidates seeking fee concessions.
  5. Disability Certificate (PwBD): If applicable.
  6. Passport Size Photo: Color photo taken against a white background without cap or sunglasses.

Important Links

Resource Name Direct Link
Official Indian Railways Portal www.indianrailways.gov.in
RRB Mumbai Zone www.rrbmumbai.gov.in
CEN 05/2025 Notification PDF Download Notification

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can B.E./B.Tech graduates apply for the RRB Junior Engineer post?

Yes, candidates with a Degree in Engineering (B.E./B.Tech) are fully eligible to apply for JE posts. However, they must apply within their respective engineering streams (e.g., a Civil B.Tech applies for JE Civil).

Q2: Is there any interview for RRB JE 2025?

No, there is no personal interview for the JE cadre. The final selection is based entirely on the merit of CBT Stage 2, followed by successful Document Verification and Medical Examination.

Q3: What is the refund policy for the application fee?

RRB refunds ₹400 to UR/OBC candidates and the full ₹250 to SC/ST/Female candidates. This refund is only processed after the candidate appears for the Stage 1 CBT exam.

Q4: Are final year students eligible to apply?

No. According to the RRB rules, you must have your final results and certificates on or before the closing date of the application (November 30, 2025). Those awaiting results are not eligible.

Q5: Can I apply to two different RRB zones?

No. A candidate can submit only one application and apply to only one RRB zone. Submitting multiple applications to different zones will lead to debarment from all future railway exams.

Q6: What is the "Normalization" process in RRB exams?

Since the exam is conducted in multiple shifts, RRB uses a mathematical formula to normalize marks to ensure that the difficulty level of any specific shift does not unfairly advantage or disadvantage a candidate.

entry.title: Supreme Court of India Recruitment 2026 for 90 Law Clerks
entry.content:
Supreme Court of India Recruitment 2026 for 90 Law Clerks

Advertisement No. F.21(LC)/2026-SC(RC): The Supreme Court of India has announced a prestigious opportunity for law graduates and final-year law students to work as Law Clerk-cum-Research Associates on a short-term contractual basis for the 2026-2027 term. A panel of approximately 90 candidates will be selected for this full-time assignment, which offers a consolidated monthly remuneration of ₹1,00,000. The selection process involves a three-phase examination, including an MCQ test, a subjective written test, and an interview. The online application window is open from January 20, 2026, to February 07, 2026.

Post Name: Law Clerk-cum-Research Associate
Last Date: 07 February 2026
Total Posts: 90 (Approximately)

The Supreme Court of India, the apex judicial body of the country, has opened its doors for aspiring legal minds to gain invaluable experience by working closely with the judiciary. Through its latest advertisement, the SCI is seeking to prepare a panel of around 90 candidates for the engagement of Law Clerk-cum-Research Associates. This role is a highly sought-after position for young law professionals, offering a unique opportunity to assist the Hon'ble Judges of the Supreme Court in their judicial and research work. It provides an unparalleled insight into the workings of the highest court of the land and exposure to a wide spectrum of legal issues.

This engagement is purely contractual for the 2026-2027 term and is a full-time commitment. Selected candidates will receive a substantial consolidated stipend of ₹1,00,000 per month. The selection process is comprehensive, designed to test the candidates' legal acumen, analytical abilities, and writing skills through a multi-stage examination scheduled for March 07, 2026. This is not just a job but a stepping stone for a distinguished career in law, be it in litigation, academia, or the corporate sector. Eligible candidates, including those in their final year of law school, are encouraged to apply online before the deadline of February 07, 2026.

Supreme Court Law Clerk Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take note of the crucial dates and details for this recruitment process. The entire process, from application to admit card generation, will be conducted online.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Supreme Court of India (SCI)
Advertisement Number F.21(LC)/2026-SC(RC)
Post Name Law Clerk-cum-Research Associate
Total Panel Size Approximately 90 Candidates
Application Start Date 20 January 2026
Application Last Date 07 February 2026
Written Examination Date 07 March 2026
Application Mode Online
Job Type Short-term Contractual Assignment
Official Website www.sci.gov.in

Vacancy and Remuneration Details

This recruitment is for the preparation of a panel for a short-term contractual assignment. The details are as follows:

Position Number of Panelists Monthly Remuneration
Law Clerk-cum-Research Associate Approximately 90 ₹1,00,000/- (Consolidated)
Note: This is a full-time contractual assignment for the term 2026-2027. The engagement will not confer any right for regular appointment or continuance in the service of the Supreme Court of India. Law Clerks are prohibited from practicing as advocates in any court during their term.

Application and Test Fee

  • All candidates are required to pay a non-refundable Application/Test Fee of ₹750/- plus applicable bank charges.

The fee must be paid online through the payment gateway provided by UCO Bank. No other mode of payment will be accepted.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all the essential qualifications and age criteria to be eligible for the Written Tests.

Criteria Eligibility Details
Educational Qualification
  1. Law Graduates: The candidate must be a Law Graduate holding a Bachelor's Degree in Law (including a 5-Year Integrated Degree Course) from a university/institution recognized by the Bar Council of India for enrolment as an Advocate. This must be acquired before taking up the assignment.
  2. Final Year Law Students: Candidates currently in the fifth year of a Five-Year Integrated Law Course or the third year of a Three-Year Law Course are also eligible to apply. However, they must furnish proof of acquiring their Law qualification before their engagement as a Law Clerk begins.
Essential Skills

Candidates must possess:

  • Strong research and analytical skills.
  • Excellent writing and comprehension abilities.
  • Proficiency in computer operations, including the ability to retrieve information from legal search engines like e-SCR, Manupatra, SCC Online, LexisNexis, Westlaw, etc.
Age Limit (as on 07.02.2026)
  • Minimum Age: 20 Years
  • Maximum Age: 32 Years

The candidate must not be below 20 years or above 32 years of age as on the closing date of the online application.

Selection Process

The selection of Law Clerk-cum-Research Associates is a rigorous three-phase process designed to identify candidates with strong legal knowledge, analytical depth, and effective communication skills.

  1. Part I: Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ) Test: This initial phase is an objective test designed to assess the candidate's foundational knowledge of the law, their ability to apply legal principles, and their comprehension skills.
  2. Part II: Subjective Written Test: Candidates clearing Part I will proceed to this stage, which is held on the same day. This descriptive test evaluates the candidate's writing abilities, analytical skills, and the capacity to articulate legal arguments coherently.
  3. Part III: Interview: Candidates who successfully qualify in both Part I and Part II will be called for a personal interview. This final stage assesses the candidate's personality, communication skills, presence of mind, and overall suitability for the role.

Shortlisting Criteria: If a large number of candidates secure the minimum qualifying marks (60%) in Part I, the answer sheets for Part II will be evaluated for candidates in a 1:10 ratio against the number of vacancies. Subsequently, candidates qualifying in both written tests will be called for the interview in a 1:3 ratio.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The written examination will be conducted in two parts on the same day across 23 cities in India. The total duration and marks for each part are structured to comprehensively test the candidates.

Part Test Name Mode Key Details
Part I Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ) Test Online (Computer Based) - Tests understanding and application of law, and comprehension skills.
- There will be negative marking of 0.25 marks for each incorrect answer.
Part II Subjective Written Test Pen-and-Paper (Hybrid Mode) - Questions will be displayed on the computer screen.
- Answers must be written on paper provided at the exam center.
- Tests writing and analytical skills.

Indicative Syllabus:

While the Supreme Court has not prescribed a detailed syllabus, based on the nature of the role, candidates should prepare thoroughly in the following areas:

  • Core Law Subjects: Constitutional Law, Jurisprudence, Indian Penal Code (IPC), Code of Civil Procedure (CPC), Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC), Indian Evidence Act, Law of Contracts, Property Law, Torts, and Family Law.
  • Specialized Laws: Company Law, Intellectual Property Rights, Environmental Law, and Administrative Law.
  • Legal and General English: Comprehension, essay writing, and legal drafting skills are crucial for the subjective paper.
  • Legal Research & Current Affairs: In-depth knowledge of recent landmark judgments of the Supreme Court and High Courts, significant legal developments, and contemporary legal issues is essential.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for the position of Law Clerk-cum-Research Associate, as it is a research-based and academic role.

How to Prepare for the Exam and Interview

A focused and disciplined approach is necessary to succeed in this highly competitive selection process.

Preparation for Written Tests (Part I & II):

  • Build a Strong Foundation: Revisit the bare acts and authoritative commentaries for all core law subjects. Conceptual clarity is key for both MCQ and subjective questions.
  • Stay Judicially Aware: Regularly read and analyze recent and landmark judgments of the Supreme Court. Follow legal news portals like LiveLaw, Bar & Bench, and the Supreme Court's official website to stay updated on legal developments.
  • Practice Writing: This is non-negotiable for the subjective paper. Practice writing case comments, legal essays, and answers to previous years' judiciary exam questions. Focus on structuring your arguments logically and writing concisely.
  • Mock Tests: Solve as many MCQs as possible from sources like CLAT PG, UGC NET (Law), and Judicial Services exam papers. This will help you manage time and improve accuracy, especially with negative marking.

Preparation for the Interview (Part III):

  • Know Your CV: Be prepared to discuss everything mentioned in your application form, including internships, papers published, and areas of interest.
  • Revise Core Concepts: The interview panel will test your fundamental understanding of the law. Be ready for in-depth questions on your favorite subjects.
  • Develop a Legal Opinion: Form well-reasoned opinions on recent controversial legal issues and landmark judgments.
  • Communication Skills: Practice articulating your thoughts clearly and confidently. Your demeanor, clarity, and legal reasoning will be assessed.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates should refer to standard textbooks and authoritative commentaries for their preparation.

  • Constitutional Law: "Introduction to the Constitution of India" by D.D. Basu; "Indian Constitutional Law" by M.P. Jain.
  • Jurisprudence: "Jurisprudence & Legal Theory" by V.D. Mahajan.
  • Criminal Law (IPC, CrPC, Evidence): Commentaries by Ratanlal & Dhirajlal; "Textbook on Indian Penal Code" by K.D. Gaur.
  • Civil Law (CPC, Contract, Property): "Code of Civil Procedure" by C.K. Takwani; "Contract & Specific Relief" by Avtar Singh.
  • For Current Legal Developments: Follow journals like SCC and legal news websites.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must follow these steps carefully:

  1. Visit the official Supreme Court of India website: www.sci.gov.in.
  2. Navigate to the 'Recruitment' section and find the link for "Engagement of Law Clerk-cum-Research Associates".
  3. Click on the application link and register by providing your basic details.
  4. Fill in the online application form with accurate personal, academic, and contact information.
  5. Upload a scanned copy of your recent photograph and signature as per the specified dimensions and size.
  6. Proceed to pay the application fee of ₹750/- through the online payment gateway.
  7. Before final submission, preview the application form to ensure all details are correct. No modifications can be made after submission.
  8. Submit the application and take a printout of the final form and the fee receipt for your records. Note down your Application Number for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

During the online application process, candidates will need to upload scanned copies of the following:

  1. A recent passport-size photograph.
  2. Signature.

Candidates should keep all their academic certificates, ID proof, and other relevant documents ready, as they will be required during the later stages of the selection process (document verification).

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification and Scheme Click Here
Apply Online Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is this a permanent government job?

No, this is a short-term contractual assignment for the 2026-2027 term only. It does not grant any right to a permanent position in the Supreme Court.

Q2: I am in the 4th year of a 5-year law course. Am I eligible to apply?

No, only students who are in their final year (5th year of a 5-year course or 3rd year of a 3-year course) are eligible to apply.

Q3: Is there any negative marking in the examination?

Yes, there is a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the Part-I (MCQ) Test.

Q4: What is the monthly salary for a Law Clerk?

The position offers a consolidated remuneration of ₹1,00,000 per month for the duration of the assignment.

entry.title: Punjab Electricity Department Recruitment 2025: Notification for 262 Technical and Admin Posts
entry.content:
Punjab Electricity Department Recruitment 2025: Notification for 262 Technical and Admin Posts

Joint Recruitment by PSTCL and PSPCL for Assistant Engineers, Junior Engineers, LDCs, and Accountants under CRA 12/2025

Post Name: Assistant Engineer (Electrical), Junior Engineer (Electrical/Civil/Comm), LDC (Typist/Accounts), Assistant Manager (IT), Accounts Officer
Last Date: December 16, 2025
Total Posts: 262 Vacancies

The Punjab State Transmission Corporation Limited (PSTCL) and the Punjab State Power Corporation Limited (PSPCL), the primary electricity entities owned by the Government of Punjab, have joined forces to launch a significant recruitment campaign for the year 2025. This joint recruitment, identified under Centralized Recruitment Advertisement Number CRA No. 12/2025, is a major initiative to bolster the technical and administrative workforce of the state's energy sector. As Punjab continues to modernize its power grid and expand its renewable energy footprint, the need for skilled engineers, financial experts, and administrative support staff has reached a critical point. This notification serves as a golden opportunity for engineering graduates, diploma holders, and commerce professionals to secure a permanent Punjab State Government job with highly competitive pay scales.

The 2025 recruitment drive aims to fill 262 vacancies across 11 different functional categories. These roles are pivotal for the generation, transmission, and efficient distribution of electricity across the state of Punjab. Whether it is managing high-voltage transmission lines as an Assistant Engineer, executing field projects as a Junior Engineer, or managing the complex financial audits of the corporation as a Divisional Accountant, each position offers a unique set of challenges and career growth. The application process is scheduled to begin on November 17, 2025, and will remain active until December 16, 2025. Successful candidates will be inducted into the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) pay levels, providing financial stability and the prestige of serving one of India's most efficient power utilities.

Working in the Punjab Power Sector is more than just employment; it is a chance to be part of the state's infrastructure backbone. The selection process is strictly merit-based, conducted through a highly transparent Online Computer Based Test (CBT). One unique and mandatory aspect of this recruitment is the Punjabi language requirement; candidates must have passed Punjabi at the Matriculation level to be eligible for any post. This 2000-word exhaustive guide provides a comprehensive analysis of post-wise vacancy distributions, detailed eligibility criteria, salary matrix components, the specific two-phase exam pattern for Group C posts, and expert-recommended preparation strategies to help you navigate the competitive landscape of the Punjab Electricity Department.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must be highly attentive to the timeline provided by PSTCL. The application portal is centralized, and it is recommended to register early to avoid the last-minute technical rush. The age and qualification eligibility will be calculated as of the start of the year 2025 to ensure fairness to all aspirants.

Event Description Dates and Details
Official Notification Number CRA No. 12/2025
Online Registration Opening Date November 17, 2025 (02:00 PM)
Last Date to Submit Application December 16, 2025 (11:55 PM)
Online Fee Payment Deadline December 16, 2025
Job Nature Permanent Basis / Punjab State Govt PSU
Job Location Punjab (Statewide)

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Salary Structure

The 262 vacancies are distributed across Group A, B, and C cadres. The salary is structured as per the 7th CPC Matrix, which has been adopted by the Punjab Government. Employees are also entitled to state-specific allowances and benefits.

Post Designation Vacancies (PSTCL + PSPCL) Initial Basic Pay (7th CPC)
Assistant Engineer (OT) / Electrical 61 ₹47,600
Assistant Manager (IT) 03 ₹47,600
Accounts Officer 02 ₹47,600
Junior Engineer (Electrical/Civil/Comm) 131 ₹35,400
Divisional Accountant / Law Officer 13 ₹35,400
Lower Division Clerk (Typist/Accounts) 50 ₹19,900
Telephone Mechanic 10 ₹19,900
Grand Total Vacancies 262 -
Remuneration Policy: As per the Government of Punjab's latest notification, during the probation period of 3 years, selected candidates will be paid only the Fixed Monthly Emoluments (Initial Basic Pay). No allowances like DA, HRA, or TA are provided until the successful completion of the probation period. Once regularized, the total gross salary will increase significantly.

Eligibility Criteria and Educational Qualifications

Eligibility for Punjab Electricity jobs is defined by academic qualifications, age, and a mandatory linguistic proficiency in Punjabi. Candidates must ensure they have obtained their degrees or diplomas from AICTE/UGC-recognized institutions.

  • Assistant Engineer (Electrical): Full-time B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc. in Electrical Engineering or Electrical & Electronics Engineering with a minimum of 55% marks.
  • Junior Engineer (Electrical): 3-year Diploma in Electrical/Electronics Engineering with 60% marks OR B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering with 50% marks.
  • Assistant Manager (IT): B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc. in CS or IT with 60% marks OR MCA with 60% marks.
  • Lower Division Clerk (LDC): Bachelor's degree in any discipline with at least 50% marks and a valid 120-hour computer course certificate. For LDC Accounts, a B.Com degree is mandatory.
  • Mandatory Language: Candidates must have passed Punjabi at the Matriculation (10th) level or its equivalent. This is a non-negotiable requirement for all posts.

Age Limit (as of Jan 1, 2025): The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum is 37 years. Upper Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST and BC of Punjab, 10 years for PwD, and up to 40 years for certain categories of women (widows/divorcees) as per Punjab State rules.

Selection Process and Examination Roadmap

The selection is based entirely on merit in an Online Computer Based Test (CBT). There is no interview for any of the posts, ensuring a transparent and meritocratic recruitment. The examination structure varies based on the group of the post.

  1. Group A & B selection: A single-stage online CBT of 100 marks. Final merit is prepared based on these marks.
  2. Group C Selection (LDC/Mechanic): A two-phase examination process.
    • Phase-I: Punjabi Language Qualifying Test (50 Marks). Qualifying in this is mandatory to move to Phase-II.
    • Phase-II: Main Competitive Exam (100 Marks). Merit is prepared based solely on Phase-II marks.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates shortlisted based on merit are called for a verification of their original certificates, including the Punjabi language proof and domicile certificates.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Understanding the syllabus is key to cracking the PSTCL/PSPCL exam. The technical posts have a high weightage on engineering subjects, while clerical posts focus on aptitude and language.

Group A & B Pattern (Assistant & Junior Engineers)

  • Technical Domain (50 Questions): Core engineering subjects (Electrical/Civil/IT) based on the degree/diploma level.
  • Punjabi Language (20 Questions): Basic grammar and vocabulary.
  • General Knowledge (10 Questions): Current affairs and Punjab GK.
  • Reasoning & Math (10 Questions): Numerical ability and logical puzzles.
  • General English (10 Questions): Basic grammar and comprehension.
  • Negative Marking: 0.25 (1/4th) marks deducted for every wrong answer.

Group C Pattern (LDC / Typist)

  • Phase-I (Qualifying): 50 MCQs on Punjabi Grammar. Minimum 25 marks (50%) required to pass. No negative marking in this phase.
  • Phase-II (Main): Static GK (20), Computer Awareness (20), Reasoning (20), Numerical Ability (20), and English (20). Total 100 Marks with 0.25 negative marking.

Strategic Preparation Roadmap for Punjab Electricity Exam

Securing a rank in the top 262 requires a disciplined 4-month preparation strategy. Since the competition is intense, balancing technical depth with linguistic proficiency is vital.

Focus on Technical Core: For JE and AE aspirants, the 50-mark technical section is the primary differentiator. For Electrical engineers, focus heavily on Power Systems, Electrical Machines, and Circuit Theory. For Civil engineers, focus on RCC, Surveying, and Soil Mechanics. Use standard GATE or IES objective books to practice high-level questions.

Punjabi Language Mastery: Do not take the Punjabi qualifying test lightly. Many technical candidates fail this stage. Use 10th-standard PSEB (Punjab School Education Board) textbooks to revise grammar, idioms, and literature basics. This is particularly important for Group C candidates who must score 50% to even have their main paper checked.

Punjab General Knowledge: Spend at least 30 minutes daily reading about Punjab’s Geography, History (Sikh History), and Current Policies. Questions on the Bhakra-Nangal project, the rivers of Punjab, and recent administrative changes in the state are very common in PSTCL exams.

Best Books for Preparation

Using the right study material for Punjab-specific exams is critical. Below are the top recommendations from successful candidates.

Electrical Engineering: Objective Electrical Technology by V.K. Mehta or R.K. Rajput.
Civil Engineering: Objective Civil Engineering by J.K. Gupta or Khurmi.
Punjabi Language: "Sadda Punjab" for GK and PSEB 10th Standard Grammar books.
General Aptitude: Quantitative Aptitude and Reasoning Ability by R.S. Aggarwal.
General Awareness: Lucent's General Knowledge and Manorama Yearbook.
Computer Awareness: Objective Computer Awareness by Arihant Publications.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step Guide

The application process is conducted through the recruitment portal of PSTCL. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure your application is valid.

  1. Visit the official website of PSTCL: www.pstcl.org.
  2. Navigate to the "Recruitment" tab and click on the link for CRA 12/2025.
  3. Phase 1 Registration: Choose "New Registration" and enter your Name, Mobile, and Email. You will receive an OTP and registration ID.
  4. Phase 2 Filling: Login and fill in your educational details, Punjab domicile status, and Punjabi Language proficiency details.
  5. Upload Documents: Scan and upload your recent passport photograph, signature, and Punjabi Language Certificate as per the specified sizes (JPEG).
  6. Payment: Pay the application fee through the online gateway (Debit Card/UPI/Net Banking). Save the transaction e-receipt.
  7. Final Submission: Review your application in the preview window. Click "Submit" and take a printout of the application form for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready in digital format before starting the application to avoid session timeouts on the registration portal.

  1. Identity Proof: Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, or PAN Card.
  2. Matriculation (10th) Certificate: Essential as proof of date of birth and Punjabi language verification.
  3. Degree / Diploma Certificate: Final marksheets and certificates of the qualifying exam (B.Tech/Diploma/B.Com).
  4. Caste Certificate: Valid SC/ST/BC certificate issued by the competent authority of the Government of Punjab.
  5. Domicile Certificate: Mandatory to claim age relaxation and reservation benefits for Punjab residents.
  6. Experience Certificate: Mandatory only for the Law Officer post (3 years experience).

Application Fee Details

  • General (UR), OBC, and EWS Candidates: ₹1416/- (₹1200 + 18% GST).
  • Scheduled Caste (SC) Candidates: ₹885/- (₹750 + 18% GST).
  • Persons with Disabilities (PwD) Candidates: ₹885/- (₹750 + 18% GST).

The fee must be paid exclusively through the online portal. Bank charges, if any, will be additional. Please note that once the fee is paid, it is strictly non-refundable and non-adjustable for any future recruitment cycle.

Important Links

Resource Description Official Direct Link
PSTCL Official Recruitment Portal Click Here to Apply
Detailed Notification PDF (CRA 12/2025) Download Here
Official PSPCL Website Visit PSPCL Site

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Are candidates from outside Punjab eligible to apply?

Yes, candidates from other states can apply for the Unreserved (UR) category. However, the Punjabi language requirement is mandatory for everyone. You must have passed the Punjabi language at the Matriculation level to be eligible for selection.

Q2: Is there a probation period for PSTCL jobs?

Yes. There is a mandatory probation period of 3 years. During this time, you will only receive the fixed monthly basic pay. Allowances and full salary benefits are granted only after the successful completion of probation.

Q3: What is the age relaxation for BC candidates?

Candidates belonging to the Backward Class (BC) of Punjab are eligible for a 5-year relaxation in the upper age limit, making the maximum age 42 years as of January 1, 2025.

Q4: Is there an interview for Assistant Engineer or Junior Engineer posts?

No. According to the 2025 recruitment rules for PSTCL/PSPCL, there are no interviews. Selection is based 100% on the merit obtained in the Computer Based Test (CBT), followed by document verification.

Q5: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you meet the eligibility criteria for each. However, you must submit separate applications and pay separate fees for each post. Ensure that the exam dates do not overlap once the schedule is released.

Q6: What is the "Punjabi Qualifying Test" for Group C?

This is a 50-mark objective test focusing on the Punjabi language. It is a state-level requirement where a candidate must score at least 25 marks (50%) to qualify. Failure to pass this test will result in immediate disqualification, even if you score high in the main competitive exam.

entry.title: Punjab Anganwadi Recruitment 2025: Huge Opening for 6,110 Workers and Helpers
entry.content:
Punjab Anganwadi Recruitment 2025: Huge Opening for 6,110 Workers and Helpers

Direct Merit-Based Recruitment for Women across All Districts of Punjab - Apply Online at sswcd.punjab.gov.in

Post Name: Anganwadi Worker (AWW) and Anganwadi Helper (AWH)
Last Date: December 10, 2025
Total Posts: 6,110 Vacancies

The Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development (SSWCD), Government of Punjab, has officially launched its largest recruitment drive for the year 2025. This massive initiative is aimed at strengthening the grassroots healthcare and educational infrastructure in rural and urban Punjab by filling 6,110 vacancies for Anganwadi Workers (AWW) and Anganwadi Helpers (AWH). This recruitment is exclusively reserved for female candidates who are residents of the local villages or municipal wards where the centers are located. By empowering local women with these roles, the Punjab government intends to improve the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS), which covers maternal health, child nutrition, and pre-school education for children under the age of six.

For women seeking a stable government career in their own locality, this is an unmatched opportunity. The 2025 drive offers 1,316 positions for Workers and 4,794 positions for Helpers. One of the most attractive features of this recruitment is that there is no written examination. The selection is based purely on a Merit List prepared using the candidate’s marks in their 10th, 12th, and Graduation exams. This ensures that academic achievers from local communities are prioritized. The application process is entirely online, having started on November 19, 2025, and will remain open until the final deadline of December 10, 2025. Successful candidates will become the primary link between the government’s welfare schemes and the common citizens of Punjab.

In the following sections of this 2000-word guide, we will explore the intricate details of district-wise vacancy distributions, the specific weightage given to academic scores, the mandatory residency and linguistic requirements, and a step-by-step tutorial on navigating the online application portal. Whether you are a graduate aiming for a Worker role or a 12th-pass candidate looking for a Helper position, this guide provides the professional insights needed to ensure your application is successful and error-free.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must stay vigilant regarding the timeline provided by the SSWCD Punjab. Unlike central government exams, Anganwadi recruitments are deeply localized. It is recommended to verify the specific vacancy in your particular Ward or Village before applying, as applications for wards other than your own will be rejected during the document verification stage.

Event Description Dates and Details
Notification Release Date November 2025
Online Registration Opening November 19, 2025
Last Date to Submit Online Application December 10, 2025
Merit List Publication (Expected) January 2026
Job Type Direct Merit Basis (Honorarium)
Gender Restriction Females Only

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown by Post

The 6,110 vacancies are distributed across all 23 districts of Punjab. While the majority of the roles are for Helpers, the Worker positions are critical for the administrative and pedagogical management of the Anganwadi centers.

Post Designation Total Vacancies Educational Requirement
Anganwadi Worker (AWW) 1,316 Graduation (Any Stream)
Anganwadi Helper (AWH) 4,794 12th Standard (Higher Secondary)
Grand Total 6,110 -
Strategic Advantage: Because the recruitment is merit-based, candidates with higher percentages in their respective qualifying exams have a much higher chance of selection. Additionally, having a B.Ed or a Master’s Degree can provide bonus points in several blocks, although the minimum requirement is Graduation for Workers.

Honorarium and Salary Structure

Anganwadi staff are considered "Voluntary Social Workers" and are paid a fixed monthly Honorarium instead of a traditional salary scale. The Government of Punjab, in coordination with the Central Government, has revised these amounts to provide better financial security to the staff.

  • Anganwadi Worker: Approximately ₹9,500 to ₹10,500 per month. This includes the central share and the state government’s top-up.
  • Anganwadi Helper: Approximately ₹5,000 to ₹6,500 per month.
  • Other Benefits: Regular staff members are also eligible for an annual increase in honorarium, mobile data allowances for digital reporting via the "Poshan Tracker" app, and periodic insurance coverage under government schemes.

Note: These amounts are subject to periodic revisions by the Punjab Cabinet. The roles also provide significant social status and the convenience of working within walking distance from your home.

District Wise Vacancy Distribution

Vacancies are allotted based on the population and the number of operational centers in each district. Below is a snapshot of major districts. Candidates are advised to check the Full Notification PDF for a village-by-village breakdown.

District Name Worker Posts Helper Posts
Ludhiana 170 627
Amritsar 85 293
Jalandhar 153 318
Hoshiarpur 125 366
Gurdaspur 104 353
Patiala 73 246
Bathinda 44 216
Ferozepur 31 230

Residency Rule (Mandatory): To apply for a post in a specific village or municipal ward, the candidate must be a permanent resident of that exact village or ward. This is verified using the Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, and a Domicile Certificate issued by the Tehsildar.

Eligibility Criteria and Qualifications

The eligibility criteria for the Punjab Anganwadi Recruitment 2025 are designed to ensure that the staff is well-educated and culturally integrated into the local community.

  • Educational Qualification for Worker: Graduation in any stream from a recognized university. Linguistic Requirement: Must have passed the Punjabi Language exam at the Matriculation (10th) level.
  • Educational Qualification for Helper: 12th Standard (10+2) pass from a recognized board. Punjabi at the 10th level is also mandatory for this post.
  • Age Limit: Minimum 18 years and maximum 37 years for General category.
  • Age Relaxation: SC/ST and BC candidates are eligible for relaxation up to 42 years. Widows and divorcees are eligible for relaxation up to 45 years as per Punjab Government norms.

Selection Process and Merit Calculation

Since there is no written test, the selection process is based on a Point-Based Merit System. The marks are calculated based on your performance in standard academic exams.

  1. Academic Marks Weightage: A major portion of the merit (out of 100) is calculated by converting the percentage obtained in the 10th, 12th, and Graduation into proportional points.
  2. Bonus Points for Higher Education: Candidates with relevant degrees such as Child Development, Nutrition, or Social Work may receive additional marks.
  3. Verification of Documents: Once the draft merit list is published, candidates are called to the District Program Officer’s (DPO) office to verify their original residency, education, and caste proofs.
  4. Final Allotment: After addressing any objections, the final list is approved, and the candidates are invited for a formal induction training program.

Required Documents for Online Application

Scanning the correct documents in high resolution is essential. If the residency proof is blurry or invalid, the application will be rejected automatically without further notice.

  • Recent Passport Size Photograph: Against a light background.
  • Educational Marksheets: 10th (as proof of Punjabi language and DOB), 12th, and Graduation Degree.
  • Domicile Certificate: Proof of residence in the specific village or ward.
  • Caste Certificate: If applying under SC/ST/BC categories.
  • Identity Proof: Aadhaar Card and Voter ID (Must show the correct residential address).
  • Experience Certificate: If the candidate has previously worked as a mobile creche worker or in a similar government-aided project (Optional).

Expert Tips for a Successful Application

Securing an Anganwadi post in 2025 is highly competitive despite the absence of an exam. Since the selection is merit-based, the accuracy of your data entry is your only "preparation." Follow this roadmap:

Check the Ward Mapping: Before filling the form, visit your local Child Development Project Officer (CDPO) office. Confirm the exact boundaries of your ward. Often, municipal boundaries change, and applying for the wrong ward is the leading cause of disqualification in Punjab.

Ensure Punjabi Language Proof: If you did not study Punjabi in your 10th standard, you must produce an equivalent certificate from the Punjab School Education Board (PSEB). This is a non-negotiable requirement for all government jobs in Punjab.

Verify Marks Calculation: During the online entry, ensure you enter the marks exactly as they appear in the original marksheet. Do not round off percentages unless the portal specifically asks for it. Even a 0.1% difference can lead to a dispute in the merit ranking.

Application Fee and Payment Portal

The application fee must be paid online through the integrated payment gateway on the SSWCD portal. The fee is used for the processing of applications and the verification of documents.

Candidate Category Worker Fee Helper Fee
General / OBC (Non-Creamy) ₹500/- ₹300/-
SC / ST / EWS of Punjab ₹250/- ₹150/-

Payment Mode: Use UPI, Debit Card, or Net Banking. Ensure you save the Transaction ID and the payment success screenshot for future reference during the document verification stage.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step Guide

Follow these steps carefully to ensure your application is registered correctly in the state database.

  1. Visit the official portal: sswcd.punjab.gov.in.
  2. Locate and click on the "Recruitment of Anganwadi Workers and Helpers 2025" link.
  3. New Registration: Register with your mobile number and email. You will receive an OTP for verification.
  4. Profile Filling: Select your District, Block, and Village/Ward from the dropdown menus.
  5. Enter your academic scores for 10th, 12th, and Graduation precisely.
  6. Upload Documents: Scan and upload the required PDF/JPEG files (marksheet, domicile, photo).
  7. Fee Payment: Complete the online payment based on your category.
  8. Final Submission: Review all details in the preview window. Click "Submit" and take a printout of the final application form.

Important Links

Resource Description Official Direct Link
Online Application Portal Apply Online Now
Full District-Wise Notification PDF Download PDF
Official Website (SSWCD Punjab) Visit Site

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can unmarried women apply for these posts?

Yes. Any female candidate meeting the residency and educational requirements can apply. While preference is often given to widows and destitute women in some categories, unmarried and married women are both equally eligible to compete on merit.

Q2: What if I have a Master’s degree? Can I get extra points?

Generally, for the post of Worker, the merit is calculated based on Graduation marks. However, if the department provides bonus points for "Higher Qualification" in the final merit criteria, your Master’s degree will be an added advantage. Always mention your highest qualification in the form.

Q3: Is it mandatory to be from the same village?

Yes. Residency is the most critical eligibility factor. If you live in Village A but apply for a vacancy in Village B, your application will be rejected even if you have 100% marks. You must have proof of residing in that specific Anganwadi center's operational area.

Q4: What is the last date to apply?

The last date for submitting the online application along with the fee is December 10, 2025. It is advised not to wait for the final day as the portal may experience heavy traffic.

Q5: Is there any interview for the Anganwadi Worker post?

No. Selection is purely 100% merit-based. There is no written test and no personal interview. The verification of documents at the district level is the final stage before joining.

Q6: Can a 12th-pass candidate apply for the Worker post?

No. As per the 2025 rules, the minimum qualification for an Anganwadi Worker (AWW) is Graduation. A 12th-pass candidate is only eligible to apply for the Anganwadi Helper (AWH) post.

entry.title: IISER Tirupati Recruitment 2025: Group A, B & C Non-Teaching Posts Open
entry.content:
IISER Tirupati Recruitment 2025: Group A, B & C Non-Teaching Posts Open

A premier Institute of National Importance invites applications for Engineer, Officer, Assistant, and other administrative and technical roles. Apply online before February 02, 2026.

Post Name: Assistant Executive Engineer, Medical Officer, Assistant Registrar, Nurse, Superintendent, Technical Assistant, Junior Office Assistant, Lab Assistant, and more.
Last Date: 02 February 2026
Total Posts: 20+ (across various posts)

The Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER), Tirupati, a premier autonomous Institute of National Importance established by the Ministry of Education, Government of India, has released a significant recruitment notification (ADVT. NO.: 24/2025) for various non-teaching positions. The institute is dedicated to the promotion of high-quality science education and research in the country and is seeking applications from Indian nationals with excellent academic records and relevant work experience to join its administrative and technical teams.

This recruitment drive offers a fantastic opportunity for individuals to build a rewarding career in a leading scientific institution. The vacancies span across Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' levels, including roles like Assistant Executive Engineer, Medical Officer, Assistant Registrar, Nurse, Superintendent, and various Technical and Laboratory Assistants. The selection process will involve a written test, skill test, or interview, depending on the post. Interested and eligible candidates are required to submit their applications online through the designated Samarth portal on or before 5:00 PM on February 02, 2026. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all the essential details of this recruitment process.

Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. This is a permanent Central Government autonomous body job opportunity located in Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER), Tirupati
Advertisement Number 24/2025
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Autonomous Institute)
Job Location Tirupati, Andhra Pradesh
Last Date to Apply Online 02 February 2026 (up to 5:00 PM)

Vacancy Details

The Institute has invited applications for a variety of posts across different groups and pay levels. A detailed breakdown of the vacancies is as follows:

Post Name Pay Level & Group Number of Vacancies
Assistant Executive Engineer (Civil) Level 10, Group A 01 - UR
Medical Officer Level 10, Group A 01 - UR
Assistant Registrar Level 10, Group A 01 - UR
Nurse Level 7, Group B 01 - UR
Private Secretary Level 7, Group B 01 - UR
Superintendent Level 6, Group B 02 - (01 UR, 01 OBC-NCL)
Technical Assistant (IT) Level 6, Group B 02 - (01 ST, 01 EWS)
Technical Assistant (Biology) Level 6, Group B 02 - (01 UR, 01 OBC-NCL)
Junior Library Superintendent Level 6, Group B 01 - UR
Junior Translator (Rajbhasha) Level 6, Group B 01 - UR
Junior Office Assistant (Multi Skill) Level 4, Group C 06 - (05 UR, 01 OBC-NCL)
Lab Assistant (Biology) Level 3, Group C 01 - SC
Lab Assistant (Chemistry) Level 3, Group C 01 - OBC-NCL (Lien Vacancy)
Lab Assistant (Physics) Level 3, Group C 01 - UR (Reserved for PwBD)
Total Vacancies 22
Note: The number of posts indicated may increase or decrease as per the Institute's requirement. Women candidates are encouraged to apply to promote gender balance.

Application Fee Details

  • For Group A Posts: ₹1000/- (₹500/- for SC/ST applicants).
  • For Group B & C Posts: ₹750/- (₹375/- for SC/ST applicants).

A fee waiver is applicable to PwBD candidates only. The application fee once paid will not be refunded or re-adjusted under any circumstances.

Eligibility Criteria

Each post has specific requirements for educational qualifications, experience, and age limits. The crucial date for determining the age limit is the closing date for receipt of applications.

Post Name Eligibility Requirements (Qualification, Experience, Age)
Assistant Executive Engineer (Civil) First Class Bachelor's Degree in Civil/Electrical Engineering. OR for employees, 5 years of relevant experience in Pay Level-7 with a First Class Bachelor's Degree in Civil/Electrical Engineering. Age: Not more than 40 years.
Medical Officer MBBS Degree and registration with a State or Indian Medical Register. OR for employees, 5 years of post-qualification experience in a reputed hospital. Age: Not more than 40 years.
Assistant Registrar Master's Degree with at least 55% marks. OR for employees, experience as Superintendent/Section Officer with a Master's Degree. Age: Not more than 40 years.
Nurse Master's Degree (with 50% marks) in Nursing OR First Class B.Sc. (Nursing) + registration with Nursing Council + 3 years of clinical experience in a minimum 50-bedded hospital. Age: Not more than 38 years.
Superintendent Master's Degree with 50% marks OR First Class Bachelor's degree. OR 5 years of experience for employees in a relevant field. Age: Not more than 35 years.
Technical Assistant (IT/Biology) B.E./B.Tech./Diploma/MCA/M.Sc./BS-MS with at least 55% marks in the appropriate field. OR 5 years of relevant experience for employees. Age: Not more than 35 years.
Junior Office Assistant (Multi Skill) Bachelor's Degree with 50% marks and excellent computer proficiency (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). OR for employees, 4 years of relevant experience. Age: Not more than 33 years.
Lab Assistant (Biology/Chemistry/Physics) B.Sc. in the respective subject with 50% marks. OR for employees, 3 years of experience in handling laboratory equipment. Age: Not more than 30 years.

Selection Process

The selection process for these non-teaching posts at IISER Tirupati will be rigorous and merit-based. The Institute reserves the right to adopt different selection methodologies depending on the post and the number of applications received.

  1. Shortlisting: The first step will be the screening of all applications received. Where the number of applications is large, the Institute reserves the right to restrict the number of candidates to be called for the selection process to a reasonable limit. This shortlisting may be done on the basis of qualifications and experience higher than the minimum prescribed in the advertisement. Therefore, candidates are advised to furnish full details of all their qualifications and experience.
  2. Selection Test: Shortlisted candidates will be called for the selection process, which may include one or more of the following:
    • Written Test: A written examination may be conducted to test the candidate's subject knowledge, general aptitude, and analytical skills.
    • Skill Test / Trade Test: For posts requiring specific skills (e.g., Junior Office Assistant, Private Secretary, Technical Assistants), a practical test may be conducted to evaluate proficiency.
    • Interview / Personality Test: For Group A and other senior posts, a personal interview will be a crucial part of the selection process to assess the candidate's suitability, leadership qualities, and domain knowledge.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates qualifying in the selection test must produce original certificates for verification. Any discrepancy between the uploaded documents and the originals will lead to the cancellation of candidature.

Exam Pattern

The Institute has not specified a fixed exam pattern for the written test. However, if a written test is conducted, it is expected to be an objective-type (MCQ) examination. The syllabus will be based on the essential qualifications for the post. A general pattern might include:

  • Part A (General Aptitude): Questions on General Intelligence & Reasoning, Numerical Ability, General English, and General Awareness.
  • Part B (Domain Knowledge): Questions specific to the role and the required qualification (e.g., questions on Civil Engineering for the AEE post, questions on Biology/Chemistry for Lab Assistant posts, questions on Central Government Rules for administrative posts).

The detailed scheme of the examination, if conducted, will be uploaded on the Institute's website. Candidates are advised to monitor the website regularly.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these non-teaching posts. The selection is based on academic qualifications, experience, and performance in the selection tests.

How to Prepare

A focused and strategic preparation plan is essential to succeed in this competitive recruitment process.

  1. Thoroughly Read the Notification: The first step is to carefully read the official notification to understand the specific requirements, job description, and desirable qualifications for the post you are applying for.
  2. Strengthen Your Domain Knowledge: The selection will heavily depend on your expertise in your field. Revise the core subjects from your degree/diploma curriculum. For technical posts, focus on practical applications and troubleshooting.
  3. Prepare for General Aptitude: For posts where a written test is likely, prepare for general sections like Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and General English. Refer to standard competitive exam books.
  4. Stay Updated: Keep abreast of the latest developments in your field and general current affairs.
  5. Practice Skill Tests: If you are applying for a post that requires a skill test (like typing or computer proficiency), practice regularly to achieve the required speed and accuracy.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended resources to aid your preparation:

  • For Technical Subjects (Engineering/Science): Standard textbooks from your B.E./B.Tech/Diploma/M.Sc. curriculum are the best resources. Objective question banks for exams like GATE/NET can be helpful.
  • For Administrative Posts: "Swamy's Handbook" for Central Government staff, books on GFR, FR-SR, and CCS Rules.
  • For General Aptitude & Reasoning: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal and "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • For General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.

How to Apply

Candidates are required to submit the application online only using the link provided in the official notification. The process is as follows:

  1. Visit the official application portal: https://iisertirupatint.samarth.edu.in.
  2. Register on the portal if you are a new user. Log in with your credentials.
  3. Carefully fill out the online application form with all your personal, academic, and experience details.
  4. Upload clear and legible scanned copies of all required documents, including your photograph, signature, educational certificates, and experience certificates.
  5. Pay the application fee online as per your category. The fee is non-refundable.
  6. Review your application thoroughly before final submission. Once submitted, no changes will be allowed.
  7. Keep a copy of the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must upload scanned copies of the following documents during the online application process. It is the sole responsibility of the applicant to ensure that all uploaded documents are clear and legible.

  1. Recent passport-size photograph.
  2. Signature.
  3. Proof of Date of Birth (10th certificate).
  4. Certificates and marksheets of all educational qualifications.
  5. Experience certificates clearly mentioning the start date, end date, pay scale, and duties performed.
  6. Caste/Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD) in the prescribed format.
  7. No Objection Certificate (NOC) and Vigilance Clearance Certificate for candidates working in Government/PSU/Autonomous bodies.
  8. Any other relevant documents supporting your eligibility.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Click Here
Download Official Notification Click Here
Official Website https://www.iisertirupati.ac.in

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply?

The last date for submitting the online application is 5:00 PM on February 02, 2026.

Question 2: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you meet the eligibility criteria. However, you will need to submit a separate application and pay a separate fee for each post.

Question 3: Is a hard copy of the application required to be sent?

The notification only mentions an online application process. You should keep a printout for your records, but sending a hard copy is generally not required unless specified later.

Question 4: Can final-year students apply?

No, candidates must possess all the essential qualifications and experience as of the closing date of the advertisement.

entry.title: RRB NTPC Under Graduate Recruitment 2025: Apply for 3058 Vacancies
entry.content:
RRB NTPC Under Graduate Recruitment 2025: 3058 Vacancies for 12th Pass Candidates

Official Notification CEN No. 07/2025 - Direct Recruitment for Commercial Clerk, Accounts Clerk, and Junior Clerk Posts

Post Name: Commercial cum Ticket Clerk, Accounts Clerk cum Typist, Junior Clerk cum Typist, Trains Clerk
Last Date: November 27, 2025
Total Posts: 3058 Vacancies

The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRB) have officially released the notification for one of the most prestigious employment opportunities for young aspirants in India. Under the Centralized Employment Notice CEN No. 07/2025, the Indian Railways aims to fill 3058 vacancies in the Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) specifically tailored for Under Graduate (12th pass) candidates. This recruitment drive is a significant milestone for students who have recently completed their higher secondary education and are looking for a stable, high-growth career in the Central Government sector. The positions offered include Commercial cum Ticket Clerk, Accounts Clerk cum Typist, Junior Clerk cum Typist, and Trains Clerk, all of which are vital to the daily operations of the world's fourth-largest railway network.

A career with the RRB NTPC provides more than just a monthly paycheck; it offers job security, social prestige, and an extensive range of benefits including medical care, travel concessions, and a robust pension system. For the 2025 recruitment cycle, the application window is set to open on October 28, 2025, and will conclude on November 27, 2025. Candidates must undergo a rigorous multi-stage selection process, which includes two stages of Computer Based Tests (CBT), followed by a Typing Skill Test for specific roles, and finally, Document Verification and Medical Examination. This exhaustive 2000-word guide is designed to provide you with all the necessary insights, from understanding the job profiles to formulating a winning preparation strategy.

As the Indian Railways continues to modernize with initiatives like Vande Bharat trains and the redevelopment of world-class railway stations, the need for efficient administrative and commercial staff has never been higher. These "popular category" jobs are highly sought after because they do not require a professional degree, yet they offer a professional working environment and clear paths for promotion to higher ministerial and officer grades. Whether you are aiming for the Commercial wing or the Accounts department, the RRB NTPC 2025 notification is your ticket to a lifelong career of service to the nation.

Important Highlights and Dates

Candidates must be mindful of the timeline to avoid any last-minute technical hurdles. RRB portals often experience heavy traffic during the final days of the application window. It is recommended to complete the registration process within the first two weeks of the opening date. Please note that once you choose an RRB zone, you cannot change it later in the process.

Event / Feature Dates and Details
Notification Number CEN No. 07/2025
Date of Notification October 4, 2025
Online Registration Opening Date October 28, 2025
Last Date for Online Application November 27, 2025 (11:59 PM)
Last Date for Online Fee Payment November 27, 2025
Expected CBT 1 Date To be announced (Expected Q1 2026)
Job Nature Permanent / Central Government

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown

The 3058 vacancies are distributed across various Under Graduate posts. Each role has its own functional area and demands a unique set of skills. While the Commercial cum Ticket Clerk is the largest category, the Clerk-Typist roles are essential for the administrative back-end of the railways.

Post Designation Pay Level (7th CPC) Number of Vacancies
Commercial cum Ticket Clerk Level 3 2424
Accounts Clerk cum Typist Level 2 394
Junior Clerk cum Typist Level 2 163
Trains Clerk Level 2 77
Total Combined Vacancies - 3058
Job Descriptions: Commercial cum Ticket Clerk: Responsible for ticket issuance and luggage booking. Junior Clerk cum Typist: Handles data entry and general office clerical work. Accounts Clerk cum Typist: Manages accounting records and payroll data. Trains Clerk: Responsible for maintaining records of train movements and carriage details.

Salary and Benefits Structure

The salary for RRB NTPC Under Graduate posts is governed by the 7th Central Pay Commission. While the initial basic pay is fixed based on the level, the actual "take-home" salary is significantly higher due to multiple government allowances.

  • Level 3 Posts: Initial Basic Pay of ₹21,700.
  • Level 2 Posts: Initial Basic Pay of ₹19,900.

Additional Benefits:

  1. Dearness Allowance (DA): Revised twice a year to combat inflation.
  2. House Rent Allowance (HRA): Varies between 9% to 27% depending on the category of city (X, Y, or Z).
  3. Transport Allowance (TA): To cover daily commuting costs.
  4. Medical Benefits: Complete healthcare coverage for the employee and their dependents through Railway Hospitals.
  5. Railway Passes: Free or concessional travel passes for self and family across India.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill the minimum eligibility requirements as of the closing date of the application. Failure to provide proof of eligibility during Document Verification will lead to disqualification.

  • Educational Qualification: Minimum 12th Standard (10+2) or its equivalent from a recognized board. No specific stream (Science, Commerce, or Arts) is required.
  • Age Limit (as of Jan 1, 2026): Minimum 18 years and maximum 30 years.
  • Age Relaxation: 3 years for OBC (NCL), 5 years for SC/ST, and up to 10 years for PwBD candidates.
  • Nationality: Candidate must be a citizen of India, Nepal, or Bhutan.

Note: For the "Typist" roles, while the qualification is 12th pass, candidates must eventually pass a proficiency test in typing with a speed of 30 words per minute (WPM) in English or 25 WPM in Hindi.

Selection Process

The RRB selection process is merit-based and highly competitive. It is designed to evaluate a candidate's speed, accuracy, and general intelligence.

Stage Evaluation Type Status
1st Stage CBT Screening Test (Objective MCQs) Compulsory for all
2nd Stage CBT Main Exam (Detailed Assessment) Merit-deciding stage
Typing Skill Test Skill-based assessment Only for Typist posts
Document Verification Scrutiny of original certificates Qualifying
Medical Exam Health and Vision Standards Qualifying

Negative Marking: There is a negative marking of 1/3 mark for every incorrect answer in both Stage 1 and Stage 2 CBTs. Normalization of marks will be done if the exam is conducted in multiple shifts.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Preparation begins with a deep dive into the exam pattern. The 1st Stage CBT is common for all posts, but the difficulty level is generally based on the 10+2 standard.

Stage 1 CBT Pattern (90 Minutes):

  • General Awareness (40 Questions): Current Events, History of India and Freedom Struggle, Physical, Social and Economic Geography of India, General Scientific and Technological Developments, Environmental issues.
