  • Mathematics (30 Questions): Number System, Decimals, Fractions, LCM, HCF, Ratio and Proportion, Mensuration, Time and Work, Time and Distance, Simple and Compound Interest.
  • General Intelligence & Reasoning (30 Questions): Analogies, Completion of Number and Alphabetical Series, Coding and Decoding, Venn Diagrams, Puzzle, Data Sufficiency.

Stage 2 CBT Pattern: The pattern remains similar to Stage 1, but the number of questions increases to 120 (50 GA, 35 Maths, 35 Reasoning), and the questions are of a higher difficulty level. This stage determines your final rank in the merit list.

Medical and Vision Standards

Railway duties often require working in shifts and being near tracks/platforms, which makes physical health and vision crucial. During the medical exam, candidates will be categorized as follows:

  • Category A-3: Distant Vision: 6/9, 6/9 with or without glasses (power of lenses not to exceed 2D). Near Vision: Sn. 0.6, 0.6. Must pass tests for Color Vision, Binocular Vision, and Night Vision.
  • Category B-2: Distant Vision: 6/9, 6/12 with or without glasses (power of lenses not to exceed 4D). Must pass tests for Binocular Vision.
  • Category C-2: Distant Vision: 6/12, Nil with or without glasses. Near Vision: 0.6 combined with or without glasses for reading.

Strategic Preparation Roadmap

Cracking the RRB NTPC exam requires a disciplined 4-6 month study plan. Because the competition is so high, relying solely on coaching is not enough; self-study and mock tests are the real game-changers.

Phase 1: Concept Clearing (Months 1-2): Start with Mathematics and Reasoning. These are scoring sections where accuracy matters most. Use 10th-standard NCERT books for General Science. For General Awareness, start reading a daily newspaper and maintain a dedicated notebook for monthly current affairs.

Phase 2: Practice and Speed (Months 3-4): Solve at least 2000 MCQs from previous years' papers. The RRB often repeats question patterns. For the Reasoning section, focus on tricky puzzles and Venn diagrams. For Mathematics, learn "Shortcuts" for calculation-heavy topics like Mensuration and Interest.

Phase 3: Mock Tests (Month 5 onwards): Take one full-length mock test every alternate day. Analyze your results to find your "weak spots." If you are losing marks in negative marking, practice the "Art of Skipping" questions you are unsure about. Improving your speed is vital, as you have only 90 minutes to solve 100 questions.

Best Books for Preparation

Investing in the right study material can save you from wasting months on irrelevant content. Here are the top-rated books for RRB NTPC 2025.

Subject Recommended Books
General Awareness Lucent's General Knowledge and Manorama Yearbook.
Mathematics Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Exams by R.S. Aggarwal.
Reasoning Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
Current Affairs Monthly magazines like Eduteria or Pratiyogita Darpan.
Previous Papers Youth Competition Times (YCT) Solved Papers for RRB NTPC.

Application Fee and Refund Policy

RRB follows a unique fee refund policy to encourage genuine candidates to attend the examination.

  • UR / OBC / EWS Candidates: ₹500. Out of this, ₹400 will be refunded after the candidate appears in the CBT-1.
  • SC / ST / Female / PwBD / Ex-Servicemen: ₹250. The full ₹250 will be refunded after the candidate appears in the CBT-1.

Note: Ensure that the bank account details you provide during registration are correct, as the refund will be automatically credited to that account after the first stage of the exam.

How to Apply Online

The application process is centralized. You can apply through any one of the regional RRB websites or the main "RRB Apply" portal.

  1. Visit the official portal at www.rrbapply.gov.in.
  2. Click on the "New Registration" button and select the RRB zone you want to apply to.
  3. Enter your Aadhaar number, Mobile number, and Email ID. Verify using OTP.
  4. Fill in the Personal details, 10th, and 12th Standard marks accurately.
  5. Upload a high-quality scanned Photograph (against a white background) and Signature.
  6. Pay the application fee using UPI, Debit Card, or Net Banking.
  7. Choose your post preferences carefully (e.g., Commercial Clerk over Junior Clerk).
  8. Save the application PDF and your registration number for future use.

Documents Needed to Apply

Scanning documents correctly is critical. Blurred images can lead to application rejection. Keep these ready:

  1. Aadhaar Card: For identity verification (Mandatory).
  2. 10th Standard Certificate: For Date of Birth and Father's name verification.
  3. 12th Standard Marksheet: As proof of minimum educational qualification.
  4. Caste Certificate (if applicable): In the prescribed Central Government format.
  5. Passport Photo: Recent color photo (JPEG format, 20kb-50kb).
  6. Signature: On white paper with black/blue ink (JPEG format, 10kb-40kb).

Important Links

Resource Name Direct Link
Official Recruitment Portal Apply Online Here
Full Notification (CEN 07/2025) Download PDF
Indian Railways Main Site indianrailways.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can 12th-appearing students apply?

No. You must have already passed your 12th Standard and possess the original marksheet/certificate on or before the closing date of the application (November 27, 2025).

Q2: Can I change my RRB zone after submission?

No. Once the application is submitted and the fee is paid, the RRB zone cannot be changed. Choose your zone based on vacancy counts and local competition levels before applying.

Q3: Is the Typing Test qualifying or merit-deciding?

The Typing Skill Test is purely qualifying in nature. Your marks in this test are not added to the final merit, but you must pass it to be eligible for theTypist posts.

Q4: What is the Normalization process?

Since the exam happens over multiple shifts, some shifts might have harder questions. Normalization is a mathematical formula used to equalize the scores across different difficulty levels to ensure fairness.

Q5: Is there any physical test like running or jumping?

No. Unlike the RRB Group D or RPF exams, NTPC posts do not have a Physical Efficiency Test (PET). There is only a Medical Fitness Test.

Q6: What is the maximum age for General category candidates?

The maximum age is 30 years as of January 1, 2026. However, if you are from a reserved category, you can avail of the age relaxation benefits mentioned above.

entry.title: NABARD Development Assistant Recruitment 2026 for 162 Posts
entry.content:
NABARD Development Assistant Recruitment 2026 for 162 Posts

Advertisement No. 08/DA/2025-26: The National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD) has released a major recruitment notification for 162 posts of Development Assistant and Development Assistant (Hindi). Applications are invited from eligible Indian citizens to join the apex development financial institution. The online application window will be open from January 17, 2026, to February 03, 2026. This is a golden opportunity for graduates to build a career in an organization pivotal to India's rural and agricultural sectors.

Post Name: Development Assistant / Development Assistant (Hindi)
Last Date: 03 February 2026
Total Posts: 162

The National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD), an institution wholly owned by the Government of India, has announced its recruitment drive for the clerical cadre. This recruitment aims to fill 159 vacancies for the post of Development Assistant and 3 vacancies for Development Assistant (Hindi) across its regional offices in various states. This role is crucial for the implementation of NABARD's developmental initiatives at the grassroots level.

The selection process is a three-tiered system involving a Preliminary Examination, a Main Examination, and a Language Proficiency Test (LPT). The exams are designed to test the candidate's aptitude, general awareness, and language skills. Selected candidates will be offered a competitive starting salary with gross monthly emoluments of approximately ₹46,500. With a clear career progression path and the prestige of working with a premier national institution, this recruitment is a highly sought-after opportunity for aspiring banking professionals.

NABARD Development Assistant Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take careful note of the key dates and details to ensure a smooth application and examination process. All updates will be published on the official NABARD website.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development (NABARD)
Advertisement Number 08/DA/2025-26
Post Names Development Assistant & Development Assistant (Hindi)
Total Vacancies 162 (159 + 3)
Application Start Date 17 January 2026
Application Last Date 03 February 2026
Phase-I (Prelims) Exam Date 21 February 2026
Phase-II (Mains) Exam Date 12 April 2026
Application Mode Online
Job Type Permanent (Government Bank)
Job Location Various States across India
Official Website www.nabard.org

Vacancy Details

A total of 162 posts are available, distributed between the two roles and across various states. Candidates can apply for a vacancy in one state only.

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Development Assistant 159
Development Assistant (Hindi) 03
Total 162

State-wise vacancies for Development Assistant include Andaman and Nicobar (1), Andhra Pradesh (7), Assam (5), Bihar (1), Chhattisgarh (5), Goa (1), Gujarat (9), Haryana (3), Himachal Pradesh (4), Jammu & Kashmir (3), Jharkhand (1), Karnataka (7), Kerala (3), Ladakh (2), Madhya Pradesh (10), Maharashtra (48), Manipur (2), Meghalaya (1), New Delhi (3), Odisha (8), Punjab (2), Rajasthan (5), Tamil Nadu (9), Uttar Pradesh (11), and West Bengal (8).

State-wise vacancies for Development Assistant (Hindi) are in Andhra Pradesh (1), Maharashtra (HO-Mumbai) (1), and Tamil Nadu (1).

Note: Candidates can apply for only one post (either Development Assistant or Development Assistant (Hindi)) and for vacancies in one state only.

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

  • For SC/ ST/ PWBD/ EXS candidates: ₹100/- (Intimation Charges only)
  • For all other candidates (General/ OBC/ EWS): ₹450/- (Application Fee) + ₹100/- (Intimation Charges) = ₹550/-

Note: The above fees are exclusive of applicable GST (18%). The final amount will be calculated and shown on the payment gateway.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 01.01.2026)

Candidates must fulfill the age and educational qualification criteria as of the cut-off date to be eligible for the recruitment.

Post Name Age Limit & Educational Qualification
Development Assistant Age Limit: Between 21 and 35 years.
Qualification: Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate (Pass class for SC/ST/PWBD/EXS candidates).
Skill: Knowledge of word processing on a PC.
Development Assistant (Hindi) Age Limit: Between 21 and 35 years.
Qualification: Bachelor's Degree from a recognized university in English/Hindi medium with Hindi and English as a compulsory or elective subject, securing a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate (Pass class for SC/ST/PWBD/EXS candidates).
OR
Bachelor's Degree with Hindi and English as main subjects with 50% marks in aggregate (Pass class for reserved categories).
Skill: Must be able to translate from English to Hindi and vice-versa, and have knowledge of word processing on a PC.

Age Relaxation: Upper age limit is relaxable by 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, 10 years for PWBD (General), and as per government rules for Ex-Servicemen.

Selection Process

The selection process for both posts will be conducted in three phases:

  1. Phase-I: Preliminary Examination (Online): This is a qualifying exam consisting of objective-type questions. Marks from this phase will not be considered for the final merit list.
  2. Phase-II: Main Examination (Online): Candidates shortlisted from the prelims (in a 1:25 ratio) will appear for the main exam. This exam consists of both objective and descriptive tests. The marks obtained in this phase will be used for final selection.
  3. Language Proficiency Test (LPT): Candidates who qualify in the main exam will have to undergo an LPT in the official language of the state they have applied for. This test is qualifying in nature. Candidates who have studied the official language in their 10th or 12th standard are exempted from the LPT.

Exam Pattern

The examination structure is different for Preliminary and Main exams, as well as for both posts. There will be a penalty of 1/4th mark for every wrong answer in the objective tests.

Development Assistant Exam Pattern

Phase-I: Preliminary Examination

Test NameNo. of QsMax. MarksDuration
Test of English Language404020 mins
Test of Numerical Ability303020 mins
Test of Reasoning303020 mins
Total10010060 mins

Phase-II: Main Examination

Test NameNo. of QsMax. MarksDuration
Test of Reasoning303030 mins
Quantitative Aptitude303030 mins
General Awareness (with special reference to agriculture, rural development, and banking)505025 mins
Computer Knowledge404020 mins
Test of English Language (Descriptive: Essay, Precis, Report/Letter Writing)035030 mins
Total-200135 mins

Development Assistant (Hindi) Exam Pattern

Phase-I: Preliminary Examination

Test NameNo. of QsMax. MarksDuration
Test of English Language404020 mins
Test of Professional Knowledge (Hindi/English)303020 mins
Test of Reasoning303020 mins
Total10010060 mins

Phase-II: Main Examination

Test NameNo. of QsMax. MarksDuration
Test of Reasoning202025 mins
Test of Professional Knowledge (Hindi/English)505035 mins
General Awareness (with special reference to agriculture, rural development, and banking)404025 mins
Computer Knowledge404020 mins
Test of English Language (Descriptive: Essay, Precis, Report/Letter Writing)035030 mins
Total-200135 mins

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for this recruitment.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A well-structured preparation plan is essential to succeed in this competitive examination.

  • Understand the Syllabus: Go through the detailed syllabus for both Prelims and Mains. Pay special attention to the General Awareness section, which focuses on agriculture, rural development, and banking.
  • Prelims Strategy: The preliminary exam is a test of speed and accuracy. Practice a high number of mock tests to improve your time management skills in Reasoning, English, and Numerical Ability.
  • Mains Strategy: For the main exam, focus on conceptual clarity. Study static banking awareness, agricultural schemes (like PM-KISAN, Fasal Bima Yojana), and recent developments in rural India. For the descriptive English paper, practice writing essays and letters on current topics.
  • Language Skills: For the Development Assistant (Hindi) post, strengthen your translation skills and grammar in both Hindi and English.
  • Stay Updated: Regularly read newspapers (like The Hindu or The Indian Express) and follow magazines like Kurukshetra and Yojana to cover the General Awareness section effectively.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for their preparation journey:

  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Reasoning Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • English Language: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi; for descriptive, "High School English Grammar and Composition" by Wren & Martin.
  • General Awareness (Agri & Rural Dev): "Agriculture at a Glance" by R K Sharma; NABARD's Annual Reports, and publications from Yojana and Kurukshetra magazines.
  • Computer Knowledge: "Objective Computer Knowledge & Literacy" by Kiran Prakashan.

How to Apply Online

The application process is conducted online through the NABARD website.

  1. Visit the official NABARD website at www.nabard.org and navigate to the "Career Notices" section.
  2. Find the link for "Recruitment to the post of Development Assistant / Development Assistant (Hindi)-2026" and click on it.
  3. Click on "Apply Online". You will be redirected to the IBPS application portal.
  4. Click on “Click here for New Registration” and provide your basic details like name, contact number, and email ID. A provisional registration number and password will be generated.
  5. Log in and fill out the detailed application form with your personal, educational, and communication details.
  6. Upload your scanned photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and a handwritten declaration as per the specified dimensions and size.
  7. Choose your preferred state for posting and examination center.
  8. Proceed to pay the application fee online.
  9. After successful payment, submit the application and take a printout of the final form and e-receipt for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Scanning)

Keep the following documents scanned and ready before starting the online application:

  1. Photograph: 4.5cm × 3.5cm (20KB – 50KB).
  2. Signature: With black ink on white paper (10KB – 20KB).
  3. Left Thumb Impression: On white paper with black or blue ink (20KB – 50KB).
  4. Handwritten Declaration: The text, as specified in the notification, written in English on white paper with black ink (50KB – 100KB).

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Can I apply for vacancies in two different states?

No, a candidate can apply for vacancies in one state only. You must also be proficient in the official language of that state.

Q2: Are the marks of the Preliminary Exam counted for final selection?

No, the Preliminary Exam is only qualifying in nature. The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Main Examination (Objective + Descriptive) only.

Q3: What is the initial monthly salary for a Development Assistant at NABARD?

The initial monthly gross emoluments for a Development Assistant are approximately ₹46,500.

Q4: I have a Bachelor's Degree with 49.5% marks. Am I eligible?

No, you must have a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate. As per the notification, a fraction of a percentage will be ignored (e.g., 49.99% will be treated as less than 50%). However, SC/ST/PWBD/EXS candidates only need to have a pass class.

entry.title: C-DAC भर्ती 2025: पूरे भारत में 230+ इंजीनियर एवं मैनेजर पदों पर बंपर वैकेंसी!
entry.content:
C-DAC भर्ती 2025: पूरे भारत में 230+ इंजीनियर एवं मैनेजर पदों पर बंपर वैकेंसी!

C-DAC (Centre for Development of Advanced Computing) ने इंजीनियर, टेक्निकल ऑफिसर, और प्रोजेक्ट मैनेजर के 230 से अधिक पदों के लिए भर्ती अधिसूचना जारी की है।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! C-DAC (Centre for Development of Advanced Computing), जो भारत सरकार के इलेक्ट्रॉनिक्स और सूचना प्रौद्योगिकी मंत्रालय के अंतर्गत एक प्रमुख अनुसंधान एवं विकास संस्था है, ने पूरे भारत में विभिन्न परियोजनाओं के लिए इंजीनियर, प्रोजेक्ट मैनेजर, और टेक्निकल असिस्टेंट पदों पर भर्ती की घोषणा की है।

ये भर्ती अभियान C-DAC के विभिन्न केंद्रों जैसे पुणे, नोएडा, बेंगलुरु, हैदराबाद, चेन्नई, मोहाली, और तिरुवनंतपुरम में आयोजित किया जाएगा। आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से ऑनलाइन है और सभी योग्य भारतीय नागरिक आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ10 जनवरी 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि31 जनवरी 2025 (रात 11:59 बजे तक)
लिखित परीक्षा (अनुमानित)फरवरी 2025 का द्वितीय सप्ताह

पदों का विवरण

पद का नामकुल पदवेतनमान
Project Engineer120₹7.5 से ₹9 लाख वार्षिक
Project Manager25₹12 से ₹14 लाख वार्षिक
Senior Project Engineer40₹9 से ₹10.5 लाख वार्षिक
Technical Officer / Assistant45₹5.5 से ₹7 लाख वार्षिक
कुल पद230+-
नोट: पदों की संख्या परियोजनाओं की आवश्यकता के अनुसार बढ़ या घट सकती है।

आवेदन शुल्क

  • सामान्य / ओबीसी उम्मीदवार: ₹500/-
  • SC / ST / PwBD / महिला उम्मीदवार: कोई शुल्क नहीं

भुगतान केवल ऑनलाइन माध्यम (डेबिट कार्ड/क्रेडिट कार्ड/नेट बैंकिंग) से किया जा सकता है।

पात्रता मानदंड

मानदंडआवश्यक योग्यता
शैक्षणिक योग्यता
  • मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से B.E / B.Tech / M.E / M.Tech / MCA / Ph.D. (Computer Science, Electronics, IT, Electrical, Mechanical, या संबंधित क्षेत्र में)।
  • कुछ प्रबंधकीय पदों के लिए MBA / PG Diploma (Project Management) वांछनीय है।
अनुभव इंजीनियर पदों के लिए 0–3 वर्ष और प्रोजेक्ट मैनेजर पदों के लिए 8–12 वर्ष का अनुभव आवश्यक है।
आयु सीमा अधिकतम 35 वर्ष (सरकारी नियमों के अनुसार आरक्षित वर्ग को छूट)।

चयन प्रक्रिया

उम्मीदवारों का चयन निम्न चरणों में किया जाएगा:

  1. लिखित परीक्षा (Written Test): Computer-based objective test, जिसमें तकनीकी और सामान्य विषय शामिल होंगे।
  2. साक्षात्कार (Interview): शॉर्टलिस्ट किए गए उम्मीदवारों को इंटरव्यू के लिए बुलाया जाएगा।
अंतिम चयन: लिखित परीक्षा और साक्षात्कार के संयुक्त अंकों के आधार पर किया जाएगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://careers.cdac.in पर जाएँ।
  2. "C-DAC Recruitment 2025" के अंतर्गत दिए गए पदों में से उपयुक्त पद चुनें।
  3. “Apply Now” पर क्लिक कर ऑनलाइन आवेदन फॉर्म भरें।
  4. अपनी फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र और अनुभव प्रमाणपत्र अपलोड करें।
  5. ऑनलाइन शुल्क जमा करें और फॉर्म सबमिट करें।
  6. भविष्य के लिए आवेदन का प्रिंट निकालकर सुरक्षित रखें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकलिंक
ऑनलाइन आवेदनयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या यह नौकरी स्थायी है?
नहीं, सभी पद प्रारंभिक रूप से अनुबंध (Contract) आधारित हैं, जिन्हें प्रदर्शन के आधार पर बढ़ाया जा सकता है।
प्रश्न 2: क्या आवेदन शुल्क refundable है?
नहीं, आवेदन शुल्क वापस नहीं किया जाएगा।
प्रश्न 3: क्या नए ग्रेजुएट आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
हाँ, Project Engineer पदों के लिए फ्रेशर्स पात्र हैं।
प्रश्न 4: परीक्षा कहाँ आयोजित होगी?
लिखित परीक्षा देश के प्रमुख शहरों में आयोजित की जाएगी — जैसे पुणे, हैदराबाद, बेंगलुरु, दिल्ली आदि।

entry.title: Bihar Police Chowkidar Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
Bihar Police Chowkidar Recruitment 2026

The Bihar Home Department has drafted the new "Bihar Chowkidar Cadre Rules, 2026" to regulate the recruitment, service conditions, and promotion of Chowkidars (Watchmen) in the state. As per the new rules, a large-scale, district-wise recruitment drive is expected to be announced soon. The selection process will now include a 75-mark written test and a 50-mark Physical Efficiency Test (PET), with the final merit list prepared out of 125 marks. This is a major opportunity for 10th-pass candidates who are permanent residents of Bihar.

Post Name: Chowkidar (Watchman)
Last Date: To Be Announced
Total Posts: To Be Announced

The Bihar Police Headquarters has initiated the process to review and finalize the new service rules for the recruitment of Chowkidars, titled the "Bihar Chowkidar Cadre Rules, 2026." This move signals that the state government is preparing for a significant recruitment drive to fill numerous Chowkidar vacancies across all districts of Bihar. The new rules, framed under Article 309 of the Constitution, lay down a revised and comprehensive procedure for selection, which is a major update from previous recruitment patterns.

The most significant change is the introduction of a new selection process totaling 125 marks. Candidates will first have to clear a 75-mark written examination based on a 10th-grade syllabus. Those who qualify will then be called for a 50-mark Physical Efficiency Test (PET), where their performance in running, high jump, and shot put will be awarded marks. The final merit list will be prepared based on the combined score of both the written test and the PET, making both academic knowledge and physical fitness equally important. This is a golden opportunity for eligible candidates to secure a government job in their home district.

Bihar Police Chowkidar Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Based on the new draft rules, here are the expected details for the upcoming recruitment. Official dates will be announced by the selection board soon.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Bihar Police Department
Selection Body Central Selection Board (Constable Recruitment) (CSBC) or equivalent
Governing Rules Bihar Chowkidar Cadre Rules, 2026
Post Name Chowkidar (Watchman)
Vacancies To be announced (District-wise)
Application Mode Online (Expected)
Job Type Government Job (District Cadre)
Job Location Home District of the Candidate in Bihar
Official Website csbc.bih.nic.in

Vacancy Details

The post of Chowkidar is a district-level cadre. The recruitment will be conducted to fill vacancies in each district of Bihar. The cadre has a clear promotion channel as follows:

  • Chowkidar (Base Cadre)
  • Dafadar (First Promotion)
  • Senior Dafadar (Second Promotion)
Note: The official notification will provide a detailed district-wise and category-wise breakdown of the total vacancies.

Application Fee

  • The details regarding the application fee will be specified in the official recruitment advertisement when it is published by the Central Selection Board (CSBC).

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet all the eligibility requirements regarding education, skills, domicile, and age to apply for the post of Chowkidar.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification Must have passed 10th Class (Matriculation) or an equivalent examination from a recognized educational institution by the last date of application.
Essential Skill Candidates must have knowledge of cycling. Those who do not know cycling at the time of appointment will be given three months to learn, failing which their service may not be confirmed.
Domicile Candidate must be a permanent resident of Bihar State. They can only apply for a vacancy in their permanent home district.
Character Must have a good moral character with no adverse criminal history. A background check will be conducted by the appointing authority.
Age Limit (as of the date specified in the advertisement)
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years (for all categories)
  • Maximum Age (General - Male/Female): 30 Years
  • Maximum Age (BC/EBC - Male): 32 Years
  • Maximum Age (BC/EBC - Female): 33 Years
  • Maximum Age (SC/ST - Male/Female): 35 Years
  • Home Guard Relaxation: 5 years relaxation in the upper age limit.

Selection Process

The selection of Chowkidars will be based on a two-stage process totaling 125 marks. The final merit list will be prepared based on the combined marks of both stages.

  1. Stage 1: Written Examination (75 Marks): An objective-type OMR-based written test will be conducted. Candidates numbering five times the total vacancies will be shortlisted for the next stage based on their performance in this test.
  2. Stage 2: Physical Efficiency Test (PET) (50 Marks): Shortlisted candidates will undergo a Physical Standards Test (PST) and a Physical Efficiency Test (PET). The PET events will be for a total of 50 marks.
  3. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared district-wise based on the total marks obtained in the Written Test (75) and the PET (50).

Exam Pattern

The examination will test both the mental and physical capabilities of the candidates.

1. Written Test (75 Marks)

Subject No. of Questions Max. Marks Duration
General Knowledge, General Mathematics, and Mental Ability (Syllabus based on 10th Class) 75 75 1 hour 30 minutes

2. Physical Test (50 Marks)

Candidates must first qualify the minimum physical standards (height). After that, they will participate in the PET events for marks.

A. Physical Standards Test (PST - Qualifying)

CategoryMinimum Height
Unreserved (UR) & Backward Class (BC) Male165 cm
Extremely Backward Class (EBC) Male162 cm
SC & ST Male160 cm
All Category Female155 cm

B. Physical Efficiency Test (PET - 50 Marks)

(i) Running (Max: 30 Marks)

GenderEventTime TakenMarks
Male1.6 KM RunUp to 5 min30
5 min 1 sec to 5 min 30 sec24
5 min 31 sec to 6 min18
6 min 1 sec to 6 min 30 sec12
Female1 KM RunUp to 4 min30
4 min 1 sec to 4 min 30 sec24
4 min 31 sec to 5 min18
5 min 1 sec to 5 min 30 sec12

(ii) High Jump (Max: 10 Marks)

GenderHeightMarks
Male5 feet or more10
4 feet 6 inches08
4 feet06
Female4 feet or more10
3 feet 6 inches08
3 feet06

(iii) Shot Put / Gola Fek (Max: 10 Marks)

GenderEventDistanceMarks
Male16 Pound ShotMore than 20 feet10
19+ to 20 feet08
18+ to 19 feet06
17+ to 18 feet04
Female12 Pound ShotMore than 16 feet10
15+ to 16 feet08
14+ to 15 feet06
13+ to 14 feet04

How to Prepare

Candidates should adopt a dual preparation strategy focusing on both the written exam and physical fitness.

  • For Written Exam: Study the Bihar Board textbooks for Class 10th in Mathematics and Social Science. Practice general knowledge and current affairs specific to Bihar. Solve previous year papers of Bihar Police Constable exams to understand the pattern.
  • For Physical Test: Since the PET carries 50 marks, physical fitness is crucial. Start a regular fitness regimen. Practice running daily to improve your timing. Work on your technique for high jump and shot put to maximize your score.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard textbooks and guides for their preparation.

  • Bihar Board (BSEB) Class 10th textbooks for Mathematics and General Studies.
  • Lucent's Samanya Gyan for General Knowledge.
  • A good book on quantitative aptitude and reasoning like R.S. Aggarwal.
  • A local Bihar-specific GK book for state-related questions.

How to Apply

The official notification with the application link will be published on the CSBC website. The expected application process is as follows:

  1. Visit the official CSBC website: csbc.bih.nic.in.
  2. Find the link for "Recruitment of Chowkidar in Bihar Police".
  3. Register on the portal using your mobile number and email ID.
  4. Log in and fill out the online application form with personal and educational details.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other required documents.
  6. Pay the application fee online through the payment gateway.
  7. Submit the final application form and take a printout for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should keep the following documents ready before applying:

  1. 10th Class (Matriculation) Mark Sheet and Certificate.
  2. Bihar Domicile Certificate (Permanent Residence Certificate).
  3. Caste Certificate (for reserved category candidates).
  4. Non-Creamy Layer (NCL) Certificate for BC/EBC candidates.
  5. Recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  6. Valid Mobile Number and Email ID.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here (Link to be updated)
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the minimum qualification for Bihar Police Chowkidar?

The minimum educational qualification is a pass in the 10th Class (Matriculation) or an equivalent examination.

Q2: Are the marks from the Physical Test added to the final merit?

Yes, as per the new rules, the Physical Efficiency Test (PET) carries 50 marks, which will be added to the 75 marks of the written test to prepare the final merit list out of 125 marks.

Q3: Can I apply for a Chowkidar post in a district other than my home district?

No, the rules clearly state that candidates can only apply for a vacancy in their permanent home district in Bihar.

Q4: What is the age limit for EBC male candidates?

The maximum age limit for Extremely Backward Class (EBC) and Backward Class (BC) male candidates is 32 years.

entry.title: CBSE Recruitment 2026: Permanent Posts for 94 Non-Teaching Vacancies
entry.content:
CBSE Recruitment 2026: Permanent Posts for 94 Non-Teaching Vacancies

A major recruitment drive for Assistant Secretary, Professor, Assistant, and other Group A, B & C roles. Online applications are open from December 2 to December 22, 2025.

Post Name: Assistant Secretary, Assistant Professor, Superintendent, Junior Assistant & Others
Last Date: 22/12/2025
Total Posts: 94

The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), an autonomous organization under the Ministry of Education, Government of India, has released a major recruitment notification (No: CBSE/Rectt.Cell/14(88)/2025) for direct recruitment to various non-teaching posts on the basis of an All India Competitive Examination. This recruitment drive, named the Direct Recruitment Quota Examination 2026 (DRQ2026), is being conducted to fill a total of 94 permanent vacancies.

This is a spectacular opportunity for qualified and talented candidates who wish to build a stable and respected career in one of the country's largest and most prestigious education boards. This recruitment includes various Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' posts, such as Assistant Secretary, Assistant Professor, Accounts Officer, Superintendent, Junior Accountant, and Junior Assistant. CBSE, known for its quality education and transparent examination systems, will conduct a multi-tiered competitive examination for this recruitment, which will include both objective and descriptive tests. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online from December 2, 2025, to December 22, 2025. Selected candidates may be posted anywhere in India at the Board's Headquarters, Regional Offices, Centres of Excellence, or Sub-Regional Offices.

CBSE Recruitment 2026 Important Points

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other information to ensure a smooth application process.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)
Application Start Date December 2, 2025
Last Date to Apply Online December 22, 2025 (up to 11:59 PM)
Job Type Permanent Central Government Job
Job Location All India

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

CBSE has announced a total of 94 vacancies for various Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' non-teaching posts. These vacancies are distributed across different categories. The post-wise breakdown is as follows:

Post Code Post Name Group Total Posts
01/25 Assistant Secretary A 08
02/25 Assistant Professor & Assistant Director (Academics) A 12
03/25 Assistant Professor & Assistant Director (Training) A 08
04/25 Assistant Professor & Assistant Director (Skill Education) A 07
05/25 Accounts Officer A 02
06/25 Superintendent B 27
07/25 Junior Translation Officer B 09
08/25 Junior Accountant C 16
09/25 Junior Assistant C 35
Grand Total 94
Note: For a detailed category-wise (UR, SC, ST, OBC, EWS, PwBD, ESM) breakdown of vacancies, candidates are requested to refer to the official notification.

Examination Fee Details

Candidates are required to pay the application fee and processing fee online as per their category.

CategoryGroup-A Posts (Level 10)Group-B & C Posts
Unreserved/OBC/EWS ₹1500 (Application) + ₹250 (Processing) = ₹1750 ₹800 (Application) + ₹250 (Processing) = ₹1050
SC/ST/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen/Women/CBSE Regular Employees Only ₹250 (Processing Fee) Only ₹250 (Processing Fee)

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Specific educational qualifications and upper age limits have been prescribed for each post. The age will be calculated as on the closing date of applications, i.e., 22.12.2025.

Post Name Educational Qualification Maximum Age
Assistant Secretary Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University. 35 years
Assistant Professor & Director (Academics/Training/Skill) Post Graduate Degree in any discipline with a minimum of 55% marks. B.Ed./M.Ed, NET/SLET/JRF, Doctorate Degree are desirable. 30 years
Accounts Officer Bachelor’s degree with Economics/Commerce/Accounts/Finance/Business Studies OR Passed SAS/JAO(C) Examination OR Post Graduate in relevant subjects OR MBA (Finance)/CA/ICWA. 35 years
Superintendent Bachelor's Degree and working knowledge of computers. A typing speed of 35 WPM (English) or 30 WPM (Hindi) is mandatory. 30 years
Junior Translation Officer Master's Degree in Hindi/English and a Diploma/Certificate course in translation or 3 years of experience. 30 years
Junior Accountant 12th Pass with subjects like Accountancy/Business Studies/Economics and a typing speed of 35 WPM (English) or 30 WPM (Hindi). 27 years
Junior Assistant 12th Pass and a typing speed of 35 WPM (English) or 30 WPM (Hindi). 27 years

Selection Process

The selection process for all posts will involve a multi-tiered examination system, consisting of Tier-1 and Tier-2 examinations. An interview or skill test will also be conducted for certain posts.

  1. Tier-1 Examination:
    • This will be an objective-type (MCQ) OMR-based examination.
    • The syllabus and level of the exam will vary for different posts.
    • A common Tier-1 exam may be conducted for posts of a similar level (bunching).
    • Marking Scheme: 3 marks will be awarded for each correct answer, and 1 mark will be deducted for each incorrect answer.
  2. Tier-2 Examination:
    • Candidates will be shortlisted for Tier-2 based on their performance in Tier-1 (usually in a 1:10 ratio).
    • This examination can be of both objective (OMR) and descriptive (Written) types.
    • The syllabus will be specific to the post applied for.
  3. Interview / Skill Test:
    • Based on performance in Tier-2, candidates will be shortlisted for an interview or skill test (like a typing test) (usually in a 1:5 ratio).
    • The skill test (typing test) will be qualifying in nature only.
    • For Group 'A' posts (Assistant Secretary, Accounts Officer), the interview will carry 50 marks.
  4. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Tier-2 examination and the interview (where applicable).

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

A clear understanding of the exam pattern and syllabus is crucial for a focused preparation strategy.

Tier-1 Exam Pattern (General Outline)

Part Subject No. of Questions Marks
I General Knowledge & Current Affairs 30 90
II Reasoning & Mathematical Ability 30 90
III General Hindi & English 30 90
IV Computer Literacy 10 30
V Domain Knowledge (Subject/Post Specific) 50 150
Total 150 450
Note: The above pattern is indicative and may vary. Candidates must refer to the detailed syllabus provided in the official notification for each post.

Tier-2 Exam Pattern

The Tier-2 exam will be more specific to the job role. For instance:

  • For Assistant Secretary: The exam will likely include papers on Administration & Finance, and Education & Academics, which could be a mix of objective and descriptive questions.
  • For Superintendent/Junior Assistant: The focus will be on office procedures, noting, drafting, and knowledge of government rules (CCS Rules), along with a mandatory qualifying typing test.
  • For Accounts Cadre: The paper will cover advanced topics in Accountancy, Financial Management, Auditing, and Taxation.

Syllabus for Tier-1 (Common Subjects)

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs: Indian History, Geography, Indian Constitution, Science & Technology, National & International Events, Sports, Awards.
  • Reasoning & Mathematical Ability: Verbal and Non-verbal Reasoning, Puzzles, Data Interpretation, Number Systems, Percentage, Profit & Loss, Time & Work.
  • General Hindi & English: Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension, Antonyms, Synonyms, Error Detection.
  • Computer Literacy: Fundamentals of Computers, MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), Internet, Networking basics.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for any of the non-teaching posts advertised in this recruitment. The selection is based entirely on performance in the competitive examinations and interview/skill tests.

How to Prepare for the CBSE Recruitment Exam

A structured and disciplined approach is essential to crack this competitive exam.

  1. Analyze the Syllabus: The first step is to thoroughly understand the detailed syllabus for both Tier-1 and Tier-2 for your specific post.
  2. Build a Strong Foundation:
    • For Tier-1, focus on strengthening your basics in Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, English, and Hindi.
    • For General Awareness, make a habit of reading a national newspaper daily and follow a monthly current affairs magazine.
  3. Master Your Domain Knowledge: The domain knowledge section in Tier-1 and the entire Tier-2 paper are crucial. Study your graduation/post-graduation subjects thoroughly. For administrative posts, focus on government rules, office procedures, and administrative aptitude.
  4. Practice for Skill Tests: If you are applying for posts like Superintendent or Junior Assistant, start practicing typing daily to achieve the required speed of 35 WPM (English) or 30 WPM (Hindi) with high accuracy.
  5. Solve Mock Tests: Regularly solve full-length mock tests to get accustomed to the exam pattern, improve your time management, and identify your weak areas. Pay attention to the negative marking scheme.
  6. Interview Preparation (for Group 'A' Posts): Prepare for the interview by researching CBSE's functions, recent initiatives in the education sector (like NEP 2020), and be ready to discuss your academic background and professional experience.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books that can aid your preparation:

  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and Manorama Yearbook.
  • Reasoning Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • English Language: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • For Administrative & Finance Rules (Superintendent/AO): "Swamy's Handbook" for Central Government Staff.
  • Domain-Specific Subjects: Refer to standard textbooks from your graduation/post-graduation.

How to Apply for CBSE Recruitment

The application process is completely online. Candidates must apply through the official CBSE recruitment website.

  1. First, visit the CBSE recruitment website (link provided below).
  2. Click on "New Registration" and complete the registration process by filling in your basic details.
  3. Log in using your registration credentials.
  4. Select the post you wish to apply for under the advertisement "DRQ2026".
  5. Fill in the application form with all the required details, such as personal information, educational qualifications, and experience, accurately.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your latest photograph, signature, and other necessary documents.
  7. Pay the application and processing fee according to your category via the online payment gateway.
  8. Preview the application form and check all the details before final submission.
  9. Take a printout of the finally submitted application form and the e-receipt for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

To complete the online application process smoothly, candidates are advised to keep scanned copies of the following documents ready:

  • Latest Passport-size Photograph: A recent and clear color photograph.
  • Signature: Scanned copy of your signature on plain white paper with a black or blue pen.
  • Proof of Date of Birth: 10th Class Certificate or Birth Certificate.
  • Identity Proof: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, or Passport.
  • Educational Qualification Certificates: All mark sheets and certificates for 10th, 12th, Graduation, Post-Graduation, and any other relevant degree/diploma.
  • Experience Certificate: For posts where experience is required, a certificate from the employer clearly stating your role, responsibilities, and period of service.
  • Reservation Category Certificate: A valid certificate issued by the competent authority in the prescribed format of the Government of India for SC/ST/OBC (NCL)/EWS/PwBD candidates.
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC): Candidates currently employed in Central/State Govt./PSUs/Autonomous Bodies will have to produce an NOC from their employer at the time of document verification.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here (Available on Website)
Apply Online Click Here
CBSE Official Website https://www.cbse.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the CBSE non-teaching recruitment?

The last date to apply online and pay the fee is December 22, 2025 (up to 11:59 PM).

Question 2: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for more than one post if you are eligible, but you will have to fill out a separate application form and pay a separate fee for each post.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection process includes a Tier-1 (MCQ) and a Tier-2 (MCQ + Descriptive) examination. An interview or skill test will also be conducted for certain posts.

Question 4: Is there negative marking in the Tier-1 exam?

Yes, there will be a deduction of 1 mark for every incorrect answer in the Tier-1 examination.

Question 5: Can final-year students apply?

No, as per the notification, a candidate must possess the essential qualification as of the closing date of the application. Candidates appearing in the final year or awaiting results need not apply.

entry.title: IPPB भर्ती 2025: इंडिया पोस्ट पेमेंट्स बैंक में जल्द होगी भर्ती!
entry.content:
IPPB भर्ती 2025: इंडिया पोस्ट पेमेंट्स बैंक में जल्द होगी भर्ती!

आवेदन प्रक्रिया की जानकारी जारी, पूरा नोटिफिकेशन जल्द ही आएगा।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! बैंकिंग सेक्टर में करियर बनाने के इच्छुक उम्मीदवारों के लिए यह एक अहम सूचना है। इंडिया पोस्ट पेमेंट्स बैंक (IPPB), जो भारत सरकार का एक उपक्रम है, ने आगामी भर्ती के लिए ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया की दिशानिर्देश जारी कर दिए हैं। हालाँकि अभी तक पदों के नाम, संख्या और योग्यता का पूरा विवरण जारी नहीं हुआ है, लेकिन आवेदन की तिथियाँ और आवश्यक निर्देश घोषित कर दिए गए हैं।

इससे यह संकेत मिलता है कि IPPB जल्द ही एक बड़ा भर्ती नोटिफिकेशन जारी कर सकता है। ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया 09 अक्टूबर 2025 से शुरू होगी और 29 अक्टूबर 2025 तक चलेगी। उम्मीदवारों को सलाह दी जाती है कि वे आवेदन हेतु आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ पहले से तैयार रखें। इस लेख में हम आपको जारी की गई दिशानिर्देशों के आधार पर आवेदन प्रक्रिया की पूरी जानकारी दे रहे हैं।

IPPB रिक्तियाँ एवं वेतनमान 2025

इंडिया पोस्ट पेमेंट्स बैंक द्वारा पदों के नाम, संख्या और वेतनमान का विवरण जल्द ही एक विस्तृत नोटिफिकेशन में जारी किया जाएगा। जैसे ही आधिकारिक अधिसूचना आती है, नीचे दी गई तालिका अपडेट कर दी जाएगी।

पद का नामरिक्तियाँवेतनमान
अभी घोषित नहींअभी घोषित नहींअभी घोषित नहीं

IPPB पात्रता मानदंड 2025

इस भर्ती के लिए शैक्षणिक योग्यता और आयु सीमा की जानकारी आधिकारिक नोटिफिकेशन में दी जाएगी। उम्मीदवारों से अनुरोध है कि आवेदन करने से पहले पूरे नोटिफिकेशन को ध्यानपूर्वक पढ़ें।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

कार्यक्रमतिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ09 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि29 अक्टूबर 2025
आवेदन शुल्क: आवेदन शुल्क का भुगतान ऑनलाइन माध्यम से किया जाएगा। शुल्क की राशि का विवरण पूर्ण नोटिफिकेशन में दिया जाएगा। यह शुल्क नॉन-रिफंडेबल होगा।

IPPB भर्ती 2025 के लिए आवेदन प्रक्रिया

इस भर्ती के लिए आवेदन केवल IPPB की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट के माध्यम से ऑनलाइन ही स्वीकार किए जाएँगे। आवेदन करने के लिए नीचे दिए गए चरणों का पालन करें:

  1. चरण 1: IPPB की वेबसाइट https://ippbonline.com/web/ippb/currentopenings पर जाएँ और "APPLY ONLINE" पर क्लिक करें।
  2. चरण 2: "Click here for New Registration" पर क्लिक करें और अपना नाम, संपर्क विवरण और ईमेल आईडी डालकर पंजीकरण करें।
  3. चरण 3: आपको एक Provisional Registration Number और Password प्राप्त होगा, इसे नोट करें।
  4. चरण 4: पंजीकरण विवरण से लॉगिन करें और आवेदन फ़ॉर्म में मांगी गई जानकारी भरें।
  5. चरण 5: अपना फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, बाएँ अंगूठे का निशान और hand-written declaration अपलोड करें।
  6. चरण 6: आवेदन फ़ॉर्म को “Preview” करें और सभी विवरण जाँचें।
  7. चरण 7: “Complete Registration” के बाद “Payment” टैब पर जाकर शुल्क का ऑनलाइन भुगतान करें।
  8. चरण 8: सबमिट करने के बाद आवेदन का प्रिंटआउट निकालकर सुरक्षित रखें।

आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

ऑनलाइन आवेदन करने से पहले उम्मीदवारों को निम्नलिखित दस्तावेज़ स्कैन कर तैयार रखने चाहिए:

दस्तावेज़आकार (Dimension)फ़ाइल आकारफ़ाइल प्रकार
Photograph200 x 230 pixels20kb – 50kbJPG/JPEG
Signature (Black Ink)140 x 60 pixels10kb – 20kbJPG/JPEG
Left Thumb Impression240 x 240 pixels20kb – 50kbJPG/JPEG
Hand-written Declaration800 x 400 pixels50kb – 100kbJPG/JPEG
Educational Certificates / NOC-20kb – 500kbPDF
Hand-written Declaration Text:
उम्मीदवार को नीचे दिया गया वाक्य अंग्रेज़ी में, काले पेन से सफेद कागज़ पर लिखकर स्कैन करना होगा:
"I, _______ (Name of the candidate), hereby declare that all the information submitted by me in the application form is correct, true and valid. I will present the supporting documents as and when required."

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकक्रिया
ऑनलाइन आवेदन (09.10.2025 से सक्रिय)यहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: IPPB भर्ती के लिए आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

ऑनलाइन आवेदन करने की अंतिम तिथि 29 अक्टूबर 2025 है।

प्रश्न 2: आवेदन में कौन-कौन से दस्तावेज़ आवश्यक हैं?

फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, बाएँ अंगूठे का निशान और hand-written declaration अपलोड करना आवश्यक है। इनके आकार और फॉर्मेट ऊपर बताए गए हैं।

प्रश्न 3: पूरा नोटिफिकेशन कब जारी होगा?

IPPB जल्द ही विस्तृत नोटिफिकेशन जारी करेगा जिसमें पदों के नाम, संख्या, योग्यता और चयन प्रक्रिया की जानकारी दी जाएगी। आधिकारिक वेबसाइट नियमित रूप से चेक करें।

entry.title: Naval Dockyard Visakhapatnam Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27
entry.content:
Naval Dockyard Visakhapatnam Apprentice Recruitment 2026-27

Advertisement No: DAS(V)/01/25: A golden opportunity for ITI-qualified candidates to join the Ministry of Defence (Navy). The Naval Dockyard Apprentices School, Visakhapatnam, is enrolling 320 Trade Apprentices for the 2026-27 batch. The last date to submit offline applications is January 2, 2026. Get all details on the application process, selection procedure, eligibility, and exam pattern here.

Post Name: Trade Apprentice in various trades
Last Date: 02 January 2026
Total Posts: 320

The Naval Dockyard Apprentices School in Visakhapatnam, under the Ministry of Defence (Navy), has announced a major recruitment drive for its 2026-27 apprenticeship training batch. This is a prestigious opportunity for Indian nationals who have completed their ITI to receive one year of comprehensive training in various designated trades. The recruitment, under advertisement number DAS(V)/01/25, aims to fill 320 vacancies across 15 different technical trades, adhering to the Apprentices Act, 1961.

This program is designed to equip young technicians with practical skills and hands-on experience in a real-world naval environment, significantly enhancing their employability in the industrial and defence sectors. The selection process is competitive, involving shortlisting based on academic merit followed by a written examination. Successful candidates will undergo a year-long training program and receive a monthly stipend. The application process is a two-step procedure involving online registration on the apprenticeship portal followed by the submission of an offline application by post. The deadline for receiving applications at the Naval Dockyard is January 2, 2026.

Naval Dockyard Apprentice Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully note the important dates and details to ensure they complete all application steps on time. This is a central government apprenticeship opportunity located in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Naval Dockyard Apprentices School, Visakhapatnam (Ministry of Defence - Navy)
Advertisement Number DAS(V)/01/25
Training Batch 2026-27
Post Name Trade Apprentice
Total Vacancies 320
Training Duration One Year
Application Mode Online Registration + Offline Application (By Post)
Last Date for Receipt of Application 02 January 2026
Written Exam Date (Tentative) 22 March 2026
Job Type Apprenticeship Training
Job Location Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh

Vacancy Details

A total of 320 vacancies are available across 15 designated trades. The detailed trade-wise distribution is as follows:

Trade Name Number of Vacancies
Fitter 60
Electrician 35
Mechanic Diesel 32
Pipe Fitter 30
Sheet Metal Worker 30
Shipwright (Wood) 30
Welder (Gas & Electric) 20
Electronics Mechanic 17
Painter (General) 15
Computer Operator and Programming Assistant (COPA) 15
Machinist 12
Mechanic Mechatronics 10
Mechanic (Central AC Plant, Industrial Cooling etc.) 6
Instrument Mechanic 5
Foundryman 3
Total 320
Note: The number of vacancies is indicative and may change. Horizontal reservation for PWD and Armed Forces categories will be adjusted against the respective UR/OBC/SC/ST vacancies.

Application Fee

  • The official notification does not specify any application fee.
  • Candidates are not required to pay any fee for this application process.

However, candidates must affix a ₹55/- postal stamp on a self-addressed empty envelope to be sent along with the application for receiving the hall ticket by Speed Post.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility criteria related to education, age, and physical standards to be considered for the apprenticeship training.

Criteria Requirement
Educational Qualification
  • SSC / Matric / Std X: Must have passed with a minimum of 50% aggregate marks.
  • ITI (NCVT/SCVT): Must have passed in the relevant trade with a minimum of 65% aggregate marks.
  • Certificates without marks or grades will not be accepted.
Age Limit
  • Minimum age is 14 years. For hazardous trades, the minimum age is 18 years.
  • Accordingly, candidates born on or before 02 May 2012 are eligible for non-hazardous trades, and those born on or before 02 May 2008 are eligible for hazardous trades.
  • There is no upper age limit for apprenticeship training as per MSDE memorandum.
Physical Standards Candidates must meet the minimum physical fitness standards as specified in Rule 4 of 'The Apprenticeship Rules 1992'. This will be verified through a medical examination.
Previous Training Candidates who have already completed or are currently pursuing apprenticeship in the same trade in any organization are not eligible to apply.

Selection Process

The selection of apprentices is a multi-stage process designed to identify the most suitable candidates based on merit and aptitude. The stages are as follows:

  1. Shortlisting for Written Exam: Candidates will be shortlisted for the written examination based on their academic marks. The marks from SSC/Matriculation and ITI will be given weightage in a 70:30 ratio respectively to prepare a merit list.
  2. Written Examination: Shortlisted candidates (in a ratio of 1:5 against vacancies) will be issued hall tickets for a common OMR-based written examination.
  3. Final Merit List: A final merit list will be prepared trade-wise and category-wise based on the marks obtained in the written examination. In case of a tie, the older candidate will be given preference, followed by the alphabetical order of names.
  4. Document Verification: Candidates who make it to the final merit list will be called for document verification. They must produce all original certificates for verification.
  5. Medical Examination: Every finally selected candidate will have to undergo a medical examination to ensure they meet the required physical standards before being engaged as an apprentice.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The written examination is the main component of the selection process. It is an OMR-based test designed to evaluate the candidate's general aptitude and knowledge.

Written Exam Pattern

  • Mode of Exam: Offline (OMR Answer Sheet)
  • Type of Questions: Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs)
  • Total Questions: 100
  • Total Marks: 100 (1 mark per question)
  • Language: The question paper will be in English.
  • Duration: 2 Hours (120 Minutes)
  • Negative Marking: There will be no negative marking for wrong answers.
  • Exam Center: Visakhapatnam

Syllabus and Subject-wise Marks Distribution

Subject Number of Questions
Mathematics 35
General Science 35
General Knowledge 30
Total 100

The level of the questions will be based on the 10th standard and ITI curriculum. Candidates should prepare basic concepts of Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, and stay updated with current affairs and general knowledge.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) for this apprenticeship recruitment. However, candidates must clear a mandatory medical examination to prove their physical fitness as per the standards laid down in the Apprenticeship Rules, 1992.

How to Prepare for the Exam

Success in this recruitment depends heavily on a good score in the written exam. A structured preparation strategy is essential.

  • Strengthen the Basics: The syllabus focuses on fundamental concepts. Thoroughly revise Mathematics and General Science from your 8th, 9th, and 10th-grade NCERT or state board textbooks.
  • Focus on Core Science: For General Science, pay attention to basic principles of Physics (like motion, work, energy, electricity), Chemistry (chemical reactions, acids-bases, metals-non-metals), and Biology (life processes, human body).
  • Stay Updated with GK: For the General Knowledge section, read newspapers, follow news portals, and use a standard GK book (like Lucent's GK). Focus on static GK related to history, geography, polity, defence, and important national facts, as well as current affairs from the last 6 months.
  • Practice Mock Papers: Solve mock tests and previous years' papers from similar exams. Since there is no negative marking, your strategy should be to attempt all 100 questions within the 2-hour time limit.
  • Time Management: Practice solving questions quickly to manage your time effectively during the exam. Allocate approximately one minute per question to ensure you can review your answers.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can use these standard books for a comprehensive preparation for the written examination:

  • Mathematics: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Science: "Lucent's General Science" and NCERT textbooks for Classes 8, 9, and 10.
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" for static GK and any reliable monthly current affairs magazine.
  • Practice Sets: Use practice books for ITI-level entrance exams or apprentice exams from publishers like Arihant or Upkar.

How to Apply

The application is a mandatory two-part process involving online registration and offline submission. Both steps must be completed.

Step 1: Online Registration on NAPS Portal

  1. First, all candidates must register themselves on the National Apprenticeship Promotion Scheme (NAPS) portal at www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. This is a mandatory step.
  2. Before registering, ensure your name and date of birth match on both your Aadhar card and SSC/Matriculation certificate. If not, get your Aadhar details updated.
  3. Complete your profile on the portal to 100% by filling in all educational details, trade preference, and uploading supporting documents.
  4. After completing your profile, search for the establishment "NAVAL DOCKYARD" (Establishment ID: E08152800002) and apply for your desired trade on the portal itself.
  5. Take a printout of your complete "Apprentice Profile" from the portal. This printout is required for the offline application.

Step 2: Offline Application Submission by Post

  1. Download the Hall Ticket format (Annexure-I) and Check-off list (Annexure-II) from the official notification.
  2. Fill two copies of the original Hall Ticket with a ballpoint pen and paste a recent passport-size photograph on each.
  3. Gather all required documents as per the Check-off list (SSC & ITI marks sheets, caste certificate, Aadhar card, etc.).
  4. Prepare a self-addressed empty envelope and affix postal stamps worth ₹55/- on it.
  5. Place the Apprentice Profile printout, two filled Hall Tickets, self-attested copies of all documents, and the self-addressed envelope inside a larger envelope.
  6. Superscribe the main envelope with the Trade Name you are applying for (e.g., "Fitter").
  7. Send the complete application package by Post to the following address, ensuring it reaches before 02 January 2026:

    The Officer-in-Charge (for Apprenticeship),
    Naval Dockyard Apprentices School, VM Naval Base S.O.,
    P.O., Visakhapatnam - 530 014, Andhra Pradesh

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates need to send self-attested photocopies of the following documents along with their offline application:

  1. Printout of the complete "Apprentice Profile" from the NAPS portal.
  2. Two copies of the duly filled original Hall Tickets with photographs.
  3. Check-off List (Annexure-II of the notification).
  4. SSC / Matriculation / Std X Marks Certificate.
  5. ITI Marks Certificate (all semesters/years).
  6. Latest Community/Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC), if applicable.
  7. Latest valid PWD Certificate, if applicable.
  8. 'Service and Release Certificate' for Ex-Servicemen or their children.
  9. Serving Certificate for children of serving Armed Force personnel.
  10. Self-attested copy of Aadhar Card.
  11. A self-addressed empty envelope with a ₹55/- postal stamp.

Important Links

Action Link
NAPS Portal Registration Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Indian Navy Official Website Click Here

FAQs

Q1: Is this a permanent job in the Indian Navy?

No, this is a one-year apprenticeship training program. There is no obligation on the part of the Naval Dockyard to offer employment after the training.

Q2: What is the last date to send the application?

The complete offline application package must reach the Naval Dockyard Apprentices School, Visakhapatnam, by 02 January 2026.

Q3: I have 64% in my ITI. Am I eligible?

No, the minimum required percentage in ITI is 65%. Applications with less than 65% in ITI will be rejected.

Q4: Is there any negative marking in the written exam?

No, the official notification clearly states that there will be no negative marking for wrong answers.

Q5: Can I submit the application in person?

No, the application must be sent only by Post. Applications sent by any other means will not be accepted.

entry.title: IIT Guwahati Recruitment 2025: 19 Vacancies for Assistant Registrar and Junior Assistant!
entry.content:
IIT Guwahati Recruitment 2025: 19 Vacancies for Assistant Registrar and Junior Assistant!

A golden opportunity for a government job in a prestigious institute. Applications are invited for graduate and master's degree holders.

Post Name: Assistant Registrar, Junior Assistant
Last Date: 13 January 2026
Total Posts: 19

The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Guwahati, one of the country's premier institutions of national importance, has released a significant employment notice (Advertisement No: IITG/R/15/2025) for recruitment to administrative posts. This is a fantastic opportunity for qualified and talented Indian citizens who wish to build a stable and respected career in one of India's top technical institutes.

Through this recruitment drive, a total of 19 posts of Assistant Registrar under Group 'A' and Junior Assistant under Group 'C' will be filled. Selected candidates will receive an attractive salary as per the 7th Pay Commission, along with all the benefits applicable to Central Government employees. The application process is entirely online and will commence on December 13, 2025, and will run until January 13, 2026. This detailed article will provide you with complete information about the post-wise vacancies, eligibility, selection process, and the application procedure.

Important Points

Details Information
Online Application Start Date 13 December 2025
Last Date for Online Application 13 January 2026
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Central Government)
Job Location Guwahati, Assam

Vacancy Details

IIT Guwahati has announced a total of 19 vacancies for two different posts:

Post Name Number of Posts Category-wise Details
Assistant Registrar 02 UR-1, EWS-1
Junior Assistant 17 UR-4, SC-1, ST-2, OBC-NCL-6, EWS-4
Grand Total 19
Note: The number of posts for Junior Assistant may change based on future promotions and administrative requirements.

Application Fee

  • Assistant Registrar (General/OBC-NCL): ₹1000/-
  • Junior Assistant (General/OBC-NCL): ₹500/-
  • For SC/ST, Women, and PwBD candidates: No Fee.

The fee is to be paid through online mode only and is non-refundable.

Eligibility Criteria

Specific educational qualifications and age limits are prescribed for each post (age will be calculated as of 13.01.2026):

Post Name Eligibility (Qualification, Experience, and Age)
Assistant Registrar Qualification: Master's Degree with at least 55% marks.
Desirable Experience: 5 years of relevant experience in academic administration (in a Group 'B' post).
Pay Level: Level-10.
Age Limit: Up to 35 years.
Junior Assistant Qualification: Bachelor's Degree with knowledge of computer office applications.
Pay Level: Level-3.
Age Limit: Up to 27 years.
Note: There is no upper age limit for regular employees of IIT Guwahati. Age relaxation for reserved categories will be applicable as per Government of India rules.

Selection Process

The selection process will vary depending on the nature of the post:

  1. Shortlisting: If the number of applications received is large, the institute may shortlist candidates by applying criteria stricter than the minimum qualifications. Candidates with higher qualifications and relevant experience will be given preference.
  2. Written Test / Skill Test / Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called for a written test, skill test (like a computer test), and/or a personal interview as per the requirement of the post.

The final authority to decide the mode of selection is reserved with the institute. The exam pattern and syllabus will be announced later on the institute's website.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus (Expected)

While the detailed scheme of examination will be notified on the IIT Guwahati website, candidates can prepare based on the general pattern of non-teaching recruitment in IITs and other central universities.

Written Test Pattern for Junior Assistant

The test is likely to be an objective-type MCQ test covering the following areas:

Subject Expected Marks
General English & Comprehension 25-30
General Knowledge & Current Affairs 25-30
Logical Reasoning & Quantitative Aptitude 25-30
Knowledge of Computer Applications (MS Office) 15-20

Written Test Pattern for Assistant Registrar

The test for Assistant Registrar will be more comprehensive and may include two papers, covering both general and administrative knowledge:

  • Paper 1 (General): Objective questions on General Knowledge, Reasoning, English, and Aptitude.
  • Paper 2 (Domain Specific): Descriptive or objective questions on Educational Administration, Government of India Rules (like GFR, FR-SR, CCS Rules), Finance & Accounts, and Establishment matters.
Skill Test: For Junior Assistant, a qualifying skill test (typing test) is highly probable. Candidates should be prepared for a typing speed of 30-35 wpm.

How to Prepare for the IIT Guwahati Exam

A good preparation is necessary for success in the IIT Guwahati recruitment exam:

  1. Focus on General Aptitude: For posts like Junior Assistant, prepare subjects like General Knowledge, Current Affairs, Reasoning, Numerical Ability, and English language.
  2. Computer Knowledge: Knowledge of computer applications (MS Office, etc.) is mandatory for both posts. Practice its practical aspects.
  3. Administrative Knowledge (for Assistant Registrar): If you are applying for Assistant Registrar, study topics related to academic administration, government rules (like GFR), establishment, finance, and accounts.
  4. Refer to Previous Papers: Analyze old question papers from similar non-teaching recruitments in other IITs or central universities. This will give you an idea of the exam pattern and difficulty level.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right study material is crucial for good preparation. Here are some recommended books for general subjects:

SubjectRecommended Books
General Knowledge and Current Affairs
  • "Lucent's Samanya Gyan"
  • Monthly current affairs magazines.
Reasoning and Numerical Aptitude
  • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" - R.S. Aggarwal
  • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" - R.S. Aggarwal
Computer Knowledge
  • "Objective Computer Knowledge & Literacy" - Kiran Prakashan
Educational Administration (Assistant Registrar)
  • Books on Central Government's GFR, FR-SR rules, and CCS rules.

How to Apply

The application process is online only. There is no need to send a hard copy of any document.

  1. Go to the official recruitment website of IIT Guwahati: https://online.iitg.ac.in/recruitment.
  2. Under the relevant advertisement (Advt. No. IITG/R/15/2025), click on the "Apply Online" link.
  3. Register with your basic details and apply for the post for which you are eligible. A separate application is required for each post.
  4. Fill in the application form with all the required information accurately.
  5. Upload scanned copies of the necessary documents (educational certificates, experience certificates, caste certificate, date of birth proof, etc.).
  6. Pay the application fee online as per your category.
  7. Review all the filled information before finally submitting the application form.
  8. Take a printout of the filled application form for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep scanned copies of the following documents ready while applying online:

  1. Your recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. All educational certificates and marksheets (from 10th to the highest qualification).
  3. Experience certificates (if applicable).
  4. Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD). The OBC/EWS certificate should not be more than one year old from the last date of application.
  5. No Objection Certificate (NOC) or an undertaking (if you are already in government service).

Important Links

Link Type Anchor Text
Apply Online Click Here (Link will be active on 13.12.2025)
Official Notification Download Here
Official Website www.iitg.ac.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Do I need to send a hard copy of the application form?

No, as per the notification, the application process is completely online. There is no need to send a hard copy of any document.

Question 2: Can final-year students apply?

No, candidates must possess all the required educational qualifications by the last date of application (January 13, 2026).

Question 3: How old should the OBC/EWS certificate be?

The OBC(NCL) and EWS certificates should not be more than one year old from the last date of application.

entry.title: Calcutta High Court Port Blair Recruitment 2025: Stenographer/P.A. Post Open, Apply Online from Dec 1
entry.content:
Calcutta High Court Port Blair Recruitment 2025: Stenographer/P.A. Post Open, Apply Online from Dec 1

The Circuit Bench of the Calcutta High Court in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands has released a recruitment notification for one post of Stenographer/Personal Assistant (reserved for SC).

An opportunity has arisen for candidates aspiring to a career in the judiciary. The Circuit Bench of the Calcutta High Court at Port Blair (Andaman and Nicobar Islands) has invited online applications from eligible Indian citizens to fill one vacant post of Stenographer/Personal Assistant (P.A.) on its establishment. This appointment will be on a temporary basis initially but is likely to be made permanent in the future.

This recruitment is exclusively reserved for candidates belonging to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category. Interested candidates who are 12th pass and are proficient in shorthand and typing can apply online from December 1, 2025, to December 31, 2025. The selection process will include a shorthand and typing test, along with an interview. This article will provide you with complete information about the recruitment, including eligibility, salary, exam pattern, and preparation tips.

Recruitment Overview

ParticularsInformation
OfficeOffice of the Registrar, Circuit Bench of Calcutta High Court, Port Blair
Post NameStenographer/P.A.
Total Posts01 (Reserved for Scheduled Caste)
ClassificationHigh Court Service Class-III
Job LocationPort Blair, Andaman and Nicobar Islands
Application ModeOnline
Last Date to ApplyDecember 31, 2025 (11:00 PM)
Official Websitewww.calcuttahighcourt.gov.in

Important Dates

Event Date
Application Start DateDecember 1, 2025 (05:00 AM)
Application Last DateDecember 31, 2025 (11:00 PM)
Admit Card Release DateTo be notified online
Exam/Interview DateTo be notified in due course

Pay Scale and Eligibility Criteria

Scale of Pay

The selected candidate will be paid as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC) under the High Court Service Class-III. In addition, other allowances as admissible under the rules will also be payable.

Description Amount / Level
Pay Matrix LevelLevel-09
Scale₹ 28,900 - ₹ 74,500
Minimum Pay₹ 32,500/- per month
Other BenefitsDA, HRA, etc. (as per rules)

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill the educational and technical qualifications by the last date of application. The age will be calculated as of the date of application.

Criteria Requirement
Educational Qualification Passed Senior Secondary/Higher Secondary (12th Class) or its equivalent examination from a recognized Board or Council.
Technical Qualification (Mandatory)
  • Shorthand Speed: 120 words per minute
  • Typewriting Speed: 30 words per minute
Desirable Qualification Working knowledge in Computers.
Age Limit 18 years to 30 years (as on the date of application).
Age Relaxation Relaxation of 5 years in the upper age limit for Scheduled Caste candidates of Andaman and Nicobar Islands and West Bengal.
Important Note: For proof of age, only the date of birth recorded in the Madhyamik or equivalent certificate will be accepted.

Mode of Selection

The selection process will be conducted in two stages:

  1. Competitive Test: A shorthand and typewriting test on a computer (in English).
  2. Interview: Candidates who are successful in the test will be called for an interview.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in both stages.

Exam Pattern

The examination for the Stenographer post will be conducted on the following pattern:

Stage of Examination Details Time Marks
Shorthand and Typewriting Test Dictation @ 120 wpm 05 minutes 600
Correction by Candidate 10 minutes
Transcription on Computer @ 30 wpm 20 minutes
Interview Personality Test - 10
Total Marks 610

How to Apply

  1. Visit the Website: To apply, go to https://erecruitment.andaman.gov.in or https://www.andamannicobar.gov.in.
  2. Registration: If you are a new user, register first.
  3. Fill the Form: Log in and fill in all the required details (personal, educational, technical qualifications) correctly in the application form.
  4. Upload Documents: Upload scanned copies of your photo, signature, and necessary certificates (such as caste certificate, educational certificates).
  5. Submit: Check the form and submit it. Take a printout of the application form for future reference.

Required Documents

The following documents will be required during the online application and at the time of the interview/test:

  • Photo ID Proof: Passport, PAN Card, Voter ID, Aadhaar Card, or Driving License (original and photocopy).
  • Educational Certificates: Madhyamik/Secondary certificate (for age proof) and Higher Secondary marksheet/certificate.
  • Caste Certificate: Scheduled Caste (SC) certificate issued by a competent authority.
  • Technical Qualification Certificate: Certificate of shorthand and typing (if available).
  • NOC: No Objection Certificate from the employer if you are already in service.
  • Photograph: Recent passport-size photograph.

Preparation Tips & Books

For this post, shorthand and typing are the most important skills.

How to Prepare:

  • Practice: Practice shorthand dictation and typing for at least 1-2 hours daily.
  • Speed and Accuracy: Regular practice is necessary to achieve a speed of 120 wpm. Pay special attention to accuracy.
  • Computer Knowledge: Practice basic computer functions and word processing.
  • English Language: Strengthen your English vocabulary and grammar as the dictation will be in English.

Recommended Books:

  • Shorthand: Pitman Shorthand Instructor and Key.
  • Typing: Any standard typing tutor software or book for high-speed typing.
  • Computer: Lucent's Computer Knowledge or Arihant Computer Awareness.

Important Links

Details Link
Official Notification (PDF) Click Here
Apply Online (from December 1) Click Here
Calcutta High Court Website Click Here

FAQs – Frequently Asked Questions

Question 1: What is the last date to apply?
The last date to submit the online application is December 31, 2025.
Question 2: Can SC candidates from all states apply?
No, only Scheduled Caste (SC) candidates from the Andaman and Nicobar Islands and the state of West Bengal are eligible.
Question 3: Will any TA/DA be provided for the exam?
No, candidates will not be given any Travelling Allowance (TA) or Dearness Allowance (DA) for participating in the test or interview.
Question 4: Where will the examination be held?
The test and interview will be conducted in Port Blair.

entry.title: Cabinet Secretariat Recruitment 2025: Direct Entry for 250 Deputy Field Officer (Technical) Vacancies
entry.content:
Cabinet Secretariat Recruitment 2025: Direct Entry for 250 Deputy Field Officer (Technical) Vacancies

Join the Elite Government Wing - Direct Recruitment through GATE Score (2023, 2024, 2025) for Group B Non-Gazetted Positions

Post Name: Deputy Field Officer (Technical) [DFO-Tech]
Last Date: December 14, 2025
Total Posts: 250 (Tentative)

The Cabinet Secretariat of India, an administrative body that reports directly to the Prime Minister, has officially launched its highly anticipated recruitment drive for the year 2025. Functioning as the vital link between the Prime Minister’s Office and various ministries, the Cabinet Secretariat is responsible for the administration of the Government of India (Allocation of Business) Rules, 1961, and the Government of India (Transaction of Business) Rules, 1961. This year, the Secretariat has released a notification for the post of Deputy Field Officer (Technical) [DFO-Tech], which is a Group ‘B’ Non-Gazetted position. This recruitment is a specialized drive aiming to induct high-caliber technical talent into the organization through the GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering) scores of 2023, 2024, and 2025.

Working for the Cabinet Secretariat is widely considered one of the most prestigious career paths in the Indian government. The organization operates with a high level of confidentiality and technical sophistication, often involving field operations that are critical to national interest. For engineering graduates and science post-graduates, this is a rare opportunity to bypass traditional written competitive exams and enter the service based on their academic and technical excellence in GATE. The 2025 drive is extensive, with approximately 250 vacancies spread across various technical disciplines, including Computer Science, Electronics, and emerging fields like Data Science and Artificial Intelligence. The application process is strictly offline, requiring candidates to send their physical forms through Ordinary Post by December 14, 2025.

The role of a Deputy Field Officer (Technical) involves the application of advanced technological solutions to administrative and field challenges. Whether it is ensuring secure communication networks, analyzing technical data, or supporting the infrastructure of various specialized departments, a DFO (Tech) is at the heart of the Secretariat’s technical wing.
