This 2000-word comprehensive guide serves as your definitive manual, providing an in-depth analysis of the salary matrix, discipline-wise eligibility, the intricacies of the "Ordinary Post" requirement, and strategic interview tips to help you secure your future in this elite government department.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must be exceptionally careful with the timeline and the mode of application. Unlike most government exams today, the Cabinet Secretariat adheres to a traditional offline verification system. Submitting your application through any mode other than the prescribed Ordinary Post will result in immediate disqualification. The cut-off for all eligibility criteria, including age and experience, is December 14, 2025.

Recruitment Feature Dates and Key Information
Official Notification Number DFO-Technical Drive 2025
Application Start Date Active Now
Last Date for Application Receipt December 14, 2025
Selection Mode GATE Score Shortlisting + Personal Interview
Job Type Group B (Non-Gazetted) / Permanent
Application Mode Offline (Ordinary Post Only)
Job Location All India (Liability to Serve Anywhere)

Discipline Wise Vacancy Breakdown

The 250 vacancies are strategically distributed among various engineering and science branches. This ensures that the Secretariat has a diverse technical workforce to handle its multifaceted operations. The Computer Science and Electronics branches have the highest share of vacancies, reflecting the organization’s focus on cybersecurity and communication.

Technical Stream / Discipline Total Vacancies (Approx) Mandatory GATE Paper Code
Computer Science / IT 124 CS
Electronics & Communication 95 EC
Data Science & Artificial Intelligence 10 DA
Physics 06 PH
Chemistry 04 CY
Other Streams (Civil/Mech/Maths) 11 CE, ME, MA, etc.
Grand Total 250 Vacancies -
Special Note on Vacancies: The total count of 250 is tentative and may be adjusted based on the functional requirements of the Secretariat at the time of final selection. Reservations for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and EWS are applicable as per the Central Government norms.

Salary Matrix and Special Allowances

The pay structure for the Deputy Field Officer (Technical) post is highly attractive. Positioned in the Level-7 Pay Matrix of the 7th CPC, it is one of the highest-paying entry-level technical roles in the civil services, comparable to that of an Inspector in Central Excise or CBI.

  • Pay Scale: ₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400 (Pay Level-7)
  • Basic Pay: ₹44,900 per month.
  • Special Security Allowance (SSA): Selected candidates are entitled to a 20% Special Security Allowance over and above their basic pay. This allowance is provided due to the sensitive nature of the organization.
  • Gross Salary: Including current DA (50%+), HRA (27% for X cities), and TA, the starting gross salary ranges between ₹80,000 to ₹88,000 per month.

Career Progression: A DFO (Tech) can advance to the ranks of Field Officer, Senior Field Officer, and Assistant Director. The career growth is structured, offering periodic promotions based on performance and departmental exams, eventually leading to high-level managerial positions within the Secretariat.

Eligibility Criteria and Qualifications

Eligibility for the Cabinet Secretariat is non-negotiable. Candidates must fulfill the academic requirements and possess a valid GATE scorecard for the specific years mentioned. Self-attested copies of all qualifying certificates must be attached to the offline application.

  • Engineering Graduates: B.E. / B.Tech or equivalent in the relevant discipline (Computer, Electronics, etc.) from a recognized university.
  • Science Post-Graduates: M.Sc. in Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, or Statistics from a recognized university.
  • GATE Requirement: A valid GATE Scorecard for the years 2023, 2024, or 2025. The score must be in the paper code corresponding to the candidate's qualification branch.
  • Age Limit: Maximum 30 years as of the closing date. Relaxations are provided: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and up to 40 years for departmental candidates.
  • Medical Standard: Candidates must be in sound mental and physical health. A clear medical certificate will be required during the final document verification stage.

Selection Process and Roadmap

The selection process for the Cabinet Secretariat is unique. It avoids the lengthy multi-tier examination system used by the SSC or UPSC, relying instead on established national standards like GATE to ensure technical quality.

  1. Shortlisting: Candidates are shortlisted based on their GATE marks and the vacancies available in each technical stream. The shortlisting ratio is typically 1:5 or 1:10.
  2. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates are invited for an interview in New Delhi. This interview is not just technical; it focuses on personality, emotional intelligence, and integrity.
  3. Character Verification: As a high-security organization, the Secretariat conducts a detailed background check of the candidate’s history, social circle, and educational records.
  4. Final Merit: The final list is prepared based on the cumulative evaluation of the GATE Score and Interview performance.

Detailed Interview Syllabus

Since there is no written test, the interview is the ultimate filter. Candidates should prepare for a three-part interaction with the board.

Part 1: Technical Depth (50% Focus): Questions will revolve around the fundamental concepts of your branch. For CS candidates, focus on Cryptography, Data Structures, and Network Security. For Electronics, focus on Signal Processing and Radar Systems.

Part 2: Logical & Situational Aptitude: The board may present hypothetical scenarios to test your decision-making abilities. You will be evaluated on your ability to stay calm and think logically under pressure.

Part 3: Awareness of National Interest: Basic knowledge of India’s internal security framework, international relations (specifically the neighborhood), and current technological trends like 5G, Quantum Computing, and AI is essential.

Expert Preparation Strategy for DFO-Technical

Success in this recruitment depends on how well you leverage your technical background during the interview. Follow this expert roadmap to maximize your chances of selection.

Polish your GATE Core Subjects: Do not assume that since you passed GATE, you don't need to study. The interview panel consists of senior scientists and intelligence officers who will test the practical application of your theoretical knowledge. Re-read your core textbooks and focus on real-world troubleshooting.

Security Awareness: The Cabinet Secretariat is involved in highly sensitive work. Read about Cybersecurity frameworks in India, the role of the National Security Advisor (NSA), and the basic hierarchy of the Cabinet Secretariat. Demonstrating an awareness of the organization’s importance will set you apart from other candidates.

Interview Etiquette: Since this is a Group B Officer-level role, your body language, attire, and communication must reflect professionalism. Practice mock interviews to refine your tone. Be honest; if you do not know the answer to a technical question, it is better to admit it than to provide wrong information, as integrity is highly valued here.

Best Books and Resources for Preparation

While your engineering textbooks are the primary source, these resources can help you prepare for the non-technical aspects of the Cabinet Secretariat interview.

Subject Area Recommended Resource
Technical Core Standard GATE Series books (Made Easy / ACE Academy Handbooks).
Cyber Security "Cryptography and Network Security" by William Stallings.
General Awareness Lucent’s General Knowledge and Manorama Yearbook.
National Security "India's National Security: A Reader" by Bajpai and Pant.
Interview Prep "The Ultimate Guide to Job Interviews" by Dr. Sandeep Goyal.

How to Apply Offline: Step-by-Step Guide

The most critical part of this recruitment is the Offline Application Process. Thousands of applications are rejected every year simply because candidates fail to follow the postage rules. Follow these steps meticulously.

  1. Download the official Prescribed Application Form from the link provided in the important links section.
  2. Fill the form in BLOCK LETTERS using a blue or black ballpoint pen. Ensure your signature is placed in all the designated boxes.
  3. Document Preparation: Attach self-attested copies of your 10th marksheet (age proof), B.E/B.Tech/M.Sc degree, GATE Scorecard, and Category Certificate.
  4. Photograph Requirement: Paste one recent passport-size photograph on the form and attach two extra photographs inside the envelope. Write your name on the back of the extra photos.
  5. The Envelope: Use a sturdy A4 size envelope. On the top of the envelope, write in BOLD CAPITAL LETTERS: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF DEPUTY FIELD OFFICER (TECHNICAL) - [YOUR GATE BRANCH NAME]".
  6. Mode of Postage: Go to your nearest post office and send the envelope via ORDINARY POST ONLY. Avoid Speed Post, Registered Post, or private couriers.
  7. Address to Send: Send it to the Post Bag address mentioned below.

Important Links and Postal Address

Resource Description Official Link / Details
Postal Address to Send Application Post Bag No. 001, Lodhi Road Head Post Office, New Delhi-110003
Download Application Form PDF Click Here to Download
Cabinet Secretariat Official Site Visit cabsec.gov.in

Documents Checklist to Avoid Rejection

Ensure you have checked all these items before sealing your envelope. A missing document or signature is the most common reason for disqualification.

  1. GATE Scorecard: Self-attested copy of 2023, 2024, or 2025 score.
  2. Birth Proof: 10th Standard passing certificate or valid birth certificate.
  3. Degree/Diploma: Final passing certificate and year-wise marksheets.
  4. Identity Proof: Clear photocopy of Aadhaar Card.
  5. Caste/Category: Valid SC/ST/OBC/EWS certificate in the format specified by the Govt of India.
  6. Two Extra Photos: Unsigned photographs attached with a paper clip to the application form.
  7. NOC: "No Objection Certificate" if you are currently working in a Government or PSU role.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply if I don't have a GATE score?

No. This specific recruitment drive (DFO-Technical) is conducted exclusively on the basis of GATE scores from 2023, 2024, or 2025. Without a valid GATE scorecard, you are not eligible for shortlisting.

Q2: Why is Ordinary Post mandatory for submission?

The Secretariat utilizes the Post Bag system at the Lodhi Road Post Office for high-security handling and efficient categorization. Ordinary post ensures that the applications are collected and processed as per the internal security protocols of the department.

Q3: Is there a probation period for the DFO (Tech) role?

Yes. Selected candidates will be on a probation period of two years. During this time, they will undergo specialized training at the department’s training academy. Performance during probation determines final confirmation.

Q4: Are final year students eligible to apply?

Yes, final year students can apply provided they have a valid GATE 2025 score and can produce their final degree certificates by the time of the personal interview, which is expected to be held in early 2026.

Q5: What is the medical vision standard for this post?

As a Group B technical field officer, candidates should have normal color vision. Sight corrected with spectacles is usually allowed, but certain field divisions may have stricter vision requirements. A general medical examination will verify this.

Q6: Can I apply for multiple branches if I am eligible?

Generally, you should apply for the branch in which you have obtained your highest GATE score. If you possess degrees in two different fields and have GATE scores for both, you must send separate applications in separate envelopes for each branch.

Disclaimer: This article is for informational purposes only. SakariJobs.blog is an independent news platform and is NOT the official website of the Cabinet Secretariat. Candidates are strictly advised to read the original notification published in the Employment News or on the official cabsec.gov.in website before applying.

entry.title: DTIDC Recruitment 2025: Apply Offline for Lower Division Clerk (LDC) Posts in Delhi
entry.content:
DTIDC Recruitment 2025: Apply Offline for Lower Division Clerk (LDC) Posts in Delhi

A fantastic opportunity for 12th Pass candidates to join a Delhi Government enterprise on a contractual basis. Last date to apply is December 10, 2025.

Post Name: Lower Division Clerk (LDC)
Last Date: 10/12/2025
Total Posts: 03

The Delhi Transport Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (DTIDC), an enterprise of the Government of NCT of Delhi, has released a significant notification (F. No. DTIDC/Admin/KG/2024-25/26) for the recruitment of Lower Division Clerks (LDC) on a contractual basis. This recruitment drive presents a wonderful opportunity for qualified and talented individuals who wish to start their careers in a clerical role within a prestigious organization of the Delhi Government. A total of 03 vacancies will be filled through this recruitment.

DTIDC is a key organization responsible for the development and maintenance of transport infrastructure in Delhi. Candidates selected through this recruitment will be appointed on a contract basis, with an initial period of one year. This term may be extended based on satisfactory performance and the corporation's requirements. This is an excellent opportunity for candidates who have passed the 12th standard and possess good typing speed and computer knowledge. The application process is entirely offline, and the last date for submission is December 10, 2025 (by 5:00 PM).

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameDelhi Transport Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (DTIDC)
Post NameLower Division Clerk (LDC)
Total Posts03
Recruitment TypeContract Basis (Initially for one year)
Job LocationDelhi
Application ModeOffline
Last Date to ApplyDecember 10, 2025 (by 5:00 PM)

Vacancy Details

Under this recruitment drive, a total of 03 posts of Lower Division Clerk (LDC) will be filled on a contract basis.

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) 03

Salary and Remuneration

The Lower Division Clerk appointed on a contract basis will be paid a consolidated remuneration. This salary is equivalent to the Level-2 of the 7th Pay Commission, which includes the admissible Dearness Allowance (DA). No other allowance will be payable.

Post Name Salary
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) ₹ 19,905/- (Basic Pay of Level-2) + admissible DA

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Candidates applying for this post must possess the following essential and desirable qualifications. The crucial date for determining eligibility is November 25, 2025.

Educational Qualification & Skills

Qualification Type Details
Essential Qualification
  1. The candidate must have passed the 12th Class or equivalent from a recognized Board or University.
  2. Skill Test on Computer:
    • A typing speed of 35 words per minute (W.P.M.) in English. OR
    • A typing speed of 30 words per minute (W.P.M.) in Hindi.
    (This corresponds to 10500 KDPH in English or 9000 KDPH in Hindi on an average of 5 key depressions for each word).
Desirable Qualification
  • The candidate should have working knowledge of computer applications such as MS-Office (Word and Excel), PPT, Internet, and E-mail.
  • An undertaking will be taken from the candidate that they have sufficient working knowledge of MS-Office.

Age Limit (as on 25.11.2025)

CriteriaAge
Minimum Age18 years
Maximum Age27 years

Selection Process

The notification has not provided detailed information about the selection process. However, the selection process for LDC posts typically involves the following stages:

  1. Screening of Applications: Received applications will be scrutinized based on the eligibility criteria (age, educational qualification). Applications that are incomplete or do not meet the criteria will be rejected.
  2. Skill Test (Typing Test): Eligible candidates will be called for a skill test on a computer to assess their typing speed as mentioned in the essential qualifications. This test is usually qualifying in nature, meaning candidates must achieve the minimum required speed to be considered for the next stage.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates who are successful in the skill test will be called for the verification of their original documents. This is a crucial step to confirm the authenticity of the claims made in the application form.
  4. Final Merit List: A final merit list will be prepared based on the performance in the selection process. As there is no written test mentioned, the merit list might be prepared based on the marks in the qualifying examination (12th Class) for candidates who clear the skill test, or based on performance in the skill test itself (e.g., higher speed with fewer errors).
Note: No interview will be conducted for the selection process, and no TA/DA will be payable for attending the skill test/document verification.

Job Responsibilities of a Lower Division Clerk (LDC)

The role of an LDC is fundamental to the smooth functioning of any government office. While the specific tasks may vary, the general duties and responsibilities in an organization like DTIDC would include:

  • Record Management: Maintaining and managing official files and records. This includes diary and dispatch of letters, filing documents, and ensuring records are kept in an organized manner.
  • Data Entry and Typing: Typing official letters, notices, circulars, and other documents. Entering data into computer systems and maintaining various registers and logs.
  • Drafting and Correspondence: Assisting senior officials in drafting routine letters, notes, and communications.
  • Clerical Assistance: Providing general clerical support to the department, such as handling incoming and outgoing mail, managing stationery, and assisting in the preparation of reports.
  • Computer Operations: Using computer applications like MS Word for typing, MS Excel for creating spreadsheets and tables, and handling email communications.
  • Public Dealing: Attending to queries from the public or other departments, both in person and over the phone, and providing them with the necessary information.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus (Expected)

Since the selection is primarily skill-based, there may not be a comprehensive written exam. The focus will be on the Skill Test.

Skill Test (Typing Test) Pattern

  • Mode: The test will be conducted on a computer.
  • Language: Candidates will have to choose between English and Hindi.
  • Duration: Typically, a 10-minute test.
  • Content: A printed passage of text will be provided, which the candidate needs to type accurately within the given time.
  • Evaluation: The evaluation is based on typing speed (Words Per Minute) and accuracy. Errors (full mistakes and half mistakes) are calculated, and the final speed is determined.
  • Qualifying Speed: 35 w.p.m. for English or 30 w.p.m. for Hindi.
Although not mentioned, DTIDC reserves the right to conduct a basic written test to screen candidates if the number of applications is large. A potential written test could cover General English, General Awareness, Basic Arithmetic, and Reasoning of the 12th standard level.

How to Prepare for the DTIDC LDC Recruitment

As the selection process is likely to be centered around the typing test, candidates should focus their preparation on the following areas:

  1. Improve Typing Speed and Accuracy:
    • This should be the primary focus of your preparation. Aim to achieve a speed well above the minimum requirement (e.g., 40-45 wpm for English, 35-40 wpm for Hindi) to be comfortable during the test.
    • Use online typing tutor websites and software for regular practice. Practice with different types of passages, including those with numbers and special characters.
    • Focus on accuracy. High speed with many errors is of no use. Aim for an accuracy of 95% or higher.
  2. Master Computer Knowledge (MS-Office):
    • Strengthen your knowledge of MS-Word, MS-Excel, PowerPoint, and internet/email operations.
    • Practice creating documents, formatting text, using formulas in Excel, creating basic presentations, and sending/receiving emails.
  3. Keep Your Documents Ready:
    • Organize all your original documents and keep self-attested photocopies ready. Document verification is a critical stage, and any discrepancy can lead to disqualification.
  4. Stay Updated: Regularly check the official DTIDC website for any updates regarding the selection process, dates for the skill test, and other announcements.

Best Books and Resources for Preparation

Since this is a skill-based role, practical resources are more important than theoretical books.

  • For Typing Practice: Online platforms like Typing.com, 10FastFingers, and dedicated typing master software are excellent resources.
  • For MS Office Skills:
    • "Computer Knowledge" by Arihant Publications for basic concepts.
    • Free online tutorials on YouTube for practical demonstrations of MS Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
  • For a Potential Written Test:
    • "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
    • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
    • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.

How to Apply (Offline Process)

This recruitment is completely offline. Interested and eligible candidates must send their application by following the steps below:

  1. Download the Application Form: First, download the application form format given in Annexure 'A' from the official DTIDC website (or the link provided below).
  2. Fill the Application Form: Fill the application form completely in all respects. All information must be accurate and clearly written.
  3. Affix Photograph: Paste your recent passport-size photograph on the application form.
  4. Attach Documents: Attach self-attested copies of all necessary documents that support your eligibility (e.g., 12th marksheet, date of birth proof, typing certificate, etc.).
  5. Prepare the Envelope: Place the filled application form and all attached documents in an envelope.
  6. Superscribe the Envelope: Clearly write "Application for the post of Lower Division Clerk (LDC)" on top of the envelope.
  7. Send/Submit the Application: Send the filled envelope or submit it in person before the last date to the following address:

    Executive Director,
    DTIDC Limited, Tower Block,
    2nd Floor, Maharana Pratap ISBT, Kashmere Gate,
    Delhi-110006

Please Note: Incomplete applications and applications received after the prescribed date will be summarily rejected.

Documents Required with the Application

It is mandatory to attach self-attested copies of the following documents with the application form:

  1. Completely filled and signed application form (Annexure 'A').
  2. Recent passport-size photograph (pasted on the form).
  3. 10th class certificate for proof of date of birth.
  4. 12th class marksheet and certificate.
  5. Certificate of typing skills (if any).
  6. Identity proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, etc.).
  7. Proof of address.
  8. Experience certificate (if any).
  9. Reservation category certificate (if applicable).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Download Application Form Click Here
DTIDC Official Website http://dtidc.co.in/

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the DTIDC LDC recruitment?

The last date for submission of the application is December 10, 2025, by 5:00 PM.

Question 2: Is this a permanent government job?

No, this post is purely on a contract basis. The initial contract will be for one year.

Question 3: What will be the selection process?

The selection process is likely to include a computer-based typing test and document verification. There will be no interview.

Question 4: How do I apply?

You need to fill out the application form in the prescribed format and submit it offline, along with self-attested copies of required documents, to the given address by post or in person.

Question 5: Can final-year students apply?

No, the candidate must possess the 12th class pass certificate as of the crucial date of eligibility (November 25, 2025).

entry.title: Sainik School Jhansi Recruitment 2025: Premium Career Opportunities in the Ministry of Defence
entry.content:
Sainik School Jhansi Recruitment 2025: Premium Career Opportunities in the Ministry of Defence

Join the Elite Residential Educational System – Recruitment for Regular, Contractual, and Temporary Teaching and Administrative Staff

Post Name: PGT, TGT, Librarian, Counselor, Music Teacher, LDC, Lab Assistant, Matron
Last Date: November 22, 2025
Total Posts: Multiple Vacancies (Regular and Contractual)

The Sainik School Society, functioning under the aegis of the Ministry of Defence (MoD), Government of India, represents one of the most prestigious residential school networks in the country. The primary mission of these schools is to prepare young students, known as cadets, for entry into the National Defence Academy (NDA) and other officer training academies. To maintain its high standards of academic excellence and holistic development, Sainik School Jhansi, located in the historic state of Uttar Pradesh, has officially announced its recruitment drive for the year 2025. This notification seeks to fill a diverse array of academic and administrative positions including Post Graduate Teachers (PGT), Trained Graduate Teachers (TGT), Counselors, Librarians, Lower Division Clerks (LDC), and specialized support staff.

Working at a Sainik School is significantly different from a traditional day school. As a residential institution, staff members are expected to contribute not just in the classroom but also to the overall development of cadets in a 24/7 environment. The Sainik School Jhansi Recruitment 2025 offers three distinct types of vacancies: Regular (Permanent), Contractual (Fixed Term), and Temporary (Ad-hoc). This variety allows experienced educators as well as young professionals to find a role that suits their career stage. The regular positions are governed by the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) levels, ensuring long-term financial security and central government benefits, while contractual roles offer consolidated high-remuneration packages. The application process is strictly offline, and all submissions must reach the school headquarters by November 22, 2025.

The selection process at Sainik School Jhansi is known for its rigor and transparency, designed to select candidates who possess high pedagogical skills, psychological balance, and a commitment to the values of the Indian Armed Forces. Whether you are a specialized teacher in Physics or Mathematics, a mental health professional aiming for a Counselor role, or an administrative expert looking for a Clerk position, this drive provides a stable gateway into a central government organization. This 2000-word guide provides a comprehensive analysis of eligibility criteria, salary breakdowns, the multi-stage selection procedure, and expert preparation strategies to help you navigate the competitive landscape of Sainik School recruitments.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must be highly attentive to the offline nature of this recruitment. Since the school relies on physical mail delivery, it is recommended to send the application via Speed Post or Registered Post at least 10 days before the deadline. Late applications, regardless of the reason, will be rejected by the selection committee.

Event Description Crucial Dates and Details
Notification Release Date November 2025
Last Date for Receipt of Application November 22, 2025
Application Mode Offline (By Post)
Job Type Regular / Contractual / Temporary
Cut-off Date for Age & Eligibility November 22, 2025
Official Website sainikschooljhansi.com

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Salary Matrix

Sainik School Jhansi has categorized its vacancies based on the functional requirements of the school. Regular posts offer a pay scale with yearly increments and allowances, while contractual posts provide a high fixed consolidated monthly salary.

Academic and Specialized Teaching Staff

Post Designation Vacancy Type Category Salary / Pay Matrix
PGT (Mathematics) Regular 01 (OBC) Level 8 (₹47,600 + DA/HRA)
PGT (Physics) Regular 01 (UR) Level 8 (₹47,600 + DA/HRA)
PGT (Computer Science) Regular 01 (SC) Level 8 (₹47,600 + DA/HRA)
PGT (Biology) Regular 01 (UR) Level 8 (₹47,600 + DA/HRA)
TGT (Social Science) Contractual 01 (UR) ₹69,595/- (Consolidated)
Librarian / Counselor Contractual 02 (UR) ₹69,595/- (Consolidated)
Music Teacher Contractual 01 (SC) ₹45,260/- (Consolidated)
PEM/PTI-cum-Matron (Female) Contractual 01 (UR) ₹45,260/- (Consolidated)
Lab Assistants (Phy/Chem/Bio) Contractual 03 (ST, OBC, UR) ₹39,525/- (Consolidated)

Administrative Support Staff

Post Designation Vacancy Type Category Salary / Pay Matrix
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) Regular 01 (ST) Level 2 (₹19,900 + DA/HRA)
Estimated Gross Salary: For a Regular PGT in Level 8, the starting gross salary including current allowances will be approximately ₹75,000 to ₹82,000. Regular LDCs in Level 2 will start with a gross of approximately ₹32,000 to ₹35,000. Additionally, many staff members in Sainik Schools are provided with Rent-Free Accommodation inside the campus and subsidized education for their children.

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

The academic requirements for Sainik Schools are strictly aligned with NCTE and CBSE norms. For teaching posts, proficiency in English communication is a mandatory requirement given the school's objective of preparing cadets for a career in the Armed Forces officers' cadre.

  • PGT (Post Graduate Teacher): Master’s degree in the relevant subject with at least 50% marks and B.Ed. from a recognized university. For PGT Computer Science, a B.Ed. is mandatory as per the 2025 school norms. Age: 21 to 40 Years.
  • TGT (Social Science): Graduation with at least 50% marks in a combination of History/Geography/Political Science/Economics (History or Geography must be one of the subjects). Mandatory: Must have passed CTET Paper II and possess a B.Ed. degree. Age: 21 to 35 Years.
  • Librarian: Degree in Library Science OR Graduate with a one-year Diploma in Library Science. Ability to manage digital library systems is preferred. Age: 21 to 35 Years.
  • Lower Division Clerk (LDC): 10th Standard (Matriculation) pass. Skill: Typing speed of at least 40 WPM in English. Knowledge of shorthand and MS Office is highly desirable. Age: 18 to 50 Years.
  • Lab Assistant: 10+2 (Intermediate) with Science in the relevant stream. Age: 18 to 50 Years.

Linguistic Proficiency: Across all posts, the candidate should have the ability to teach/work in both English and Hindi. English is the primary medium of instruction in Sainik Schools.

Selection Process and Evaluation roadmap

Sainik School Jhansi utilizes a comprehensive selection methodology. Since the roles involve residential duties, the school looks for candidates who are psychologically fit to manage young cadets.

  1. Written Examination: This is the primary stage. It tests subject knowledge, pedagogical understanding, and general aptitude. For LDCs, it focuses on grammar, math, and office procedures.
  2. Class Demonstration / Skill Test: Mandatory for teaching posts. Candidates are asked to deliver a 20-30 minute lesson to actual cadets while being observed by a panel of experts. LDCs undergo a typing speed verification test.
  3. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates from the written and demonstration rounds are called for an interview with the Local Board of Administration (LBA). The interview focuses on personality, commitment to the Sainik School system, and communication skills.
  4. Medical Fitness: As per the standards of the Sainik Schools Society, final selection is subject to medical clearance.

Qualifying Criteria: A candidate must score at least 50% in the written exam to be considered for the next stage. Merit is prepared by combining marks from all evaluation stages.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Understanding the syllabus is critical to clearing the written hurdle. The exam is typically 100 marks and conducted in OMR or descriptive format depending on the post level.

Teaching Posts (PGT/TGT):

  • Core Subject (50 Marks): Advanced topics from the Master's/Bachelor's level syllabus. For TGT Social Science, focus on the NCERT Grade 6 to 12 curriculum.
  • Pedagogy and Child Psychology (25 Marks): Learning theories, classroom management, and child development milestones.
  • General English and GK (25 Marks): Grammar, current affairs, and basic awareness of the Indian Armed Forces.

Non-Teaching Posts (LDC/Lab Asst):

  • General Intelligence: Logical reasoning and series (25 Marks).
  • Numerical Ability: 10th standard level arithmetic (25 Marks).
  • Office Management (LDC only): Letter writing, filing, and basic accounts (25 Marks).
  • General awareness: Indian History, Geography, and Defense news (25 Marks).

Expert Preparation Strategy for Sainik School Recruitment

Preparation for a Sainik School job requires a mix of academic depth and awareness of the military-academic environment. Candidates often make the mistake of preparing like it's a general state government exam.

Focus on NCERT: Sainik Schools strictly follow the CBSE curriculum. If you are applying for TGT or PGT roles, ensure you have a "line-by-line" understanding of NCERT textbooks from Grade 6 to 12. Many questions in the written exam are derived directly from NCERT concepts.

Master the Classroom Demo: For teachers, the demonstration is where most are filtered out. Practice delivering lessons that are interactive. Focus on your Board Work (writing clearly and structured), eye contact with students, and the ability to explain complex topics in simple English. The panel looks for a "Leader" in the classroom, not just a lecturer.

Administrative Speed for LDC: If you are aiming for the LDC role, typing speed is your biggest hurdle. Practice on a standard keyboard (not a laptop) to achieve a consistent speed of 45 WPM to safely clear the 40 WPM requirement during the test. For Lab Assistants, revise the practical laboratory experiments of Class 11 and 12.

Best Books for Preparation

Having the right books keeps your preparation targeted. Below are the recommended study materials for the Sainik School Jhansi exam.

Post Area Recommended Book List
PGT / TGT Subjects NCERT Textbooks and Youth Competition Times (YCT) Subject-wise Solved Papers.
Pedagogy "Child Development and Pedagogy" by Sandeep Kumar or Arihant Expert.
LDC / Clerk Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi and Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal.
General Knowledge Lucent’s General Knowledge and monthly Pratiyogita Darpan.
Defense Awareness Manorama Yearbook (Defense Section) or official MoD annual reports.

How to Apply Offline: Step-by-Step Guide

The application process for Sainik School Jhansi is strictly manual. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure your envelope is not rejected due to clerical errors.

  1. Visit the official school website: www.sainikschooljhansi.com.
  2. Go to the "Careers" section and download the Prescribed Application Format.
  3. Fill out the form in BLOCK LETTERS using a blue or black ballpoint pen. Ensure your name and father's name match your 10th-standard certificate.
  4. Prepare the Demand Draft (DD): Go to any SBI branch and obtain a DD in favor of "Principal, Sainik School Jhansi", payable at SBI Bundelkhand University Branch (Code: 03808). (₹500 for Gen/OBC, ₹250 for SC/ST).
  5. Attach self-attested photocopies of all marksheets, degree certificates, experience letters, and your category certificate.
  6. Place the signed form, the original DD, and documents in an envelope. Clearly write on top: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF [NAME OF POST] - [CATEGORY]".
  7. Send the envelope via Speed Post or Registered Post to the official address mentioned in the links section below.

Documents Needed to Apply

Documentation is a critical part of the initial screening. Ensure all copies are Self-Attested. Do not send original documents by post.

  1. Educational Proof: 10th Standard Passing Certificate (for Age Proof) and all marksheets from 12th to your highest degree.
  2. Professional Proof: B.Ed. Degree and CTET Marks Card (for TGT roles).
  3. Experience Certificates: Clear proof from previous school principals on official letterheads.
  4. Demand Draft: The original DD must be attached with a paper clip to the application form.
  5. Identity Proof: Clear photocopy of Aadhaar Card or PAN Card.
  6. Caste Certificate: If applying under SC/ST/OBC categories (Central Government format).

Important Links and Address

Resource Description Direct Official Link / Details
Postal Address The Principal, Sainik School Jhansi, PO: Bhagwantpura, District: Jhansi (UP), Pin- 284127
Application Form Download Download Here
Official Notification PDF View Details

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply through Email or an Online Portal?

No. Sainik School Jhansi does not accept digital applications for this drive. All applications must be submitted in the Offline Prescribed Format and sent via post. Applications sent via email will not be acknowledged.

Q2: Is CTET mandatory for PGT (Post Graduate Teacher) posts?

No. As per CBSE and Sainik School Society rules, CTET is not mandatory for PGT positions. It is, however, an absolute requirement for the TGT (Trained Graduate Teacher) vacancies.

Q3: What is the "Regular" post probation period?

For permanent/regular posts, selected candidates are placed on a probation period of one year, which can be extended to two years. Your performance during this time determines your final confirmation in the service.

Q4: Are candidates from states other than Uttar Pradesh eligible?

Yes. This is a central government-aided recruitment, and all Indian citizens meeting the eligibility criteria from any state are eligible to apply. Please note that reservation benefits for SC/ST/OBC are provided as per the Central List.

Q5: Will the school provide a travel allowance (TA) for the interview?

No. According to the official notification, candidates must bear their own expenses for travel and stay when attending the written test, class demonstration, and interview in Jhansi.

Q6: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, if you meet the eligibility for multiple posts, you can apply. However, you must send separate applications in separate envelopes and pay the application fee separately for each post.

entry.title: Jharkhand Kakshpal Vacancy 2026 for 526 Posts
entry.content:
Jharkhand Kakshpal Vacancy 2026 for 526 Posts

Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC) has invited online applications for the Jharkhand Kakshpal Competitive Examination (JKCE) 2025-26. This recruitment drive aims to fill 526 vacancies (485 Regular + 41 Backlog) for the post of Kakshpal (Jail Warder). This is a major opportunity for 10th-pass candidates to secure a government job in Jharkhand. The online application process is from January 01, 2026, to January 31, 2026.

Post Name: Kakshpal (Jail Warder)
Last Date: 31 January 2026
Total Posts: 526

The Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC) has released the official brochure for the Jharkhand Kakshpal (Jail Warder) Competitive Examination, initiating the recruitment process for a significant number of vacancies under the Home, Jail and Disaster Management Department. This drive seeks to induct physically fit and eligible individuals into the state's prison system, offering a stable and respected career path.

The selection process for the Kakshpal post is a rigorous three-stage procedure designed to select the most suitable candidates. Uniquely, the process begins with a Physical Efficiency Test (PET), which is qualifying in nature. Candidates who successfully clear the PET will then undergo a Medical Examination, and only the medically fit candidates will be allowed to appear for the final OMR-based Written Examination. The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the written test. The application is to be submitted online through the official JSSC portal.

Jharkhand Kakshpal Vacancy 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. The selection process starts with a Physical test, which is a crucial qualifying stage.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC)
Exam Name Jharkhand Kakshpal Competitive Examination (JKCE)
Post Name Kakshpal (Jail Warder)
Total Vacancies 526 (485 Regular + 41 Backlog)
Registration Start Date 01 January 2026
Registration Last Date 31 January 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Jharkhand
Official Website jssc.jharkhand.gov.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment aims to fill a total of 526 vacancies, comprising regular and backlog posts.

Post Name Vacancies Pay Scale
Kakshpal (Jail Warder) - Regular 485 Pay Matrix Level-2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200)
Kakshpal (Jail Warder) - Backlog 41
Total 526
Note: For a detailed district-wise and category-wise reservation breakdown, candidates must refer to the official notification brochure.

Application Fee

  • For Unreserved (UR), EWS, EBC, and BC candidates: ₹100/-
  • For SC and ST candidates of Jharkhand domicile: ₹50/-

The fee must be paid online. Candidates with disabilities are exempted from the fee.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions regarding education, age, and physical standards.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification Must have passed 10th Class (Matriculation) from a recognized educational institution in India.
Age Limit (as per dates in notification)
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age (UR/EWS - Male): 25 Years
  • Maximum Age (EBC/BC - Male): 27 Years
  • Maximum Age (All Category - Female): 28 Years
  • Maximum Age (SC/ST - Male/Female): 30 Years
Age relaxation for Ex-servicemen is applicable as per rules.
Physical Standards Test (PST)

Candidates must meet the following minimum physical standards:

CategoryMinimum HeightChest (Male only)
Unreserved (UR) / EWS / EBC / BC160 cm81 cm (Minimum)
SC / ST155 cm79 cm (Minimum)
Female (All Categories)148 cmNot Applicable

Selection Process

The selection is a three-stage process, with the Physical Test being the first and foremost stage.

  1. Stage 1: Physical Efficiency Test (PET): All eligible applicants will first be called for the PET. This test is qualifying in nature. Candidates who fail to complete the run within the specified time will be disqualified.
  2. Stage 2: Medical Examination: Candidates who successfully qualify in the PET will undergo a mandatory medical examination to ensure they meet the required medical standards.
  3. Stage 3: Written Examination (OMR-based): Only those candidates who are declared medically fit will be issued admit cards for the written examination. The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in this examination.

Exam Pattern (Written Test)

The OMR-based written examination will consist of two papers, both with objective-type multiple-choice questions.

Paper-I: Language Knowledge (Qualifying)

  • Total Questions: 120 | Total Marks: 360 | Duration: 2 Hours
  • Subjects: Hindi Language (80 questions) and English Language (40 questions).
  • Qualifying Nature: Candidates must score a minimum of 30% marks in this paper to be eligible for the evaluation of Paper-II. Marks from this paper will NOT be added to the final merit list.

Paper-II: General Knowledge and Numerical Ability (Merit Paper)

  • Total Questions: 100 | Total Marks: 300 | Duration: 2 Hours
  • Syllabus:
    • Part A (Hindi Language): 50 questions
    • Part B (General Knowledge): 25 questions
    • Part C (Numerical Ability): 25 questions
  • Marking Scheme: 3 marks for every correct answer.
  • Negative Marking: 1 mark will be deducted for every incorrect answer.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the normalized marks obtained in Paper-II.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

The PET is the first stage of the selection process and is mandatory and qualifying. It consists of only one event: Running.

Gender Event Qualifying Standard
Male Candidates 10 KM Run To be completed within 60 minutes.
Female Candidates 5 KM Run To be completed within 40 minutes.

How to Prepare

A dual preparation strategy focusing on physical endurance and written exam syllabus is crucial.

  • For PET: This is the first and most critical hurdle. Start a rigorous running practice immediately. Gradually increase your distance and stamina to ensure you can comfortably complete the 10 km (for males) or 5 km (for females) run within the time limit.
  • For Written Exam: Once you are confident about the PET, focus on the written syllabus. For Paper-I, brush up on Hindi and English grammar up to the 10th-grade level. For Paper-II, study Hindi literature and grammar, basic General Knowledge (with a focus on Jharkhand), and practice 10th-grade level Mathematics.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for comprehensive preparation.

  • Hindi/English: "Samanya Hindi" by Lucent and "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and a dedicated book on Jharkhand GK (e.g., by Arihant or Udaan Publications).
  • Numerical Ability: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal (focus on 10th-level topics).
  • Practice Sets: Books with previous year papers of JSSC exams (like Constable, Excise Constable) will be very helpful.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the official JSSC website.

  1. Visit the official JSSC website: www.jssc.nic.in or the application portal: jkce2025.cbtexam.in/Jssc/index.html.
  2. Find the link for "Online Application for JKCE-2025" and click on it.
  3. Register on the portal by providing your basic details like name, mobile number, and email ID.
  4. Log in with your credentials and fill out the detailed application form.
  5. Upload your recent photograph and signature in the prescribed format.
  6. Pay the application fee online.
  7. Review your application carefully and submit it.
  8. Download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for your records.

Jharkhand Kakshpal Vacancy 2026 Jharkhand Document List

Candidates should have the following documents ready for the online application and subsequent verification:

  1. 10th Class (Matriculation) Certificate for proof of age and educational qualification.
  2. Scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  3. Jharkhand Domicile Certificate (Local Resident Certificate).
  4. Caste Certificate (for SC/ST/EBC/BC candidates) and EWS certificate, if applicable.
  5. A valid Mobile Number and Email ID.
  6. Aadhaar Card or other valid ID proof.

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official JSSC Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the first stage of the Jharkhand Kakshpal selection process?

The first stage is the Physical Efficiency Test (PET), which involves a 10 KM run for males and a 5 KM run for females. It is a qualifying test.

Q2: Is there a negative marking in the written exam?

Yes, there is negative marking in Paper-II of the written exam. For every correct answer, you get 3 marks, and for every incorrect answer, 1 mark is deducted.

Q3: I am from Bihar. Can I apply for the Jharkhand Kakshpal vacancy?

The notification specifies requirements like a Jharkhand Domicile Certificate for availing reservation. Candidates from other states are generally considered under the Unreserved (UR) category, but they must fulfill all other eligibility criteria. It is advisable to read the brochure carefully regarding domicile rules.

Q4: What is the minimum qualification for the Kakshpal post?

The minimum educational qualification is a pass in the 10th Class (Matriculation) from a recognized board in India.

entry.title: SBI CBO Recruitment 2026 for 2273 Circle Based Officer Posts
entry.content:
SBI CBO Recruitment 2026 for 2273 Circle Based Officer Posts

Advertisement No. CRPD/CBO/2025-26/18: The State Bank of India (SBI) has invited online applications for the recruitment of 2,273 Circle Based Officers (CBO) across its various circles in India. This is a major opportunity for experienced officers working in other Scheduled Commercial Banks or Regional Rural Banks to join SBI. The online application window is open from January 29, 2026, to February 18, 2026.

Post Name: Circle Based Officer (CBO)
Last Date: 18 February 2026
Total Posts: 2273

The State Bank of India, the country's largest public sector bank, has announced a significant recruitment drive for the position of Circle Based Officer (CBO). This recruitment aims to fill a total of 2,050 regular and 223 backlog vacancies, bringing the total to 2,273 posts. Unlike probationary officer roles, this position is for candidates who already possess a minimum of two years of experience as an officer in a Scheduled Commercial Bank or a Regional Rural Bank. The selected candidates will be posted in the specific circle they apply for and will not be eligible for inter-circle transfers for a significant period.

The selection process is a comprehensive four-stage procedure designed to select the most suitable candidates. It includes an Online Examination (comprising both Objective and Descriptive tests), a Screening of applications to verify experience, a Personal Interview, and a Local Language Proficiency Test. The starting basic pay is attractive, with two advance increments in the JMGS-I scale. This is a golden opportunity for experienced banking professionals to join the prestigious State Bank of India.

SBI CBO Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take note of the crucial dates and other important details for this specialized recruitment drive.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization State Bank of India (SBI)
Advertisement Number CRPD/CBO/2025-26/18
Post Name Circle Based Officer (CBO)
Total Vacancies 2273 (2050 Regular + 223 Backlog)
Application Start Date 29 January 2026
Application Last Date 18 February 2026
Online Test Date (Tentative) March 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Bank Job (for experienced officers)
Job Location Various Circles across India
Official Website sbi.bank.in/web/careers

Circle-wise Vacancy and Language Details

The recruitment is being conducted for specific circles. Candidates must apply for one circle only and must be proficient in one of its specified local languages.

Circle State/UT Local Language(s) Total Vacancies (Regular + Backlog)
AmaravatiAndhra PradeshTelugu/Urdu98
BengaluruKarnatakaKannada211
BhopalMadhya Pradesh, ChhattisgarhHindi114
BhubaneswarOdishaOdia81
ChandigarhJ&K, Ladakh, HP, Haryana, PunjabUrdu/Hindi/Punjabi107
ChennaiTamil Nadu, PondicherryTamil204
GandhinagarGujarat, D&N Haveli, Daman & DiuGujarati236
GuwahatiAssam, Arunachal Pradesh, Manipur, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Nagaland, TripuraAssamese, Bengali, Bodo, etc.79
HyderabadTelanganaTelugu/Urdu80
JaipurRajasthanHindi108
KolkataWest Bengal, A&N Islands, SikkimBengali/Nepali/Hindi231
LucknowUttar PradeshHindi/Urdu221
Mumbai MetroMaharashtra, GoaMarathi/Konkani145
MaharashtraMaharashtraMarathi204
New DelhiDelhi, Uttarakhand, Haryana, UPHindi82
ThiruvananthapuramKerala, LakshadweepMalayalam80
Total2273

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

  • For General, EWS, and OBC candidates: ₹750/-
  • For SC, ST, and PwBD candidates: NIL (No Fee)

The fee must be paid online. Candidates working in SBI in the clerical or supervisory cadre are not eligible to apply.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 31.12.2025)

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility conditions regarding education, experience, and age to be considered for the post.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification Graduation in any discipline from a recognized University or any equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government. This includes qualifications like Medical, Engineering, Chartered Accountant, Cost Accountant, etc.
Experience Minimum 2 years' experience (post essential academic qualification) as an officer in any Scheduled Commercial Bank or any Regional Rural Bank as listed in the Second Schedule of the RBI Act.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 21 Years
  • Maximum Age: 30 Years
(i.e., must have been born between 01.01.1996 and 31.12.2004). Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories (5 years for SC/ST, 3 for OBC, etc.).
Local Language Must be proficient (reading, writing, and understanding) in one of the specified local languages of the circle for which they are applying. A language test will be conducted for those who cannot provide proof of having studied the language in their 10th or 12th standard.

Selection Process

The selection process for the CBO post is a comprehensive 4-phase procedure.

  1. Phase 1: Online Examination: This consists of an Objective Test (120 marks) and a Descriptive Test (50 marks). The marks from both tests will be considered for the final merit list.
  2. Phase 2: Screening: The online applications and supporting documents of candidates who qualify in the online test will be screened by a committee to verify their eligibility and match their job profile with that of an SBI Scale-I Generalist Officer.
  3. Phase 3: Interview: Candidates who clear the screening phase will be called for a personal interview, which will be for 50 marks.
  4. Phase 4: Local Language Proficiency Test (LPT): Provisionally selected candidates (based on merit from online test and interview) will have to undergo a language test. This is qualifying in nature.

The final merit list will be prepared by aggregating the normalized marks from the Online Test (out of 75) and the Interview (out of 25).

Exam Pattern

The online examination consists of two parts, conducted one after another.

1. Objective Test

Section No. of Questions Max. Marks Duration
English Language303030 minutes
Banking Knowledge404040 minutes
General Awareness/Economy303030 minutes
Computer Aptitude202020 minutes
Total1201202 Hours

Important: There is NO penalty for wrong answers in the Objective Tests.

2. Descriptive Test

  • Duration: 30 minutes
  • Total Marks: 50
  • Content: A test of English Language with two questions (Letter Writing & Essay).
  • Mode: The test is conducted online immediately after the objective test, and candidates have to type their answers on the computer.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for the post of Circle Based Officer.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A smart preparation strategy is required, focusing on the specific demands of the CBO exam.

  • Banking Knowledge is Key: This section has the highest weightage. As an experienced officer, you should already have a strong foundation. Revise RBI circulars, banking regulations (like Negotiable Instruments Act, SARFAESI), and general banking principles.
  • General Awareness/Economy: Focus on economic and banking-related current affairs from the last 6-8 months. Pay attention to union budgets, economic surveys, and major government schemes.
  • Descriptive English: Practice typing essays and formal letters on banking and economic topics. Since this test is online, improving your typing speed and accuracy is crucial.
  • Prepare Your Job Profile Document: The screening stage is critical. Prepare a detailed job profile document and get it certified by your employer. Ensure it accurately reflects your duties and aligns with the responsibilities of a generalist officer.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard resources.

  • Banking Knowledge: "Handbook on Banking" by N.S. Toor; RBI's official website and publications.
  • General Awareness/Economy: A national newspaper like The Hindu or The Economic Times; "Indian Economy" by Ramesh Singh.
  • English Language: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi for basics, and practice writing essays on current topics.
  • Computer Aptitude: "Objective Computer Awareness" by Arihant Publications.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the SBI careers page.

  1. Visit the SBI careers website: https://sbi.bank.in/web/careers/current-openings.
  2. Find the advertisement for "RECRUITMENT OF CIRCLE BASED OFFICERS" and click on "Apply Online".
  3. Register on the portal by providing your basic information to generate a provisional registration number and password.
  4. Log in and fill out the detailed application form with your personal, educational, and experience details.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, ID proof, date of birth proof, resume, educational certificates, experience certificates, and other required documents as per the notification.
  6. Pay the application fee online through the payment gateway.
  7. Review the application carefully before final submission and take a printout of the final form and e-receipt for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

Candidates must have clear, scanned copies of the following documents ready in PDF format (unless specified otherwise):

  1. Recent Photograph and Signature (jpg/jpeg).
  2. ID Proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, PAN Card).
  3. Proof of Date of Birth (e.g., 10th Certificate).
  4. Brief Resume detailing your qualifications and experience.
  5. All Educational Certificates (Degree/Marksheets).
  6. All Experience Certificates from previous/current employers.
  7. Job Profile certified by your employer.
  8. Latest Salary Slip and Form-16.
  9. Caste/PwBD certificate, if applicable.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: I work as an officer in an NBFC. Am I eligible to apply?

No, the eligibility criteria specifically require a minimum of 2 years of experience as an officer in a Scheduled Commercial Bank or a Regional Rural Bank only.

Q2: Is there any negative marking in the online objective test?

No, the official notification clearly states that there will be no penalty for wrong answers in the Objective Tests.

Q3: Can I get a transfer to another circle after joining?

No, the selected candidates will not be entitled to any inter-circle transfer until their promotion to SMGS-IV Grade or 12 years of service, whichever is later.

Q4: I have not studied my state's local language in 10th or 12th. Can I still apply?

Yes, you can apply. However, if you are provisionally selected, you will have to appear for and qualify a Local Language Proficiency Test before you can be appointed.

entry.title: RSMSSB जमादार भर्ती 2025: आबकारी विभाग में 72 पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
RSMSSB जमादार भर्ती 2025: आबकारी विभाग में 72 पदों पर भर्ती!

राजस्थान कर्मचारी चयन बोर्ड ने जमादार ग्रेड-II पदों के लिए CET-2024 पास उम्मीदवारों से ऑनलाइन आवेदन मांगे हैं। अंतिम तिथि 15 नवम्बर 2025 है।

राजस्थान में सरकारी नौकरी का इंतज़ार कर रहे युवाओं के लिए खुशखबरी! राजस्थान कर्मचारी चयन बोर्ड (RSSB), जयपुर ने राजस्थान आबकारी विभाग के अंतर्गत जमादार ग्रेड-II के पदों पर भर्ती के लिए अधिसूचना (विज्ञापन संख्या 07/2025) जारी की है। इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत कुल 72 पदों को भरा जाएगा।

यह भर्ती उन्हीं उम्मीदवारों के लिए है जिन्होंने सामन पात्रता परीक्षा (CET) सीनियर सेकेंडरी स्तर – 2024 पास की है। योग्य उम्मीदवारों को बोर्ड की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट के माध्यम से ऑनलाइन आवेदन करना होगा।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ17 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि15 नवम्बर 2025 (रात 11:59 बजे तक)
ऑफलाइन (OMR) परीक्षा तिथि27 दिसम्बर 2025

रिक्तियों का विवरण

कुल 72 पद गैर-अनुसूचित (Non-Scheduled) और अनुसूचित (Scheduled) क्षेत्रों में इस प्रकार विभाजित हैं:

क्षेत्रकुल पद
गैर-अनुसूचित क्षेत्र64
अनुसूचित क्षेत्र08
कुल योग72
आरक्षण नोट: महिला, भूतपूर्व सैनिक और उत्कृष्ट खिलाड़ी वर्गों के लिए आरक्षण क्षैतिज (horizontal) होगा। इस भर्ती में दिव्यांगजन के लिए कोई पद आरक्षित नहीं हैं।

वन टाइम रजिस्ट्रेशन (OTR) एवं शुल्क

इस भर्ती के लिए “वन टाइम रजिस्ट्रेशन” (OTR) प्रणाली लागू है। जिन्होंने पहले OTR नहीं किया है, उन्हें निम्न शुल्क देना होगा:

  • सामान्य / क्रीमी लेयर OBC/MBC: ₹600/-
  • राजस्थान के Non-Creamy OBC/MBC, EWS, SC/ST: ₹400/-
  • सभी दिव्यांगजन: ₹400/-

महत्वपूर्ण: जिन्होंने पहले OTR शुल्क जमा किया है, उन्हें दोबारा भुगतान की आवश्यकता नहीं है।

पात्रता मानदंड

मापदंडविवरण
CET स्कोरउम्मीदवार को सामन पात्रता परीक्षा (CET) सीनियर सेकेंडरी स्तर – 2024 में शामिल होकर उत्तीर्ण होना आवश्यक है।
शैक्षणिक योग्यता
  • किसी मान्यता प्राप्त बोर्ड से सीनियर सेकेंडरी (12वीं) परीक्षा उत्तीर्ण।
  • साथ ही देवनागरी लिपि में हिंदी का ज्ञान और राजस्थान की संस्कृति की जानकारी होनी चाहिए।
आयु सीमान्यूनतम आयु 18 वर्ष और अधिकतम 40 वर्ष (आरक्षित वर्गों को नियमानुसार छूट)।
शारीरिक मापदंड
  • पुरुष: ऊँचाई 168 सेमी, सीना 81 सेमी (5 सेमी फुलाव सहित)।
  • महिला: ऊँचाई 152 सेमी, वजन 47.5 किलो।
  • पहाड़ी / आदिवासी क्षेत्रों के उम्मीदवारों को छूट दी जाएगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया एवं परीक्षा पैटर्न

  1. लिखित परीक्षा: CET-2024 के योग्य उम्मीदवारों को OMR आधारित परीक्षा के लिए बुलाया जाएगा।
    • कुल प्रश्न: 100
    • कुल अंक: 100
    • समय: 2 घंटे
    • नकारात्मक अंकन: हर गलत उत्तर पर 1/3 अंक काटे जाएंगे।
    • सिलेबस: सामान्य ज्ञान, समाज अध्ययन, भूगोल, इतिहास, कला एवं संस्कृति, दैनिक विज्ञान, गणित और समसामयिक घटनाएँ (राजस्थान केंद्रित)।
  2. शारीरिक दक्षता परीक्षा (PET): लिखित परीक्षा उत्तीर्ण उम्मीदवारों को बुलाया जाएगा। इसमें दौड़ शामिल होगी और यह क्वालिफाइंग नेचर की होगी।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक SSO पोर्टल http://sso.rajasthan.gov.in पर जाएं।
  2. यदि आप नए उपयोगकर्ता हैं तो पहले पंजीकरण करें।
  3. लॉगिन करने के बाद “Citizen Apps (G2C)” सेक्शन में “Recruitment Portal” चुनें।
  4. यहां “One Time Registration (OTR)” करें और शुल्क जमा करें।
  5. OTR पूर्ण होने के बाद “Jamadar Grade-II Recruitment 2025” के लिए आवेदन करें।
  6. CET-2024 का आवेदन नंबर डालें और विवरण सत्यापित करें।
  7. सारी जानकारी भरें, फोटो अपलोड करें और आवेदन सबमिट करें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन (SSO पोर्टल)यहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक RSSB वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्र.1: क्या इस पद के लिए CET-2024 जरूरी है?
हाँ, केवल वही उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं जिन्होंने CET (Senior Secondary Level)-2024 उत्तीर्ण किया है।
प्र.2: क्या राजस्थान से बाहर के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
हाँ, लेकिन उन्हें सामान्य वर्ग में माना जाएगा और आरक्षण का लाभ नहीं मिलेगा।
प्र.3: क्या परीक्षा में नकारात्मक अंकन है?
हाँ, प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर पर 1/3 अंक काटे जाएंगे।
प्र.4: क्या दिव्यांगजन के लिए आरक्षण है?
नहीं, इस पद की प्रकृति फील्ड आधारित है इसलिए दिव्यांग वर्ग के लिए कोई आरक्षण नहीं है।

entry.title: Rajasthan Vidyut Vibhag Recruitment 2025: Mega Drive for 150+ ITI Technician Posts
entry.content:
Rajasthan Vidyut Vibhag Recruitment 2025: Mega Drive for 150+ ITI Technician Posts

Joint Recruitment by RVUNL and JVVNL for Technician-III, Operator-III, and Plant Attendant-III Cadres

Post Name: Technician-III, Operator-III, Plant Attendant-III (ITI)
Last Date: March 20, 2025
Total Posts: 150+ Vacancies

The Rajasthan State Electricity Board (RSEB), now divided into five state-owned power companies, has officially launched a massive recruitment campaign for the year 2025. This specialized drive, identified under Advertisement Number RVUN/Rectt./03, targets the technical backbone of the state’s power infrastructure: ITI-qualified professionals. The Rajasthan Rajya Vidyut Utpadan Nigam Limited (RVUNL), acting as the nodal agency, is leading this joint recruitment for itself and the Jaipur Vidyut Vitran Nigam Limited (JVVNL). This is a highly anticipated opportunity for candidates who have completed their vocational training in various trades and are seeking a stable, permanent career within the Rajasthan State Government PSU sector.

The 2025 recruitment aims to fill over 150 vacancies for the posts of Technician-III, Operator-III, and Plant Attendant-III. These roles are critical for the generation, transmission, and distribution of electricity across the desert state. Whether it is maintaining massive steam turbines at a thermal power plant in Suratgarh or ensuring line stability in the urban grid of Jaipur, these technical positions offer a challenging yet rewarding professional life. The application process is scheduled to begin on February 21, 2025, and will accept online submissions until March 20, 2025. Successful candidates will be inducted as "Probationer Trainees" for a period of two years, following which they will be regularized into the 7th Pay Commission pay levels.

Working in the Rajasthan Power Sector provides more than just a monthly paycheck; it offers job security, social prestige, and a clear path for professional advancement through departmental examinations. This recruitment follows a transparent two-tier selection process consisting of a Preliminary screening test and a Main competitive examination. Unlike many other state exams, this drive emphasizes technical trade proficiency alongside the rich history and culture of Rajasthan. This 2000-word exhaustive article serves as your definitive manual, providing deep insights into company-wise vacancy breakdowns, trade-specific eligibility, the detailed two-phase syllabus, and expert preparation strategies to help you secure your future in the Rajasthan Vidyut Vibhag.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Dates

Adherence to the timeline is essential. Rajasthan power companies use a centralized online application portal. Candidates are encouraged to complete their registration early to avoid the last-minute rush, especially since the portal handles a high volume of traffic from across the state. The age and qualification eligibility will be calculated as of the specified cut-off date.

Event Description Dates and Details
Official Notification Release February 2025
Online Application Start Date February 21, 2025
Last Date to Apply Online March 20, 2025
Online Fee Payment Deadline March 20, 2025
Expected Phase-I Exam Date April / May 2025
Job Nature Permanent State PSU (Government of Rajasthan)
Posting Location Rajasthan (Statewide)

Company Wise Vacancy Breakdown

The vacancies are distributed between the generation wing (RVUN) and the distribution wing (JVVN). Candidates must choose their company preference during the application process. Most vacancies are available in the Non-TSP (Non-Tribal Area) category.

1. Rajasthan Rajya Vidyut Utpadan Nigam Limited (RVUN)

RVUN is responsible for the operation of thermal and hydel power plants. The 90 vacancies are divided into four functional groups based on the ITI trades.

Group Designation Total Vacancies Eligible ITI Trades
Group-I (Electrical) 60 Electrician / Power Electrician / Wireman
Group-II (Electronics) 30 Electronics Mechanic / COPA
Group-III (Plant Ops) 30 (Cumulative) Boiler Attendant / Steam Turbine Operator
Group-IV (Mechanical) 30 (Cumulative) Welder (Gas & Electric) / Fitter

2. Jaipur Vidyut Vitran Nigam Limited (JVVN)

JVVN manages the power distribution in the Jaipur zone. It has announced 66 vacancies for the role of Technician-III.

Post Name Vacancies Eligible ITI Trades
Technician-III 66 Electrician / Wireman / Lineman / SBA / Power Electrician
Special Note: The vacancies are subject to the reservation policies of the Rajasthan State Government. Horizontal reservation for PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and Outstanding Sports Persons is applicable as per the latest amendments in the state service rules.

Salary Structure and Remuneration

Selected candidates will follow the standardized pay matrix of the Government of Rajasthan. The initial two years are critical, as the employee is treated as a trainee under probation.

  • Probation Period (02 Years): During this time, the employee will receive a fixed consolidated remuneration (Stipend) of ₹13,500 per month. No allowances like DA or HRA are provided during probation.
  • Regularization (Post-Probation): Upon successful completion of the two-year training, candidates are fixed at the minimum of Pay Level-4 (L-4). The initial basic pay will be ₹19,200 per month.
  • Allowances: Regularized employees are entitled to Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), City Compensatory Allowance (if applicable), and medical insurance under the RGHS (Rajasthan Government Health Scheme).

Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility for the Rajasthan Vidyut Vibhag ITI posts is defined by educational qualification, technical certification, and age. Candidates must possess the required certificates before the document verification stage.

  • Academic Qualification: Candidates must have passed the Secondary (10th) Exam from RBSE, CBSE, or any board recognized by the Government.
  • Technical Qualification: Must possess an ITI (NCVT/SCVT) or National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in the relevant trade specified for the group. For example, a candidate applying for RVUN Group-I must have an ITI in Electrician or Wireman.
  • Language Proficiency: Knowledge of Hindi written in Devanagari script and an understanding of Rajasthan’s local culture is essential.
  • Age Limit (as of Jan 1, 2026): Minimum age is 18 years. The maximum age is 31 years for RVUN and 30 years for JVVN.

Upper Age Relaxation: Rajasthan residents from reserved categories are eligible for the following relaxations: 5 years for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/MBC (Male), 5 years for UR Women (RVUN only), and 10 years for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/MBC (Women - RVUN only). PwBD candidates get a general 5-year relaxation.

Selection Process and Examination Stages

The selection is based entirely on merit in an objective competitive exam. There is no interview for these technical posts. The process is conducted in two distinct phases.

  1. Phase-I (Preliminary Examination): This is a computer-based screening test. It is qualifying in nature. Its primary purpose is to shortlist candidates for the Main exam. The prelims marks are NOT added to the final merit.
  2. Phase-II (Main Examination): Candidates equal to 10 times the number of vacancies are called for the Main exam based on Prelims merit. The final selection is based 100% on the marks obtained in Phase-II.
  3. Document Verification (DV): Successful candidates are called for a verification of their original ITI marksheets, caste certificates, and domicile proofs.

Detailed Exam Pattern and Syllabus

Success in the Vidyut Vibhag exam requires a balanced focus on technical trade theory and Rajasthan-centric general knowledge.

Phase-I: Preliminary Exam (100 Marks, 90 Minutes)

  • Technical Knowledge (60 Questions): Questions based on the standard ITI/NAC syllabus of the respective trade.
  • Rajasthan General Awareness (20 Questions): History, Geography, and Culture of Rajasthan.
  • General Awareness (20 Questions): Current affairs and basics of the Indian Constitution/Economy.
  • Note: There is no negative marking in the Preliminary exam.

Phase-II: Main Exam (150 Marks, 120 Minutes)

  • Technical Knowledge (100 Questions): Advanced trade-specific theory and practical safety protocols.
  • General Science & Math (20 Questions): Basic arithmetic and general science (10th standard level).
  • Rajasthan GK & General Studies (30 Questions): Detailed questions on Rajasthan’s history, heritage, and current policies.
  • Negative Marking: Every incorrect answer in the Main exam results in a deduction of 1/4th of the marks.

How to Prepare for the ITI Technician Exam

Securing a rank in the top 150 requires a disciplined 4-month preparation strategy. Since the main exam is technical-heavy, trade theory should be your priority.

Master Your ITI Trade: Revisit your NCVT textbooks. For electricians, focus heavily on Transformers, AC/DC Motors, and Electrical Measuring Instruments. For fitters, focus on Lathe Machines, Welding Symbols, and Workshop Calculation. Most questions are based on standard NCERT/DGT (Directorate General of Training) modules.

Rajasthan GK Strategy: The Rajasthan section is the "tie-breaker." Focus on Rajasthan’s Climate, Irrigation Projects (Indira Gandhi Canal), and the dynasties of Mewar and Marwar. Read daily newspapers for Rajasthan-specific current affairs like the state budget or new renewable energy policies. Use maps to memorize district-wise minerals and industrial centers.

Speed and Accuracy: In the Main exam, you have only 120 minutes for 150 questions. This means you have less than a minute per question. Practice with online mock tests to improve your OMR/CBT speed. Focus on eliminating wrong options quickly in the reasoning and science sections.

Best Books for Preparation

Using the right study material for the Rajasthan Power Sector exams is critical. Below are the top recommendations from successful ITI candidates.

Electrician Trade: Arihant ITI Electrician Theory or Asian Publishers Theory Books.
Rajasthan GK: "Lakshya Rajasthan" or "Dharohar" by Dr. Kunwar Kanak Singh.
General Aptitude: Lucent's General Science and Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal.
Reasoning: A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
Previous Year Papers: Youth Competition Times (YCT) Solved Papers for Electrical/Mechanical.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step Guide

The application process is conducted through a centralized portal. Follow these steps meticulously to avoid the rejection of your form.

  1. Visit the official website of RVUN: https://energy.rajasthan.gov.in/rrvun.
  2. Click on the link "Recruitment for ITI Technician-III 2025" and select "Apply Online."
  3. Phase 1 Registration: Choose "New Registration" and enter your Name, Mobile, and Email. You will receive an OTP and registration ID.
  4. Phase 2 Filling: Login and fill in your educational details, ITI trade, and Company Preference (RVUN or JVVN).
  5. Upload Documents: Scan and upload your passport photograph (white background), signature (black ink), left thumb impression, and Hand-written Declaration as per the specified sizes (JPEG).
  6. Payment: Pay the application fee through the online gateway (Debit Card/UPI/Net Banking).
  7. Final Submission: Review your application in the preview window. Click "Submit" and take a printout of the application form and e-receipt.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready in digital format before starting the application to prevent session timeouts on the portal.

  1. Aadhar Card: Essential for identity and biometric verification during the exam.
  2. Secondary (10th) Marksheet: As proof of date of birth and father's name.
  3. ITI / NAC Certificate: Valid NCVT or SCVT certificate in the relevant trade.
  4. Caste Certificate: Valid SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS certificate issued by the Rajasthan State Government.
  5. Domicile Certificate: Mandatory to claim age relaxation and reservation benefits for Rajasthan residents.
  6. Disability/Sports Certificate: If applying under these special reservation quotas.

Application Fee Structure

The fee is non-refundable and includes the portal charges and GST. The amount varies based on the candidate's social category and income status.

Candidate Category Total Fee (Incl. GST)
General (UR) / OBC (Creamy Layer) ₹1,000/-
SC / ST / OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS / MBC ₹500/-
PwBD / Sahariya Tribe ₹500/-

Important Links

Resource Description Direct Official Link
RVUN Official Recruitment Portal Click Here to Apply
Detailed Notification PDF Download Here
Jaipur Discom (JVVNL) Portal Visit Site

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can candidates from other states apply for Rajasthan Electricity jobs?

Yes, candidates from other states can apply for General category seats. However, they will not be eligible for age relaxation or fee concessions provided to Rajasthan residents. Knowledge of Rajasthani culture is essential as questions on Rajasthan GK are mandatory.

Q2: Is the ITI certificate mandatory at the time of application?

Candidates currently in the final semester of their ITI or NAC can apply, provided they produce the final passing certificate and marksheet at the time of Document Verification. Failure to produce the certificate by then will lead to disqualification.

Q3: What is the "TSP" and "Non-TSP" distinction?

TSP (Tribal Sub-Plan) refers to specific tribal areas in Rajasthan like Banswara or Pratapgarh. Non-TSP refers to the rest of the state. Most vacancies in this 2025 drive are for the Non-TSP area, meaning anyone meeting the eligibility can apply for them.

Q4: What happens if I miss the 2-year probation salary?

The ₹13,500 salary is a fixed stipend during probation as per Rajasthan Government service rules. You cannot claim allowances or yearly increments until you successfully complete the two-year training period and get regularized in Level-4.

Q5: Is there any interview for Technician-III or Plant Attendant-III?

No. As per the latest Government of Rajasthan directives, interviews have been completely abolished for Group C technical posts. The selection is 100% based on the marks of the Main online examination.

Q6: What is the syllabus for the technical section of RVUN Group-II?

Group-II targets Electronics and COPA. The syllabus includes Digital Electronics, Computer Hardware, Operating Systems, Basic Networking, and troubleshooting of electronic circuits, based on the standard ITI curriculum.

entry.title: MP फॉरेस्ट विभाग भर्ती 2025: स्टेट प्रोजेक्ट ऑफिसर के लिए भर्ती, सैलरी ₹1,48,000 प्रति माह!
entry.content:
MP फॉरेस्ट विभाग भर्ती 2025: स्टेट प्रोजेक्ट ऑफिसर के लिए भर्ती, सैलरी ₹1,48,000 प्रति माह!

मध्य प्रदेश फॉरेस्ट विभाग के ग्रीन इंडिया मिशन ने GoI-GEF-UNDP प्रोजेक्ट के तहत स्टेट प्रोजेक्ट ऑफिसर के पद के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं। आवेदन ऑफलाइन या ईमेल के माध्यम से भेजना होगा।

मध्य प्रदेश में पर्यावरण और वन संरक्षण के क्षेत्र में करियर बनाने का शानदार अवसर! मध्य प्रदेश फॉरेस्ट विभाग के Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (Green India Mission Wing) ने “State Project Officer” पद के लिए भर्ती अधिसूचना (No./GIM/2025/689) जारी की है।

यह भर्ती एक प्रतिष्ठित GoI-GEF-UNDP Project के अंतर्गत की जा रही है, जिसका उद्देश्य Land Degradation Neutrality (LDN) हासिल करना है। योग्य उम्मीदवारों को निर्धारित प्रारूप में ऑफलाइन या ईमेल द्वारा आवेदन करना होगा।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
नोटिफिकेशन जारी होने की तिथि03 October 2025
आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि26 October 2025 (शाम 5:00 बजे तक)

वेतन (Salary)

  • चयनित उम्मीदवार को ₹1,48,000 प्रति माह वेतन मिलेगा।
  • हर 6 महीने में प्रदर्शन के आधार पर वेतन की समीक्षा की जाएगी।
  • TA/DA मध्य प्रदेश के Class I अधिकारियों के समान लागू होगा।

पात्रता मानदंड

मापदंडआवश्यकता
आयु सीमान्यूनतम 25 वर्ष और अधिकतम 50 वर्ष।
अनिवार्य योग्यता
  • Post-Graduate Degree/Diploma in Forestry Management या
  • Post-Graduate Degree/Diploma in Management (Finance specialization) या
  • Master’s Degree in Environmental Science / Sustainable Development / Finance & Management।
वांछनीय योग्यता
  • पोस्ट-ग्रेजुएशन से ऊपर की अतिरिक्त योग्यता।
  • कम से कम 4 वर्ष का अनुभव — लैंडस्केप रिस्टोरेशन, प्राकृतिक संसाधन प्रबंधन या वानिकी प्रोजेक्ट्स में।
  • सरकारी या UN एजेंसियों (World Bank, UNDP आदि) के साथ कार्य का अनुभव।
  • Financial Management और Project Handling का अनुभव।
