  • Hindi और English दोनों में Communication तथा MS Office, Excel, PowerPoint में दक्षता।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. Shortlisting: आवेदन में दी गई जानकारी के आधार पर उम्मीदवारों की प्रारंभिक शॉर्टलिस्टिंग होगी।
  2. Presentation: चयनित उम्मीदवारों को 15 मिनट का प्रस्तुतीकरण देना होगा।
  3. Interview: प्रस्तुतीकरण के बाद इंटरव्यू आयोजित किया जाएगा।
अंक विभाजन (Marking Scheme):
  • Desirable Qualification – 12%
  • Additional Qualification – 13%
  • Presentation – 55%
  • Interview – 20%

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

आवेदन प्रक्रिया ऑफलाइन या ईमेल दोनों माध्यमों से की जा सकती है:

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट www.mpforest.gov.in से आवेदन फॉर्म डाउनलोड करें।
  2. फॉर्म को स्पष्ट रूप से भरें और ऊपर दाएं कोने में हाल की फोटो लगाएं।
  3. सभी आवश्यक दस्तावेजों की स्वयं-सत्यापित प्रतियां संलग्न करें।
  4. फॉर्म को नीचे दिए गए पते पर डाक से भेजें या ईमेल करें।
  5. आवेदन 26 October 2025, शाम 5:00 बजे तक पहुँचना चाहिए।

पता:
Office of PCCF (Green India Mission),
First Floor, Block-C, Van Bhawan,
Tulsi Nagar, Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh – 462003

Email ID: apccfgim@mp.gov.in

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्रवाई
एमपी फॉरेस्ट की आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
ऑफिशियल नोटिफिकेशन एवं फॉर्म“Recruitment” सेक्शन देखें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: कौन आवेदन कर सकता है?
जिनके पास संबंधित विषय में Post-Graduate डिग्री/डिप्लोमा और कम से कम 4 वर्ष का अनुभव है, वे आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
Q2: क्या लिखित परीक्षा होगी?
नहीं, चयन प्रक्रिया में कोई लिखित परीक्षा नहीं होगी। केवल प्रस्तुतीकरण और इंटरव्यू होगा।
Q3: क्या यह नौकरी स्थायी है?
नहीं, यह नियुक्ति Contract Basis पर होगी, जो प्रोजेक्ट की अवधि तक मान्य रहेगी।

entry.title: NID MP भर्ती 2025: वॉर्डन/केयरटेकर के पद पर भर्ती, ऑफलाइन आवेदन करें!
entry.content:
NID MP भर्ती 2025: वॉर्डन/केयरटेकर के पद पर भर्ती, ऑफलाइन आवेदन करें!

नेशनल इंस्टिट्यूट ऑफ डिज़ाइन, मध्य प्रदेश (NID MP) ने पुरुष उम्मीदवारों से वॉर्डन/केयरटेकर के पद पर कॉन्ट्रैक्ट बेसिस पर आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं। आवेदन ऑफलाइन भेजना होगा।

नेशनल इंस्टिट्यूट ऑफ डिज़ाइन, मध्य प्रदेश (NID MP) ने भर्ती सूचना (Notification No: NIDMP/1-70/(18) Rectt.- Admin/2025) जारी की है। संस्थान अपने भोपाल कैम्पस के लिए Warden/Caretaker पद पर कॉन्ट्रैक्ट आधार पर योग्य पुरुष उम्मीदवारों से आवेदन मांग रहा है। नियुक्ति शुरू में 1 साल के लिए होगी, जिसे आवश्यकता अनुसार 3 साल तक बढ़ाया जा सकता है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
नोटिफिकेशन जारी होने की तिथि16 October 2025
आवेदन प्राप्त होने की अंतिम तिथि20 November 2025

रिक्तियाँ और वेतन विवरण

इस भर्ती के तहत केवल एक पद के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित हैं:

पद का नामपदों की संख्याश्रेणीवेतन
Warden/Caretaker (Male)01OBC₹46,136/- प्रति माह
वेतन नोट: यह वेतन Pay Level 5 के प्रथम सेल + 58% DA के अनुसार है, जो समय-समय पर परिवर्तित हो सकता है।

आवेदन शुल्क

उम्मीदवारों को ₹500/- का नॉन-रिफंडेबल डिमांड ड्राफ्ट बनाना होगा। यह "National Institute of Design Madhya Pradesh" के नाम पर, Bhopal में पेएबल होना चाहिए।

Note: OBC-Ex-Servicemen उम्मीदवारों को शुल्क से छूट है, पर उन्हें प्रमाणपत्र संलग्न करना होगा।

पात्रता मानदंड

मानदंडविवरण
पद का नामWarden/Caretaker (Group C)
आयु सीमा30 वर्ष से अधिक नहीं (as on 20.11.2025)। OBC को 3 वर्ष की छूट।
शैक्षणिक योग्यता
  • अनिवार्य: किसी मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक (Bachelor’s Degree) और कंप्यूटर ज्ञान।
  • वांछनीय: किसी प्रतिष्ठित संस्थान/विश्वविद्यालय में हॉस्टल प्रबंधन का अनुभव।
लिंगकेवल पुरुष उम्मीदवार पात्र हैं।

जॉब प्रोफाइल: वॉर्डन/केयरटेकर की जिम्मेदारियाँ

  • लड़कों के हॉस्टल का पूर्ण प्रबंधन और अनुशासन बनाए रखना।
  • छात्रों की शिकायतों का समाधान और रिकॉर्ड बनाए रखना।
  • हॉस्टल की साफ-सफाई, सुरक्षा और सुविधाओं का ध्यान रखना।
  • बीमार छात्रों की देखभाल और मेडिकल सहायता उपलब्ध कराना।
  • नियमों का पालन सुनिश्चित करना और अनुशासन बनाए रखना।
  • CCTV निगरानी और रिपोर्ट तैयार करना।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक नोटिफिकेशन से आवेदन फॉर्म (Annexure-I) डाउनलोड करें।
  2. फॉर्म भरें, फोटो लगाएं और हस्ताक्षर करें।
  3. ₹500/- का डिमांड ड्राफ्ट संलग्न करें।
  4. सभी आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ (शैक्षणिक, अनुभव, जाति प्रमाणपत्र आदि) की स्वप्रमाणित प्रतियां लगाएं।
  5. फॉर्म को एक बंद लिफाफे में डालें और ऊपर "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF WARDEN/CARETAKER (MALE)" लिखें।
  6. आवेदन Registered Post/Speed Post से नीचे दिए पते पर भेजें ताकि यह 20 November 2025 तक पहुँच जाए।
पता:
The Administrative Officer,
Establishment Section,
National Institute of Design, Madhya Pradesh,
Village - Acharpura, Eint Khedi, Bhopal
Distt. - Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, Pin-462038

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकक्रिया
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
ऑफलाइन आवेदन फॉर्मयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: क्या महिलाएँ आवेदन कर सकती हैं?
नहीं, यह भर्ती केवल पुरुष उम्मीदवारों के लिए है।
Q2: आवेदन प्रक्रिया क्या है?
पूरी प्रक्रिया ऑफलाइन है। आवेदन Registered Post/Speed Post से भेजना होगा।
Q3: क्या OBC उम्मीदवारों को आयु में छूट मिलेगी?
हाँ, OBC (NCL) उम्मीदवारों को 3 वर्ष की छूट दी जाएगी।
Q4: क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?
नहीं, यह नौकरी पूरी तरह से कॉन्ट्रैक्ट बेसिस पर है, जिसे अधिकतम 3 साल तक बढ़ाया जा सकता है।

entry.title: NIT Durgapur Recruitment 2025: Apply for 100+ Non-Teaching Vacancies
entry.content:
NIT Durgapur Recruitment 2025: Apply Online for 105 Non-Teaching Vacancies

Join the National Institute of Technology (NIT), Durgapur - An Institute of National Importance under the Ministry of Education, Government of India

Post Name: Principal Scientific Officer, Superintending Engineer, Technical Assistant, Junior Engineer, Technician, Superintendent, Junior Assistant, Lab Attendant
Last Date: 21 Days from Publication in Employment News (Expected December 2025)
Total Posts: 105 Vacancies

The National Institute of Technology (NIT), Durgapur, established in 1960, stands as one of India's premier technical institutions and is recognized as an Institute of National Importance by an Act of Parliament. Located in the industrial hub of West Bengal, NIT Durgapur has consistently been a leader in technical education, research, and innovation. To further strengthen its administrative, technical, and support framework, the institute has officially released a massive recruitment notification for the year 2025. Under Advertisement Number NITD/Estt./01/05/Non-Teaching/2025, the institution is looking to recruit highly skilled and motivated Indian citizens for 105 permanent non-teaching vacancies across Group A, B, and C cadres.

Working at an NIT is a prestigious career path that offers the perfect blend of academic environment and the stability of a Central Government job. The 2025 recruitment cycle is one of the largest in recent years, covering a diverse range of roles from top-tier management positions like the Principal Scientific/Technical Officer and Superintending Engineer to essential field roles like Technical Assistants, Technicians, and Office Attendants. The application process is entirely digital, beginning on November 12, 2025. Candidates selected through this rigorous merit-based process will be entitled to pay scales governed by the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), along with extensive central government allowances and benefits. This recruitment serves not only as a job opportunity but as a chance to contribute to the nation’s technological advancement and student development.

The 2025 notification adheres strictly to the Recruitment Rules (2019) for NITs, ensuring a fair and standardized selection procedure. Whether you are an experienced professional with over 15 years in civil engineering or a fresh ITI/Diploma holder looking for your first government assignment, this drive provides a dedicated slot for various academic backgrounds. This 2000-word guide serves as your comprehensive manual, providing an in-depth analysis of vacancy distribution, post-wise eligibility criteria, salary breakdowns, the detailed three-tier selection process, and strategic preparation advice to help you secure your future at NIT Durgapur.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must strictly follow the schedule provided by NIT Durgapur. The recruitment portal is centralized, and candidates are encouraged to complete their registration early to avoid technical bottlenecks on the final day. The cut-off date for calculating age, qualification, and experience is the last date for online application submission.

Recruitment Feature Key Dates and Details
Official Notification Number NITD/Estt./01/05/Non-Teaching/2025
Online Registration Opens November 12, 2025 (10:00 AM)
Last Date to Submit Online Application 21 Days from Employment News Publication (Dec 2025)
Application Mode Online Only (No Offline Forms Accepted)
Nature of Employment Permanent Basis / Central Govt Status
Job Location Durgapur (West Bengal)

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Salary Matrix

The 105 vacancies are categorized into Group A, B, and C based on the level of authority, technical skill, and pay matrix level. The salary structure follows the latest 7th CPC recommendations, providing high basic pay and significant allowances such as HRA, DA, and TA.

1. Group-A Vacancies (Officers)

Post Designation Total Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC)
Principal Scientific/Technical Officer 02 Level-14 (Initial Basic: ₹1,44,200)
Superintending Engineer (Civil) 01 Level-13 (Initial Basic: ₹1,23,100)
Deputy Librarian / Senior SAS Officer 02 Level-12 (Initial Basic: ₹78,800)
Medical Officer / Assistant Registrar 03 Level-10 (Initial Basic: ₹56,100)
Technical Officer / Assistant Librarian 02 Level-10 (Initial Basic: ₹56,100)

2. Group-B and Group-C Vacancies (Technical and Support)

Post Designation Total Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC)
Technical Assistant / Junior Engineer 25 Level-6 (Initial Basic: ₹35,400)
Superintendent / Library Assistant 06 Level-6 (Initial Basic: ₹35,400)
Senior Technician 13 Level-4 (Initial Basic: ₹25,500)
Senior Assistant 07 Level-4 (Initial Basic: ₹25,500)
Technician 26 Level-3 (Initial Basic: ₹21,700)
Junior Assistant 14 Level-3 (Initial Basic: ₹21,700)
Lab Attendant / Office Attendant 17 Level-1 (Initial Basic: ₹18,000)
Organizational Benefits: In addition to the basic pay, selected candidates are entitled to central government allowances. The gross salary for a Level-14 officer can exceed ₹2,20,000 per month, while a Level-1 Attendant will start with a gross salary of approximately ₹32,000 to ₹35,000 including HRA and DA. All permanent employees are covered under the New Pension Scheme (NPS) and CGHS-linked medical facilities.

Application Fee Structure

  • Group-A Posts (Officers): ₹1500/- for UR/OBC/EWS candidates.
  • Group-B and Group-C Posts: ₹1000/- for UR/OBC/EWS candidates.
  • Exempted Categories: SC, ST, PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, and All Female Candidates are exempted from paying any application fee.

The application fee must be paid online during the registration process. Fees once paid are strictly non-refundable. Candidates applying for more than one post must submit separate applications and pay the fee for each post individually.

Eligibility Criteria and Qualifications

NIT Durgapur seeks a combination of academic excellence and relevant industrial or administrative experience. Qualifications must be from institutions recognized by AICTE, UGC, or the respective boards. Below is the detailed requirement for the core categories.

  • Superintending Engineer: First Class B.E./B.Tech in Civil Engineering. Experience: 15 years as an Engineer, with at least 5 years at the level of Executive Engineer in Level-12. Age Limit: Maximum 56 Years.
  • Technical Assistant / JE: First Class B.E./B.Tech/MCA in relevant discipline OR First Class Diploma in Engineering OR First Class B.Sc degree. Age Limit: Maximum 30 Years.
  • Superintendent: First Class Bachelor's Degree in any discipline OR Master’s Degree with at least 50% marks. Proficiency in computer applications is mandatory. Age Limit: Maximum 30 Years.
  • Technician: 10+2 with Science (60%) OR 10+2 with ITI Course (2 years) in relevant trade OR Diploma in Engineering (3 years). Age Limit: Maximum 27 Years.
  • Junior Assistant: 10+2 (Higher Secondary) from a recognized board. Technical Skill: Typing speed of at least 35 words per minute (WPM) and proficiency in MS Office. Age Limit: Maximum 27 Years.
  • Lab Attendant: 10+2 with Science stream from a recognized board. Age Limit: Maximum 27 Years.

Age Relaxation: Standard relaxations apply: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC-NCL, and 10-15 years for PwBD candidates. There is also a provision for age relaxation for existing departmental candidates as per NIT Council rules.

Selection Process and Roadmap

The selection process for NIT Durgapur is designed to be multi-layered to ensure that only the most technically competent candidates are shortlisted. The process varies according to the pay level of the post.

  1. Screening of Applications: The institute will first verify the data provided in the online form. If the number of applicants for a post is more than 10 times the vacancies, a preliminary written test will be conducted for screening.
  2. Written Examination (Tier-1): This is the primary competitive stage. It tests general aptitude, language skills, and core subject knowledge. The marks from the written test are used to prepare the shortlist for the next stage.
  3. Trade Test / Skill Test (Tier-2): Mandatory for posts like Junior Assistant (Typing Test), Technician (Trade-specific task), and Technical Assistant. This stage is usually qualifying in nature.
  4. Personal Interview: Conducted only for Group-A Officer posts. For Group B and C, selection is based purely on the written exam merit as per Government of India guidelines.
  5. Document Verification (DV): The final stage where all original certificates, experience letters, and category proofs are scrutinized.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Success in the NIT Durgapur exam requires a deep understanding of the two-part exam structure. The exam is typically conducted in OMR or CBT mode.

Part A: General Aptitude (40% Weightage):

  • General English: Grammar, comprehension, and vocabulary (10 Marks).
  • Logical Reasoning & Math: Profit and Loss, Time and Work, Series, and Analogies (15 Marks).
  • General Awareness: Current affairs, basics of the Indian Constitution, and static GK (15 Marks).

Part B: Technical / Domain Knowledge (60% Weightage): This is the most crucial section. For Junior Engineers, questions will be from Civil/Electrical engineering theory. For Technicians, it will focus on workshop calculation, safety, and specific trade tools. For Junior Assistants, it will cover office procedures, drafting, noting, and Computer Applications. The total duration is usually 120 minutes with a negative marking of 0.25 for incorrect answers.

Strategic Preparation Roadmap for NIT Durgapur

Securing a rank in a premier institute like NIT Durgapur requires a balanced preparation of technical and administrative knowledge. Follow this subject-wise roadmap for success.

Focus on Core Technical Proficiency: For Technical Assistants and Technicians, the technical section (60%) is the differentiator. Revisit your B.Tech/Diploma/ITI textbooks. For laboratory posts, focus on lab safety protocols, chemical storage, and instrument calibration. Practice with previous year papers of other NITs or the Staff Selection Commission (SSC JE/Phase Exams) as the difficulty level is similar.

Master Office Administration: For administrative roles like Superintendent or Junior Assistant, reading about Central Civil Services (CCS) Rules, leave rules, and Fundamental Rules (FRSR) is highly beneficial. Even though these are domain-specific, having a basic understanding of government office hierarchy and noting/drafting can help you score exceptionally well in the main paper.

General Awareness Balance: Since NIT Durgapur is under the Ministry of Education, stay updated on the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, Digital India initiatives, and new academic frameworks. For the math and reasoning section, solve 10th-standard level NCERT math problems daily to improve speed and accuracy.

Best Books for Preparation

Using standard study materials will keep your preparation focused and aligned with the NIT selection pattern. Below are the top recommendations from successful candidates.

Subject Area Recommended Book List
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude and Logical Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
General English Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi (Arihant Publications).
Technical Core (Elec/Mech/Civil) Made Easy Handbooks or R.K. Rajput Objective Question banks.
Administrative Knowledge Swamy’s Handbook for Central Government Staff (Brief Edition).
General Knowledge Lucent’s General Knowledge and Manorama Yearbook.
Computer Awareness Objective Computer Awareness by Arihant Expert.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step Guide

The application process is strictly online through the recruitment portal of NIT Durgapur. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure your application is valid.

  1. Visit the official website: https://nitdgp.ac.in/p/careers.
  2. Find the link "Non-Teaching Recruitment 2025" and click on the "Apply Online" portal link.
  3. New Registration: Register with your valid Email ID and Mobile Number. You will receive an activation link or OTP.
  4. Fill the Form: Login with your credentials. Enter personal details, academic scores, and detailed work experience. Ensure the spelling of your name matches your 10th-standard certificate.
  5. Upload Scanned Images: Upload your recent passport-size photograph (white background), signature, and all academic/category certificates in a Single PDF format (not exceeding 10MB).
  6. Payment: Pay the application fee using the online payment gateway (UPI/Net Banking/Card). Save the transaction receipt.
  7. Final Submission: Review the application in the preview window. Click "Submit" and take a printout of the final application form for the Document Verification stage.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready in digital format before starting the application to avoid session timeouts. All documents should be clear and legible.

  1. Identity and Age Proof: Aadhaar Card and 10th Standard Passing Certificate or Birth Certificate.
  2. Academic Proof: All semester marksheets and final Degree/Diploma/ITI certificates.
  3. Experience Certificates: Clear proof from current/previous employers on official letterheads specifying the dates and pay level.
  4. Category Certificate: Valid SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificate in the Central Government format.
  5. NOC (No Objection Certificate): Mandatory for candidates currently working in other Government, PSU, or Autonomous bodies (to be produced at the time of Skill Test/Interview).
  6. Disability/Sports Certificate: If applying under these special reservation quotas.

Important Links

Resource Description Official Direct Link
NIT Durgapur Official Website www.nitdgp.ac.in
Online Application Portal Apply Online Here
Official Notification PDF Download Notification

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply for more than one post in NIT Durgapur?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you are eligible for them. However, you must fill out a separate application form and pay the separate application fee for each post category. Note that exams for different posts may be scheduled simultaneously.

Q2: Is there any application fee for female candidates?

No. According to the official notification, all female candidates regardless of their category are completely exempted from paying any application fee for this recruitment cycle.

Q3: What is the last date to apply for NIT Durgapur 2025 recruitment?

The application portal will be open for 21 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in the Employment News (Rozgar Samachar). It is expected to conclude by mid-December 2025. Check the portal for the exact closing time.

Q4: Is the Technical Assistant post open for B.Sc graduates?

Yes. Candidates with a First Class Bachelor’s Degree in Science (B.Sc) in a relevant subject from a recognized university are eligible to apply for the Technical Assistant post, as per the 2019 NIT Recruitment Rules.

Q5: Is there an interview for the Technician or Junior Assistant post?

No. As per the latest Government of India guidelines for Group C and Group B non-gazetted posts, there is no personal interview. Selection will be based 100% on the merit of the written examination, subject to qualifying the skill test.

Q6: Can final-year students apply?

No. Candidates must possess the completed degree/diploma and the required experience certificates as of the closing date of the application. Result-awaited candidates are generally not eligible unless specified otherwise.

entry.title: नीति आयोग भर्ती 2025: दिल्ली में सरकारी नौकरी का सुनहरा मौका, सैलरी ₹70,000!
entry.content:
नीति आयोग भर्ती 2025: दिल्ली में सरकारी नौकरी का सुनहरा मौका, सैलरी ₹70,000!

भारत सरकार के think-tank नीति आयोग ने Young Professionals और Consultants के पदों पर भर्ती के लिए दिशा-निर्देश जारी किए हैं। यहाँ जानिए पात्रता, सैलरी और आवेदन प्रक्रिया की पूरी जानकारी।

दिल्ली में सरकारी नौकरी की तलाश कर रहे युवाओं के लिए शानदार खबर! भारत सरकार का शीर्ष सार्वजनिक नीति संस्थान नीति आयोग (NITI Aayog) ने Young Professional, Consultant और Senior Consultant के पदों पर भर्ती की घोषणा की है।

यह NITI Aayog Young Professional Vacancy 2025 अलग तरह की भर्ती है क्योंकि इसमें आवेदन “Rolling Basis” पर होते हैं — यानी आप सालभर किसी भी समय आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

पदों का विवरण, सैलरी और आयु सीमा

नीति आयोग में विभिन्न अनुभव स्तरों के अनुसार चार श्रेणियों में नियुक्ति की जाती है:

पद का नामअनुभव (वर्षों में)अधिकतम आयुवेतन (प्रति माह)
Young Professional1 वर्ष32 वर्ष₹70,000
Consultant Grade-13 – 8 वर्ष45 वर्ष₹80,000 – ₹1,45,000
Consultant Grade-28 – 15 वर्ष50 वर्ष₹1,45,000 – ₹2,65,000
Senior Consultant15 वर्ष से अधिक62 वर्ष₹2,65,000 – ₹3,30,000
नोट: Ph.D. धारक उम्मीदवारों को 3 वर्ष का अनुभव माना जाएगा, भले ही उन्होंने काम न किया हो।

शैक्षणिक योग्यता

  • आवश्यक योग्यता:
    • संबंधित विषय में Master’s Degree या
    • BE/B.Tech या
    • 2 वर्ष का PG Diploma in Management या
    • MBBS / LLB / CA / ICWA या
    • 10+2 के बाद 4 वर्ष या उससे अधिक की कोई भी Professional Degree।
  • वांछनीय योग्यता: M.Phil, M.Tech, MS, LLM, Ph.D. या शोध कार्य का अनुभव रखने वाले उम्मीदवारों को प्राथमिकता दी जाएगी।

नियुक्ति की अवधि

प्रारंभिक नियुक्ति 2 वर्ष के लिए होगी, जिसे प्रदर्शन के आधार पर हर साल बढ़ाया जा सकता है। अधिकतम अवधि 5 वर्ष तक होगी। 65 वर्ष की आयु के बाद कोई विस्तार नहीं मिलेगा।

चयन प्रक्रिया

नीति आयोग की चयन प्रक्रिया पारंपरिक सरकारी भर्ती से अलग है:

  1. Resource Pool Portal: नीति आयोग एक पोर्टल पर उम्मीदवारों का प्रोफाइल डेटाबेस रखता है, जहाँ आप कभी भी आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
  2. Shortlisting: जब भी किसी विभाग को विशेषज्ञों की आवश्यकता होती है, तो इस पोर्टल से उम्मीदवारों की प्रोफाइल शॉर्टलिस्ट की जाती है।
  3. Screening Committee: चयन समिति प्रोफाइल की समीक्षा करके 3 योग्य उम्मीदवारों की सूची तैयार करती है।
  4. Consultancy Evaluation Committee: यह समिति साक्षात्कार या अन्य मूल्यांकन के माध्यम से अंतिम चयन करती है।

यह राष्ट्रीय स्तर की भर्ती है — किसी राज्य विशेष जैसे UP या Bihar तक सीमित नहीं है।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. नीति आयोग की वेबसाइट www.niti.gov.in पर जाएं।
  2. "Work with NITI" सेक्शन में जाकर आवेदन से जुड़ी जानकारी देखें।
  3. Resource Pool Portal पर अपनी प्रोफाइल बनाएं और आवश्यक डिटेल्स भरें।
  4. प्रोफाइल तैयार होने के बाद, आवश्यकता होने पर नीति आयोग आपकी प्रोफाइल को शॉर्टलिस्ट करेगा।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
नीति आयोग की आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
Work with NITI (Career Page)यहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: नीति आयोग में भर्ती कब होती है?
नीति आयोग में भर्ती Rolling Basis पर होती है — यानी पूरे साल आवेदन स्वीकार किए जाते हैं।
Q2: Young Professional पद के लिए न्यूनतम अनुभव कितना चाहिए?
कम से कम 1 वर्ष का अनुभव आवश्यक है। Ph.D. उम्मीदवारों को अनुभव में छूट मिलती है।
Q3: क्या यह स्थायी सरकारी नौकरी है?
नहीं, यह एक Contract Basis पर Individual Consultancy है, जो 2 वर्ष तक मान्य होती है और अधिकतम 5 वर्ष तक बढ़ाई जा सकती है।

entry.title: BSNL Recruitment 2026 for 120 Senior Executive Trainee Posts
entry.content:
BSNL Recruitment 2026 for 120 Senior Executive Trainee Posts

Advertisement No. BSNLCO-11/12(11)/1/2025-RECTT-CO-Part (1): Bharat Sanchar Nigam Ltd. (BSNL) has invited online applications for the direct recruitment of 120 Senior Executive Trainees (SET) in the Telecom and Finance streams. This is a significant opportunity for Engineering graduates, CAs, and CMAs to join the E-3 pay scale in a major public sector undertaking. The online application process is scheduled from February 05, 2026, to March 07, 2026.

Post Name: Senior Executive Trainee (Telecom & Finance)
Last Date: 07 March 2026
Total Posts: 120

Bharat Sanchar Nigam Ltd. (BSNL), a Government of India Enterprise, is seeking to recruit dynamic and qualified professionals for the key position of Senior Executive Trainee (SET). This direct recruitment drive aims to fill 95 vacancies in the Telecom Operations stream and 25 vacancies in the Finance stream. The SET role is a crucial entry-point into the executive cadre of BSNL, offering a promising career path with opportunities for growth up to higher management levels.

The selection for these prestigious posts will be through a single-stage "Computer based-Multiple Choice-Objective Type Test". This test is designed to assess the candidates' aptitude and their core technical knowledge in either Telecom or Finance. The selected candidates will undergo a comprehensive 52-week training program and will be on probation for two years. The application process is entirely online through the official BSNL external exam website.

BSNL SET Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. All applications must be submitted online through the BSNL exam portal.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Bharat Sanchar Nigam Limited (BSNL)
Post Name Senior Executive Trainee (SET)
Streams Telecom Operations & Finance
Total Vacancies 120
Application Start Date 05 February 2026 (10:00 Hrs)
Application Last Date 07 March 2026 (10:00 Hrs)
Examination Date (Tentative) 29 March 2026 (10:00 Hrs)
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent PSU Job (E-3 Scale)
Job Location Anywhere in India
Official Website www.bsnl.co.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive is distributed across two professional streams, both in the E-3 IDA pay scale.

Stream Total Vacancies Category-wise Breakup (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR) Pay Scale (pre-revised)
Senior Executive Trainee (Telecom) 95 SC-14, ST-07, OBC-25, EWS-09, UR-40 IDA Pay Scale [E-3] of ₹24,900 - ₹50,500/-
Senior Executive Trainee (Finance) 25 SC-04, ST-02, OBC-06, EWS-02, UR-11
Total 120

Application Fee

  • For OC, OBC, and EWS candidates: ₹2500/-
  • For SC, ST, and PwBD candidates: ₹1250/-

The fee must be paid online through Internet Banking, Credit Card, or Debit Card only. The fee is non-refundable.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet the specific age and educational qualifications as of the closing date of the application.

Stream Age Limit Essential Educational Qualifications
SET (Telecom Operations) Min: 21 years
Max: 30 years
(Relaxation: 5 yrs for SC/ST, 3 yrs for OBC, 10-15 yrs for PwBD)		 Bachelor of Engineering/Bachelor of Technology (B.E./B.Tech) degree or equivalent with a minimum of 60% marks in:
  • Electronics and Telecommunication
  • Electronics
  • Computer Science
  • Information Technology
  • Electrical
  • Instrumentation
SET (Finance) Chartered Accountant (CA) OR Cost and Management Accountancy (CMA).

Selection Process

The selection process for the SET post is straightforward and based on a single-stage examination.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT): All eligible applicants will appear for a "Computer based-Multiple Choice-Objective Type Test". The test will have two parts: a common aptitude section and a core technical section.
  2. Merit List: A final merit list will be prepared based on the total marks obtained in both Part-I and Part-II of the CBT, provided the candidate secures the minimum qualifying marks in each part and in aggregate.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates shortlisted based on the merit list will be called for Document Verification.
  4. Medical Examination: Appointment is subject to passing the company's Medical Examination as per the prescribed standards.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The examination will be a single paper with two parts, conducted in one shift for a total duration of 180 minutes.

Part Section No. of Questions Max. Marks Total Duration
Part-I (Common) Aptitude Test 40 40 180 Minutes
Part-II (Core) Technical Test (Telecom or Finance) 160 160
Total 200 200
  • Marking Scheme: 1 mark for each correct answer.
  • Negative Marking: 0.25 marks will be deducted for each wrong answer.
  • Minimum Qualifying Marks: 40% in each part and 50% overall for General candidates. For reserved categories, 35% in each part and 45% overall.
  • Syllabus:
    • Part-I (Aptitude): Quantitative Ability & Data Sufficiency, Reasoning (Analytical, Logical, Critical), and Verbal Ability & Reading Comprehension.
    • Part-II (Telecom): Covers topics like Basic Telecommunications, Wireless Technologies, Wireline Technologies, Telecom Infrastructure, IP/Data Networks, Emerging Technologies, and Project Management.
    • Part-II (Finance): Covers topics like Advanced Accounting, Taxation Rules, Cost Accounting, Financial Statement Analysis, Corporate Law, and Audit Assurance.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for the post of Senior Executive Trainee.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A focused preparation strategy is essential, given the vast technical syllabus.

  • Master the Core Subject: The technical paper (Part-II) has a weightage of 160 marks out of 200. This should be your primary focus. Thoroughly revise all subjects from your engineering degree or CA/CMA curriculum as per the detailed syllabus in Annexure-B.
  • Aptitude Section: Do not neglect Part-I. Practice aptitude questions regularly to build speed and accuracy. This section can be a score booster.
  • Solve Mock Tests: Attempt online mock tests for similar PSU executive trainee exams (e.g., GATE for technical concepts, other PSU management trainee papers) to get used to the pattern, time constraints, and negative marking.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard textbooks and competitive exam guides.

  • Telecom Stream: Standard engineering textbooks for GATE (ECE, CSE, EE) preparation are highly recommended. "Communication Systems" by Simon Haykin and books on Data Communications by Forouzan are good resources.
  • Finance Stream: Study material from ICAI (for CA) and ICMAI (for CMA) is the most comprehensive source.
  • Aptitude Section: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal and "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the BSNL external exam portal.

  1. Visit the BSNL external exam website: www.externalexam.bsnl.co.in.
  2. Find the link for the "Recruitment of Senior Executive Trainee (DR) in BSNL".
  3. Read the instructions carefully and proceed with the online registration.
  4. Fill out the application form with your personal and educational details.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your recent photograph, signature, and other required documents as specified.
  6. Pay the examination fee through the online payment gateway.
  7. Review the application form before final submission.
  8. Take a printout of the computer-generated registration form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Scanning)

Candidates should keep the following scanned documents ready before starting the application process:

  1. Recent passport-size colour photograph (20kb-50kb, jpg/jpeg).
  2. Scanned copy of your signature (10kb-20kb, jpg/jpeg).
  3. Class 10th Certificate for verification of Date of Birth.
  4. Degree Certificate of Essential Qualification (B.E./B.Tech/CA/CMA).
  5. Consolidated Marksheet/Transcript for all semesters/years of the essential qualification.
  6. Valid Photo ID proof (Aadhaar, Passport, etc.).
  7. Caste/EWS/PwBD certificate, if applicable.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Available on BSNL Website
Apply Online Click Here (Link to be active from 05.02.2026)
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the age limit for the BSNL SET post?

The maximum age limit for General category candidates is 30 years as on the closing date of the application. Age relaxation is applicable for reserved categories.

Q2: Is there a service bond for this post?

Yes, selected candidates will have to execute a bond of ₹9 Lakhs to serve BSNL for a minimum period of five years.

Q3: I am a final year engineering student. Can I apply?

The notification states that candidates must possess the required educational qualification as on the closing date of receipt of the application. Therefore, final year students awaiting results are not eligible.

Q4: What is the negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there is negative marking. For each incorrect answer, 0.25 marks will be deducted.

entry.title: EdCIL Recruitment 2025: Executive Director पद पर शानदार सरकारी नौकरी!
entry.content:
EdCIL Recruitment 2025: Executive Director पद पर शानदार सरकारी नौकरी!

EdCIL (India) Limited ने Executive Director (Corporate Planning) पद के लिए भर्ती का नोटिफिकेशन जारी किया है। आवेदन 15 अक्टूबर से 4 नवंबर 2025 तक स्वीकार किए जाएंगे।

EdCIL (India) Limited, जो भारत सरकार के अधीन एक “Mini Ratna Category-I” CPSE है, ने Executive Director (Corporate Planning) पद के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं। यह पद संगठन की रणनीतिक योजना और व्यवसाय विकास से जुड़ा है। यह अवसर केवल OBC Category के उम्मीदवारों के लिए है।

पद एवं वेतन विवरण

विवरणजानकारी
पद का नामExecutive Director (Corporate Planning) – E7 Level
वेतनमान₹1,00,000 – ₹2,60,000 (IDA Scale)
कुल पद1 (केवल OBC)
कार्यकाल5 वर्ष (प्रदर्शन के आधार पर 2 वर्ष तक बढ़ाया जा सकता है)
नोट: चयनित उम्मीदवार को Basic Pay के साथ IDA, HRA, PRP, PF और मेडिकल सुविधाएँ भी मिलेंगी।

पात्रता मानदंड (Eligibility Criteria)

अनिवार्य योग्यता

मानदंडआवश्यकता
शैक्षणिक योग्यताPost Graduate in Engineering & Technology या Management (Full-time 2 वर्ष का प्रोग्राम)
कुल अनुभवकम से कम 20 वर्ष का अनुभव शिक्षा, परामर्श, या प्रोजेक्ट मैनेजमेंट क्षेत्र में।
PSU अनुभवकम से कम 2 वर्ष किसी PSU/CPSE में ₹90,000–₹2,40,000 वेतनमान पर या CTC ₹26 लाख/वर्ष से अधिक।
अधिकतम आयु50 वर्ष (04.11.2025 तक)

वांछनीय योग्यता

  • PhD डिग्री (प्रासंगिक क्षेत्र में)
  • Consulting, IT/ICT, Education Technology या PSU सेक्टर में वरिष्ठ स्तर का अनुभव।

चयन प्रक्रिया (Selection Process)

  1. Application Screening – योग्य उम्मीदवारों की शॉर्टलिस्टिंग।
  2. Personal Interview – Leadership और Vision पर आधारित मूल्यांकन।
  3. Document Verification – अंतिम चरण।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया (How to Apply)

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://www.edcilindia.co.in/ECareers पर जाएँ।
  2. “Executive Director (Corporate Planning)” लिंक पर क्लिक कर ऑनलाइन आवेदन करें।
  3. फॉर्म को 04 नवंबर 2025 (शाम 5:00 बजे) तक सबमिट करें।
नोट: सरकारी कर्मचारियों को अपने आवेदन की हार्ड कॉपी Proper Channel से भेजनी होगी — Manager (HR & Admin), EdCIL House, Plot No.18A, Sector-16A, Noida – 201301

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक (Important Links)

लिंक शीर्षकलिंक
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
ईमेल सहायताhrhelpdesk@edcil.co.in

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: क्या यह पद केवल OBC उम्मीदवारों के लिए है?

हाँ, यह पद केवल OBC Category के उम्मीदवारों के लिए आरक्षित है।

Q2: क्या आवेदन शुल्क है?

नहीं, इस भर्ती में किसी प्रकार का आवेदन शुल्क नहीं लिया जाएगा।

Q3: क्या लिखित परीक्षा होगी?

नहीं, चयन केवल Personal Interview के आधार पर होगा।

Q4: न्यूनतम अनुभव कितना होना चाहिए?

कम से कम 20 वर्ष का Post-Qualification अनुभव आवश्यक है।

entry.title: JKSSB SI Recruitment 2025: Notification for 83 Sub-Inspector Posts in Telecommunication Wing
entry.content:
JKSSB SI Recruitment 2025: Notification for 83 Sub-Inspector Posts in Telecommunication Wing

A golden opportunity for engineering graduates to join the J&K Home Department. Online applications open from December 15, 2025, to January 13, 2026.

Post Name: Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication)
Last Date: 13/01/2026
Total Posts: 83

The Jammu and Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) has released a major recruitment notification for the prestigious posts of Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication) in the Home Department of Jammu and Kashmir. Under Advertisement No. 11 of 2025, a total of 83 vacancies have been announced, presenting a golden opportunity for engineering graduates to secure a stable and respected government job in the Union Territory. This recruitment drive is aimed at qualified candidates who are domiciles of Jammu and Kashmir and wish to build a career in the dynamic field of telecommunications and technology within the security apparatus.

This recruitment is for UT Cadre posts, meaning that selected candidates will be liable to serve in any part of Jammu and Kashmir. JKSSB, known for its fair and transparent selection processes, will conduct this recruitment. The role of a Sub-Inspector in the Telecommunication wing is crucial as it involves maintaining and managing critical communication networks for law enforcement agencies, thereby directly contributing to the security and communication infrastructure of the region. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online from December 15, 2025. This comprehensive article explains the application process, eligibility criteria, selection procedure, and other important details to help candidates navigate the application process without difficulty.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other key information to ensure a smooth application process.

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameJammu and Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB)
Advertisement Number11 of 2025
Post NameSub-Inspector (Telecommunication)
Total Posts83
Job LocationJammu and Kashmir (UT Cadre)
Application ModeOnline
Application DatesDecember 15, 2025, to January 13, 2026

Vacancy Details and Category-wise Breakdown

Under this notification issued by JKSSB, a total of 83 posts of Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication) will be filled. These vacancies are classified for various categories as per the Jammu and Kashmir reservation rules. This classification ensures that all sections of society get an equal opportunity. Candidates must apply under their respective categories and will be required to produce a valid category certificate. The detailed category-wise breakdown of vacancies is given in the table below:

Sr. No. Category Number of Posts
1 Open Merit (OM) 34
2 Scheduled Caste (SC) 7
3 Scheduled Tribe (ST-1) 8
4 Scheduled Tribe (ST-2) 8
5 Other Backward Classes (OBC) 7
6 Residents of Area Adjoining Actual Line of Control/International Border (ALC/IB) 3
7 Residents of Backward Areas (RBA) 8
8 Economically Weaker Section (EWS) 8
Total Posts 83

Salary and Pay Scale

Candidates selected for the post of Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication) by JKSSB will be offered an attractive salary package. This post falls under Pay Level-6C, which ensures a competitive and respectable salary. In addition to the pay scale, selected candidates will also receive various allowances such as Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and other applicable benefits as per the rules of the Government of Jammu and Kashmir. This not only provides financial stability but also paves the way for excellent career progression.

Post Name Pay Level Pay Scale
Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication) Level-6C ₹ 35,700 - ₹ 1,13,100

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Candidates applying for this post must possess a specific technical qualification. JKSSB has ensured that only candidates with relevant expertise apply for this critical role. Candidates must have acquired the prescribed qualification by the last date of application, i.e., January 13, 2026.

Educational Qualification

Post Name Minimum Educational Qualification
Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication) The candidate must possess a 4-year Bachelor of Engineering (B.E.) / Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech.) degree in any of the following streams from a recognized University:
  1. Computer Science Engineering
  2. Electronics and Communication Engineering
  3. Telecommunication Engineering
  4. Information Technology
  5. Mechatronics
  6. Data Science
  7. Electrical and Electronics Engineering
  8. Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning
  9. Cyber Security

Age Limit (as on 01.01.2025)

JKSSB has set the age limit for this recruitment with the reference date as January 1, 2025. There is also a provision for relaxation in the upper age limit for candidates from various categories. The detailed age limit and date of birth cut-offs are given in the table below:

Category Minimum Age Maximum Age Not born before Not born after
Open Merit (OM) 18 years 28 years 01.01.1997 01.01.2007
SC, ST-1 & ST-2, RBA, ALC/IB, EWS, OBC 18 years 28 years 01.01.1997 01.01.2007
In-Service Police Personnel 18 years 30 years 01.01.1995 01.01.2007
Ex-Servicemen The period of service rendered in the armed forces shall be deducted from the actual age. The resultant age should not exceed the maximum age limit by more than 3 years.

Selection Process

The selection process for the post of Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication) is a multi-stage procedure designed to assess the knowledge, physical fitness, and overall suitability of the candidates. Candidates must qualify in each stage for final selection. The main stages of the selection process are as follows:

  1. Written Examination: This is the first and most important stage of the selection process. The exam will consist of objective type, multiple-choice questions (MCQs). The question paper will be in English only. There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer. Candidates holding National Cadet Corps (NCC) certificates will be given bonus marks.
  2. Physical Standard Test (PST): Based on the merit of the written examination, candidates will be shortlisted for the PST in a ratio of 1:6 for each category. This test is qualifying in nature. The physical standards are:
    GenderStandardMeasurement
    MaleMinimum Height5' 6"
    Chest (un-expanded/expanded)32" / 33 ½"
    FemaleMinimum Height5' 2"
    Note: A relaxation of 2 inches in minimum height will be given to candidates belonging to the Gorkha community and Bott Tribe.
  3. Physical Endurance Test (PET): Candidates who qualify the PST will have to appear for the PET. This test is also qualifying in nature.
    GenderEventStandard
    MaleLong Race1600 meters in 6 ½ minutes
    Push-ups20 (one cycle of up and down)
    FemaleLong Race1000 meters in 6 ½ minutes
    Shot Put (4 Kg)14 ½ feet in three attempts
  4. Document Verification (DV): Candidates successful in all stages will be called for the verification of their original documents.

How to Prepare for the JKSSB SI Exam

Success in the JKSSB Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication) exam requires a well-planned and disciplined preparation strategy. It is a competitive exam, so candidates must work hard for both the written and physical tests. Below are some important tips to help you in your preparation:

  1. Understand the Syllabus and Exam Pattern: The first step is to thoroughly understand the syllabus and exam pattern. JKSSB will release the detailed syllabus on its website. Keep the negative marking in mind and focus on accuracy.
  2. Create an Effective Study Plan: Make a daily or weekly timetable to cover all subjects. Allocate more time for difficult subjects and revise the easier ones regularly. Include time for physical fitness in your study plan.
  3. Select the Right Study Material: Study standard textbooks related to your engineering stream (e.g., Computer Science, Electronics). Use high-quality books and online resources for General Knowledge, Current Affairs, and Reasoning.
  4. Solve Mock Tests and Previous Years' Papers: Taking mock tests gives you an experience of the exam environment and improves time management. Solving previous years' papers helps you understand the types of questions and their difficulty level. Analyze your performance and work on your weak areas.
  5. Preparation for Physical Tests:
    • Practice Running: Start practicing running regularly. Begin with shorter distances and gradually increase your stamina and speed. Aim to complete the 1600/1000 meters race within the prescribed time.
    • Push-ups and Shot Put: Men should practice push-ups and women should practice shot put regularly. It is important to learn the correct technique to perform without any injury.
    • Healthy Lifestyle: Eat a balanced and nutritious diet. Avoid junk food. Drink enough water to keep your body hydrated.
    • Adequate Sleep: 7-8 hours of sleep daily is essential for physical and mental health.

How to Apply Online

The application process for the JKSSB Sub-Inspector recruitment is entirely online. Candidates must fill out the application form on the official JKSSB website. Follow the steps below to apply:

  1. Visit the Official Website: First, go to the official website of the Jammu and Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) at www.jkssb.nic.in.
  2. Registration: If you are a new user, click on the 'Candidate Registration' link. Complete your registration by entering your name, email ID, mobile number, and other required details. You will receive a User ID and password.
  3. Login: Log in to the website using your User ID and password.
  4. Select Advertisement: On the dashboard, go to the 'Apply for Various Posts' section and click on the "Advertisement Notification No. 11 of 2025 for Sub-Inspector (Telecommunication)" link.
  5. Fill the Application Form: Carefully fill in your personal details, educational qualifications, address, and other information in the application form. Ensure all information is correct, as it cannot be edited after final submission.
  6. Upload Documents: Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph, signature, and other required documents (like category certificate) in the prescribed format and size.
  7. Pay the Application Fee: Pay the application fee as per your category through the online payment gateway (Net Banking/Credit Card/Debit Card).
  8. Final Submission: After fee payment, review your application form and click on the 'Final Submit' button.
  9. Take a Printout: It is mandatory to take a printout of your filled application form and fee receipt for future reference.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here (Link will be active when available)
Apply Online Click Here (Active from 15.12.2025)
Official Website https://jkssb.nic.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: When will the application for JKSSB SI (Telecommunication) recruitment begin?

The online application process for this recruitment will start on December 15, 2025.

Question 2: What is the last date to apply?

The last date to apply online is January 13, 2026.

Question 3: Can candidates from outside Jammu and Kashmir apply for this recruitment?

No, as per the notification, it is mandatory for the candidate to be a Domicile of the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

Question 4: Is there negative marking in the written examination?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the written test.

Question 5: What is the minimum educational qualification for this post?

The candidate must possess a 4-year B.E./B.Tech. degree in Computer Science, Electronics, IT, or other specified related streams.

Question 6: What are the stages of the selection process?

The selection process mainly includes three stages: a Written Examination, a Physical Standard Test (PST), and a Physical Endurance Test (PET), followed by Document Verification.

entry.title: KVS NVS Recruitment 2025: Joint Notification for 14,967 Vacancies Out
entry.content:
KVS NVS Recruitment 2025: Joint Notification for 14,967 Vacancies Out

Mega Recruitment Drive for PGT, TGT, PRT, and Non-Teaching Staff under Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan and Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti

Post Name: Principal, PGT, TGT, PRT, Librarian, Stenographer, Assistant Section Officer, MTS
Last Date: December 4, 2025
Total Posts: 14,967 Vacancies

The Kendriya Vidyalaya Sangathan (KVS) and Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti (NVS), two of India’s most prestigious autonomous educational bodies under the Ministry of Education, have jointly announced a massive recruitment drive for the year 2025. This historic notification, identified as Joint Recruitment CEN 01/2025, aims to fill over 14,000 vacancies across a wide array of teaching and non-teaching roles. The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has been entrusted with the responsibility of conducting this joint recruitment examination, ensuring a transparent and centralized selection process for schools located in every corner of India, as well as several international locations.

For millions of teaching aspirants, KVS and NVS represent the gold standard of school education in the country. While KVS primarily caters to the children of transferable central government employees in urban areas, NVS focuses on providing high-quality residential education to talented students in rural India. This joint recruitment provides a unique "single-window" opportunity for candidates to apply for positions in both organizations. The vacancies range from high-level administrative roles like Assistant Commissioner and Principal to specialized teaching roles such as PGT, TGT, and PRT, along with essential support staff like Junior Secretariat Assistants and Lab Attendants. The application window is scheduled to open on November 14, 2025, and will remain active until December 4, 2025.

Working in KVS or NVS is more than just a teaching job; it is an opportunity to be part of an elite educational system that fosters national integration. These organizations offer salaries based on the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) levels, ensuring financial security and a plethora of central government benefits. Whether you are an experienced educator or a fresh graduate with a B.Ed. and CTET qualification, this 2025 recruitment cycle is your chance to build a permanent career in the central government sector. This exhaustive 2000-word article serves as your definitive guide, providing a deep dive into eligibility criteria, category-wise vacancy distribution, the newly revised two-tier exam pattern, and professional strategies to help you crack the CBSE-conducted selection process.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must be highly attentive to the timeline of this joint recruitment. Since the process is centralized through CBSE, the application portal will handle a massive volume of data. It is recommended to register early to avoid technical bottlenecks. Candidates have the option to apply for both organizations (KVS and NVS) for the same post, provided they meet the specific organizational requirements, often through a single fee payment system for integrated preferences.

Event Description Crucial Dates and Information
Official Notification Number CEN No. 01/2025
Online Registration Opens November 14, 2025 (10:00 AM)
Last Date for Application Submission December 4, 2025 (11:50 PM)
Eligibility Cut-off Date December 4, 2025
Exam Conducting Body Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE)
Official Web Portals kvsangathan.nic.in / navodaya.gov.in

Detailed Vacancy Analysis and Category Breakdown

The total 14,967 vacancies are strategically divided between KVS and NVS. While KVS has a higher concentration of Primary Teacher (PRT) roles, NVS has more vacancies for residential roles and TGT positions in regional languages. Below is a comprehensive breakdown of the core teaching and administrative vacancies.

Post Category KVS Vacancies NVS Vacancies Combined Total
Principal & Assistant Commissioner 200 102 302
Post Graduate Teacher (PGT) 1465 1531 2996
Trained Graduate Teacher (TGT) 2794 3421 6215
Primary Teacher (PRT) 3365 - 3365
Non-Teaching (ASO, JSA, Steno) 1200 889 2089
Grand Total Vacancies 9024 5943 14,967
Special Note on NVS Allowance: Candidates joining the Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti (NVS) are eligible for a 10% Special Allowance over and above their basic pay due to the residential nature of the duties, which involve supervising students beyond school hours. This allowance is not applicable for Assistant Commissioner roles.

Salary Structure and Central Government Benefits

The pay scales for KVS and NVS are among the highest in the Indian school education sector. They strictly follow the 7th CPC levels, ensuring high basic pay and substantial allowances for teachers and administrators.

Post Designation Pay Level Basic Pay Matrix
Principal / AC Level 12 ₹78,800 - ₹2,09,200
PGT (All Subjects) Level 8 ₹47,600 - ₹1,51,100
TGT / Librarian Level 7 ₹44,900 - ₹1,42,400
PRT / ASO Level 6 ₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400
Senior Sec. Assistant Level 4 ₹25,500 - ₹81,100
Junior Sec. Assistant Level 2 ₹19,900 - ₹63,200

Allowances: Employees are entitled to Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA) depending on the city category (X, Y, Z), and Transport Allowance (TA). Furthermore, KVS and NVS staff receive comprehensive medical coverage for themselves and their dependents under the CGHS or CS (MA) rules. Educational allowances for children and LTC (Leave Travel Concession) are also part of the lucrative package.

Application Fee Structure

  • Group A (AC, Principal, VP): ₹2800 (Integrated Fee).
  • PGT, TGT, PRT, Librarian: ₹2000 per post category.
  • Non-Teaching Support Staff (JSA, SSA): ₹1700.
  • Exempted Categories: Candidates from SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen categories are exempted from paying the examination fee, although a nominal processing fee of ₹500 is applicable.

Fees must be paid through the online portal using Net Banking, Debit Card, or Credit Card. Once the fee is paid, it is non-refundable, so candidates must ensure they meet the eligibility criteria before proceeding with the payment.

Eligibility Criteria and Qualifications

Eligibility for KVS and NVS teaching roles is divided into academic qualifications, professional certification (B.Ed.), and central eligibility tests (CTET). The requirements vary significantly across post levels.

Post Category Academic and Professional Requirements
Principal Master’s degree with at least 50% marks and B.Ed. Experience: Relevant administrative and teaching experience in senior secondary schools as specified in the recruitment manual.
PGT (Post Graduate Teacher) Master’s degree in the concerned subject with 50% marks and B.Ed. Proficiency in teaching in Hindi and English medium. Note: B.Ed. is not required for PGT Computer Science.
TGT (Trained Graduate Teacher) Bachelor’s degree in the concerned subject with 50% marks and B.Ed. Mandatory: Must have passed CTET Paper II conducted by CBSE.
PRT (Primary Teacher) 12th Standard with 50% marks and 2-year Diploma in Elementary Education (D.El.Ed/JBT) or B.El.Ed. Mandatory: Must have passed CTET Paper I conducted by CBSE.
Librarian Degree in Library Science or Graduate with a one-year Diploma in Library Science. Knowledge of Computer Applications is essential.

Age Relaxation: Upper age relaxation is provided to various categories: SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), Women (10 years for teaching posts), and PwBD (10-15 years). The age is calculated as of December 4, 2025.

Selection Process

The 2025 selection process follows a standardized multi-tier format to ensure that only the most qualified and pedagogically sound candidates enter the central education system.

  1. Tier-1 (Preliminary Examination): An objective-type test focusing on reasoning, aptitude, and language proficiency. This serves as a screening test to filter candidates for the main round.
  2. Tier-2 (Main Examination): A more specialized exam that tests deep subject knowledge and pedagogical methodology. This is the primary merit-deciding stage.
  3. Interviews: Conducted for Group A and teaching posts (PGT, TGT, PRT). The interview evaluates communication skills, psychological aptitude, and actual classroom teaching skills.
  4. Skill Test: Mandatory for non-teaching roles like Stenographer (Shorthand test) and JSA (Typing test). This is qualifying in nature.
  5. Document Verification: The final stage where original academic and caste certificates are scrutinized before the issuance of appointment letters.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

The exam pattern for KVS/NVS 2025 has been revised to align with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020. The emphasis has shifted from rote learning to conceptual clarity and teaching aptitude.

Tier-1 Exam Pattern (100 Marks, 120 Minutes):

  • General Reasoning & Numeric Ability: 40 Questions (120 Marks). Focus on series, analogies, syllogism, and basic arithmetic.
  • Basic Computer Literacy: 20 Questions (60 Marks). Hardware, Software, MS Office, and Internet basics.
  • Language Proficiency (English/Hindi/Regional): 20 Questions (60 Marks). Grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension.
  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs: 20 Questions (60 Marks). Focus on Indian History, Polity, and educational updates.

Tier-2 Exam Pattern (Subject Knowledge): This consists of 70 questions (100 Marks) focused entirely on the subject applied for. For example, a TGT Science candidate will face questions from Physics, Chemistry, and Biology up to Grade 10-12 level. This section also includes questions on Pedagogy and Perspectives on Education (including NEP 2020 and Child Development).

Negative Marking: There is a negative marking of 1/3rd mark for every incorrect answer in Tier-1, and 1/4th mark in Tier-2 objective questions.

Physical and Residential Requirements for NVS

Since the Navodaya Vidyalaya Samiti (NVS) consists of residential schools, candidates must be physically fit to stay on campus. Teachers are required to act as "House Masters" or "House Mistresses," overseeing student welfare during residential hours. While there is no Physical Endurance Test (PET), a clear medical fitness certificate from a government medical officer is mandatory. Candidates should be mentally prepared for the 24/7 nature of NVS duties, which is compensated by free accommodation and special allowances.

How to Prepare for KVS NVS 2025

Preparation for a central teaching exam requires a mix of academic depth and psychological readiness. Follow this subject-wise roadmap for success.

Perspectives on Education and Leadership: This is the most critical non-technical section. Focus on the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, Right to Education (RTE) Act 2009, and Piaget/Vygotsky theories on Child Development. Understanding the psychological nuances of adolescent students is key for PGT and TGT roles.

Subject Specific Mastery: For TGT/PGT, rely on NCERT textbooks from Grade 6 to 12. CBSE tends to pick questions directly from NCERT concepts. Solve the last 5 years of KVS and NVS papers to understand the trend. For PRT, the focus should be on foundational literacy and numeracy as per the NIPUN Bharat mission.

General Aptitude: Don't neglect the Reasoning and Math sections in Tier-1. Since teaching candidates often focus only on their subjects, doing well in the general section can significantly boost your overall percentile. Regular practice of mock tests is essential to manage the 120-minute time limit effectively.

Best Books for Preparation

Having the right resources will keep your preparation streamlined. Below are the top recommended books for the 2025 KVS NVS recruitment.

General Paper: KVS/NVS General Paper Guide by Arihant or R. Gupta.
Pedagogy & Perspectives: Perspectives on Education by Pearson or Child Development & Pedagogy by Sandeep Kumar.
Subject Specific: NCERT Textbooks (Std VI to XII) and Subject Guides by Youth Competition Times (YCT).
English/Hindi: Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi and Samanya Hindi by Lucent.
Reasoning: Modern Approach to Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.

How to Apply Online

The application process is conducted through a unified portal managed by CBSE. Follow these steps for an error-free registration:

  1. Visit the official portal at www.cbse.gov.in or the recruitment links on kvsangathan.nic.in.
  2. Click on "New Registration" for Joint Recruitment 01/2025.
  3. Provide your basic details (Name, Mother's Name, Father's Name) and verify your email and mobile via OTP.
  4. Select your post and organization preference (KVS, NVS, or Both).
  5. Fill in your educational details, including B.Ed. marks and CTET Roll Number. The CTET data will be automatically verified via DigiLocker.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph and signature (JPEG format, 10kb-50kb).
  7. Pay the application fee using the online payment gateway (UPI, Net Banking, or Card).
  8. Download and print the Confirmation Page. This is mandatory for the Document Verification stage.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready to ensure a smooth application process. All certificates must be issued before the cut-off date (December 4, 2025).

  1. Aadhaar Card: Essential for identity verification.
  2. Educational Marksheets: 10th, 12th, Graduation, and Post-Graduation certificates.
  3. Professional Certificates: B.Ed. Degree and Marksheet.
  4. CTET Marksheet: Valid CTET Certificate (Paper I or II) as per the post requirement.
  5. Caste Certificate: For SC/ST/OBC-NCL candidates in the central government format.
  6. Experience Certificate: Required for Principal and administrative roles.
  7. Photograph & Signature: Scanned clear images as per the specifications.

Important Links

Resource Name Official URL Link
Official Joint Application Portal Click Here to Apply
KVS Official Recruitment Page View KVS Announcements
NVS Official Career Page View NVS Notifications

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply for both KVS and NVS for the post of PGT?

Yes, the 2025 joint notification allows candidates to select preferences for both organizations. If you meet the eligibility criteria for both, you can apply through a single integrated application form.

Q2: Is CTET mandatory for PGT (Post Graduate Teacher) posts?

No, CTET is not required for PGT roles. It is mandatory only for TGT (Trained Graduate Teacher) and PRT (Primary Teacher) positions. However, a B.Ed. degree is essential for PGTs (except Computer Science).

Q3: What is the age relaxation for female candidates?

In KVS/NVS recruitments, female candidates are provided a significant 10-year age relaxation for all teaching posts (PGT, TGT, PRT, and Librarian). This is a great opportunity for women looking to re-enter the workforce.

Q4: Can I apply for TGT if I am appearing for my B.Ed. final year?

No. You must have completed your professional qualification and have your results in hand by the closing date of the application (December 4, 2025). Candidates with "Appearing" status are not eligible.

Q5: What is the negative marking for the KVS NVS exam?

The joint exam conducted by CBSE follows a negative marking system. In Tier-1, 0.33 marks (1/3rd) are deducted for wrong answers, and in Tier-2 objective sections, 0.25 marks (1/4th) are deducted.

Q6: What is the "integrated fee" mentioned in the notification?

If a candidate applies for the same post in both KVS and NVS, they may be eligible for a combined fee structure as per the CBSE portal’s integration rules. Check the payment page for post-wise integrated options.

entry.title: Odisha State Pollution Control Board Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
Odisha State Pollution Control Board Recruitment 2026

The Odisha State Pollution Control Board (OSPCB) has launched a massive recruitment drive for over 145 Group-B and Group-C positions. This is a golden opportunity for qualified individuals to build a career in a premier environmental regulatory body in Odisha. The online application process is open from January 20, 2026, to February 19, 2026. This comprehensive guide provides all the details for each of the 8 different posts advertised.

Post Name: Assistant Environmental Engineer, Scientist, ASO, Accountant, Junior Assistants, and more
Last Date: 19 February 2026
Total Posts: 145+

The Odisha State Pollution Control Board (OSPCB), the primary environmental regulatory agency of the state, has released a series of notifications inviting online applications from eligible Indian citizens. This large-scale recruitment initiative aims to fill numerous vacancies across technical, scientific, and administrative cadres. The drive includes high-profile Group-B positions like Assistant Environmental Engineer, Assistant Environmental Scientist, Senior Scientific Assistant, Accountant, and Assistant Section Officer (ASO), as well as essential Group-C roles such as Junior Accountant, Junior Laboratory Assistant, and Junior Assistant.

This mega recruitment offers a stable and prestigious government career with attractive pay scales under the 7th Pay Commission matrix. The selection process for all posts is competitive, involving a written examination and, for certain posts, an interview or a practical skill test. The application process has been streamlined through a unified online portal, allowing candidates to register and apply for one or more eligible posts within the stipulated deadline. This is a significant opportunity for graduates and post-graduates to contribute to the cause of environmental protection while building a secure career in the Government of Odisha.

OSPCB Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

The core application timeline is common for all 8 recruitment advertisements. Candidates must adhere to these dates strictly.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Odisha State Pollution Control Board (OSPCB)
Application Start Date 20 January 2026
Application Last Date 19 February 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Odisha
Official Website www.ospcboard.odisha.gov.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive is distributed across 8 different posts, each with its own notification, pay scale, and vacancy count.

Post Name Group Vacancies (Tentative) Pay Scale (as per ORSP Rules, 2017)
Assistant Environmental EngineerGroup-B20Level-10 (₹44,900 - ₹1,42,400)
Assistant Environmental ScientistGroup-B15Level-10 (₹44,900 - ₹1,42,400)
Senior Scientific AssistantGroup-B15Level-9 (₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400)
AccountantGroup-B10Level-9 (₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400)
Assistant Section Officer (ASO)Group-B15Level-9 (₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400)
Junior AccountantGroup-C15Level-7 (₹25,500 - ₹81,100)
Junior Laboratory AssistantGroup-C10Level-5 (₹21,700 - ₹69,100)
Junior AssistantGroup-C25Level-5 (₹21,700 - ₹69,100)
Total 125+
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may change. For a detailed category-wise reservation breakdown, candidates must refer to the individual official notifications.

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

For Group-B Posts (Engineer, Scientist, ASO, Accountant, Sr. Scientific Asst.):

  • For Unreserved (UR) and SEBC candidates: ₹1000/-
  • For SC, ST, and PwD candidates of Odisha: ₹500/-

For Group-C Posts (Jr. Assistant, Jr. Accountant, Jr. Lab Asst.):

  • For Unreserved (UR) and SEBC candidates: ₹500/-
  • For SC, ST, and PwD candidates of Odisha: ₹250/-

The fee must be paid online. Candidates applying for multiple posts must pay the fee for each post separately.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 01.01.2026)

Candidates must fulfill common eligibility criteria as well as specific educational qualifications for the post they are applying for.

Criteria Details
Common Eligibility
  1. Citizenship: Must be a Citizen of India.
  2. Age Limit: Must be between 21 and 38 years. (Age relaxation is applicable: 5 years for SC/ST/SEBC/Women, 10 years for PwD).
  3. Odia Language Proficiency: Must be able to read, write, and speak Odia and have passed M.E. School Examination with Odia as a language subject or equivalent as per government norms.
  4. Character: Must be of good character and sound health.
Post-wise Qualifications
Post NameMinimum Educational Qualification
Assistant Environmental EngineerB.E./B.Tech. in Environmental/Civil/Chemical Engineering.
Assistant Environmental ScientistM.Sc. in Chemistry/Botany/Zoology/Environmental Science/Life Science/Biotechnology.
Senior Scientific AssistantM.Sc. in a relevant science discipline (e.g., Chemistry, Life Sciences).
AccountantBachelor's Degree in Commerce (B.Com) with proficiency in computer applications.
Assistant Section Officer (ASO)Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognized University with computer knowledge.
Junior AccountantBachelor's Degree in Commerce (B.Com) with a Diploma in Computer Application (DCA).
Junior Laboratory AssistantBachelor's Degree in Science (B.Sc.) with a Diploma in Computer Application (DCA).
Junior AssistantBachelor's Degree in any discipline with a Diploma in Computer Application (DCA).

Selection Process

The selection process is distinct for Group-B and Group-C posts.

  1. For Group-B Posts (Engineer, Scientist, ASO, Accountant, SSA): The selection will be based on a two-stage process. First, a Written Examination (200 marks) will be conducted. Candidates shortlisted from the written test will then be called for a Viva-Voce (Interview) for 25 marks. The final merit list will be prepared based on the combined score of the written test and the interview.
  2. For Group-C Posts (Junior Assistant, Junior Accountant, JLA): The selection will also be a two-stage process. First, a Written Examination will be conducted. Candidates shortlisted from the written test (in a 1:3 ratio) will be called for a Practical Skill Test. This skill test will be qualifying in nature. The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the marks obtained in the written examination.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer in all objective written tests. The duration and structure vary by post.

Pattern for Assistant Environmental Scientist & Engineer:

  • Total Marks: 200 | Duration: 3 Hours
  • Paper-I (General Studies): 50 Marks (GK, Current Affairs, English, Computer, Reasoning)
  • Paper-II (Technical Paper): 150 Marks (Based on the candidate's core discipline - e.g., Chemistry, Civil Engg., etc.)
  • Syllabus: The detailed syllabus for each technical discipline is provided in the official notifications.

Pattern for Accountant & ASO:

  • The exam will likely consist of papers on General English, Odia, General Awareness, Reasoning, and a major paper on the core subject (Accountancy for Accountant, and a mix of subjects for ASO).

Pattern for Junior Assistant:

  • Total Marks: 100 | Duration: 2 Hours
  • Subjects: Mathematics (20), General Knowledge (20), English (20), Odia (20), and Computer Knowledge (20).
  • Skill Test: A 50-mark, 1-hour practical test on MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Access) and Windows OS, which is qualifying in nature.

Pattern for Junior Accountant & Junior Laboratory Assistant:

  • The written test will contain questions on general aptitude subjects as well as a significant portion dedicated to the core subjects (Commerce/Accounts for Jr. Accountant, and Science for Jr. Lab Assistant). This will be followed by a qualifying skill test.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for any of the posts in this recruitment drive.

How to Prepare for the Exams

A strategic approach is necessary to tackle these competitive exams.

  • Analyze the Syllabus: The first step is to download the notification for your target post and thoroughly analyze the detailed syllabus for both the general and technical papers.
  • Strengthen Core Subjects: For technical posts, the core subject paper has the highest weightage (150/200 marks). Dedicate most of your preparation time to revising your graduation/post-graduation subjects.
  • General Studies and Aptitude: For the general paper, focus on Odisha GK, current environmental news, and standard aptitude topics. Do not neglect this section as it can be a deciding factor.
  • Practice for Skill Tests: For posts like Junior Assistant and Junior Accountant, practical knowledge of MS Office is crucial. Practice typing and working with Word, Excel, and PowerPoint to ensure you can comfortably clear the qualifying skill test.
  • Mock Tests: Solve previous year papers from OPSC/OSSC for similar level posts and take online mock tests to improve your speed, accuracy, and time management.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard textbooks and competitive exam guides for their preparation.

  • Technical Subjects (Engg./Science): Standard textbooks from your B.E./B.Tech/M.Sc. curriculum are the best source.
  • Accountancy: Graduation-level books on Financial Accounting, Corporate Accounting, and Cost Accounting.
  • General Studies & Odisha GK: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and "Odisha Parichaya" or other books on Odisha GK.
  • Aptitude & English: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal and "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Computer Knowledge: "Objective Computer Awareness" by Arihant Publications.

How to Apply Online

The application process is unified through a common portal. Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Use the "Common Registration Link" provided in the links section below to create your profile on the application portal by entering your name, mobile number, and email ID.
  2. After successful registration, use the "Common Login Link" to access your dashboard.
  3. Inside the dashboard, you will find options to apply for the different posts. Select the post you wish to apply for.
  4. Fill out the detailed application form with accurate personal, educational, and contact information.
  5. Upload clear, scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other required certificates as per the specifications.
  6. Pay the application fee for the post using the integrated online payment gateway.
  7. Review all details carefully before final submission, as no changes can be made later.
  8. If you are applying for multiple posts, repeat the process from step 3 for each post and pay the fee separately.
  9. Download and print the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Scanning)

Candidates must keep clear, scanned copies of the following documents ready before starting the application process:

  1. Recent passport-size colour photograph.
  2. Full signature on white paper.
  3. 10th Class (HSC) certificate for age proof.
  4. All relevant educational certificates and mark sheets (e.g., Degree, M.Sc., B.E., B.Tech, DCA).
  5. Certificate proving you have passed Odia as a language subject in your schooling.
  6. Caste/SEBC/PwD/Ex-servicemen/Sportsperson certificate, if applicable, issued by the competent authority in Odisha.
  7. Valid Photo ID proof (e.g., Aadhaar Card, Voter ID).

Important Links

Use the common links below to register and log in for all posts.

Action Link
Common Registration Link Click Here to Register
Common Login Link Click Here to Login

Individual Notification PDFs

To read the detailed advertisement for a specific post, please use the links below.

Sl No. Name of Post Download Detailed Notification PDF
1Assistant Environmental EngineerClick Here
2Assistant Environmental ScientistClick Here
3Senior Scientific AssistantClick Here
4AccountantClick Here
5Assistant Section OfficerClick Here
6Junior AccountantClick Here
7Junior Laboratory AssistantClick Here
8Junior AssistantClick Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Can I apply for both Assistant Environmental Engineer and Assistant Environmental Scientist?

Yes, if you meet the distinct educational qualifications for both posts, you can apply for both. You will have to submit separate applications and pay the application fee for each post.

Q2: What is the last date to apply for the OSPCB recruitment?

The last date for submitting the online application for all 8 posts is February 19, 2026.

Q3: Is there a negative marking in the written exam?

Yes, for every incorrect answer in the objective written tests, there will be a penalty of 0.25 marks.

Q4: I do not have a DCA, but I have computer science as a subject in my degree. Can I apply for Junior Assistant?

The notification specifically asks for a "Diploma in Computer Application (DCA)". You should carefully read the detailed advertisement for the Junior Assistant post to see if any equivalency clauses are mentioned. If not, the DCA is likely a mandatory requirement.

entry.title: BHU Recruitment 2026 for 60 Non-Teaching, Scientific & Technical Posts
entry.content:
BHU Recruitment 2026 for 60 Non-Teaching, Scientific & Technical Posts

Advertisement No. 04/2024-25: Banaras Hindu University (BHU), Varanasi, has invited online applications for 60 temporary or deputation-based posts for its Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) and various ICAR-All India Coordinated Research Projects (AICRPs). The positions range from Senior Scientist and Subject Matter Specialist to Technical Assistants and support staff. The last date for online application is February 23, 2026, and for receiving the hard copy is February 28, 2026.

Post Name: Senior Scientist, Subject Matter Specialist, Assistant, Technician, Driver, and more
Last Date: 23 February 2026
Total Posts: 60

Banaras Hindu University (BHU), an Institution of National Importance, has announced a significant recruitment drive to fill various scientific, technical, and non-teaching positions. These appointments are temporary and co-terminus with the projects they are associated with, primarily the Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) at RGSC, Barkachha, Mirzapur, and several ICAR-AICRPs focusing on Rice, Wheat, Maize, Pulses, and more. This recruitment offers a unique opportunity for professionals to work in a leading academic and research environment on crucial agricultural projects.

The recruitment covers a wide spectrum of roles, from senior research positions requiring a Ph.D. and extensive experience, such as Senior Scientist and Principal Scientist, to support roles like Driver and Skilled Supporting Staff that require a High School pass. The selection process is tailored to the post, involving interviews for senior scientific positions and written/skill tests for non-teaching roles. The application process is a two-step procedure requiring both online submission via the Samarth/CU Chayan portal and the subsequent dispatch of the hard copy of the application to the university.

BHU Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take note of the two-step application deadline and other important details for this multi-post recruitment drive.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Banaras Hindu University (BHU), Varanasi
Advertisement Number 04/2024-25
Post Names Various Scientific, Technical, and Non-Teaching Posts
Total Vacancies 60
Last Date for Online Application & Fee Payment 23 February 2026 (up to 5:00 PM)
Last Date for Receipt of Hard Copy 28 February 2026 (up to 5:00 PM)
Application Mode Online submission + Offline (Hard Copy) dispatch
Job Type Temporary / Project-based
Job Location Varanasi / RGSC, Barkachha, Mirzapur
Official Website www.bhu.ac.in/rac

Vacancy Details

The 60 vacancies are distributed across Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) and various ICAR-AICRPs projects. A summary of posts is provided below.

Project/Section Post Name Vacancies Pay Level
Krishi Vigyan Kendra (KVK) Posts
KVKSenior Scientist-cum-Head1AL-13A
