KVKSubject Matter Specialist (Various Disciplines)6L-10
KVKAssistant, Programme Assistant, Farm Manager3L-6
KVKStenographer Gr III, Programme Assistant (Lab Tech)2L-6 / L-4
KVKDriver & Skilled Supporting Staff3L-2 / L-1
ICAR-AICRPs Scientific/Technical Posts
Various AICRPsPrincipal Scientist, Chief Scientist, Senior Scientist, Agronomist8AL-14 / AL-13A
Various AICRPsJunior Scientist, Junior Breeder, Junior Agronomist, etc.9AL-10
Various AICRPsSenior Technical Assistant, Technical Assistant11L-6 / L-5 / L-3
Various AICRPsField Man, Head Clerk, Drivers, Peon7L-1 to L-6
Total 60
Note: For a detailed post-wise and category-wise breakdown, candidates must refer to the official notification.

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

  • For Scientific/Technical & KVK posts (Pay Level AL-14 to L-10): ₹1000/- (for Gen, EWS, OBC candidates).
  • For Non-teaching posts under ICAR-AICRPs & KVK (Pay Level L-6 to L-1): ₹500/- (for Gen, EWS, OBC candidates).

No application fee will be charged for candidates belonging to SC, ST, PwBDs categories, and for Women candidates. The fee must be paid online.

Eligibility Criteria

The eligibility criteria vary widely depending on the post. A summary is provided below.

Post Name / Category Essential Qualifications & Experience
Senior Scientist / Principal Scientist / Chief Scientist Qualification: Ph.D. in the relevant agricultural discipline (e.g., Genetics, Plant Breeding, Agronomy, Soil Science).
Experience: 8 to 10 years of experience as a Scientist/Assistant Professor in the relevant field, with a proven research record.
Subject Matter Specialist / Junior Scientist / Agronomist Qualification: Master's Degree (M.Sc./M.V.Sc./M.Tech) in the relevant agricultural discipline (e.g., Agronomy, Horticulture, Plant Pathology, Genetics, etc.). For many scientific posts, qualifying the ICAR/UGC/CSIR NET is mandatory.
Assistant / Head Clerk Qualification: Bachelor's Degree from a recognized university. For Head Clerk, 3 years of experience as a Junior Clerk and computer typing knowledge are required.
Programme Assistant / Technical Assistant Qualification: Bachelor's Degree in a relevant field like Agriculture, Computer Science, or B.Sc./M.Sc. depending on the specific post. Some posts require experience.
Stenographer Gr III Qualification: 12th class pass. Requires passing a dictation test at 80 w.p.m. in English or Hindi.
Driver (Tractor/Jeep) Qualification: High School pass with a valid driving license for heavy vehicles and two years of driving experience.
Skilled Supporting Staff / Field Man / Peon Qualification: High School pass or its equivalent. For Field Man, an Agri./Science pass with experience is required.

Selection Process

The selection methodology is different for scientific and non-teaching positions.

  1. For Scientific/Technical Posts (Group A): The university will shortlist candidates based on their academic qualifications, research score, experience, and other criteria. Shortlisted candidates will be called for an Interview. A written test may also be conducted for further shortlisting if the number of applicants is large.
  2. For Non-Teaching Posts (Group B & C): The selection for posts like Assistant, Stenographer, Driver, etc., will be based on a two-stage process. First, a Written Test will be conducted. Candidates shortlisted from the written test will then be called for a Skill Test (e.g., Typing Test, Steno Test, Driving Test), which will be qualifying or scoring as per the rules for that post.

Exam Pattern

The detailed exam pattern and syllabus for the written tests (for non-teaching posts) are not specified in the advertisement. Candidates are advised to check the official BHU website (`www.bhu.ac.in/rac`) for updates.

For skill tests, the standards are defined:

  • Stenographer Gr III: A dictation test in English or Hindi at 80 w.p.m. for 10 minutes. Transcription time on computer will be 50 minutes for English and 65 minutes for Hindi.

The written tests for other non-teaching posts will likely cover General Knowledge, Reasoning, Language (English/Hindi), and subject-specific knowledge relevant to the post.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for any of the posts in this recruitment drive.

How to Prepare

A post-specific preparation strategy is required.

  • For Scientific Posts: Focus on your research publications, Ph.D. thesis, and core area of expertise. Prepare a presentation on your work and be ready for an in-depth technical interview.
  • For Non-Teaching Posts: Prepare for a standard competitive written exam covering general aptitude, reasoning, English/Hindi, and GK. For technical posts, revise your diploma/degree syllabus.
  • For Skill-Based Posts: Daily practice is key. For Stenographer, practice dictation and transcription. For Driver, ensure you are proficient with heavy vehicle driving.

Best Books for Preparation

Preparation material should be chosen based on the post applied for.

  • Scientific Posts: Standard postgraduate textbooks, research journals, and publications in your specific field of agriculture.
  • Non-Teaching (General Subjects): "Lucent's General Knowledge" for GK, "Quantitative Aptitude" by R.S. Aggarwal, and standard books for English/Hindi grammar.
  • Non-Teaching (Technical Subjects): Your own academic textbooks from your Degree/Diploma are the best resource.

How to Apply

The application is a two-step process: online submission followed by sending the hard copy.

  1. Step 1 (Online Application): Visit the BHU website (`www.bhu.ac.in/rac`) or the Samarth/CU Chayan Portal. Register and fill out the online application form with all correct details.
  2. Upload your scanned photograph, signature, and all other required documents.
  3. Pay the application fee online. The deadline for online submission and fee payment is February 23, 2026 (5:00 PM).
  4. After final submission, download a PDF copy of your completed application form.
  5. Step 2 (Hard Copy Submission): Take a printout of the downloaded application form. Attach self-attested copies of all supporting documents (educational, experience, caste, etc.).
  6. Send this complete set via post to the address below, ensuring it reaches on or before February 28, 2026 (5:00 PM).

Submission Address:
Office of the Registrar,
Recruitment & Assessment Cell, Holkar House,
Banaras Hindu University (BHU),
Varanasi - 221005 (U.P.)

Documents Needed to Apply (for Hard Copy Submission)

You must send self-attested copies of the following documents along with the downloaded application form:

  1. Printout of the online application form.
  2. All educational mark sheets and certificates (10th, 12th, Diploma, Degree, Master's, Ph.D.).
  3. NET qualification certificate, if applicable.
  4. Experience certificates in the prescribed format.
  5. Caste (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) and PwBD certificates in the Government of India format.
  6. All research publications, reprints of best papers, and other documents supporting your research score (for scientific posts).
  7. Valid Photo ID proof.

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online (Samarth Portal) Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official BHU Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Are these jobs permanent?

No, the notification clearly states that these are temporary positions, co-terminus with the respective KVK or ICAR-AICRP project. There is no claim for absorption into permanent posts.

Q2: I have submitted the online form. Is my application complete?

No. After submitting the online form, you must also send a hard copy (printout) of the application along with all supporting documents to the Registrar's office at BHU by post. The deadline for the hard copy to reach is February 28, 2026.

Q3: Is there any application fee for female candidates?

No, female candidates, along with SC, ST, and PwBD candidates, are exempted from paying the application fee.

Q4: Is NET qualification mandatory for all scientific posts?

For most Junior Scientist/Specialist posts at Pay Level AL-10, qualifying the ICAR/UGC/CSIR NET is listed as an essential qualification. However, NET is not required for disciplines where it is not conducted, or for senior posts where a Ph.D. with significant experience is the primary requirement.

entry.title: ULB Haryana Recruitment 2025: 300 Engineering Associates की भर्ती
entry.content:
ULB Haryana Recruitment 2025: 300 Engineering Associates की भर्ती

Directorate of Urban Local Bodies (ULB), Haryana ने Civil, Electrical और Horticulture streams में 300 Engineering Associates की भर्ती के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं।

ULB Haryana ने 300 पदों पर भर्ती का विज्ञापन जारी किया है। यह भर्ती Civil, Electrical और Horticulture विभागों में Contract Basis पर होगी। इच्छुक उम्मीदवार 31 अक्टूबर 2025 तक ऑनलाइन आवेदन कर सकते हैं। चयनित उम्मीदवारों को हरियाणा के विभिन्न नगर निकायों (Urban Local Bodies) में तैनात किया जाएगा।

रिक्तियाँ और वेतन विवरण

पदकुल पदमासिक वेतन
Engineering Associate (Civil)200₹30,000/-
Engineering Associate (Electrical)50₹30,000/-
Engineering Associate (Horticulture)50₹30,000/-
नोट: अनुबंध प्रारंभिक रूप से 1 वर्ष का होगा, जिसे प्रदर्शन के आधार पर 3 वर्ष तक बढ़ाया जा सकता है।

शैक्षणिक योग्यता

  • Civil: B.Tech/B.E. in Civil Engineering
  • Electrical: B.Tech/B.E. in Electrical Engineering
  • Horticulture: Agriculture में First Class Degree या M.Sc. in Horticulture/Botany

चयन प्रक्रिया

चयन दो चरणों में होगा — Merit (50 अंक) और Written Test (50 अंक)।

Part A – Merit & Experience (50 Marks)

  • B.Tech/B.E. के अंक प्रतिशत के अनुसार (अधिकतम 35 अंक)
  • Master’s Degree – 5 अंक
  • 1 वर्ष का अनुभव – 5 अंक
  • अतिरिक्त अनुभव – प्रति वर्ष 1 अंक (अधिकतम 5 अंक)

35 अंक से कम पाने वाले उम्मीदवार Written Test के लिए योग्य नहीं होंगे।

Part B – Written Test (50 Marks)

लिखित परीक्षा 50 अंकों की होगी। इसमें भी न्यूनतम 35 अंक प्राप्त करना आवश्यक है। Final Merit दोनों भागों के कुल अंकों पर आधारित होगी।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://ulbharyana.gov.in/recruitment पर जाएँ।
  2. अपना पंजीकरण करें और आवेदन पत्र भरें।
  3. आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ जैसे फोटो, हस्ताक्षर और शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र अपलोड करें।
  4. फॉर्म सबमिट करने के बाद प्राप्त Application Number सुरक्षित रखें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंककार्यवाही
Online Applyयहाँ क्लिक करें
Official Recruitment Detailsयहाँ देखें
Official Notification PDFडाउनलोड करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 31 अक्टूबर 2025 है।

Q2: क्या आवेदन शुल्क है?

नहीं, इस भर्ती में किसी प्रकार का आवेदन शुल्क नहीं है।

Q3: क्या Final Year छात्र आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

नहीं, आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि तक आवश्यक योग्यता पूरी होनी चाहिए।

Q4: क्या अनुभव अनिवार्य है?

अनुभव अनिवार्य नहीं है, लेकिन अनुभव वालों को अतिरिक्त अंक दिए जाएँगे।

entry.title: United India Insurance (UIIC) Apprentice Recruitment 2025: 153 Graduate Apprentice Posts Open
entry.content:
United India Insurance (UIIC) Apprentice Recruitment 2025: 153 Graduate Apprentice Posts Open

A golden opportunity for graduates to join a leading public sector insurance company for a one-year apprenticeship. Selection based on merit, no examination.

Post Name: Apprentice (Under Graduate in any discipline)
Last Date: 20 January 2026
Total Posts: 153

United India Insurance Co. Ltd (UIIC), a leading public sector general insurance company, has announced a significant opportunity for young graduates as part of its skill development initiative. The company has invited online applications from eligible Indian citizens who have completed their graduation in any discipline for a one-year apprenticeship training program under the Apprenticeship (Amendment) Act, 1973. This drive aims to fill 153 training seats across various states in India.

This apprenticeship program is a fantastic opportunity for recent graduates (passed between July 2021 and 2025) to gain valuable hands-on experience in the insurance sector. A key highlight of this recruitment is that the selection process does not involve any written examination. Candidates will be shortlisted purely on the basis of the percentage of marks obtained in their qualifying graduate degree examination. This merit-based approach makes it a transparent and accessible opportunity for academically bright students. Selected apprentices will receive a monthly stipend of ₹9,000 during the one-year training period. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all the important details of this recruitment.

Important Points

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this apprenticeship drive. This is a one-year apprenticeship training program in a Central Government undertaking.

Particulars Details
Organization Name United India Insurance Co. Ltd (UIIC)
Post Name Apprentice (Graduate)
Job Type Apprenticeship Training (1 Year)
Job Location Various States across India
Online Application Start Date 18 December 2025
Online Application Last Date 20 January 2026

Vacancy Details

The recruitment aims to fill 153 apprentice training seats across various states. The distribution of these seats is as follows:

State Number of Seats
ANDHRA PRADESH 3
ASSAM 1
BIHAR 2
CHANDIGARH 2
CHATTISGARH 4
DELHI 9
GOA 2
GUJARAT 8
HARYANA 1
JHARKHAND 1
KARNATAKA 26
KERALA 10
MADHYA PRADESH 6
MAHARASHTRA 23
ODISHA 1
PUDUCHERRY 4
PUNJAB 2
RAJASTHAN 18
TAMIL NADU 19
TELANGANA 2
UTTARAKHAND 5
WEST BENGAL 4
Total Vacancies 153
Note: The number of training seats mentioned above is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the company. Candidates can apply for engagement in ONE STATE only.

Stipend and Application Fee

  • Monthly Stipend: Selected apprentices will be paid a monthly stipend of ₹9,000/- during the one-year training period.
  • Application Fee: There is no application fee for this recruitment process.

The apprenticeship is a training program and does not guarantee permanent employment with the company upon completion.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet the eligibility criteria regarding age and educational qualifications as of December 1, 2025.

Criteria Requirement Details
Educational Qualification
  • Candidates must have passed an Undergraduate degree (Graduation) in any discipline from a University/Institution recognized by AICTE/DOTE/UGC.
  • The course must be a regular, full-time program.
  • Candidates must have passed their graduation not earlier than July 1, 2021. This means candidates who have passed in the years July 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, and 2025 are eligible.
Exclusions (Who are NOT eligible)
  • Candidates who possess a Post-Graduate Degree or have completed an Integrated Postgraduate Course.
  • Candidates who have graduated from an institution not approved by AICTE/DOTE/UGC.
  • Candidates who possess one year or more of work experience in any Government, Government Undertaking, or Private Organization.
  • Candidates who have previously undergone or are currently pursuing Apprenticeship Training.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 21 years
  • Maximum Age: 28 years (i.e., candidates must have been born not earlier than 01.12.1997 and not later than 01.12.2004).
  • Relaxation in the upper age limit is applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PwBD candidates as per Government of India guidelines.

Selection Process

The selection process for this apprenticeship is unique as it is entirely merit-based and does not involve any written examination or interview. The Board of Apprenticeship Training (Southern Region) is entrusted with the shortlisting process.

  1. Shortlisting Based on Merit: The shortlisting of candidates will be carried out based on the percentage of marks (up to two decimal places) secured in the qualifying Graduate Degree Examination. This is subject to the candidate fulfilling all other prescribed eligibility criteria.
  2. Tie-Breaker Rule: In cases where two or more candidates secure the same percentage of marks, their position in the merit list will be determined according to their date of birth, with the older candidate being placed higher in the order of merit.
  3. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates will be intimated through their registered Email IDs to appear in person for the verification of their documents at various offices of United India Insurance Co. Ltd.
  4. Final Engagement List: The final engagement list will be prepared after verifying all the details entered by the candidate in the NATS portal against the original documents. The final selection is subject to the availability of vacancies in the respective state.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for this recruitment. The selection is purely based on the marks obtained in the qualifying graduation degree. Therefore, candidates do not need to prepare for any exam pattern or syllabus.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for this apprenticeship program, as it is an office-based training role.

How to Prepare

Since the selection is merit-based and does not involve an exam, the "preparation" for this opportunity lies in ensuring a flawless and accurate application process. Here are the key steps to focus on:

  1. Accurate Profile on NATS Portal: Your entire selection depends on the information you provide on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS) portal. Ensure that your profile is 100% complete.
  2. Correct Percentage Entry: Double-check the percentage of marks you enter. If your university provides grades (CGPA/OGPA), you must convert them into a percentage by multiplying the grade by a factor of 10, as specified in the notification. Furnishing incorrect marks will lead to disqualification.
  3. Document Readiness: Keep all your original documents, such as your degree certificate, consolidated mark sheet, Aadhaar card, bank passbook, and latest photograph, ready and uploaded on your NATS profile.
  4. Regularly Check Emails: All communication, including the call for document verification, will be sent to your registered email ID. Keep your email active and check it frequently.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no written examination, no preparation books are required. Candidates should focus on the application process itself.

Not applicable for this recruitment process.

How to Apply

The application process is a mandatory two-step online procedure. Candidates must first enroll/apply on the NATS portal and then apply on the UIIC specific link if provided.

  1. Step 1: Enrollment on NATS Portal: All candidates must first enroll themselves on the National Web Portal (NATS) at https://nats.education.gov.in.
    • Go to the portal, click on "Student," and then "Student Register."
    • Complete the application form to generate a unique 12-digit Enrollment Number (ATN...).
    • Ensure you upload all required documents, including your Degree Certificate/Consolidated Marksheet, Aadhaar Card, Bank Passbook, and Photograph, to complete your profile.
  2. Step 2: Applying for the UIIC Opportunity:
    • Log in to the NATS portal with your email ID and password.
    • Under the "Apply against advertised vacancies" section, search for "UNITED INDIA INSURANCE COMPANY LTD" (User ID: STNCHC100938).
    • Click "Apply" against the vacancy. You will see the status as "applied".
  3. Step 3: Keep Track of Updates: After applying, regularly check your registered email for any communication from the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) or UIIC regarding shortlisting and document verification.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must ensure that the following documents are clearly uploaded in their NATS student profile, as this will be the basis for shortlisting:

  1. Degree Certificate or Provisional Certificate.
  2. Consolidated Mark sheet of all semesters/years.
  3. Aadhaar Card.
  4. Bank Passbook (in the candidate's name).
  5. Latest Passport-size Photograph.
  6. Caste/Category certificate (if applicable).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online (NATS Portal) Click Here
Download Official Notification View Here
UIIC Official Website https://uiic.co.in/

FAQs

Question 1: Is there any written exam for this recruitment?

No, there is no written examination. The selection is purely based on the percentage of marks obtained in the graduation degree.

Question 2: Can final-year students apply?

No, candidates must have passed their graduation and received their degree certificate between July 2021 and 2025 to be eligible.

Question 3: Is this a permanent job?

No, this is a one-year apprenticeship training program. UIIC has no obligation to offer permanent employment after the completion of the training.

Question 4: I have a postgraduate degree. Can I apply?

No, candidates who possess a Post-Graduate Degree are not eligible to apply for this apprenticeship program.

entry.title: NSUT फैकल्टी भर्ती 2025: असिस्टेंट और एसोसिएट प्रोफेसर के 176 पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
NSUT फैकल्टी भर्ती 2025: असिस्टेंट और एसोसिएट प्रोफेसर के 176 पदों पर भर्ती!

नेताजी सुभाष यूनिवर्सिटी ऑफ टेक्नोलॉजी (NSUT), दिल्ली ने इंजीनियरिंग विभागों में फैकल्टी पदों के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं।

टीचिंग क्षेत्र में करियर बनाने की इच्छा रखने वालों के लिए शानदार मौका! नेताजी सुभाष यूनिवर्सिटी ऑफ टेक्नोलॉजी (NSUT), दिल्ली ने अपने विभिन्न इंजीनियरिंग विभागों में असिस्टेंट प्रोफेसर और एसोसिएट प्रोफेसर के पदों पर भर्ती हेतु अधिसूचना (No. NSUT/FACULTY/2025/01) जारी की है। कुल 176 पदों पर भर्ती की जाएगी।

यह भर्ती ग्रुप 'A' (Teaching Category) के अंतर्गत है। उम्मीदवारों को पहले ऑनलाइन आवेदन करना होगा और फिर आवेदन की हार्ड कॉपी आवश्यक दस्तावेजों के साथ विश्वविद्यालय को भेजनी होगी। यह दिल्ली की एक प्रतिष्ठित राज्य विश्वविद्यालय में नौकरी पाने का सुनहरा अवसर है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन शुरू6 अक्टूबर 2025 (सोमवार)
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि11 नवम्बर 2025 (मंगलवार)
हार्ड कॉपी जमा करने की अंतिम तिथि26 नवम्बर 2025 (बुधवार) शाम 5:00 बजे तक

रिक्तियों का विवरण

कुल 176 पदों में से 126 असिस्टेंट प्रोफेसर और 50 एसोसिएट प्रोफेसर के लिए हैं।

असिस्टेंट प्रोफेसर (कुल: 126 पद)

विभागकुल पद
कंप्यूटर साइंस एंड इंजीनियरिंग / आईटी68
इलेक्ट्रॉनिक्स एंड कम्युनिकेशन इंजीनियरिंग26
इलेक्ट्रिकल इंजीनियरिंग17
इंस्ट्रूमेंटेशन एंड कंट्रोल इंजीनियरिंग15

एसोसिएट प्रोफेसर (कुल: 50 पद)

विभागकुल पद
कंप्यूटर साइंस एंड इंजीनियरिंग / आईटी26
इलेक्ट्रॉनिक्स एंड कम्युनिकेशन इंजीनियरिंग12
इलेक्ट्रिकल इंजीनियरिंग8
इंस्ट्रूमेंटेशन एंड कंट्रोल इंजीनियरिंग4

आवेदन शुल्क

  • सामान्य / OBC / EWS: ₹1,000 पंजीकरण शुल्क + ₹1,000 प्रोसेसिंग शुल्क = ₹2,000/- कुल
  • SC / ST / PwBD: पंजीकरण शुल्क NIL + प्रोसेसिंग शुल्क ₹1,000 = ₹1,000/- कुल

शुल्क केवल ऑनलाइन माध्यम से जमा करनी होगी और यह non-refundable है।

पात्रता मानदंड

पद का नामशैक्षणिक योग्यता एवं अनुभवआयु सीमा
असिस्टेंट प्रोफेसरसंबंधित विषय में B.E./B.Tech./B.S. और M.E./M.Tech./M.S. या Integrated M.Tech. में से किसी एक में प्रथम श्रेणी या समकक्ष ग्रेड।35 वर्ष
एसोसिएट प्रोफेसरसंबंधित क्षेत्र में Ph.D. डिग्री आवश्यक। बैचलर या मास्टर स्तर पर प्रथम श्रेणी अनिवार्य। कम से कम 6 शोध प्रकाशन (SCI/UGC/AICTE approved journals) और 8 वर्ष का शिक्षण/अनुसंधान अनुभव आवश्यक।50 वर्ष
आयु में छूट: SC/ST/OBC/PwBD को दिल्ली सरकार के नियमों के अनुसार छूट दी जाएगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया

उम्मीदवारों का चयन योग्यता, अनुभव और साक्षात्कार के आधार पर किया जाएगा।

  1. स्क्रीनिंग: आवेदन और दस्तावेज़ों की प्रारंभिक जांच होगी।
  2. लिखित परीक्षा (केवल असिस्टेंट प्रोफेसर के लिए): आवश्यकता पड़ने पर लिखित परीक्षा आयोजित की जा सकती है।
  3. टीचिंग एप्टीट्यूड असेसमेंट और इंटरव्यू: शॉर्टलिस्ट उम्मीदवारों का इंटरव्यू होगा।

अंतिम चयन मेरिट के आधार पर किया जाएगा, जिसमें शैक्षणिक योग्यता, शोध कार्य और इंटरव्यू प्रदर्शन को जोड़ा जाएगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट www.nsut.ac.in पर जाएं।
  2. “Recruitment” सेक्शन में जाकर “Apply Online” पर क्लिक करें।
  3. फॉर्म भरें और शुल्क का भुगतान करें।
  4. फाइनल सबमिट के बाद प्रिंटआउट निकालें।
  5. आवेदन प्रिंटआउट के साथ आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ों की स्वयं-सत्यापित प्रतियां लगाकर निम्न पते पर भेजें:

    6. The Registrar, Netaji Subhas University of Technology, Azad Hind Fauj Marg, Sector-3, Dwarka, New Delhi – 110078

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन / आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्र.1: क्या एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
हाँ, लेकिन प्रत्येक पद के लिए अलग आवेदन और शुल्क देना होगा।
प्र.2: यदि हार्ड कॉपी नहीं भेजी तो क्या आवेदन मान्य होगा?
नहीं, आवेदन अस्वीकृत कर दिया जाएगा। ऑनलाइन आवेदन के बाद हार्ड कॉपी भेजना आवश्यक है।
प्र.3: क्या दिल्ली के बाहर के OBC उम्मीदवारों को आरक्षण का लाभ मिलेगा?
नहीं, केवल दिल्ली सरकार द्वारा प्रमाणित OBC उम्मीदवारों को आरक्षण मिलेगा। अन्य को सामान्य वर्ग माना जाएगा।
प्र.4: असिस्टेंट प्रोफेसर के चयन की प्रक्रिया क्या है?
जरूरत पड़ने पर लिखित परीक्षा ली जा सकती है। अंतिम चयन इंटरव्यू और अकादमिक रिकॉर्ड के आधार पर होगा।

entry.title: ICMR Scientist-B (Non-Medical) Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
ICMR Scientist-B (Non-Medical) Recruitment 2026

Notice No. 377/2025: The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi, on behalf of the Indian Council of Medical Research (ICMR), has invited online applications for the recruitment of Scientist-B (Non-Medical). A total of 27 vacancies are available across various disciplines. The online registration process is open from February 02, 2026, to March 01, 2026.

Post Name: Scientist-B (Non-Medical)
Last Date: 01 March 2026
Total Posts: 27

AIIMS New Delhi is conducting a recruitment examination for the direct recruitment of Scientist-B (Non-Medical) posts for ICMR Headquarters and its Institutes/Centres across India. This drive aims to fill 27 vacancies in specialized fields, including General Non-Medical Science, IT/Computer Science, and Pharmacology. These are Group 'A' gazetted posts offering a Level-10 pay scale, providing a prestigious career path in medical research.

The selection process involves a two-stage evaluation: a Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by an Interview. The CBT will serve as a screening test, and the final merit list will be based solely on the performance in the interview. Candidates must possess a Master's degree in a relevant field or a B.E./B.Tech degree for the IT stream. The application process is entirely online through the AIIMS exams portal.

ICMR Scientist-B Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. The entire application process is managed by AIIMS New Delhi.

Particulars Details
Conducting Body AIIMS, New Delhi (on behalf of ICMR)
Advertisement Notice No. 377/2025
Post Name Scientist-B (Non-Medical)
Total Vacancies 27
Online Registration Start Date 02 February 2026
Online Registration Last Date 01 March 2026 (up to 5:00 PM)
Correction Window 10 March 2026 to 13 March 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Group A)
Job Location ICMR Hqrs or any of its 28 Institutes/Centres across India
Official Website www.aiimsexams.ac.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment is for 27 posts of Scientist-B (Non-Medical) in Level-10 of the Pay Matrix (₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500).

Post Discipline Vacancies Category Breakup
Scientist-B (Non-Medical) 25 UR-16, OBC-4, SC-3, ST-2 (includes 4 PwBD)
Scientist-B (Non-Medical IT/Computer Science) 1 UR-1
Scientist-B (Non-Medical Pharmacology) 1 UR-1
Total 27
Note: The location of vacancies will be Delhi (NCR) and any of the 28 Institutes/Centres of ICMR across the country.

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

  • For General / OBC Candidates: ₹3000/-
  • For SC / ST / EWS Candidates: ₹2400/-
  • For Persons with Disabilities (PwD): Exempted (No Fee)

The fee must be paid online through Debit Card, Credit Card, or Net Banking. Transaction charges are extra.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill the specific educational qualifications and age limits for the respective posts.

Post Name Age Limit Essential Qualifications
Scientist-B (Non-Medical) Not exceeding 35 years Master’s degree from a recognized university in a subject related to Biomedical Sciences.
Scientist-B (IT/Computer Science) Not exceeding 35 years ME/M.Tech (Computer Science) OR ME/M.Tech (CS)/MCA OR 4 years B.E./B.Tech/B.Sc. (Engg.) in Computer Science/IT with Ph.D. in a relevant subject.
Scientist-B (Pharmacology) Not exceeding 35 years Master’s degree from a recognized university in Pharmacy (Pharmacology) OR Pharm-D degree recognized by UGC/PCI.

Desirable Qualification: Experience in Research & Development related to Biomedical Sciences in Govt./Public/Private institutions is desirable for all posts.

Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and 10 years for PwBD candidates.

Selection Process

The selection process consists of two stages:

  1. Stage-I: Computer Based Test (CBT): A written examination of 100 marks. This is a screening test. Candidates obtaining 50% or more marks (45% for SC/ST/PwBD) will be treated as qualified for the next stage.
  2. Stage-II: Interview: Candidates numbering five times the vacancies will be called for an interview based on merit in Stage-I. The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the performance in the Interview.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus (Stage-I)

The Computer Based Test (CBT) will be objective type.

  • Total Questions: 100 (Objective Type)
  • Total Marks: 100
  • Marking Scheme: 1 mark for each correct answer.
  • Negative Marking: 1/3 mark will be deducted for each wrong answer.
  • Language: English only.

Syllabus Breakdown (100 Questions):

The questions will cover the following fields/disciplines:

  • Research Methodology (Basics)
  • Basic Biostatistics
  • Validity & Reliability of Diagnostic & Screening Tests
  • Bioethics
  • Communicable & Non-Communicable Diseases
  • Reproductive & Child Nutrition
  • Logical Reasoning
  • General Knowledge
  • English Language Aptitude

How to Prepare for the Exam

A focused preparation strategy is essential for this specialized exam.

  • Focus on Research Methodology & Biostatistics: These are core topics. Study standard textbooks on research methods and basic statistics used in medical research.
  • Public Health Knowledge: Revise topics related to communicable and non-communicable diseases, nutrition, and bioethics, as these are directly mentioned in the syllabus.
  • General Aptitude: Dedicate time to Logical Reasoning, GK, and English. Since there is negative marking (1/3rd), accuracy is key.
  • Interview Preparation: Since the final selection is based only on the interview, start preparing for it early. Be ready to discuss your academic background, research interests, and knowledge of the biomedical field.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard academic books and competitive guides.

  • Research Methodology: "Research Methodology: Methods and Techniques" by C.R. Kothari.
  • Biostatistics: "Methods in Biostatistics" by B.K. Mahajan.
  • Public Health: "Park's Textbook of Preventive and Social Medicine".
  • Aptitude & English: Standard books by R.S. Aggarwal and S.P. Bakshi.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the AIIMS Exams website.

  1. Visit the official website: www.aiimsexams.ac.in.
  2. Click on the "Recruitment" tab and find the link for "Scientist-B (Non-Medical) for ICMR".
  3. Register by providing your basic details like Name, Date of Birth, Email, and Mobile Number.
  4. Log in with your credentials and fill out the detailed application form.
  5. Upload scanned images of your photograph, signature, and thumb impression as per the instructions.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Debit/Credit Card or Net Banking.
  7. Submit the application and take a printout of the confirmation page for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

Candidates must have scanned copies of the following ready for the online application:

  1. Recent passport-size photograph (white background).
  2. Scanned signature.
  3. Left thumb impression.
  4. Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS), if applicable.
  5. PwD Certificate, if applicable.
  6. No Objection Certificate (NOC) if currently employed in Govt./PSU (to be uploaded or submitted later as per instructions).

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is the CBT score used for the final merit list?

No, the CBT is only a screening test. The final selection will be based 100% on the performance in the Interview.

Q2: Is there negative marking in the CBT?

Yes, there is a negative marking of 1/3 (one-third) mark for every incorrect answer.

Q3: Can I edit my application after submission?

Candidates who have paid the fee may be allowed to edit certain fields during the correction window from 10.03.2026 to 13.03.2026.

Q4: What is the exam center location?

The recruitment examination (CBT) will be conducted in Delhi/NCR only.

entry.title: IGMCRI नर्सिंग ऑफिसर भर्ती 2025: 226 पदों पर सीधी भर्ती!
entry.content:
IGMCRI नर्सिंग ऑफिसर भर्ती 2025: 226 पदों पर भर्ती!

पुडुचेरी में B.Sc. Nursing और GNM डिग्रीधारकों के लिए सरकारी नौकरी, बिना परीक्षा मेरिट पर चयन।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! नर्सिंग क्षेत्र में करियर बनाने के इच्छुक उम्मीदवारों के लिए एक बड़ी खबर है। पुडुचेरी स्थित Indira Gandhi Medical College & Research Institute (IGMCRI) ने Nursing Officer (Group 'B') पदों पर सीधी भर्ती के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है। इस भर्ती के अंतर्गत कुल 226 पदों पर नियुक्तियाँ की जाएँगी।

इस भर्ती की सबसे विशेष बात यह है कि इसमें कोई लिखित परीक्षा नहीं होगी। चयन पूरी तरह से academic merit, अनुभव, और COVID ड्यूटी के अंकों पर आधारित होगा। यह भर्ती केवल Union Territory of Puducherry के मूल निवासियों के लिए है। आवेदन प्रक्रिया offline है और 07 अक्टूबर 2025 से शुरू होकर 06 नवम्बर 2025 तक चलेगी।

IGMCRI नर्सिंग ऑफिसर रिक्तियाँ 2025

वर्गरिक्तियाँ
UR90
EWS22
MBC40
OBC26
EBC4
BCM5
SC35
ST2
BT2
कुल226
PwBD (क्षैतिज)10

वेतनमान (Salary)

नर्सिंग ऑफिसर पद 7वें वेतन आयोग के अनुसार Pay Level-7 में आता है, जिसमें आकर्षक वेतन के साथ भत्ते भी शामिल हैं जैसे DA, HRA, TA और Nursing Allowance।

पदपे लेवलवेतनमानइन-हैंड वेतन
Nursing Officer (Group 'B')Level 7₹44,900 – ₹1,42,400₹75,000+ (लगभग)

पात्रता मानदंड

मानदंडविवरण
शैक्षणिक योग्यताB.Sc. Nursing या GNM डिप्लोमा, तथा किसी भी State Nursing Council में पंजीकरण अनिवार्य।
आयु सीमा (06.11.2025 तक)न्यूनतम 18 वर्ष और अधिकतम 35 वर्ष।
निवासउम्मीदवार पुडुचेरी का मूल निवासी या पिछले 5 वर्षों से निवासी होना चाहिए।
आयु में छूट: MBC/OBC/EBC/BCM/BT को 3 वर्ष, SC/ST को 5 वर्ष, और PwBD उम्मीदवारों को 10 वर्ष तक की छूट मिलेगी।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
आवेदन प्रारंभ07 अक्टूबर 2025 (सुबह 10 बजे)
आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि06 नवम्बर 2025 (शाम 5 बजे)
आवेदन शुल्क:
General/EWS/OBC/MBC/EBC/BCM/BT: ₹250
SC/ST: ₹125
PwBD उम्मीदवारों को शुल्क से छूट दी गई है।

चयन प्रक्रिया

चयन पूरी तरह से मेरिट पर आधारित होगा। मेरिट सूची कुल 120 अंकों पर बनेगी:

मानदंडअधिकतम अंक
शैक्षणिक अंक (50% H.Sc. + 50% Degree/Diploma)100
Employment Exchange Seniority15
COVID Duty Incentive2–5
कुल120

नोट: प्रत्येक अतिरिक्त प्रयास के लिए 5% अंक घटाए जाएँगे।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया (Offline)

  1. IGMCRI की वेबसाइट से फॉर्म डाउनलोड करें।
  2. Capital Letters में फॉर्म भरें और पासपोर्ट फोटो लगाएँ।
  3. शुल्क का Demand Draft “The Director, Indira Gandhi Medical College & Research Institute, Puducherry” के नाम बनवाएँ।
  4. आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ों की स्वयं-सत्यापित प्रतियाँ जोड़ें।
  5. लिफ़ाफे पर लिखें — “APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF NURSING OFFICER, IGMCRI - 2025”
  6. रजिस्टर्ड पोस्ट या स्वयं जाकर निम्न पते पर जमा करें:
पता:
The Director,
Indira Gandhi Medical College and Research Institute,
Vazhudhavur Road, Kathirkamam,
Puducherry – 605009

आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

  • Demand Draft (Original)
  • Birth Certificate / SSLC Marksheet
  • H.Sc. Marksheets
  • Degree/Diploma Certificates
  • State Nursing Council Registration
  • Community Certificate
  • Nativity/Residence Certificate
  • EWS / Disability Certificate (यदि लागू हो)
  • Employment Exchange Card
  • COVID Duty Certificate (यदि लागू हो)

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
Official Notification & Application Form (PDF)यहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या परीक्षा होगी?

नहीं, चयन केवल मेरिट के आधार पर होगा — Academic Marks, Employment Seniority, और COVID Duty के अनुसार।

प्रश्न 2: आवेदन प्रक्रिया क्या है?

आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह Offline है। फॉर्म डाउनलोड कर भरें और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ों के साथ डाक से भेजें।

प्रश्न 3: क्या अन्य राज्यों के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

नहीं, केवल पुडुचेरी के निवासी या पिछले 5 वर्षों से रह रहे उम्मीदवार ही आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

entry.title: CSIR-NML Recruitment 2025: 05 Junior Stenographer Posts Open, Golden Opportunity for 12th Pass
entry.content:
CSIR-NML Recruitment 2025: 05 Junior Stenographer Posts Open, Golden Opportunity for 12th Pass

A prestigious government job opening at the CSIR-National Metallurgical Laboratory, Jamshedpur. Online applications are open from December 1 to December 31, 2025.

Post Name: Junior Stenographer
Last Date: 31/12/2025
Total Posts: 05

The CSIR-National Metallurgical Laboratory (NML), Jamshedpur, a premier constituent laboratory of the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) an autonomous body under the Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India has released a detailed advertisement (No: 07/2025) for recruitment to the post of Junior Stenographer. This presents a wonderful opportunity for eligible and interested Indian citizens who are seeking a stable and respected government job in one of the country's leading Research & Development organizations.

CSIR-NML is a world-class organization dedicated to research in metallurgy and materials engineering. With its state-of-the-art infrastructure and expert scientific and technical manpower, the laboratory is engaged in industrial and socially relevant research activities crucial to the national interest. Through this recruitment drive, NML aims to strengthen its administrative framework by recruiting skilled stenographers. This Group 'C' (Non-Gazetted) post offers all the benefits and allowances applicable to Central Government employees.

A total of 05 vacancies have been announced for this recruitment, which are reserved across various categories. The selection process will involve a competitive written examination and a proficiency test in stenography. Interested candidates can apply online from December 1, 2025, to December 31, 2025. In this article, we provide you with all the essential details about this recruitment, including vacancy breakdown, pay scale, educational qualifications, age limit, selection process, and how to apply.

CSIR-NML Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a concise overview of the key details of this recruitment drive. Aspirants are advised to take note of this information for a smooth application process.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization CSIR-National Metallurgical Laboratory (CSIR-NML)
Advertisement Number 07/2025
Post Name Junior Stenographer
Total Vacancies 05
Job Location Jamshedpur, Jharkhand (with a possibility of posting anywhere in India, including the Chennai center)
Application Mode Online
Online Application Dates December 1, 2025, to December 31, 2025

Vacancy Details and Reservation

CSIR-NML has advertised a total of 05 posts for Junior Stenographer. These vacancies are categorized according to the reservation rules of the Government of India.

Category Number of Posts
Unreserved (UR) 02
Economically Weaker Section (EWS) 01
Scheduled Caste (SC) 01
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 01
Total 05
* Reserved for PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities): 01 Post (under B-Blind/LV-Low Vision category)

Salary and Emoluments

  • Pay Level: Level 4 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix
  • Pay Scale: ₹ 25,500 - ₹ 81,100
  • Total Approximate Monthly Emoluments: Around ₹ 48,000/- per month in a 'Y' class city like Jamshedpur.

In addition to the basic pay, selected candidates will be entitled to other allowances such as Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Transport Allowance (TA) as per Central Government rules. They will also be covered under the National Pension System (NPS).

Eligibility Criteria: Education and Age Limit

Candidates must meet the following minimum eligibility criteria to apply for the post of Junior Stenographer. The cut-off date for determining the age limit and qualifications is the last date of online application, i.e., 31.12.2025.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Details
Essential Qualification Must have passed 10+2/XII or its equivalent examination from a recognized board.
Stenography Proficiency Proficiency in Stenography as per the norms fixed by the Department of Personnel and Training (DoPT) from time to time. This includes a dictation for 10 minutes at a speed of 80 words per minute (w.p.m), which needs to be transcribed.

Age Limit (as on 31.12.2025)

Category Minimum Age Maximum Age
All Categories 18 years 27 years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

  • SC/ST: 5 years
  • OBC (NCL): 3 years
  • Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD): 10 years (for UR), 13 years (for OBC), 15 years (for SC/ST)
  • Ex-Servicemen: 3 years after deduction of the military service rendered from the actual age.
  • Widows/Divorced Women: Up to 35 years (40 years for SC/ST).

Selection Process

The selection for the post of Junior Stenographer will be a two-stage process, consisting of a competitive written examination and a proficiency (skill) test.

  1. Competitive Written Examination:
    • The exam may be OMR-based or a Computer Based Test (CBT). It will consist of Multiple Choice Objective Type Questions.
    • The question paper will have a total of 200 questions, and the total duration of the exam will be 2 hours.
    • The medium of questions will be both English and Hindi (except for the English language part).
    • Negative Marking: There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every wrong answer.
  2. Proficiency Test in Stenography (Qualifying):
    • This test is mandatory and qualifying in nature. Its marks will not be added to the final merit list.
    • Candidates will be given a dictation for 10 minutes in English or Hindi (as opted by the candidate) at a speed of 80 w.p.m.
    • The dictated matter must be transcribed on a computer. The time allowed for transcription is:
      • For English Transcription: 50 minutes
      • For Hindi Transcription: 65 minutes
  3. Merit List Preparation: The final merit list will be prepared based only on the marks obtained by the candidates in the competitive written examination. Only those candidates who qualify in the stenography proficiency test will be considered for the merit list.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Written Examination Pattern

Part Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
I General Intelligence & Reasoning 50 50 2 Hours
(120 Minutes)
II General Awareness 50 50
III English Language & Comprehension 100 100
Total 200 200

Detailed Syllabus (Expected)

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: This section will include questions on verbal and non-verbal reasoning. Topics include analogies, similarities and differences, spatial visualization, problem-solving, analysis, judgment, decision making, visual memory, coding and decoding, statement conclusion, syllogistic reasoning, number series, non-verbal series, and classification.
  • General Awareness: Questions will be designed to test the candidate's general awareness of the environment around them and its application to society. Topics will include current events of national and international importance, history, culture, geography, economic scene, general polity, Indian Constitution, and scientific research. Special emphasis may be given to science & technology news, given the nature of the organization.
  • English Language & Comprehension: This is the most crucial section with the highest weightage. It will test the candidate's understanding of the English language. Topics include vocabulary (synonyms, antonyms, one-word substitution, idioms & phrases), grammar (error spotting, sentence improvement, fill in the blanks, active/passive voice, direct/indirect speech), spelling correction, and reading comprehension passages.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

No Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) is required for this recruitment. The selection is based purely on performance in the written examination and the stenography skill test.

How to Prepare for the CSIR-NML Exam

A systematic preparation strategy is required to succeed in the CSIR-NML Junior Stenographer examination.

  1. Preparation for the Written Examination:
    • General Intelligence & Reasoning: This section is all about practice. Solve a variety of questions from previous years' papers of similar exams (like SSC Stenographer). Focus on topics like series, coding-decoding, and analogies.
    • General Awareness: Stay updated with current affairs by reading a national newspaper daily. Refer to a standard GK book like Lucent's for static topics. Also, gain some basic knowledge about CSIR and NML.
    • English Language & Comprehension: Since this section carries 100 marks, dedicate significant time to it. Build your vocabulary daily, revise grammar rules, and practice reading comprehension passages to improve speed and accuracy.
  2. Preparation for the Stenography Proficiency Test:
    • This test is qualifying but mandatory. Do not neglect it.
    • Practice taking dictation at 80 w.p.m. regularly. Use audio dictations on various topics to get comfortable with different accents and terminologies.
    • Focus on accuracy along with speed, as there are penalties for errors.
    • Practice transcribing the dictated passage on a computer within the stipulated time. Improve your typing speed and accuracy.
  3. Mock Tests and Time Management: Solving 200 questions in 120 minutes requires excellent time management. Regularly take full-length mock tests to assess your speed and accuracy, analyze your performance, and work on your weak areas.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of highly recommended books that can guide your preparation:

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and a yearly current affairs magazine like Manorama Yearbook.
  • English Language & Comprehension: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi and "Plinth to Paramount" by Neetu Singh.
  • Stenography Practice: "Kailash Chandra Magazine" for dictation practice and any standard shorthand practice book.
  • Previous Year Papers: SSC Stenographer Grade 'C' & 'D' solved papers are an excellent resource for practice.

How to Apply for CSIR-NML Recruitment

The application process is completely online. Candidates must apply through the official CSIR-NML website.

  1. Visit the official CSIR-NML website: https://www.nml.res.in.
  2. Look for the recruitment advertisement and click on the provided link for online application (https://itapps.nmlindia.org/JRSTNG).
  3. Register yourself using your personal details, a valid email ID, and a mobile number.
  4. Fill out the online application form with your educational qualifications, experience, and other required details.
  5. Upload Documents:
    • Upload your latest passport-size color photograph (15KB to 30KB) and signature (5KB to 20KB).
    • Scan and upload all required documents (10th/12th marksheets, caste certificate, etc.) as a single PDF file (not exceeding 4.5 MB).
    • A self-handwritten declaration in the prescribed format also needs to be uploaded.
  6. Pay the Application Fee: If applicable, pay the application fee of ₹500/- through the SBI Collect portal and save the e-receipt.
  7. Final Submission: Carefully review all the details before finally submitting the application form.
  8. Take a Printout: It is mandatory to take a printout of the computer-generated application form for future reference.

Documents Required for Uploading

It is mandatory to scan and attach the following documents in a single PDF file (less than 4.5 MB) with the online application:

  • 10th/Matriculation Certificate and Marksheet (for proof of date of birth).
  • 12th/Intermediate Certificate and Marksheet.
  • Receipt of application fee payment (if applicable).
  • Self-handwritten declaration (as per the format given in the advertisement).
  • Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD), if applicable.
  • Discharge certificate / PPO or undertaking for Ex-Servicemen.
  • No Objection Certificate (NOC) if already in government service.
  • Any document related to a change in name (like a marriage certificate or gazette notification), if applicable.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (From 01.12.2025)
Official Website https://www.nml.res.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the CSIR-NML Junior Stenographer recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is December 31, 2025 (until 11:00 PM).

Question 2: What is the selection process for this post?

The selection process includes a competitive written examination (200 marks) and a qualifying stenography proficiency test.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the written exam?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer.

Question 4: What is the required speed for the stenography test?

A dictation will be given for 10 minutes at a speed of 80 words per minute for the stenography test.

Question 5: Is there an application fee exemption for female candidates?

Yes, all female candidates are exempted from the payment of the application fee.

entry.title: Sukma Recruitment 2026: District Employment & Self Employment Guidance Center
entry.content:
Sukma Recruitment 2026: District Employment & Self Employment Guidance Center

A one-day placement camp is being organized by the District Employment & Self Employment Guidance Center, Sukma (Chhattisgarh), to recruit 2000 Helpers for a manufacturing industry in Chennai, Tamil Nadu. Eligible candidates with 10th/12th/Graduate/ITI/Diploma qualifications can attend the camp on February 09, 2026.

Post Name: Helper (Production Quality Assembly)
Last Date: 09 February 2026
Total Posts: 2000

The District Employment and Self Employment Guidance Center, Sukma, Chhattisgarh, has issued a press release announcing a significant recruitment opportunity for job seekers. A private sector employer, Drive Management Services from Chennai, Tamil Nadu, is looking to hire 2000 candidates for the position of Helper (Production Quality Assembly/Associates Helper) in the Electrical/Electronics/Automobile manufacturing sector. The job location for these positions will be in Chennai, Tamil Nadu.

This placement camp is a direct recruitment drive open to both male and female candidates from Sukma and other districts. Interested candidates are required to attend the camp in person with their original documents. The selection process will be conducted on the spot, offering immediate employment opportunities to eligible individuals. The recruitment is free of cost, and candidates can secure a job with a salary ranging from ₹17,500 to ₹22,000 per month.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Candidates should note the following key details regarding the placement camp and the job offer.

Particulars Details
Organizer District Employment & Self Employment Guidance Center, Sukma
Employer Name Drive Management Services, Chennai
Post Name Helper (Production Quality Assembly)
Total Vacancies 2000
Placement Camp Date 09 February 2026
Time 11:00 AM to 03:00 PM
Venue Livelihood College, Sukma, District - Sukma (C.G.)
Job Location Chennai, Tamil Nadu
Application Mode Walk-in Interview (Placement Camp)

Vacancy Details & Pay Scale

The recruitment drive aims to fill 2000 vacancies for the Helper position. The salary offered is competitive for the entry-level role.

Post Name Total Vacancies Salary (Per Month)
Production Quality Assembly (Associates Helper) 2000 ₹17,500/- to ₹22,000/-
Total 2000
Note: The job involves working in Manufacturing Industries (Electrical / Electronics / Automobile Company).

Application Fee

  • There is NO Application Fee for participating in this placement camp. The entire process is free of cost for all candidates.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet the following educational and physical standards to be eligible for the Helper post.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification Minimum 10th Pass or 12th Pass.
Candidates with Graduation (BA/B.Sc/B.Com), ITI, or Diploma in Engineering are also eligible.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 30 Years
Physical Standards
  • Minimum Height: 145.5 cm
  • Minimum Weight: 45 Kg
Target Candidates Both Male and Female candidates from Sukma and other districts can apply.

Selection Process

The selection will be conducted through a direct placement camp.

  1. Document Verification: Candidates must present their original documents at the camp for verification.
  2. Interview/Screening: The employer (Drive Management Services) will conduct interviews or screening of candidates at the venue.
  3. Selection: Selected candidates will be offered the job on the spot or informed about the joining process for the Chennai location.

How to Apply (Walk-in Procedure)

Interested candidates do not need to apply online. They must attend the placement camp physically.

  1. Prepare a set of your documents including original certificates and photocopies.
  2. Visit the venue: Livelihood College, Sukma, District Sukma (Chhattisgarh).
  3. Report at the venue on 09 February 2026 between 11:00 AM and 03:00 PM.
  4. Register yourself at the camp and participate in the selection process conducted by the private employer.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must bring the following documents (Original and Photocopies) to the placement camp:

  1. Educational Qualification Certificates (10th/12th/ITI/Diploma/Degree Marksheets).
  2. Domicile Certificate (Niwas Praman Patra).
  3. Caste Certificate (Jati Praman Patra), if applicable.
  4. Aadhaar Card.
  5. Employment Registration Card (Rojgar Panjiyan).
  6. Passport Size Photographs.
  7. Any other relevant documents.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Who can apply for this placement camp?

Candidates from Sukma and other districts who have passed 10th, 12th, Graduation, ITI, or Diploma and are between 18-30 years of age can apply.

Q2: Is there any fee to attend the camp?

No, the placement camp is completely free of cost for all candidates.

Q3: What is the job location?

The selected candidates will be posted in Chennai, Tamil Nadu.

entry.title: NEEPCO भर्ती 2025: GATE 2025 के जरिए Executive Trainee के 30 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!
entry.content:
NEEPCO भर्ती 2025: GATE 2025 के जरिए Executive Trainee के 30 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!

North Eastern Electric Power Corporation Limited (NEEPCO) ने GATE 2025 स्कोर के आधार पर Electrical, Mechanical, Civil और IT शाखाओं में Executive Trainee के पदों के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं।

इंजीनियरिंग ग्रेजुएट्स के लिए शानदार मौका! North Eastern Electric Power Corporation Limited (NEEPCO), जो कि NTPC की सहायक कंपनी और एक Mini Ratna PSU है, ने Executive Trainee (E-2 Grade) के पदों पर भर्ती का नोटिफिकेशन जारी किया है।

यह भर्ती पूरी तरह GATE 2025 स्कोर पर आधारित होगी। इस अभियान के तहत Electrical, Mechanical, Civil और IT शाखाओं में कुल 30 पद भरे जाएंगे। चयनित उम्मीदवारों को ₹50,000 – ₹1,60,000 के आकर्षक पे-स्केल पर नियुक्त किया जाएगा।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन शुरू होने की तिथि28 October 2025
आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि17 November 2025

रिक्तियाँ और आयु सीमा (01-07-2025 तक)

पदकुल पदUR/EWSOBCSC/ST
Executive Trainee (Electrical/Mechanical)1830 वर्ष33 वर्ष35 वर्ष
Executive Trainee (Civil)1030 वर्ष33 वर्ष35 वर्ष
Executive Trainee (IT)0230 वर्ष-35 वर्ष

आवेदन शुल्क

  • General / EWS / OBC (NCL): ₹560/-
  • SC / ST / PwBD / ESM / महिला उम्मीदवार: शुल्क नहीं देना होगा।

शुल्क का भुगतान SBI ePay के माध्यम से ऑनलाइन किया जाएगा।

पात्रता मानदंड

  • GATE आवश्यक: उम्मीदवार को GATE 2025 परीक्षा में शामिल होकर क्वालिफाई करना अनिवार्य है।
  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता:
    • Electrical/Mechanical: BE/B.Tech/AMIE में Electrical या Mechanical शाखा में कम से कम 65% अंक (SC/ST के लिए 55%)।
    • Civil: Civil Engineering में BE/B.Tech/AMIE में कम से कम 65% अंक (SC/ST/PwBD के लिए 55%)।
    • IT: Computer Science या IT शाखा में BE/B.Tech/AMIE में कम से कम 65% अंक (SC के लिए 55%)।
  • GATE Paper: संबंधित शाखा के GATE पेपर में क्वालिफाई होना आवश्यक है (उदा. Electrical के लिए “EE” पेपर)।

चयन प्रक्रिया और वेतन

Selection: इस भर्ती में कोई Interview या Group Discussion नहीं होगा। चयन केवल GATE 2025 स्कोर के आधार पर किया जाएगा।

Salary: चयनित उम्मीदवारों को E-2 Grade में ₹50,000 – 3% – ₹1,60,000 (IDA Pay Scale) पर रखा जाएगा। प्रारंभिक वेतन लगभग ₹1,00,000 प्रति माह (सभी भत्तों सहित) होगा। सफलतापूर्वक 1 वर्ष की ट्रेनिंग के बाद उम्मीदवारों को E-3 Grade में प्रमोट किया जाएगा।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. Step 1: आधिकारिक वेबसाइट www.neepco.co.in पर जाएं और “Careers” सेक्शन में जाएं।
  2. Step 2: Advertisement No. NEEPCO/09/2025 को ध्यान से पढ़ें।
  3. Step 3: यदि आप General/EWS/OBC वर्ग से हैं, तो पहले आवेदन शुल्क जमा करें।
  4. Step 4: आवेदन फॉर्म में अपनी व्यक्तिगत, शैक्षणिक और GATE 2025 से संबंधित जानकारी भरें।
  5. Step 5: आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ (Marksheet, GATE Scorecard, Caste Certificate, Photo, Signature) अपलोड करें।
  6. Step 6: फॉर्म सबमिट करें और Registration Slip का प्रिंटआउट निकाल लें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
NEEPCO की आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: क्या GATE 2025 जरूरी है?
हाँ, यह भर्ती केवल GATE 2025 स्कोर के आधार पर होगी।
Q2: क्या Final Year के छात्र आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, केवल वे उम्मीदवार जिनका अंतिम परिणाम घोषित हो चुका है, वही आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
Q3: क्या इंटरव्यू होगा?
नहीं, चयन प्रक्रिया में कोई इंटरव्यू या ग्रुप डिस्कशन नहीं होगा।

entry.title: UPPSC Technical Education (Teaching) Service Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for 513 Lecturer & Workshop Superintendent Posts
entry.content:
UPPSC Technical Education (Teaching) Service Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for 513 Lecturer & Workshop Superintendent Posts

A golden opportunity for engineering graduates and postgraduates to secure a gazetted teaching position in Uttar Pradesh. Online applications are open from December 2, 2025, to January 2, 2026.

Post Name: Lecturer (Various Disciplines), Workshop Superintendent
Last Date: 02/01/2026
Total Posts: 513

The Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC), Prayagraj, has released a detailed advertisement (No: A-11/E-1/2025) for recruitment to Group 'B' Gazetted posts within the Technical Education Department of Uttar Pradesh. This recruitment will be conducted through the U.P. Technical Education (Teaching) Service Examination-2025, aiming to fill a total of 513 vacancies in government polytechnics and other institutions. This is a golden opportunity for qualified and talented engineering and post-graduate candidates who aspire to build a career in the prestigious field of teaching in Uttar Pradesh.

This recruitment drive will appoint Lecturers in various engineering and non-engineering disciplines, as well as Workshop Superintendents. These positions not only offer an attractive pay scale and the security of a government job but also provide a significant opportunity to shape the future of technical education in the state. UPPSC, known for its fair and transparent selection processes, will conduct a written examination followed by an interview for this recruitment. The application process is entirely online, and candidates must apply through UPPSC's One Time Registration (OTR) platform. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all important information related to this recruitment, including post-wise vacancies, salary, eligibility, selection process, and application procedure.

UPPSC Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other key information to ensure a smooth application process. This is a state government gazetted job opportunity located in Uttar Pradesh.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Uttar Pradesh Public Service Commission (UPPSC)
Examination Name U.P. Technical Education (Teaching) Service Examination-2025
Application Start Date December 2, 2025
Last Date for Fee Payment & Application Submission January 2, 2026
Job Type State Government Gazetted Job (Group 'B')
Job Location Uttar Pradesh

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

UPPSC has announced a total of 513 vacancies under this recruitment. These vacancies are distributed across various subjects and reservation categories. A detailed subject-wise and category-wise breakdown of vacancies (as provided in Appendix-4 of the notification) is given below:

Sr. No. Subject Name Total Posts UR SC ST OBC EWS Women PH
1 Mechanical Engineering 140 48 39 02 37 14 28 05
2 Electrical Engineering 52 12 15 00 20 05 10 02
3 Civil Engineering 62 20 11 02 23 06 12 02
4 Electronics Engineering 77 18 21 00 31 07 15 03
5 Chemical Engineering 14 05 03 00 05 01 02 00
6 Computer 43 14 11 00 14 04 08 01
7 Paint Technology 03 03 00 00 00 00 00 00
8 Plastic Mould Technology 01 01 00 00 00 00 00 00
9 Textile Technology 04 00 01 00 03 00 00 00
10 Workshop Superintendent 10 05 01 00 03 01 02 00
11 Architecture 06 02 01 00 03 00 01 00
12 Physics 24 11 06 00 05 02 04 00
13 Chemistry 21 11 04 00 04 02 04 00
14 English 18 08 03 00 06 01 03 00
15 Mathematics 32 15 06 00 08 03 06 01
- Other Subjects (Total) 07 (Refer to notification for details)
Grand Total 513 176 124 04 163 46 95 14

Application Fee

Candidates must pay the prescribed fee online during the application process as per their category.

Category Exam Fee + Online Processing Fee Total Fee
Unreserved / EWS / OBC ₹ 200 + ₹ 25 ₹ 225/-
SC / ST ₹ 80 + ₹ 25 ₹ 105/-
Persons with Disabilities (PH) ₹ 0 + ₹ 25 ₹ 25/-
Ex-Servicemen ₹ 80 + ₹ 25 ₹ 105/-

Eligibility Criteria: Education and Age Limit

Specific educational qualifications have been prescribed for each post. Candidates must ensure that they possess all the required qualifications by the last date of application (January 2, 2026).

Educational Qualification

Post Name Qualification
Lecturer (Engineering & Technical Branches) B.E./B.Tech./B.S. degree in the relevant discipline with First Class. (The relevant discipline will be as per the list of AICTE approved diploma courses).
Lecturer (Architecture) B.Arch. or a 4-year degree in the relevant field with First Class.
Workshop Superintendent Bachelor's or Master's degree in Mechanical Engineering with First Class.
Lecturer (Non-Engineering Branches - Physics, Chemistry, English, Maths) Master's Degree in the relevant subject with First Class.
Note: If no class/division is awarded in a degree, 60% marks will be considered equivalent to First Class. The formula provided in the advertisement will be used to convert CGPA to percentage.

Age Limit (as on July 1, 2025)

Category Minimum Age Maximum Age
General (Unreserved) 21 years 40 years
PH (Disabled) Candidates 21 years 55 years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

  • For SC, ST, OBC, Skilled Players, and State Govt. Employees of Uttar Pradesh: 5 years.
  • For Persons with Disabilities (PH) of Uttar Pradesh: 15 years.
  • For Ex-Army Personnels of Uttar Pradesh: 3 years + length of service in the army.

Selection Process

The selection for the U.P. Technical Education (Teaching) Service Examination will be completed in two stages:

  1. Written Examination:
    • A written test will be conducted for all eligible candidates.
    • The examination will consist of two papers, both of which will be of the objective (MCQ) type.
    • Negative Marking: For each incorrect answer, one-third (0.33) of the marks assigned to that question will be deducted.
    • Minimum Qualifying Marks: 35% for SC/ST candidates and 40% for all other categories. Candidates scoring below these marks will not be considered for the merit list.
  2. Interview:
    • Based on the results of the written examination, candidates equal to three times the number of vacancies will be called for an interview.
    • The interview will carry a total of 100 marks.

The final selection list will be prepared based on the total marks obtained in the written examination and the interview.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The written examination is the cornerstone of the selection process. It is divided into two papers, each carrying significant weightage.

Written Examination Pattern

Paper Subject Questions Marks Total Marks Duration
Paper - I 1. General Hindi 25 75 375 2.5 Hours
2. Main Subject - I 100 300
Paper - II 1. General Studies 25 75 375 2.5 Hours
2. Main Subject - II 100 300
Interview 100 -
Grand Total 850 -

Syllabus Outline

  • General Hindi (Paper I): The syllabus will be of High School standard, covering Hindi grammar, synonyms, antonyms, idioms and phrases, vocabulary, and unseen passages.
  • General Studies (Paper II): This section will cover topics of general importance, including the History of India, Indian National Movement, Indian and World Geography, Indian Polity and Economy, Current National and International Important Events, Indian Agriculture, General Science, and basic Elementary Mathematics up to Class 10th level.
  • Main Subject (Paper I & II): The syllabus for the main subject (e.g., Mechanical Engineering, Physics, etc.) will be comprehensive and based on the graduate/post-graduate level curriculum. The detailed syllabus for each subject is provided in Appendix-3 of the official notification.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for these teaching posts. The selection is based entirely on the candidate's performance in the written examination and interview.

How to Prepare for the UPPSC Lecturer Exam

Success in this prestigious examination requires dedicated and strategic preparation. Here is a comprehensive guide to help you formulate your study plan.

  1. Analyze the Syllabus Thoroughly: The first and most crucial step is to download the detailed syllabus for your subject from the commission's website (Appendix-3 of the notification) and analyze it in depth.
  2. Strengthen Your Core Subject (750 Marks):
    • The main subject carries the highest weightage in the selection. Conduct a thorough study of your standard graduate and postgraduate level textbooks.
    • Solve previous years' question papers from GATE, ESE (IES), and other state engineering service examinations. This will help you understand the pattern and difficulty level of objective questions.
    • Create concise notes of important formulas, concepts, and definitions for quick revision.
  3. Prepare for General Studies and General Hindi (150 Marks):
    • General Studies: This includes the history, geography, polity, and constitution of India, as well as current events of national and international importance. Read standard books and follow newspapers and current affairs magazines regularly.
    • General Hindi: This will be of High School standard. Focus on topics like Hindi grammar, synonyms, antonyms, phrases, and unseen passages.
  4. Time Management and Practice:
    • Regularly take mock tests to practice solving all questions within the given time limit.
    • Focus on accuracy to avoid negative marking. Try to skip questions you are not sure about.
  5. Interview Preparation:
    • After the written exam, focus on your subject, current affairs, and your perspective on teaching. Work on your communication skills and confidence. Be prepared to discuss your projects and practical knowledge.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books that can aid your preparation for the general papers and guide your technical study.

  • General Studies:
    • "Indian Polity" by M. Laxmikanth
    • "A Brief History of Modern India" by Spectrum Books
    • "Certificate Physical and Human Geography" by G.C. Leong
    • A standard yearbook like Manorama for current affairs.
    • "General Knowledge" by Lucent Publications.
  • General Hindi:
    • "Samanya Hindi" by Hardev Bahri or a similar standard book for competitive exams.
  • Main Subject (Engineering/Technical):
    • Standard textbooks from your B.E./B.Tech. curriculum.
    • Objective question banks from publishers like Made Easy, ACE Engineering Academy, or Khanna Publishers.
    • Previous years' solved papers of GATE and ESE (IES) are indispensable.

How to Apply for UPPSC Recruitment

The application process is completely online and requires a One Time Registration (OTR) number.

  1. One Time Registration (OTR):
    • If you have not yet obtained an OTR number, first visit the commission's OTR website: https://otr.pariksha.nic.in and complete your OTR registration.
    • It is mandatory to obtain the OTR number at least 72 hours before submitting the application.
  2. Online Application:
    • After obtaining the OTR number, visit the official UPPSC website: https://uppsc.up.nic.in.
    • Click on the "ALL NOTIFICATIONS/ADVERTISEMENTS" link.
    • Click the "Apply" button against the relevant advertisement (A-11/E-1/2025).
    • Authenticate with your OTR number. Your personal details will be auto-populated from the OTR.
    • Fill in only the details regarding the essential and preferential qualifications required for the post.
    • Pay the application fee online.
    • Finally, submit the application form and take a printout for future reference.

Last Date: The last date for paying the examination fee in the bank and submitting the online application is January 2, 2026.

Documents Needed to Apply and for Verification

Candidates do not need to upload any documents while applying online but must keep self-attested copies of the following documents ready for submission when required by the commission or at the time of the interview:

  1. Printout of the online application form.
  2. High School (10th) certificate (for proof of date of birth).
  3. Marksheets and degree certificates for all years/semesters of all essential educational qualifications (B.E./B.Tech./M.E./M.Tech./M.Sc., etc.).
  4. Caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) in the prescribed format issued by the Government of Uttar Pradesh for claiming reservation.
  5. Domicile certificate of Uttar Pradesh (to avail reservation benefits).
  6. Certificates for Dependents of Freedom Fighters, PH, and Ex-Servicemen (if applicable).
  7. No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the competent authority for government employees.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (From 02.12.2025)
One Time Registration (OTR) Click Here
Official Website https://uppsc.up.nic.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for this recruitment?

The last date to pay the online application fee and submit the form is January 2, 2026.

Question 2: Is OTR mandatory for applying?

Yes, obtaining a One Time Registration (OTR) number is mandatory before applying for this recruitment.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection process consists of two stages: a written examination (two papers, 750 marks total) and an interview (100 marks).

Question 4: Is there negative marking in this exam?

Yes, there will be a deduction of 0.33 (one-third) marks for every incorrect answer in the written examination.

Question 5: Can candidates from other states avail reservation benefits?

No, the benefits of reservation and age relaxation are only available to candidates who are domiciles of Uttar Pradesh. Candidates from other states will be considered under the Unreserved (General) category.

entry.title: UP Police Constable Recruitment 2025: Bumper 32,679 Vacancies Out
entry.content:
UP Police Constable Recruitment 2025: Bumper 32,679 Vacancies Out

A landmark recruitment opportunity unfolds as the Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB) announces a massive drive for 32,679 posts of Constable and equivalent roles. Online applications start from December 31, 2025. Get all details on vacancies, eligibility, selection process, physical standards, and how to apply.

Post Name: Constable Civil Police, Constable PAC, Constable Special Security Force, Jail Warder, and more
Last Date: 30 January 2026
Total Posts: 32679

The Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB) has released a much-awaited notification, bringing monumental news for government job aspirants. Under notification number पीआरपीबी-बी (आरक्षी पीएसी एवं अन्य संवर्ग)-07/2025, the board has invited online applications for a staggering 32,679 vacancies for the posts of Constable and its equivalent ranks in various wings of the state police force. This recruitment drive is one of the largest in recent years and presents a golden chance for the youth of the state to join the prestigious Uttar Pradesh Police force.

The vacancies are spread across several key departments, including Civil Police, Provincial Armed Constabulary (PAC), Special Security Force, Women Battalion, Horse Rider Police, and Jail Warder. The application process is completely online and is scheduled to commence on December 31, 2025. Interested candidates who meet the eligibility criteria are urged to prepare their documents and get ready to apply. This article provides a comprehensive guide covering all aspects of the recruitment, including detailed vacancy breakdown, eligibility norms, selection procedure, exam pattern, physical standards, and a step-by-step application guide.

Important Points

Candidates must carefully note the key details and timelines associated with this recruitment process. This is a government job opportunity exclusively within the state of Uttar Pradesh.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Uttar Pradesh Police Recruitment and Promotion Board (UPPRPB)
Post Names Constable Civil Police, Constable PAC, Jail Warder & Other Equivalent Posts
Advertisement Number PRPB-B(Constable PAC & Others)-07/2025
Total Vacancies 32,679
Application Start Date 31st December 2025
Last Date to Apply Online 30th January 2026
Last Date for Fee Payment 30th January 2026
Pay Scale Pay Level-3 (₹21,700 - ₹69,100)
Mode of Application Online
Job Type State Government Job
Job Location Uttar Pradesh
Official Website www.uppbpb.gov.in

Vacancy Details

The recruitment is for a total of 32,679 posts distributed among various wings. The detailed post-wise vacancy breakdown is as follows:

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Constable Civil Police (Male/Female) 10,469
Constable PAC / Armed Police (Male Only) 15,131
Constable Special Security Force (Male Only) 1,341
Female Constable for Women Battalion (Female Only) 2,282
Constable Horse Rider Police (Male Only) 71
Jail Warder (Male) 3,279
Jail Warder (Female) 106
Total 32,679
Note: The number of vacancies is subject to change. Reservation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, and other categories will be applicable as per the prevailing rules of the Uttar Pradesh government.

Application Fee

  • For General / EWS / Other Backward Class (OBC) candidates: ₹500/-
  • For Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) candidates: ₹400/-

The application fee must be paid online through Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, or UPI. The fee is non-refundable.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill the following eligibility criteria concerning nationality, education, and age to be considered for the posts. The crucial date for determining the age limit is July 1, 2025.

Criteria Requirement Details
Nationality The candidate must be a citizen of India.
Educational Qualification Candidates must have passed the 12th Class (Intermediate) examination from a board established by law in India or any examination recognized by the government as its equivalent. Candidates who are appearing or have appeared for the exam but whose results are not yet declared are NOT eligible.
Age Limit (Male) Candidates must have attained the age of 18 years and must not have attained the age of 22 years as of July 1, 2025. This means the candidate should not have been born before July 2, 2003, and not after July 1, 2007.
Age Limit (Female) Candidates must have attained the age of 18 years and must not have attained the age of 25 years as of July 1, 2025. This means the candidate should not have been born before July 2, 2000, and not after July 1, 2007.
Age Relaxation Relaxation in the upper age limit will be provided to candidates belonging to SC, ST, OBC, and other categories who are original residents (domicile) of Uttar Pradesh, as per the government rules.
Preferential Qualification In case of a tie in marks, preference will be given to candidates possessing: (i) DOEACC/NIELIT Society 'O' level certificate in computer, OR (ii) Served in the Territorial Army for a minimum period of two years, OR (iii) Obtained 'B' certificate of the National Cadet Corps (NCC).

Selection Process

The selection process for the UP Police Constable and equivalent posts is a multi-stage process designed to test the candidate's knowledge, physical fitness, and medical suitability. The key stages are:

  1. Written Examination: An offline, OMR-based written test will be conducted. This is the primary stage for shortlisting candidates.
  2. Document Verification (DV) and Physical Standard Test (PST): Candidates who qualify the written exam will be called for document verification and measurement of physical standards (height, chest). This stage is qualifying in nature.
  3. Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Candidates who clear the DV & PST stage will have to undergo a Physical Efficiency Test, which includes a race. This is also qualifying in nature.
  4. Final Merit List: The final selection list will be prepared based on the marks obtained by the candidates in the Written Examination, subject to their qualifying in all subsequent stages.

Exam Pattern & Syllabus

The written examination is the most critical part of the selection process. Candidates must be thoroughly familiar with its pattern and syllabus.

Exam Pattern:

  • Mode of Exam: Offline (OMR Answer Sheet Based)
  • Type of Questions: Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs)
  • Total Questions: 150
  • Total Marks: 300
  • Marks per Question: 2 marks for each correct answer.
  • Negative Marking: The notification specifies a formula for negative marking, which implies that marks will be deducted for wrong answers. Candidates should attempt questions carefully.
  • Duration: 2 Hours (120 Minutes)
  • Normalization: If the exam is conducted in multiple shifts or on multiple dates, a normalization process will be applied to ensure fairness.

Syllabus: The written test will cover four main subjects:

  1. General Knowledge: General Science, History of India, Indian Constitution, Indian Economy & Culture, Indian Agriculture, Commerce & Trade, Population, Environment & Urbanization, Geography of India & World, Natural Resources, UP Specific knowledge (Education, Culture, Social Practices), Revenue, Police & General Administrative System, Human Rights, Internal Security & Terrorism, India and its Neighbouring Countries, Current Affairs (National & International), Important Organizations, Cyber Crime, Awards and Honors, Books and Authors, etc.
  2. General Hindi: Hindi and other Indian Languages, Hindi Grammar basics (Varnamala, Tatsam-Tadbhav, Paryayavachi, Vilom, Ling, Vachan, Karak, Sarvanam, Visheshan, Kriya, Kaal, etc.), Comprehension, Famous Poets, Writers and their works, Awards in Hindi language, Miscellaneous.
    3. .
  3. Numerical & Mental Ability:
