    • Numerical Ability: Number System, Simplification, Decimals and Fraction, HCF & LCM, Ratio and Proportion, Percentage, Profit and Loss, Discount, Simple & Compound Interest, Partnership, Average, Time and Work, Time and Distance, Use of Tables and Graphs, Mensuration.
    • Mental Ability: Logical Diagrams, Symbol-Relationship Interpretation, Perception Test, Word Formation, Letter and Number Series, Common Sense Test, Logical Interpretation of Data, Forcefulness of Argument.
  4. Mental Aptitude, I.Q. and Reasoning Ability:
    • Mental Aptitude: Public Interest, Law and Order, Communal Harmony, Crime Control, Rule of Law, Ability of Adaptability, Professional Information, Police System, Contemporary Police Issues.
    • I.Q.: Relationship and Analogy Test, Spotting out the dissimilar, Series Completion, Coding-Decoding, Blood Relation, Venn Diagram, Arranging in order.
    • Reasoning Ability: Analogies, Similarities, Differences, Space visualization, Problem solving, Analysis judgment, Decision-making, Visual memory, Arithmetical reasoning.

Physical Standard Test (PST)

Candidates must meet the following minimum physical standards to be eligible for the next stage. These measurements will be taken during the Document Verification process.

Category Gender Height Chest
General / OBC / SC Male 168 cm 79 cm (un-expanded)
84 cm (expanded)
Female 152 cm Not Applicable
Scheduled Tribe (ST) Male 160 cm 77 cm (un-expanded)
82 cm (expanded)
Female 147 cm Not Applicable

Note: A minimum chest expansion of 5 cm is mandatory for all male candidates. For female candidates, a minimum weight of 40 kg is required.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

This test is qualifying in nature and is conducted to assess the physical endurance of the candidates. The requirements are as follows:

Gender Task Time Limit
Male 4.8 Km Race Within 25 Minutes
Female 2.4 Km Race Within 14 Minutes

Candidates who fail to complete the race within the stipulated time will be disqualified from the recruitment process.

How to Prepare

Given the high number of vacancies and the expected competition, a dedicated preparation strategy is crucial.

  • Understand the Syllabus: Begin by thoroughly understanding the detailed syllabus for each section of the written exam.
  • Create a Study Plan: Allocate dedicated time for each subject. Focus more on your weaker areas but ensure regular revision of all topics.
  • Focus on Physical Fitness: Do not wait for the written exam results to start preparing for the PET. Incorporate running and physical exercise into your daily routine to build stamina and endurance.
  • Practice Previous Year Papers: Solving previous years' question papers of UP Police exams will give you an excellent idea of the exam pattern, difficulty level, and important topics.
  • Take Mock Tests: Regularly attempt online and offline mock tests to improve your speed, accuracy, and time management skills.
  • Stay Updated: Pay special attention to current affairs, especially those related to Uttar Pradesh, national events, and important government schemes.

Best Books for Preparation

While there are many resources available, here are some standard books that can be beneficial for preparation:

  • General Knowledge: Lucent's General Knowledge, and a dedicated book on Uttar Pradesh GK (like घटना चक्र or परीक्षा वाणी).
  • General Hindi: 'Samanya Hindi' by Lucent Publication or 'Adhunik Hindi Vyakaran aur Rachna' by Dr. Vasudevnandan Prasad.
  • Numerical & Mental Ability: 'Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations' by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Reasoning Ability: 'A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning' by R.S. Aggarwal.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must first complete the One Time Registration (OTR) process.

  1. Visit the official website of UPPRPB: www.uppbpb.gov.in.
  2. Find the link for "Constable Recruitment-2025" and click on it.
  3. You will be redirected to the application portal. First, complete the One Time Registration (OTR) by providing your basic details, email, and mobile number.
  4. After OTR, log in with your credentials and fill out the main application form.
  5. Provide your personal, educational, and communication details accurately.
  6. You will be required to provide a Real-time (Live) Photograph through your device's webcam. Ensure good lighting and a plain background.
  7. Upload a scanned copy of your signature in the specified format.
  8. Upload other necessary documents (educational, caste, domicile certificates), preferably through the DigiLocker facility integrated with the portal.
  9. Provide your preference for the various posts available. This is a mandatory step.
  10. Proceed to pay the application fee through the online payment gateway.
  11. After successful payment, preview your application form one last time and submit it.
  12. Download and print the final submitted application form for your future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep the following documents ready before you start the application process:

  1. A working Webcam for the live photograph.
  2. Scanned image of your signature.
  3. Mobile number and Email ID (to be used for OTR and communication).
  4. Aadhaar Card for authentication.
  5. DigiLocker account (recommended for easy document upload).
  6. Educational Qualification certificates and mark sheets (10th & 12th).
  7. Valid Caste Certificate (for reserved category candidates from UP).
  8. EWS Certificate (if applicable, valid for the financial year 2025-26).
  9. Domicile Certificate (for UP residents seeking reservation benefits).
  10. Certificate for preferential qualification (NCC, Territorial Army), if any.

Important Links

Link Description URL
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website Click Here

FAQs

Q1: What is the total number of vacancies in the UP Police Recruitment 2025?

A total of 32,679 vacancies have been announced for Constable and equivalent posts.

Q2: What is the last date to apply for this recruitment?

The last date for submitting the online application and paying the fee is January 30, 2026.

Q3: Can candidates from other states apply for this recruitment?

Yes, candidates from other states can apply. However, they will be considered under the Unreserved (General) category and will not be eligible for any reservation benefits like age relaxation.

Q4: What is the minimum educational qualification required?

The candidate must have passed the 12th Class (Intermediate) from a recognized board.

Q5: Is there negative marking in the written exam?

Yes, the notification mentions that there will be negative marking for incorrect answers in the written examination.

entry.title: CSC आधार सुपरवाइज़र भर्ती 2025: 12वीं पास के लिए सरकारी नौकरी, सभी राज्यों में भर्ती!
entry.content:
CSC आधार सुपरवाइज़र भर्ती 2025: 12वीं पास के लिए सरकारी नौकरी, सभी राज्यों में भर्ती!

CSC ई-गवर्नेंस सर्विसेज इंडिया लिमिटेड ने पूरे भारत में आधार सुपरवाइज़र/ऑपरेटर के पदों के लिए अनुबंध (Contract) पर आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं।

12वीं पास युवाओं के लिए सरकारी प्रोजेक्ट में काम करने का सुनहरा मौका! CSC e-Governance Services India Limited ने पूरे भारत के 23 से अधिक राज्यों में Aadhaar Supervisor/Operator पदों पर भर्ती की अधिसूचना जारी की है। ये नियुक्तियाँ Aadhaar Seva Kendra (ASK) में एक वर्ष के अनुबंध पर होंगी।

यह भर्ती आंध्र प्रदेश, बिहार, छत्तीसगढ़, गुजरात, हरियाणा, झारखंड, कर्नाटक, केरल, मध्य प्रदेश, महाराष्ट्र, उत्तर प्रदेश सहित कई राज्यों में जिला स्तर पर की जा रही है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
नोटिफिकेशन जारी07 October 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि11 November 2025

पात्रता शर्तें

मानदंडआवश्यक योग्यता
आयु सीमाकम से कम 18 वर्ष या उससे अधिक।
शैक्षणिक योग्यता
  • 12वीं पास (Intermediate) या समकक्ष।
  • या 10वीं + 2 वर्ष का ITI कोर्स।
  • या 10वीं + 3 वर्ष का Polytechnic Diploma।
आधार सर्टिफिकेटUIDAI द्वारा मान्यता प्राप्त संस्था से जारी Aadhaar Operator/Supervisor Certificate अनिवार्य है।
कंप्यूटर ज्ञानबेसिक कंप्यूटर ज्ञान आवश्यक है।
ध्यान दें: Village Level Entrepreneurs (VLEs) इस पद के लिए आवेदन करने के पात्र नहीं हैं।

वेतनमान

चयनित उम्मीदवारों को संबंधित राज्य के semi-skilled manpower के लिए निर्धारित न्यूनतम मजदूरी के अनुसार वेतन मिलेगा।

अगर आपके पास Aadhaar Supervisor Certificate नहीं है?

अगर आपके पास अभी UIDAI का सर्टिफिकेट नहीं है, तो आप नीचे दिए गए चरणों से इसे प्राप्त कर सकते हैं:

  1. सबसे पहले https://uidai.nseitexams.com/UIDAI/LoginAction_input.action पर जाकर रजिस्ट्रेशन करें।
  2. आपका अनुरोध राज्य टीम को जाएगा। स्वीकृति मिलने के बाद आप परीक्षा दे पाएंगे।
  3. परीक्षा पास करने के बाद आपको LMS (Learning Management System) लॉगिन आईडी और पासवर्ड मिलेगा।
  4. LMS परीक्षा के लिए https://e-learning.uidai.gov.in पर लॉगिन करें।

यह सर्टिफिकेट प्राप्त करने के बाद ही आप इस पद के लिए पात्र बनेंगे।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. CSC की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://cscspsv.in/job.html पर जाएँ।
  2. अपने राज्य के सामने "Apply Now" पर क्लिक करें।
  3. अपने जिले का चयन करें और फॉर्म भरें।
  4. सभी आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करके आवेदन सबमिट करें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकक्रिया
आधिकारिक जॉब पोर्टलयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधार सुपरवाइज़र परीक्षा पोर्टलयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: कौन आवेदन कर सकता है?
कोई भी भारतीय नागरिक जो 18 वर्ष से ऊपर है, 12वीं पास है और जिसके पास मान्य Aadhaar Supervisor सर्टिफिकेट है।
Q2: अगर सर्टिफिकेट नहीं है तो?
पहले UIDAI परीक्षा पास करें और सर्टिफिकेट प्राप्त करें, तभी आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
Q3: यह नौकरी स्थायी है या अस्थायी?
यह नौकरी एक वर्ष के अनुबंध पर होगी।
Q4: VLEs आवेदन क्यों नहीं कर सकते?
CSC के नियमों के अनुसार VLE उम्मीदवार इस भर्ती के पात्र नहीं हैं।

entry.title: Gujarat University Recruitment 2025: Administrative Posts Open
entry.content:
Gujarat University Recruitment 2025: Various Administrative Posts Open

Gujarat University, Ahmedabad invites online applications for numerous Group A, B, and C non-teaching positions. Apply online from December 29, 2025.

Post Name: Registrar, Director, Librarian, Engineer, Clerk, and various PwD posts
Last Date: 13 January 2026
Total Posts: 100+ (across various advertisements)

Gujarat University, Ahmedabad, a premier institution accredited with a Grade 'A+' by NAAC, has released a major employment notification (No.GU/Admin/2025-26) for various administrative posts. The university invites online applications in the prescribed proforma from qualified Indian citizens to fill up these non-teaching positions. This recruitment is being conducted in accordance with the approvals from the Education Department, Government of Gujarat, and under the provisions of "The Gujarat Public Universities Act-2023".

This is a massive recruitment drive that includes a wide range of posts from senior administrative roles like Registrar and Director to clerical and technical positions like Junior Clerk and Laboratory Assistant. The recruitment also includes a special drive for Persons with Disabilities (PwD), covering posts from Deputy Registrar to Jr. Clerk. This presents a golden opportunity for candidates with diverse qualifications to secure a stable and respected career in one of Gujarat's leading universities. The application process is entirely online through the university's recruitment portal, with the link being activated on **December 29, 2025**. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all the essential details of this recruitment process.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government positions in a State Public University located in Ahmedabad, Gujarat. Key dates and details are as follows:

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Gujarat University, Ahmedabad
Job Type Permanent Government Job (State University)
Job Location Ahmedabad, Gujarat
Online Application Start Date 29 December 2025
Last Date for Online Application 13 January 2026 (up to 05:00 PM)

Vacancy Details

The university has announced numerous vacancies across Group A, B, and C, including a special drive for PwD candidates. The details are as follows:

General Administrative Posts (ADMIN-01 to ADMIN-23)

Adv. No. Name of the Posts No. of Posts Category Pay Level
ADMIN-01 Registrar 1 UR 14
ADMIN-06 Librarian 1 UR 11
ADMIN-08 Assistant Registrar 1 ST 10
ADMIN-10 University Engineer 1 UR 10
ADMIN-14 Deputy Engineer (Civil) 1 UR 7
ADMIN-21 Senior Clerk 7 Multi-category 4
ADMIN-22 Junior Clerk 84 Multi-category 2
ADMIN-23 Laboratory Assistant 2 UR-01, SEBC-01 2

PwD Posts (PH-ADMIN-24 TO PH-ADMIN-36)

Adv. No. Name of the Posts No. of Posts Category Pay Level
PH-ADMIN-24 Deputy Registrar 1 (PH) 01 (B,LV) PML-7
PH-ADMIN-26 Warden (Hostel) 1 (PH) 01 (LV) PML-7
PH-ADMIN-28 Stenographer Gr.II 1 (PH) 01 (B,LV) PML-6
PH-ADMIN-33 Jr. Clerk 4 (PH) Multi-disability PML-2
Note: This is a summary of key posts. The full advertisement details vacancies for over 36 different post types. Many posts are on a fixed pay for the first five years as per Gujarat Government norms.

Application Fee

  • For General category candidates: Rs. 750/- (Non-refundable) for each post.
  • For candidates belonging to SC/ST/SEBC/EWS categories: Rs. 500/- (Non-refundable) for each post.
  • For PwD candidates, the processing fee is NIL.

Payment is to be made online only. Separate applications and fees are required for each post a candidate wishes to apply for.

Eligibility Criteria

The eligibility criteria vary significantly for each post. Candidates must possess the required educational qualifications on the last date of online application submission. A summary for some key posts is provided below:

Post Name Eligibility Requirements (Qualification, Experience, Age)
Registrar Master’s Degree with at least 55% marks and at least 15 years of experience as an Assistant Professor or 8 years as an Associate Professor with experience in educational administration. Age: Preferably below 57 years.
Librarian Master's Degree in Library Science/Information Science with at least 55% marks, a Ph.D. in the same field, and at least ten years of experience as a Librarian at any level in a University Library. Age: Preferably below 57 years.
Assistant Registrar Master’s Degree with at least 55% marks and three years of experience in an educational/research institution. Age: 40 years.
Senior/Junior Clerk Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized university. For Senior Clerk, experience is required. For both, knowledge of computer applications is essential. Age: Varies (e.g., 35 years for Junior Clerk).
Laboratory Technician/Assistant Bachelor's Degree in the relevant science subject (e.g., Microbiology, Physics, Chemistry) from a recognized university. Basic computer knowledge is required. Age: Varies (e.g., 35 years for Lab Assistant).
Note: For almost all posts, a Certificate of Basic Knowledge of Computer Applications (CCC) as prescribed by the Government of Gujarat is mandatory. Adequate knowledge of Gujarati, Hindi, and English is also required.

Selection Process

The university reserves the right to short-list candidates and decide the method of selection. The process may include the following stages:

  1. Shortlisting: Based on the number of applications, the university may shortlist candidates for the next stage using merit-based criteria, an aptitude test, or a written test.
  2. Written Examination: For many Group 'B' and 'C' posts, a competitive written examination will be the primary basis for selection. The exam will be an objective MCQ type.
  3. Interview: For Group 'A' and other senior posts, candidates who are shortlisted will be called for a personal interview.
  4. Document Verification: Candidates must produce all original testimonials and certificates at the time of the interview or as and when called for verification.

The exact selection process, exam pattern, and syllabus will be declared and published on the University website.

Exam Pattern

The detailed exam pattern and syllabus for each post will be published on the university's website. However, based on the common syllabus provided for some posts in a similar previous recruitment (Ref. Advertisement No. NT03-12/2025), a two-part MCQ exam can be expected for many Group C and B posts.

Part-I: General Knowledge (100 Marks)

Subject Questions / Marks
Reasoning & Data Interpretation 30
Quantitative Aptitude 20
English 15
Gujarati 15
Computer Fundamentals 20

Part-II: Subjective/Post-Specific Syllabus (100 Marks)

This part will contain 100 MCQs based on the specific technical/academic syllabus related to the post's essential qualification. For example, for a Computer Operator, questions will be on programming, databases, and networking. For a Lab Technician (Chemistry), questions will be from the B.Sc. Chemistry curriculum.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these administrative and technical posts.

How to Prepare

A focused preparation strategy is essential to succeed in this competitive recruitment.

  1. Analyze the Syllabus: As soon as the detailed syllabus for your post is released on the university website, analyze it thoroughly to understand the weightage of different topics.
  2. Focus on Part-II (Subject Specific): This section is crucial. Revise your graduation/post-graduation level syllabus in detail. For technical posts, focus on core concepts.
  3. Prepare for Part-I (General Knowledge):
    • Reasoning and Aptitude: Practice regularly from standard competitive exam books.
    • Languages (English & Gujarati): Focus on grammar, vocabulary, and reading comprehension.
    • Computer Fundamentals: Prepare basic topics like Operating Systems, MS Office, and the Internet.
  4. Stay Updated: Keep abreast of current affairs and any updates posted on the university website.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended resources for your preparation:

  • For General Knowledge & Aptitude: Standard competitive exam books by R.S. Aggarwal (for Aptitude & Reasoning), Lucent's General Knowledge.
  • For Languages (English & Gujarati): "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi and standard Gujarati grammar books.
  • For Computer Fundamentals: "Objective Computer Awareness" by Arihant Publications.
  • For Domain-Specific Subjects: Refer to the standard textbooks from your respective degree curriculum.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online. Candidates applying for these posts are **not required to submit any hardcopy** of the application initially.

  1. Visit the university's recruitment portal: https://www.gujaratuniversity.ac.in/career.
  2. Find the relevant employment notice (No.GU/Admin/2025-26) and click on the "Apply Online" link.
  3. Register yourself by providing your basic details, and then log in to fill out the application form.
  4. Fill the form carefully with all your personal, educational, and experience details.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your recent photograph, signature, and other required documents.
  6. Pay the application fee online via the payment gateway.
  7. Review your application carefully before final submission and take a printout for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

While applying online, you will need to upload scanned copies of various documents. It is advisable to keep them ready.

  1. Recent Passport-size Photograph and Signature.
  2. Proof of Date of Birth (10th marksheet or School Leaving Certificate).
  3. All educational marksheets and certificates (10th, 12th, Diploma, Degree, Master's).
  4. CCC certificate for computer knowledge.
  5. Caste/Category Certificate and Non-Creamy Layer Certificate (if applicable).
  6. Experience certificates.
  7. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, etc.).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website Click here

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply?

The last date to submit the online application is January 13, 2026, up to 05:00 PM.

Question 2: Do I need to send a hard copy of the application form?

No, the notification explicitly states that applicants are not required to submit any hardcopy of the application.

Question 3: Is there a fee for PwD candidates?

No, for PwD candidates, the processing fee is NIL.

entry.title: कैनरा बैंक अप्रेंटिस भर्ती 2025: 800+ पदों पर भर्ती की संभावना!
entry.content:
कैनरा बैंक अप्रेंटिस भर्ती 2025: 800+ पदों पर भर्ती की उम्मीद!

स्नातक उम्मीदवारों के लिए बैंकिंग सेक्टर में करियर शुरू करने का सुनहरा अवसर।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! जो युवा बैंकिंग सेक्टर में अपना करियर शुरू करना चाहते हैं उनके लिए यह बहुत अच्छी खबर है। देश के प्रमुख सार्वजनिक क्षेत्र के बैंकों में से एक, Canara Bank जल्द ही Apprentice के पदों पर भर्ती के लिए अधिसूचना जारी करने वाला है। यह भर्ती Apprentices Act, 1961 के अंतर्गत की जाएगी, जिसका उद्देश्य युवाओं को बैंकिंग संचालन में व्यावहारिक प्रशिक्षण देना है।

हर वर्ष Canara Bank देशभर की अपनी शाखाओं में Apprentices की भर्ती करता है। यद्यपि 2025 का अधिसूचना अभी जारी नहीं हुआ है, पिछले वर्षों के पैटर्न के आधार पर यहां आपको संभावित योग्यता, चयन प्रक्रिया और अन्य जानकारी दी गई है ताकि आप पहले से तैयारी कर सकें।

कैनरा बैंक अप्रेंटिस रिक्तियाँ 2025 (अनुमानित)

पिछले वर्षों के अनुभवों के आधार पर, Canara Bank द्वारा लगभग 800+ पदों पर भर्ती की संभावना है। इन पदों का राज्यवार और श्रेणीवार विभाजन सरकारी आरक्षण नियमों के अनुसार किया जाएगा।

पद नामअनुमानित रिक्तियाँ
Apprentice800+ (अनुमानित)

कैनरा बैंक अप्रेंटिस स्टाइपेंड 2025

चयनित Apprentices को प्रशिक्षण अवधि के दौरान एक निश्चित मासिक Stipend दिया जाएगा। यह राशि Apprenticeship Rules के अनुसार तय की जाती है और राज्य के अनुसार थोड़ी भिन्न हो सकती है।

पद नामप्रशिक्षण अवधिअनुमानित मासिक स्टाइपेंड
Apprentice1 वर्ष (12 महीने)₹9,500 – ₹12,000 प्रति माह (अनुमानित)

कैनरा बैंक अप्रेंटिस पात्रता मानदंड 2025 (अनुमानित)

आवेदन करने से पहले उम्मीदवारों को नीचे दी गई शैक्षणिक योग्यता और आयु सीमा को पूरा करना आवश्यक है:

मापदंडविवरण
शैक्षणिक योग्यताउम्मीदवार के पास किसी मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से किसी भी विषय में स्नातक (Graduate) की डिग्री होनी चाहिए।
आयु सीमान्यूनतम आयु 20 वर्ष और अधिकतम आयु 28 वर्ष होनी चाहिए।
स्थानीय भाषाउम्मीदवार को उस राज्य की स्थानीय भाषा पढ़ने, लिखने और बोलने में दक्ष होना चाहिए जहाँ से वह आवेदन कर रहा है।
आयु में छूट: सरकारी नियमों के अनुसार OBC को 3 वर्ष, SC/ST को 5 वर्ष, और PwBD उम्मीदवारों को 10 वर्ष की छूट प्रदान की जाएगी।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ (संभावित)

Canara Bank Apprentice 2025 की अधिसूचना जल्द जारी होने की उम्मीद है। नीचे दी गई तिथियाँ जारी होते ही अपडेट कर दी जाएँगी:

कार्यक्रमसंभावित तिथि
अधिसूचना जारी होने की तिथिजल्द घोषित होगी
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभजल्द घोषित होगी
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथिजल्द घोषित होगी
ऑनलाइन परीक्षा तिथिजल्द घोषित होगी
आवेदन शुल्क (अनुमानित):
General / OBC / EWS: ₹800 + GST
SC / ST / PwBD / Women: ₹600 + GST

कैनरा बैंक अप्रेंटिस चयन प्रक्रिया 2025

Apprentice पद के लिए चयन प्रक्रिया सामान्यतः एक ऑनलाइन लिखित परीक्षा पर आधारित होती है। चयन के मुख्य चरण निम्नलिखित हैं:

चरणपरीक्षा / कार्यक्रमविवरण
चरण 1ऑनलाइन लिखित परीक्षाइसमें वस्तुनिष्ठ (Objective) प्रकार के प्रश्न होंगे, जिनमें तर्कशक्ति, अंग्रेज़ी, गणितीय योग्यता और कंप्यूटर ज्ञान जैसे विषय शामिल रहेंगे।
चरण 2स्थानीय भाषा परीक्षणलिखित परीक्षा में पास उम्मीदवारों की स्थानीय भाषा दक्षता की जांच की जाएगी।
चरण 3दस्तावेज़ सत्यापनअंतिम चयन से पहले उम्मीदवारों के सभी मूल दस्तावेज़ों का सत्यापन किया जाएगा।

कैनरा बैंक अप्रेंटिस परीक्षा पैटर्न (अनुमानित)

ऑनलाइन परीक्षा में वस्तुनिष्ठ प्रकार के प्रश्न होंगे। प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर के लिए 1/4 अंक की नकारात्मक अंकन प्रणाली लागू हो सकती है।

विषयप्रश्नों की संख्याअधिकतम अंक
Reasoning और Computer Aptitude5050
General English5050
Quantitative Aptitude5050
General / Financial Awareness5050
कुल200200

ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया एवं आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

इस भर्ती के लिए आवेदन केवल ऑनलाइन माध्यम से किया जाएगा, जो सामान्यतः IBPS Portal या Canara Bank की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट पर उपलब्ध होगा।

  1. चरण 1: Canara Bank की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट canarabank.com पर ‘Careers’ सेक्शन में जाएँ।
  2. चरण 2: “Recruitment of Apprentices” लिंक पर क्लिक करें।
  3. चरण 3: “New Registration” पर क्लिक करें और अपनी मूल जानकारी भरें।
  4. चरण 4: पंजीकृत ईमेल/मोबाइल पर प्राप्त Registration Number और Password से लॉगिन करें।
  5. चरण 5: आवेदन फॉर्म में व्यक्तिगत, शैक्षणिक और अन्य जानकारी भरें और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें।
  6. चरण 6: आवेदन शुल्क का ऑनलाइन भुगतान करें और फॉर्म को अंतिम रूप से सबमिट करें।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन के लिए आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

  • पासपोर्ट आकार का फोटो और हस्ताक्षर की स्कैन कॉपी।
  • Left Thumb Impression और Handwritten Declaration की स्कैन कॉपी।
  • Graduation की मार्कशीट और डिग्री प्रमाणपत्र।
  • Caste/EWS/PwBD प्रमाणपत्र (यदि लागू हो)।
  • मान्य पहचान पत्र (Valid ID Proof)।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें (लिंक जल्द सक्रिय होगा)
आधिकारिक अधिसूचना PDFयहाँ क्लिक करें (जल्द उपलब्ध होगा)
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या Canara Bank Apprentice एक स्थायी नौकरी है?

नहीं, यह एक स्थायी नौकरी नहीं है। यह केवल 1 वर्ष की प्रशिक्षण (Apprenticeship) है। प्रशिक्षण पूर्ण होने के बाद बैंक पर स्थायी नियुक्ति देने की बाध्यता नहीं होती।

प्रश्न 2: इस प्रशिक्षण का क्या लाभ है?

यह प्रशिक्षण उम्मीदवारों को बैंकिंग क्षेत्र में व्यावहारिक अनुभव प्रदान करता है। भविष्य में बैंकिंग परीक्षाओं (PO/Clerk आदि) में यह अनुभव उपयोगी सिद्ध हो सकता है।

प्रश्न 3: क्या चयन प्रक्रिया में साक्षात्कार (Interview) होगा?

सामान्यतः Apprentice भर्ती में साक्षात्कार नहीं होता है। चयन केवल ऑनलाइन परीक्षा के अंकों के आधार पर किया जाता है।

entry.title: Rubber Board Recruitment 2025: Notification Out for 51 Scientist and Technical Posts
entry.content:
Rubber Board Recruitment 2025: Notification Out for 51 Scientist and Technical Posts

Join the Statutory Body under the Ministry of Commerce & Industry - Detailed Guide on Eligibility, Salary, and Preparation

Post Name: Scientist (A/B/C), Mechanical Engineer, Assistant Director, Statistical Inspector, Scientific Assistant
Last Date: December 01, 2025
Total Posts: 51 Vacancies

The Rubber Board of India, a statutory body established under the Rubber Act of 1947, has officially announced its recruitment drive for 2025. Headquartered in the scenic city of Kottayam, Kerala, the Rubber Board operates under the administrative control of the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, Government of India. This organization plays a pivotal role in the development of the natural rubber industry in India, ranging from scientific research and extension services to market regulation and export promotion. Under Advertisement Number 2025-03, the Board is looking to recruit 51 high-caliber professionals across various technical, scientific, and administrative cadres. These roles are critical for the Board's mission to enhance India's rubber production and technological edge in the global market.

Working for the Rubber Board is highly prestigious, particularly for those in the scientific community. The 2025 notification offers a wide range of opportunities, including Scientist positions at levels A, B, and C, along with vacancies for Mechanical Engineers, Statistical Inspectors, and Scientific Assistants. These roles are not just "jobs" but opportunities to contribute to the Rubber Research Institute of India (RRII), one of the foremost rubber research organizations in the world. For candidates looking for a permanent central government career that offers both academic challenge and professional stability, this recruitment is a significant opportunity. The application window is currently open and will accept submissions until December 1, 2025.

India is one of the world's largest producers and consumers of natural rubber, and the Rubber Board is the guardian of this massive ecosystem. As a Scientist or Engineer at the Rubber Board, you will be involved in cutting-edge research in plant pathology, rubber technology, and sustainable harvesting. The selection process is rigorous, often involving a combination of written examinations and personal interviews to ensure that only the most qualified candidates are inducted. This 2000-word comprehensive guide provides you with an exhaustive look at the eligibility criteria, the detailed syllabus for technical exams, salary structures based on the 7th CPC, and strategic preparation tips to help you secure a position in this esteemed organization.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates are advised to stay updated with the official recruitment portal of the Rubber Board. The recruitment cycle follows a disciplined schedule, and missing the application deadline or failing to pay the fee within the window will lead to immediate disqualification. Please note that the Board has provided a generous window of over 40 days for the application process.

Event / Feature Date and Details
Official Notification Number Advt. No. 2025-03
Date of Publication October 21, 2025
Online Application Start Date October 2025 (Active)
Last Date for Online Application December 1, 2025 (11:59 PM)
Job Type Permanent / Central Government (Statutory)
Primary Job Location Kottayam (Kerala) with All India Liability

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Distribution

The 51 vacancies are categorized into Group A, B, and C based on the level of technical expertise and the seniority of the position. Scientist roles form the largest part of this recruitment, emphasizing the Board's focus on research and innovation.

Post Designation Group Total Vacancies
Scientist 'C' Group A 05
Scientist 'B' Group A 19
Scientist 'A' Group A 05
Assistant Director (Systems) Group A 01
Mechanical Engineer Group A 01
Scientific Assistant Group B 10
Statistical Inspector Group B 02
Electrician Group C 03
Hindi Typist Group C 01
Others (Vigilance/Admin) Group A/B 04
Grand Total - 51
Reservation Policy: Vacancies are further categorized as per the Government of India reservation norms for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), EWS, and PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities). Ensure that your caste or category certificates are issued in the Central Government format to be eligible for reservation benefits.

Salary Structure and Organizational Perks

Rubber Board employees are compensated according to the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC). The gross salary for these positions is highly competitive, especially when combined with the lower cost of living in cities like Kottayam compared to major metros.

  • Scientist 'C' (Level 11): Initial Basic Pay of ₹67,700. Gross monthly salary approximately ₹1,05,000 - ₹1,15,000.
  • Scientist 'B' / Engineer (Level 10): Initial Basic Pay of ₹56,100. Gross monthly salary approximately ₹88,000 - ₹95,000.
  • Scientist 'A' (Level 7): Initial Basic Pay of ₹44,900. Gross monthly salary approximately ₹68,000 - ₹75,000.
  • Scientific Assistant / Statistical Inspector (Level 5/6): Initial Basic Pay ranges from ₹29,200 to ₹35,400. Gross salary approximately ₹48,000 - ₹60,000.

Benefits and Allowances: In addition to the basic pay, selected candidates are eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), and Leave Travel Concession (LTC). Scientists often receive additional professional growth allowances and access to high-end laboratory infrastructure for research publications.

Eligibility Criteria

The academic requirements for the Rubber Board are highly specialized. The organization looks for candidates who have a strong foundation in agriculture, technology, or computer science. Below is a detailed breakdown of the requirements.

  • Scientist 'C': Master’s degree in the relevant subject (Botany/Agriculture/Chemistry/Rubber Tech) with at least 5 to 10 years of research experience. Ph.D. is highly desirable. Age Limit: Up to 50 years.
  • Scientist 'B': Master’s degree in the relevant subject (Agriculture/Chemistry/Botany) with a minimum of 3 years of research experience. Age Limit: Up to 40 years.
  • Scientist 'A': B.E./B.Tech in Rubber Technology/Polymer Tech or Master’s degree in Science. Experience is not mandatory but preferred. Age Limit: Up to 35 years.
  • Mechanical Engineer: B.E./B.Tech in Mechanical Engineering with a first-class degree and relevant experience in machinery maintenance. Age Limit: Up to 35 years.
  • Statistical Inspector: Degree in Statistics or Mathematics with Statistics as a major subject. Experience in data collection is preferred. Age Limit: Up to 30 years.
  • Scientific Assistant: Degree in Science (B.Sc) with Chemistry or Botany as a major subject. Age Limit: Up to 30 years.

Note: Age relaxation is applicable as per Government of India rules: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and up to 10 years for PwBD candidates.

Selection Process and Examination Roadmap

The selection process at the Rubber Board is designed to be comprehensive, ensuring that candidates possess both technical knowledge and the aptitude required for research and administrative roles.

  1. Written Examination (CBT/Offline): This is the primary filtering round for all posts. For Group A posts, the level of the exam is based on Post-Graduate subjects. For Group B and C, it is based on Graduation or 12th standard subjects.
  2. Personal Interview: Mandatory for all Group A Scientist and Engineer positions. For Group B and C posts, selection is primarily based on the written test merit, though a skill test may be conducted.
  3. Skill Test: Specifically for posts like Hindi Typist or Electrician to verify practical competence.
  4. Document Verification: Scrutiny of original academic mark sheets, experience certificates, and caste/category proofs.

Qualifying Marks: Candidates generally need to score a minimum of 50% in the written exam (40% for reserved categories) to be considered for the interview stage.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

The written examination consists of objective-type multiple-choice questions (MCQs). The total marks are usually 100, and the duration is 120 minutes. There is typically a negative marking of 0.25 for every incorrect answer.

Technical Syllabus for Scientist Posts:

  • Plant Pathology & Botany: Anatomy of Hevea brasiliensis, rubber breeding, pests and diseases of rubber trees.
  • Chemistry: Polymer chemistry, latex processing, compounding of rubber, and vulcanization processes.
  • Agriculture: Soil science specifically related to rubber plantations, nutrient management, and climate impact on rubber yields.

Non-Technical Syllabus (Common for all):

  • General Awareness: Current events of national and international importance, basics of the Indian economy, and geography of India.
  • Reasoning & Aptitude: Logical reasoning, quantitative aptitude (10th standard level), and data interpretation.
  • English Language: Basic grammar, vocabulary, and sentence completion.

Strategic Preparation for Rubber Board Exam

Since the Rubber Board is a specialized body, the exam focuses heavily on "Domain Knowledge." Preparing purely with general competition books might not be enough for scientific roles.

Focus on Rubber Research: Spend time reading the official "Rubber Board Handbooks" and research publications from the RRII. Understanding the geography of rubber production in India (Kerala, Tripura, Assam) and the history of the Board can help you score well in both the written exam and the interview.

Subject Core Revision: For Scientists and Engineers, your university textbooks are your best friends. Revise your Master’s level notes. For the Statistical Inspector role, focus heavily on probability, sampling techniques, and data processing. For the Mechanical Engineer role, focus on fluid mechanics and thermodynamic cycles, as these are often relevant to latex processing plants.

Mock Exams: Practice with previous year question papers of other statutory bodies under the Ministry of Commerce, such as the Tea Board or Spices Board, as their administrative exam patterns are often similar.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right books will help you stay focused on the relevant syllabus. Here are some top recommendations.

Subject / Role Recommended Book List
Rubber Technology "Rubber Technology" by Maurice Morton or "Hand Book of Rubber Technology" by Smithers Rapra.
Agriculture/Botany "Fundamentals of Agriculture" by Arun Katyayan or "Objective Botany" by Arihant.
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal and Lucent's General Knowledge.
Statistics "Fundamental of Mathematical Statistics" by S.C. Gupta & V.K. Kapoor.
Reasoning A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.

Application Fee and Payment Process

The application fee is categorized based on the group of the post. Fees are non-refundable and must be paid through the online portal.

  • Group A Posts (Scientists/Directors): ₹1500
  • Group B Posts (Inspectors/Assistants): ₹1000
  • Group C Posts (Electricians/Typists): ₹500
  • Exempted Categories: SC, ST, PwBD, and All Female Candidates are exempted from paying the application fee.

Payment Mode: The fee can be paid using Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, or UPI through the integrated payment gateway on the recruitment website. Ensure you save the payment receipt for future reference.

How to Apply Online

The application process is centralized through the official Rubber Board recruitment website. Follow these steps for a successful registration.

  1. Visit the official recruitment portal: recruitments.rubberboard.org.in.
  2. Read the "Detailed Instructions" provided on the homepage carefully.
  3. Click on "New Registration" and provide your basic details like Name, Email, and Mobile Number.
  4. Login with your registration ID and password.
  5. Fill in Academic Details: Enter your marks for 10th, 12th, Graduation, and Post-Graduation. Ensure your experience details are entered accurately.
  6. Upload Scanned Images: Recent passport photo (white background) and signature in the prescribed format (JPEG, 20kb-50kb).
  7. Pay the Application Fee: Complete the payment (if applicable) and check for the transaction success status.
  8. Final Submission: Review your application and click on "Submit." Take a printout of the completed application form.

Documents Needed to Apply

Scanned copies of the following documents should be kept ready before you start the online application process to avoid session timeouts.

  1. Educational Proof: 10th and 12th Standard mark sheets and certificates for date of birth verification.
  2. Professional Proof: Degree and Master’s certificates and semester-wise mark sheets.
  3. Experience Certificates: Mandatory for Scientist 'B' and 'C' roles (Scanned in PDF format).
  4. Identification: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID.
  5. Category Proof: SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificates if seeking reservation or fee exemption.
  6. NOC (No Objection Certificate): For candidates currently working in Government/PSU organizations.

Important Links

Resource Name Official URL Link
Rubber Board Recruitment Portal Apply Online Here
Official Rubber Board Website www.rubberboard.gov.in
Detailed Notification PDF Download Notification

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: What is the last date to apply for Rubber Board 2025?

The final date for submitting the online application is December 1, 2025, until 11:59 PM. It is advisable to apply at least a week before the deadline to avoid server congestion.

Q2: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you meet the eligibility criteria for each. However, you must submit separate applications and pay separate fees for each post. Ensure that the exam dates do not overlap once announced.

Q3: Is the application fee refundable?

No. The application fee is strictly non-refundable under any circumstances, including the cancellation of recruitment or disqualification of the candidate.

Q4: What are the primary job locations?

Most vacancies are for the head office in Kottayam, Kerala, or the Rubber Research Institute of India. However, since the Rubber Board has regional offices in states like Tripura, Assam, and Meghalaya, you may be posted anywhere in India.

Q5: Is there an interview for Group C posts?

No. As per the Government of India directives, there are no interviews for Group B (Non-Gazetted) and Group C posts. Selection for Statistical Inspector and Electrician will be based purely on written and skill test scores.

Q6: What is the nature of the "Scientist" job?

It is a research-oriented permanent role. You will be tasked with identifying new rubber clones, studying climate resilience, or developing better latex processing technologies. It offers a balanced academic and government career.

entry.title: CSIR NML Recruitment 2026 for Multi Tasking Staff (MTS)
entry.content:
CSIR NML Recruitment 2026 for Multi Tasking Staff (MTS)

Advertisement No. 10/2025: CSIR-National Metallurgical Laboratory (NML) invites online applications for 22 Group 'C' Multi Tasking Staff posts. A great opportunity for 10th Pass and ITI holders. Check detailed vacancy information, eligibility criteria, salary, exam pattern, and the application process here.

Post Name: Multi Tasking Staff (MTS) in various trades
Last Date: 06 February 2026
Total Posts: 22

The Council of Scientific & Industrial Research (CSIR) - National Metallurgical Laboratory (NML), Jamshedpur, has announced a significant recruitment drive for the position of Multi Tasking Staff (MTS). As a premier R&D organization under the Ministry of Science & Technology, Government of India, CSIR-NML is looking for dedicated and eligible Indian citizens to fill 22 Group ‘C’ Non-Gazetted vacancies. This recruitment, under Advertisement No. 10/2025, offers a stable and promising career path for candidates with 10th/Matriculation and ITI qualifications in various trades.

The positions are spread across different technical specializations, including Electrician, Carpenter, Fitter, Plumber, AC & Refrigeration, and Computer Operator and Programming Assistant (COPA), in addition to general MTS roles. This is an excellent opportunity for young aspirants to become part of a prestigious national laboratory engaged in cutting-edge research in metallurgy and materials engineering. The online application portal will be active from January 5, 2026, to February 6, 2026. Interested candidates are advised to carefully read the detailed notification and ensure their eligibility before applying.

CSIR-NML MTS Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates should take note of the crucial dates and details for this government job opportunity located in Jamshedpur, Jharkhand.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization CSIR-National Metallurgical Laboratory (NML)
Advertisement No. 10/2025
Post Name Multi Tasking Staff (MTS)
Post Group Group 'C', Non-Gazetted
Total Vacancies 22
Application Start Date 05 January 2026 (11:00 AM)
Application Last Date 06 February 2026 (5:00 PM)
Application Mode Online
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Jamshedpur, Jharkhand (with potential posting in Chennai/Digha)
Official Website https://www.nml.res.in

Vacancy Details

A total of 22 posts for Multi Tasking Staff are available across seven different post codes, based on qualification and trade specialization. The category-wise breakup is as follows: UR: 07, EWS: 04, OBC: 03, ST: 08.

Post Code Required Qualification/Trade Number of Vacancies & Reservation
M 01 10th/Matriculation or equivalent 14 [UR:03; EWS:03; OBC(NCL):01; ST:07]
M 02 ITI in Electrician Trade 02 [UR:01; OBC(NCL):01]
M 03 ITI in Carpenter Trade 01 [UR:01]
M 04 ITI in Fitter Trade 01 [UR:01]
M 05 ITI in Plumber Trade 01 [EWS:01]
M 06 ITI in AC & Refrigeration Trade 01 [UR:01]
M 07 ITI in COPA Trade 02 [OBC(NCL):01; ST:01]
Total 22
Note: Out of the 22 total posts, 02 posts are reserved for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) and 02 posts are reserved for Ex-Servicemen (ESM) on a horizontal basis. The number of vacancies is provisional and subject to change.

Application Fee

  • For General, EWS, and OBC candidates: ₹500/-
  • Candidates belonging to SC/ST/PwBD/Women/CSIR Employees/Ex-Servicemen categories are exempted from payment of the application fee.

The fee must be paid online through the SB Collect link provided in the application portal. The payment receipt must be preserved for future reference.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet the specific educational qualification, age limit, and job requirements for the post code they are applying for. The crucial date for determining age and qualifications is the last date of online application, i.e., February 6, 2026.

Post Code Essential Qualification, Age Limit & Job Description
M 01 Qualification: 10th/Matriculation or its equivalent pass.
Age Limit: 18-25 Years.
Job Description: Physical maintenance of records, general cleanliness, carrying files, photocopying, assisting in routine office work on computer, delivering documents, watch and ward duties, opening/closing rooms, cleaning furniture, and upkeep of parks/lawns.
M 02 Qualification: 10th Pass + ITI pass in Electrician Trade.
Age Limit: 18-25 Years.
Job Description: Installation, maintenance, and repair of electrical machinery, motors, transformers, switch gears, lighting fixtures, and other electrical equipment. Involves connecting and soldering terminals.
M 03 Qualification: 10th Pass + ITI pass in Carpenter Trade.
Age Limit: 18-25 Years.
Job Description: Construction, repair, and installation of structures like doors, windows, furniture, cabinets, shelves, and roofs. Operation of hand and power saws, planers, lathes, drills, and other carpentry tools.
M 04 Qualification: 10th Pass + ITI pass in Fitter Trade.
Age Limit: 18-25 Years.
Job Description: Assisting superiors in general fitting work, which includes filling, cutting, and threading of ferrous and non-ferrous materials.
M 05 Qualification: 10th Pass + ITI pass in Plumber Trade.
Age Limit: 18-25 Years.
Job Description: Laying, assembly, installation, and maintenance of sanitary fittings, fixtures, sewage, drainage systems, and water pipelines. Involves reading drawings, marking positions, drilling holes, and fixing brackets.
M 06 Qualification: 10th Pass + ITI pass in Air Condition (AC) & Refrigeration Trade.
Age Limit: 18-25 Years.
Job Description: Repair and operation of air-conditioning and refrigeration systems for various purposes, including preserving food, industrial processes, and cooling/warming buildings.
M 07 Qualification: 10th Pass + ITI pass in Computer Operator and Programming Assistant (COPA) Trade.
Age Limit: 18-25 Years.
Job Description: Operating computer terminals, monitoring systems for errors, responding to error messages, loading software, manipulating data to produce reports, and assisting computer users with problems.

Age Relaxation: The upper age limit is relaxable by 5 years for SC/ST candidates and 3 years for OBC (NCL) candidates. Age relaxation for PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, Widows, and Divorced Women will be as per Government of India norms.

Selection Process

The selection of candidates for the Multi Tasking Staff posts will be made through a transparent and competitive process. The stages are as follows:

  1. Shortlisting of Candidates: A screening committee will scrutinize all applications and shortlist candidates who fulfill all the qualifications and conditions of the advertisement.
  2. Competitive Written Examination: All shortlisted candidates will be required to appear for an objective-type Computer Based Test (CBT).
  3. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the performance of the candidates in the Competitive Written Examination.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The Competitive Written Examination is the sole determinant of selection. Candidates should prepare thoroughly according to the prescribed pattern and syllabus.

Exam Pattern

  • Mode of Examination: Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Type of Questions: Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs)
  • Medium of Exam: The questions will be set in both English and Hindi, except for the English Language section.
  • Standard of Exam: Class X (Matriculation) level.
  • Total Number of Questions: 150
  • Total Time Allotted: 2 Hours (120 minutes). For candidates eligible for a scribe, the total time will be 2 hours and 40 minutes.
  • Marking Scheme:
    • +3 marks for every correct answer.
    • -1 mark (one negative mark) for every wrong answer.

Detailed Exam Structure

Part Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Negative Marks
I General Intelligence 25 75 -1 for each wrong answer
II Quantitative Aptitude 25 75 -1 for each wrong answer
III General Awareness 50 150 -1 for each wrong answer
IV English Language 50 150 -1 for each wrong answer
Total 150 450

Indicative Syllabus

The notification specifies the standard of the exam is Class X. Based on this, the expected syllabus for each section is as follows:

  • General Intelligence: Analogies, similarities and differences, spatial visualization, problem-solving, analysis, judgment, decision making, visual memory, observation, relationship concepts, arithmetical reasoning, verbal and figure classification, arithmetical number series, non-verbal series.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: Number Systems, Computation of Whole Numbers, Decimals and Fractions, relationship between numbers, Fundamental arithmetical operations, Percentages, Ratio and Proportion, Averages, Interest, Profit and Loss, Discount, use of Tables and Graphs, Mensuration, Time and Distance, Ratio and Time, Time and Work.
  • General Awareness: Questions will be designed to test the candidate's general awareness of the environment around him/her and its application to society. Questions will also be designed to test knowledge of current events and of such matters of everyday observation and experience in their scientific aspect as may be expected of an educated person. The test will also include questions relating to India and its neighboring countries especially pertaining to Sports, History, Culture, Geography, Economic Scene, General Polity, Indian Constitution, and Scientific Research etc.
  • English Language: Basics of English Language, its vocabulary, grammar, sentence structure, synonyms, antonyms and its correct usage, etc. Reading comprehension, para jumbles, error spotting, fill in the blanks, and sentence correction.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) mentioned in the recruitment notification for the post of Multi Tasking Staff.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A systematic preparation strategy is key to scoring well in this competitive exam, especially with the provision of negative marking.

  1. Understand the Weightage: Note that General Awareness and English Language sections have double the weightage (50 questions each) compared to General Intelligence and Quantitative Aptitude (25 questions each). Prioritize your study time accordingly.
  2. Master the Basics: Since the exam standard is 10th level, focus on strengthening your fundamental concepts in Mathematics, English Grammar, and basic Science/Social Studies.
  3. Practice with Caution: The negative marking of -1 for a wrong answer against +3 for a correct one is significant. Practice mock tests to develop a strategy for attempting questions. It's better to leave a question unanswered than to guess incorrectly.
  4. Stay Updated: For the General Awareness section, regularly read newspapers, follow current affairs websites, and revise static GK topics like history, geography, and polity.
  5. Time Management: Practice solving 150 questions within the 120-minute timeframe. This requires good speed and accuracy. Time-bound practice sessions are crucial.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for building a strong foundation for the written test.

  • General Intelligence: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal or books by Arihant Publication.
  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge" is a comprehensive book for static GK. For current affairs, follow a reliable monthly magazine or online source.
  • English Language: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi or "High School English Grammar and Composition" by Wren & Martin.

How to Apply

The application process is completely online. Candidates are advised to follow these steps carefully:

  1. Visit the official CSIR-NML website: https://nml.res.in.
  2. Navigate to the "Recruitment" or "Careers" section and find the link for "Recruitment of Multi Tasking Staff (Advt. No. 10/2025)".
  3. Go through the instructions for applying online carefully before starting the application.
  4. Click on the "Apply Online" link to start the registration process. Fill in your basic details to create a login ID and password.
  5. Log in and fill out the online application form with accurate personal, educational, and contact details.
  6. Upload a scanned copy of your recent passport-size photograph (20KB-100KB) and signature (10KB-50KB) in the prescribed format.
  7. Upload scanned PDF copies (max 4 MB each) of all required documents, including educational certificates, ITI certificates, caste certificates, and a handwritten declaration.
  8. Pay the application fee of ₹500/- (if applicable) through the SB Collect portal. Save the payment receipt.
  9. Review the entire application form before final submission to ensure all details are correct.
  10. Submit the application form and take a printout of the computer-generated application for your future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must keep scanned copies of the following documents ready for uploading with the online application:

  1. 10th / Matriculation certificate and mark sheet.
  2. ITI pass certificate and mark sheet (wherever applicable).
  3. Copy of the application fee payment receipt (if applicable).
  4. A handwritten declaration on plain white paper with black ink, as specified in the notification.
  5. Caste/Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD) in the prescribed government format.
  6. For Ex-Servicemen: Undertaking, Serving Defence Personnel Certificate, or Discharge Certificate/PPO as applicable.
  7. No Objection Certificate (NOC) if already employed in a government organization.
  8. Any other relevant certificate for claiming age relaxation or other benefits.

Important Links

Link Description URL
Apply Online Click Here (Link will be active from 05.01.2026)
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website Click Here

FAQs

Q1: What is the last date to apply for the CSIR-NML MTS Recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is February 6, 2026, up to 5:00 PM.

Q2: Is there any negative marking in the written exam?

Yes, there is a negative marking of one (-1) mark for every incorrect answer.

Q3: I am a woman candidate. Do I have to pay the application fee?

No, all women candidates, along with SC, ST, PwBD, CSIR Employees, and Ex-Servicemen, are exempted from paying the application fee.

Q4: Can I apply for multiple trades/post codes?

Yes, if you are eligible for multiple post codes, you must submit a separate application form and pay the fee (if applicable) for each post code.

Q5: What is the age limit for the MTS post?

The age limit is 18 to 25 years. Relaxations in the upper age limit are available for reserved category candidates as per Government of India rules.

entry.title: UCO बैंक अप्रेंटिस भर्ती 2024-25: ऑनलाइन परीक्षा की तारीख घोषित! जानिए एग्जाम पैटर्न और चयन प्रक्रिया
entry.content:
UCO बैंक अप्रेंटिस भर्ती 2024-25: ऑनलाइन परीक्षा की तारीख घोषित! जानिए एग्जाम पैटर्न और चयन प्रक्रिया

यूसीओ बैंक ने NATS पोर्टल पर आवेदन करने वाले अभ्यर्थियों के लिए ऑनलाइन परीक्षा की तारीख जारी कर दी है। यह परीक्षा BFSI सेक्टर स्किल काउंसिल ऑफ इंडिया द्वारा 10 नवंबर 2024 को आयोजित की जाएगी।

यूसीओ बैंक में अप्रेंटिस बनने का सपना देखने वाले उम्मीदवारों के लिए बड़ी खबर! जिन उम्मीदवारों ने NATS पोर्टल (https://nats.education.gov.in) पर UCO Bank Apprenticeship Program FY 2024-25 के लिए आवेदन किया था, उनके लिए चयन प्रक्रिया का अगला चरण शुरू हो गया है।

बैंक ने घोषणा की है कि सभी पात्र उम्मीदवारों को एक ऑनलाइन प्रवेश परीक्षा (Online Entrance Exam) देनी होगी, जिसे BFSI Sector Skill Council of India (BFSI SSC) आयोजित करेगा।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि / समय
परीक्षा शुल्क जमा करने की अवधि21 अक्टूबर 2024 से 30 अक्टूबर 2024 तक
ऑनलाइन प्रवेश परीक्षा की तारीख10 नवंबर 2024 (सुबह 10:00 बजे)

परीक्षा शुल्क विवरण

ऑनलाइन परीक्षा में शामिल होने के लिए उम्मीदवारों को शुल्क जमा करना आवश्यक है:

श्रेणीशुल्क (GST सहित)
PwBD₹472
महिला / SC / ST₹708
सामान्य / OBC / EWS₹944
ध्यान दें: शुल्क केवल ऑनलाइन जमा किया जा सकता है और यह वापस नहीं किया जाएगा।

चयन प्रक्रिया

अभ्यर्थियों का चयन निम्नलिखित चरणों पर आधारित होगा:

  1. ऑनलाइन परीक्षा: सभी उम्मीदवारों को एक ऑनलाइन ऑब्जेक्टिव परीक्षा देनी होगी।
  2. स्थानीय भाषा परीक्षा: यदि आवश्यक हुआ तो स्थानीय भाषा का टेस्ट भी लिया जा सकता है।

अंतिम मेरिट सूची राज्यवार और श्रेणीवार परीक्षा के अंकों के आधार पर तैयार की जाएगी।

ऑनलाइन परीक्षा पैटर्न

ऑनलाइन परीक्षा की अवधि 60 मिनट की होगी जिसमें कुल 100 प्रश्न होंगे:

विषयप्रश्नों की संख्याअधिकतम अंक
जनरल / फाइनेंशियल अवेयरनेस2525
जनरल इंग्लिश2525
रीजनिंग एबिलिटी और कंप्यूटर एप्टीट्यूड2525
क्वांटिटेटिव एप्टीट्यूड2525
कुल100100
  • परीक्षा का माध्यम: हिंदी और अंग्रेज़ी दोनों।
  • निगेटिव मार्किंग: हर गलत उत्तर पर 0.25 अंक की कटौती होगी।
  • पासिंग मार्क्स: प्रत्येक विषय में अलग से पास होना आवश्यक नहीं है, लेकिन कुल अंक बैंक द्वारा तय किए जाएंगे।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

महत्वपूर्ण: यह नया आवेदन नहीं है। यह केवल उन उम्मीदवारों के लिए है जिन्होंने पहले ही NATS पोर्टल पर यूसीओ बैंक अप्रेंटिसशिप के लिए आवेदन किया था।

  1. योग्य उम्मीदवारों को BFSI SSC की ओर से एक ईमेल प्राप्त होगा।
  2. उस ईमेल में परीक्षा शुल्क जमा करने और परीक्षा के लिए आवेदन करने का लिंक होगा।
  3. उम्मीदवारों को 21 अक्टूबर से 30 अक्टूबर 2024 तक उस लिंक से शुल्क जमा करना होगा।
  4. परीक्षा से एक दिन पहले, BFSI SSC की ओर से परीक्षा लिंक और लॉगिन विवरण वाला ईमेल मिलेगा।
  5. परीक्षा आप अपने घर से ही कैमरा-सक्षम डेस्कटॉप, लैपटॉप या स्मार्टफोन पर दे सकते हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
UCO Bank करियर वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
NATS पोर्टलयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: क्या अब नया आवेदन किया जा सकता है?
नहीं, यह प्रक्रिया केवल उन्हीं उम्मीदवारों के लिए है जिन्होंने पहले NATS पोर्टल पर आवेदन किया था।
Q2: परीक्षा कहाँ होगी?
परीक्षा पूरी तरह ऑनलाइन होगी और आप इसे अपने घर से दे सकते हैं।
Q3: शुल्क जमा करने की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?
ऑनलाइन परीक्षा शुल्क जमा करने की अंतिम तिथि 30 अक्टूबर 2024 है।

entry.title: AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Recruitment 2025: Apply for 69 Group A, B, and C Posts
entry.content:
AIIMS Gorakhpur Non-Faculty Recruitment 2025: Guide to 69 Vacancies

Direct Recruitment for Group A, B, and C Posts - Apply Online at aiimsgorakhpur.edu.in

Post Name: Tutor, AAO, Junior Accounts Officer, Store Keeper, OT Assistant, Lab Technologist, and others
Last Date: November 30, 2025
Total Posts: 69 Vacancies

The All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), Gorakhpur, is a premier healthcare and educational institution established under the Pradhan Mantri Swasthya Suraksha Yojana (PMSSY). Since its inception, AIIMS Gorakhpur has emerged as a cornerstone of advanced medical services in eastern Uttar Pradesh and neighboring regions. To further strengthen its administrative and technical backbone, the institute has officially announced the Non-Faculty Recruitment 2025. This drive aims to fill 69 vacancies across Group A, B, and C cadres. These positions are essential for the hospital’s operational efficiency, ranging from nursing education (Tutor/Clinical Instructor) to technical laboratory support and administrative management.

Working at AIIMS is widely considered a pinnacle of a career in the Indian healthcare sector. As an autonomous body under the Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, AIIMS Gorakhpur offers a work environment that combines the stability of a central government job with the prestige of a world-class medical facility. The 2025 recruitment notification is particularly inclusive, offering opportunities for candidates with diverse backgrounds such as Nursing, Commerce, Pharmacy, Physiotherapy, and General Management. The application process is entirely online, beginning on November 1, 2025, and concluding on November 30, 2025. Candidates selected through this rigorous process will be entitled to pay scales based on the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), along with various central government allowances.

The selection process for these posts is merit-based and primarily relies on a Computer Based Test (CBT). For technical roles like Junior Medical Lab Technologist and Operation Theatre Assistant, the exam will test both general aptitude and core technical proficiency. This 2000-word comprehensive guide provides an exhaustive breakdown of post-wise eligibility, salary components, a detailed exam syllabus, and strategic tips to help you secure your place in this esteemed institute. For any aspirant looking to contribute to India's healthcare revolution while securing their future, AIIMS Gorakhpur is the place to be.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Dates

Candidates must strictly follow the official timeline. Missing the application deadline or failing to pay the fee within the window will lead to the immediate rejection of the candidature. AIIMS Gorakhpur utilizes a dedicated recruitment portal to manage applications, and candidates are advised to keep their mobile numbers and email IDs active throughout the process for official communication.

Event Description Dates and Details
Date of Official Notification November 1, 2025
Online Registration Start Date November 1, 2025
Last Date to Apply Online November 30, 2025 (Up to 11:59 PM)
Application Mode Online Only
Exam City Centers Major Cities in India (To be specified in Admit Card)
Job Type Permanent / Central Government (Autonomous)

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown by Post and Group

The 69 vacancies are distributed across three groups (A, B, and C) based on the level of responsibility and the pay matrix. While Group C contains the highest number of vacancies (Junior Medical Lab Technologist), Group A and B consist of specialized supervisory and administrative roles.

Post Name Group Pay Level Total Vacancies
Tutor / Clinical Instructor A Level 10 02
Assistant Administrative Officer (AAO) B Level 07 01
Junior Accounts Officer B Level 06 02
Store Keeper B Level 06 03
Junior Physiotherapist B Level 06 01
Technician (Radiology) B Level 06 04
Operation Theatre Assistant C Level 05 10
Junior Medical Lab Technologist C Level 05 40
Pharmacist C Level 05 01
Mortuary Attendant C Level 01 01
Total Combined Posts - - 69
Note on Reservations: Vacancies are further categorized into UR, OBC, SC, ST, and EWS categories as per the Government of India reservation policy. Horizontal reservation for PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) is also applicable for identified posts.

Salary Structure and Organizational Perks

Employees at AIIMS Gorakhpur are compensated according to the 7th CPC. The "Gross Salary" is a combination of Basic Pay plus several significant allowances. For many, the central government benefit package is the primary motivator for applying to these roles.

  • Group A (Level 10): Basic Pay starts at ₹56,100. Including DA, HRA, and TA, the gross salary is approximately ₹95,000 to ₹1,05,000.
  • Group B (Level 6/7): Basic Pay starts at ₹35,400 / ₹44,900. Gross salary ranges between ₹65,000 to ₹85,000.
  • Group C (Level 5): Basic Pay starts at ₹29,200. Gross salary is approximately ₹48,000 to ₹55,000.

Allowances and Benefits:

  1. Dearness Allowance (DA): Revised bi-annually based on inflation.
  2. House Rent Allowance (HRA): Provided if government accommodation is not available.
  3. Transport Allowance (TA): Fixed monthly allowance for commuting.
  4. NPS (New Pension Scheme): Mandatory contribution towards retirement benefits.
  5. Medical Facility: Coverage for self and family members.
  6. LTC (Leave Travel Concession): For traveling to home-town or across India periodically.

Eligibility Criteria

Each post has a highly specific set of requirements regarding educational background and work experience. Candidates must check if they meet the exact criteria mentioned for their chosen post, as AIIMS is very strict during the document verification stage.

  • Tutor / Clinical Instructor: B.Sc. (Nursing) degree from a recognized institute and 3 years of experience in a teaching/nursing setup.
  • AAO: Degree from a recognized University or its equivalent and 5 years of experience in an administrative capacity in a Govt. or reputed private organization.
  • Junior Accounts Officer: Graduate in Commerce (B.Com) with 2 years of experience in handling accounts work.
  • Junior Medical Lab Technologist: B.Sc. in Medical Laboratory Technology (MLT) OR 12th in Science plus a Diploma in MLT with 2 years of experience.
  • OT Assistant: B.Sc. (Medical Technology) OR 12th in Science with 5 years of experience in an Operation Theatre.
  • Pharmacist: B.Pharma OR Diploma in Pharmacy with 3 years of experience. Must be a registered Pharmacist.
  • Store Keeper: Graduate with a Post Graduate degree/Diploma in Material Management OR a Bachelor's degree in Material Management.

Age Limit: Generally, for Group C, the age limit is 18–30 years. For Group B, it is 21–35 years. For Group A (Tutor), the limit is up to 35 years. Age relaxation applies for SC/ST (5 years), OBC (3 years), and PwBD (10 years).

Selection Process

AIIMS Gorakhpur follows a standardized and transparent selection process. The goal is to evaluate both the general intelligence of the candidate and their professional competence.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT): This is the primary hurdle. All candidates must appear for an objective-type exam. The difficulty level of the questions corresponds to the educational level of the post.
  2. Skill Test: For posts like OT Assistant, Junior Lab Technologist, and Physiotherapist, a skill test or practical exam may be conducted to check hands-on proficiency. This is usually qualifying in nature.
  3. Interview: Conducted only for Group A posts (Tutor/Clinical Instructor). Group B and C posts do not have an interview.
  4. Document Verification (DV): Candidates are called for DV in a specific ratio (e.g., 1:3) based on CBT merit.
  5. Medical Examination: A final health check to ensure the candidate is fit for hospital service.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Success in the CBT requires a strategic understanding of the exam sections. The total marks are usually 100, with a duration of 90 to 120 minutes.

Part 1: General Aptitude (40% Weightage):

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: Puzzles, analogies, coding-decoding, blood relations, and syllogism.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: Number systems, profit & loss, time and work, interest, and basic algebra.
  • English Language: Grammar, synonyms, antonyms, and comprehension.
  • General Awareness: Current affairs, basic science, and static GK related to the health sector and India.

Part 2: Domain/Subject Knowledge (60% Weightage): This section is critical. For a Pharmacist, questions will focus on pharmacology, pharmaceutics, and jurisprudence. For a Lab Technologist, it will cover biochemistry, hematology, and microbiology. Candidates should refer to their professional degree/diploma textbooks for this part.

Negative Marking: There is usually a negative marking of 0.25 for every incorrect answer.

Expert Preparation Strategy for AIIMS Exams

AIIMS exams are known for being slightly more difficult than other state-level recruitment exams. The technical section is particularly robust.

Master Your Trade: If you are applying for a technical role (Lab Tech, Radiographer, OT Asst), dedicate 70% of your time to your professional subjects. Use previous years' papers of AIIMS Delhi or AIIMS Rishikesh, as the exam pattern is often similar across different AIIMS institutes.

Aptitude and Time Management: The non-technical part (Reasoning/Maths) is where you can gain an edge. Since health professionals often neglect mathematics, practicing 10th-standard level quant daily can significantly boost your rank. Use a timer while solving mock tests to improve your speed.

Stay Updated with Health GK: For General Awareness, focus on recent health schemes launched by the Government of India (like Ayushman Bharat), important days in health (World Health Day, Nurses Day), and the structure of AIIMS itself.

Best Books for Preparation

Quality study material is the foundation of success. Here are the top recommendations categorized by subject area.

Subject / Discipline Recommended Book List
Nursing (Tutor) Target High by Muthuvenkatachalam & Ambili or PR Yadav.
Medical Lab Tech (MLT) MCQs in Medical Laboratory Technology by Z.B. Bhatti.
Pharmacy Pharmacology by KD Tripathi and Pharmacy Quiz by Piyush Publication.
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude by RS Aggarwal and Lucent’s General Knowledge.
Reasoning A Modern Approach to Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning by RS Aggarwal.

Application Fee Details

AIIMS Gorakhpur has set an application fee that covers the administrative costs of the recruitment process. The fee is non-refundable.

  • General and OBC Candidates: ₹1770 (₹1500 Application Fee + 18% GST).
  • SC, ST, and EWS Candidates: ₹1416 (₹1200 Application Fee + 18% GST).
  • PwBD Candidates: Exempted (No Fee).

Note: Fees must be paid online using Credit Card, Debit Card, or Net Banking. Candidates should save the transaction receipt as proof of payment.

How to Apply Online

The application process is designed to be user-friendly. However, candidates must be careful with data entry, as modifications may not be allowed after the final submission.

  1. Visit the official website of AIIMS Gorakhpur: https://aiimsgorakhpur.edu.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the "Recruitment" tab and select "Non-Faculty Recruitment 2025."
  3. Click on "New Registration" and provide your Name, Email ID, and Mobile Number.
  4. You will receive a User ID and Password via SMS/Email. Log in using these credentials.
  5. Fill in all the personal details, academic scores, and work experience details.
  6. Upload Scanned Documents: Recent passport photo, signature, and relevant degree/category certificates in the specified format (JPEG/PDF).
  7. Proceed to pay the application fee through the online gateway.
  8. Review your form one last time and click "Submit."
  9. Download and print the confirmation page for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Scanning documents in high resolution is essential. Blur or illegible documents can lead to the cancellation of your application.

  1. Identity Proof: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID.
  2. Educational Marksheets: 10th, 12th, and professional Degree/Diploma certificates and all-semester marksheets.
  3. Registration Certificate: Mandatory for Pharmacists, Nurses, and Physiotherapists (State or Indian Council).
  4. Experience Certificate: Clear certificate on employer letterhead for posts requiring experience.
  5. Category Certificate: SC/ST/OBC (NCL) or EWS certificate in the format prescribed by the Central Government.
  6. NOC: "No Objection Certificate" for candidates already working in Govt/PSU organizations.

Important Links

Resource Name Direct Link
AIIMS Gorakhpur Official Website Visit Site
Online Application Portal Apply Online Here
Official Notification PDF Download PDF

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you meet the eligibility criteria for each. However, you must submit separate applications and pay the separate application fees for each post.

Q2: Is the experience from a private hospital valid?

Yes, experience from a reputed private hospital is usually accepted, provided the hospital meets the bed-capacity requirements (if specified) and the experience is in a relevant field.

Q3: When will the CBT be conducted?

The exam date has not been officially announced yet. Typically, AIIMS conducts exams within 2-3 months after the application deadline. Keep checking the website for admit card updates.

Q4: What is the refund policy for the application fee?

The application fee is non-refundable. However, in some AIIMS recruitments, SC/ST candidates receive a refund after appearing in the exam. Check the detailed notification for specific refund clauses for Gorakhpur.

Q5: Is there any interview for the Junior Lab Technologist post?

No. According to the latest government guidelines, there is no interview for Group B and C posts. The selection is based entirely on the CBT and Skill Test (if applicable).

Q6: What happens if I am unable to produce my original certificates during DV?

AIIMS is very strict during Document Verification. If you fail to produce any original document or registration certificate, your candidature will be cancelled, and the next person in the merit list will be considered.

entry.title: Nanaji Deshmukh Veterinary University Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
Nanaji Deshmukh Veterinary University Recruitment 2026

Advertisement No. 316/स्था.II/विज्ञापन/2026: The Nanaji Deshmukh Pashu Chikitsa Vigyan Vishwavidyalaya (NDVSU), Jabalpur, has invited offline applications for 40 non-teaching posts on a contractual basis. The vacancies are for Accountant, Computer Operator, Lab Technician, Lab Attendant, and Peon/Watchman in its Animal Husbandry Diploma colleges. The last date to submit the application is February 04, 2026.

Post Name: Accountant, Computer Operator/Clerk, Lab Technician, Lab Attendant, Peon/Watchman
Last Date: 04 February 2026
Total Posts: 40

The Nanaji Deshmukh Veterinary Science University (NDVSU) in Jabalpur has released an employment notification for the recruitment of non-teaching staff for its Animal Husbandry Diploma colleges located in Jabalpur, Mhow, Rewa, Morena, and Bhopal. The recruitment aims to fill 40 vacancies for various Group-C level posts on a contractual (samvida) basis under the self-financed diploma program.

The selection for these posts will be based on a direct interview. There is no written examination, making it an excellent opportunity for eligible candidates to directly present their credentials and skills. The application process is entirely offline, requiring candidates to download the form, prepare a bank draft for the application fee, and submit the completed application package by post or in person at the university's Jabalpur office. Only candidates who are domiciles of Madhya Pradesh are eligible to apply.

NDVSU Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. The application must be submitted offline via post or by hand.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Nanaji Deshmukh Pashu Chikitsa Vigyan Vishwavidyalaya (NDVSU), Jabalpur
Post Names Accountant, Computer Operator, Lab Technician, Attendant, Peon
Total Vacancies 40
Last Date for Application Submission 04 February 2026 (up to 11:00 AM)
Application Mode Offline (Post or By Hand)
Job Type Contractual Government Job
Job Location Jabalpur, Mhow, Rewa, Morena, Bhopal (Madhya Pradesh)
Official Website www.ndvsu.org

Vacancy and Monthly Salary Details

The recruitment drive is distributed across five different posts with a fixed consolidated monthly salary.

Post Name Total Vacancies Consolidated Monthly Salary
Accountant (लेखापाल) 05 ₹28,700/-
Computer Operator/Clerk (कम्प्यूटर ऑपरेटर/लिपिक) 10 ₹19,500/-
Laboratory Technician (प्रयोगशाला तकनीशियन) 05 ₹25,300/-
Laboratory Attendant (प्रयोगशाला परिचारक) 05 ₹18,000/-
Peon/Watchman (भृत्य/चौकीदार) 15 ₹15,500/-
Total 40
Note: Increments on the basic salary will be based on the AICPIN (Consumer Price Index) as per university rules.

Application Fee

  • For Unreserved (UR) and OBC candidates: ₹800/-
  • For SC and ST candidates: ₹400/-

The fee must be paid in the form of a Bank Draft drawn in favor of "Nanaji Deshmukh Pashu Chikitsa Vigyan Vish ASST REG (GEN)", payable at Jabalpur.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet all the eligibility requirements, including domicile, employment registration, age, and education.

Criteria Details
Mandatory for All Posts
  1. Must be a Domicile of Madhya Pradesh.
  2. Must have a live registration with any Employment Exchange in Madhya Pradesh.
  3. Age Limit: 18 to 45 years (Age relaxation for reserved categories as per M.P. Govt. rules).
Post-wise Qualifications
Post NameMinimum Educational Qualification
AccountantBachelor's Degree in Commerce (B.Com) AND a Certificate/Diploma in Computer Application.
Computer Operator/ClerkHigher Secondary (12th) pass AND a Certificate/Diploma in Computer Application AND a valid CPCT scorecard from MAP-IT.
Laboratory TechnicianHigher Secondary (10+2) with Science stream passed in Second Division (min 50% marks) AND a Bachelor's Degree in Science.
Laboratory AttendantHigher Secondary (10+2) with Science stream.
Peon/Watchman8th Class pass.

Selection Process

The selection for all posts will be based solely on a personal interview. There will be no written examination.

  1. Application Scrutiny: The applications received will be scrutinized for eligibility.
  2. Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called for a personal interview. The schedule and list of candidates will be published on the university website: www.ndvsu.org.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates must bring all their original documents and two sets of self-attested photocopies to the interview for verification.
  4. Final Selection: The final merit list will be prepared based on the candidate's performance in the interview.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for this recruitment. The selection is based purely on the performance of the candidates in the personal interview. Candidates will be assessed on their subject knowledge, communication skills, and suitability for the post.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of the posts in this recruitment drive.

How to Prepare for the Interview

Since the interview is the only selection stage, preparation is key.

  • Know the Role and University: Research the Nanaji Deshmukh Veterinary Science University and understand the specific duties of the post you have applied for (e.g., accounting principles for Accountant, lab procedures for Technician).
  • Revise Core Subjects: Brush up on the basics of your qualifying degree/diploma. For the Computer Operator post, be prepared for questions on MS Office and general computer operations.
  • General Awareness: Have basic knowledge about Madhya Pradesh and current affairs.
  • Organize Your Documents: Arrange all original documents and photocopies neatly in a file to present at the interview.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no written test, no specific exam books are required. However, for interview preparation, candidates can refer to:

  • For Accountant: Basic books on Financial Accounting and Tally.
  • For Computer Operator: A practical guide to MS Office and computer fundamentals.
  • For Lab Technician/Attendant: Your 12th/Graduation level science textbooks.

How to Apply

The application process is strictly offline. Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Download the detailed notification and application form from the university website: www.ndvsu.org.
  2. Fill out the application form with all the required details.
  3. Prepare a Bank Draft for the applicable fee. The DD should be in favor of "Nanaji Deshmukh Pashu Chikitsa Vigyan Vish ASST REG (GEN)".
  4. Gather self-attested photocopies of all the required documents.
  5. Place the filled-in application form, the original Bank Draft, and all document photocopies in an envelope.
  6. Superscribe the envelope clearly with the name of the post you are applying for.
