  7. Submit the application by post or in person to the address below, ensuring it reaches before 11:00 AM on February 04, 2026.

Submission Address:
The Registrar's Office,
Nanaji Deshmukh Pashu Chikitsa Vigyan Vishwavidyalaya,
Administrative Building, Live Stock Farm, Adhartal,
Jabalpur - 482004 (M.P.)

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must attach photocopies of the following documents with their application form:

  1. Proof of Educational Qualification (8th, 12th, Degree, Diploma, CPCT, etc., as applicable).
  2. Proof of Age (e.g., 10th Class Mark Sheet).
  3. Madhya Pradesh Domicile Certificate.
  4. Live Registration Certificate from an MP Employment Exchange.
  5. Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC), if applicable.
  6. No Objection Certificate (NOC) if currently employed in a government organization.
  7. Any other relevant documents.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification & Application Form Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there a written test for this recruitment?

No, the notification clearly states that the selection will be based on a personal interview only.

Q2: Can I apply if I am not a resident of Madhya Pradesh?

No, one of the mandatory conditions is that the applicant must be a domicile (मूल निवासी) of Madhya Pradesh.

Q3: How do I pay the application fee?

The application fee must be paid via a Bank Draft. The details of the payee are mentioned in the notification. The original Bank Draft must be sent along with the application form.

Q4: Is a CPCT scorecard mandatory for the Computer Operator post?

Yes, the notification explicitly states that a valid CPCT scorecard issued by MAP-IT is a mandatory qualification for the post of Computer Operator/Clerk.

entry.title: UPPCB Recruitment 2025: Golden Opportunity for 224 Contractual Posts
entry.content:
UPPCB Recruitment 2025: Golden Opportunity for 224 Contractual Posts

A direct interview-based selection for Assistant Environmental Engineers, Scientific Officers, Clerks, and other roles. Last date to apply is November 29, 2025.

Post Name: Assistant Environmental Engineer, Junior Engineer, Scientific Officer, Clerk, Steno & Others
Last Date: 29/11/2025
Total Posts: 224

An exceptional career opportunity has emerged for skilled professionals and aspirants in Uttar Pradesh who are passionate about environmental conservation and public service. The Uttar Pradesh Pollution Control Board (UPPCB), Lucknow, has officially released its recruitment notification under Advertisement No. H34965/2025. This special recruitment drive aims to fill a total of 224 vacant positions across various technical and administrative domains to strengthen the state's environmental monitoring and regulatory framework.

This recruitment is being conducted on a contractual basis, with an initial tenure of three months, which may be extended based on performance and departmental requirements. One of the most significant highlights of this drive is the selection process. UPPCB has decided to forgo the traditional written examination, opting for a direct walk-in-interview method. This streamlines the hiring process, making it an excellent chance for candidates with relevant experience in engineering, science, and clerical fields to secure a prestigious position quickly. If you meet the eligibility criteria, this is your chance to contribute to a greener Uttar Pradesh.

UPPCB Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other key information to ensure a smooth application process. This is a state government contractual job opportunity located in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Authority Uttar Pradesh Pollution Control Board (UPPCB)
Application Start Date Announced (Candidates can apply now)
Last Date to Submit Application 29th November 2025, up to 6:00 PM
Interview Date To be announced later on the official website
Job Type State Government Contractual Job
Job Location Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

The UPPCB has announced a total of 224 vacancies distributed among several key positions. Each role comes with specific responsibilities and a competitive consolidated pay scale. Below is a detailed table outlining the number of posts for each position and the corresponding monthly salary.

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Assistant Environmental Engineer 27
Assistant Scientific Officer 15
Junior Engineer (JE) 38
Junior Assistant (Clerk) 33
Stenographer 29
Lab Assistant 40
Data Entry Operator 42
Total Vacancies 224
Note: The number of vacancies is tentative and subject to change at the discretion of the Uttar Pradesh Pollution Control Board. Candidates are advised to check the official website for any updates or corrigenda.

Application Fee Details

  • General / OBC / EWS Candidates: ₹0/- (No Fee)
  • SC / ST / PH Candidates: ₹0/- (No Fee)

As per the official notification, there is no application fee for any category of candidates. This initiative ensures that all eligible individuals have an equal opportunity to apply for these positions without any financial burden.

UPPCB Recruitment 2025 Eligibility Criteria

Before applying, candidates must carefully review the eligibility criteria, including educational qualifications and age limits, to ensure they qualify for their desired post. The cut-off date for calculating the age limit is July 1, 2025.

Post Name Eligibility Requirements
Assistant Environmental Engineer Candidates must possess a B.E./B.Tech degree in Civil, Chemical, or Environmental Engineering from a recognized university. A minimum of 3 years of post-qualification experience in pollution control, environmental monitoring, or a related field is mandatory.
Assistant Scientific Officer A Master's degree (M.Sc) in Chemistry, Biology, Environmental Science, or a related discipline. Candidates with a Ph.D. in a relevant field will be preferred. Relevant research or practical experience is required.
Junior Engineer (JE) A 3-year Diploma in Civil, Mechanical, or Environmental Engineering from a recognized polytechnic institute. At least 3 years of practical experience in the relevant field is essential.
Junior Assistant (Clerk) Must have passed the Intermediate (12th Class) examination. A valid CCC certificate from NIELIT (DOEACC) is mandatory. Typing speed of 25 WPM in Hindi and 30 WPM in English on a computer is required.
Stenographer A Bachelor's degree from any recognized university. A stenography speed of 80 WPM in Hindi/English and a typing speed of 30 WPM are required. Knowledge of office software is essential.
Lab Assistant A Bachelor's degree in Science (B.Sc) with Chemistry/Biology as a major subject. Experience working in a laboratory setting, handling samples, and preparing reports will be an advantage.
Data Entry Operator Must have passed Intermediate (12th Class) and possess a diploma in computer applications. A high typing speed with excellent accuracy is required. Experience in handling large datasets is preferred.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 21 Years
  • Maximum Age: 40 Years
  • Age relaxation will be provided to reserved category candidates (SC/ST/OBC) from Uttar Pradesh as per government rules.
    		•

    Selection Process

    The selection process for the UPPCB Recruitment 2025 is designed to be swift and efficient, focusing on identifying candidates with the right skills and experience. There will be no written examination. The entire process will be completed in two main stages:

    1. Application Screening: In the first stage, all applications received before the deadline will be meticulously scrutinized. The board will verify the information provided against the eligibility criteria mentioned in the notification. Applications that are incomplete, incorrect, or do not meet the minimum qualifications will be rejected. A shortlist of eligible candidates will then be prepared for the next stage.
    2. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called for a Personal Interview at the UPPCB headquarters in Lucknow. The interview panel will consist of subject matter experts and senior officials who will assess the candidate's technical knowledge, practical skills, problem-solving abilities, and suitability for the contractual role. The final selection will be based purely on the candidate's performance in the interview.

    Interview Pattern and Syllabus

    Since the selection is based solely on an interview, there is no formal written exam pattern. However, candidates must prepare thoroughly for the interview, which will serve as a comprehensive test of their knowledge and skills. The interview will focus on the following areas:

    • Technical Knowledge: Candidates will face questions directly related to their field of specialization. For instance, engineers can expect questions on waste management, air and water pollution control technologies, and environmental laws. Scientific officers will be tested on chemical analysis, biological indicators, and laboratory protocols.
    • Practical and Situational Questions: The panel may present hypothetical scenarios to judge a candidate's decision-making and problem-solving skills. For example, "How would you handle a report of an industrial chemical spill?"
    • Knowledge about UPPCB: Candidates should be well-versed with the functions, objectives, and recent initiatives of the Uttar Pradesh Pollution Control Board.
    • Current Environmental Issues: A good understanding of current national and state-level environmental challenges, policies, and news is crucial.
    • For Clerical/Admin Roles: For posts like Junior Assistant and DEO, the interview will focus on computer proficiency (MS Office), typing skills, and general office procedures. Stenographers will be tested on their shorthand and transcription accuracy.

    Physical Efficiency Test (PET) Details

    Candidates should note that there is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for any of the posts advertised in the UPPCB Recruitment 2025. The roles are primarily office-based or involve fieldwork that does not demand strenuous physical activity. The selection is based entirely on educational qualifications, work experience, and performance in the personal interview. Therefore, applicants do not need to prepare for any physical tests.

    How to Prepare for the UPPCB Interview

    Acing the interview is the only gateway to securing a position in this recruitment. A structured preparation strategy is key. Here’s a guide on how to prepare effectively:

    1. Deep Dive into Your Core Subjects: Revisit the fundamentals of your engineering, science, or commerce degree. Be prepared to answer in-depth technical questions. Focus on practical applications rather than just theoretical knowledge.
    2. Research UPPCB Thoroughly: Visit the official UPPCB website (uppcb.up.gov.in). Understand its vision, mission, organizational structure, and key functions. Read their latest annual reports, press releases, and guidelines.
    3. Stay Updated on Environmental Laws: Familiarize yourself with major environmental legislations in India, such as the Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974; Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981; and the Environmental Protection Act, 1986.
    4. Prepare for Common HR Questions: Be ready to answer questions like "Tell me about yourself," "Why do you want to work for UPPCB?" and "Describe a challenging situation you faced in your previous job." Prepare your answers in advance.
    5. Mock Interviews: Practice with a friend or mentor. This helps in building confidence, improving body language, and refining your answers. Record yourself to identify areas for improvement.
    6. Organize Your Documents: The interview call will require you to present original documents. Ensure you have all your certificates, mark sheets, experience letters, and other necessary documents neatly arranged in a folder.

    Best Books for Preparation

    While there is no written test, self-study using standard reference books can greatly enhance your knowledge for the technical interview. Here are some recommended books for different posts:

    • For Engineers (Environmental/Civil/Chemical):
      • "Environmental Engineering" by Howard S. Peavy, Donald R. Rowe, and George Tchobanoglous.
      • "Water Supply Engineering" by S.K. Garg.
      • "Introduction to Environmental Engineering and Science" by Gilbert M. Masters.
    • For Scientific Officers (Chemistry/Environmental Science):
      • "A Textbook of Environmental Chemistry and Pollution Control" by S.S. Dara.
      • "Vogel's Textbook of Quantitative Chemical Analysis" by G.H. Jeffery.
    • For Junior Assistant/DEO/Stenographer:
      • "Lucent's Computer" for fundamental computer knowledge.
      • Any standard book on Hindi and English typing practice.
      • "General English for All Competitive Examinations" by S.C. Gupta.

    How to Apply for UPPCB Recruitment 2025

    The application process is completely offline. Candidates must send their application form via post. Follow these steps carefully:

    1. First, download the official Application Form from the link provided in the "Important Links" section below. Take a clear printout of the form on A4 size paper.
    2. Fill in all the required details in the application form accurately, including your personal information, educational qualifications, and work experience. Paste a recent passport-sized photograph in the designated space.
    3. Gather self-attested photocopies of all the required documents (a detailed list is provided in the next section).
    4. Prepare an affidavit on a non-judicial stamp paper stating that there are no criminal cases pending against you.
    5. Place the filled application form, all self-attested documents, and the original affidavit in a large envelope. On the envelope, clearly write "Application for the post of [Your Post Name]".
    6. Send the envelope via Speed Post or Registered Post only to the following address:

      To,
      The Member Secretary,
      Uttar Pradesh Pollution Control Board,
      T.C.-12V, Vibhuti Khand, Gomti Nagar,
      Lucknow - 226010
    7. Ensure your application reaches the office on or before the last date, i.e., November 29, 2025, by 6:00 PM.

    Documents Needed to Apply

    You must attach self-attested copies of the following documents with your application form. Failure to attach any of these may lead to the rejection of your application.

    1. Printout of the filled Application Form.
    2. Class 10th Mark Sheet and Certificate (as proof of date of birth).
    3. Class 12th (Intermediate) Mark Sheet and Certificate.
    4. Mark Sheets and Degree/Diploma certificates for Graduation/Post-Graduation.
    5. CCC Certificate (for Junior Assistant post).
    6. Work Experience Certificates from previous employers.
    7. Caste Certificate (for SC/ST/OBC candidates, if claiming reservation).
    8. Domicile Certificate (for residents of Uttar Pradesh).
    9. Aadhaar Card or any other valid Photo ID proof.
    10. Original Affidavit regarding no criminal record.
    11. Two additional recent passport-sized photographs.

    Important Links

    Action Link
    Download Official Notification Click Here
    Download Application Form Click Here
    Official UPPCB Website Visit Website

    Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

    Question 1: Is this a permanent government job?

    No, this is a contractual recruitment. The initial appointment will be for a period of three months. The contract may be extended based on the board's requirements and the candidate's performance.

    Question 2: What is the last date to send the application form?

    Your completed application form, along with all the required documents, must reach the UPPCB office in Lucknow by 6:00 PM on November 29, 2025.

    Question 3: Can candidates from outside Uttar Pradesh apply for this recruitment?

    Yes, candidates from all over India are eligible to apply. However, age relaxation benefits will only be applicable to candidates who are domiciled in Uttar Pradesh and belong to the reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC).

    Question 4: Is there a written exam for the selection?

    No, there will be no written examination. The selection will be based directly on the performance in the personal interview after the initial screening of applications.

    Question 5: Do I need to pay any application fee?

    No, the UPPCB has not specified any application fee for this recruitment. The application is free for all categories.

entry.title: DRDO CEPTAM-11 Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for 764 STA-B & Technician Posts
entry.content:
DRDO CEPTAM-11 Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for 764 STA-B & Technician Posts

A Golden Opportunity to Join DRDO with Pride and Serve the Nation. Online Applications Expected to Start from December 9, 2025.

Post Name: Senior Technical Assistant-B (STA-B), Technician-A (Tech-A)
Last Date: To be announced
Total Posts: 764

India's premier defense research and development organization, the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO), has announced a major recruitment drive through its Centre for Personnel Talent Management (CEPTAM). An indicative advertisement, CEPTAM-11, has been released for the recruitment of 764 posts of Senior Technical Assistant-B (STA-B) and Technician-A (Tech-A). This is heralded as a "golden opportunity to join DRDO with pride and serve the nation" for young individuals aspiring to build a career in the field of science and technology.

This DRDO CEPTAM Recruitment campaign opens a prestigious pathway for talented diploma holders, science graduates, and ITI-certified candidates from across the country to contribute directly to the advancement of Indian defense technology. Selected candidates will have the privilege of working in various DRDO laboratories and establishments spread across the nation, where they will be involved in cutting-edge defense systems and technologies. The application process will be entirely online, tentatively expected to commence on December 9, 2025. This article provides all the available information, expected eligibility criteria, and a preparation strategy for this highly anticipated DRDO Recruitment.

DRDO CEPTAM-11 Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive. Aspirants are advised to keep these points in mind for future reference.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
Recruitment Agency Centre for Personnel Talent Management (CEPTAM)
Advertisement Number CEPTAM-11
Post Names Senior Technical Assistant-B (STA-B) & Technician-A (Tech-A)
Total Vacancies 764
Job Location All India (in various DRDO labs/establishments)
Application Mode Online
Tentative Application Start Date December 9, 2025

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

Under the CEPTAM-11 recruitment, a total of 764 posts have been announced, which are divided into two main categories. These posts form a crucial part of DRDO's technical cadre, providing vital support to the organization's research and development activities.

Sr. No. Post Name Number of Vacancies
01 Senior Technical Assistant-B (STA-B) 561
02 Technician-A (Tech-A) 203
Grand Total 764

Application Fee (Expected)

  • General (UR), OBC, and EWS Male Candidates: ₹ 100/-
  • All Female Candidates, SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen: NIL (Fee Exempted)

The application fee details will be confirmed in the detailed notification. Payment will be accepted through online modes only.

Eligibility Criteria: Education and Age Limit

Disclaimer: The detailed educational qualifications will be available in the official notification. The information below is indicative and based on previous DRDO CEPTAM recruitment patterns.

Educational Qualification (Expected)

Post Name Expected Educational Qualification
Senior Technical Assistant-B (STA-B) A Diploma in Engineering or Technology in the relevant subject from a recognized institute.
OR
A Bachelor's Degree in Science (B.Sc.) in the relevant subject from a recognized university.
(Relevant subjects may include Mechanical, Electrical, Electronics, Computer Science, Civil, Chemical Engineering, Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics, Library Science, etc.)
Technician-A (Tech-A) Passed 10th Class from a recognized board.
AND
A certificate from a recognized Industrial Training Institute (ITI) in the relevant trade.
(Potential trades may include Fitter, Turner, Machinist, Electrician, Electronics Mechanic, Welder, COPA, Carpenter, etc.)

Age Limit (Expected)

The age limit for both posts is expected to be uniform, with relaxations provided to reserved category candidates as per Government of India norms.

Post Name Minimum Age Maximum Age (UR)
STA-B and Tech-A 18 years 28 years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

  • SC/ST: 5 years
  • OBC (Non-Creamy Layer): 3 years
  • Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD): 10 years (UR), 13 years (OBC), 15 years (SC/ST)
  • Ex-Servicemen (ESM): 3 years after deduction of the military service rendered from the actual age.

Selection Process

The DRDO CEPTAM selection process is a multi-tiered, computer-based testing (CBT) system designed to ensure fairness and meritocracy.

  1. Tier-I (CBT) - Screening Test:
    • This is likely to be a common screening test for both STA-B and Tech-A posts.
    • It is a qualifying examination, and its marks are not typically used for the final merit list of STA-B but are used for Tech-A.
    • Candidates who clear this stage will be shortlisted for Tier-II in a specified ratio.
  2. Tier-II - Final Selection Stage:
    • For Senior Technical Assistant-B (STA-B): This will be a CBT that tests the candidate's knowledge in their specific discipline (e.g., Mechanical Engineering, Physics, etc.). The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the marks obtained in this Tier-II exam.
    • For Technician-A (Tech-A): This stage will be a practical Trade Test. It will assess the hands-on skills of the candidate in their respective ITI trade. This test is qualifying in nature (Pass/Fail). The final merit list for Tech-A will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Tier-I CBT.
  3. Document Verification and Medical Examination: Finally selected candidates will have to undergo document verification and a medical examination as per DRDO standards before the final appointment.

DRDO CEPTAM-11 Exam Pattern and Syllabus (Expected)

Understanding the exam pattern and syllabus is the first step toward a successful preparation strategy.

Tier-I CBT Exam Pattern (Common for STA-B & Tech-A)

Subject Number of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
Quantitative Ability/Aptitude 120 120 90 Minutes
General Intelligence & Reasoning Ability
General Awareness
English Language (Basic Knowledge)
General Science

Tier-II CBT Exam Pattern (For STA-B only)

Subject Number of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
Test specific to the subject of post-code (Diploma/B.Sc. Level) 100 100 90 Minutes

Syllabus for Tier-I (Common)

  • Quantitative Aptitude: Number System, HCF, LCM, Simplification, Decimals, Fractions, Ratio & Proportion, Percentage, Profit & Loss, Simple & Compound Interest, Time & Work, Time & Distance, Mensuration.
  • Reasoning Ability: Analogies, Similarities and Differences, Space Visualization, Problem Solving, Analysis, Judgment, Decision Making, Visual Memory, Coding and Decoding, Number Series, Non-Verbal Series.
  • General Awareness: Current events of national and international importance, History, Culture, Geography, Economic Scene, General Polity, Scientific Research, Defence related news.
  • English Language: Vocabulary, Grammar, Sentence Structure, Synonyms, Antonyms, Comprehension, Error Detection, Fill in the Blanks.
  • General Science: Physics, Chemistry, and Biology (up to 10th standard level).

Syllabus for Tier-II (STA-B)

The syllabus will be based on the essential qualification required for the post. For example, for Mechanical Engineering, topics will include Thermodynamics, Fluid Mechanics, Theory of Machines, Machine Design, Production Technology, etc. For Physics, topics would cover Mechanics, Optics, Electromagnetism, Modern Physics, etc.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for the posts of STA-B and Tech-A in DRDO CEPTAM recruitment. The selection is based entirely on performance in the written/trade tests.

How to Prepare for the DRDO CEPTAM-11 Exam

The DRDO CEPTAM exam is highly competitive. A disciplined and strategic approach is essential for success.

  1. Understand the Syllabus and Pattern: Thoroughly analyze the exam pattern and syllabus. Identify your strong and weak areas.
  2. Build a Strong Foundation (Tier-I):
    • Strengthen your fundamental concepts in General Science, Mathematics, and Reasoning up to the 10th-grade level using NCERT books.
    • Practice quantitative aptitude and reasoning questions regularly. Solving previous years' papers is highly beneficial.
    • For General Awareness, stay updated with the last 6-8 months of national and international events, with a special focus on news related to science, technology, and defense.
  3. Master Your Domain (Tier-II):
    • For STA-B: Conduct an in-depth study of your Diploma or B.Sc. curriculum. Focus on core principles and their practical applications. Practice solving objective-type questions from standard textbooks.
    • For Tech-A: Reinforce both the theoretical and practical aspects of your ITI trade. Pay special attention to workshop safety rules and the correct use of tools and equipment.
  4. Practice with Mock Tests: Regularly take online mock tests to assess your preparation level, improve time management, and get accustomed to the exam environment. Analyze your performance after each test to work on your weak spots.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books that can aid your preparation:

  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Fast Track Objective Arithmetic" by Rajesh Verma or "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Reasoning Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and regular reading of newspapers like The Hindu or The Indian Express.
  • General Science: NCERT textbooks from Class 6 to 10.
  • For STA-B Technical Subjects: Standard diploma/degree level textbooks for your specific engineering/science discipline (e.g., "Mechanical Engineering" by R.K. Jain, "Concepts of Physics" by H.C. Verma).
  • For Tech-A Trade Theory: ITI trade-specific books by Arihant Publications or Asian Publishers.

How to Apply for DRDO CEPTAM Recruitment

The application process will be conducted exclusively in online mode through the official DRDO website.

  1. Visit the official DRDO website: www.drdo.gov.in.
  2. Navigate to the "Careers" or "What's New" section and find the link for the CEPTAM advertisement board.
  3. Carefully read the detailed notification for "CEPTAM-11" to understand all eligibility criteria and instructions.
  4. Click on the "Apply Online" link (This link will be activated tentatively on December 9, 2025).
  5. Register by providing your basic details, a valid email ID, and a mobile number.
  6. Fill out the application form with accurate personal, educational, and other details.
  7. Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph, signature, and necessary educational and category certificates in the prescribed format.
  8. Pay the application fee, if applicable, through the online payment gateway.
  9. Review all the filled details before final submission. After submitting, take a printout of the application form for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

You will need the following documents while applying online and during document verification:

  1. 10th Class Marksheet and Certificate (for proof of date of birth).
  2. All Marksheets and Final Certificate of ITI / Diploma / B.Sc.
  3. Recent passport-size color photograph.
  4. Scanned copy of your signature.
  5. Reserved category certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD) in the format prescribed by the Government of India.
  6. A valid photo ID proof (such as Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, Passport).
  7. Discharge certificate for Ex-Servicemen (if applicable).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Detailed Notification Click Here (Link will be active soon)
Apply Online Click Here (Tentatively from 09.12.2025)
Official Website https://www.drdo.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: When will the online application for DRDO CEPTAM-11 start?

As per the indicative advertisement, the online application link is tentatively expected to be activated on the DRDO website on December 9, 2025.

Question 2: What are the total vacancies for STA-B and Tech-A?

There are 561 vacancies for STA-B and 203 vacancies for Tech-A, making a total of 764 vacancies.

Question 3: Can final-year students apply for these posts?

Typically, candidates must have completed their essential educational qualification by the closing date of the application. Final-year students are usually not eligible unless specified otherwise in the detailed notification.

Question 4: What is the selection process for these posts?

The selection process is expected to have two tiers. For STA-B, both tiers will be computer-based tests. For Tech-A, Tier-I will be a CBT, and Tier-II will be a practical trade test.

Question 5: Is there an interview for DRDO CEPTAM recruitment?

Based on previous CEPTAM patterns, there is no interview for the posts of STA-B and Tech-A. The selection is based purely on the performance in the written examination and/or trade test.

entry.title: Jammu & Kashmir Police Recruitment 2026: 1815 Constable Posts Open
entry.content:
Jammu & Kashmir Police Recruitment 2026: 1815 Constable Posts Open

A major recruitment drive by JKSSB for Constable (Executive) positions in the J&K Home Department. Online applications open from January 19 to February 17, 2026.

Post Name: Constable (Executive Police)
Last Date: 17 February 2026
Total Posts: 1815

The Jammu & Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) has released a significant employment notification (Advertisement Notification No. 12 of 2025) for the recruitment of Constables (Executive) in the J&K Police, Home Department. Based on an indent from the Home Department, the JKSSB is set to fill a massive 1815 posts of Constable (Executive Police) for the Divisional Cadre of Jammu and Kashmir under direct recruitment. This is a golden opportunity for the domiciled youth of Jammu & Kashmir who aspire to don the uniform and serve the people of the Union Territory.

This large-scale recruitment drive is a crucial step towards strengthening the police force and providing a stable and respected career path for 10th-pass candidates. The selection process will be a comprehensive evaluation of the candidates' mental and physical abilities, involving a Written Examination, a Physical Standard Test (PST), and a Physical Endurance Test (PET). The application process is entirely online, and eligible candidates are invited to apply through the official JKSSB website starting from January 19, 2026. This article provides a detailed breakdown of all essential information, including vacancies, eligibility, the multi-stage selection process, and a step-by-step guide on how to apply.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government positions in the J&K Police force, with postings across the Union Territory. Key dates and details are as follows:

Particulars Details
Recruiting Body Jammu & Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB)
Department Home Department, J&K Police
Job Type Permanent Government Job (UT Cadre)
Job Location Jammu & Kashmir
Online Application Start Date 19 January 2026
Last Date for Online Application 17 February 2026

Vacancy Details

A total of 1815 posts of Constable (Executive Police) have been advertised. These vacancies are divided between the Jammu Division and the Kashmir Division, with further reservations for various social and horizontal categories as per J&K Government rules.

Division Total Vacancies
Jammu Division 934
Kashmir Division 881
Total Vacancies 1815
Note: The actual number of posts may undergo increase or decrease during the selection process. The detailed category-wise breakup (OM, SC, ST, OBC, EWS, RBA, etc.) is provided in Annexure-A of the official notification.

Application Fee

  • Fee for General Category candidates: Rs. 700/- (Rupees Seven Hundred only).
  • Fee for candidates belonging to SC, ST-1, ST-2, and EWS categories: Rs. 600/- (Rupees Six Hundred only).

The fee can be paid only through online modes such as Net Banking, Credit, or Debit cards. The fee is non-refundable.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill all eligibility criteria regarding domicile, age, and educational qualification as of the cut-off date (17.02.2026) to be considered for the posts.

Criteria Requirement Details
Domicile The candidate must be a Domicile of the Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir and must possess a valid Domicile Certificate issued by the Competent Authority on or before the cut-off date.
Educational Qualification The minimum educational qualification required is Matriculation (10th pass) from a recognized board of school education. Candidates must produce the relevant certificate as proof of qualification on or before the cut-off date.
Age Limit (as on 01.01.2025)
  • Minimum Age: 18 years
  • Maximum Age (OM): 28 years
  • Maximum Age (In-Service Police Personnel): 30 years
  • Relaxation for Ex-Servicemen is applicable as per rules.
  • Candidates should not have been born before 01.01.1997 or after 01.01.2007 (for OM category).

Selection Process

The selection criteria for the post of Constable shall comprise a three-stage process designed to test the candidates' knowledge, physical standards, and endurance.

  1. Written Test: The first stage is a Written Examination to assess the merit of the candidates. It will be an objective-type MCQ test. Candidates possessing NCC Certificates will be awarded bonus marks (5% for 'C' Cert, 3% for 'B' Cert, 2% for 'A' Cert).
  2. Physical Standard Test (PST): Based on the merit of the written test, candidates will be shortlisted for the PST in a ratio of 1:6 for each category. The PST is qualifying in nature and assesses whether the candidate meets the minimum physical standards.
  3. Physical Endurance Test (PET): Candidates who qualify the PST will be required to undergo the PET. This test is also of a qualifying nature and assesses the physical fitness and endurance of the candidate through various events.
  4. Medical Examination and Document Verification (DV): Candidates who clear all the above stages will be medically examined and their documents will be verified before the final selection list is prepared.

Exam Pattern

The first stage of selection is a competitive written examination. The details of the exam pattern are as follows:

  • Mode of Exam: Objective Type Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs).
  • Language: The questions will be set in English Language only.
  • Negative Marking: There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.
  • Syllabus: The detailed syllabus for the written examination will be notified separately and made available on the official website of JKSSB. Generally, it is expected to cover topics like General Knowledge & Current Affairs, Reasoning Ability, General English, and Elementary Mathematics.

PET

The Physical Standard Test (PST) and Physical Endurance Test (PET) are mandatory qualifying stages. The standards are detailed in Annexure-B of the notification.

Physical Standard Test (PST)

  • For Males:
    • Height: 5'-6" (minimum)
    • Chest girth: 32" (unexpanded)
    • Chest girth: 33 ½" (expanded)
  • For Females:
    • Height: 5'-2" (minimum)
  • Relaxation: A height relaxation of 2" is available for candidates belonging to the Gorkha Community and Bot Tribe.

Physical Endurance Test (PET)

  • For Male Candidates:
    • Long Race: 1600 meters in 6 ½ minutes (Six minutes thirty seconds)
    • Pushups: 20 (one cycle of up and down to be counted as one)
  • For Female Candidates:
    • Long Race: 1000 meters in 6 ½ minutes (Six minutes thirty seconds)
    • Shot Put (4Kgs): 14 ½ feet in three attempts
  • Relaxation for Ex-Servicemen: Relaxed standards for PET are applicable for Ex-Servicemen based on their age group (Upto 30 years, 30-40 years, and Above 40 years).

How to Prepare

Success in the JKSSB Police Constable recruitment requires a balanced preparation for both the written test and the physical tests.

Preparation for Written Test

  1. Understand the Syllabus: As soon as the syllabus is released on the JKSSB website, analyze it thoroughly. Expected topics include General Knowledge (with a focus on J&K), Reasoning, Basic Mathematics, and General English.
  2. Build a Strong Foundation: Study from standard books for each subject. For GK, read newspapers daily. For Maths, practice from 10th-grade level books.
  3. Practice Regularly: Solve previous years' JKSSB question papers and take mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy. Pay attention to the negative marking scheme.

Preparation for Physical Tests

  1. Start Early: Do not wait for the written test results. Start your physical preparation as soon as you apply.
  2. Running: Begin with jogging and gradually increase your distance and pace. Use a stopwatch to track your timing for the 1600m/1000m race.
  3. Strength Training: For push-ups (males) and shot put (females), focus on building upper body strength. Practice the correct form to avoid injuries and maximize performance.
  4. Maintain a Healthy Lifestyle: A balanced diet, adequate hydration, and proper rest are crucial for improving physical performance.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended books for the written exam:

  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and "Know Your State - Jammu & Kashmir" by Arihant.
  • Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Elementary Mathematics: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online through the JKSSB's official portal.

  1. Visit the official JKSSB website: https://jkssb.nic.in.
  2. If you are a new user, you will need to register first by creating a login ID and password.
  3. Log in and find the "Apply for Various Posts" link under the advertisement notification "12 of 2025".
  4. Fill out the application form carefully with your personal details, educational qualifications, and other required information.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your recent photograph, signature, and other required documents like Domicile Certificate and Category Certificate.
  6. Pay the application fee online using Net Banking, Credit, or Debit card.
  7. Review your application carefully before final submission and take a printout of the submitted form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates need to have the following documents ready (in original and for uploading) during the application and verification process:

  1. A valid Domicile Certificate of the UT of J&K.
  2. Matriculation (10th) Diploma/Certificate for proof of date of birth.
  3. Valid Category Certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.) as on the cut-off date.
  4. Certificate for Ex-Servicemen (Form-XIV), if applicable.
  5. Two recent passport-size color photographs.
  6. A valid Photo ID Proof in original (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online (from 19.01.2026) Click Here
Download Official Notification Click Here
Official JKSSB Website Click Here

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the JKSSB Constable recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is February 17, 2026.

Question 2: What is the minimum educational qualification required?

The candidate must have passed the Matriculation (10th class) examination from a recognized board.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the written test?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer.

Question 4: Can candidates from outside J&K apply?

No, only candidates who are Domiciles of the Union Territory of Jammu & Kashmir are eligible to apply for these posts.

entry.title: Golden Job Opportunity in Israel: 5000 Home Based Caregivers to be Recruited
entry.content:
Golden Job Opportunity in Israel: 5000 Home Based Caregivers to be Recruited

A fantastic chance for Indian nationals to work abroad with a monthly salary of up to ₹1.37 Lakh, facilitated by NSDC and HKRN.

Post Name: Home Based Caregiver
Last Date: Applications are ongoing
Total Posts: 5000

A major opportunity has emerged for Indian citizens aspiring to work abroad. In collaboration with the National Skill Development Corporation (NSDC) and the Haryana Kaushal Rozgar Nigam (HKRN), a detailed Job Description (JD) has been issued for the recruitment of 5000 Home Based Caregivers in Israel. This recruitment drive is aimed at skilled and trained individuals who wish to build a career in the field of Caregiving Services and are eager to gain international experience.

This recruitment is open to both male and female candidates, with 90% of the posts reserved for women and 10% for men. Selected candidates will be appointed to care for Persons with Disabilities (PWDs) in Israel, particularly elderly disabled individuals. This is a live-in caregiver role, where candidates will be required to reside at the employer's home and assist with their daily activities and household management. This program not only offers an attractive salary but also gives candidates a chance to experience the culture and work environment of a new country. It is crucial to note that while HKRN and NSDC are acting as the sole facilitators in this process, the final decision of selection and rejection rests entirely with the Israeli employer.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details of this international recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Facilitating OrganizationsNSDC (National Skill Development Corporation) & HKRN (Haryana Kaushal Rozgar Nigam)
Post NameHome Based Caregiver
Total Posts5000
Job LocationIsrael
Contract DurationTwo years (Renewable)
Application ModeOnline via NSDC/HKRN Portals

Vacancy Breakdown and Gender Distribution

A total of 5000 Home Based Caregiver positions are to be filled through this recruitment drive. These posts are divided based on gender to meet the specific requirements of the employers.

Gender Percentage of Posts Estimated Number of Posts
Female 90% 4500
Male 10% 500

Salary and Benefits Package

Selected candidates will be offered an attractive monthly salary along with other facilities. However, certain deductions will also be made from the candidate's salary.

Description Amount/Information
Gross Monthly Salary 5880.02 NIS (Israeli Shekel), which is approximately 1612 USD or around ₹ 1,37,745 per month.
Medical Insurance Provided by the employer, but 164.91 NIS per month will be deducted from the candidate's salary.
Accommodation Provided by the employer, but a deduction ranging from 305.34 NIS to 559.90 NIS per month will be made from the candidate's salary, depending on the location.
Food Provided by the employer, but if the candidate dines at the workplace, approximately 10% of the gross salary may be deducted. The total deduction will not exceed 25% of the monthly salary.
Working Days 6 days a week.
Holidays 9 days of religious/national holidays + 16 days of annual leave.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates applying for this role must meet the following physical, educational, and professional criteria:

Criteria Type Requirement
Age Limit Between 25 and 45 years.
Minimum Education At least 10th class pass.
Physical Standards Minimum height of 1.5 meters and minimum weight of 45 kilograms. The candidate must be physically and mentally healthy.
Language Skills Certified English language skills at an intermediate level.
Passport A passport with at least 3 years of validity is mandatory.
Professional Qualification and Certification The candidate must have one of the following qualifications:
  1. A training diploma in Nursing, Physiotherapy, Nurse Assistant, or Midwifery from a recognized Indian training provider.
  2. Qualification in GDA (General Duty Assistant) / ANM / GNM / B.Sc Nursing / Post B.Sc Nursing.
  3. A certificate of successful completion of a caregiving course of at least 990 hours (including OJT), recognized or supervised by a competent Indian regulatory authority.
Other Conditions The candidate should not have worked in Israel before. The candidate's spouse, parents, or children must not be currently working or residing in Israel.

Selection Process and Job Duties

The selection of candidates will be through a straightforward process that primarily involves a video interview. The final authority for selection is reserved with the Israeli employer.

Selection Process

  1. Application and Screening: Initial screening of applications received through the NSDC/HKRN portal will be conducted.
  2. Video Interview: Eligible candidates will have to go through a recorded video interview process.
  3. Final Selection: The final selection will be made by the Israeli employer based on the interview and documents.

Job Description and Key Responsibilities

The selected caregiver will work as a live-in caretaker. Their main duties will include:

  • Assisting the Person with Disability (PWD) with daily activities such as dressing, bathing, hygiene, and using the toilet.
  • Preparing meals, cooking, feeding, and cleaning up.
  • Administering and supervising prescribed medications.
  • Lifting, transferring, and assisting the PWD with mobility.
  • Cleaning the PWD's living quarters, shopping for groceries, and doing laundry.
  • Accompanying the PWD outside the home and performing any other special duties required for their care.

Application and Recruitment Related Costs

Candidates will have to bear certain costs themselves at various stages of the recruitment process. These costs will be payable by the candidate directly to the respective departments or agencies. A detailed breakdown of the costs is given below:

Service/Process Estimated Cost (in INR) When to Pay
NSDC Registration & Processing Fee (including pre-departure orientation) ₹ 10,261.80 + GST To NSDC after the contract letter is issued.
Medical Examination Fee ₹ 5,644 At the medical center before the interview.
Police Report Fee ₹ 513.08 To the concerned department before the interview.
Passport Issuance Fee ₹ 2,052.28 To the concerned department before the interview.
Visa Fee ₹ 3,798.43 At the time of the visa process after receiving the contract letter.
Recorded Video Interview Cost ₹ 4,275.46 + GST To NSDC before the video interview.
Flight Expenses (One-way) As per market cost After the visa is issued.
Fee for services to the Israeli Placement Bureau ₹ 1,54,564 (in three installments) First installment before or within 3 days of arrival in Israel, and the remaining two after arrival.
Additional cost to the Placement Bureau after 26 and 38 months ₹ 26,575 (each) After 26 and 38 months of employment, respectively.
Disclaimer: All the costs mentioned above are indicative and may change based on actual exchange rates and market conditions.

How to Prepare for This Opportunity

Success in securing this international job requires diligent preparation, not for a written exam, but for the overall process.

  1. Documentation: The most critical step is to get all your documents in order. Ensure your educational and professional certificates are authentic and readily available. Apply for a passport immediately if you don't have one with at least 3 years of validity.
  2. Language Skills: Brush up on your English communication skills. Since the interview will be recorded, practice speaking clearly and confidently. Your ability to understand and respond in English will be a key selection factor.
  3. Prepare for the Video Interview:
    • Research common caregiver interview questions (e.g., "Why do you want to be a caregiver?", "How do you handle a difficult patient?", "Describe your experience with elderly care.").
    • Practice recording yourself answering these questions. Pay attention to your body language, tone of voice, and clarity of speech.
    • Dress professionally for the recorded interview.
  4. Understand the Role: Deeply understand the duties and responsibilities mentioned in the job description. Be prepared to talk about how your training and skills make you a suitable candidate for a live-in caregiver role.
  5. Financial Planning: Review the cost sheet carefully. Ensure you have the necessary funds arranged for various stages like medical tests, visa fees, and the placement bureau fee.

How to Apply for the Caregiver Position in Israel

The application process for this recruitment will be conducted online through the portals of NSDC or the respective State Skill Development Corporation (like HKRN). Interested candidates should follow these general steps:

  1. Visit the Official Portal: Go to the official website of NSDC International or HKRN and look for the advertisement related to this recruitment.
  2. Registration: If you are a new user, register yourself on the portal. Enter all your personal, educational, and professional information accurately.
  3. Fill the Application Form: Fill out the application form for the "Home Based Caregiver - Israel" vacancy.
  4. Upload Documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport, educational certificates, professional qualification certificates, and other required documents.
  5. Fee Payment: Pay the registration and other related fees at the specified time.
  6. Submit Application: After reviewing all the information, submit your application finally.

Candidates are advised to regularly check the official websites of NSDC and HKRN for the latest updates and detailed instructions.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Official Information (via HKRN) Click Here
NSDC International https://nsdcinternational.com/
Haryana Kaushal Rozgar Nigam (HKRN) https://hkrnl.itiharyana.gov.in/

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Who can apply for this job?

Male and female candidates between the ages of 25 and 45, who have passed at least 10th grade, know English, and have a prescribed qualification or diploma in nursing/caregiving, can apply.

Question 2: What is the monthly salary?

The gross monthly salary is approximately ₹1.37 lakh, from which some deductions will be made for medical insurance, accommodation, and food.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection process primarily involves an online or recorded video interview, based on which the Israeli employer will make the final decision.

Question 4: Is any prior experience required for this recruitment?

The notification does not directly mention experience, but it is mandatory to have a training or diploma in caregiving/nursing from a recognized institute, which may include on-the-job training (OJT).

Question 5: Are there any costs involved in the recruitment process?

Yes, candidates will have to pay various fees themselves for NSDC registration, medical examination, visa, flight tickets, and services of the Israeli placement bureau.

entry.title: Punjab National Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 for 5138 Posts
entry.content:
Punjab National Bank Apprentice Recruitment 2026 for 5138 Posts

Notification No: HRD/APPRENTICE/ADVT/2025-26: Punjab National Bank (PNB) has invited online applications for the engagement of 5138 Apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961. This is a massive opportunity for graduates to gain hands-on banking experience. The online registration process is open from February 08, 2026, to February 24, 2026.

Post Name: Apprentice
Last Date: 24 February 2026
Total Posts: 5138

Punjab National Bank (PNB) has released a detailed notification for the engagement of Apprentices for the Financial Year 2025-26. The bank aims to fill 5138 training seats across all states and union territories in India. This one-year apprenticeship program offers young graduates a chance to learn the nuances of banking operations, customer service, and financial products directly from industry professionals.

The selection process involves an Online Written Test followed by a Test of Local Language and a Medical Examination. Selected candidates will be paid a monthly stipend ranging from ₹12,300 to ₹15,000 depending on the branch category. The application process is entirely online through the PNB and BFSI SSC portals. This engagement is strictly for training purposes and does not guarantee permanent employment in the bank.

PNB Apprentice Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. The online exam is tentatively scheduled for the first week of March 2026.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Punjab National Bank (PNB)
Post Name Apprentice
Total Vacancies 5138
Application Start Date 08 February 2026
Application Last Date 24 February 2026
Online Exam Date (Tentative) 1st week of March 2026
Training Duration 1 Year (12 Months)
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Apprenticeship Training
Official Website www.pnb.bank.in

Complete State-wise Vacancy Details

The 5138 seats are distributed across various states and union territories. Candidates can apply for vacancies in one state only. Below is the complete list:

State/UT Total Seats State/UT Total Seats
Andaman & Nicobar1Lakshadweep1
Andhra Pradesh56Madhya Pradesh180
Arunachal Pradesh3Maharashtra232
Assam132Manipur13
Bihar310Meghalaya8
Chandigarh58Mizoram3
Chhattisgarh81Nagaland2
Dadra & Nagar Haveli1Odisha142
Daman & Diu1Puducherry2
Delhi417Punjab462
Goa7Rajasthan363
Gujarat138Sikkim3
Haryana213Tamil Nadu121
Himachal Pradesh162Telangana69
Jammu & Kashmir53Tripura28
Jharkhand95Uttar Pradesh814
Karnataka64Uttarakhand152
Kerala83West Bengal415
Leh2Grand Total5138
Note: The number of training seats is provisional and may vary. Candidates must refer to the notification for circle-wise breakdowns within states.

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

  • For General/OBC/EWS Candidates: ₹800/- + GST (Total ₹944/-)
  • For Female (All Categories)/SC/ST/PwBD Candidates: ₹600/- + GST (Total ₹708/-) for Female, ₹200/- + GST (Total ₹236/-) for SC/ST/PwBD

The fee includes application processing, examination, and intimation charges. It must be paid online.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 01.01.2026)

Candidates must fulfill the age, educational qualification, and local language proficiency criteria to be eligible.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification Must possess a Graduate Degree in any discipline from a recognized University/Institute. The result must have been declared on or before 31.12.2025. Candidates registered under NATS must have passed graduation not more than 4 years before the cut-off date.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 20 Years
  • Maximum Age: 28 Years
(Candidates must have been born between 02.01.1998 and 01.01.2006).
Age Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and 10 years for PwBD candidates.
Local Language Candidates applying for a particular state should be proficient (reading, writing, speaking, and understanding) in the local language of that state. (See Annexure in notification for state-wise languages).

Selection Process

The selection process consists of multiple stages:

  1. Online Written Test: All eligible candidates will have to appear for an online objective-type examination.
  2. Test of Local Language: Candidates who qualify in the written test and do not have evidence of studying the local language in 10th/12th standard will have to undergo a language proficiency test.
  3. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates will be called for verification of their original documents.
  4. Medical Examination: Final engagement is subject to being declared medically fit.

Exam Pattern

The online written examination will consist of objective-type questions.

Section No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
General / Financial Awareness252560 Minutes (Composite Time)
General English2525
Quantitative & Reasoning Aptitude2525
Computer Knowledge2525
Total100100
  • Medium of Test: English and Hindi (except for the General English section).
  • Negative Marking: There is no mention of negative marking in the summary, but candidates should check the detailed instructions in the exam call letter.

Stipend Details

Apprentices will receive a monthly stipend based on the branch category where they are posted.

Branch Category Monthly Stipend
Rural / Semi-Urban / Urban Branches ₹12,300/-
Metro Branches ₹15,000/-

How to Prepare for the Exam

A balanced preparation strategy covering all four sections is essential.

  • Financial Awareness: Focus on banking terms, RBI policies, PNB's products, and current financial news.
  • Reasoning & Quant: Practice standard banking exam questions on puzzles, seating arrangement, data interpretation, and arithmetic. Speed is key.
  • English: Brush up on grammar, vocabulary, and reading comprehension.
  • Computer Knowledge: Study basic computer concepts, MS Office, and internet usage.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard banking preparation books.

  • Banking Awareness: "Banking Awareness" by Arihant Experts.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.

How to Apply Online

The application process involves registration on the apprenticeship portal and the BFSI SSC portal.

  1. NAPS Registration: Register on the NAPS portal (www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) as a candidate. Note down your registration number.
  2. Apply for Exam: Visit the BFSI SSC website (www.bfsissc.com) or click the link on the PNB website.
  3. Register for the PNB Apprenticeship Program 2025-26.
  4. Fill in your personal and educational details and upload the required documents (photo, signature, caste certificate).
  5. Enter your NAPS registration number.
  6. Pay the examination fee online.
  7. Submit the application and take a printout for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

Candidates must have scanned copies of the following documents ready:

  1. Recent passport-size photograph (JPEG, <1 MB).
  2. Aadhaar Card (Front & Back) (JPEG, <1 MB).
  3. PAN Card (JPEG, <1 MB).
  4. Qualifying Degree Marksheet/Certificate (PDF, <1 MB).
  5. Caste Certificate (if applicable) (PDF, <1 MB).
  6. PwBD Certificate (if applicable) (PDF, <1 MB).

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification PDF Click Here
Apply Online (BFSI SSC Portal) Click Here
NAPS Registration Portal Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is this a permanent job in PNB?

No, this is a one-year apprenticeship training program. The bank has no obligation to offer regular employment after the completion of the training.

Q2: Can I apply for multiple states?

No, a candidate can apply for training seats in one state only.

Q3: Is the local language test mandatory?

It is mandatory only if you cannot produce a 10th or 12th standard mark sheet evidencing that you have studied the specified local language of the applied state.

Q4: What is the training duration?

The total duration of the training is 1 year, which includes 2 weeks of basic training and 50 weeks of on-the-job training.

entry.title: RRB Group D Recruitment 2026 for 22,000+ Posts
entry.content:
RRB Group D Recruitment 2026 for 22,000+ Posts

Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) are set to announce a massive recruitment drive for approximately 22,000 Level-1 (formerly Group D) posts across various zones of Indian Railways. This is a golden opportunity for candidates who have passed the 10th standard or have an ITI qualification. The selection will be based on a Computer Based Test (CBT) followed by a Physical Efficiency Test (PET). The official notification with application dates is expected soon.

Post Name: Level-1 Posts (Track Maintainer, Helper, Assistant Pointsman, etc.)
Last Date: To Be Announced
Total Posts: 22,000+ (Approx.)

The Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs), on behalf of Railway Recruitment Cells (RRCs), are preparing to release a Centralised Employment Notice (CEN) for a large-scale recruitment of various Level-1 posts. According to the latest updates, approximately 22,000 vacancies will be filled in this drive. These posts form the operational backbone of the Indian Railways and include crucial roles like Track Maintainer Grade-IV, Helper/Assistant in various technical departments (Electrical, Mechanical, S&T), and Assistant Pointsman.

This upcoming recruitment is open to candidates across India and offers a stable and secure career with the Government of India. The selection process is expected to follow the established multi-stage procedure, starting with a single-stage Computer Based Test (CBT), followed by a Physical Efficiency Test (PET) for shortlisted candidates, and finally, Document Verification and Medical Examination. The application process will be entirely online through the official RRB websites.

RRB Group D Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must keep an eye on the official RRB websites for the upcoming notification with the final dates and details.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs)
Post Name Level-1 (Group D) Posts
Total Vacancies 22,000+ (Approximately)
Application Start Date To Be Announced
Application Last Date To Be Announced
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Central Government Job
Job Location Various Railway Zones across India
Official Website List of all RRB Websites

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill over 22,000 vacancies for various Level-1 posts across all zones of Indian Railways.

Post Category Pay Level (7th CPC) Total Vacancies
Level-1 Posts (Track Maintainer, Helper, Assistant Pointsman, Porter, etc.) Level-1 (Pay Matrix) 22,000+
Note: The detailed zone-wise and post-wise vacancy table will be available in the official notification. Candidates must choose only one RRB/RRC for their application.

Application Fee (Expected)

  • For all candidates (UR/OBC/EWS): ₹500/- (Out of this, ₹400 is typically refunded after appearing in the CBT).
  • For SC/ST, Ex-Servicemen, PwBD, Female, Transgender, and Economically Backward Class candidates: ₹250/- (This entire amount is typically refunded after appearing in the CBT).

The fee must be paid online. The refund is credited to the bank account from which the payment was made.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet the age and educational qualification criteria as specified in the notification.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification 10th Pass (Matriculation) from a recognized Board.
OR
ITI from institutions recognized by NCVT/SCVT or equivalent.
OR
National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) granted by NCVT.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 33 Years
(The age reckoning date will be specified in the official notification).
Age Relaxation: Upper age limit is relaxable by 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and as per rules for Ex-Servicemen and PwBD candidates.

Selection Process

The selection process for RRB Level-1 posts is a multi-stage procedure designed to ensure a fair and comprehensive evaluation.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT): All eligible candidates will have to undergo a single-stage CBT. The marks from this test will be normalized to account for variations in difficulty levels across different shifts.
  2. Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Based on the merit in the CBT, candidates numbering three times the community-wise vacancies will be shortlisted for the PET. This test is mandatory and qualifying in nature.
  3. Document Verification (DV) and Medical Examination: Candidates who qualify in the PET will be called for Document Verification. Following successful DV, candidates will be sent for a mandatory medical fitness test conducted by the Railway Administration to ensure they meet the prescribed medical standards for the post.
  4. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the marks obtained in the Computer Based Test (CBT), provided the candidate has qualified in the PET and passed the Medical Examination.

Exam Pattern (Computer Based Test)

The CBT is the primary stage of the selection process and will be an objective-type test.

Subject Number of Questions Maximum Marks
General Science2525
Mathematics2525
General Intelligence & Reasoning3030
General Awareness and Current Affairs2020
Total100100
  • Duration: 90 minutes (120 minutes for eligible PwBD candidates).
  • Negative Marking: There will be negative marking, and 1/3rd mark shall be deducted for each wrong answer.
  • Syllabus:
    • General Science: Physics, Chemistry, and Life Sciences of the 10th standard level.
    • Mathematics: Number system, BODMAS, Decimals, Fractions, LCM, HCF, Ratio and Proportion, Percentages, Mensuration, Time and Work, Time and Distance, SI & CI, Profit and Loss, etc.
    • General Intelligence & Reasoning: Analogies, Coding and Decoding, Syllogism, Jumbling, Venn Diagram, Data Interpretation, etc.
    • General Awareness and Current Affairs: Current Affairs in Science & Technology, Sports, Culture, Personalities, Economics, Politics.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

The PET is mandatory and qualifying in nature. Passing the PET is essential to be considered for the next stage.

Gender Event Qualifying Standard
Male Candidates Weight Lifting Should be able to lift and carry 35 Kg of weight for a distance of 100 metres in 2 minutes in one chance without putting the weight down.
Running Should be able to run for a distance of 1000 metres (1 KM) in 4 minutes and 15 seconds in one chance.
Female Candidates Weight Lifting Should be able to lift and carry 20 Kg of weight for a distance of 100 metres in 2 minutes in one chance without putting the weight down.
Running Should be able to run for a distance of 1000 metres (1 KM) in 5 minutes and 40 seconds in one chance.

How to Prepare

A disciplined approach covering both the CBT and PET is required.

  • For CBT: Build a strong foundation in Mathematics and General Science from NCERT books up to the 10th standard. Practice reasoning questions daily. Stay updated with current affairs for at least the last 6-8 months before the exam. Solve previous years' RRB Group D papers to understand the pattern and difficulty level.
  • For PET: Do not neglect your physical fitness. Start with light jogging and gradually build stamina to meet the running criteria. Practice lifting and carrying the specified weight to get accustomed to the task. A balanced diet and regular exercise are key.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for their CBT preparation.

  • General Science: NCERT Textbooks for Class 9th & 10th; "Lucent's General Science".
  • Mathematics: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Awareness & Current Affairs: A national newspaper and a monthly competitive magazine like Pratiyogita Darpan or Speedy Current Affairs.

How to Apply Online

The application process will be online through any of the official RRB websites.

  1. Go to the official website of the RRB you wish to apply for (e.g., RRB Ranchi, RRB Chandigarh, etc.).
  2. Find the link for the Centralised Employment Notice (CEN) for Level-1 Posts and click on it.
  3. Read the instructions carefully and click on "New Registration".
  4. Fill in your basic details like name, date of birth, mobile number, and email ID to register.
  5. Log in with your registration credentials and fill out the detailed application form with your educational and personal details.
  6. Select your preferred posts and railway zones in order of priority.
  7. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other required certificates (like caste certificate).
  8. Pay the application fee online.
  9. Review your application and submit it. Download and take a printout of the final application for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should have the following scanned documents ready before starting the online application:

  1. Recent passport-size colour photograph.
  2. Scanned signature.
  3. 10th Class / Matriculation certificate for proof of date of birth.
  4. ITI/NAC certificate (if applicable).
  5. SC/ST certificate in the prescribed format (for fee waiver and reservation).
  6. OBC-NCL certificate in the prescribed format.
  7. A valid mobile number and email ID.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
List of All Official RRB Websites Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is an ITI certificate mandatory for applying to RRB Group D posts?

No, candidates who have passed the 10th standard (Matriculation) from a recognized board are also eligible to apply, in addition to candidates with ITI or NAC qualifications.

Q2: Will my application fee be refunded?

Yes, typically a portion of the fee (₹400 for General/OBC) or the full fee (₹250 for reserved categories/female) is refunded to your bank account after you appear for the Computer Based Test (CBT).

Q3: Is the Physical Efficiency Test (PET) for marks?

No, the PET is mandatory but only qualifying in nature. No marks from the PET will be added to the final merit list. However, you must pass it to be considered for the next stage.

Q4: Is there negative marking in the CBT?

Yes, there is a negative marking of 1/3rd of the marks for each incorrect answer in the Computer Based Test.

entry.title: Cochin Shipyard Recruitment 2025: 132 Workmen Posts for Diploma Holders
entry.content:
Cochin Shipyard Recruitment 2025: 132 Workmen Posts for Diploma Holders

A premier Miniratna PSU invites online applications for Senior Ship Draftsman, Junior Technical Assistant, and other permanent roles. Apply from December 26, 2025.

Post Name: Senior Ship Draftsman, Junior Technical Assistant, Laboratory Assistant, Storekeeper, Assistant
Last Date: 12 January 2026
Total Posts: 132

Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL), a listed premier Mini Ratna Schedule 'A' Company of the Government of India under the Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways, has announced a significant recruitment drive for permanent positions in the Workmen category. Through Vacancy Notification Ref No. CSL/P&A/RECTT/PERMANENT/WORKMEN/2025/2, CSL invites online applications from eligible and dynamic Indian citizens to fill up a total of 132 vacancies for various technical and non-technical roles.

This is a stellar opportunity for candidates with Diplomas in Engineering, B.Sc degrees, or other graduate qualifications to secure a permanent job in one of India's leading shipbuilding and ship repair yards. The recruitment covers a wide range of posts, including Senior Ship Draftsman, Junior Technical Assistant, Laboratory Assistant, Storekeeper, and Assistant. The selection process will involve a multi-stage examination, including an Objective Type Online Test and, for some posts, a Practical/Descriptive Test. The online application portal will be open from December 26, 2025, to January 12, 2026. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all essential details of this recruitment, including post-wise eligibility, selection methodology, exam patterns, and the application procedure.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government PSU positions with postings at CSL, Kochi, or any other CSL units/project sites across India. Key dates and details are as follows:

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL)
Job Type Permanent (Workmen Category)
Job Location CSL units/project sites across India
Online Application Start Date 26 December 2025
Online Application Last Date 12 January 2026

Vacancy Details

The recruitment aims to fill 132 permanent posts in the Workmen category. The vacancies are distributed across various technical and administrative roles with different pay scales (W7 and W6).

Post Name Pay Scale Total Vacancies
Senior Ship Draftsman (Mechanical) W7 20
Senior Ship Draftsman (Electrical) W7 7
Senior Ship Draftsman (Electronics) W7 1
Senior Ship Draftsman (Instrumentation) W7 2
Junior Technical Assistant (Mechanical) W7 36
Junior Technical Assistant (Electrical) W7 11
Junior Technical Assistant (Electronics) W7 3
Junior Technical Assistant (Civil) W7 1
Junior Technical Assistant (Instrumentation) W7 2
Laboratory Assistant (Mechanical) W7 4
Laboratory Assistant (Chemical) W7 2
Store Keeper W7 9
Assistant W6 34
Total Vacancies 132
Note: CSL reserves the right to increase or decrease the number of vacancies as per its requirement. The detailed category-wise reservation (UR, OBC, SC, EWS) is available in the official notification.

Application Fee

  • An application fee of ₹700/- (non-refundable, plus bank charges) is applicable for general category candidates.
  • Applicants belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC) / Scheduled Tribe (ST) / Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) need not pay the application fee.

The fee must be paid online between December 26, 2025, and January 12, 2026. Candidature will be considered only upon receipt of the application fee.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must fulfill the specific educational qualifications and post-qualification experience for each post as of the last date of online application (January 12, 2026).

Post Name Educational Qualification & Experience Max. Age Limit
Senior Ship Draftsman (All Disciplines) Three-year Diploma in the respective Engineering discipline (Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics/Instrumentation) with a minimum of 60% marks. Experience: Minimum two years of post-qualification experience/training as a Ship Draftsman or Draftsman in a Shipyard/Engineering Company. Desirable: Experience in 3D modeling software like Tribon, CADMATIC. 35 years
Junior Technical Assistant (All Disciplines) Three-year Diploma in the respective Engineering discipline (Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics/Civil/Instrumentation) with a minimum of 60% marks. Experience: Minimum four years of post-qualification experience related to the installation & commissioning of systems in a Shipyard/Engineering Company. 35 years
Laboratory Assistant (Mechanical) Three-year Diploma in Mechanical/Metallurgical Engineering with 60% marks. Experience: Minimum four years of experience in a Testing Laboratory or in Quality Control activities in a Shipyard/Engineering Company. 35 years
Laboratory Assistant (Chemical) Graduate (B.Sc) in Chemistry with 60% marks. Experience: Minimum four years of experience in a Metal/Water/Oil/Fuel/Paint testing lab, preferably NABL accredited. 35 years
Storekeeper Graduate with Post Graduate Diploma in Materials Management OR Diploma in Engineering (Mechanical/Electrical). Experience: Minimum four years of experience in storekeeping in a Shipyard/Engineering Company. 35 years
Assistant Bachelor's Degree in Arts (other than Fine Arts/Performing Arts), Science, Commerce, Computer Applications, or Business Administration with a minimum of 60% marks. Experience: Minimum four years of experience in office work, data entry, record maintenance, etc., in a Shipyard/Engineering Company. 35 years

Selection Process

The method of selection varies for different posts and will be conducted in phases. The process is designed to test both theoretical knowledge and practical skills.

For the posts of Senior Ship Draftsman:

  1. Phase I: Objective Type Online Test (70 Marks): This test will have a duration of 75 minutes and will consist of two parts - General (General Knowledge, English, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude - 20 marks) and Discipline related (50 marks). There will be no negative marking.
  2. Phase II: Practical Test in CAD (30 Marks): Candidates who qualify in Phase I and clear certificate verification will be called for a Practical Test in CAD software, which will be held at CSL, Kochi.

The final rank list will be prepared based on the aggregate marks secured in both Phase I and Phase II tests.

For the posts of Junior Technical Assistant, Laboratory Assistant, Store Keeper & Assistant:

  1. Objective Type Online Test (70 Marks): This test will be of 75 minutes duration, comprising 70 multiple-choice questions. It will have two parts: General (Knowledge, Reasoning, Aptitude, English - 20 marks) and Discipline related (50 marks). There will be no negative marking.
  2. Descriptive Type Online Test (30 Marks): This test will be of 60 minutes duration and will consist of discipline-related descriptive questions.

The answer sheets of the Descriptive Test will be evaluated only for those candidates who secure the minimum pass mark in the Objective Test. The final rank list will be prepared based on the aggregate marks in both tests.

Exam Pattern

The selection tests for this recruitment will be conducted online at various centers in Kerala. The pattern differs for Draftsman posts and other posts.

Pattern for Senior Ship Draftsman

  • Phase I - Objective Test (70 Marks, 75 mins):
    • General Knowledge: 5 questions
    • General English: 5 questions
    • Reasoning: 5 questions
    • Quantitative Aptitude: 5 questions
    • Discipline Related: 50 questions
  • Phase II - Practical Test (30 Marks): A practical test using CAD software.

Pattern for JTA, Lab Asst, Storekeeper, Asst.

  • Objective Test (70 Marks, 75 mins):
    • General Knowledge: 5 Marks
    • Reasoning: 5 Marks
    • Quantitative Aptitude: 5 Marks
    • General English: 5 Marks
    • Discipline Related: 50 Marks
  • Descriptive Test (30 Marks, 60 mins): Discipline-related questions.
Minimum Pass Marks: For unreserved posts, the minimum pass mark is 50% in each test. For OBC, it is 45%, and for SC, it is 40%.

How to Prepare

A comprehensive preparation strategy covering both general and technical subjects is essential to succeed.

  1. Master Your Domain: The discipline-related section carries the highest weightage in all tests. Thoroughly revise your diploma/degree syllabus. For Draftsman posts, extensive practice on CAD software (like AutoCAD, Tribon, CADMATIC) is crucial for the practical test.
  2. Prepare for General Aptitude: The general section includes GK, Reasoning, Quantitative Aptitude, and English. Although the weightage is less, these marks can be a deciding factor. Practice from standard competitive exam books.
  3. Practice Descriptive Writing: For JTA and other posts, the descriptive test is important. Practice writing clear and concise answers to technical questions. Your ability to explain concepts will be tested.
  4. Time Management: Practice with timed online mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy, especially for the objective tests. Since there is no negative marking, your goal should be to attempt all questions.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended resources for your preparation:
