  • For Technical/Discipline Subjects: Standard diploma-level textbooks for your specific engineering branch are the best resources. Objective question books for JE/AE level exams (e.g., by R.S. Khurmi, J.B. Gupta) can be very helpful for practice.
  • For General Aptitude & Reasoning: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal and "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • For General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • For General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge".

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online through the Cochin Shipyard website.

  1. Go to the CSL website www.cochinshipyard.in and navigate to the "Career page→CSL, Kochi".
  2. The application consists of two phases: Registration and Submission of application. Complete the one-time Registration process.
  3. After registration, log in and fill out the online application form with your personal, academic, and experience details. Upload all required documents and certificates.
  4. Pay the application fee of ₹700/- (if applicable) using the online payment options.
  5. Ensure all entries are correct and submit the application successfully. After applying, retain a soft copy/printout of the online application containing the unique registration number for your reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates need to upload scanned copies of the following documents during the online application process. All documents must be self-attested.

  1. Recent passport-size photograph.
  2. Signature.
  3. Proof of Date of Birth (10th certificate).
  4. All educational certificates (Diploma/Degree, Marksheets).
  5. Experience certificates from all employers, clearly indicating the date of joining and relieving.
  6. Caste/Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD) in the prescribed format.
  7. A valid Photo ID Proof.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online (from 26.12.2025) Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website Click Here

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the CSL recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is January 12, 2026.

Question 2: Is this a permanent job?

Yes, all these posts are for permanent appointment in the Workmen category of Cochin Shipyard Limited.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the online tests?

No, the notification for both categories of posts states that there shall be no negative marks.

Question 4: Can I apply for more than one post?

No, the notification states that online exams for various posts will be conducted simultaneously. Hence, applicants shall apply for only one post.

entry.title: HCL Junior Manager Recruitment 2025: Notification for 64 E0 Grade Vacancies
entry.content:
HCL Junior Manager Recruitment 2025: Notification for 64 E0 Grade Vacancies

Join Hindustan Copper Limited, a Navratna PSU under the Ministry of Mines - Apply Online for Engineering, Finance, and HR Cadres

Post Name: Junior Manager (E0 Grade) - Multiple Disciplines
Last Date: December 17, 2025
Total Posts: 64 Vacancies

Hindustan Copper Limited (HCL), a public sector undertaking under the administrative control of the Ministry of Mines, Government of India, is the nation's only vertically integrated copper producer. From mining and beneficiation to smelting and refining, HCL manages the entire value chain of copper production. As a Miniratna Category-I PSU, it plays a strategic role in ensuring the availability of copper, a vital metal for the electrical, defense, and infrastructure sectors. To bolster its supervisory workforce, HCL has officially announced the HCL Recruitment 2025 for the post of Junior Manager (E0 Grade). Under Advertisement Number Estt. /1/2029/2025-26, the organization is looking for experienced technical and administrative professionals to join its ranks.

The E0 grade represents the entry-level supervisory cadre within HCL, providing a gateway into the executive management hierarchy of a Navratna company. This recruitment cycle is particularly significant as it targets 13 different functional disciplines, including Mining, Geology, Electrical, Mechanical, Finance, and HR. Unlike general entry-level roles, the Junior Manager position at HCL requires candidates to possess prior industrial experience, ensuring that selected individuals can handle the complexities of large-scale mining operations and corporate management from day one. The application process is scheduled to begin on November 27, 2025, and will remain active through December 17, 2025. Successful candidates will be posted at HCL units across India, including prominent locations like the Khetri Copper Complex in Rajasthan, Malanjkhand Copper Project in Madhya Pradesh, or Indian Copper Complex in Jharkhand.

Working at Hindustan Copper Limited is not just a job; it is an opportunity to contribute to India's self-reliance in the mineral sector. The organization offers an attractive pay scale and a comprehensive benefits package aligned with central PSU norms. The selection process is strictly merit-based, involving a Computer-Based Test (CBT) followed by document verification. There is no personal interview for this cadre, making the written test the ultimate deciding factor for your selection. This 2000-word exhaustive guide will walk you through the vacancy distribution, discipline-specific eligibility, salary matrix, exam syllabus, and professional preparation strategies required to secure your future at HCL.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must strictly adhere to the schedule provided by HCL. The recruitment portal is centralized, and candidates should complete their registration well before the deadline to avoid technical congestion on the server. Please note that the cut-off date for calculating age and experience is November 1, 2025.

Recruitment Detail Dates and Information
Official Notification Number Estt. /1/2029/2025-26
Online Registration Start Date November 27, 2025 (11:00 AM)
Last Date for Application Submission December 17, 2025 (Midnight)
Eligibility Cut-off Date (Age/Exp) November 01, 2025
Job Type Permanent / Central Government PSU
Posting Liability All India Basis (HCL Units)

Discipline Wise Vacancy Breakdown

The 64 vacancies are distributed across various technical and administrative disciplines. This ensures that HCL has a balanced supervisory workforce to manage its diversified operations. The vacancies are also categorized as per the Government of India reservation policy for SC, ST, OBC (NCL), EWS, and UR categories.

Discipline / Cadre Number of Vacancies Category-wise Reservation
Mining 13 UR-5, OBC-4, SC-2, ST-1, EWS-1
Mechanical 08 UR-3, OBC-2, SC-1, ST-1, EWS-1
Geology 08 UR-3, OBC-2, SC-1, ST-1, EWS-1
Civil 06 UR-2, OBC-1, SC-1, ST-1, EWS-1
Finance 06 UR-2, OBC-1, SC-1, ST-1, EWS-1
Mineral Processing 06 UR-2, OBC-1, SC-1, ST-1, EWS-1
Environment 03 UR-2, OBC-1
Electrical 03 UR-1, OBC-1, SC-1
Law 03 UR-2, OBC-1
Admin / HR / M&C 08 UR-4, OBC-2, SC-1, ST-1
Total Combined Vacancies 64 UR-26, OBC-16, SC-10, ST-6, EWS-6
Special Note: The 64 vacancies include horizontal reservation for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD). Candidates under the PwBD category should ensure they possess a disability certificate of 40% or more to be eligible for reservation benefits.

Salary Structure and Organizational Benefits

Selected candidates for the post of Junior Manager will be placed in the E0 Grade. The pay scale and allowances at HCL are designed to attract and retain experienced professionals in the competitive mineral industry.

  • Pay Scale: ₹30,000 - 3% - ₹1,20,000
  • Basic Pay: Starting at ₹30,000 per month.
  • Gross Emoluments: In addition to the basic pay, employees are entitled to Industrial Dearness Allowance (IDA), which is revised quarterly. House Rent Allowance (HRA) is provided as per city categorization (X, Y, Z).
  • Perks & Allowances: Employees can avail of various perks under the "Cafeteria Approach" up to 35% of the basic pay, including washing allowance, education allowance, and professional pursuit allowance.

Service Agreement Bond: Selected candidates must execute a service bond to serve the company for a minimum period of three years from the date of joining. The bond amount is ₹3,00,000 (Three Lakhs). If a candidate resigns before the completion of the bond period, they must pay the specified amount to HCL.

Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility for the HCL Junior Manager role is a combination of specific educational qualifications and mandatory post-qualification experience. All qualifications must be from universities or institutes recognized by AICTE or UGC.

  • Mining/Mechanical/Electrical/Civil:
    • Diploma Track: 3-year Diploma in the respective engineering field with at least 05 years of post-qualification experience.
    • Degree Track: 4-year Bachelor’s Degree (B.E./B.Tech) in the respective field with at least 02 years of post-qualification experience.
  • Geology: Post-Graduate Degree in Geology with 02 years of experience OR Diploma in Draughtsmanship with 05 years of experience.
  • Finance: CA/ICWA Intermediate with 03 years of experience OR MBA (Finance)/Post-Graduate Diploma in Finance with 02 years of experience.
  • HR/Personnel: Graduate with 05 years of experience OR MBA (HR)/Post-Graduate Degree in Personnel Management/Social Work with 02 years of experience.
  • Law: Bachelor’s Degree in Law (LLB) with 02 years of post-qualification experience as an advocate or law officer.
  • Age Limit: Maximum 40 years as of November 1, 2025. Relaxations apply for SC/ST (5 years), OBC (3 years), and PwBD (10 years).

Selection Process and Examination Pattern

HCL follows a transparent and merit-based selection process. For the supervisory cadre, the emphasis is placed on technical proficiency and general aptitude through a standardized examination.

  1. Computer-Based Test (CBT): This is the primary and only competitive stage. The test evaluates the candidate's core technical knowledge in their respective discipline as well as their general mental ability.
  2. Document Verification (DV): Candidates who clear the CBT merit will be called for verification of their original academic certificates, experience letters, and category certificates.
  3. Medical Fitness: Final selection is subject to the candidate being found medically fit as per HCL’s pre-employment medical standards.

Qualifying Marks in CBT: The minimum qualifying marks for General and OBC candidates is 30%, whereas for SC, ST, and PwBD candidates, it is 20%. Final selection is strictly based on merit amongst the qualifying candidates.

Detailed Exam Syllabus

The CBT is divided into two sections. Since HCL does not conduct interviews for this grade, your performance in the technical section is crucial for a high rank.

Section 1: Technical/Domain Knowledge (80% Weightage):

  • Mining: Mine ventilation, surface mining, underground mining methods, blasting technology, and Mines Act/CMR 2017.
  • Mechanical: Thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, heavy earth moving machinery (HEMM), and strength of materials.
  • Electrical: Power systems, electrical machines, instrumentation, and maintenance of high voltage mining equipment.
  • Finance: Corporate accounting, taxation (GST/Income Tax), auditing, and financial management in PSUs.
  • HR: Industrial relations, labor laws, compensation management, and organizational behavior.

Section 2: General Aptitude (20% Weightage): Includes General English, Reasoning Ability, Quantitative Aptitude, and General Awareness of the mineral sector in India.

Preparation Strategy for HCL Junior Manager

Preparing for a PSU exam in the mining sector requires a balance between academic theory and practical industrial knowledge. Follow this roadmap for success.

Focus on Core Engineering: Since 80% of the paper is technical, revisit your diploma or degree textbooks. For engineers, solving previous years' GATE or IES objective questions is highly recommended. For Mining candidates, a thorough reading of the Coal Mines Regulations (CMR) and Metalliferous Mines Regulations (MMR) is vital.

Experience-Based Knowledge: HCL often asks questions related to practical field troubleshooting. Relate the theoretical concepts to your 2-5 years of work experience. For Finance candidates, staying updated with the latest accounting standards and PSU audit requirements is essential.

General Awareness: Dedicate at least 30 minutes daily to reading about the mining industry in India, specifically copper production trends, HCL’s annual reports, and new mineral policies launched by the Ministry of Mines. This will give you an edge in the general awareness section.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right reference books will keep your preparation streamlined. Below are the top recommendations for the HCL exam.

Subject / Discipline Recommended Book List
Mining Theory "Elements of Mining Technology" by D.J. Deshmukh (Vol I, II, III).
Mechanical/Electrical Handbooks by Made Easy Publications or Objective Question banks by R.K. Rajput/J.B. Gupta.
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude and Reasoning Ability by R.S. Aggarwal.
General English Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi.
Finance/HR Professional guides for PSU examinations (Taxmann for Finance/Trueman for HR).

How to Apply Online

The application process for HCL is entirely online. Candidates should ensure they have all their experience certificates and marksheets handy before starting the process.

  1. Visit the official Hindustan Copper Limited website: www.hindustancopper.com.
  2. Navigate to the "Career" section and click on the "Apply Online" link for Junior Manager (E0) Recruitment.
  3. Register as a new candidate using a valid Email ID and Mobile Number.
  4. Login with your registration ID and fill in your academic scores and detailed work experience.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your recent passport photograph and signature (JPEG format).
  6. Pay the application fee of ₹500 (General/OBC/EWS) through the online payment gateway. SC/ST/PwBD are exempted.
  7. Review your application form carefully. Once submitted, details cannot be modified.
  8. Submit the application and print the confirmation page and acknowledgement slip for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Scanning documents correctly is critical to avoid application rejection. Ensure that your experience certificates clearly mention the dates of joining and leaving.

  1. Age Proof: 10th Standard Passing Certificate or Birth Certificate.
  2. Academic Proof: Final Degree/Diploma certificates and semester-wise mark sheets.
  3. Experience Proof: Experience letters or service certificates from all previous and current employers proving 2 to 5 years of post-qualification work.
  4. Category Proof: SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificates (as per the central government format).
  5. Identification: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID.
  6. NOC (No Objection Certificate): For candidates currently working in other Government/PSU organizations (Mandatory at the time of DV).

Important Links

Resource Description Official Link
HCL Official Career Portal View Opportunities
Online Application Link Apply Online Here
Official HCL Homepage www.hindustancopper.com

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can freshers apply for the Junior Manager post in HCL?

No. HCL requires a minimum of 02 years of post-qualification experience for Degree holders and 05 years for Diploma holders. This recruitment is intended for experienced industrial professionals.

Q2: Is there a personal interview for the HCL E0 Grade recruitment?

No. According to the 2025 notification, selection for the Junior Manager post is based entirely on the Computer-Based Test (CBT) merit and successful document verification. There is no interview stage.

Q3: What is the Service Agreement Bond at HCL?

Selected candidates are required to sign a bond to serve HCL for at least three years. If they leave the company before this period, they are liable to pay ₹3,00,000 (Three Lakhs) to the organization.

Q4: What are the qualifying marks for the written exam?

General, OBC, and EWS candidates must score at least 30% marks in the CBT, while SC, ST, and PwBD candidates must score at least 20% to be considered for the merit list.

Q5: Can I apply for more than one discipline?

No. Candidates are advised to apply for only one discipline because the written examination for all disciplines will likely be held simultaneously on the same day. Multiple applications may lead to rejection.

Q6: What is the age limit for OBC candidates?

The upper age limit is 40 years for UR. OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates are eligible for a 3-year relaxation, making their upper age limit 43 years as of the cut-off date.

entry.title: दिल्ली SC ST OBC छात्रवृत्ति 2025: कॉलेज छात्रों को मिलेंगे ₹1,860 हर महीने! जानिए योग्यता, अंतिम तिथि और आवेदन प्रक्रिया
entry.content:
दिल्ली SC ST OBC छात्रवृत्ति 2025: कॉलेज छात्रों को मिलेंगे ₹1,860 हर महीने! जानिए योग्यता, अंतिम तिथि और आवेदन प्रक्रिया

दिल्ली सरकार के SC/ST/OBC कल्याण विभाग ने कॉलेज, तकनीकी और प्रोफेशनल संस्थानों में पढ़ रहे छात्रों के लिए छात्रवृत्ति योजना शुरू की है। आवेदन e-District पोर्टल के माध्यम से होंगे।

दिल्ली के SC, ST, OBC और अल्पसंख्यक समुदाय के छात्रों के लिए बड़ी खुशखबरी! दिल्ली सरकार ने SC ST OBC Scholarship 2025 योजना शुरू की है। इस योजना का उद्देश्य कॉलेज, प्रोफेशनल और टेक्निकल कोर्स करने वाले छात्रों को आर्थिक सहायता देना है। इस योजना के तहत छात्रों को हर महीने ₹420 से लेकर ₹1,860 तक की छात्रवृत्ति दी जाएगी। नीचे आप जानेंगे आवेदन प्रक्रिया, पात्रता, आवश्यक दस्तावेज और अंतिम तिथि के बारे में।

छात्रवृत्ति आवेदन कब शुरू होंगे?

दिल्ली सरकार जल्द ही इस छात्रवृत्ति के लिए आवेदन शुरू करेगी। आवेदन और अंतिम तिथि की जानकारी e-District दिल्ली पोर्टल पर जारी की जाएगी। उम्मीदवारों को सलाह दी जाती है कि वे नियमित रूप से पोर्टल चेक करते रहें।

छात्रवृत्ति राशि और लाभ

छात्रवृत्ति की राशि इस बात पर निर्भर करती है कि छात्र किस कोर्स में है और वह Hosteller है या Day Scholar। यह राशि पूरे 12 महीनों के लिए दी जाती है।

कोर्स समूहकोर्स विवरणHosteller (प्रति माह)Day Scholar (प्रति माह)
Group-AMedical, Engineering, B.Sc (Agri), BAMS, PG Courses₹1,620 - ₹1,860₹900 - ₹960
Group-BDiploma Courses (Engg, Tech, etc.), PG in Science₹1,110₹720
Group-CCertificate Courses (1 साल से अधिक)₹930₹630
Group-DGraduate Courses (Arts, Commerce)₹804 - ₹930₹420 - ₹630

पात्रता (Eligibility)

  • निवास: आवेदक दिल्ली का स्थायी निवासी होना चाहिए।
  • श्रेणी: आवेदक SC, ST, OBC या अल्पसंख्यक समुदाय से होना चाहिए।
  • शिक्षा: छात्र किसी मान्यता प्राप्त स्कूल, कॉलेज या संस्थान में अध्ययनरत होना चाहिए।
  • अंक: पिछली परीक्षा में कम से कम 60% अंक आवश्यक हैं।
  • आय सीमा:
    • SC/ST छात्रों के लिए कोई आय सीमा नहीं है।
    • OBC और Minority छात्रों के परिवार की वार्षिक आय ₹2,00,000 से अधिक नहीं होनी चाहिए।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया

Step 1: रजिस्ट्रेशन

  1. e-District Delhi पोर्टल पर जाएं।
  2. "Login/Register" पर क्लिक करें और "Register" विकल्प चुनें।
  3. Aadhaar नंबर डालकर मोबाइल OTP से वेरिफाई करें।
  4. रजिस्ट्रेशन के बाद आपको Login ID और Password मिल जाएगा।

Step 2: आवेदन फॉर्म भरना

  1. Login ID और Password से लॉगिन करें।
  2. “Apply Service” में जाकर छात्रवृत्ति योजना चुनें।
  3. सभी जरूरी जानकारी भरें और आवश्यक दस्तावेज अपलोड करें।
  4. “Submit” पर क्लिक करें और आवेदन नंबर नोट करें।

आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

  • आय प्रमाणपत्र (केवल OBC और Minority छात्रों के लिए)
  • जाति प्रमाणपत्र (SC/ST/OBC छात्रों के लिए)
  • दिल्ली निवास प्रमाणपत्र
  • Minority छात्रों के लिए 3 साल का निवास प्रमाण
  • Minority घोषणा पत्र (Parents द्वारा)
  • Gap affidavit (यदि 3 साल से अधिक का अध्ययन अंतराल है)

छात्रवृत्ति स्टेटस कैसे जांचें?

  1. e-District पोर्टल पर "Track Your Application" सेक्शन खोलें।
  2. अपना आवेदन नंबर दर्ज करें।
  3. “Submit” पर क्लिक करते ही आपका स्टेटस स्क्रीन पर दिख जाएगा।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
आधिकारिक e-District दिल्ली पोर्टलयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?
अभी इसकी घोषणा नहीं हुई है। कृपया आधिकारिक पोर्टल पर अपडेट देखते रहें।
Q2: क्या यह स्कॉलरशिप स्कूल छात्रों के लिए है?
नहीं, यह केवल कॉलेज और प्रोफेशनल कोर्स करने वाले छात्रों के लिए है।
Q3: रिन्यूअल (Renewal) कैसे होगा?
हर साल पात्रता मानदंड पूरे करने के बाद पोर्टल पर जाकर रिन्यूअल आवेदन करना होगा।

entry.title: JPSC Combined Civil Services Examination 2026 for 103 Posts
entry.content:
JPSC Combined Civil Services Examination 2026 for 103 Posts

Advertisement No. 01/2026: The Jharkhand Public Service Commission (JPSC) has invited online applications for the Jharkhand Combined Civil Services Competitive Examination 2025. This recruitment aims to fill 103 vacancies for prestigious posts like Deputy Collector, Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP), and other administrative roles in various state government departments. The last date to submit the online application is February 14, 2026.

Post Name: Deputy Collector, DSP, District Commandant, and various other posts
Last Date: 14 February 2026
Total Posts: 103

The Jharkhand Public Service Commission (JPSC) has released its most anticipated notification of the year for the Combined Civil Services Examination. This competitive exam is the gateway to the premier administrative, police, and other allied services of the state of Jharkhand. The current drive seeks to fill 103 high-ranking positions, including Deputy Collector (the highest administrative post at this level), Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP), District Commandant, and Probation Officer, among others.

The selection for these esteemed posts is a rigorous three-stage process designed to select the most capable candidates. It begins with a Preliminary Examination (Objective Type), followed by a Main Examination (Written/Descriptive), and concludes with a Personal Interview. A Bachelor's degree from any recognized university is the minimum educational qualification, making it accessible to a wide range of aspirants. The application process is entirely online through the JPSC's official website. This is a golden opportunity for graduates to embark on a prestigious career in public service in Jharkhand.

JPSC Civil Services Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details for this competitive examination. All applications must be submitted online.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Jharkhand Public Service Commission (JPSC)
Advertisement Number 01/2026
Exam Name Jharkhand Combined Civil Services Competitive Examination 2025
Total Vacancies 103
Application Start Date 31 January 2026 (10:00 AM)
Application Last Date 14 February 2026 (05:00 PM)
Last Date for Fee Payment 16 February 2026 (05:00 PM)
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent State Government Job
Job Location Jharkhand
Official Website www.jpsc.gov.in

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 103 vacancies across 9 different services. The detailed category-wise reservation is as follows:

Post Name Total UR SC ST EBC-I BC-II EWS
Deputy Collector28120307020202
Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP)42170411030304
District Commandant02010001000000
Assistant Registrar02010001000000
Assistant Municipal Commissioner / Executive Officer10040103010001
Superintendent of Prison02010001000000
Assistant Director-cum-District Public Relations Officer10040103010001
Probation Officer04020001000100
Assistant Director (Social Security)03010101000000
Grand Total 103 43 10 28 07 06 08
Note: Reservation benefits are applicable only to candidates who are domiciles of Jharkhand. Candidates from other states will be treated under the Unreserved (UR) category.

Application Fee

  • For Unreserved (UR), EWS, EBC (Annexure-I), and BC (Annexure-II) candidates: ₹100/-
  • For SC and ST candidates of Jharkhand domicile: ₹50/-
  • For Persons with Benchmark Disability (PwBD) candidates: NIL (Fee Exempted)

The fee must be paid online. Fee is non-refundable.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility conditions regarding education, age, and physical standards.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification A candidate must hold a Bachelor's Degree or equivalent from any recognized university.
Age Limit (as on 01.08.2026)
  • Minimum Age: 21 Years
  • Maximum Age (UR/EWS): 35 Years
  • Maximum Age (EBC/BC): 37 Years
  • Maximum Age (Female - UR/EBC/BC/EWS): 38 Years
  • Maximum Age (SC/ST - Male/Female): 40 Years
Age relaxation for PwBD (10 years) and Ex-Servicemen (5 years) is applicable as per rules.

Selection Process

The selection is a three-stage competitive examination process.

  1. Preliminary Examination: An objective-type (MCQ) exam consisting of two papers. This is a screening test, and its marks are not counted for the final merit list.
  2. Main Examination: A descriptive written examination consisting of six papers. Candidates shortlisted from the prelims (approx. 15 times the vacancies) will appear for this exam.
  3. Interview: Candidates who qualify in the Main exam (approx. 2.5 times the vacancies) will be called for a personal interview, which is for 100 marks. There is no minimum qualifying mark in the interview.
  4. Final Merit List: The final selection will be based on the total marks obtained in the Main (Written) Examination and the Interview.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The examination is comprehensive, covering general studies and specific knowledge about Jharkhand.

Preliminary Examination Pattern (Objective)

  • Total Marks: 400 | Negative Marking: No
  • Paper-I (General Studies-I): 100 questions, 200 Marks, 2 Hours. Covers History of India, Geography of India, Indian Polity, Economy, Science & Tech, Jharkhand Specific questions, and Current Events.
  • Paper-II (General Studies-II): 100 questions, 200 Marks, 2 Hours. This paper is entirely focused on Jharkhand, covering its history, movements, specific identity, literature, education, sports, land laws, policies, geography, and current affairs.

Main Examination Pattern (Descriptive)

  • Paper-I (General Hindi & English): 100 Marks, 3 Hours. (Qualifying nature, min 30 marks required).
  • Paper-II (Language and Literature): 150 Marks, 3 Hours. (Candidate to choose one from 15 listed languages like Hindi, English, Santhali, etc.).
  • Paper-III (Social Sciences): 200 Marks, 3 Hours. (History & Geography).
  • Paper-IV (Indian Constitution & Polity, Public Administration): 200 Marks, 3 Hours.
  • Paper-V (Indian Economy, Globalization, Sustainable Development): 200 Marks, 3 Hours.
  • Paper-VI (General Sciences, Environment & Technology): 200 Marks, 3 Hours.

Total Marks for Merit: 950 (Mains Written) + 100 (Interview) = 1050 Marks.

Physical Standards Test (PST) for Uniformed Posts

For posts like DSP, District Commandant, Superintendent of Prison, and Probation Officer, candidates must meet the following minimum physical standards.

CategoryMinimum HeightChest (Male only)
Unreserved (UR) / BC / EBC Male165 cm81 cm (Unexpanded)
SC / ST Male162 cm79 cm (Unexpanded)
Unreserved / BC / EBC Female155 cmNot Applicable
SC / ST Female152 cmNot Applicable

How to Prepare

A dedicated and long-term preparation strategy is essential.

  • Master the Syllabus: The syllabus is vast. Break it down and create a study plan. Pay special attention to Paper-II of the prelims, which is entirely on Jharkhand.
  • Focus on Jharkhand GK: This is the key to clearing the prelims. Study the history, geography, land laws (CNT/SPT Acts), policies, and culture of Jharkhand in depth.
  • Answer Writing Practice: For the mains, practice writing descriptive answers daily. Develop a clear and concise writing style.
  • Solve Previous Year Papers: Analyze previous years' JPSC papers to understand the pattern, important topics, and difficulty level.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard resources.

  • Jharkhand GK: "Jharkhand Samanya Gyan" by Dr. Manish Rannjan (Prabhat Publication) or "Udaan" Publication's Jharkhand book.
  • General Studies (Prelims & Mains): Standard books like "Indian Polity" by M. Laxmikanth, "A Brief History of Modern India" by Spectrum, "Indian Economy" by Ramesh Singh, and NCERT textbooks.
  • Language Paper (Mains): Specific books for the chosen optional language.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the JPSC website.

  1. Visit the official JPSC website: www.jpsc.gov.in.
  2. The application will be accepted through the One Time Registration (OTR) System. If not already registered, complete your OTR.
  3. Find the link for "Online Application for Jharkhand Combined Civil Services Competitive Examination-2025".
  4. Log in with your OTR credentials and fill out the application form.
  5. Upload your scanned photograph and signature as per the specifications.
  6. Pay the application fee online through the payment gateway.
  7. Review your application carefully before final submission.
  8. Download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed for Application & Verification

Candidates should have the following documents ready for filling the form and for verification at the interview stage:

  1. Matriculation (10th) certificate for proof of age.
  2. Bachelor's Degree Certificate and Mark Sheets.
  3. Jharkhand Domicile Certificate (Local Resident Certificate) for claiming reservation.
  4. Caste Certificate (SC/ST/EBC/BC) and EWS certificate in the prescribed format.
  5. Disability Certificate, if applicable.
  6. No Objection Certificate (NOC) if currently employed in a government service.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Link to be active from 31.01.2026)
Official JPSC Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there negative marking in the JPSC Preliminary Exam?

No, the official notification clearly states that there will be no negative marking for incorrect answers in the Preliminary Examination.

Q2: What is the minimum educational qualification for this exam?

A Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognized university is the minimum educational qualification.

Q3: Is Paper-II of the prelims (Jharkhand GK) mandatory?

Yes, both Paper-I and Paper-II are compulsory, and the combined score of both papers is used to shortlist candidates for the Main Examination.

Q4: I am from another state. Can I apply for the JPSC Civil Services exam?

Yes, Indian citizens from any state can apply. However, they will be considered under the Unreserved (General) category and will not be eligible for any reservation benefits.

entry.title: YIL Trade Apprentice Recruitment for 3979 Posts
entry.content:
YIL Trade Apprentice Recruitment for 3979 Posts

Advertisement No. 1457: Yantra India Limited (YIL) has invited online applications for the engagement of 3,979 Trade Apprentices (both Ex-ITI and Non-ITI) for the 59th Batch. This is a massive opportunity for 10th pass and ITI qualified candidates to get training in the prestigious Indian Ordnance and Ordnance Equipment Factories across India under the Skill India Mission. The online application process is scheduled from February 01, 2026, to March 03, 2026.

Post Name: Trade Apprentice (Ex-ITI & Non-ITI)
Last Date: 03 March 2026
Total Posts: 3979

Yantra India Limited (YIL), a key defence public sector undertaking, has released a major notification for its 59th Batch of Trade Apprentices. In line with the Government of India's Skill India Mission and the Apprentices Act, 1961, YIL is offering approximately 3,979 apprenticeship training slots across its various Ordnance and Ordnance Equipment Factories located in different states. This drive is open to both Ex-ITI candidates who have a trade certificate and Non-ITI candidates who have passed their 10th standard.

The selection for this apprenticeship is purely merit-based, eliminating the need for a written examination. The merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the 10th standard (for Non-ITI) and a combination of 10th and ITI marks (for Ex-ITI). This straightforward process makes it a highly accessible opportunity for young individuals to gain invaluable hands-on training and skills in a premier industrial environment, setting a strong foundation for their technical careers. The entire application process is online via the official recruitment portal.

YIL Apprentice Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. All applications must be submitted online through the designated portal.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Yantra India Limited (YIL)
Advertisement Number 1457
Post Name Trade Apprentice (59th Batch)
Total Vacancies 3979 (Approx.)
Application Start Date 01 February 2026
Application Last Date 03 March 2026 (up to 23:59 Hrs)
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Apprenticeship Training (Under Skill India Mission)
Job Location Various Ordnance Factories across India
Official Website https://recruit-gov.com

Vacancy and Stipend Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill approximately 3979 apprenticeship slots across two main categories.

Category Vacancies Monthly Stipend
Ex-ITI Apprentices 2843 ₹9,600/-
Non-ITI Apprentices 1136 ₹8,200/-
Total 3979
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional. The detailed factory-wise and trade-wise vacancy distribution is provided in Annexure-I of the official notification. Candidates can choose only one Ordnance Factory to apply for.

Application Fee

  • For UR & OBC Candidates: ₹200/- + GST
  • For SC, ST, Women, PWD, and Transgender Candidates: ₹100/- + GST

The fee is non-refundable and must be paid online. Payments made via UPI and RuPay Debit Card will have zero transaction fees.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 03.03.2026)

Candidates must meet the specific educational and age criteria as of the closing date of the application.

Category Age Limit & Essential Qualifications
Non-ITI Category Educational Qualification: Must have passed Madhyamik (Class 10th or equivalent) with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate. Additionally, must have secured a minimum of 40% marks in both Mathematics and Science individually.

Age Limit: Minimum 14 years, Maximum 35 years.
Ex-ITI Category Educational Qualification: Must have passed Madhyamik (Class 10th or equivalent) with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate. In addition, must have passed the relevant trade test from an NCVT/SCVT recognized institute with a minimum of 50% marks in the ITI examination.

Age Limit: Minimum 14 years, Maximum 35 years.

Selection Process

The selection process for both Non-ITI and Ex-ITI categories is purely based on academic merit. There will be no written examination or interview.

  1. Merit List Preparation: Two separate merit lists will be prepared for Non-ITI and Ex-ITI candidates for each Ordnance Factory.
  2. For Non-ITI Category: The merit list will be prepared based on the overall percentage of marks in Madhyamik (Class 10th). The trades will be allotted based on merit-cum-choice.
  3. For Ex-ITI Category: The merit list will be prepared factory-wise and trade-wise. It will be based on the simple average of the percentage of marks in both Matriculation (10th) and the ITI examination.
  4. Document Verification & Medical Examination: Provisionally selected candidates based on the merit list will be called for Document Verification and a mandatory Medical Examination at the respective Ordnance Factory.

Tie Resolution: In case of a tie in marks, the older candidate will be given preference. If the tie persists, the candidate who passed Matriculation in an earlier year will be preferred.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for this apprenticeship recruitment. The selection is entirely based on the marks obtained by the candidates in their qualifying academic examinations (10th Class and/or ITI).

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) for this recruitment. However, selected candidates must pass a mandatory Medical Examination conducted by the respective Ordnance Factory hospital to ensure they meet the minimum standards of physical fitness required for the training.

How to Prepare

As the selection is merit-based, no specific exam preparation is needed. However, candidates should focus on ensuring a flawless application process.

  • Accurate Marks Calculation: The most crucial step is to calculate and enter your marks/percentage from your 10th and ITI mark sheets exactly as per the formula or guidelines of your board. Any discrepancy can lead to rejection during document verification.
  • Document Readiness: Keep clear, legible scanned copies of all your documents ready before starting the application. This includes your photograph, signature, Aadhaar card, 10th mark sheet, ITI mark sheets, NTC certificate, and caste/PwD certificate.
  • Informed Choice: Research the Ordnance Factories listed in the notification to make an informed choice. You can only apply to one factory.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no written test, no preparation books are required. The selection is based on your past academic performance.

Focus should be on reviewing the official notification and ensuring all required documents are in order.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the designated government portal.

  1. Visit the official application website: https://recruit-gov.com.
  2. Find the link for "YIL Trade Apprentice Recruitment - 59th Batch" (Advertisement No. 1457).
  3. Read the detailed instructions and the full advertisement thoroughly before starting.
  4. Fill in the online application form with your personal details, educational qualifications, and marks obtained.
  5. Choose the Ordnance Factory you wish to apply for. You can only select one.
  6. Upload clear scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and all other required documents in the specified format and size.
  7. Pay the application fee online through the payment gateway.
  8. Review all details carefully before final submission. After submission, a soft copy of your application will be sent to your registered email ID.
  9. Download and save a copy of the final application form for your records. Do not send any hard copy to YIL.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

Candidates must have clear, scanned copies of the following documents ready (100 KB to 200 KB in PDF/JPG/JPEG format, unless specified otherwise):

  1. Recent colour photograph (20-70 KB).
  2. Signature (20-30 KB).
  3. Aadhaar Card or another valid ID proof.
  4. SSC (10th Class) Mark Sheet.
  5. Consolidated ITI Mark Sheet (for Ex-ITI candidates).
  6. National Trade Certificate (NTC) issued by NCVT/SCVT (for Ex-ITI candidates).
  7. Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC Non-Creamy Layer) issued within the last three years, if applicable.
  8. Physical Disability (PwD) certificate, if applicable.

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online Click Here (Link will be active from 01.02.2026)
Official Notification Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there a written exam for this apprenticeship?

No, there is no written examination. The selection is done purely on the basis of merit, calculated from the marks in your 10th and/or ITI examinations.

Q2: Can I apply for both Non-ITI and Ex-ITI categories?

No, the notification clearly states that a candidate can submit only one application. Applying for both categories will lead to the cancellation of your candidature.

Q3: I am still in my final year of ITI. Can I apply?

No, the notification specifies that candidates must have already passed the prescribed qualification on the closing date of the application (03.03.2026). Candidates awaiting results are not eligible.

Q4: Do I need to re-apply if I have already applied on the www.apprenticeship.gov.in portal?

Yes, the notification clearly states that all candidates, even those who applied on the main apprenticeship portal, are required to re-apply through the new website: https://recruit-gov.com.

entry.title: CCRH Recruitment 2025: Massive Job Opportunities in the Central Council for Research in Homoeopathy
entry.content:
CCRH Recruitment 2025: Massive Job Opportunities in the Central Council for Research in Homoeopathy

Join the Ministry of Ayush - Official Notification for 80+ Research Officer, Staff Nurse, and LDC Vacancies - Apply Online Today

Post Name: Research Officer, Staff Nurse, Lab Technologist, LDC, Stenographer, Pharmacist
Last Date: November 26, 2025
Total Posts: 80+ Vacancies

The Central Council for Research in Homoeopathy (CCRH), an apex autonomous research organization under the Ministry of Ayush, Government of India, has officially announced its extensive recruitment drive for the year 2025. Dedicated to the formulation and execution of scientific research in Homoeopathy, the CCRH operates a vast network of research institutes, regional centers, and clinical units across the nation. This latest recruitment notification is a major initiative to bolster the technical, clinical, and administrative workforce of the Council. With over 80 vacancies advertised for Group A, B, and C cadres, the CCRH is inviting applications from qualified and motivated Indian citizens who wish to contribute to the advancement of traditional medicine through modern scientific research protocols. This drive offers a prestigious and permanent Central Government career for medical professionals, laboratory technicians, and administrative aspirants alike.

Working at CCRH is widely considered a significant professional milestone, especially for those in the medical and research communities. The 2025 notification covers a wide array of roles, including Research Officer (Homoeopathy), Staff Nurse, Laboratory Technologist, Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Stenographer, and Pharmacist. Each of these positions plays a vital role in the Council's daily operations—from conducting clinical trials and managing patient care to ensuring smooth administrative governance. Selected candidates will be entitled to pay scales governed by the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), ensuring financial security alongside a sense of national service. The application process is being conducted through a centralized online recruitment portal, and interested candidates must submit their applications by November 26, 2025. Whether you are a post-graduate in Homoeopathy (M.D.) or a 12th-pass student looking for a stable clerical role, this notification provides a comprehensive pathway to a fulfilling career.

In this exhaustive 2000-word guide, we provide a deep-dive analysis of every technical and administrative aspect of the CCRH Recruitment 2025. We will explore the category-wise vacancy distribution, detailed salary matrix components, specific eligibility requirements for each cadre, and the multi-stage selection process involving Computer Based Tests (CBT). Furthermore, this article provides an expert-recommended preparation strategy, covering the detailed syllabus and best resources to help you crack the upcoming exams. For aspirants ready to join the Ministry of Ayush and contribute to India's holistic healthcare revolution, this guide is the most important document you will read today. In the following sections, we will break down the official notification into actionable insights to ensure your application is successful and competitive.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must stay vigilant regarding the timeline provided by the Council. Adherence to deadlines is critical in Central Government recruitments. It is recommended to complete the online registration at least one week before the final date to avoid technical congestion on the server. The eligibility criteria regarding age and qualifications will be calculated as of the closing date of the application.

Recruitment Feature Dates and Details
Organization Name Central Council for Research in Homoeopathy (CCRH)
Ministry Ministry of Ayush, Government of India
Total Number of Vacancies 80+ Posts (Group A, B, C)
Online Registration Opening Active Now
Last Date to Submit Application November 26, 2025 (11:59 PM)
Application Mode Online via eapplynow.com
Job Nature Permanent / Regular Basis

Discipline Wise Vacancy Details

The 80+ vacancies are distributed across various technical and administrative cadres to ensure the smooth functioning of the Council's research institutes and administrative offices. Each post has a dedicated category-wise reservation as per the Government of India norms.

Post Designation Total Vacancies Selection Mode
Research Officer (Homoeopathy) 14 CBT + Interview
Staff Nurse 09 CBT Only
Laboratory Technologist 28 CBT Only
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) 27 CBT + Skill Test
Stenographer Grade-II 03 CBT + Skill Test
Pharmacist (Homoeopathy) 03 CBT Only
Grand Total 84 Vacancies -
Reservation Policy: Vacancies include reservations for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), EWS, and Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) as per the Central Government rules. Ensure your certificates are in the central format to avail of these benefits.

Salary Matrix and Organizational Benefits

Employees at CCRH are compensated as per the 7th CPC Matrix. The salary structure is highly competitive, offering high basic pay along with substantial central government allowances. Below is the breakdown of the estimated monthly packages for major roles.

  • Research Officer (Level 10): Initial Basic Pay of ₹56,100. Gross salary approximately ₹95,000 - ₹1,05,000.
  • Staff Nurse (Level 7): Initial Basic Pay of ₹44,900. Gross salary approximately ₹75,000 - ₹82,000.
  • Laboratory Technologist (Level 6): Initial Basic Pay of ₹35,400. Gross salary approximately ₹58,000 - ₹65,000.
  • Lower Division Clerk (Level 2): Initial Basic Pay of ₹19,900. Gross salary approximately ₹32,000 - ₹38,000.

Allowances and Social Security: Selected candidates will receive Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Transport Allowance (TA). Furthermore, employees are covered under the New Pension Scheme (NPS) and are entitled to medical facilities under CGHS, Leave Travel Concession (LTC), and specialized research allowances for officers.

Application Fee Details

  • General, OBC, and EWS (Male) Candidates: ₹1,000 (Group A) or ₹500 (Group B/C).
  • SC, ST, and PwBD Candidates: EXEMPTED (Nil).
  • All Female Candidates: EXEMPTED (Nil).
  • Ex-Servicemen: EXEMPTED (Nil).

The fee must be paid exclusively through the online recruitment portal. Please ensure the payment status is successful before closing the portal. Fees once paid are non-refundable under any circumstances.

Eligibility Criteria and Educational Qualifications

The CCRH maintains high academic standards for its professional workforce. Each post requires a specific background, and technical roles often mandate registration with the respective medical or technical councils.

Post Designation Mandatory Academic Qualification & Requirements
Research Officer Post Graduate degree (M.D.) in Homoeopathy from a recognized university. Must be registered in the National/State Register of Homoeopathy. Age Limit: 40 Years.
Staff Nurse B.Sc. Nursing (Hons) OR Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing. Must be registered with the Indian Nursing Council. Experience: 6 months in a 50-bed hospital. Age: 30 Years.
Lab Technologist Bachelor’s Degree in Medical Laboratory Science (B.MLS) OR B.Sc. with a Diploma in MLT and 2 years of experience. Age Limit: 30 Years.
LDC (Clerk) 12th Standard (Intermediate) pass from a recognized board. Typing Skill: 35 wpm in English OR 30 wpm in Hindi on a computer. Age Limit: 18-27 Years.
Pharmacist Diploma or Degree in Pharmacy (Homoeopathy) from a recognized institute. Must be a registered pharmacist. Age Limit: 25 Years.

Upper Age Relaxation: Government rules provide age relaxation to specific categories: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (NCL), and 10-15 years for PwBD candidates as per Central Government norms.

Selection Process and Evaluation roadmap

The selection process for CCRH Recruitment 2025 is designed to be meritocratic and transparent. The Council uses a tiered assessment model to filter the best talent for its institutes.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT): This is the primary stage for all applicants. It is an objective-type exam testing technical depth and general aptitude. For most posts, the CBT marks are the final deciding factor for the merit list.
  2. Skill Test: Mandatory for administrative posts like LDC and Stenographer. This is a qualifying only stage where typing and shorthand speeds are verified. Marks are not added to the merit list.
  3. Personal Interview: Conducted only for Group A Research Officer positions. Group B and C positions do not have an interview stage as per current central government policy.
  4. Document Verification: The final stage where original certificates and experience letters are scrutinized for authenticity.
  5. Medical Examination: Selected candidates must undergo a standard medical check-up to ensure fitness for duty.

Negative Marking: Candidates should be cautious as there is a negative marking of 0.25 (1/4th) for every incorrect answer in the CBT. Normalization of scores will be done if the exam is held in multiple shifts.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Success in the CCRH examination requires a strategic focus on both domain-specific knowledge and general mental ability. The difficulty level corresponds to the educational level required for the post.

1. Group C Pattern (LDC / Steno) - 100 Marks

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: 25 Questions. Analogy, series completion, coding-decoding, and logical puzzles.
  • Numerical Ability: 25 Questions. Arithmetic up to 10th standard level, profit and loss, time and work, and averages.
  • General Awareness: 25 Questions. Current affairs, basics of the Indian Constitution, and static GK.
  • General English: 25 Questions. Grammar, vocabulary, and reading comprehension.

2. Technical Posts (Nurse / Technologist) - 120 Marks

  • Technical Domain (70 Marks): Post-specific subjects. For Nurses: Anatomy, Pharmacology, and Nursing ethics. For Technologists: Biochemistry and Microbiology.
  • Aptitude Section (50 Marks): Common reasoning, English, and General Knowledge.

Expert Preparation Strategy for CCRH Recruitment

Securing a position in a premier research organization like CCRH requires more than just academic knowledge. Follow this professional roadmap to success.

Master the Subject Core: For research and clinical roles, your university textbooks are the primary resource. Nurses should focus on clinical management and surgical care, as CCRH institutes often participate in large-scale clinical trials. Laboratory Technologists must be thorough with the protocols for diagnostic equipment maintenance and safety standards.

Typing Accuracy for LDC: If you are aiming for the LDC role, typing speed is your ultimate hurdle. Practice using specialized typing software daily to reach a consistent speed of 40 WPM. Maintaining a buffer over the 35 WPM requirement ensures you pass even under the stress of the actual exam hall.

General Awareness Balance: Don't ignore the GA section. Stay updated with current affairs of the last 6-8 months. Specifically, read about the Ministry of Ayush initiatives, the "Mission LiFE" program, and recent developments in Homoeopathy globally. This knowledge is particularly useful during the Research Officer interviews.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing standard study material will keep your preparation streamlined and aligned with the national testing pattern. Below are the top recommendations.

Subject / Discipline Recommended Book List
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude and Reasoning Ability by R.S. Aggarwal.
General English Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi (Arihant Publications).
Nursing Theory Target High by Muthuvenkatachalam & Ambili or PR Yadav.
General Knowledge Lucent’s General Knowledge and monthly Pratiyogita Darpan.
Laboratory Technical MCQs in Medical Laboratory Technology by Z.B. Bhatti.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step Guide

The application process for CCRH is conducted through a unified online recruitment portal. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure a successful submission.

  1. Visit the official CCRH recruitment website: www.ccrhindia.nic.in.
  2. Navigate to the 'Recruitment' tab and locate the link for "Direct Recruitment 2025."
  3. You will be redirected to the eapplynow.com portal. Click on "New Registration".
  4. Register with your valid Email ID and Mobile Number. Verify your number using the OTP.
  5. Login and fill in the detailed application form, entering your personal, academic, and experience details precisely.
  6. Upload Documents: Scan and upload your recent passport photograph (white background), signature, and academic certificates in JPEG format as per the size limits.
  7. Fee Payment: Pay the application fee online using UPI, Debit Card, or Net Banking. Ensure you save the payment receipt.
  8. Final Submission: Review your application in the preview window. Click "Submit" and take a printout of the final application form for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready in digital format before starting the registration to avoid session timeouts. All documents should be clear and readable.

  1. Identity and Age Proof: Aadhaar Card and 10th Standard Marksheet or Birth Certificate.
  2. Educational Marksheets: All semester marksheets and final Degree/Diploma certificates.
  3. Professional Registration: Mandatory for Research Officers, Nurses, and Pharmacists (NCH or State Register).
  4. Experience Certificates: Essential for posts requiring prior clinical or technical experience.
  5. Category Certificate: SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificate in the Central Government format.
  6. Photograph & Signature: Clear scanned images as per the specified dimensions.

Important Links

Resource Description Official Direct Link
Online Application Portal (Apply Now) Register and Apply Here
Official Notification PDF Download Detailed PDF
Official CCRH Website Visit ccrhindia.nic.in

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply for more than one post at CCRH?

Yes, candidates can apply for multiple posts if they meet the eligibility criteria for each. However, you must submit separate application forms and pay the separate fees for each post category. Ensure you check that exam dates do not overlap.

Q2: Is the experience from a private hospital valid for the Staff Nurse post?

Yes, experience from a reputed private hospital is valid, provided it is a registered hospital with at least 50 beds. The experience must be gained after the completion of your nursing degree.

Q3: Is there an interview for the Lower Division Clerk (LDC) post?

No. As per the latest Government of India recruitment rules for Group C and Group B non-gazetted posts, there is no personal interview. Selection will be based purely on the CBT merit and qualifying the Skill Test.

Q4: What is the fee for female candidates?

According to the official CCRH notification, all female candidates regardless of their social category are completely exempted from paying any application fee for this recruitment cycle.

Q5: When will the CBT be conducted?

The specific date for the online exam has not been announced yet. Generally, central organizations conduct exams within 3-4 months after the application deadline. Candidates should keep checking the portal for admit card updates.

Q6: Are candidates from states other than Delhi eligible?

Yes. CCRH is a Central Government organization. Indian citizens from all states are eligible to apply. Reservations will be applicable as per the Central List of the Government of India.

entry.title: झारखंड होम गार्ड भर्ती 2025: 2500+ पदों पर बंपर वैकेंसी! 7वीं और 10वीं पास के लिए सुनहरा मौका!
entry.content:
झारखंड होम गार्ड भर्ती 2025: 2500+ पदों पर बंपर वैकेंसी! 7वीं और 10वीं पास के लिए सुनहरा मौका!

सेरैकैला-खरसावां, धनबाद, हजारीबाग, गढ़वा, लातेहार और अन्य जिलों में ग्रामीण व शहरी गृह रक्षक के पदों पर भर्ती।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! झारखंड के युवाओं के लिए बड़ी खबर है — Jharkhand Home Defense Corps यानी झारखंड गृह रक्षा वाहिनी ने राज्य के कई जिलों में ग्रामीण (Rural) और शहरी (Urban) गृह रक्षकों के नामांकन के लिए भर्ती अभियान शुरू किया है। सैरेकेला-खरसावां, धनबाद, गढ़वा, लातेहार, हजारीबाग, गिरिडीह और अन्य जिलों के लिए नोटिफिकेशन जारी किए गए हैं।

यह उन उम्मीदवारों के लिए सुनहरा अवसर है जो निःस्वार्थ भाव से देश और समाज की सेवा करना चाहते हैं। ध्यान दें — होम गार्ड एक स्वयंसेवी संगठन है, यह कोई स्थायी सरकारी नौकरी नहीं है। चयनित उम्मीदवारों को ड्यूटी के दिनों के आधार पर मानदेय (भत्ता) दिया जाएगा।

जिला-वार पद विवरण

जिलाकुल अनुमानित पदआवेदन तिथि
सैरेकेला-खरसावां44615.10.2025 से 29.10.2025
गढ़वा708अक्टूबर–नवंबर 2025 (संभावित)
धनबाद638अक्टूबर–नवंबर 2025 (संभावित)
लातेहार233अक्टूबर–नवंबर 2025 (संभावित)
हजारीबाग385अक्टूबर–नवंबर 2025 (संभावित)
गिरिडीह574जल्द घोषित होगी
नोट: पदों की संख्या और तिथियाँ अनुमानित हैं। नवीनतम अपडेट के लिए आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in देखें।

पात्रता मानदंड

निवास संबंधी पात्रता

  • ग्रामीण गृह रक्षक: उम्मीदवार को उसी प्रखंड का स्थायी निवासी होना चाहिए जहाँ से आवेदन कर रहा है।
  • शहरी गृह रक्षक: उम्मीदवार को संबंधित जिले के शहरी क्षेत्र का निवासी होना चाहिए।
मानदंडग्रामीणशहरी
आयु सीमा (01.01.2025 तक)19 से 40 वर्ष
शैक्षणिक योग्यतान्यूनतम 7वीं पासन्यूनतम 10वीं (मैट्रिक) पास

शारीरिक मापदंड

पैरामीटरपुरुषमहिला
लंबाई162 से.मी. (सामान्य/OBC/BC)148 से.मी. (सभी के लिए)
157 से.मी. (SC/ST)
छाती79 से.मी. (सामान्य/OBC/BC)लागू नहीं
76 से.मी. (SC/ST)

चयन प्रक्रिया

चयन प्रक्रिया में तीन चरण शामिल होंगे — शारीरिक परीक्षा, हिंदी लेखन परीक्षा और तकनीकी परीक्षा।

1️⃣ शारीरिक परीक्षा

इसमें 1 मील दौड़, ऊँची कूद, लंबी कूद और गोला फेंक जैसी प्रतियोगिताएँ होंगी। प्रत्येक इवेंट के लिए अंक निर्धारित हैं।

2️⃣ हिंदी लेखन परीक्षा

जो उम्मीदवार शारीरिक परीक्षा पास करेंगे, उन्हें 100 अंकों की हिंदी लेखन परीक्षा देनी होगी (ग्रामीण हेतु 7वीं स्तर, शहरी हेतु 10वीं स्तर)।

3️⃣ तकनीकी दक्षता परीक्षा

यह केवल शहरी तकनीकी गृह रक्षकों के लिए होगी, जो विशेष कौशल (जैसे ड्राइविंग, कंप्यूटर, इलेक्ट्रिकल आदि) रखते हैं।

बोनस अंक: राज्य/राष्ट्रीय स्तर के खिलाड़ी या सरकारी सेवा अनुभव वाले उम्मीदवारों को अतिरिक्त अंक दिए जाएँगे।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://recruitment.jharkhand.gov.in पर जाएँ।
  2. अपने जिले की होम गार्ड भर्ती अधिसूचना खोलें और निर्धारित तिथि के भीतर ऑनलाइन आवेदन करें।
  3. ₹100 आवेदन शुल्क ऑनलाइन जमा करें।
  4. आवेदन के साथ फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, निवास, जाति और शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र अपलोड करें।
  5. महत्वपूर्ण: आवेदन सबमिट करने के बाद उसका प्रिंटआउट निकालें और सभी दस्तावेज़ों के साथ अपने जिले के जिला समादेश्टा कार्यालय में जमा करें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदनयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?
नहीं। झारखंड होम गार्ड एक स्वयंसेवी संगठन है। इसमें केवल ड्यूटी के दिनों के अनुसार भत्ता मिलता है।
प्रश्न 2: क्या दूसरे जिले के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं। उम्मीदवार को उसी जिले का निवासी होना चाहिए जहाँ से आवेदन कर रहे हैं।
प्रश्न 3: आवेदन प्रक्रिया क्या है?
आवेदन ऑनलाइन होगा, पर प्रिंटआउट और दस्तावेज़ जिला कार्यालय में जमा करने होंगे।
प्रश्न 4: तकनीकी दक्ष गृह रक्षक कौन होते हैं?
जैसे ड्राइवर, इलेक्ट्रिशियन, कंप्यूटर ऑपरेटर, प्लंबर आदि। इनके लिए अलग शारीरिक व तकनीकी परीक्षा होती है।

entry.title: टेरीटोरियल आर्मी रैली 2025: साउदर्न कमांड में सोल्जर GD और ट्रेड्समैन की भर्ती!
entry.content:
टेरीटोरियल आर्मी रैली 2025: साउदर्न कमांड में सोल्जर GD और ट्रेड्समैन की भर्ती!

भारतीय सेना की टेरीटोरियल आर्मी (TA) साउदर्न कमांड ने विभिन्न यूनिट्स के लिए भर्ती रैली की घोषणा की है। रैलियाँ नवम्बर 2025 में आयोजित होंगी।

देश सेवा का जुनून रखने वाले युवाओं के लिए यह एक सुनहरा अवसर है। भारतीय सेना ने टेरीटोरियल आर्मी (TA) साउदर्न कमांड के अंतर्गत आने वाली इन्फेंट्री बटालियनों में भर्ती के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है। इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत सोल्जर (जनरल ड्यूटी) और सोल्जर (ट्रेड्समैन) के 1372 से अधिक पदों पर भर्ती की जाएगी। रैलियाँ 15 नवम्बर 2025 से 1 दिसम्बर 2025 तक विभिन्न केंद्रों पर आयोजित की जाएँगी।

टेरीटोरियल आर्मी में चयन पूरी तरह निष्पक्ष और पारदर्शी प्रक्रिया से होता है। उम्मीदवारों को किसी एजेंट या दलाल के झांसे में नहीं आना चाहिए। यह भर्ती पूरे साउदर्न कमांड के राज्यों के लिए मान्य है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ (रैली शेड्यूल)

घटनातिथि
रैली प्रारंभ15 नवम्बर 2025
रैली समाप्त01 दिसम्बर 2025
नोट: हर राज्य और ज़िले के लिए तारीख़ें अलग-अलग हैं। उम्मीदवारों को अपने क्षेत्र की निर्धारित तिथि पर ही रिपोर्ट करनी होगी। रिपोर्टिंग समय सुबह 5:00 बजे तक है, 10:00 बजे के बाद प्रवेश बंद हो जाएगा।

रिक्तियों का विवरण

पद का नामकुल पद
सोल्जर (जनरल ड्यूटी)1372
सोल्जर (शेफ कम्युनिटी)19
सोल्जर (क्लर्क)07
सोल्जर (हेयर ड्रैसर)05
अन्य ट्रेड्समैन (वॉशरमैन, टेलर आदि)15+

रैली केंद्रों की जानकारी

  • कोल्हापुर (महाराष्ट्र)
  • सिकंदराबाद (तेलंगाना)
  • बेलगावी (कर्नाटक)
  • देवलाली (महाराष्ट्र)
  • श्री विजया पुरम (अंडमान और निकोबार)

पात्रता मानदंड

मापदंडआवश्यक योग्यता
आयु सीमारैली की तारीख तक उम्मीदवार की आयु 18 से 42 वर्ष के बीच होनी चाहिए।
शैक्षणिक योग्यता
  • सोल्जर GD: 10वीं पास, कुल 45% अंक और प्रत्येक विषय में 33% अंक आवश्यक।
  • सोल्जर क्लर्क: 12वीं पास (किसी भी विषय से), कुल 60% अंक, प्रत्येक विषय में 50% अंक, इंग्लिश और गणित/एकाउंट्स में 50% अंक अनिवार्य।
  • ट्रेड्समैन (10वीं पास): साधारण 10वीं पास, प्रत्येक विषय में 33% अंक।
  • ट्रेड्समैन (8वीं पास): हाउसकीपर/मेस कीपर हेतु 8वीं पास।
शारीरिक मापदंड
  • ऊंचाई: 160 सेमी (गोरखा, गढ़वाली, लद्दाखी आदि को छूट)।
  • छाती: 77 सेमी (बिना फुलाए), 82 सेमी (फुलाने पर)।
  • वजन: आयु और ऊंचाई के अनुसार मानक।
निवासउम्मीदवार को संबंधित राज्य/क्षेत्र का निवासी होना चाहिए।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. शारीरिक दक्षता परीक्षा (PFT): 1.6 किमी दौड़, पुल-अप्स, बैलेंस और 9 फीट डिच शामिल हैं।
  2. शारीरिक माप परीक्षा (PMT): लंबाई, वजन और सीने का मापन किया जाएगा।
  3. चिकित्सा परीक्षण (Medical Exam): शारीरिक परीक्षण पास करने वालों की मेडिकल जांच होगी।
  4. लिखित परीक्षा (CEE): मेडिकल फिट उम्मीदवारों की सामान्य प्रवेश परीक्षा होगी, जिसमें निगेटिव मार्किंग होगी।
अंतिम मेरिट: फिजिकल और लिखित परीक्षा में प्राप्त अंकों के आधार पर तैयार की जाएगी।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

यह एक ओपन रैली भर्ती है, इसके लिए कोई ऑनलाइन आवेदन नहीं भरना है।

  1. उम्मीदवारों को अपने सभी मूल दस्तावेज़ों और दो-दो स्वप्रमाणित प्रतियों के साथ निर्धारित तारीख पर रैली स्थल पर पहुँचना होगा।
  2. आवश्यक दस्तावेज़: शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र, निवास प्रमाणपत्र, जाति प्रमाणपत्र, चरित्र प्रमाणपत्र, 20 पासपोर्ट साइज फोटो, पैन/आधार कार्ड।
  3. रिपोर्टिंग सुबह 05:00 बजे तक करनी होगी। 10:00 बजे के बाद प्रवेश नहीं मिलेगा।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
आधिकारिक भारतीय सेना वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
टेरीटोरियल आर्मी की जानकारीयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्र.1: टेरीटोरियल आर्मी क्या है? क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?
टेरीटोरियल आर्मी एक स्वैच्छिक, पार्ट-टाइम सेवा है। यह स्थायी नौकरी नहीं है। प्रारंभिक अवधि 7 वर्ष की होती है।
प्र.2: क्या इसके लिए ऑनलाइन आवेदन करना होगा?
नहीं, यह ओपन रैली भर्ती है। उम्मीदवारों को सीधे रैली स्थल पर पहुँचना होगा।
प्र.3: क्या लिखित परीक्षा में निगेटिव मार्किंग है?
हाँ, प्रत्येक गलत उत्तर पर 1 अंक काटा जाएगा।
प्र.4: क्या दूसरे राज्य के उम्मीदवार किसी भी केंद्र पर जा सकते हैं?
नहीं, उम्मीदवार केवल अपने राज्य/क्षेत्र के लिए निर्धारित केंद्र पर ही जा सकते हैं।

entry.title: IIT Jammu Recruitment 2026 for Non-Teaching Positions
entry.content:
IIT Jammu Recruitment 2026 for Non-Teaching Positions

Advertisement No. IIT Jammu/02/2025: Golden opportunity to secure a government job at the prestigious Indian Institute of Technology Jammu. A total of 27 vacancies are open for various roles including Officer, Assistant, and Registrar. Check detailed eligibility, salary, selection process, and apply online.

Post Name: Public Relations Officer, Assistant Registrar, Sports Officer, Fire and Safety Officer, Laboratory Officer, Senior Assistant, and more
Last Date: 03 February 2026
Total Posts: 27

The Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu), an institute of national importance, has released an official notification for the recruitment of various non-teaching positions through direct recruitment or deputation. This recruitment drive, under Advertisement No. IIT Jammu/02/2025, aims to fill 27 vacancies across different departments and administrative sections. This presents a remarkable opportunity for qualified Indian nationals seeking a stable and prestigious career in a central government autonomous body.

The available positions span across Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' categories, catering to a wide range of educational qualifications and professional experiences. Posts such as Public Relations Officer, Assistant Registrar, Sports Officer, Laboratory Officer, and Senior Assistant are open for applications. Interested candidates are advised to carefully read the detailed eligibility criteria, job responsibilities, and application procedure outlined in this article before applying. The online application portal will be active from January 3, 2026, to February 3, 2026.

IIT Jammu Non-Teaching Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Here are the key highlights of the IIT Jammu Recruitment drive. Candidates should note the important dates to ensure they complete the application process on time. This is a government job opportunity located in Jammu.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Indian Institute of Technology Jammu (IIT Jammu)
Post Name Various Non-Teaching Posts (Group A, B, C)
Advertisement Number IIT Jammu/02/2025
Total Vacancies 27
Application Start Date 03 January 2026
Last Date to Apply 03 February 2026 (upto 5:00 PM)
Mode of Application Online
Job Type Government Job
Job Location Jammu
Official Website iitjammu.ac.in

Vacancy Details

A total of 27 posts are being filled through this recruitment drive. The vacancies are distributed across various positions as detailed in the table below. Reservation policies as per Government of India norms are applicable.

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Public Relations Officer 01
Assistant Registrar 01
Sports Officer 01
Fire and Safety Officer 01
Senior Laboratory Officer 01
Laboratory Officer 03
Junior Section Officer 04
Assistant Sports Officer 01
Laboratory Assistant 07
Senior Assistant 07
Total 27
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may change based on the institute's requirements. Horizontal reservation for PwD candidates is applicable on select posts as mentioned in the official notification.

Application Fee

  • Group 'A' Posts: ₹1000/- (₹800 Application Fee + ₹200 Processing Fee)
  • Group 'B' & 'C' Posts: ₹500/- (₹300 Application Fee + ₹200 Processing Fee)
  • SC/ST, PwD, and Women Candidates: These candidates are exempted from paying the application fee. However, they are required to pay a non-refundable processing fee of ₹200/-.

The fee must be paid online through the link provided in the application portal. The fee is non-refundable under any circumstances.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet the specific age limit and educational qualification requirements for the post they are applying for. The cut-off date for determining the age limit is the last date of the application submission, i.e., February 3, 2026.

Post Name Age Limit & Eligibility Details
Public Relations Officer (Level-10) Age Limit: 45 years
Essential Qualification: Master's degree in Mass Communication and Journalism with 60% marks OR Master's Degree in any discipline with 60% marks and a PG Diploma in Journalism/Mass Communication.
Experience: At least 5 years of relevant experience in a newspaper, news agency, media organization, or as a PRO in a recognized university.
Assistant Registrar (Level-10) Age Limit: 45 years
Essential Qualification: Master's degree or equivalent in any discipline with at least 60% marks and a good academic record.
Experience: At least 5 years of relevant administrative experience in a supervisory capacity in a Govt. office, university, or technological institution in Pay Level 7/8.
Sports Officer (Level-10) Age Limit: 45 years
Essential Qualification: Master's degree with 60% marks in Physical Education/Sports Science with 5 years of experience OR Bachelor's degree (B.P.Ed) with 60% marks and 7 years of experience. Must have represented a University/Institute at the State/National level and have a specialization in a sport like Hockey, Athletics, Football, etc.
Fire and Safety Officer (Level-8) Age Limit: 40 years
Essential Qualification: Bachelor's Degree with 60% marks, plus a diploma/certification of at least one year in Fire and Safety Management or equivalent.
Experience: 5 years of experience as a Supervisor or equivalent in firefighting in a Civil/Defence Fire Service, PSU, or reputed organization.
Senior Laboratory Officer (Level-8) Age Limit: 40 years
Essential Qualification: Master's Degree in Engg./Science with 60% marks and 5 years experience OR B.E./B.Tech with 60% marks and 7 years experience OR B.Sc. with 60% marks and 8 years experience OR 3-year Diploma in Engg. with 60% marks and 8 years experience in the relevant field. Experience must be in Pay Level 6 or equivalent.
Laboratory Officer (Level-6) Age Limit: 35 years
Essential Qualification: Master's Degree in Engg./Science with 60% marks and 3 years experience OR B.E./B.Tech with 60% marks and 5 years experience OR B.Sc. with 60% marks and 6 years experience OR 3-year Diploma in Engg. with 60% marks and 6 years experience. Experience must be in Pay Level 4 or equivalent.
Junior Section Officer (Level-6) Age Limit: 35 years
Essential Qualification: Master's Degree in any discipline with 60% marks and 3 years experience OR Bachelor's Degree with 60% marks and 5 years experience in Administration/Accounts/Audit/Purchase as a Senior Assistant or equivalent in Pay Level 4.
Assistant Sports Officer (Level-6) Age Limit: 35 years
Essential Qualification: Bachelor of Physical Education (B.P.Ed) or equivalent with 60% marks. Must have represented a University/Institute at the State/National level and possess specialization in a sport.
Experience: 3 years of relevant experience in a recognized institution.
Laboratory Assistant (Level-5) Age Limit: 33 years
Essential Qualification: Master's Degree in Engg./Science with 60% marks and 1 year experience OR B.E./B.Tech with 60% marks and 2 years experience OR B.Sc. with 60% marks and 3 years experience OR 3-year Diploma in Engg. with 60% marks and 3 years experience. Experience must be in Pay Level 2 or equivalent.
Senior Assistant (Level-5) Age Limit: 33 years
Essential Qualification: Master's Degree in any discipline with 60% marks and 1 year experience OR Bachelor's Degree with 60% marks and 3 years experience in Administration/Accounts/Audit/Purchase as a Junior Assistant or equivalent in Pay Level 2.

Selection Process

The selection of candidates will be based on a multi-stage assessment process designed to evaluate their suitability for the respective roles. The Institute reserves the right to set benchmarks and call only eligible candidates for the tests. The process will broadly consist of the following stages:

  1. Screening of Applications: All applications will be screened based on the information provided by the candidates.
  2. Written Test: Shortlisted candidates will be called for a written examination. This test will assess their general aptitude, subject knowledge, and skills relevant to the post. For Group 'B' and 'C' posts, this will be Stage I.
  3. Skill/Proficiency/Computer Test: Candidates who qualify the written test (for Group B & C posts) will be called for the next stage, which could be a Proficiency Test, Computer Test, or Trade Test, depending on the job requirements. This will be Stage II.
  4. Interview: For Group 'A' posts, an interview will be a crucial part of the selection process, following the initial screening/written test.

Note: Each stage can be an elimination round. The minimum qualifying mark for each stage is 50%, with a final aggregate qualifying mark of 60% (relaxations applicable for reserved categories as per GoI norms).

Exam Pattern

The official notification does not specify a detailed exam pattern or syllabus for the written tests. IIT Jammu will likely release this information on its official website closer to the examination date. However, candidates can prepare based on a general expected pattern for such competitive examinations.

For Administrative Posts (e.g., Assistant Registrar, Junior Section Officer, Senior Assistant):

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: Puzzles, Seating Arrangement, Coding-Decoding, Syllogism.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: Arithmetic, Algebra, Geometry, Data Interpretation.
  • English Language: Comprehension, Grammar, Vocabulary, Error Spotting.
  • General Awareness: Current Affairs, Indian History, Geography, Polity, Basic Science.
  • Domain Knowledge: Questions on Government rules (GFR 2017), office procedures, noting, drafting, establishment rules, etc.

For Technical Posts (e.g., Laboratory Officer/Assistant):

  • Part A (General Aptitude): Similar to the administrative posts, covering Reasoning, English, and General Awareness.
  • Part B (Technical/Domain Knowledge): In-depth questions from the specific engineering or science discipline (e.g., Civil, Electrical, Chemical, Bioscience) mentioned in the eligibility criteria. This section will test the candidate's theoretical and practical knowledge.

Candidates are strongly advised to regularly check the official IIT Jammu website for the latest updates on the syllabus and exam scheme.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

The detailed advertisement (IIT Jammu/02/2025) does not mention a mandatory Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for any of the advertised positions, including the Fire and Safety Officer and Sports Officer roles. However, for posts like Fire and Safety Officer, the notification does mention that the candidate "Must be able to handle fire arms with sound health and active habits." The selection committee may assess physical fitness through other means during the selection process.

How to Prepare for the IIT Jammu Recruitment Exam

A structured preparation strategy is key to success. Here’s a guide on how to prepare for both the written test and subsequent stages.

  1. Understand the Job Role: Thoroughly read the job responsibilities for the post you are applying for. This will give you clues about the potential topics for the domain-specific part of the exam.
  2. Master the Basics: For the general aptitude section, focus on building a strong foundation in Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, and English. Practice regularly from standard competitive exam books.
  3. Stay Updated: Keep abreast of current affairs, especially national and international events from the last 6-8 months. Read newspapers and monthly magazines.
  4. Revise Your Core Subjects: For technical posts, revisit your graduation/diploma syllabus. Focus on fundamental concepts and their practical applications.
  5. Practice Mock Tests: Once the exam pattern is released, solve as many mock tests and previous year papers (from similar organizations like other IITs or PSUs) as possible. This will improve your speed and accuracy.
  6. Prepare for Skill Tests: If applying for an administrative post, practice typing and become proficient in MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). For lab posts, refresh your knowledge of lab procedures and equipment handling.
  7. Prepare for the Interview: For Group A posts, prepare for the interview by researching IIT Jammu, understanding the challenges of the role, and being ready to discuss your experience and qualifications in detail.

Best Books for Preparation

While the exact syllabus is pending, candidates can start their preparation with these standard and highly recommended books.

  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi or "High School English Grammar and Composition" by Wren & Martin.
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" is a comprehensive book for static GK. For current affairs, follow a monthly magazine like Pratiyogita Darpan.
  • Domain/Technical Subjects: Refer to the standard textbooks and reference books from your specific engineering/science stream during your diploma/degree/master's program.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online. Follow these steps to submit your application successfully:

  1. Visit the official IIT Jammu application portal: https://apply.iitjammu.ac.in.
  2. Read the advertisement carefully to ensure you meet the eligibility criteria for your desired post.
  3. Register on the portal using a valid email ID and mobile number to create your login credentials.
  4. Log in and fill out the online application form with accurate personal, academic, and professional details.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your recent passport-size photograph, signature, and other necessary documents in the specified format.
  6. Pay the application fee online via the integrated payment gateway.
  7. Review the application form thoroughly before final submission to avoid any errors.
  8. After submission, download and print a copy of the completed application form for your records. This printout will be required at a later stage of the selection process.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should keep the following documents ready before starting the online application process and for future verification:

  1. Scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph.
  2. Scanned copy of the candidate's signature.
  3. Valid Email ID and Mobile Number for communication.
  4. Certificates and mark sheets of all educational qualifications (10th, 12th, Diploma, UG, PG, etc.).
  5. Experience certificates detailing the period of employment, pay level, and job responsibilities.
  6. Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwD) in the format prescribed by the Government of India.
  7. Photo ID proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, Passport, etc.).
  8. No Objection Certificate (NOC) if currently employed in a Government/Semi-Govt./PSU organization (to be produced at the time of the test/interview).

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online Click Here (Not Active Yet)
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Q1: What is the last date to apply for the IIT Jammu Non-Teaching Recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is February 3, 2026, up to 5:00 PM.

Q2: How many vacancies are available in this recruitment?

There are a total of 27 vacancies across 10 different non-teaching posts, including Officer, Assistant, and Registrar roles.

Q3: Is there any application fee?

Yes, the fee is ₹1000 for Group 'A' posts and ₹500 for Group 'B' & 'C' posts. However, SC, ST, PwD, and all women candidates are exempted from the application fee but must pay a ₹200 processing fee.

Q4: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, if you are eligible, you can apply for multiple posts. You will need to fill out a separate application form and pay the fee for each post.

Q5: What is the selection procedure for these posts?

The selection process is multi-staged and may include a Written Test, a Skill/Proficiency Test (like a computer test or trade test), and an Interview, depending on the specific requirements of the post.

entry.title: RCF Management Trainee (Safety) Recruitment 2025: A 'Navratna' Opportunity for Engineers
entry.content:
RCF Management Trainee (Safety) Recruitment 2025: A 'Navratna' Opportunity for Engineers

A fantastic career opening for Chemical, Petrochemical, and Fire & Safety Engineers in one of India's leading Public Sector Undertakings. Online applications are open from December 6 to December 20, 2025.

Post Name: Management Trainee (Safety)
Last Date: 20/12/2025
