Total Posts: 08

Rashtriya Chemicals and Fertilizers Limited (RCF Ltd), a leading and profit-making 'Navratna' Public Sector Undertaking of the Government of India, has announced an excellent career opportunity for engineering graduates. Under Advertisement No. 15072025, RCF has invited applications for recruitment to the prestigious post of Management Trainee (Safety). This is a golden chance for qualified and dynamic Chemical, Petrochemical, or Fire & Safety Engineering graduates who aspire to build their careers in a leading company in India's fertilizer and industrial chemical sector.

With a revenue of approximately Rs. 16,933.64 crores, RCF Ltd is a leading name in the manufacturing and marketing of fertilizers and industrial chemicals. The company's manufacturing units are located at Thal (District-Raigad) and Trombay (Chembur, Mumbai) in Maharashtra, with a nationwide marketing network. As a 'Navratna' company, RCF offers its employees excellent career growth opportunities, a competitive salary package, and a professional work environment. This recruitment drive is aimed at grooming future leaders in the Safety department, who will play a critical role in ensuring safety standards in the company's operations.

Selected candidates will undergo an intensive one-year training period, after which they will be permanently absorbed in the E1 Grade. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online from December 6, 2025, to December 20, 2025. In this article, we will provide you with all the essential information regarding this recruitment, including vacancy details, educational qualifications, age limit, pay scale, selection process, and how to apply.

RCF Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Aspirants are advised to take note of these key points.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Rashtriya Chemicals and Fertilizers Limited (RCF Ltd)
Advertisement Number 15072025
Post Name Management Trainee (Safety)
Total Vacancies 08
Job Location Trombay/Thal (Maharashtra) and All India Marketing Offices
Application Mode Online
Online Application Dates December 6, 2025, to December 20, 2025

Vacancy Details and Reservation

RCF Ltd has released a notification for a total of 08 posts of Management Trainee (Safety). These vacancies are distributed among various categories as per the reservation guidelines of the Government of India:

Category Number of Posts
Unreserved (UR) 04
Scheduled Caste (SC) 01
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 01
Other Backward Classes (OBC-NCL) 02
Economically Weaker Section (EWS) -
Total 08
Note: No vacancies are reserved for the PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) category under this post.

Salary and Other Benefits

RCF Ltd offers its Management Trainees an attractive salary and benefits package, which is among the best in the industry.

  • During the Training Period (1 Year):
    • A consolidated monthly stipend of ₹ 60,000/- per month.
    • Free hostel accommodation will be provided at Trombay/Thal.
    • Medical facility for self at the RCF Hospital.
    • Access to the RCF sports complex.
  • On Confirmation (in E1 Grade):
    • Pay Scale: ₹ 40,000 - ₹ 1,40,000/-.
    • The minimum total monthly gross emoluments will be approximately ₹ 86,320/- per month (including Basic Pay + VDA + Perks + HRA).
    • Other Benefits: Company accommodation (subject to availability), Performance Related Pay (PRP), Gratuity, Contributory Provident Fund (CPF), Accident Insurance, Pension Scheme, and Social Security Schemes.

Service Bond: Selected candidates will be required to execute a service bond of ₹ 2 Lakhs at the time of joining, for a period of 4 years (including the training period).

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Candidates applying for this post must have a specific engineering background and an additional qualification in industrial safety. The cut-off date for eligibility is August 1, 2025.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Option Details
Option A Regular full-time 4-year B.E./B.Tech. Engineering Degree (in Chemical/Petrochemical/Chemical Technology) AND a one-year Advanced Diploma in Industrial Safety from a recognized institute.
Option B (Lateral Entry) After a regular full-time 3-year Diploma in Engineering, a regular full-time 3-year B.E./B.Tech. Engineering Degree (in Chemical/Petrochemical/Chemical Technology) AND a one-year Advanced Diploma in Industrial Safety.
Option C Regular full-time 4-year B.E./B.Tech. Engineering Degree (in Fire & Safety) AND a one-year Advanced Diploma in Industrial Safety.
Option D (Lateral Entry) After a regular full-time 3-year Diploma in Engineering, a regular full-time 3-year B.E./B.Tech. Engineering Degree (in Fire & Safety) AND a one-year Advanced Diploma in Industrial Safety.

Minimum Percentage: A minimum of 60% aggregate marks (55% for SC/ST candidates) in the final year of the Engineering graduation.

Important Note: The Advanced Diploma in Industrial Safety course must have been completed only after passing the main engineering degree.

Age Limit (as on August 1, 2025)

Category Maximum Age Limit
Unreserved (UR) 27 years
Other Backward Classes (OBC-NCL) 30 years
Scheduled Caste / Scheduled Tribe (SC/ST) 32 years
Children/Family members of the victims of 1984 riots Additional 5 years relaxation

Selection Process

The selection process for the post of Management Trainee (Safety) will be conducted in two stages: an Online Test and a Personal Interview.

  1. Online Test:
    • Eligible candidates will have to appear for a computer-based online objective-type test. The duration of the test will be 90 minutes.
    • There will be a total of 100 questions, divided into two parts:
      • Discipline-related: 50 questions (2 marks each) from the curriculum of your engineering degree and safety diploma.
      • Aptitude-related: 50 questions (1 mark each) covering General English, Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, and General Knowledge/Awareness.
    • Negative Marking: There will be negative marking for wrong answers.
    • Test Centers: Bhopal, Delhi/NCR, Lucknow, Hyderabad, Bengaluru, Chennai, Guwahati, Kolkata, Mumbai/Navi Mumbai/Thane, and Nagpur.
  2. Document Verification and Personal Interview:
    • Based on the performance in the online test, candidates will be shortlisted for document verification and personal interview in the ratio of 1:7 as per the vacancies in each category.
    • Candidates securing zero or negative marks in the online test will be disqualified.
    • Candidates found eligible after document verification will appear for the personal interview. A minimum of 50% marks is mandatory to qualify in the interview.
  3. Final Selection:
    • The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the online test and the personal interview.
    • The weightage for the online test will be 80%, and for the personal interview, it will be 20%.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

A clear understanding of the exam pattern and syllabus is crucial for effective preparation.

Online Test Pattern

Part Subject No. of Questions Marks per Question Total Marks
Discipline Knowledge Chemical / Fire & Safety Engineering + Industrial Safety 50 2 100
Aptitude General English 50 1 50
Quantitative Aptitude
Reasoning Ability
General Knowledge / Awareness
Grand Total 150

Syllabus (Expected)

  • Discipline Knowledge:
    • Chemical/Petrochemical Engineering: Process Calculations, Thermodynamics, Fluid Mechanics, Heat Transfer, Mass Transfer, Chemical Reaction Engineering, Process Control, Chemical Technology.
    • Fire & Safety Engineering: Fire prevention, protection and control systems, fire dynamics, explosion control, building codes.
    • Industrial Safety Diploma: The Factories Act, Safety Management, Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA), Industrial Hygiene and Occupational Health, Chemical Safety, Electrical Safety, Emergency Planning and Response.
  • Aptitude:
    • General English: Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension, Error Spotting.
    • Quantitative Aptitude: Percentage, Profit & Loss, Time & Work, Data Interpretation, Averages.
    • Reasoning Ability: Puzzles, Seating Arrangement, Coding-Decoding, Series Completion, Syllogism.
    • General Knowledge: Current national and international events, particularly related to science, technology, and industry.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for this recruitment. The selection is based purely on performance in the online test and personal interview.

How to Prepare for the RCF MT (Safety) Exam

A well-structured strategy is needed to succeed in the RCF Management Trainee (Safety) examination.

  1. Understand the Syllabus: The exam has two parts - discipline knowledge and aptitude. Understand the weightage and marking scheme for both.
  2. Master the Discipline Knowledge (100 Marks):
    • This is the most critical section. Thoroughly study your Chemical/Petrochemical/Fire & Safety Engineering graduate-level books.
    • Pay special attention to the syllabus of the Industrial Safety Diploma. This will include topics like safety laws, occupational health, HIRA, Safety Management Systems (SMS), and emergency planning.
      • - Practice relevant topics from previous years' GATE (Chemical Engineering) question papers.
  3. Strengthen Your Aptitude (50 Marks):
    • General English: Focus on grammar, vocabulary, and comprehension.
      • - Quantitative Aptitude: Practice topics like percentage, profit-loss, time and work, and data interpretation. - Reasoning: Practice puzzles, seating arrangements, coding-decoding, and series completion. - General Knowledge: Keep an eye on recent national and international events, especially news related to science, technology, and industry.
  4. Mock Tests and Time Management:
      - Speed and accuracy are vital to solve 100 questions in 90 minutes. - Keeping negative marking in mind, attempt only those questions you are sure about. - Regularly assess your preparation by taking online mock tests.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books that can aid your preparation:

  • For Discipline Knowledge:
    • "Introduction to Chemical Engineering Thermodynamics" by Smith, Van Ness.
    • "Unit Operations of Chemical Engineering" by McCabe, Smith.
    • "Industrial Safety, Health and Environment Management Systems" by R.K. Jain and Sunil S. Rao.
    • Handbooks and notes from the Industrial Safety Diploma course.
  • For Aptitude Section:
    • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
    • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
    • "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
    • A standard GK book like "Lucent's General Knowledge" and regular newspaper reading.

How to Apply for RCF Recruitment

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must apply through the official website of RCF Ltd.

  1. Pre-registration Preparation: Before applying, keep the following scanned documents ready:
    • Recent passport-size photograph (4.5cm x 3.5cm, 20kb-50kb).
    • Signature with black ink (10kb-20kb).
    • Left thumb impression (on white paper with black/blue ink, 20kb-50kb).
    • A self-handwritten declaration (with black ink, 50kb-100kb). The text for the declaration is given in the advertisement.
  2. Application Process:
    • Go to the RCF Ltd website: www.rcfltd.com.
    • Click on the "APPLY ONLINE" option.
    • Choose the "Click here for New Registration" tab and enter your name, contact details, and email-ID.
    • A provisional registration number and password will be generated, which you should note down.
    • Log in again with the registration number and password and fill in all the required details in the application form.
    • Upload the scanned photograph, signature, thumb impression, and declaration.
    • Carefully check all the information in the application form and click the "COMPLETE REGISTRATION" button.
    • If applicable, pay the application fee through the online gateway.
    • Take a printout of the final submitted application form and the e-receipt.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Active from 06.12.2025)
Official Website http://www.rcfltd.com

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for RCF Management Trainee (Safety)?

The last date to apply online is December 20, 2025, at 5:00 PM.

Question 2: Can final-year engineering students apply?

No, as per the notification, the candidate must possess all the required qualifications as of the cut-off date (01.08.2025).

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection process includes an Online Test and a Personal Interview. The final selection will be based on a weightage of 80% for the online test and 20% for the interview.

Question 4: Is there an application fee for female candidates?

No, female candidates are exempted from the payment of the application fee.

Question 5: Is there negative marking for wrong answers in the online test?

Yes, there will be negative marking for wrong answers in the online test.

entry.title: IMD and BOAT Recruitment 2025: New Career Opportunities in Central Institutions
entry.content:
IMD and BOAT Recruitment 2025: New Career Opportunities in Central Institutions

Direct Recruitment and Project-Based Vacancies for Scientists, Assistants, and LDC Staff - Comprehensive Guide for Aspirants

Post Name: Project Scientist (E, III, II, I), Scientific Assistant, Lower Division Clerk (LDC)
Last Date: December 01 / 14, 2025
Total Posts: 130+ Vacancies (Combined)

The technical and administrative landscape of the Government of India is supported by several specialized institutions that drive research, monitoring, and vocational training. Two such prominent organizations, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) and the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT), Western Region, Mumbai, have officially announced their recruitment drives for the year 2025. While IMD functions under the Ministry of Earth Sciences and is responsible for meteorological observations and weather forecasting, BOAT operates under the Ministry of Education, facilitating apprenticeship programs for engineering and diploma graduates. These recruitments cater to different professional backgrounds, ranging from high-end scientific research to essential administrative support.

The IMD Recruitment 2025 is centered around the prestigious Mission Mausam scheme, a multi-departmental initiative aimed at enhancing India's weather prediction capabilities through advanced R&D. This drive seeks to fill over 100 project-based vacancies, including various grades of Project Scientists and Scientific Assistants. Simultaneously, the BOAT Mumbai Recruitment 2025 is looking for a dedicated individual for the post of Lower Division Clerk (LDC) on a permanent basis (initially temporary). Both organizations offer competitive salaries and the opportunity to work in a central government environment. While IMD’s application process is primarily online, starting from November 24, 2025, BOAT requires an offline submission by December 1, 2025.

Working in these institutions provides an entry into an elite ecosystem of service and innovation. IMD scientists play a crucial role in disaster management and agricultural planning, while BOAT administrative staff ensure the smooth implementation of the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS). This exhaustive 2000-word article provides a comprehensive analysis of both notifications, offering detailed insights into eligibility, salary matrices, selection methodologies, and strategic preparation roadmaps to help you secure a position in these critical national organizations.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must be highly attentive to the individual timelines of both organizations. IMD follows a project-specific schedule for its contractual staff, while BOAT follows the standard central government recruitment cycle for its permanent cadre. It is recommended to keep all self-attested documents ready before the portals open or the postage deadline approaches.

Organization Application Start Date Last Date to Apply Application Mode
India Meteorological Dept (IMD) November 24, 2025 December 14, 2025 Online (mausam.imd.gov.in)
BOAT (Western Region), Mumbai Active Now December 01, 2025 Offline (Registered Post)
Job Category Project Based (IMD) / Permanent Govt (BOAT)

IMD Project Staff: Detailed Vacancy Breakdown

The vacancies at IMD are part of a time-bound project under "Mission Mausam." These roles are highly specialized and focused on atmospheric science, numerical modeling, and electronic instrumentation. The emoluments are structured to attract the best scientific talent in the country.

Post Designation Total Vacancies Emoluments (7th CPC Based) Max Age Limit
Project Scientist - E 01 ₹1,23,100 + HRA 50 Years
Project Scientist - III 11+ ₹78,000 + HRA 45 Years
Project Scientist - II 26+ ₹67,000 + HRA 40 Years
Project Scientist - I 65+ ₹56,000 + HRA 35 Years
Scientific Assistant 25 ₹29,200 + HRA 30 Years
Admin Assistant 02 ₹29,200 + HRA 30 Years
Project Tenure: Initial appointment at IMD is for one year. However, based on the satisfactory performance of the candidate and the requirement of the Mission Mausam project, the tenure can be extended up to March 31, 2026, or the completion of the project, whichever is earlier.

BOAT Mumbai: Lower Division Clerk Vacancy

BOAT Mumbai is a vital administrative unit facilitating technical education across the western states. The LDC position is essential for the management of the NATS portal and candidate documentation.

Post Name Total Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC) Job Location
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) 01 (Unreserved) Level 2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200) Sion, Mumbai

Salary and Perks: A newly appointed LDC in Mumbai will receive a total gross salary of approximately ₹38,948 per month. This includes the high HRA percentage applicable to Mumbai (X-Category city) and other central government perks like Transport Allowance and medical benefits.

Institutional Eligibility Criteria

Both organizations require specific academic credentials. While IMD focuses on Master's degrees in science and engineering, BOAT follows the standard matriculation-based entry for its clerical cadre.

1. Scientific and Research Roles at IMD

  • Project Scientist (General): M.Sc. in Physics, Mathematics, Meteorology, Atmospheric Science, or related subjects with 60% marks OR B.E./B.Tech in Computer Science, IT, or Electronics.
  • Experience for Scientists: Scientist-III requires 7 years of R&D experience, while Scientist-E requires 11 years. Scientist-I and II require 0-3 years of experience in specialized fields like satellite data processing or numerical weather prediction.
  • Scientific Assistant: Bachelor’s Degree in Science (B.Sc.) with Physics as one of the subjects OR a Bachelor’s in Computer Applications/Engineering/IT.

2. Administrative Support at BOAT Mumbai

  • LDC Qualification: Matriculation (10th Standard) pass from a recognized board or its equivalent.
  • Technical Skill: A minimum typing speed of 30 words per minute in English is mandatory. Candidates should also be proficient in using personal computers and MS Office applications.
  • Age Limit: Maximum 30 years as of December 1, 2025. Standard age relaxations for SC/ST (5 years) and OBC (3 years) are applicable as per Central Government rules.

Salary Matrix and Benefits Analysis

Selected candidates in these organizations are compensated as per the 7th CPC standards. The emoluments for IMD scientists are particularly attractive because the HRA is calculated based on the project location (often major metros). A Project Scientist-III can earn over ₹1,00,000 per month including HRA.

Statutory Benefits: For the permanent LDC post at BOAT, benefits include the New Pension Scheme (NPS), Leave Travel Concession (LTC), and Government Medical Facilities. Contractual staff at IMD are generally provided with a consolidated amount plus HRA, but they do not receive pension benefits due to the project-based nature of the role.

Selection Process and Evaluation roadmap

The selection methodology is designed to filter candidates based on technical expertise for IMD and administrative aptitude for BOAT.

  1. Initial Screening: For IMD, applications are screened based on UGC/AICTE marks and relevant research publications. For BOAT, screening is based on 10th-standard marks and typing proficiency.
  2. Written Test / Computer Based Test: IMD may conduct a technical test for Scientific Assistants. BOAT Mumbai will conduct a Psychometric Test and a written test to evaluate general intelligence.
  3. Skill Test: Mandatory for the LDC post at BOAT. Candidates must clear the 30 WPM typing hurdle on a computer to be considered for the final merit list.
  4. Personal Interview: Conducted for all Project Scientist grades at IMD. The interview evaluates the candidate's understanding of atmospheric physics, numerical modeling, and data science.

Detailed Exam Pattern and Syllabus

Preparation must be post-specific. While clerical roles focus on general aptitude, scientific roles require deep subject knowledge.

IMD Scientific Assistant Syllabus: Includes Physics (Mechanics, Thermodynamics, Optics), Computer Science (Operating Systems, Networking, Data Structures), and basic Numerical Ability. Questions often revolve around the application of these subjects in meteorology.

BOAT LDC Syllabus:

  • General Intelligence: Coding-decoding, series completion, analogies, and logical puzzles.
  • General Awareness: Current events, Indian History, and basics of the Indian Apprenticeship Act.
  • Numerical Aptitude: Basic arithmetic, profit and loss, time and distance, and percentages.
  • General English: Grammar, vocabulary, and reading comprehension.

Expert Preparation Strategy for IMD and BOAT

Securing a position in these institutions requires a blend of academic depth and practical skills. Follow this expert roadmap to success.

Focus on Atmospheric Physics for IMD: Even for IT graduates, having a basic understanding of atmospheric physics and fluid dynamics is beneficial during IMD interviews. Review the basics of "Remote Sensing" and "Satellite Meteorology" as these are current priorities for the Mission Mausam project.

Master the Typing Test for BOAT: For the LDC post, the typing test is the primary filter. Don't wait for the call letter to start practicing. Use typing software daily to reach a consistent speed of 35-40 WPM to comfortably clear the 30 WPM requirement during the actual test. Focus on accuracy over speed initially.

Psychometric Test Awareness: BOAT Mumbai mentions a psychometric test in its selection process. These tests evaluate personality traits, behavioral tendencies, and cognitive styles. Practicing basic situational judgment tests (SJT) can help you perform better in this section.

Best Books for Preparation

Quality study material keeps your preparation streamlined. Below are the top recommendations for the 2025 recruitment cycle.

Subject Area Recommended Book List
General Aptitude (LDC) Quantitative Aptitude and Reasoning Ability by R.S. Aggarwal.
General English Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi (Arihant).
Meteorology Basics (IMD) "The Atmosphere" by Frederick K. Lutgens or IMD departmental manuals.
Computer Science Objective Computer Science by Dr. Alok Kumar or Gate Handbooks.
General Knowledge Lucent’s General Knowledge and Manorama Yearbook.

How to Apply Online and Offline

The application procedures differ significantly between the two organizations. Follow these steps meticulously.

Applying for IMD Project Staff:

  1. Visit the official website: mausam.imd.gov.in.
  2. Click on the "Recruitment" tab and find Advertisement No. 02/2025 IMD.
  3. Registration: Register with a valid Email ID and Mobile Number from November 24, 2025.
  4. Fill Application: Login and enter academic scores. Upload scanned copies of your research papers (if any) and experience letters.
  5. Final Submission: Review the application and click "Submit." Save the generated application number.

Applying for BOAT Mumbai LDC:

  1. Download the application format from boatwr.education.gov.in.
  2. Fill the form in BLOCK LETTERS. Paste a recent self-attested passport-size photograph.
  3. Fee Payment: Obtain a Demand Draft (DD) or IPO of ₹300/- in favor of "Director, Board of Apprenticeship Training (Western Region), Mumbai."
  4. Attach self-attested copies of 10th marksheet, category certificate, and ID proof.
  5. Postage: Send the envelope via Registered/Speed Post to the address mentioned in the links section below. Mark the envelope: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF LOWER DIVISION CLERK".

Application Fee Structure

The application fee details for IMD will be provided in the detailed online portal. For BOAT Mumbai, the fee is standard for a clerical cadre.

Organization General (UR) / OBC / EWS Fee SC / ST / PwBD / Female Fee
IMD Project Staff Refer to Mausam Portal EXEMPTED (Nil)
BOAT Mumbai LDC ₹300/- (DD/IPO) EXEMPTED (Nil)

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready to ensure a smooth application process. All certificates must be issued before the respective closing dates.

  1. Identity Proof: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID.
  2. Educational Marksheets: 10th Standard (Clerk), Degree, and Master’s (Scientist) certificates.
  3. Caste Certificate: Valid SC/ST/NC-OBC certificate in the Central Government format.
  4. Experience Certificates: Essential for higher scientist grades at IMD. Must be on official letterheads.
  5. Demand Draft: Original DD/IPO (Mandatory for BOAT applicants).
  6. NOC: Required for candidates currently working in other Government or PSU organizations.

Important Links and Address

Resource Description Official URL Link / Details
IMD Official Recruitment Site mausam.imd.gov.in
BOAT Mumbai Official Website boatwr.education.gov.in
BOAT Postal Address The Director, BOAT (WR), II Floor, New Administrative Bldg, Sion (E), Mumbai-400022
Full Notification PDF (IMD) Download Here

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Are the IMD Project Scientist posts permanent?

No. The Project Scientist and Scientific Assistant posts at IMD under "Mission Mausam" are purely contractual and project-based. The tenure depends on the life of the project, though it offers an excellent research platform.

Q2: Can I apply for the BOAT LDC post via email?

No. BOAT Mumbai requires candidates to submit their applications in the Offline Prescribed Format via post. Applications sent through email or any other online medium will not be considered.

Q3: Is the application fee for BOAT refundable?

No. The ₹300/- application fee paid via Demand Draft or IPO is strictly non-refundable under any circumstances.

Q4: What is the age relaxation for SC/ST candidates in BOAT Mumbai?

As per Central Government norms, SC and ST candidates are eligible for a 5-year relaxation in the upper age limit. OBC candidates receive a 3-year relaxation.

Q5: Is experience mandatory for the Scientific Assistant post at IMD?

No. According to the current notification, the Scientific Assistant post is an entry-level technical role that does not mandate prior research experience, making it ideal for fresh science graduates.

Q6: When will the IMD online application link become active?

The online application portal for IMD Project Staff is scheduled to go live on November 24, 2025, on the official mausam.imd.gov.in website.

entry.title: THDC Recruitment 2025: Apply for Mine Surveyor and Junior Overman Posts
entry.content:
THDC Recruitment 2025: Notification for Mine Surveyor and Junior Overman Posts

Join THDC India Limited, a premier Mini-Ratna PSU under the Ministry of Power - Official Advt. No. 09/2025

Post Name: Mine Surveyor (S-2) and Mine Junior Overman (S-2)
Last Date: December 06, 2025
Total Posts: 05 Vacancies

THDC India Limited (THDCIL), formerly known as Tehri Hydro Development Corporation Limited, is a prominent Mini-Ratna Category-I Public Sector Undertaking (PSU). While historically renowned for its massive hydroelectric projects, most notably the Tehri Dam, the organization has diversified into various energy sectors, including solar, wind, and thermal power. As part of its strategic expansion into the coal mining sector to support its thermal power initiatives, THDCIL has announced the THDC Recruitment 2025 for technical mining personnel. Under Advertisement Number 09/2025, the corporation is seeking experienced professionals for the posts of Mine Surveyor and Mine Junior Overman in the S-2 Grade for its coal mining projects.

Mining operations require a high degree of technical precision and stringent adherence to safety protocols. A Mine Surveyor is responsible for the accurate mapping and measurement of the coal blocks, ensuring that the extraction follows the designated technical plans. On the other hand, the Junior Overman is a critical safety and supervisory link between the management and the workforce at the coal face, ensuring that production targets are met without compromising safety standards as per the Coal Mines Regulations (CMR). These roles are located in THDC's mining project sites, providing a challenging yet rewarding career path for individuals with the required Directorate General of Mines Safety (DGMS) certifications and hands-on mining experience.

For engineering diploma holders who possess the mandatory competency certificates, this recruitment represents a rare opportunity to join a PSU that offers central government-aligned pay scales and extensive social security benefits. The application process is scheduled to begin on November 7, 2025, and will conclude on December 6, 2025. Unlike general entry-level roles, these positions require a minimum of four years of post-qualification experience, emphasizing THDC's need for "ready-to-work" professionals who can contribute immediately to their mining ventures. This 2000-word guide covers every nuance of the notification, including pay matrix details, eligibility breakdown, examination syllabus, and expert tips for cracking the PSU selection process.

Important Highlights and Dates

Adherence to timelines is essential when applying for PSU jobs. THDCIL utilizes an online portal for its recruitment, and candidates must ensure they register within the specified dates. Any application received through other modes or after the deadline will be rejected. The age and experience calculation date is fixed to ensure a transparent merit process.

Event Description Dates and Details
Notification Number Advt. No. 09/2025
Online Application Start Date November 07, 2025 (10:00 AM)
Last Date for Submission December 06, 2025 (06:00 PM)
Eligibility Calculation Date July 01, 2025
Job Nature Permanent / Full-Time PSU Job
Selection Mode Computer Based Test (CBT)

Vacancy Details and Post Breakdown

The 2025 recruitment aims to fill a total of five vacancies. While the number may seem small, these are specialized supervisory roles in the S-2 Grade. The distribution is done among different categories to provide equal opportunities as per the reservation policies of the Government of India.

Post Designation Unreserved (UR) OBC (NCL) SC Total Vacancies
Mine Surveyor (S-2) 02 01 - 03
Mine Junior Overman (S-2) 01 - 01 02
Grand Total 03 01 01 05
Placement Note: Selected candidates will be primarily posted at THDC's Coal Mine project sites, such as the Amelia Coal Mine in Madhya Pradesh or any other project as per the organizational requirement. The roles involve field duty and shift-based work as is standard in the mining industry.

Salary Structure and Organizational Benefits

The S-2 Grade at THDCIL offers an excellent pay package that is far superior to private sector mining roles at the same level. In addition to a high basic pay, employees are entitled to several statutory and performance-based benefits.

  • Grade: S-2 Grade (Supervisory)
  • Pay Scale: ₹29,400 – 3% – ₹1,19,200
  • Initial Basic Pay: ₹29,400 per month
  • Estimated Monthly Gross Salary: Approximately ₹55,000 to ₹65,000 (including DA and other perks).

Allowances and Social Security:

  1. Dearness Allowance (DA): Paid at central PSU rates and revised quarterly.
  2. Cafeteria Approach: Employees can choose various perks like washing allowance, education allowance, and professional pursuit allowance up to a certain percentage of their basic pay.
  3. PRP (Performance Related Pay): Annual bonus based on the company's profitability and individual performance.
  4. Medical Benefits: Comprehensive medical coverage for self and family members (including parents).
  5. Retirement Benefits: Includes Provident Fund (PF), Gratuity, and the New Pension Scheme (NPS).

Eligibility Criteria

THDC is looking for candidates with a specific technical pedigree. Since mining is a highly regulated industry in India, possessing certificates of competency issued by the Directorate General of Mines Safety (DGMS) is an absolute mandatory requirement.

  • Mine Surveyor (S-2):
    • Education: Full-time Diploma in Mine Surveying, Mining Engineering, or Civil Engineering with at least 60% marks.
    • Certificate: Valid Mine Surveyor's Certificate of Competency (Coal) issued by DGMS.
    • Experience: Minimum 04 years of post-qualification experience in mine surveying, preferably in open-cast coal mines.
  • Mine Junior Overman (S-2):
    • Education: Full-time Diploma in Mining Engineering with at least 60% marks.
    • Certificate: Valid Overman Certificate of Competency (Coal) issued by DGMS, along with valid First Aid and Gas Testing certificates.
    • Experience: Minimum 04 years of post-qualification experience as an Overman in coal mining operations.
  • Age Limit: Maximum 30 years as of July 1, 2025. Standard relaxations apply (3 years for OBC, 5 years for SC/ST).

Selection Process and Examination Pattern

The selection process is meritocratic and relies on a Computer Based Test (CBT). There is no interview for these supervisory roles, ensuring that the final selection is determined by technical and general aptitude scores.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT): A two-hour online exam consisting of multiple-choice questions (MCQs).
  2. Document Verification (DV): Candidates shortlisted in the CBT will be called for verification of their original DGMS certificates and experience letters.
  3. Medical Examination: Since mining requires high physical fitness and specific vision standards, selected candidates must clear the medical exam as per THDC standards.

Qualifying Criteria: To be considered for the merit list, UR/OBC/EWS candidates must score at least 50% in the CBT, while SC/ST/PwBD candidates need at least 40%.

Detailed Exam Syllabus

The CBT is divided into two distinct parts. Mastering both sections is essential to secure a rank in the merit list. The exam is typically available in both Hindi and English.

Part 1: Technical Knowledge (80% Weightage): This section is specific to the discipline. For Mine Surveyors, it covers survey instruments (Total Station, Theodolite, GPS), leveling, contouring, and Coal Mines Regulations (CMR) 2017 related to survey duties. For Overmen, it focuses on mining methods, strata control, explosives and blasting, ventilation, and CMR 2017 safety chapters.

Part 2: General Aptitude (20% Weightage): This section tests your mental agility and includes:

  • General English (Grammar and Sentence correction).
  • Reasoning Ability (Puzzles and Logic).
  • General Awareness (Current Affairs and basic General Science).
  • Quantitative Aptitude (Mathematics up to 10th Standard).

Preparation Strategy for THDC Mining Exam

Cracking a PSU exam in the mining sector requires a deep dive into the Coal Mines Regulations (CMR) 2017. Most technical questions revolve around statutory requirements and safety norms.

Technical Mastery: Mine Surveyors should practice numerical problems related to traversing and volume calculation. Overmen should focus on the duties and responsibilities mentioned under the CMR. Since you already have 4 years of experience, relate your field knowledge to the theoretical concepts in your textbooks.

Non-Technical Balance: Don't ignore the 20% general aptitude section. In many PSU exams, candidates with excellent technical scores fail to make the cut because they neglect reasoning or current affairs. Dedicate at least one hour daily to solving aptitude problems from standard competitive books.

Previous Year Papers: While THDC specific mining papers might be rare, you can practice with papers from Coal India Limited (CIL) or NLC India for Overman and Surveyor roles, as the technical difficulty level is comparable.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right books will help you stay focused on the relevant syllabus. Below are the recommended titles for THDC mining aspirants.

Subject Area Recommended Book List
Mining Theory (Overman) "Elements of Mining Technology" by D.J. Deshmukh (Vol I, II, III).
Mining Regulations Official "Coal Mines Regulations (CMR) 2017" and the Mines Act 1952.
Mine Surveying "Surveying and Levelling" by B.C. Punmia or "Mining Surveying" by S. Ghatak.
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal and Lucent's General Knowledge.
Objective Mining "Objective Mining Engineering" by various authors like S. Ghatak.

Application Fee and Payment Process

The application fee is non-refundable and must be paid online. Candidates from reserved categories are provided exemptions to ensure social inclusivity.

  • General, OBC (NCL), and EWS Candidates: ₹600.
  • SC, ST, PwBD, and Ex-Servicemen: No Fee (Exempted).
  • Departmental Candidates: No Fee (Internal candidates of THDC).

Payment Mode: Fees can be paid using Debit Card, Credit Card, Net Banking, or UPI through the integrated payment gateway on the THDC portal. Ensure you save the transaction receipt as it may be required during the verification stage.

How to Apply Online

The registration process is entirely digital. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure your application is successful.

  1. Visit the official THDC website: www.thdc.co.in.
  2. Go to the "Career" section and click on "New Openings."
  3. Locate Advertisement No. 09/2025 and click on the "Apply Online" link.
  4. Register yourself using a valid Email ID and Mobile Number.
  5. Login with your credentials and fill in your academic scores and 4-year experience details.
  6. Upload Scanned Images: Recent passport photo (white background), signature, and mandatory DGMS competency certificates.
  7. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
  8. Preview your application form and click "Submit." Print the confirmation page for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Scanning documents correctly is critical. Ensure your DGMS certificate is clear, as it is the most vital document for your eligibility.

  1. Date of Birth Proof: 10th Standard Passing Certificate or Birth Certificate.
  2. Academic Proof: Final Diploma Certificate and all-semester marksheets.
  3. Competency Proof: Valid DGMS Certificate (Surveyor/Overman) for Coal.
  4. Experience Proof: Experience letters from previous employers clearly mentioning the dates of service.
  5. Caste Certificate: For SC/ST/OBC candidates (Central Government format).
  6. Identification: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID.

Important Links

Resource Name Official Link
Official THDC Website www.thdc.co.in
THDC Career Portal View All Jobs
Full Notification PDF Download Here

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can candidates with a degree in Civil Engineering apply for Mine Surveyor?

Yes, candidates with a Diploma or Degree in Civil Engineering can apply for the Mine Surveyor post, provided they possess the mandatory Mine Surveyor's Certificate of Competency (Coal) issued by the DGMS. Without this certificate, the engineering degree alone is not sufficient.

Q2: What is the "S-2" Grade in THDCIL?

S-2 is a supervisory grade in the PSU hierarchy. It is higher than the S-1 entry-level grade. It provides a better pay scale and higher responsibilities, which is why THDC requires at least four years of prior experience for this grade.

Q3: Is the 4-year experience mandatory?

Yes. The notification explicitly mentions that candidates must have a minimum of 04 years of post-qualification experience as on July 1, 2025. Experience gained before obtaining the diploma will not be counted.

Q4: What are the qualifying marks for the CBT?

For General, EWS, and OBC candidates, the qualifying mark is 50%. For SC, ST, and PwBD candidates, the qualifying mark is 40%. Scoring below this will disqualify you regardless of the vacancies available.

Q5: Can I apply for both posts?

Usually, the exam for both posts is conducted at the same time or in different shifts on the same day. However, since both roles require different DGMS certificates, a candidate can only apply if they have the specific certificate for that post.

Q6: What is the age relaxation for OBC candidates?

OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) candidates are eligible for a 3-year relaxation in the upper age limit. This means the maximum age for them is 33 years as of the cut-off date. This relaxation is only for the reserved seats or as per government directives.

entry.title: ICFRE Recruitment 2025: 47 Posts for Conservator & Deputy Conservator of Forests on Deputation Basis
entry.content:
ICFRE Recruitment 2025: 47 Posts for Conservator & Deputy Conservator of Forests on Deputation Basis

A rolling advertisement for experienced IFS and SFS officers to join India's premier forestry research organization.

Post Name: Conservator of Forests (CF), Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF)
Last Date: Rolling Advertisement (No specified last date)
Total Posts: 47

The Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE), an autonomous council under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change, Government of India, has issued a significant recruitment advertisement (F. No. 32-20/2025-ICFRE). This is a "Rolling Advertisement" through which ICFRE is inviting applications to fill the posts of Conservator of Forests (CF) and Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) on a deputation basis across its various institutes and centers.

This recruitment is specifically for experienced officers from the Indian Forest Service (IFS) and State Forest Service (SFS) who wish to take on a challenging and impactful role in the field of forestry research and education. ICFRE is a leading organization in forestry research in India, with institutes located in major cities such as Dehradun, Jabalpur, Bengaluru, Jodhpur, Coimbatore, Shimla, Ranchi, and Hyderabad.

A total of 47 vacancies are to be filled through this recruitment drive, including 27 posts for CF and 20 for DCF. As this is a rolling advertisement, the application process is continuous, and applications will be processed based on the availability of vacancies. Interested and eligible officers must send their applications through the proper channel to the Secretary, ICFRE, Dehradun.

ICFRE Deputation Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here's a brief overview of this special recruitment drive. Eligible officers should note the key details before proceeding with the application.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE)
Advertisement Number F. No. 32-20/2025-ICFRE
Post Names Conservator of Forests (CF) & Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF)
Total Vacancies 47
Mode of Recruitment Deputation Basis
Job Location All India (including Dehradun, Jabalpur, Bengaluru, Jodhpur, etc.)
Application Mode Offline (in prescribed proforma)
Application Last Date This is a rolling advertisement; no last date is specified.

Vacancy Details: Institute-wise Breakdown

ICFRE has announced a total of 47 posts across its various institutes and centers. The number of vacancies may change over time, and candidates are advised to check the latest vacancy status on the ICFRE website before applying.

Institute/Centre Name Posts for Conservator of Forests (CF) Posts for Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF)
ICFRE HQ, Dehradun 08 01
FRI, Dehradun 06 02
FRIDU, Dehradun 02 02
FRC-ER, Allahabad 01 02
HFRI, Shimla 01 02
IFP, Ranchi 01 02
IFGTB, Coimbatore - -
IWST, Bengaluru - -
IFB, Hyderabad 01 -
FRC-CE, Visakhapatnam - 01
RFRI, Jorhat 02 02
FRC-BR, Mizoram 01 01
FRC-LEC, Agartala - -
TFRI, Jabalpur 01 03
FRC-SD, Chhindwara 01 02
AFRI, Jodhpur 02 -
Total 27 20

Application Fee Details

  • All Candidates (IFS/SFS Officers): ₹ 500/-

The non-refundable application fee must be paid via a Demand Draft (DD) drawn in favor of the "Accounts Officer, ICFRE" and payable at Dehradun.

Eligibility Criteria and Pay Scale

Selected officers will receive a salary according to the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC). The eligibility criteria vary by post.

Post Name Pay Level Eligibility Criteria
Conservator of Forests (CF) Level 13 (₹1,23,100 - ₹2,15,900)
and
Level 13A (₹1,31,100 - ₹2,16,600)
  • Only officers of the Indian Forest Service (IFS).
  • Must be holding a post in Pay Level 13 or 13A.
  • Officers in Level 14 (CCF) can also apply, and their pay will be protected upon selection.
Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) Level 10 (₹56,100 - ₹1,77,500)
Level 11 (₹67,700 - ₹2,08,700)
Level 12 (₹78,800 - ₹2,09,200)
Level 13 (₹1,23,100 - ₹2,15,900)
  • Officers of the Indian Forest Service (IFS) or State Forest Service (SFS).
  • Must be holding a post in Pay Level 10, 11, 12, or 13.
Note on Tenure: The period of deputation will be governed by the prevailing rules of the Government of India. Pay and allowances will be regulated accordingly upon appointment.

Selection Process

As this is a recruitment on a deputation basis, it does not involve a written examination or a standard interview process. The selection will be conducted as follows:

  1. Application Processing: Received applications will be processed based on the requirements of ICFRE and the availability of vacancies.
  2. Selection Committee Evaluation: A selection committee constituted by ICFRE will evaluate the applications, service records, Confidential Reports (CRs), and Vigilance Clearance of the candidates.
  3. Final Decision: Based on the recommendations of the committee, the final decision on selection will be made by the competent authority.

ICFRE reserves the right not to fill any or all of the advertised vacancies or to increase/decrease the number of vacancies without assigning any reason.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

There is no written examination for this recruitment. The selection is based on the evaluation of the service records of the applying officers. Therefore, no exam pattern or syllabus is applicable.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

No Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) is required for this deputation-based recruitment.

How to Prepare for the Selection Process

Preparation for this selection involves ensuring your application and service records are impeccable. Key steps include:

  1. Ensure your application proforma is filled accurately and completely.
  2. Coordinate with your parent department to ensure timely forwarding of your application along with complete Confidential Reports (CRs) for the last five years.
  3. Ensure that the Vigilance Clearance certificate is in the prescribed 13-point CVC proforma and is duly signed by the competent authority.

Best Books for Preparation

No specific books are required for preparation, as the selection is not based on a test but on past performance and service records.

Not applicable for this recruitment process.

How to Apply for ICFRE Recruitment

This is an offline application process. Interested and eligible IFS/SFS officers must follow the steps below:

  1. Download the Application Proforma: Download the application form from the official ICFRE website, www.icfre.org, or from the link provided in the official notification.
  2. Fill the Application Form: Fill in all the required details (name, cadre, current post, experience, etc.) in block letters.
  3. Prepare Required Documents: Attach all necessary documents as listed in the next section with the application form.
  4. Send Through Proper Channel: The filled application form and all documents must be forwarded through your current parent department/ministry.
  5. Dispatch the Application: The complete application package should be sent to the following address:

    The Secretary,
    Indian Council of Forestry Research and Education (ICFRE),
    P.O. New Forest, Dehradun - 248006, Uttarakhand

Documents to be Sent with the Application

It is mandatory to enclose the following documents with the application form:

  1. A completely filled and duly signed application proforma.
  2. Attested copies of Confidential Reports (CRs) for the last five years.
  3. Vigilance Clearance in the 13-point CVC proforma issued by the State/UT Government.
  4. A non-refundable fee of ₹ 500/- in the form of a Demand Draft drawn in favor of the "Accounts Officer, ICFRE," payable at Dehradun.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Official Notification & Application Proforma Click Here
Official Website http://www.icfre.org

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Who can apply for this recruitment?

Only currently serving officers of the Indian Forest Service (IFS) and State Forest Service (SFS) who are working in the pay levels specified in the advertisement can apply for deputation. This is not a direct recruitment for the general public.

Question 2: What is the last date to apply?

This is a "rolling advertisement," which means there is no fixed last date. Applications can be accepted and processed throughout the year based on the availability of vacancies.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection is based on an evaluation of the candidate's service records, Confidential Reports, and Vigilance Clearance by a selection committee. There is no written test or interview.

Question 4: How should the application be sent?

The application must be filled in the prescribed proforma and sent with all necessary documents through the proper channel (via your parent department) to the Secretary, ICFRE, Dehradun.

entry.title: UP पुलिस दरोगा भर्ती 2025: 4543 पदों पर बड़ी भर्ती, जानिए पात्रता, परीक्षा तिथि और पूरी जानकारी!
entry.content:
UP पुलिस दरोगा भर्ती 2025: 4543 पदों पर बड़ी भर्ती, जानिए पात्रता, परीक्षा तिथि और पूरी जानकारी!

उत्तर प्रदेश पुलिस भर्ती एवं प्रोन्नति बोर्ड (UPPRPB) ने सब-इंस्पेक्टर (दरोगा) के 4543 पदों पर भर्ती का नोटिफिकेशन जारी कर दिया है। ऑनलाइन आवेदन 12 अगस्त 2025 से शुरू होंगे।

उत्तर प्रदेश पुलिस में नौकरी का सपना देखने वाले युवाओं के लिए बड़ी खुशखबरी! UP पुलिस सब-इंस्पेक्टर भर्ती 2025 के लिए अधिसूचना जारी कर दी गई है। इस भर्ती के तहत सब-इंस्पेक्टर सिविल पुलिस (पुरुष/महिला), प्लाटून कमांडर PAC (पुरुष) और विशेष सुरक्षा बल (पुरुष) के कुल 4543 पदों पर भर्ती की जाएगी।

यह अवसर सभी ग्रेजुएट उम्मीदवारों के लिए है जो पुलिस विभाग में एक सम्मानजनक करियर बनाना चाहते हैं। नीचे पूरी जानकारी दी गई है — पात्रता, आयु सीमा, चयन प्रक्रिया, परीक्षा पैटर्न और आवेदन प्रक्रिया।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन शुरू12 August 2025
आवेदन और शुल्क जमा करने की अंतिम तिथि11 September 2025
शुल्क समायोजन की अंतिम तिथि13 September 2025
परीक्षा तिथिजल्द घोषित की जाएगी

कुल पदों का विवरण

पद का नामकुल पदUREWSOBCSCST
SI Civil Police (Male/Female)42421705422114389082
Platoon Commander PAC (Male)135561336282
SI Special Security Force (Male)6025616121
Mahila Battalion SI (Female)106471027211

आवेदन शुल्क

  • सामान्य / OBC / EWS: ₹500
  • SC / ST: ₹400

शुल्क केवल ऑनलाइन माध्यम से ही जमा किया जा सकता है।

पात्रता मानदंड

  • राष्ट्रीयता: आवेदक भारत का नागरिक होना चाहिए।
  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता: किसी मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से ग्रेजुएशन पास उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं। Appearing उम्मीदवार पात्र नहीं हैं।
  • आयु सीमा: 01 जुलाई 2025 को न्यूनतम आयु 21 वर्ष और अधिकतम 28 वर्ष होनी चाहिए। आरक्षित वर्गों को नियमानुसार छूट मिलेगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. OTR पंजीकरण
  2. लिखित परीक्षा (400 अंक)
  3. दस्तावेज़ सत्यापन और शारीरिक मानक परीक्षण (PST)
  4. शारीरिक दक्षता परीक्षा (PET)

परीक्षा पैटर्न

विषयप्रश्नअंक
सामान्य हिंदी40100
संविधान / सामान्य ज्ञान40100
सांख्यिकीय और मानसिक योग्यता40100
तार्किक / बुद्धि क्षमता परीक्षा40100
कुल160400
उत्तीर्णांक: प्रत्येक विषय में न्यूनतम 35% और कुल मिलाकर 50% अंक आवश्यक हैं।

शारीरिक मानक

पुरुष:

  • ऊंचाई – सामान्य/OBC/SC: 168 सेमी, ST: 160 सेमी
  • छाती – सामान्य/OBC/SC: 79–84 सेमी, ST: 77–82 सेमी
  • दौड़ – 4.8 कि.मी. 28 मिनट में

महिला:

  • ऊंचाई – सामान्य/OBC/SC: 152 सेमी, ST: 147 सेमी
  • वजन – न्यूनतम 40 किग्रा
  • दौड़ – 2.4 कि.मी. 16 मिनट में

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://uppbpb.gov.in/ पर जाएँ।
  2. OTR (One Time Registration) पूरा करें।
  3. लॉगिन कर “उपनिरीक्षक भर्ती 2025” लिंक खोलें।
  4. फॉर्म भरें, आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें।
  5. शुल्क जमा करें और आवेदन सबमिट करें।
  6. फॉर्म का प्रिंट निकालकर सुरक्षित रखें।

वेतनमान

चयनित उम्मीदवारों को पे-स्केल ₹9300–₹34800 ग्रेड पे ₹4200 के अंतर्गत वेतन मिलेगा। कुल इन-हैंड सैलरी लगभग ₹45,000 – ₹55,000 प्रतिमाह होगी।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकक्रिया
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
नोटिफिकेशन डाउनलोड करेंयहाँ से डाउनलोड करें

entry.title: BBAU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2025: Notification for 42+ Group A, B, and C Vacancies
entry.content:
BBAU Non-Teaching Recruitment 2025: Notification for 42+ Group A, B, and C Vacancies

Join Babasaheb Bhimrao Ambedkar University, Lucknow - A Central University of Excellence - Apply Online for LDC, Assistant Registrar, and Support Staff

Post Name: Assistant Registrar, Private Secretary, LDC, Nurse, Driver, Lab Attendant, Technical Assistant
Last Date: December 14, 2025
Total Posts: 42+ Vacancies (Advt BBAU/NT/01/2025)

The Babasaheb Bhimrao Ambedkar University (BBAU), Lucknow, is one of the premier Central Universities in India, established by an Act of Parliament in 1994. Named after the architect of the Indian Constitution, the university is committed to social justice, inclusion, and academic excellence. To further strengthen its administrative and technical wings at both its main campus in Lucknow and its satellite center in Amethi, the university has officially announced a major recruitment drive for the year 2025. Under Advertisement Number BBAU/NT/01/2025, the institute is looking to recruit dedicated and qualified Indian citizens for over 42 non-teaching positions. These roles are critical for maintaining the high standards of university administration, student support, and laboratory infrastructure.

Working at a Central University like BBAU is highly sought after because it offers the stability of a permanent central government career. The positions advertised cover a vast spectrum of professional backgrounds, from high-level management roles like Assistant Registrar and Internal Audit Officer to essential technical roles like Technical Assistant, Junior Engineer, and Nurse, and entry-level support roles such as Lower Division Clerk (LDC) and Lab Attendant. This recruitment drive follows the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) levels, ensuring that selected candidates receive competitive salaries along with an array of central government benefits. The application process is entirely online, starting on November 14, 2025, and will remain active until December 14, 2025. Successful candidates will find themselves in a vibrant academic environment in the historic city of Lucknow or the developing educational hub of Amethi.

The 2025 recruitment notification emphasizes merit and transparency. The selection process is designed to be rigorous, utilizing a multi-tiered evaluation system that includes a preliminary objective test, a descriptive domain-specific test, and skill tests for technical cadres. For senior Group A positions, a personal interview is also part of the final merit assessment. This exhaustive 2000-word guide serves as your definitive manual, providing an in-depth analysis of category-wise vacancies, discipline-specific eligibility criteria, the detailed examination syllabus, and expert-recommended strategies to help you navigate this competitive process and secure your place at BBAU.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must strictly adhere to the timeline provided by the university. Since BBAU is a Central University, the application portal manages a high volume of data. It is recommended to register early to avoid technical bottlenecks. Candidates applying for multiple posts must ensure they check the feasibility of attending multiple exams if scheduled on the same day.

Recruitment Feature Key Dates and Details
Official Notification Number BBAU/NT/01/2025
Online Registration Opening November 14, 2025
Last Date for Online Submission December 14, 2025 (11:59 PM)
Fee Payment Deadline December 14, 2025
Age & Eligibility Cut-off Date December 14, 2025
Job Nature Permanent / Central Govt Status
Job Location Lucknow & Amethi (Uttar Pradesh)

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Salary Matrix

The vacancies at BBAU are categorized into Group A, B, and C based on the level of responsibility and the pay matrix. The salary is structured as per the 7th Central Pay Commission, ensuring that employees receive a high basic pay along with central allowances like DA, HRA, and TA.

Lucknow Campus Vacancies

Post Designation Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC) Max Age Limit
Assistant Registrar 01 (UR) Level 10 40 Years
Assistant Librarian 01 (UR) Academic Level 10 40 Years
Security Officer 01 (UR) Level 7 40 Years
Private Secretary 02 (UR) Level 7 35 Years
Nurse 01 (UR) Level 6 35 Years
Technical Assistant 01 (UR) Level 5 32 Years
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) 08 (UR, OBC, ST, EWS) Level 2 30 Years
Laboratory Attendant 03 (UR, EWS) Level 1 30 Years

Amethi Satellite Center Vacancies

Post Designation Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC) Max Age Limit
Assistant Registrar 01 (UR) Level 10 40 Years
Assistant (Admin) 01 (SC) Level 6 35 Years
Estimated Gross Salary: For Level 10 posts (Assistant Registrar), the gross monthly salary is approximately ₹95,000 - ₹1,05,000. For Level 2 posts (LDC), the gross monthly salary is approximately ₹32,000 - ₹38,000 including all central allowances. Employees are also eligible for medical benefits, LTC, and pension benefits under the New Pension Scheme (NPS).

Application Fee Details

  • General (UR), OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and EWS Candidates: ₹1,000/- per post.
  • SC, ST, and Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) Candidates: ₹500/- per post.
  • Female Candidates (All Categories): ₹500/- per post.

The application fee must be paid through the online payment gateway integrated within the recruitment portal. Fees once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances, so candidates are advised to verify their eligibility before making the payment.

Eligibility Criteria and Educational Qualifications

BBAU maintains stringent academic and professional requirements for its non-teaching staff. Each post requires a specific background, and in some cases, mandatory technical skills like typing or shorthand. Below is the detailed breakdown for the major positions.

Post Designation Educational Qualification & Requirements
Assistant Registrar Master’s Degree with at least 55% marks or an equivalent grade. Desirable: 5 years of supervisory experience in administration or finance in a university setup.
Lower Division Clerk (LDC) Bachelor’s Degree from a recognized university. Mandatory Skills: English Typing speed of 35 WPM or Hindi Typing speed of 30 WPM on a computer.
Private Secretary Bachelor’s Degree. Experience: 3 years as a Personal Assistant or 5 years as a Stenographer. Skills: Shorthand @ 120 WPM (English) or 100 WPM (Hindi).
Nurse B.Sc. Nursing from a recognized university or institute. Must be registered with the Indian/State Nursing Council. Experience: 2 years in a reputed hospital.
Driver 10th Standard pass from a recognized board. Licensing: Valid commercial license for LMV/HMV. Experience: 3 years of motor vehicle driving.
Lab Attendant 10+2 with Science stream from a recognized board. Basic knowledge of laboratory safety and maintenance is preferred.

Age Relaxation: Upper age relaxation is provided to various categories: SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), and PwBD (10-15 years). Crucially, there is no upper age limit for internal/departmental candidates of BBAU applying for these permanent posts through direct recruitment.

Selection Process and Evaluation Roadmap

The selection process at BBAU is designed to ensure a fair and merit-based recruitment. The process follows a tiered structure that varies according to the pay level of the post.

  1. Screening of Applications: BBAU will first screen the data provided in the online forms. If the number of applicants is high, higher academic cut-offs may be applied to shortlist candidates for the written tests.
  2. Tier-1 Written Test (Objective): This is a screening test for Group B and C posts. It evaluates general aptitude, language proficiency, and basic reasoning skills.
  3. Tier-2 Descriptive Test (Domain Specific): For posts Level 4 and above, this test evaluates deep knowledge of university administration, finance, or technical subjects related to the post.
  4. Skill Test / Trade Test: Mandatory for posts like LDC (Typing), Private Secretary (Shorthand), and Driver (Driving test). This stage is purely qualifying in nature.
  5. Personal Interview: Conducted only for Group A posts (Assistant Registrar, Assistant Librarian, Security Officer). The interview carries a 20% weightage in the final merit.

Final Merit: For most Group B and C posts, the final merit is prepared based on the marks obtained in the written examinations (Tier-1 and Tier-2). No interview is conducted for these grades in accordance with Central Government policy.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Understanding the syllabus is key to cracking the BBAU exam. The written test is usually divided into two papers for most technical and administrative roles.

Paper I: General Aptitude (Common for most posts):

  • General English / Hindi: Grammar, comprehension, and vocabulary (30 Marks).
  • Numerical Ability & Reasoning: Profit and loss, time and work, series, and logical puzzles (40 Marks).
  • General Awareness: Current affairs, basics of the Indian Constitution, and History of Lucknow/UP (30 Marks).
  • Basic Computer Literacy: Fundamentals of MS Office, Email, and Internet operations (20 Marks).

Paper II: Domain Knowledge (Post Specific): This section focuses on the specific requirements of the post. For Assistant Registrars, it will cover the Central Universities Act, FRSR rules, and GFR 2017. For LDCs, it will focus on office management, drafting, and noting. For Nurses and Lab Technicians, it will cover their respective professional subjects.

Expert Preparation Strategy for BBAU Recruitment

Securing a place in a Central University requires a blend of general aptitude and institutional knowledge. Candidates often focus too much on the subject and ignore the general awareness section.

Master the University Rules: For administrative roles like Assistant Registrar or Private Secretary, questions on "Central University Administration" are frequent. Read the BBAU Act and Statutes, as well as the New Education Policy (NEP) 2020, which is a favorite topic in Central University interviews and exams.

Focus on Speed for Skill Tests: For LDCs and Private Secretaries, the skill test is a major hurdle. Don't wait for the written results to start practicing. Use typing software daily to reach a consistent speed of 40 WPM to comfortably clear the 35 WPM requirement under exam stress. For Private Secretaries, practice dictation from Parliament speeches or legal texts.

General Awareness Balance: Since BBAU is located in Lucknow, stay updated on Uttar Pradesh current affairs alongside national news. The "Computer Literacy" section is scoring; ensuring you know keyboard shortcuts and MS Excel formulas can provide the extra marks needed for a high merit rank.

Best Books for Preparation

Using the right study material for a Central University exam is crucial. Below are the top recommendations from previous successful candidates of BBAU and other Central Universities like DU and AMU.

Subject / Discipline Recommended Book List
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations by R.S. Aggarwal.
Reasoning Ability A Modern Approach to Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
General English Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi (Arihant Publications).
University Admin Knowledge Swamy’s Handbook for Central Government Staff (FRSR and Leave Rules).
General Knowledge Lucent’s General Knowledge and monthly Pratiyogita Darpan.
Computer Awareness Objective Computer Awareness by Arihant Publications.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step Guide

The application process for BBAU is entirely digital. Candidates must follow these steps carefully to ensure their application is valid and error-free.

  1. Visit the official university website: www.bbau.ac.in.
  2. On the homepage, navigate to the "Recruitment" or "Career" tab and find Advertisement No. BBAU/NT/01/2025.
  3. Click on the "Apply Online" link. You will be redirected to the recruitment portal (often hosted by Samarth e-Gov).
  4. New Registration: Register with a valid Email ID and Mobile Number. Verify your email using the link sent to your inbox.
  5. Fill the Application: Login with your credentials and fill in all personal, educational, and experience details. Ensure the spelling of your name matches your 10th-standard certificate.
  6. Document Upload: Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and all academic/category certificates in the prescribed format (PDF/JPEG).
  7. Fee Payment: Pay the application fee through the online payment gateway using Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card, or UPI.
  8. Final Submission: Preview your application one last time. Click "Submit" and download the final PDF form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keeping these documents ready in digital format before starting the application will save time and prevent session timeouts on the portal. All documents should be clear and legible.

  1. Identity and Age Proof: Aadhar Card and 10th Standard Passing Certificate/Admit Card.
  2. Academic Proof: Marksheets and Degree certificates from Graduation to Post-Graduation (as applicable).
  3. Professional Registration: Nursing Council certificate (for Nurse) or Driving License (for Driver).
  4. Experience Certificates: Official letters from current or previous employers specifying dates and nature of duties.
  5. Category Certificate: SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificate issued by the competent authority in the Central Government format.
  6. PwBD Certificate: For candidates seeking disability-based reservation (minimum 40% disability).
  7. NOC (No Objection Certificate): Mandatory for candidates currently working in other Government, PSU, or Autonomous bodies.

Important Links and Address

Resource Description Direct Official Link
BBAU Official Website www.bbau.ac.in
Online Application Portal Apply Now
Detailed Notification PDF Download Notification

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply for more than one post in BBAU?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you are eligible for each. However, you must fill out a separate application form and pay the separate application fee for each post category. Be aware that exams for different posts may be conducted on the same day.

Q2: Is CTET mandatory for any post in this notification?

No. This notification (BBAU/NT/01/2025) is for Non-Teaching posts. CTET is only mandatory for teaching roles (PGT, TGT, PRT) which are usually released under separate notifications.

Q3: What is the fee structure for female candidates?

All female candidates, regardless of their social category (UR/OBC/SC/ST/EWS), are eligible for a concessional application fee of ₹500/- per post.

Q4: Are candidates from states other than Uttar Pradesh eligible?

Yes. BBAU is a Central University, and Indian citizens from all states are eligible to apply. Reservations will be applicable as per the Central Government rules (OBC-NCL, SC, ST, EWS).

Q5: Is there an interview for the Lower Division Clerk (LDC) post?

No. For Group C posts like LDC, Driver, and Attendant, the selection is based entirely on the Written Test merit and qualifying the Skill Test. There is no personal interview for these grades.

Q6: What is the last date to apply for BBAU 2025 recruitment?

The final date for submitting the online application along with the fee is December 14, 2025, until 11:59:59 PM. It is advisable to apply a few days earlier to avoid potential server issues.

entry.title: NISER भर्ती 2025: वैज्ञानिक सहायक और जूनियर हिन्दी अनुवादक के पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
NISER भर्ती 2025: वैज्ञानिक सहायक और जूनियर हिन्दी अनुवादक के पदों पर भर्ती!

National Institute of Science Education and Research, भुवनेश्वर में नियमित आधार पर सीधी भर्ती का अवसर।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! National Institute of Science Education and Research (NISER), भुवनेश्वर ने एक नई भर्ती अधिसूचना (Advt. No.: NISER/RC/2025/NA/01) जारी की है। यह संस्थान Department of Atomic Energy (DAE), भारत सरकार के अधीन एक प्रतिष्ठित केंद्र है। इस भर्ती के अंतर्गत Scientific Assistant-B और Junior Hindi Translator के पदों के लिए योग्य भारतीय नागरिकों से ऑनलाइन आवेदन आमंत्रित किए गए हैं।

सभी पद नियमित (regular) आधार पर भरे जाएंगे। नीचे आपको इस भर्ती से जुड़ी सभी प्रमुख जानकारी जैसे पद, पात्रता, आयु सीमा, चयन प्रक्रिया और आवेदन प्रक्रिया विस्तार से दी गई है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
अधिसूचना जारी होने की तिथि14 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि17 दिसम्बर 2025

पद एवं वेतन

कुल 04 पदों पर भर्ती की जाएगी। विस्तृत विवरण नीचे दिया गया है:

पोस्ट कोडपद का नामश्रेणीवेतन स्तर
01Scientific Assistant-B (Medical Laboratory Technician)PwBD - 01Level-6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400)
02Scientific Assistant-B (Clinical Psychologist)ST - 01Level-6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400)
03Junior Hindi TranslatorUR - 01Level-6 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400)
नोट: PwBD का अर्थ है “Person with Benchmark Disability” यानी कम से कम 40% निर्धारित विकलांगता वाले उम्मीदवार।

आवेदन शुल्क

शुल्क: उम्मीदवारों को ₹500/- का आवेदन शुल्क ऑनलाइन जमा करना होगा।

शुल्क में छूट: SC, ST, PwBD, महिला एवं पूर्व सैनिक (Ex-Servicemen) उम्मीदवारों को आवेदन शुल्क से पूर्ण छूट दी गई है।

पद अनुसार पात्रता

पोस्ट कोड 01: Scientific Assistant-B (Medical Laboratory Technician)

  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता: B.Sc. में न्यूनतम 50% अंक + Medical Lab Technology (DMLT/MLT) में 60% अंकों के साथ 1 वर्ष का डिप्लोमा। या B.Sc. in Medical Lab Tech. में न्यूनतम 50% अंक।
  • वांछनीय: किसी शोध प्रयोगशाला में 2 वर्ष का अनुभव।
  • आयु सीमा: 17.12.2025 तक — Gen-PwBD: अधिकतम 40 वर्ष, OBC-PwBD: 43 वर्ष, SC/ST-PwBD: 45 वर्ष।

पोस्ट कोड 02: Scientific Assistant-B (Clinical Psychologist)

  • अनिवार्य योग्यता: Clinical Psychology में परास्नातक (Post Graduate) डिग्री में न्यूनतम 50% अंक।
  • आयु सीमा: ST उम्मीदवारों के लिए अधिकतम 35 वर्ष (18.12.1990 या उसके बाद जन्म)।

पोस्ट कोड 03: Junior Hindi Translator

  • अनिवार्य योग्यता: हिन्दी/अंग्रेज़ी में स्नातकोत्तर (Master’s) डिग्री, साथ ही डिग्री स्तर पर अंग्रेज़ी/हिन्दी एक मुख्य विषय के रूप में। (अन्य विकल्पों हेतु आधिकारिक अधिसूचना देखें)।
  • आयु सीमा: UR श्रेणी के लिए अधिकतम 28 वर्ष (18.12.1997 या उसके बाद जन्म)।

चयन प्रक्रिया

वैज्ञानिक सहायक और हिन्दी अनुवादक दोनों पदों के लिए चयन प्रक्रिया अलग-अलग होगी।

Scientific Assistant-B (Post Code 01 और 02):

  • चरण-I: Trade Test (Qualifying)
  • चरण-II: Written Test (60% Weightage)
  • चरण-III: Personal Interview (40% Weightage)

चरण I और II के लिए न्यूनतम योग्यता अंक 30% निर्धारित हैं।

Junior Hindi Translator (Post Code 03):

  • चरण-I: Objective Type Written Test (50% Weightage)
  • चरण-II: Descriptive Test (Translation + Essay) (50% Weightage)

दोनों चरणों में न्यूनतम 30% अंक आवश्यक हैं।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. उम्मीदवार केवल NISER की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट (https://www.niser.ac.in) के माध्यम से ऑनलाइन आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
  2. आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 17 दिसम्बर 2025 है।
  3. आवेदन करने से पहले उम्मीदवार अपने फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र, जाति प्रमाणपत्र, अनुभव प्रमाणपत्र आदि की स्कैन कॉपी तैयार रखें।
  4. शुल्क भुगतान ऑनलाइन माध्यम से करना होगा। जो उम्मीदवार शुल्क से मुक्त नहीं हैं, उन्हें ₹500/- जमा करना होगा।
  5. फॉर्म जमा करने से पहले सभी विवरणों की सावधानीपूर्वक जांच करें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें
अधिसूचना PDFडाउनलोड करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या यह नौकरी स्थायी है?
हाँ, यह भर्ती नियमित (Regular) आधार पर सीधी नियुक्ति के लिए है।
प्रश्न 2: महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए आवेदन शुल्क कितना है?
महिला उम्मीदवारों को आवेदन शुल्क से पूर्ण छूट दी गई है।
प्रश्न 3: क्या ऑफलाइन आवेदन स्वीकार होगा?
नहीं, केवल ऑनलाइन आवेदन ही स्वीकार किए जाएंगे।
प्रश्न 4: Scientific Assistant पद के लिए चयन कैसे होगा?
तीन चरणों — Trade Test, Written Test, और Interview — के माध्यम से चयन होगा। अंतिम मेरिट सूची लिखित परीक्षा और साक्षात्कार के अंकों के आधार पर बनेगी।
UNICCM SCHOOL https://www.uniccm.com/

entry.title: Uttar Pradesh Anganwadi Vacancy 2026: 1352 Vacancies Open for Females
entry.content:
Uttar Pradesh Anganwadi Vacancy 2026: 1352 Vacancies Open for Females

A massive recruitment drive for Anganwadi Helpers in Ghazipur district, Uttar Pradesh. Apply online before January 08, 2026.

Post Name: Anganwadi Sahayika (Anganwadi Helper)
Last Date: 08 January 2026
Total Posts: 1352

The office of the District Program Officer (ICDS), Ghazipur, has released a significant recruitment notification for the engagement of Anganwadi Sahayika (Anganwadi Helpers) on a contractual, honorarium basis. This recruitment drive is being conducted as per the directives of the Government of Uttar Pradesh, Child Development Services and Nutrition Department. A total of 1352 vacancies open for females have been announced, presenting a golden opportunity for local candidates who wish to contribute to child development and community welfare at the grassroots level.

This direct recruitment process is a crucial step in strengthening the Integrated Child Development Services (ICDS) scheme in the district. The role of an Anganwadi Sahayika is pivotal in assisting the Anganwadi Worker in the day-to-day functioning of the Anganwadi Centre, including supplementary nutrition distribution, health check-ups, and pre-school education activities. The selection will be purely merit-based, calculated on the academic scores of the candidates, with no written examination or interview. The application process is entirely online through the departmental website http://upanganwadibharti.in. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all essential details of this recruitment.

Important Points

This recruitment is for a contractual, honorarium-based position within the government's ICDS scheme in Ghazipur, Uttar Pradesh. It is open only to local female residents.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization District Program Officer (ICDS), Ghazipur (under Govt. of U.P.)
Job Type Contractual (Honorarium-based) Government Scheme
Job Location Respective Gram Sabha/Ward in Ghazipur District, Uttar Pradesh
Last Date for Online Application 08 January 2026 (up to 12:00 Midnight)

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 1352 vacancies for the post of Anganwadi Sahayika across 17 different child development projects in Ghazipur district. The project-wise breakdown of vacancies is as follows:

Project Name Total Vacancies
Barachawar 68
Shahar (City) 16
Reotipur 97
Jamania 42
Bhanwarkol 63
Kasimabad 144
Manihari 125
Deokali 40
Karanda 68
Saidpur 41
Jakhanian 149
Virno 108
Mardah 95
Sadar 52
Sadat 70
Mohammadabad 126
Varachakwar 48
Total Vacancies 1352
Note: The detailed ward-wise/gram sabha-wise vacancy list, along with the reservation details for each center, is available on the departmental website http://upanganwadibharti.in and has been displayed on the notice boards of the Collectorate, Vikas Bhawan, Tehsil, and Block offices in Ghazipur.

Application Fee

  • There is no application fee for any category of candidates.

The application process is completely free of cost for all eligible applicants.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet all the eligibility criteria regarding residency, education, and age to be considered for the post.

Criteria Requirement Details
Gender Only Female candidates are eligible to apply.
Residency The applicant must be a permanent resident of the Gram Sabha/Ward for which the vacancy exists. Applications from residents of other districts or states will be rejected. A digitally signed residence certificate issued by the Tehsil is mandatory.
Educational Qualification The minimum educational qualification for Anganwadi Sahayika is Intermediate (12th Class) or its equivalent. The final merit list will be prepared based on marks obtained from Intermediate up to Post Graduation.
Age Limit (as on 01.07.2025)
  • Minimum Age: 18 years
  • Maximum Age: 35 years
  • The date of birth as mentioned in the High School (10th Class) marksheet/certificate will be considered valid.

Selection Process

The selection for the post of Anganwadi Sahayika is done through a direct recruitment process based on merit. There is no written examination or interview.

  1. Merit List Preparation: The selection will be based purely on a merit list prepared from the academic scores of the candidates. The marks will be calculated as follows:
    • The percentage of marks obtained in 10th, 12th, and Graduation will be divided by 10. For example, if a candidate has 60% in 10th, their score will be 6.
    • The final merit score will be the sum of scores from 10th, 12th, Graduation, and Post Graduation (if applicable).
    • No weightage will be given for qualifications higher than Post Graduation.
  2. Preference Order (Order of Priority): In case of multiple eligible candidates from the same Gram Sabha/Ward, selection will be made based on a specific order of preference as defined by the government. The preference is given first to candidates from Below Poverty Line (BPL) families, and then to candidates from Above Poverty Line (APL) families, in the following order:
    1. Widow candidate.
    2. Legally divorced/destitute woman.
    3. Other women from the family.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates shortlisted on the basis of the merit list will be called for document verification. Final selection is subject to the successful verification of all original documents.

How to Prepare

Since this is not an exam-based recruitment, "preparation" involves ensuring your application is accurate and all your documents are in order.

  1. Gather All Documents: Before applying, collect all your documents in one place. This includes marksheets (10th, 12th, Graduation, Post Graduation), caste certificate, residence certificate, income certificate, and any other special category certificate (like widow/divorcee certificate).
  2. Ensure Digital Certificates: The notification clearly states that only digitally signed online certificates for residence, caste, and income issued from the Tehsil will be valid. Ensure your certificates are verifiable online.
  3. Accurate Information: While filling the online form, be extremely careful. Enter your marks (obtained and total) for each qualification correctly. Any error can lead to the cancellation of your application.
