  4. Understand the Preference Order: Read the preference criteria carefully. If you belong to a priority category (e.g., a widow from a BPL family), make sure you have the necessary documents to prove it (Death certificate of husband, BPL list proof, etc.).
  5. Scan and Upload Correctly: All documents must be self-attested, clear, and legible. Upload them in the correct format and size as specified on the portal.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online through the departmental website. Offline applications will not be accepted.

  1. Visit the official departmental website: http://upanganwadibharti.in.
  2. Read the detailed Government Order (Shasanadesh) and instructions available on the website carefully before applying.
  3. Find the link for the online application for Ghazipur district and click on it.
  4. Fill out the application form with all required details like personal information, address, category, and academic qualifications. Be very careful while entering the marks obtained and total marks for each exam.
  5. Upload self-attested, clear, and legible scanned copies of all the required documents.
  6. Review the entire application form carefully before final submission. Any error or false information will lead to the cancellation of the candidature.
  7. Submit the application form. No fee is required. Keep a printout of the final submitted application for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

The following self-attested documents must be scanned and uploaded with the online application. The documents should be clean and readable.

  1. Recent Passport-size Photograph and Signature.
  2. Residence Certificate (Digitally signed by Tehsil, mentioning the ward number for urban areas).
  3. Caste Certificate (Digitally signed by Tehsil).
  4. Income Certificate (Digitally signed by Tehsil, for BPL category).
  5. Marksheets and Certificates for 10th, 12th, Graduation, and Post Graduation.
  6. Widow Certificate (Death certificate of husband and certified copy of family register), if applicable.
  7. Legal order of divorce/destitution from a competent court, if applicable.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Click Here

FAQs

Question 1: Who is eligible to apply for this post?

Only female candidates who are permanent residents of the specific Gram Sabha/Ward where the vacancy exists and have passed at least the 12th standard are eligible.

Question 2: Is there any written exam or interview?

No, the selection is purely based on a merit list calculated from the marks obtained in academic qualifications (from 12th to Post Graduation).

Question 3: What is the last date to apply?

The last date to submit the online application is January 08, 2026, until midnight.

Question 4: Can I submit the application offline?

No, only online applications submitted through the official portal http://upanganwadibharti.in will be accepted. Offline applications or documents sent by post will be rejected.

entry.title: Visva-Bharati Santiniketan Recruitment 2025: Apply for French Guest Faculty, Honorarium up to ₹50,000
entry.content:
Visva-Bharati Santiniketan Recruitment 2025: Apply for French Guest Faculty, Honorarium up to ₹50,000

The Centre for Modern European Languages, Literature and Culture Studies (CMELLCS) at Visva-Bharati, Santiniketan, has invited applications via email for one post of Guest Faculty in French.

A golden opportunity has arisen for academic professionals looking for a chance to serve at one of India's most prestigious educational and cultural institutions, Visva-Bharati, Santiniketan. The Centre for Modern European Languages, Literature and Culture Studies (CMELLCS), functioning under Bhasha-Bhavana, has invited applications from eligible candidates for one (01) Unreserved (UR) post of Guest Faculty in French. This appointment will be on a purely 'per-lecture' basis.

This position is for candidates who have expertise in the French language and wish to contribute to a unique academic environment. The duration of the appointment will be for one year (excluding long vacations) or until the permanent post is filled, whichever is earlier. Interested candidates are required to send their documents via email. The last date for application is two weeks from the date of publication of this advertisement.

Recruitment Overview

ParticularsInformation
Institution NameVisva-Bharati, Santiniketan
Centre NameCentre for Modern European Languages, Literature and Culture Studies (CMELLCS)
Post NameGuest Faculty (French)
Number of Posts01 (Unreserved - UR)
Basis of AppointmentPer-lecture basis
Application ModeEmail
Last Date to ApplyTwo weeks from the date of advertisement publication (Approx. December 03, 2025)
Official Websitewww.visva-bharati.ac.in

Honorarium & Engagement Period

  • Honorarium: The selected candidate will be paid ₹1,500/- per lecture.
  • Monthly Cap: The maximum honorarium per month will be ₹50,000/-.
  • Engagement Period: One year (excluding long vacations) or until the permanent post is filled, whichever is earlier.

Eligibility Criteria

The qualifications required for this post are the same as those for a regular Assistant Professor as prescribed by the UGC and Visva-Bharati.

Required Qualification

  • A Master of Arts (M.A.) degree in French with a minimum of 55% marks (with applicable relaxation for reserved categories as per rules).
  • Must have cleared the National Eligibility Test (NET) in the subject of French.

Desirable Qualification

  • A Ph.D. degree in French.
  • Specialization in at least one of the following areas:
    • French as a Foreign Language
    • Translation Studies
    • Literary & Culture Studies
Note: The time taken to acquire M.Phil and/or Ph.D. will not be considered as teaching/research experience.

Selection Process

The selection process will be conducted as per the rules of Visva-Bharati.

  1. Screening of Applications: The received applications will be scrutinized, and eligible candidates will be shortlisted based on UGC/Visva-Bharati norms.
  2. Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be called for an interview. The final decision will be based on the performance in the selection process.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online (via email). There is no need to send a hard copy.

  1. Prepare your detailed CV containing your name, date of birth, nationality, caste, full details of educational qualifications, and experience (teaching and post-doctoral research, if any).
  2. Prepare scanned PDF copies of the following documents:
    • Detailed CV
    • Post-Graduate Marksheet and Certificate
    • NET Certificate
    • List of Publications, if any
    • All other relevant documents
  3. Attach all the documents together and send them to the following email ID:
    cmellcs@visva-bharati.ac.in
Note the Deadline: Your application via email must reach within two weeks from the date of the advertisement's publication (19.11.2025).

Important Links

Details Link
Official Notification Click Here
Visva-Bharati Official Website Click Here

FAQs – Frequently Asked Questions

Question 1: How do I apply?
Applications will only be accepted via email. You need to send scanned copies of your CV and all required documents to cmellcs@visva-bharati.ac.in.
Question 2: What is the last date to apply?
The last date for application is two weeks from the date of publication of the advertisement (November 19, 2025).
Question 3: Is there any application fee for this post?
No, there is no application fee for this recruitment.
Question 4: Is clearing NET mandatory?
Yes, having cleared NET in the subject of French along with an M.A. is a mandatory qualification.

entry.title: Assam Police SI Recruitment 2026 for 515 Posts
entry.content:
Assam Police SI Recruitment 2026 for 515 Posts

The State Level Police Recruitment Board (SLPRB), Assam, is inviting online applications for the recruitment of 515 Sub-Inspectors (UB), SI (Communication), and Station Officers. The last date to submit applications is February 15, 2026. This is a major opportunity for graduates to secure a prestigious position in the Assam Police, Assam Police Radio Organisation (APRO), and Fire & Emergency Services (F&ES). The application process is entirely online and free of cost.

Post Name: Sub-Inspector (UB), SI (Communication), Station Officer
Last Date: 15 February 2026
Total Posts: 515

The State Level Police Recruitment Board (SLPRB), Assam, has announced a large-scale recruitment drive to fill 515 vacancies for various Sub-Inspector level posts across different departments. This includes the coveted post of Sub-Inspector of Police (Unarmed Branch) in Assam Police, the technical role of Sub-Inspector of Police (Communication) in APRO, and the critical position of Station Officer in the Fire & Emergency Services.

This recruitment offers an excellent career path for graduate candidates with the honor of serving the state of Assam. The selection process for these prestigious posts is rigorous, involving a Written Test, followed by a Physical Standard Test (PST) and a Physical Efficiency Test (PET). With a handsome pay scale and the pride of wearing the uniform, this is a highly anticipated opportunity for the youth of Assam.

Assam Police SI Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take note of the deadlines and other important details for this recruitment drive.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization State Level Police Recruitment Board (SLPRB), Assam
Post Names Sub-Inspector (UB), SI (Communication), Station Officer
Total Vacancies 515
Last Date to Apply Online 15 February 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Application Fee NIL (No Fee)
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Assam
Official Website slprbassam.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 515 positions across three key departments, all offering a competitive pay scale.

Post Name Department Vacancies Pay Scale
Sub-Inspector of Police (UB) Assam Police 210 ₹14,000 - 60,500 (Pay Band-2) + Grade Pay as applicable
Sub-Inspector of Police (Communication) Assam Police Radio Organisation (APRO) 295
Station Officer Fire & Emergency Services (F&ES), Assam 10
Total 515
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and subject to change. Candidates must refer to the official notification for a detailed category-wise reservation breakdown.

Application Fee

  • There is NO application fee for any candidate applying for these posts. The entire application process is free of cost.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet all the eligibility requirements regarding nationality, domicile, age, and educational qualifications.

Criteria Details
Common Eligibility
  1. Must be an Indian Citizen and a permanent resident of Assam.
  2. Must be registered with a local Employment Exchange in Assam.
  3. Must speak Assamese or any other State language fluently.
Age Limit (as on 01-01-2026)
  • For SI (UB) & SI (Communication): 20 to 26 years.
  • For Station Officer: 20 to 24 years.

Upper Age Limit Relaxation: 5 years for SC/ST(P)/ST(H) and 3 years for OBC/MOBC.
Post-wise Qualifications
Post NameMinimum Educational Qualification
Sub-Inspector of Police (UB)Graduate in Arts, Science, Commerce, or equivalent stream from a recognized College / Institution affiliated with a recognized University.
Sub-Inspector of Police (Communication)B.E./B.Tech. in Electronics & Telecommunication / Computer / Electrical / Instrumentation / IT from a recognized University, OR a 3-year Diploma in the same fields along with a 2-year work experience.
Station OfficerB.Sc. (Science) with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM) from a recognized University.

Selection Process

The selection process is a multi-stage procedure designed to select the most physically and mentally fit candidates.

  1. Written Test: All eligible candidates will first appear for a 100-mark OMR-based written examination.
  2. Physical Standard Test (PST) & Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Candidates who are shortlisted based on their performance in the written test will be called for PST (measurement) and PET (physical tasks). Both PST and PET are qualifying in nature.
  3. Oral/Viva-Voce and Extra-Curricular Activities: Candidates who clear PST/PET will be called for the final stage which includes an Oral/Viva-Voce test and marks for NCC/Sports achievements.
  4. Final Merit List: The final selection will be based on the marks obtained in the Written Test, Oral/Viva-Voce, and Extra-Curricular Activities.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The recruitment process starts with a comprehensive written test.

Written Test Pattern:

  • Total Marks: 100
  • Question Type: Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs), OMR-based.
  • Duration: 3 hours.
  • Negative Marking: There will be NO negative marking for wrong answers.
  • Syllabus:
    1. Logical Reasoning, Aptitude, Comprehension
    2. Matters relating to the History and Culture of Assam and India
    3. General Knowledge and Current Affairs
    4. For SI (Communication) & Station Officer, there will also be questions from their respective technical subjects.

Physical Standard Test (PST) & Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

PST and PET are mandatory and qualifying in nature. Failure to meet the standards will lead to disqualification.

Physical Standards (PST):

CategoryHeight (Male)Height (Female)Chest (Male only)
Gen/OBC/MOBC/SC162.56 cm154.94 cm80cm (Normal), 85cm (Expanded)
ST(H)/ST(P)160.02 cm152.40 cm78cm (Normal), 83cm (Expanded)

Physical Efficiency Test (PET - Qualifying):

GenderEventQualifying Standard
Male1600 meters raceTo be completed within 450 seconds (7.5 minutes)
Long JumpMinimum 335 cm (3 chances)
Female800 meters raceTo be completed within 240 seconds (4 minutes)
Long JumpMinimum 244 cm (3 chances)

How to Prepare

A balanced preparation focusing on both the written exam and physical fitness is key.

  • For Written Exam: Focus on the history, geography, and culture of Assam. Study standard books for logical reasoning and aptitude. Stay updated with national and state-level current affairs. Technical candidates must revise their core engineering/science subjects.
  • For Physical Test: Do not wait for the written test results. Start your physical conditioning immediately. Practice running to build stamina and improve your timing. Work on your long jump technique to clear the minimum distance comfortably.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these books for a structured preparation.

  • "A New Approach to Reasoning" by B.S. Sijwali & Indu Sijwali.
  • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • "Lucent's General Knowledge" for static GK.
  • "History of Assam" by Sir Edward Gait.
  • A local year book or monthly magazine for Assam-specific current affairs.
  • Relevant engineering/science textbooks for technical posts.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the SLPRB portal.

  1. Visit the official SLPRB website: slprbassam.in.
  2. Find the advertisement for the recruitment of SI (UB), SI (Communication) & Station Officer.
  3. Click on the "Apply Online" link. You will be redirected to the application portal.
  4. Register on the portal by providing your personal details, mobile number, and other information.
  5. Log in with your credentials and fill out the detailed application form with your educational and other particulars.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other required documents.
  7. Review your application carefully before the final submission.
  8. Download and save a copy of your submitted application for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should have the following documents ready for the online application:

  1. Certificate for proof of age (Admit Card of HSLC or equivalent).
  2. Pass certificate and mark sheet of Graduation (B.A / B.Sc / B.Com / B.E / B.Tech).
  3. Certificate of registration in Employment Exchange in Assam.
  4. Caste Certificate from the competent authority for reserved category candidates.
  5. Recent passport-size photograph and a scanned copy of your signature.
  6. Certificate of NCC or achievements in sports, if any.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Last Date Extension Notice Click Here
Official Advertisement PDF Click Here
Apply Online / Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the last date to apply for the Assam Police SI recruitment?

The last date for submitting the online application is February 15, 2026.

Q2: Is there any application fee?

No, the application process is completely free of cost for all candidates.

Q3: Is the Physical Efficiency Test (PET) for marks or is it qualifying?

The PET, which includes the race and long jump, is mandatory but only qualifying in nature. No marks from the PET will be added to the final merit list.

Q4: Can a candidate from another state apply for these posts?

No, candidates must be permanent residents of Assam and be registered with a local Employment Exchange in Assam to be eligible for this recruitment.

entry.title: Jharkhand Home Guard Vacancy 2025: Simdega में 510 Home Guard के पदों पर भर्ती! 7th/10th पास करें आवेदन!
entry.content:
Jharkhand Home Guard Vacancy 2025: Simdega में 510 Home Guard के पदों पर भर्ती! 7th/10th पास करें आवेदन!

Jharkhand Home Defense Corps, Simdega ने ग्रामीण और शहरी Home Guard के कुल 510 पदों पर नामांकन के लिए ऑनलाइन आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं।

Jharkhand के Simdega ज़िले के युवाओं के लिए देश सेवा से जुड़ने का सुनहरा अवसर आया है। Jharkhand Griha Raksha Vahini, Simdega ने विज्ञापन संख्या – 01/2025 के तहत Rural और Urban Home Guard के नए नामांकन (New Enrollment) के लिए आवेदन मांगे हैं।

इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत कुल 510 पद भरे जाएंगे। ध्यान दें, Home Guard एक स्वैच्छिक संगठन है — यह नियमित सरकारी नौकरी नहीं है; इसमें प्रतिदिन काम के अनुसार सरकारी दर पर भत्ता दिया जाता है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि / समय
ऑनलाइन आवेदन शुरू होने की तिथि24 अक्टूबर 2025
आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि07 नवंबर 2025 (शाम 5:00 बजे तक)

रिक्तियों का विवरण

कुल 510 पद इस प्रकार विभाजित हैं:

  • Rural Home Guard: 467 पद
  • Urban Home Guard: 43 पद
महिला आरक्षण: दोनों श्रेणियों में कुल पदों का 50% महिलाओं के लिए आरक्षित है। इसके अतिरिक्त, 5% पद तलाकशुदा / विधवा / परित्यक्ता महिलाओं के लिए आरक्षित हैं।

आवेदन शुल्क

सभी उम्मीदवारों को ₹200 का आवेदन शुल्क ऑनलाइन माध्यम से जमा करना आवश्यक है।

पात्रता मानदंड

मानदंडRural Home GuardUrban Home Guard
निवासSimdega ज़िले के संबंधित प्रखंड का स्थायी निवासी होना चाहिए।Simdega शहर क्षेत्र का सामान्य निवासी होना चाहिए।
आयु सीमा01.01.2025 को 19 से 40 वर्ष (जन्म 01/01/1985 से 31/12/2005 के बीच)।
शैक्षणिक योग्यतान्यूनतम 7वीं पासन्यूनतम 10वीं पास

शारीरिक मानक

  • लंबाई:
    • पुरुष: 162 सेमी (General/OBC/BC), 157 सेमी (SC/ST)
    • महिला: 148 सेमी (सभी वर्गों के लिए)
  • सीना (पुरुष): 79 सेमी (General/OBC/BC), 76 सेमी (SC/ST)
  • वजन: महिलाओं के लिए निर्धारित नहीं

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. शारीरिक परीक्षा: 1 मील दौड़, ऊँची कूद, लंबी कूद और शॉट पुट शामिल होंगे।
  2. हिंदी लेखन क्षमता परीक्षा: शारीरिक परीक्षा पास करने वाले उम्मीदवारों की हिंदी लेखन क्षमता जांची जाएगी।
  3. तकनीकी दक्षता परीक्षा: Urban Home Guard (Technical) के लिए अतिरिक्त परीक्षा होगी।
  4. मेरिट सूची: शारीरिक परीक्षा में मिले अंकों और अतिरिक्त अंकों (शिक्षा/खेलकूद) के आधार पर।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आवेदन केवल JAP IT Ranchi द्वारा उपलब्ध कराए गए लिंक से ही करें।
  2. ऑनलाइन आवेदन अवधि 24 अक्टूबर से 07 नवंबर 2025 तक रहेगी।
  3. फॉर्म भरते समय फोटो, हस्ताक्षर और आवश्यक दस्तावेज अपलोड करना अनिवार्य है।
  4. शुल्क का ऑनलाइन भुगतान करें।
  5. ऑफलाइन या डाक द्वारा भेजे गए आवेदन स्वीकार नहीं होंगे।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
Simdega आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
ऑनलाइन आवेदन लिंकजल्द जारी होगा

entry.title: Privacy Policy
entry.content:
Your Privacy is Important to Us

Introduction

Hello friends! Welcome to Sarkari Jobs. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting it. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and secure your information when you use our website (sarkarijobs.com). By using our services, you agree to the terms outlined in this policy.

Information We Collect

We may collect two types of information from you:

  • Personal Information: This is information that you provide to us yourself, such as your name and email address, when you subscribe to our newsletter or contact us.
  • Non-Personal/Technical Information: When you visit our website, we automatically collect some technical information such as your IP address, browser type, device type, and which pages you viewed on our site. This information helps us improve our website.

How We Use Your Information

We use the information you provide for the following purposes:

  • To provide you with the latest job updates and relevant content.
  • To improve our website and services.
  • To answer your questions and provide assistance.
  • To send you email newsletters if you subscribe.
  • To analyze website traffic and user behavior (using tools like Google Analytics).

Cookies Policy

Cookies are small text files that are saved in your browser. We use cookies to improve your experience, such as remembering your preferences. You can disable cookies in your browser settings, but doing so may cause some features of our website to not work properly.

An Important Note: We do not sell your personal information (such as name or email) to any third party. Your trust is our greatest asset.

Sharing Information with Third Parties

We use some trusted third-party services, such as:

  • Google Analytics: To track website traffic.
  • Google AdSense: To display advertisements on the website. These services also collect data and have their own separate privacy policies.

Data Security

We take necessary technical and administrative steps to keep your data secure. Our website uses SSL (HTTPS) encryption, which keeps data secure between your browser and our server. However, no method on the internet is 100% secure, so we cannot guarantee absolute security.

Third-Party Links

You may find links to other government websites or other portals on our website. Please note that we are not responsible for the content or privacy practices of those websites. Be sure to read their privacy policy before visiting any external site.

Changes to this Policy

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Whenever we make changes, we will update the "Last Updated" date on this page. We request that you check this page regularly.

Contact Us

If you have any questions or suggestions about this Privacy Policy, please email us at sakshya14.20@gmail.com. We are always ready to assist you.

entry.title: EME Odisha Recruitment 2025: Apply Offline for Fitter and Cook Posts for 10th/ITI Pass
entry.content:
EME Odisha Recruitment 2025: Apply Offline for Fitter and Cook Posts for 10th/ITI Pass

A golden opportunity to join the Indian Army's EME Corps as a civilian employee in Gopalpur, Odisha. Last date is 21 days from the date of publication in Employment News.

Post Name: Fitter (Skilled), Cook
Last Date: Within 21 days from publication (Approx. 13/12/2025)
Total Posts: 02

An important employment notification has been issued for direct recruitment to Group 'C' (Defence Civilian) posts by the Air Defence Static Workshop, Gopalpur (Odisha), under the Directorate General of the Electronics and Mechanical Engineers (EME) Corps of the Indian Army. This is a golden opportunity for Indian citizens who wish to contribute to nation-building by serving in a stable and respected civilian post with the Indian Armed Forces. Through this recruitment drive, the posts of Fitter (Skilled) and Cook will be filled.

This recruitment will be conducted entirely through an offline process, requiring candidates to send their typed application in the prescribed format via Ordinary Post. It is an excellent chance for candidates with technical skills who want to be part of a defense establishment. Selected candidates will be offered an attractive salary package as per the 7th Central Pay Commission, along with other government allowances. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all the important details related to this recruitment, ensuring you have all the information needed to apply correctly and prepare effectively. The last date for receipt of applications is 21 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in the Employment News.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Recruiting OrganizationCorps of Electronics and Mechanical Engineers (EME), Indian Army
Unit NameAir Defence Static Workshop, Gopalpur
Post NamesFitter (Skilled) and Cook
Total Vacancies02
Job LocationGopalpur Military Station, Odisha
Application ModeOffline (Only by Ordinary Post)
Application DatesWithin 21 days from publication in Employment News (Estimated Last Date: December 13, 2025)

Vacancy Details and Reservation

A total of 02 posts will be filled through this recruitment drive. Both posts are for the Unreserved (UR) category, which means that candidates from any category (General, SC, ST, OBC) can apply for these positions based on merit.

Post Name Number of Vacancies Category
Fitter (Skilled) 01 UR (Unreserved)
Cook 01 UR (Unreserved)

Salary and Other Benefits

Selected candidates will be placed in Level 2 of the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC) Pay Matrix. This is a stable and attractive salary package that includes Basic Pay along with other allowances like Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Transport Allowance (TA) as applicable from time to time. The benefits of being a central government defence civilian employee are numerous, providing job security and a promising career path.

Post Name Pay Matrix Level Pay Scale
Fitter (Skilled) and Cook Level 2 ₹ 19,900 - ₹ 63,200/-

The approximate initial in-hand salary, including all allowances, would be around ₹28,000 - ₹32,000 per month. In addition to the salary, employees are entitled to benefits such as Leave Travel Concession (LTC), medical facilities for self and dependents under the Central Government Health Scheme (CGHS), and coverage under the National Pension System (NPS).

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

The minimum required educational qualifications and experience for both posts are detailed below:

Educational Qualification

Post Name Required Qualification
Fitter (Skilled)
  • ITI certificate from a recognized Industrial Training Institute in the respective trade. OR
  • Armed Forces personnel or Ex-Servicemen from the appropriate trade and minimum grade I.
  • Desirable: Certificate from the National Council for Vocational Training (NCVT).
Cook
  • Matriculation (10th Class) pass or equivalent.
  • Must have knowledge of Indian cooking and proficiency in their trade.

Age Limit

The age of the candidates will be calculated as of the last date of receipt of the application. The age limit for the General category is 18 to 25 years. Age relaxation for reserved categories will be applicable as per government rules, though these posts are unreserved.

CategoryAge LimitRelaxation Details
Unreserved (UR) 18 - 25 years -
Ex-Servicemen (ESM) After deducting the period of total military service from the actual age, the resultant age should not exceed the prescribed upper age limit by more than 3 years.
Departmental Candidates Up to 40 years of age (45 years for SC/ST). (With a minimum of 3 years of continuous service).
Widows, Divorced Women Up to 35 years of age (40 years for SC/ST).
Note: Since both posts are unreserved (UR), SC/ST/OBC candidates who apply for these posts will not be given any age relaxation and will be treated as General candidates.

Selection Process

The selection process will be completed in multiple stages, primarily involving a written examination. The selection of candidates will be based on the following steps:

  1. Shortlisting of Applications: Received applications will be shortlisted based on the percentage of marks obtained in the essential qualification. Only shortlisted candidates will be issued admit cards for the written examination.
  2. Written Examination: The exam will be offline (OMR-based) and of an objective type. It will consist of 150 questions for 150 marks, with a duration of 2 hours. The question paper will be bilingual (English and Hindi). There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each wrong answer.
  3. Document Verification and Biometrics: Candidates called for the written examination will undergo biometric registration and verification of their original documents. A practical/skill test may be conducted if required, although it is not explicitly mentioned as a separate stage in this notification.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Written Examination Pattern for Fitter (Skilled)

PartSubjectQuestionsMarks
Part-IGeneral Intelligence & Reasoning2525
Part-IIGeneral Awareness2525
Part-IIIGeneral English2525
Part-IVNumerical Aptitude2525
Part-VTrade Specific5050
Total150150

Written Examination Pattern for Cook

PartSubjectQuestionsMarks
Part-IGeneral Intelligence & Reasoning5050
Part-IIGeneral Awareness5050
Part-IIIGeneral English2525
Part-IVNumerical Aptitude2525
Total150150

Detailed Syllabus

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: This section will include questions on verbal and non-verbal reasoning, analogies, similarities and differences, spatial visualization, problem-solving, analysis, judgment, decision making, visual memory, and coding-decoding.
  • General Awareness: Questions will test the candidate’s awareness of the environment. Topics include current events, India and its neighboring countries, sports, history, culture, geography, and scientific research.
  • General English: This section will test the candidate’s understanding of basic English, including vocabulary, grammar, sentence structure, synonyms, and antonyms.
  • Numerical Aptitude: This part will include questions on Number Systems, Percentages, Profit & Loss, Ratio, Averages, and Time & Distance.
  • Trade Specific (For Fitter only): This is the most crucial section for the Fitter post, carrying 50 marks. The syllabus will be based on the ITI Fitter trade curriculum. Topics include Workshop Safety, Hand and Machine Tools, Measuring Instruments (Vernier Caliper, Micrometer), Limits, Fits and Tolerances, Metallurgy and Heat Treatment, Fasteners, Welding, Brazing, Soldering, Lathe Operations, Drilling, and Grinding.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for these Group 'C' civilian posts. Selection is based on merit in the written examination.

How to Prepare for the EME Recruitment Exam

A structured preparation strategy is essential to succeed in this examination. Candidates should focus on the following areas based on the syllabus:

  1. General Intelligence and Reasoning: This section involves logical and analytical reasoning. Practice topics like analogies, series, coding-decoding, directions, and non-verbal reasoning.
  2. General Awareness: This section will cover current events related to India and its neighbors, history, culture, geography, and scientific research. Regular reading of newspapers and a standard GK book is recommended.
  3. General English: This section will test your basic understanding of the English language, including vocabulary, grammar, and sentence structure.
  4. Numerical Aptitude: This section will include questions on basic mathematical topics like number systems, percentages, profit-loss, ratio, averages, and time and distance.
  5. Trade Specific (For Fitter only): This is the most important section, carrying 50 marks. Study the syllabus of your ITI Fitter trade in depth. Focus on topics like machine tools, fitting, welding, measurements, and safety precautions.
  6. Practice: Since the exam is OMR-based, practice filling OMR sheets. Focus on accuracy to avoid negative marking.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books to aid your preparation:

  • General Intelligence & Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Numerical Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Trade Specific (Fitter): ITI Trade Theory books for the Fitter trade by Nimmi Pattern or Arihant Publications.

How to Apply (Offline Process)

This recruitment is completely offline. Interested candidates must follow the steps below to send their application:

  1. Prepare the Application Form: The application must be neatly typed on an A4 size paper as per the format given in the advertisement. Handwritten applications will not be accepted.
  2. Fill the Form: Fill in all the details (Name, Father's Name, Date of Birth, Address, etc.) in BLOCK LETTERS.
  3. Affix Photographs: Paste a recent passport-size photograph on the application form and the admit card/acknowledgment slip.
  4. Prepare Required Documents: Prepare self-attested photocopies of all the certificates listed in the "Documents Required" section below.
  5. Self-Addressed Envelope: Take a blank envelope (size 10.5 cm x 25 cm), write your complete postal address on it, and affix a postage stamp of ₹5. The admit card will be sent back to you in this envelope.
  6. Prepare the Main Envelope: Place your typed application form, all photocopies of documents, and the self-addressed envelope with the ₹5 stamp inside a larger envelope.
  7. Superscribe the Envelope: On top of the main envelope, clearly write "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF _________" (write the name of the post in the blank space).
  8. Send the Application: Send the filled envelope only through ORDINARY POST to the following address:

    Officer Commanding,
    Air Defence Static Workshop, Gopalpur,
    Gopalpur Military Station, PO-Golabandha,
    Dist-Ganjam, Odisha, Pin-761052

  9. Last Date: Ensure your application reaches the above address before the last date. Applications sent through any other mode (Speed Post, Registered Post, or By Hand) will not be accepted.

Documents Required with the Application

It is mandatory to enclose self-attested photocopies of the following documents with the application form:

  1. Typed and signed Application Form.
  2. Filled Acknowledgment/Admit Card form.
  3. Two additional self-attested passport-size photographs (write your name and father's/mother's name on the back).
  4. Educational qualification certificates (10th Marksheet/Certificate).
  5. ITI Certificate (for the Fitter post).
  6. Date of Birth certificate (if not in the 10th certificate).
  7. Caste certificate (though the posts are unreserved).
  8. Copy of Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, etc.).
  9. Discharge Certificate for Ex-Servicemen.
  10. One self-addressed envelope with a ₹5 postage stamp affixed.

Important Links

Link Type Description
Official Notification and Application Form This recruitment was published in the Employment News dated 22-28 November 2025. The format of the form is given in the advertisement.
Employment News Website www.employmentnews.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: How do I submit the application form?

The application form, which must be typed on A4 paper, needs to be sent along with all required documents only through Ordinary Post to the address provided.

Question 2: Is there any application fee?

No, there is no application fee. However, you must send a self-addressed envelope with a ₹5 postage stamp affixed with your application.

Question 3: How will I receive the admit card?

If your application is shortlisted, the recruitment agency will use the self-addressed envelope you provided to send the admit card to you via ordinary post.

Question 4: Is there negative marking in this exam?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the written examination.

Question 5: Can I send the application by Speed Post or Registered Post?

No, the notification clearly states that applications will be accepted only through Ordinary Post. Applications sent through any other mode will be rejected.

entry.title: UPSC Civil Services Exam 2026 Notification: Apply Online for 933 IAS, IPS & IFS Posts
entry.content:
UPSC Civil Services Exam 2026 Notification: Apply Online for 933 IAS, IPS & IFS Posts

Examination Notice No. 05/2026-CSE: The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has released the detailed notification for the Civil Services Examination (CSE) 2026. This exam is the gateway to 21 prestigious services including IAS, IPS, IFS, and IRS. The commission aims to fill approximately 933 vacancies. The online application window is open from February 04, 2026, to February 24, 2026.

Post Name: All India Services (IAS, IPS, IFS) & Central Services (Group A & B)
Last Date: 24 February 2026
Total Posts: 933 (Approx.)

The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) conducts the Civil Services Examination (CSE) annually to select officers for the Indian Administrative Service (IAS), Indian Foreign Service (IFS), Indian Police Service (IPS), and various Central Services (Group A and Group B). For 2026, the commission has announced approximately 933 vacancies. This examination is a three-stage process testing academic knowledge, analytical ability, and personality.

While the IAS and IFS do not have specific physical requirements beyond general health, services like the IPS, IRMS (Group A), and RPF (Group A) have strict physical standards regarding height, chest expansion, and vision. This article provides a detailed breakdown of the services, physical requirements, and the comprehensive syllabus to help aspirants prepare effectively.

UPSC CSE 2026 (Important Points)

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Union Public Service Commission (UPSC)
Exam Name Civil Services Examination (CSE) 2026
Total Vacancies Approx. 933 (Includes 33 vacancies for PwBD)
Application Dates 04 Feb 2026 to 24 Feb 2026 (6:00 PM)
Preliminary Exam Date 24 May 2026
Main Exam Date Likely in September 2026
Official Website www.upsc.gov.in

Service-wise Details

The examination recruits for the following categories of services:

1. All India Services:

  • Indian Administrative Service (IAS)
  • Indian Police Service (IPS)
  • Indian Forest Service (IFS) - (Prelims is common, Mains is separate)

2. Group 'A' Central Services:

  • Indian Foreign Service (IFS)
  • Indian Audit and Accounts Service (IA&AS)
  • Indian Civil Accounts Service (ICAS)
  • Indian Corporate Law Service (ICLS)
  • Indian Defence Accounts Service (IDAS)
  • Indian Defence Estates Service (IDES)
  • Indian Information Service (IIS)
  • Indian Postal Service (IPoS)
  • Indian P&T Accounts and Finance Service (IP&TAFS)
  • Indian Railway Management Service (IRMS)
  • Indian Railway Protection Force Service (IRPFS)
  • Indian Revenue Service (Customs & Indirect Taxes) (IRS-C&IT)
  • Indian Revenue Service (Income Tax) (IRS-IT)
  • Indian Trade Service (Gr. III)

3. Group 'B' Central Services:

  • Armed Forces Headquarters Civil Service (Section Officer's Grade)
  • DANICS (Delhi, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, etc. Civil Service)
  • DANIPS (Delhi, Andaman & Nicobar Islands, etc. Police Service)
  • Pondicherry Civil Service (PONDICS)
  • Pondicherry Police Service (PONDIPS)

Physical Standards for Specific Services

While general good health is required for all posts, technical and police services have specific physical criteria.

1. For IPS, IRPFS, DANIPS, PONDIPS

Criteria Male Candidates Female Candidates
Height 165 cm (min)
(Relaxable by 5 cm for ST and races such as Gorkhas, Garhwalis, Assamese, Kumaonis, Nagas)		 150 cm (min)
(Relaxable by 5 cm for ST and races such as Gorkhas, Garhwalis, Assamese, Kumaonis, Nagas)
Chest Min 84 cm (Expansion 5 cm) Min 79 cm (Expansion 5 cm)
Vision Distant Vision: 6/6 or 6/9 in better eye; 6/12 or 6/9 in worse eye.
Near Vision: J1 in better eye; J2 in worse eye.
High-grade color vision is required.

2. For IRMS (Group A)

The physical standards vary based on the specific post within the railways but generally follow the technical service guidelines.

  • Height: Male - 152 cm; Female - 150 cm (Relaxable for specific categories).
  • Chest: Chest expansion of 5 cm is generally required.
  • Vision: Distinct standards apply for different functional branches (Traffic, Engineering, etc.), often requiring 6/6 or 6/9 vision with or without glasses.

3. For Other Services (IAS, IFS, IRS, etc.)

  • No specific height/chest measurement is mandatory, but candidates must be free from any chronic disease and have good mental and physical health.
  • Vision: Correction with glasses is permitted. Color blindness is not a disqualification for non-technical services like IAS or IRS, but it is for services like IPS and RPF.

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

Stage 1: Preliminary Examination (400 Marks)
This is a screening test. Marks are not counted for the final rank.

  • Paper I (General Studies): 100 Qs, 200 Marks. (Merit deciding). Syllabus: Current events, History of India, Indian & World Geography, Indian Polity & Governance, Economic & Social Development, Environmental Ecology, General Science.
  • Paper II (CSAT): 80 Qs, 200 Marks. (Qualifying - 33% required). Syllabus: Comprehension, Interpersonal skills, Logical reasoning, Decision making, General mental ability, Basic numeracy (Class X level).

Stage 2: Main Examination (1750 Marks)
Descriptive written exam. Marks are counted for the final rank.

Paper Subject Marks
Paper AIndian Language (Qualifying)300
Paper BEnglish (Qualifying)300
Paper IEssay250
Paper IIGeneral Studies I (Indian Heritage, History, Geography, Society)250
Paper IIIGeneral Studies II (Governance, Constitution, Polity, Social Justice, IR)250
Paper IVGeneral Studies III (Technology, Economic Dev, Bio-diversity, Security, Disaster Mgmt)250
Paper VGeneral Studies IV (Ethics, Integrity, Aptitude)250
Paper VIOptional Subject Paper 1250
Paper VIIOptional Subject Paper 2250

Stage 3: Personality Test (Interview) (275 Marks)
Assesses the candidate's intellectual curiosity, critical powers of assimilation, balance of judgment, integrity, and leadership potential.

Grand Total: 2025 Marks.

How to Apply

  1. OTR Registration: Visit upsconline.nic.in and register on the One Time Registration (OTR) platform. This is mandatory.
  2. Fill Application: Log in using OTR details. Go to the active notification for CSE 2026.
  3. Part-I: Fill in personal details, educational qualification, and address.
  4. Part-II: Pay the fee (₹100 for Gen/OBC/EWS), select your exam center, and upload your photograph and signature.
  5. Documents: Upload scanned Photo (20-300 KB) and Signature (20-300 KB) in JPG format. Ensure the photo is recent (not older than 10 days from notification) and has the date of taking the photograph printed on it (as per recent UPSC trends).
  6. Submit: Agree to the declaration and submit. Print the final application.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online (OTR) Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there a physical test for IAS?

No, there is no physical efficiency test (running, jumping) for the IAS. Candidates only need to meet the general medical standards prescribed by the Health Ministry.

Q2: What is the height requirement for IPS?

For IPS, the minimum height is 165 cm for men and 150 cm for women. There is a relaxation of 5 cm for ST candidates and certain races.

Q3: Can I choose my own language for the interview?

Yes, candidates can choose to be interviewed in English or in any of the Indian languages listed in the 8th Schedule of the Constitution, provided they have opted for it in the Mains application form.

entry.title: IIIT Ranchi Recruitment 2026 for 19 Non-Teaching Posts
entry.content:
IIIT Ranchi Recruitment 2026 for 19 Non-Teaching Posts

Advertisement No. IIITR/RG/2026/Advt./01: The Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Ranchi, an Institute of National Importance, has invited online applications for the recruitment of 19 non-teaching positions. The vacancies include Deputy Registrar, Assistant Registrar, Junior Superintendent, Junior Assistant, and Junior Technician (CSE/ECE/Library). The online application portal is open until February 28, 2026, and the hard copy must reach the institute by March 10, 2026.

Post Name: Deputy Registrar, Assistant Registrar, Jr. Superintendent, Jr. Assistant, Jr. Technician
Last Date: 28 February 2026
Total Posts: 19

The Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Ranchi, established under the PPP mode by the Ministry of Education, Government of India, and the Government of Jharkhand, is looking for qualified and motivated individuals to join its administrative and technical team. The institute has announced 19 vacancies across Group A, B, and C categories to support its academic and research mission. This is a significant opportunity to work in a prestigious autonomous institute.

The recruitment covers various levels, from senior administrative roles like Deputy Registrar (Pay Level 12) to entry-level support positions like Junior Assistant and Junior Technician (Pay Level 3). The selection process involves a mix of personal interviews for Group A posts and domain-specific written/skill tests for Group B and C posts. Candidates must apply online through the Samarth portal and subsequently send the hard copy of their application along with self-attested documents to the institute.

IIIT Ranchi Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and instructions. Note that submitting the hard copy is mandatory.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Indian Institute of Information Technology (IIIT) Ranchi
Advertisement Number IIITR/RG/2026/Advt./01
Post Names Deputy Registrar, Assistant Registrar, Superintendent, Assistant, Technician
Total Vacancies 19
Online Application Last Date 28 February 2026 (up to 11:59 PM)
Hard Copy Submission Last Date 10 March 2026 (up to 5:00 PM)
Application Mode Online + Offline (Hard Copy)
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Autonomous Institute)
Job Location Ranchi, Jharkhand
Official Website www.iiitranchi.ac.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The 19 vacancies are distributed across six different posts with varying pay levels as per the 7th CPC.

Post Name Group Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC)
Deputy RegistrarA01Level 12 (PB-3: ₹15600-39100 + GP ₹7600)
Assistant RegistrarA02Level 10 (PB-3: ₹15600-39100 + GP ₹5400)
Junior SuperintendentB03Level 06 (PB-2: ₹9300-34800 + GP ₹4200)
Junior AssistantC07Level 03 (PB-1: ₹5200-20200 + GP ₹2000)
Junior Technician (CSE/ ECE)C05Level 03 (PB-1: ₹5200-20200 + GP ₹2000)
Junior Technician (Library)C01Level 03 (PB-1: ₹5200-20200 + GP ₹2000)
Total19
Note: Vacancies include reservations for OBC-NCL, SC, ST, and EWS categories as per the table in the notification. The number of vacancies may increase or decrease.

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

For Group A Posts:

  • Unreserved (UR): ₹2000/-
  • OBC-NCL/EWS/SC/ST/PwD: ₹1500/-

For Group B & C Posts:

  • Unreserved (UR): ₹1000/-
  • OBC-NCL/EWS/SC/ST/PwD: ₹750/-

The fee must be paid online through SBI Collect. Candidates applying for multiple posts must pay the fee separately for each.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet the specific age and qualification requirements for the post they are applying for.

Post Name Age Limit Essential Qualifications & Experience
Deputy Registrar 55 years Master's Degree with at least 55% marks. 5 years of administrative experience as Assistant Registrar (Level 10) or equivalent.
Assistant Registrar 45 years Master's Degree with at least 55% marks and an excellent academic record. Experience in administration/finance/stores is desirable.
Junior Superintendent 32 years First Class Bachelor's Degree with 6 years of experience in relevant area.
Junior Assistant 27 years Bachelor's Degree with knowledge of computer operations.
Junior Technician (CSE/ ECE) 27 years Diploma in Engineering / Bachelor's Degree in Engineering OR ITI with 2 years of experience in the relevant field.
Junior Technician (Library) 27 years Degree from a recognized University with Diploma in Library Science OR 3-year Degree in Library Science.

Selection Process

The selection methodology varies based on the group of the post.

  1. For Group A (Deputy/Assistant Registrar): Selection will be primarily based on a Personal Interview. For Assistant Registrar, a screening test may be conducted to shortlist candidates if required.
  2. For Group B & C (Superintendent, Assistant, Technician): Selection will be based on a Domain Written Test and Skills Test. A screening test may also be conducted for shortlisting if the number of applicants is large.

Exam Pattern

The detailed exam pattern is not specified in the advertisement but will be decided by the institute. However, candidates can expect the following based on the selection mode mentioned:

  • Screening Test: If conducted, this will likely be an objective-type test to shortlist candidates for the next stage.
  • Domain Written Test: This will assess the candidate's technical knowledge relevant to the post (e.g., Computer Science/Electronics for Technician, Office Procedures/Accounts for Superintendent).
  • Skills Test: This will be a practical test to assess proficiency in areas like computer operations, typing, laboratory equipment handling, etc.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for any of the posts in this recruitment drive.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A post-specific preparation strategy is recommended.

  • For Administrative Posts: Study Government of India rules such as FR/SR, GFR, CCS (Conduct) Rules, and office procedures. Practice noting and drafting.
  • For Technical Posts: Thoroughly revise the core subjects from your Diploma/Degree curriculum. Focus on practical aspects of your trade (CSE/ECE/Library Science).
  • For All Posts: Brush up on computer proficiency, especially MS Office tools, as this is a common requirement across most positions.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard resources for their preparation.

  • Administrative Rules: "Swamy's Handbook for Central Government Staff".
  • Technical Subjects: Standard textbooks from your B.E./Diploma/ITI course.
  • Computer Knowledge: "Objective Computer Awareness" by Arihant Publications.

How to Apply Online & Offline

The application process involves two mandatory steps: Online Application and Hard Copy Submission.

  1. Step 1: Pay Application Fee: Visit SBI Collect (link in notification) and pay the fee under "Educational Institutions" -> "IIIT Ranchi" -> "Recruitment". Save the payment receipt.
  2. Step 2: Apply Online: Visit the Samarth portal: https://iiitranchint.samarth.edu.in. Register and fill out the online application form. Enter the SBI Collect transaction details. Upload scanned copies of all original certificates. Submit the form by 28 February 2026.
  3. Step 3: Send Hard Copy: Take a printout of the submitted online application. Sign it and attach self-attested photocopies of all relevant documents (fee receipt, educational/experience certificates, caste certificate, etc.).
  4. Step 4: Dispatch: Send the complete application package by Speed Post/Registered Post/Courier to the address below. Superscribe the envelope with "Application for the post of [Post Name] vide advt. no. [Advt No.]". It must reach by 10 March 2026.

Submission Address:
The Registrar,
Indian Institute of Information Technology Ranchi,
ARTTC BSNL Campus, Getlatu, Near Jumar River Bridge,
P.O.: Neori Vikas Vidyalaya SO, Hazaribagh Road,
Ranchi - 835217 (Jharkhand)

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must attach self-attested copies of the following documents with the hard copy:

  1. Payment Receipt of Application Fee.
  2. Signed printout of the online application form.
  3. Matriculation (10th) certificate for Date of Birth.
  4. Mark sheets and Certificates of all educational qualifications (10th onwards).
  5. Experience Certificates (if applicable).
  6. Caste/Category Certificate (OBC-NCL/SC/ST/EWS/PwD) in Central Govt. format.
  7. NOC from present employer (if currently employed).
  8. Valid ID Proof (Aadhaar/PAN/Voter ID).

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online (Samarth Portal) Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is the hard copy submission mandatory?

Yes, sending the signed hard copy of the application along with all self-attested documents is mandatory. If the hard copy is not received by March 10, 2026, the candidature will be rejected.

Q2: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, candidates can apply for multiple posts. However, they must submit a separate application and pay the application fee separately for each post.

Q3: Is there a skill test for the Junior Assistant post?

Yes, for Group C posts like Junior Assistant, a Skills Test will be conducted along with a Domain Written Test to assess proficiency.

Q4: What is the age limit for the Junior Technician post?

The upper age limit for the Junior Technician post is 27 years. Age relaxation is applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PwD/Ex-servicemen candidates as per Government of India norms.

entry.title: NERIST Recruitment 2025: Apply for 70+ Non-Teaching Vacancies in Arunachal Pradesh
entry.content:
NERIST Non-Teaching Recruitment 2025: Notification for 70+ Group A, B, and C Vacancies

Direct Recruitment at North Eastern Regional Institute of Science & Technology - Apply Offline for Administrative and Technical Positions

Post Name: Deputy Registrar, Clerk, Technician, Lab Assistant, Skilled Worker, Driver, Nurse
Last Date: January 07, 2026
Total Posts: 70+ Vacancies

The North Eastern Regional Institute of Science & Technology (NERIST), Nirjuli, Itanagar, is a premier technical and management institute established by the Government of India. Functioning as a Deemed to be University under the Ministry of Education, NERIST is unique for its modular pattern of education, catering to the specific developmental needs of the North Eastern region. To further bolster its administrative and technical infrastructure, the institute has officially released a major recruitment notification for 2025-26 under Advertisement No. 02/2025. This drive seeks to fill more than 70 non-teaching vacancies across Group A, B, and C categories. These roles are pivotal for the institute's operations, ranging from senior administrative positions like Deputy Registrar to technical lab assistants and essential support staff like Skilled Workers and Drivers.

Working at NERIST offers more than just a central government salary; it provides an opportunity to contribute to the educational upliftment of Arunachal Pradesh and the entire Seven Sister states. The 2025 recruitment drive is particularly significant as it includes a large number of backlog vacancies specifically reserved for Scheduled Tribe (ST) candidates, emphasizing the institute's commitment to inclusive representation. The application process is strictly offline, requiring candidates to download the prescribed form, fill it manually, and send it via post along with the required Demand Draft. The deadline for receipt of applications is January 7, 2026. Candidates selected through this process will enjoy the benefits of the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC) and the job security associated with a premier autonomous institution of the Government of India.

The selection process for NERIST non-teaching posts is designed to ensure technical competency and administrative efficiency. While higher-level posts involve a personal interview, Group C positions primarily rely on written tests and skill-based trade tests. This exhaustive 2000-word guide provides a comprehensive roadmap for aspirants. We will explore every detail of the notification, including discipline-wise eligibility, salary matrix levels, the specific offline application procedure, and strategic tips to prepare for the technical and general aptitude examinations. For those looking for a stable and prestigious career in the "Land of the Rising Sun," this guide is your definitive first step.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Unlike online recruitment processes, offline applications require extra attention to postage times and document compilation. Candidates must ensure that their application reaches the Registrar’s office well before the cutoff date. The institute will not be responsible for any postal delays. It is highly recommended to use Speed Post or Registered Post for tracking purposes.

Recruitment Feature Key Dates and Details
Official Notification Number Advt. No. 02/2025 (Date: 14.11.2025)
Application Start Date November 2025
Last Date for Receipt of Application January 07, 2026
Job Nature Permanent / Central Govt Autonomous Body
Application Mode Offline (Prescribed Format)
Job Location Nirjuli (Itanagar), Arunachal Pradesh

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Salary Structure

The vacancies at NERIST are categorized based on the functional requirements of the academic departments and the central administration. A large portion of the recruitment is focused on the Laboratory and Technical Assistant cadre, which is essential for the institute's extensive research facilities.

Post Name Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC) Basic Pay Scale
Deputy Registrar 01 Level 12 ₹78,800 - ₹2,09,200
Sports Officer 01 Level 6 ₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400
Nurse 01 Level 6 ₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400
Laboratory / Tech. Assistant 34 Level 5 ₹29,200 - ₹92,300
Computer Programmer 01 Level 5 ₹29,200 - ₹92,300
UDC / Caretaker 03 Level 4 ₹25,500 - ₹81,100
Stenographer-III / Technician 08 Level 4 ₹25,500 - ₹81,100
LDC / Driver / Skilled Worker 26 Level 2 ₹19,900 - ₹63,200
Grand Total Vacancies 70+ - -
Backlog Vacancy Note: Out of the total posts, a significant number of roles (including LDC, Driver, and Technician) are marked as ST Backlog vacancies. Only candidates belonging to the Scheduled Tribe category are eligible for these specific backlog seats.

Eligibility Criteria and Educational Qualifications

The eligibility criteria at NERIST are aligned with the standards of the Ministry of Education. Each post requires a specific academic background and, in some cases, mandatory professional experience in a government or educational institution.

  • Deputy Registrar: Master's Degree with at least 55% marks. Experience: 9 years as an Assistant Professor in Level 10 or 5 years of administrative experience as an Assistant Registrar in a university.
  • Nurse: 10+2 (Science) plus a 3-year Diploma in General Nursing and Midwifery (GNM) with 55% marks. Must be an 'A' grade registered nurse with 3 years of experience.
  • Laboratory / Technical Assistant: 4-year B.E/B.Tech in relevant Engineering branch with 55% marks and 2 years experience OR 3-year Diploma with 3 years experience.
  • Lower Division Clerk (LDC): 12th pass from a recognized board. Typing: 30 wpm in English. Basic knowledge of computer applications is mandatory.
  • Skilled Worker: ITI Certificate in relevant trade (Electrical, Fitter, etc.) with 10th pass.
  • Driver: 10th pass with a valid driving license for LMV/HMV and 3 years of driving experience. Knowledge of motor mechanism is desirable.

Age Limit: The maximum age for Deputy Registrar is 50 years. For most other Group B and C posts, the age limit is 30 years. Age relaxations apply as per GOI norms: 5 years for SC/ST and 3 years for OBC. PwBD candidates are eligible for up to 10-15 years of relaxation.

Selection Process

The selection process at NERIST follows a multi-tier structure designed to test both the theoretical knowledge and practical abilities of the candidates. The complexity of the process increases with the pay level of the post.

  1. Scrutiny of Applications: Since this is an offline process, NERIST will first verify all documents and Demand Drafts. Applications with missing signatures or documents will be rejected at this stage.
  2. Shortlisting: The institute reserves the right to shortlist candidates based on their academic marks and years of experience if the number of applicants is high.
  3. Written Test: Conducted for almost all posts to test general aptitude, language proficiency, and technical knowledge.
  4. Skill Test / Trade Test: Mandatory for posts like LDC (Typing), Stenographer (Shorthand), Driver (Driving), and Technician (Trade-specific task). This stage is qualifying in nature.
  5. Personal Interview: Conducted for Group A and B positions (Deputy Registrar, Sports Officer) to assess leadership and administrative judgment.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Candidates should focus their preparation on two main areas: General Aptitude and Post-Specific Knowledge. The exam is typically OMR-based.

Part 1: General Paper (Common for all):

  • General English: Grammar, comprehension, and business communication (Letter/Report writing).
  • General Awareness: Current affairs, basics of the Indian Constitution, and specifically, the History, Geography, and Culture of the Northeast Region (Arunachal Pradesh).
  • Quantitative Aptitude: Basic mathematics up to 10th standard level.
  • Logical Reasoning: Verbal and non-verbal reasoning puzzles.

Part 2: Domain Specific Paper: For Technical Assistants and Technicians, this section will include questions from the relevant engineering or science branch (Civil, Electrical, Mechanical, Physics, etc.). For Clerks, the focus will be on "Office Procedures," noting, drafting, and CCS Rules.

Strategic Preparation Roadmap

Preparation for a Northeastern central institute requires a unique approach, especially regarding the General Knowledge section. Candidates often lose marks in regional GK.

Master the Northeast Context: Expect questions on the various tribes of Arunachal Pradesh, the Brahmaputra river system, and regional festivals like Solung or Losar. Knowledge of the "New Education Policy 2020" is also essential as NERIST is under the Ministry of Education.

Technical Core: For the 34 Lab Assistant posts, candidates must revisit their Diploma or B.Tech textbooks. The questions are usually practical and revolve around laboratory safety, instrument calibration, and experimental procedures. Practice with GATE-level (lower difficulty) or SSC-JE previous year papers.

Skill Readiness: For LDCs, don't wait for the written test results. Start practicing typing daily using software like "Typing Master." Achieving a consistent speed of 35-40 wpm is necessary to safely clear the 30 wpm requirement under exam pressure.

Best Books for Preparation

Using the right study material for a specialized institute like NERIST is crucial. Below are the top recommendations from previous successful candidates.

General GK: "The Wonderland Arunachal Pradesh" by Jagmohan Balokhra or Lucent's GK.
Aptitude & Reasoning: Quantitative Aptitude and Verbal/Non-Verbal Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
English: Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi (Arihant).
Technical Core: R.K. Rajput (Mechanical/Electrical) or J.K. Gupta (Civil Engineering Objective).
Office Procedure: Swamy’s Handbook for Central Government Staff.

Offline Application Guide

Since the process is offline, even a small error in the envelope can lead to rejection. Follow these steps meticulously:

  1. Download the Prescribed Application Form from the NERIST website (www.nerist.ac.in).
  2. Fill in all details in Capital Letters using a blue/black ballpoint pen.
  3. Paste a recent passport-size photograph and sign across it where required.
  4. Prepare the Demand Draft (DD): Go to any SBI branch and make a DD of the prescribed amount in favor of "Director, NERIST", payable at SBI Nirjuli (Code No. 18744).
  5. Place the filled form, the original DD, and self-attested copies of all certificates in a sturdy A4 size envelope.
  6. On the top of the envelope, clearly write: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF [NAME OF POST]".
  7. Send the envelope via Registered or Speed Post only to the address mentioned in the links section below.

Documents Needed to Apply

Ensure you have self-attested copies of all the following documents. Original documents should never be sent via post; they are only required for verification.

  1. Educational Certificates: Mark sheets and Passing Certificates from 10th Standard to your highest qualification.
  2. Date of Birth Proof: 10th Standard Certificate or Birth Certificate.
  3. Experience Certificates: Clear proof from current/previous employers on official letterheads.
  4. Caste Certificate: If applying under ST/SC/OBC-NCL categories (Central GOI format).
  5. Demand Draft: The original DD must be attached to the application form.
  6. ID Proof: Copy of Aadhaar Card or PAN Card.
  7. NOC: If you are currently working in a Government/PSU role.

Application Fee Details

  • General, OBC, and EWS Candidates: ₹500/- (Non-refundable).
  • SC and ST Candidates: ₹200/- (Non-refundable).
  • PwBD Candidates: Exempted (No Fee).

Payment must be made through a Demand Draft only. Fees paid through UPI, Online Transfer, or Cash will not be accepted and will lead to the rejection of the application.

Important Links and Address

Resource / Action Details / Direct Link
Postal Address to Send Application The Registrar, NERIST, Nirjuli, PIN – 791109, Arunachal Pradesh
Official NERIST Website www.nerist.ac.in
Official Notification PDF Download Notification

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I apply for more than one post in NERIST?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you are eligible for them. however, you must send separate application envelopes and separate Demand Drafts for each post. Ensure you do not put multiple applications in one envelope.

Q2: Is the ST backlog vacancy only for Arunachal residents?

The backlog vacancies are for candidates belonging to the Scheduled Tribe (ST) category. While candidates from all over India can apply, those from the Northeast region usually find it easier to appear for the offline exams and skill tests held at the Nirjuli campus.

Q3: What happens if my Speed Post reaches after January 7, 2026?

Applications received after the deadline will be strictly rejected, regardless of when they were posted. It is advised to send your application by mid-December to account for any postal delays in the hilly terrain of Arunachal Pradesh.

Q4: Is there an interview for the Lower Division Clerk (LDC) post?

No. For Group C posts like LDC and Driver, selection is based on the Written Test and the qualifying Skill Test. Personal interviews have been discontinued for these grades in Central Government organizations.

Q5: Can I pay the fee through online bank transfer?

No. The notification specifically mentions that the fee must be paid through a Demand Draft. Online receipts will not be considered valid for this offline recruitment process.

Q6: Are candidates currently in the final year of their degree eligible?

No. Candidates must possess the completed degree/diploma and the required experience certificates as of the closing date of the application (January 7, 2026).

entry.title: MP High Court Recruitment 2025: Apply for 41 Data Processing Assistant Posts
entry.content:
MP High Court Recruitment 2025: Notification for 41 Data Processing Assistant Vacancies

Direct Recruitment at the High Court of Madhya Pradesh, Jabalpur – Official Notification 52/Exam/DPA/2025

Post Name: Data Processing Assistant (DPA)
Last Date: November 19, 2025
Total Posts: 41 Vacancies

The High Court of Madhya Pradesh (MPHC), headquartered in Jabalpur, has officially invited online applications for the recruitment of Data Processing Assistants (DPA) for the year 2025. This specialized recruitment, identified under Advertisement Number 52/Exam/DPA/2025, is part of the judiciary's ongoing commitment to digitizing legal processes and enhancing the efficiency of court management systems. In an era where "e-Courts" and virtual hearings have become the norm, the role of a Data Processing Assistant is critical. These professionals act as the technical backbone of the court, managing vast legal databases, ensuring the security of electronic case files, and assisting in the seamless operation of judicial software. A total of 41 vacancies have been announced, providing a unique opportunity for IT and Computer Science professionals to serve in a prestigious government institution.

Working for the Madhya Pradesh High Court is not just about technical maintenance; it is about being an integral part of the justice delivery system. The DPA position is a permanent government role that offers long-term stability, a structured pay scale, and the professional pride of working within one of the highest pillars of the Indian Constitution. For candidates who possess a B.Sc in Computer Science, BCA, or B.Sc in IT, along with the required technical experience, this notification is a gateway to a lifelong career. The application window is set to open on October 29, 2025, and will remain active until November 19, 2025. Due to the high level of technicality involved, the selection process is rigorous, comprising a preliminary screening, a practical exam, and a personal interview.

The judicial technical wing in Madhya Pradesh is responsible for maintaining the state's Integrated Court Management System (ICMS). As a Data Processing Assistant, your responsibilities will extend beyond simple data entry; you will be involved in database administration, network troubleshooting, and providing technical support to judges and court staff. This 2000-word guide serves as your definitive resource, providing deep insights into eligibility criteria, category-wise vacancy distribution, the detailed syllabus for technical tests, and expert tips to help you navigate the competitive landscape of MPHC recruitment.

Recruitment Overview and Important Dates

Candidates must strictly follow the recruitment timeline provided by the High Court. The MPHC is known for its disciplined recruitment cycles. It is highly recommended to complete the online registration early to avoid technical issues on the final day. Furthermore, a specific correction window is provided to ensure that minor errors do not lead to the rejection of your candidature.

Event Description Dates and Deadlines
Official Advertisement Number 52/Exam/DPA/2025
Date of Notification October 28, 2025
Online Application Start Date October 29, 2025
Last Date for Application Submission November 19, 2025 (11:55 PM)
Online Application Fee Payment Deadline November 19, 2025
Correction Window for Application November 24 – November 26, 2025
Job Type Permanent State Government (Judiciary)

Category Wise Vacancy Distribution

The 41 vacancies are distributed according to the reservation policies of the Madhya Pradesh Government. While 19 seats are available for Unreserved (UR) candidates, specific quotas are reserved for SC, ST, and OBC categories. Candidates from other states are also eligible to apply but will be considered exclusively under the UR category, regardless of their original caste status.

Category Name Total Vacancies
Unreserved (UR) 19
Scheduled Caste (SC) 07
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 09
Other Backward Classes (OBC) 06
Total Posts 41
Horizontal Reservation: Within these categories, reservations for Persons with Disabilities (PwD) and Ex-Servicemen are applicable as per the prevailing MP State Government norms. Female candidates are also encouraged to apply as there is equal opportunity across all technical cadres.

Salary and Career Benefits

The Data Processing Assistant role at the MP High Court is a sought-after position due to its competitive salary structure. The role follows the 6th Central Pay Commission grade, which is subsequently updated to the corresponding 7th Pay Commission levels once the probationary period is successfully completed.

  • Pay Scale: ₹5200 – ₹20200
  • Grade Pay: ₹2400
  • Gross Salary: Including Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Medical Allowance, a newly appointed DPA can expect a starting gross salary in the range of ₹34,000 to ₹38,000 per month.

Growth Opportunities: Beyond the financial rewards, the High Court offers a structured career path. DPAs are eligible for departmental promotions to higher technical grades, such as Computer Programmer or System Analyst, after completing the requisite years of service and passing internal qualifying exams. Employees also enjoy job security and state government pension benefits under the New Pension Scheme (NPS).

Eligibility Criteria

Eligibility is the most critical hurdle. Unlike general clerk roles, the DPA position requires technical academic background and a significant amount of professional experience. Candidates must ensure they meet these criteria before paying the application fee.

  • Educational Qualification: Graduation in B.Sc (Computer Science), BCA, or B.Sc (IT) from a recognized university. Minimum Marks: Candidates must have secured at least 60% aggregate marks in their qualifying degree.
  • Work Experience: Minimum 3 years of experience in Operating Systems, Office Applications, and Data Entry operations. This experience must be from a recognized government organization, PSU, or a reputed private firm.
  • Age Limit (as of Jan 1, 2025): Minimum 18 years and maximum 35 years.
  • Age Relaxation: SC, ST, and OBC candidates from Madhya Pradesh are eligible for an upper age relaxation of up to 40 or 45 years as per the latest state government amendments.

Selection Process

The selection for MP High Court DPAs is designed to test both theoretical IT knowledge and actual practical application. It is a three-stage filter that ensures only technically sound candidates are recruited.

Selection Stage Evaluation Mode Maximum Marks
Stage 1 Online Preliminary Screening (CBT) 100 (Qualifying)
Stage 2 Practical Technical Examination 30 (Merit Deciding)
Stage 3 Personal Interview 30 (Merit Deciding)

Important Note: The Preliminary Screening is purely qualifying. Marks obtained in the screening are not added to the final merit list. The final merit is calculated based on the combined marks of the Practical Exam (30) and the Interview (30), totaling 60 marks. Candidates must score a minimum of 40% in these stages to be considered for the final selection.

Online Preliminary Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The Preliminary exam is an objective-type Computer Based Test (CBT). It is designed to screen out candidates and select those who possess the fundamental knowledge of IT and Computer Science. The exam usually consists of 100 questions to be solved in 120 minutes.

Technical Core Syllabus (80% Weightage):

  • Operating Systems: Concepts of Windows and Linux, Shell commands, File systems, and basic administrative tasks.
  • Networking: OSI Model, TCP/IP protocols, Network hardware (Hubs, Switches, Routers), and basic Network Security.
  • Database Management (DBMS): SQL queries, Normalization, ER diagrams, and basic database administration.
  • Data Structures & Algorithms: Basic concepts of Arrays, Linked Lists, Stacks, Queues, and sorting algorithms.
  • Web Technologies: HTML, CSS, and basic concepts of web hosting and server management.

General Studies (20% Weightage): This includes General English (Grammar and Vocabulary), Basic General Knowledge of Madhya Pradesh, and Current Affairs related to technology and law.

Practical Examination Details

Candidates who clear the preliminary round in a 1:3 ratio will be called for the Practical Examination. This 30-mark test is the true assessment of your "Data Processing" skills. During this stage, you will be expected to perform tasks on a live computer system.

  • Database Queries: Writing SQL queries to retrieve specific data from a given database table.
  • Spreadsheet Management: Using advanced MS Excel formulas (VLOOKUP, Pivot Tables, Macros) for data analysis.
  • Typing and Data Entry: Accurate and fast data entry of judicial records in both English and Hindi (where required).
  • Software Installation: Basic troubleshooting and installation of peripheral devices like printers and scanners.

Preparation Strategy for MPHC DPA Exam

Since the final selection depends on the practical and interview stages, your preparation should be split into academic study and technical practice. Here is an expert-recommended roadmap.

Stage 1: Mastering the Theory: Focus on the core subjects of your BCA/B.Sc IT. Use "Computer Science Distilled" or Lucent's Computer Awareness for a quick revision of terminologies. Ensure you understand the differences between various networking protocols and operating system kernels.

Stage 2: Practical Proficiency: Don't just read about SQL; install MySQL or PostgreSQL on your computer and practice writing complex queries. For data entry, use online typing tutors to increase your words per minute (WPM). Since you have 3 years of experience, leverage that knowledge by practicing troubleshooting scenarios that commonly occur in an office environment.

Stage 3: Interview Readiness: The interview panel usually includes judicial officers and technical experts. Be prepared to answer questions about the e-Courts project in India, digital signatures, and information security laws (IT Act 2000). Your confidence and clarity of thought in explaining technical concepts will be the deciding factor.

Best Books for Preparation

Selecting the right study material is vital for a focused preparation. Below is a list of recommended books for the MP High Court DPA exam.

Subject Area Recommended Books
Computer Science Core Objective Computer Science by Dr. Alok Kumar or Arihant Expert.
DBMS & SQL Database System Concepts by Korth or SQL Practice Books.
Operating Systems Operating System Concepts by Galvin.
English & GK Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi and Lucent's MP GK.
Previous Papers MPHC Solved Papers by various local publications like Ghatna Chakra.

Application Fee and Payment Process

The application fee is mandatory and includes the portal charges of MP Online. The fee varies depending on the domicile and category of the candidate.

  • Unreserved (UR) & All Candidates outside MP: ₹943.40 (Total including portal fees).
  • Reserved Categories (SC, ST, OBC) of MP Domicile: ₹743.40 (Total including portal fees).
  • PwD Candidates of MP Domicile: ₹743.40.

Note: Fees must be paid online via Net Banking, Credit Card, Debit Card, or UPI. Ensure you keep the payment receipt safe, as it contains your transaction ID required for downloading the admit card.

How to Apply Online

The application process is centralized through the MP High Court recruitment portal. Follow these steps for a successful registration.

  1. Visit the official High Court website: www.mphc.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the "Recruitment / Result" button.
  3. Locate the link for DPA Recruitment-2025 and click on "Apply Online."
  4. Register as a new candidate by providing your Mobile Number and Email ID.
  5. Login with your generated ID and fill in all personal, educational, and work experience details.
  6. Upload Scanned Images: Recent color photograph and your signature (as per the specified dimensions).
  7. Pay the application fee through the MP Online gateway.
  8. Check all details in the preview window before the final submission.
  9. Download the final submitted application form and take a printout for the Document Verification stage.

Documents Needed to Apply
