Proper documentation is essential. During the online application, you will need to enter data from these documents, and during document verification, you will need to produce the originals.

  1. Proof of Date of Birth: 10th Standard Passing Certificate or Birth Certificate.
  2. Educational Marksheets: Graduation Degree (BCA/B.Sc CS) and all semester marksheets.
  3. Experience Certificate: Clear certificate proving 3 years of work in IT/Data Entry (on official letterhead).
  4. Domicile Certificate: Issued by MP Government (if seeking reservation/fee concession).
  5. Caste Certificate: Valid certificate for SC/ST/OBC (NCL) candidates.
  6. Identification Proof: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID.

Important Links

Resource Name Direct Link
Official MP High Court Portal www.mphc.gov.in
Recruitment & Results Page Click Here
Online Application Link Apply Now

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can candidates with a general B.Sc (Non-Computer) apply?

No. The eligibility specifically requires a degree in B.Sc (Computer Science), BCA, or B.Sc (IT). Candidates with general degrees are not eligible unless they have the specific IT specialization mentioned in the degree certificate.

Q2: Is the 3-year experience mandatory?

Yes. The High Court requires candidates to have practical work experience in data entry and operating systems for a minimum of 3 years to ensure they can handle the technical workload from day one.

Q3: Can I apply if I am from Rajasthan or Uttar Pradesh?

Yes, candidates from other states can apply. However, they will be treated as General/Unreserved candidates and will not be eligible for age relaxation or fee concessions.

Q4: What is the correction window?

The High Court provides a small window (Nov 24 to Nov 26) where you can log in and correct minor errors in your application form by paying a small correction fee. However, some fields like Name and Category may not be changeable.

Q5: Is there any physical test for this role?

No. Being a technical/administrative role, there is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET). The selection is based entirely on your mental and technical aptitude.

Q6: What is the nature of the DPA job?

It is a permanent technical role. Duties include managing case databases, providing technical support during virtual hearings, maintaining system hardware, and administrative data entry tasks.

entry.title: ONGC अप्रेंटिस भर्ती 2025: पूरे भारत में 2600+ पदों पर बंपर वैकेंसी!
entry.content:
ONGC अप्रेंटिस भर्ती 2025: पूरे भारत में 2600+ पदों पर बंपर वैकेंसी!

ITI, Diploma और Graduates के लिए Maharatna PSU ONGC में Apprentices Act, 1961 के तहत सुनहरा अवसर।

Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC), जो भारत की सबसे बड़ी ‘महानवरत्न’ ऊर्जा कंपनी है, ने अप्रेंटिस पदों के लिए एक विशाल भर्ती अभियान की घोषणा की है। अधिसूचना संख्या ONGC/APPR/1/2025 के तहत पूरे देश के 25 वर्क सेंटर्स में कुल 2623 अप्रेंटिस सीटें भरी जाएंगी। यह ONGC के Skill Building Initiative का हिस्सा है।

यह उन सभी युवाओं के लिए सुनहरा मौका है जिन्होंने ITI, Diploma या Graduation पूरी की है और व्यावहारिक अनुभव प्राप्त करना चाहते हैं। यह एक वर्ष की ट्रेनिंग होगी और चयन पूरी तरह से मेरिट के आधार पर किया जाएगा — कोई परीक्षा या इंटरव्यू नहीं होगा।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
अधिसूचना जारी होने की तिथि16 अक्टूबर 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि06 नवंबर 2025
चयन परिणाम जारी होने की तिथि26 नवंबर 2025

सेक्टरवार रिक्तियों का विवरण

सेक्टरकुल सीटें
नॉर्दर्न सेक्टर (देहरादून, दिल्ली, जोधपुर)165
मुंबई सेक्टर (मुंबई, गोवा, हजीरा, उरण, पनवेल, न्हावा)569
वेस्टर्न सेक्टर (वडोदरा, अहमदाबाद, अंकलेश्वर, कंबाय, मेहसाणा)856
ईस्टर्न सेक्टर (जोरहाट, सिलचर, नाजिरा, शिवसागर)458
सदर्न सेक्टर (चेन्नई, काकीनाडा, राजमुंदरी, कराईकल)322
सेंट्रल सेक्टर (अगरतला, कोलकाता, बोकारो)253
कुल योग2623
नोट: उम्मीदवार केवल एक वर्क सेंटर और एक ही ट्रेड के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं। एक से अधिक आवेदन करने पर फॉर्म रद्द किया जा सकता है।

मासिक स्टाइपेंड विवरण

  • Graduate Apprentice (B.A/B.Com/B.Sc/BBA/B.E/B.Tech): ₹12,300 प्रति माह
  • Diploma Apprentice: ₹10,900 प्रति माह
  • Trade Apprentice (ITI – 2 वर्ष): ₹10,560 प्रति माह
  • Trade Apprentice (ITI – 1 वर्ष): ₹9,600 प्रति माह
  • Trade Apprentice (10वीं/12वीं पास): ₹8,200 प्रति माह

पात्रता मानदंड

मानदंडआवश्यक योग्यता
शैक्षणिक योग्यता
  • Trade Apprentices: 10वीं पास और संबंधित ट्रेड में ITI प्रमाणपत्र।
  • Diploma Apprentices: संबंधित शाखा में इंजीनियरिंग डिप्लोमा।
  • Graduate Apprentices: B.A, B.Com, B.Sc, BBA, B.E या B.Tech में स्नातक।
आयु सीमा06.11.2025 को न्यूनतम 18 वर्ष और अधिकतम 24 वर्ष। SC/ST को 5 वर्ष और OBC को 3 वर्ष की छूट।
निवासउम्मीदवार केवल उसी वर्क सेंटर के लिए पात्र है जो उसके डोमिसाइल जिले के अंतर्गत आता हो या जहाँ से उसने अपनी योग्यता प्राप्त की हो।

चयन प्रक्रिया

ONGC अप्रेंटिस चयन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह पारदर्शी और मेरिट आधारित है।

  • चयन योग्यता परीक्षा (ITI/Diploma/Graduation) में प्राप्त अंकों के आधार पर होगा।
  • अगर दो उम्मीदवारों के अंक समान हों तो अधिक उम्र वाले को प्राथमिकता दी जाएगी।
  • कोई परीक्षा या इंटरव्यू नहीं लिया जाएगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. स्टेप 1: रजिस्ट्रेशन करें
    • ITI ट्रेड्स के लिए NAPS Portal पर जाएँ: https://apprenticeshipindia.gov.in
    • Diploma/Graduate ट्रेड्स के लिए NATS Portal पर जाएँ: https://nats.education.gov.in
    • रजिस्ट्रेशन के बाद प्राप्त यूनिक नंबर नोट करें।
  2. स्टेप 2: ONGC पोर्टल पर आवेदन करें
    • ONGC की आधिकारिक साइट पर जाएँ: www.ongcapprentices.ongc.co.in
    • फॉर्म भरें, अपना NAPS/NATS रजिस्ट्रेशन नंबर दर्ज करें और डॉक्यूमेंट्स अपलोड करें।
    • वर्क सेंटर और ट्रेड सावधानीपूर्वक चुनें और आवेदन सबमिट करें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकलिंक
Apply Online (ONGC Portal)यहाँ क्लिक करें
NAPS Portal (ITI Trades)यहाँ क्लिक करें
NATS Portal (Graduate/Diploma)यहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?
नहीं, यह एक वर्ष की अप्रेंटिसशिप ट्रेनिंग है। ट्रेनिंग पूरी होने पर नियमित नौकरी की गारंटी नहीं है।
प्रश्न 2: क्या चयन के लिए परीक्षा होगी?
नहीं, चयन केवल मेरिट के आधार पर किया जाएगा। कोई परीक्षा या इंटरव्यू नहीं है।
प्रश्न 3: क्या अन्य राज्य के उम्मीदवार आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
हाँ, यदि वे उस वर्क सेंटर के पात्र जिलों के निवासी हैं या वहीं से पढ़ाई की है।
प्रश्न 4: क्या कोई आवेदन शुल्क है?
नहीं, NAPS/NATS और ONGC दोनों पोर्टल पर आवेदन निःशुल्क है।

entry.title: CSIR IIIM भर्ती 2025: जूनियर हिंदी ट्रांसलेटर और स्टेनोग्राफर के पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
CSIR IIIM भर्ती 2025: जूनियर हिंदी ट्रांसलेटर और स्टेनोग्राफर के पदों पर भर्ती!

12वीं पास और मास्टर डिग्री धारकों के लिए जम्मू में सरकारी नौकरी का सुनहरा मौका।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! यदि आप सरकारी नौकरी की तलाश में हैं, तो यह आपके लिए बेहतरीन अवसर है। CSIR - Indian Institute of Integrative Medicine (IIIM), जम्मू — जो Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) के अंतर्गत एक प्रमुख संस्थान है — ने गैर-शैक्षणिक पदों पर भर्ती के लिए आधिकारिक अधिसूचना (Advertisement No. 04R/2025) जारी की है।

इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत Junior Hindi Translator (Group B) और Junior Stenographer (Group C) पदों पर नियुक्तियाँ की जाएँगी। ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया 15 अक्टूबर 2025 से शुरू होकर 13 नवम्बर 2025 तक चलेगी। नीचे पूरी जानकारी दी गई है — पात्रता, वेतन, चयन प्रक्रिया, परीक्षा पैटर्न आदि।

CSIR IIIM रिक्तियाँ 2025

CSIR-IIIM ने दो पदों के लिए कुल 04 रिक्तियाँ घोषित की हैं।

Post Codeपद नामकुल पदआरक्षण
JHT-01Junior Hindi Translator01UR
JST-02Junior Stenographer03UR-02, EWS-01
कुल योग04-

CSIR IIIM वेतनमान 2025

चयनित उम्मीदवारों को 7वें वेतन आयोग के अनुसार आकर्षक वेतन दिया जाएगा। जम्मू (Class 'Y' City) के अनुसार Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), और Transport Allowance (TA) भी मिलेगा।

पद नामवेतन स्तरवेतनमान (Basic Pay)
Junior Hindi TranslatorLevel 6₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400
Junior StenographerLevel 4₹25,500 – ₹81,100

CSIR IIIM पात्रता मापदंड 2025

दोनों पदों के लिए शैक्षणिक योग्यता और आयु सीमा अलग-अलग निर्धारित की गई है।

पद नामअधिकतम आयुशैक्षणिक योग्यता
Junior Hindi Translator30 वर्षहिंदी या अंग्रेज़ी विषय में मास्टर डिग्री, जिसमें एक विषय वैकल्पिक/अनिवार्य रूप में हो। साथ ही
अनुवाद में मान्यता प्राप्त डिप्लोमा/प्रमाणपत्र या 2 वर्ष का अनुभव।
Junior Stenographer27 वर्ष12वीं पास (10+2) या समकक्ष योग्यता और 80 शब्द प्रति मिनट की शॉर्टहैंड दक्षता।
आयु में छूट: SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen उम्मीदवारों को सरकार के नियमों के अनुसार छूट दी जाएगी।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि व समय
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ15 अक्टूबर 2025 (सुबह 10:00 बजे)
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि13 नवम्बर 2025 (रात 09:59 बजे)
आवेदन शुल्क:
UR / EWS / OBC: ₹500
SC / ST / PwBD / Women / Ex-Servicemen / Regular CSIR Employees — शुल्क से मुक्त।

CSIR IIIM चयन प्रक्रिया 2025

चयन प्रक्रिया में लिखित परीक्षा और कौशल परीक्षा (Skill Test) दोनों शामिल होंगे।

पद नामचयन चरण
Junior Hindi Translator• Paper-I (Objective – केवल योग्यता हेतु)
• Paper-II (Descriptive – मेरिट हेतु)
Junior Stenographer• शॉर्टहैंड दक्षता परीक्षा (80 w.p.m.)
• ऑब्जेक्टिव टाइप लिखित परीक्षा (मेरिट हेतु)

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

आवेदन केवल ऑनलाइन माध्यम से स्वीकार किए जाएँगे। प्रक्रिया दो चरणों में होगी: शुल्क भुगतान और पंजीकरण।

  1. चरण 1: SBI Collect के माध्यम से ₹500 का भुगतान करें (यदि लागू हो)। Payment Category में ‘APPLICATION FEE - Advt. 04R/2025’ चुनें।
  2. चरण 2: आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://recruit.iiim.res.in या https://iiim.res.in पर जाएँ।
  3. चरण 3: अपनी ईमेल आईडी से रजिस्टर करें और वेरिफिकेशन लिंक के ज़रिए खाता सक्रिय करें।
  4. चरण 4: लॉगिन कर व्यक्तिगत, शैक्षणिक और अनुभव संबंधी विवरण भरें तथा शुल्क जानकारी जोड़ें।
  5. चरण 5: फोटो, हस्ताक्षर और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें।
  6. चरण 6: आवेदन ‘Final Submit’ करें और प्रिंटआउट लेकर सुरक्षित रखें। हार्ड कॉपी भेजने की आवश्यकता नहीं है।

आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

  • आवेदन शुल्क भुगतान की ई-रसीद
  • हाल ही का पासपोर्ट आकार का फोटो (JPEG)
  • हस्ताक्षर (JPEG)
  • 10वीं प्रमाणपत्र (जन्म तिथि हेतु)
  • 12वीं, स्नातक और स्नातकोत्तर अंकों की प्रतियाँ
  • मान्य वर्ग प्रमाणपत्र (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD) यदि लागू हो
  • सेवारत उम्मीदवारों के लिए NOC

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करें (15.10.2025 से सक्रिय)यहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक अधिसूचना PDFयहाँ क्लिक करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 13 नवम्बर 2025 (रात 09:59 बजे) है।

प्रश्न 2: क्या आवेदन शुल्क देना होगा?

हाँ, General/OBC/EWS उम्मीदवारों को ₹500 देना होगा। जबकि SC, ST, PwBD, Women, Ex-Servicemen और Regular CSIR कर्मचारियों को शुल्क से छूट है।

प्रश्न 3: चयन प्रक्रिया क्या है?

Junior Hindi Translator के लिए दो-स्तरीय लिखित परीक्षा (Paper-I और Paper-II) तथा Junior Stenographer के लिए शॉर्टहैंड टेस्ट और ऑब्जेक्टिव टाइप परीक्षा होगी।

entry.title: APSRTC Recruitment 2025: Apply Online for 297 ITI Apprentice Posts
entry.content:
APSRTC Recruitment 2025: 297 ITI Apprentice Posts in 7 Districts, Apply Online

A golden opportunity for ITI pass-outs to gain practical skills. The online application window is open from November 15 to November 30, 2025.

Post Name: ITI Trade Apprentice (Diesel Mechanic, Motor Mechanic, Electrician, Welder, etc.)
Last Date: 30/11/2025
Total Posts: 297

The Andhra Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation (APSRTC), through its Zonal Staff Training College in Vijayawada, has released a significant recruitment notification. This recruitment drive is being conducted to provide training to ITI-passed candidates in various technical trades under the Apprenticeship Act. This is a golden opportunity for young individuals who wish to kickstart their careers by acquiring practical skills in a prestigious state organization.

Only candidates who have passed their ITI from colleges located in the districts of NTR, Krishna, West Godavari, Eluru, Guntur, Bapatla, and Palnadu are eligible to apply. The application process is entirely online, and candidates must apply through the Government of India's official Apprenticeship portal: www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. The online application window is open from November 15, 2025, to November 30, 2025. In this article, we will provide you with all the essential information regarding this recruitment, including vacancy details, eligibility, selection process, and how to apply.

APSRTC Apprentice Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a concise overview of the APSRTC Apprentice Recruitment 2025. Candidates should carefully note the key details to ensure a smooth application process. This is a government apprenticeship opportunity located across seven specified districts in Andhra Pradesh.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Authority Andhra Pradesh State Road Transport Corporation (APSRTC)
Post Name ITI Trade Apprentice
Total Vacancies 297
Application Start Date 15th November 2025
Last Date to Apply Online 30th November 2025
Job Type Government Apprenticeship
Job Location NTR, Krishna, W. Godavari, Eluru, Guntur, Bapatla, Palnadu districts

Vacancy Details: District and Trade-wise Breakdown

APSRTC has announced a total of 297 apprentice positions across various technical trades in 7 districts. The detailed breakdown of vacancies by district and trade is provided in the table below:

District Diesel Mechanic Motor Mechanic Electrician Welder Painter Machinist Fitter Draughtsman (Civil) Total Posts
Krishna 26 1 7 1 0 0 2 1 38
NTR 56 1 17 2 1 3 6 1 87
Guntur 28 1 8 1 1 0 2 0 41
Bapatla 16 0 4 0 0 0 2 0 22
Palnadu 30 1 8 1 0 0 3 1 44
Eluru 20 1 6 0 1 0 2 0 30
West Godavari 20 1 6 1 0 0 1 0 29
Grand Total 196 6 56 6 3 3 18 3 297
Note: Selected apprentices will receive a monthly stipend as per the rules of the Apprenticeship Act. The exact stipend amount will be communicated at the time of document verification or in the appointment letter.

Application and Processing Fee

  • Online Application Fee: ₹0/- (There is no fee for submitting the online application).
  • Processing Fee: Candidates who are shortlisted and called for document verification will be required to pay a processing fee of ₹118/- (₹100 + 18% GST).

This fee is non-refundable and must be paid at the time of the verification process at the designated center.

Eligibility Criteria for APSRTC Apprentice Recruitment

Candidates must ensure they meet the following educational and regional eligibility requirements before applying for the apprenticeship program.

Criteria Requirement Details
Educational Qualification
  • Must have passed the 10th Class (SSC) or its equivalent examination from a recognized board.
  • Must possess an ITI certificate in the relevant trade, recognized by the National Council for Vocational Training (NCVT).
Regional Eligibility Candidates must have passed their ITI course from colleges located exclusively within the districts of NTR, Krishna, West Godavari, Eluru, Guntur, Bapatla, and Palnadu. Candidates from other districts are not eligible.

Selection Process

The selection process for the APSRTC Apprentice recruitment is straightforward and merit-based. There will be no written examination. The selection will be conducted in the following stages:

  1. Online Application: Eligible candidates must register and apply on the Apprenticeship India portal within the specified dates.
  2. Merit List Preparation: A merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained by the candidates in their respective ITI trades.
  3. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates from the merit list will be called for document verification at the Zonal Staff Training College, Vijayawada. The dates for verification will be announced later in newspapers and on the official APSRTC website.
  4. Final Selection: Candidates who successfully clear the document verification process will be considered for the final selection for apprenticeship training.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

There is no written examination for this apprenticeship recruitment. The selection is purely based on the merit of the marks secured in the ITI course. Therefore, candidates do not need to prepare for any exam or syllabus. The focus should be on ensuring the application is filled correctly and all required documents are in order for verification.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

No Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) is required for this recruitment. The selection is based on academic merit, not physical standards.

How to Prepare for the Application Process

While there's no test to prepare for, careful preparation is needed for the online application and document verification stages to avoid rejection:

  1. Understand the Portal: Familiarize yourself with the Apprenticeship India portal (www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in) before starting the application.
  2. Complete Your Profile: Ensure your profile on the portal is 100% complete. This includes mandatory Aadhaar e-KYC, and correctly entering all educational details.
  3. Organize Your Documents: Scan and keep digital copies of all required documents ready. The 10th certificate, ITI marks memo, and NCVT certificate must be merged into a single PDF file for uploading.
  4. Keep Originals Ready: Arrange all original documents and a set of photocopies in a folder, ready for the verification stage.

Best Books for Preparation

Since there is no written examination, no specific preparation books are required. Candidates are advised to focus on the application process itself and ensure all their academic documents and certificates are authentic and readily available.

No books are required for preparation as the selection is merit-based.

How to Apply Online Step-by-Step

The application process is entirely online through the Apprenticeship India portal. Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Portal Registration:
    • Visit the official portal: www.apprenticeshipindia.gov.in.
    • On the homepage, click on "Register" and then select the "Candidate" option.
    • Complete the registration by entering your personal details like name, contact number, and email ID. You will receive a registration number (e.g., A0320541687).
    • Activate your account by clicking the activation link sent to your registered email.
  2. Complete Your Profile to 100%:
    • Log in with your registration number and password.
    • It is mandatory to complete the Aadhaar e-KYC in your profile.
    • Go to the "Education Qualification" section and enter your 10th and ITI details. Merge your 10th certificate, ITI marks memo, and NCVT certificate into a single PDF file and upload it.
    • Ensure your profile completion status shows 100%.
  3. Apply for APSRTC Apprenticeship:
    • On your dashboard, click on "Apprentice Opportunities."
    • Go to "Search by Establishment Name" and search for the APSRTC establishment according to your district (e.g., APSRTC NTR, APSRTC KRISHNA).
    • Select your ITI trade and click the "Apply" button.
    • A "SUCCESSFULLY APPLIED" message will confirm your application.

Documents Needed to Apply and for Verification

Shortlisted candidates must bring the following original documents along with one set of photocopies for verification:

  1. Printout of the registration profile from the Apprenticeship India portal.
  2. 10th Class (SSC) Marks Memo.
  3. ITI Consolidated Marks Memo.
  4. NCVT Certificate.
  5. Caste Certificate (for SC/ST/BC candidates).
  6. Disability Certificate (if applicable).
  7. Certificate for children of Ex-servicemen (if applicable).
  8. NCC/Sports Certificates (if applicable).
  9. Aadhaar Card.
  10. PAN Card / Driving License.
  11. Two recent passport-size photographs.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
APSRTC Official Website Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Can candidates from all districts of Andhra Pradesh apply?

No, only candidates who have completed their ITI from colleges located in NTR, Krishna, West Godavari, Eluru, Guntur, Bapatla, and Palnadu districts are eligible to apply.

Question 2: What is the selection process? Will there be an exam?

No, there will be no written examination. The selection is based purely on the merit list prepared from the marks obtained in the ITI course, followed by document verification.

Question 3: What is the last date to apply?

The last date to submit your application online is November 30, 2025.

Question 4: What is the most important step while updating my profile on the apprenticeship portal?

You must complete your Aadhaar e-KYC and correctly enter both your 10th and ITI qualification details. It is mandatory to merge the 10th certificate, ITI marksheet, and NCVT certificate into a single PDF file for uploading.

entry.title: India Post GDS Recruitment 2026 for BPM, ABPM & Dak Sevak Posts
entry.content:
India Post GDS Recruitment 2026 for BPM, ABPM & Dak Sevak Posts

Notification No: 17-37/2025-GDS (Pt.I): The Department of Posts, Government of India, has invited online applications for engagement as Gramin Dak Sevaks (GDS). This recruitment drive is for the posts of Branch Postmaster (BPM), Assistant Branch Postmaster (ABPM), and Dak Sevak across various postal circles in India. The online application submission will be active from February 02, 2026, to February 16, 2026.

Post Name: Gramin Dak Sevak (BPM, ABPM, Dak Sevak)
Last Date: 16 February 2026
Total Posts: Not Specified (Circle/Division-wise in Annexure-I)

The Department of Posts has announced its first schedule for the online engagement of Gramin Dak Sevaks (GDS) for the year 2026. This is a massive, nationwide opportunity for candidates who have passed their 10th standard to join the postal network in their local areas. The GDS cadre includes Branch Postmasters (BPM), who manage the Branch Post Offices; Assistant Branch Postmasters (ABPM), who assist in operations and sales; and Dak Sevaks, who are involved in mail delivery and other departmental duties.

A key feature of the GDS engagement is its unique selection process, which is completely merit-based and does not involve any written examination or interview. Candidates are shortlisted based on a system-generated merit list derived from the marks obtained in their 10th standard examination. This makes it a highly accessible opportunity for candidates with a strong academic record. It is important to note that GDS are not regular government employees but are holders of Civil Posts outside the regular civil services, with their emoluments paid in the form of a Time Related Continuity Allowance (TRCA).

India Post GDS Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the schedule and instructions. The entire process, from registration to application, is online.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Department of Posts, Ministry of Communication, Govt. of India
Post Names Gramin Dak Sevak (BPM, ABPM, Dak Sevak)
Total Vacancies To be announced (Detailed circle-wise list in Annexure-I)
Online One-Time Registration 31 January to 14 February 2026
Online Application & Fee Payment 02 February to 16 February 2026 (up to 17:00 Hrs)
Edit/Correction Window 18 February to 19 February 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Not a regular Govt. job (Civil Post outside regular services)
Job Location Various Postal Circles/Divisions across India
Official Website indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement

Vacancy and Emoluments (TRCA) Details

The number of vacancies is extensive and distributed across all postal circles in India. The detailed circle-wise, division-wise, and post-wise vacancy list will be available in Annexure-I on the official portal. GDS are paid a Time Related Continuity Allowance (TRCA) instead of a regular salary.

Category TRCA Slab
Branch Postmaster (BPM) ₹12,000/- to ₹29,380/-
Assistant Branch Postmaster (ABPM) / Dak Sevak ₹10,000/- to ₹24,470/-
Note: GDS are not regular government employees and their emoluments are not at par with central government pay scales. They are, however, entitled to Dearness Allowance on the TRCA and other benefits as per GDS Rules.

Application Fee

  • For all male applicants of UR, OBC, and EWS categories: ₹100/-
  • For all Female applicants, SC/ST applicants, PwBD applicants, and Transwomen applicants: NIL (No Fee)

The fee must be paid online. Fee once paid will not be refunded.

Eligibility Criteria (as on the date of notification)

Candidates must ensure they fulfill all eligibility conditions regarding education, age, and other qualifications.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification
  1. Must have passed Secondary School Examination (10th standard) with passing marks in Mathematics and English from any recognized Board of School Education.
  2. The applicant must have studied the local language of the respective postal circle at least up to the 10th standard.
Other Qualifications
  • Knowledge of Computers.
  • Knowledge of Cycling.
  • Adequate means of livelihood: The candidate must have another source of income to support their family, as the GDS allowance alone may not be sufficient.
Age Limit (as on the last date of application)
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 40 Years
Age Relaxation: Upper age limit is relaxable by 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC, and 10-15 years for PwBD candidates as per rules. There is no age relaxation for EWS candidates.

Selection Process

The selection process for Gramin Dak Sevaks is unique and does not involve any competitive examination. The selection is based purely on merit.

  1. System-Generated Merit List: Applicants will be shortlisted based on a merit list automatically generated by the online system.
  2. Basis of Merit: The merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the 10th standard examination. The system will aggregate the marks to a percentage with an accuracy of up to four decimal places. For boards that award grades/points, the marks will be calculated by the system using a multiplication factor of 9.5.
  3. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates will be called for physical verification of their original documents at the respective Divisional/Unit Head's office.
  4. Final Engagement: An 'Offer of provisional engagement' will be issued immediately after successful document verification.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for the GDS engagement process. Selection is made exclusively based on the merit of marks obtained in the 10th standard. Therefore, there is no exam pattern or syllabus to prepare for.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) for the GDS posts. However, candidates must declare that they have knowledge of cycling, which is considered essential for performing the duties of a GDS.

How to Prepare for Selection

Since the selection is merit-based, no exam preparation is needed. The key is to ensure an accurate and flawless application.

  • Accurate Marks Entry: This is the most critical step. You must enter your 10th standard marks/grades exactly as they appear on your mark sheet. Any error can lead to the cancellation of your candidature during document verification.
  • One Registration Only: A candidate is allowed only one registration. Submitting multiple or duplicate applications is strictly prohibited and will lead to disqualification.
  • Document Readiness: Although documents are not uploaded during the initial application (except for photo and signature), you must have all original documents (10th mark sheet, community certificate, PwBD certificate, etc.) ready for physical verification.
  • Preference Selection: After selecting a Division, you will see a list of eligible posts. You must carefully give your preferences. You will only be considered for the posts for which you provide a preference.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no written test, no preparation books are required. Your 10th-grade performance is the sole selection criterion.

Candidates should focus on carefully reading the detailed notification and instructions to ensure they fill the application form correctly.

How to Apply Online

The application is a two-part process: One-Time Registration and Application Submission.

  1. Part 1 (One-Time Registration): Visit the official portal: https://indiapost.gov.in/gdsonlineengagement. Click on "New Candidate Registration" and complete the OTR process by providing your mobile number, email, and other personal details. You will receive a unique Registration Number.
  2. Part 2 (Application Submission): Log in to the portal using your Registration Number. The application submission and fee payment window is from February 02 to February 16, 2026.
  3. Fill out the online application form with your educational details, and upload your recent photograph and signature.
  4. Select your preferred Division and give your preferences for the vacant posts listed.
  5. Pay the application fee of ₹100 online (if applicable).
  6. Preview your application carefully before final submission.
  7. Download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

During the online application, only your photograph and signature need to be uploaded.

  1. Recent Photograph: JPG/JPEG format, size between 30 KB to 100 KB.
  2. Signature: JPG/JPEG format, size between 20 KB to 100 KB.

However, you must have all original documents ready for physical verification if you are shortlisted.

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Download from Official Website

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there a written exam for the GDS selection?

No, there is no written examination. The selection is based purely on the merit of marks obtained in the 10th standard.

Q2: What is the salary of a Gramin Dak Sevak?

GDS are not paid a regular salary. They receive a Time Related Continuity Allowance (TRCA). The TRCA slab for a Branch Postmaster (BPM) starts at ₹12,000, and for an ABPM/Dak Sevak, it starts at ₹10,000.

Q3: I am still awaiting my 10th class results. Can I apply?

No, candidates must have already passed their 10th standard examination as of the date of notification to be eligible.

Q4: Can I apply for GDS posts in more than one state or circle?

No, a candidate can only register once and can only apply for vacant posts in one selected Division. Applying for multiple divisions is not allowed.

entry.title: Gujarat Police Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for 13,591 PSI and Lokrakshak Posts
entry.content:
Gujarat Police Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for 13,591 PSI and Lokrakshak Posts

A golden opportunity for graduates and 12th-pass candidates to join the Gujarat Police Force. Online applications open from December 3 to December 23, 2025.

Post Name: Police Sub-Inspector (PSI), Constable, Jail Sepoy (Lokrakshak)
Last Date: 23/12/2025
Total Posts: 13,591

The Gujarat Police Recruitment Board (GPRB) has unveiled a massive and comprehensive recruitment notification (Advertisement No: GPRB/202526/1) for direct recruitment to Class-3 posts within the Gujarat State Police Force. This extensive recruitment drive aims to fill a staggering total of 13,591 vacancies in the Police Sub-Inspector (PSI) Cadre and the Lokrakshak (Constable/Jail Sepoy) Cadre. This presents a golden opportunity for the qualified and energetic youth of Gujarat who dream of joining the police force and serving the state and its society.

This recruitment encompasses a wide variety of roles, including Unarmed PSI, Armed PSI, Jailor, Unarmed Constable, Armed Constable, SRPF Constable, and Jail Sepoy (Male and Female). It offers opportunities for candidates with two different educational qualifications: a graduate degree for the PSI Cadre and a 12th-pass certificate for the Lokrakshak Cadre. The application process is entirely online through the OJAS (Online Job Application System) portal, scheduled from December 3, 2025, to December 23, 2025. This article provides a detailed breakdown of everything you need to know about this recruitment, from post-wise vacancies and eligibility criteria to the selection process and application procedure.

Gujarat Police Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick glance at the essential details of this mega recruitment drive. Aspirants are advised to take note of these key points to stay informed.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Gujarat Police Recruitment Board (GPRB)
Advertisement Number GPRB/202526/1
Post Names Police Sub-Inspector (PSI) and Lokrakshak (Constable/Jail Sepoy)
Total Vacancies 13,591
Job Location Gujarat
Application Mode Online (via OJAS Portal)
Online Application Dates December 3, 2025, to December 23, 2025

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

A total of 13,591 posts will be filled through this recruitment drive. These vacancies are systematically distributed across various cadres, posts, and reservation categories.

Sr. No. Post Name Total Posts Unreserved (M/F) EWS (M/F) SEBC (M/F) SC (M/F) ST (M/F)
1 Unarmed PSI 659 274 (91) 65 (21) 177 (58) 45 (15) 98 (32)
2 Armed PSI (Platoon Commander) 129 50 18 33 10 18
3 Jailor, Group-2 (Male) 70 31 7 18 4 10
4 Unarmed Constable 6942 2908 (960) 722 (238) 1792 (591) 478 (158) 1042 (344)
5 Armed Constable 2458 1132 (374) 301 (99) 498 (164) 158 (52) 369 (122)
6 Jail Sepoy (Male) 300 123 30 81 21 45
7 Jail Sepoy (Female) 31 13 3 8 2 5
8 Armed Constable (SRPF) 3002 1232 300 810 210 450
Grand Total 13,591 5763 (1425) 1446 (358) 3417 (813) 928 (225) 2037 (498)
Note: The numbers in parentheses indicate posts reserved for women. A total of 1359 posts (10% of total vacancies) are reserved for Ex-Servicemen.

Application Fee Details

  • General Category Candidates:
    • For PSI Cadre only: ₹ 100/-
    • For Lokrakshak Cadre only: ₹ 100/-
    • For Both Cadres (PSI + Lokrakshak): ₹ 200/-
  • EWS, SC, ST, SEBC, and Ex-Servicemen Candidates: No Fee

Candidates are required to pay the applicable bank service charges in addition to the application fee. The fee must be paid through the online portal.

Eligibility Criteria: Education, Age, and Physical Standards

The recruitment board has set detailed eligibility criteria for candidates aspiring to join the force. These criteria cover educational qualifications, age limits, and physical standards.

Educational Qualification

Cadre Minimum Educational Qualification
PSI Cadre A Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognized university.
Lokrakshak Cadre Must have passed the 12th Class (HSC) or an equivalent examination from a recognized board.

Age Limit (as of 23/12/2025)

Cadre Minimum Age Maximum Age (General Category)
PSI Cadre 21 years 35 years
Lokrakshak Cadre 18 years 33 years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

  • General Category Women: 5 years
  • Reserved Category Men (EWS, SEBC, SC, ST): 5 years
  • Reserved Category Women: 10 years
  • Sportspersons: 5 years
  • Note: The maximum age limit for any candidate applying for the PSI cadre, after all relaxations, shall not exceed 45 years.

Physical Standards

For Male Candidates:

Category Minimum Height Chest (Unexpanded) Chest (Expanded)
Scheduled Tribes (ST) of Gujarat Domicile 162 cm 79 cm 84 cm
All Others 165 cm 79 cm 84 cm

For Female Candidates:

Category Minimum Height Minimum Weight
Scheduled Tribes (ST) of Gujarat Domicile 150 cm 40 kg
All Others 155 cm 40 kg

Selection Process

The selection process for both cadres is distinct but begins with a common physical test. Candidates applying for both cadres will undergo the physical test only once.

Stage 1: Physical Test (Qualifying for All Candidates)

  1. Physical Efficiency Test (PET): This test assesses the physical endurance of the candidates.
    • Male Candidates: 5000 meters run to be completed within 25 minutes.
    • Female Candidates: 1600 meters run to be completed within 9 minutes and 30 seconds.
    • Ex-Servicemen: 2400 meters run to be completed within 12 minutes and 30 seconds.
  2. Physical Standard Test (PST): Candidates who qualify in the PET will have their height, chest (for males), and weight (for females) measured to ensure they meet the prescribed standards.

Stage 2: Written Examination

Candidates who clear the physical tests will proceed to the written examination, which has different patterns for each cadre.

For PSI Cadre (Main Examination - 300 Marks):

  • Paper 1 (MCQ - 200 Marks): This paper will test general knowledge and aptitude.
    • Part A: General Studies (100 Marks)
    • Part B: Reasoning & Quantitative Aptitude (100 Marks)
  • Paper 2 (Descriptive - 100 Marks): This paper will assess language skills.
    • Part A: Gujarati Language Skills (60 Marks)
    • Part B: English Language Skills (40 Marks)

For Lokrakshak Cadre (MCQ Test - 200 Marks):

  • Part A (80 Marks): Reasoning Ability, Quantitative Aptitude, and Gujarati Language Comprehension.
  • Part B (120 Marks): Constitution of India, Current Affairs, Science & Technology, General Knowledge, History, Geography, and Cultural Heritage of Gujarat and India.
Bonus Marks Provision: In both cadres, additional weightage (bonus marks) will be awarded to candidates holding an NCC 'C' Certificate, a degree/diploma from Rashtriya Raksha University (RRU) or National Forensic Sciences University (NFSU), for sports achievements at recognized levels, and to widow candidates, as per government rules.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus in Detail

The written examination is the most critical stage of the selection process. A clear understanding of the exam pattern is essential for effective preparation. Negative Marking: There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each incorrect answer in all MCQ-based tests.

Syllabus for PSI Cadre Main Exam

  • General Studies: History of India & Gujarat, Geography, Art & Culture, Indian Constitution & Polity, General Science, Environment, Information & Communication Technology, Current Affairs of national and international importance.
  • Reasoning & Quantitative Aptitude: Logical reasoning, analytical ability, data interpretation, basic numeracy (Class 10 level).
  • Gujarati & English: Essay writing, precis writing, comprehension, translation, grammar, and vocabulary.

Syllabus for Lokrakshak Cadre MCQ Test

  • Reasoning & Aptitude: Similar to the PSI syllabus but at a 12th-grade level.
  • Gujarati Comprehension: Testing understanding of Gujarati prose and grammar.
  • Part-B Subjects: In-depth knowledge of the Indian Constitution, recent government schemes, major events, scientific developments, Indian history (with a focus on the freedom struggle), and the geography and cultural heritage of Gujarat.

How to Prepare for Physical and Written Tests

A dual-pronged strategy is required to clear both the physical and written hurdles of this recruitment.

Preparation for Physical Tests (PET & PST)

  • Build Stamina: Start with jogging and gradually increase your running distance and pace. Aim to complete the required distance well within the time limit.
  • Practice Regularly: Consistency is key. Practice running at least 4-5 times a week.
  • Monitor Health: Maintain a balanced diet, stay hydrated, and get adequate rest to avoid injuries. Ensure you meet the height, weight, and chest criteria.

Preparation for Written Examinations

  • Create a Study Plan: Divide the syllabus into manageable sections and allocate time for each subject daily.
  • Focus on Fundamentals: Strengthen your basics in Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, and Grammar.
  • Stay Updated: Regularly read newspapers and magazines to keep abreast of current affairs.
  • Solve Previous Year Papers: This will help you understand the exam pattern and the types of questions asked.
  • Take Mock Tests: Regularly attempt mock tests to improve your speed, accuracy, and time management skills.

Best Books for Preparation

While numerous resources are available, here are some standard and highly recommended books for preparation:

  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Reasoning Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" is a comprehensive resource for static GK.
  • Indian Constitution: "Indian Polity" by M. Laxmikanth (for in-depth study for PSI).
  • Gujarat Specific GK: Publications from Yuva Upanishad or Liberty Publications are popular for Gujarat-related topics.
  • Gujarati Language: Standard grammar books for Class 10 and 12 (GSEB curriculum).

How to Apply Online for Gujarat Police Recruitment

The application process is to be completed online through the OJAS portal. Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Visit the official OJAS website: https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in.
  2. Navigate to the "Current Advertisement" section and find the GPRB advertisement (GPRB/202526/1). Click on "Apply".
  3. If you are a new user, complete the "New Registration" process. Existing users can log in with their credentials.
  4. Fill out the application form with accurate personal, educational, and contact details.
  5. You will be given an option to apply for "PSI Cadre only," "Lokrakshak Cadre only," or "Both." Choose the option as per your eligibility and preference.
  6. Upload a scanned copy of your recent photograph (15 KB) and signature (15 KB) in JPG format.
  7. Review the filled application form carefully and then click on "Confirm" to submit it. Note down the confirmation number generated.
  8. If applicable, proceed to pay the examination fee through the online payment gateway.
  9. Take a printout of the final confirmed application form for your future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

While filling the online form, keep the following information and scanned documents ready:

  1. Valid Mobile Number and Email ID.
  2. Scanned Photograph and Signature (as per specified size).
  3. Details from your 10th, 12th, and Graduation mark sheets.
  4. Caste Certificate (EWS/SEBC/SC/ST) number, if applicable.
  5. Details of any additional qualifications (NCC, RRU/NFSU, Sports) for claiming bonus marks.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Official Notification PDF Click Here
Apply Online (OJAS Portal) Click Here
Gujarat Police Recruitment Board Website Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Can I apply for both PSI and Lokrakshak posts?

Yes, if you meet the educational eligibility for both posts (i.e., you are a graduate), you can apply for both cadres in a single application by selecting the "Both (PSI and Lokrakshak Cadre)" option in the online form.

Question 2: Is the physical test the same for both cadres?

Yes, the Physical Efficiency Test (running) and Physical Standard Test are common for both cadres. If you apply for both, you only need to appear for the physical test once.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the written exam?

Yes, there is a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the MCQ-based examinations for both cadres.

Question 4: Who is eligible for the bonus marks?

Bonus marks will be awarded to candidates with an NCC 'C' Certificate, degrees/diplomas from RRU/NFSU, specified sports certificates, and to widow candidates, according to the rules detailed in the official notification.

Question 5: Can candidates from other states apply?

While the official notification will provide final clarity, typically in Gujarat Police recruitments, benefits of reservation and age relaxation are only available to candidates who are domiciled in Gujarat.

entry.title: HPPSC Medical Officer Recruitment 2025: Apply for 232 Vacancies in Himachal Pradesh
entry.content:
HPPSC Medical Officer Recruitment 2025: Mega Drive for 232 Group-A Vacancies Out

Join the Himachal Pradesh Health and Family Welfare Department – Apply Online for Medical Officer (General Wing) Job Trainee Positions

Post Name: Medical Officer (General Wing) - Group A
Last Date: December 12, 2025
Total Posts: 232 Vacancies

The Himachal Pradesh Public Service Commission (HPPSC) has officially released one of the most anticipated recruitment notifications for the medical fraternity in the state for the year 2025. Functioning as the premier constitutional body for civil service recruitments in the hill state, HPPSC has invited applications for 232 vacancies for the post of Medical Officer (General Wing), Group-A. This recruitment drive is conducted on behalf of the Department of Health and Family Welfare, Government of Himachal Pradesh. The primary objective of this massive drive is to strengthen the healthcare infrastructure in rural and semi-urban areas of Himachal Pradesh, ensuring that every citizen has access to qualified medical professionals. The positions are being filled under the "Job Trainee" category, which provides a structured pathway into the state's permanent medical service.

Working as a Medical Officer in Himachal Pradesh is a unique and noble challenge. Given the state's geographical diversity, from the low-lying plains of Una to the high-altitude terrains of Lahaul and Spiti, a Medical Officer plays a pivotal role in community health, emergency response, and the implementation of national health programs. The HPPSC Recruitment 2025 (Advertisement No. 45/11-2025) offers a stable government career with the prestige of a Group-A officer rank. For MBBS graduates who have completed their mandatory internship and are registered with the Medical Council, this is an unparalleled opportunity to serve the "Devbhoomi" (Land of Gods). The application window is currently active through the Online Recruitment Application (ORA) portal and will remain open until December 12, 2025.

The selection process for these 232 vacancies is designed to be comprehensive, ensuring that only the most qualified and dedicated doctors join the state service. It involves a multi-stage evaluation including a Preliminary Screening Test and a Subject Aptitude Test (SAT), followed by a personal interaction or Personality Test. Whether you are a fresh MBBS graduate or an experienced practitioner looking for a permanent government role, this notification provides a transparent merit-based entry. This exhaustive 2000-word guide provides a definitive roadmap, covering detailed category-wise vacancy splits, eligibility nuances, salary matrix details, and expert strategies to help you navigate the competitive landscape of HPPSC medical recruitments.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must be highly attentive to the timeline provided by the commission. HPPSC follows a strict disciplinary schedule for its examinations. It is highly recommended that aspirants complete their One Time Registration (OTR) early to avoid last-minute server congestion. The eligibility in terms of age and qualification will be determined as of the specified cut-off dates.

Event Description Dates and Details
Official Advertisement Number 45/11-2025
Online Registration Opening November 2025 (Active)
Last Date for Online Submission December 12, 2025 (11:59 PM)
Cut-off Date for Age Calculation January 01, 2025
Job Nature Group-A Job Trainee (Permanent Track)
Official Web Portal hppsc.hp.gov.in

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Categorization

The total 232 vacancies represent a mix of fresh positions and a significant number of Backlog Vacancies from previous recruitment cycles. The distribution is done meticulously to provide equal opportunities to various social categories of the state as per the HP Government reservation policy.

Social Category Total Vacancies (Including Backlog)
Unreserved (UR) 49
UR - Ortho PH of HP 18 (14 Backlog)
UR - Ex-Servicemen (ESM) 35 (22 Backlog)
UR - Ward of ESM of HP 40 (Backlog)
Scheduled Caste (SC) of HP 14
SC - ESM / Ward of ESM 13 (Backlog)
Scheduled Tribe (ST) of HP 07
ST - Ortho PH of HP 03 (02 Backlog)
Other Backward Classes (OBC) of HP 20 (07 Backlog)
OBC - ESM of HP 03 (02 Backlog)
EWS of HP (Economically Weaker Section) 12
Grand Total 232
Reservation Eligibility: The benefits of reservation (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/ESM/PH) are strictly reserved for Bonafide Residents of Himachal Pradesh. Candidates from other states belonging to reserved categories will be treated as "General/Unreserved" candidates for all purposes.

Salary Matrix and Remuneration Details

Selected candidates for the post of Medical Officer will initially be appointed as Job Trainees. This status allows the government to evaluate the performance and adaptability of the candidates in various clinical settings across the state.

  • Monthly Fixed Emoluments: Selected trainees will receive a consolidated fixed remuneration of ₹34,000 per month.
  • Regularization: Upon successful completion of the training period (as per state policy), candidates will be regularized into the standard Pay Matrix of the Health Department, which carries the high basic pay of a Group-A Gazetted officer.
  • Benefits: Although the initial pay is a fixed stipend, regularized officers enjoy perks like Non-Practicing Allowance (NPA), Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and remote area incentives if posted in tribal or difficult terrains.

Eligibility Criteria and Educational Qualifications

Eligibility is the primary filter in the HPPSC process. Since this is a Group-A medical post, candidates must possess a recognized primary medical degree and have completed all statutory requirements as per the National Medical Commission (NMC).

  • Academic Qualification: Candidates must possess an M.B.B.S. Degree or an equivalent medical qualification included in the First, Second, or Third Schedule of the Indian Medical Council Act, 1956.
  • Compulsory Internship: Applicants must have completed their Compulsory Rotating Internship on or before the closing date of the application (December 12, 2025).
  • Professional Registration: Valid registration with the Himachal Pradesh Medical Council or the National Medical Commission is mandatory.
  • Desirable Qualification: Candidates possessing a Post Graduate degree or diploma from a recognized institution will be given preference. Additionally, knowledge of Himachal Pradesh’s customs, dialects, and traditions is essential for effective patient-doctor communication.

Age Limit (as of Jan 1, 2025): Minimum 18 years and maximum 45 years. Upper age relaxation of 5 years is provided to SC, ST, OBC, WFF, and PwD candidates of Himachal Pradesh, as well as to existing government employees as per state rules.

Selection Process and Evaluation Roadmap

The HPPSC follows a transparent and meritocratic two-tier selection model. For medical officers, the emphasis is placed on both general aptitude and deep technical medical knowledge.

  1. Paper-I (Screening Test): This is an objective-type test of 100 marks. It is conducted as a Computer Based Test (CBT) or OMR test. It serves as a qualifying round to filter candidates for the main SAT.
  2. Paper-II (Subject Aptitude Test - SAT): This is the descriptive or advanced objective subject test carrying 120 marks. This paper evaluates the clinical and theoretical knowledge acquired during the MBBS course.
  3. Personality Test (Interview): Candidates who clear the SAT will be called for a 30-mark Personality Test. This stage evaluates the candidate's communication skills, ethics, and suitability for the medical profession in a government setup.
  4. Final Merit: The final selection is based on the combined marks of SAT (120) and Personality Test (30), totaling 150 marks. Paper-I marks are not added to the final merit.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Success in the HPPSC Medical Officer exam requires a balanced preparation of both general studies and core clinical subjects. The exam follows the latest HPPSC pattern introduced in 2023.

Paper-I Pattern (Qualifying - 100 Marks)

  • Himachal Pradesh GK: 30 Marks. Focus on history, geography, and socio-economic updates of HP.
  • National & International Awareness: 30 Marks. Current affairs, static GK, and health-related schemes of India.
  • Language Proficiency: Hindi (20 Marks) and English (20 Marks). Grammar and comprehension basics.
  • Negative Marking: Every incorrect answer results in a deduction of 0.25 marks.

Paper-II Pattern (SAT - 120 Marks)

The SAT syllabus is based on the professional MBBS curriculum. Candidates should focus on the following high-yield areas:

  • Medicine & Allied: Cardiology, Neurology, Endocrinology, and Infectious Diseases.
  • Surgery: General Surgery, Trauma, and Orthopedics.
  • Obstetrics & Gynaecology: Antenatal care, Labor management, and Rural health initiatives.
  • Preventive & Social Medicine (PSM): National Health Programs, Immunization, and Epidemiology.

Expert Preparation Strategy for Medical Officers

Preparing for HPPSC while managing clinical duties can be stressful. Follow this expert-recommended roadmap to secure a rank in the top 232.

Mastering Himachal GK: The 30 marks dedicated to HP GK in Paper-I are the differentiator. Use "The Wonderland Himachal Pradesh" by Jag Mohan Balokhra. Focus specifically on the Rivers of HP, Dynasties of Kangra and Kullu, and recent state health policies like the Mukhya Mantri HIMCARE scheme.

SAT Revision Strategy: Since the SAT is MBBS-based, do not try to read new textbooks. Revise your notes from PrepLadder, Marrow, or standard textbooks like Harrison (Medicine) and Bailey & Love (Surgery). Focus heavily on PSM and Pediatrics, as these subjects are extremely relevant to the duties of a Medical Officer in Primary Health Centers (PHCs).

Handling Negative Marking: HPPSC exams are notorious for their tricky options. In Paper-I, only attempt questions you are 100% sure about. For SAT, practice at least 50 MCQs daily to improve your clinical reasoning speed. Managing the 3-hour duration of SAT requires consistent mock test practice.

Best Books for Preparation

Using standard study materials will keep your preparation aligned with the HPPSC selection pattern. Below are the top recommendations for the 2025 recruitment.

Subject Area Recommended Book List
Himachal Pradesh GK "Himachal Darpan" by Akash Publications or Balokhra’s Wonderland.
Medical Subjects (SAT) Standard textbooks (Medicine-Harrison, Surgery-Bailey & Love, OBG-Dutta).
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal and Lucent's General Knowledge.
Current Affairs Monthly "Pratiyogita Darpan" and HP Economic Survey (Latest).
English / Hindi Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi and Lucent’s Hindi Grammar.

How to Apply Online: Step-by-Step ORA Guide

The application process is strictly online through the HPPSC Online Recruitment Application (ORA) portal. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure a successful registration.

  1. Visit the official HPPSC website: www.hppsc.hp.gov.in/hppsc.
  2. Click on the "Apply Online" tab and select One Time Registration (OTR).
  3. Create Profile: Enter your basic details to generate a User ID and Password. Keep these safe for downloading the admit card.
  4. Fill Application: Login with your credentials and select "Advertisement No. 45/11-2025" for Medical Officers.
  5. Enter all academic details, including MBBS marks, Internship completion dates, and Medical Council registration number.
  6. Upload Scanned Images: Recent color passport photograph and signature in JPEG format (as per specified size).
  7. Payment: Pay the application fee through the integrated online gateway (UPI, Net Banking, or Card).
  8. Final Submission: Preview the form and click "Submit." Download the final PDF and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Needed to Apply

Scanning and self-attesting the correct documents is mandatory for the ORA process. Ensure all files are clear and readable to avoid rejection during the automated screening.

  1. Age Proof: 10th Standard (Matriculation) certificate or Birth Certificate.
  2. Degree Certificates: MBBS Degree and Semester-wise marksheets.
  3. Internship Proof: Compulsory Rotating Internship Completion Certificate.
  4. Registration: Valid Medical Council Registration Certificate (State or National).
  5. Domicile Proof: Bonafide Himachali Certificate (Essential for reservation benefits).
  6. Category Proof: Valid SC/ST/OBC/EWS certificate issued by the competent HP authority.
  7. NOC: "No Objection Certificate" if already working in a Government or PSU role.

Application Fee Structure

The fee is non-refundable and must be paid through the online portal. HPPSC provides concessions to ensure that the recruitment process is accessible to all eligible doctors.

Candidate Category Total Fee
General / EWS (Not BPL) / Other State Candidates ₹600/-
SC / ST / OBC / EWS (BPL) of Himachal Pradesh ₹150/-
Ex-Servicemen (Blind/Visually Impaired) of HP EXEMPTED (Nil)
Persons with Disabilities (PwD) of HP EXEMPTED (Nil)

Important Links

Resource Description Official Link
HPPSC Official ORA Portal Apply Online Here
Detailed Notification PDF Download Here
Official HPPSC Website Visit Site

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can MBBS graduates from other states apply for HPPSC Medical Officer?

Yes, candidates from other states can apply. However, they will be treated as General/Unreserved category candidates. They will not be eligible for any age relaxation, fee concession, or vertical reservation benefits provided to Bonafide residents of Himachal Pradesh.

Q2: Is a Post Graduate (MD/MS) degree mandatory for this post?

No. The essential qualification is an MBBS Degree. While a PG degree or diploma is mentioned as a "Desirable" qualification (meaning it gives you an advantage during selection), it is not mandatory to apply for the Medical Officer (General Wing) post.

Q3: What is the "Job Trainee" period and salary?

Initially, candidates are appointed as Job Trainees. During this period, you will receive a fixed consolidated salary of ₹34,000 per month. Regularization into the full pay matrix happens after a certain period of satisfactory service as per the latest HP Government rules.

Q4: What happens if I make a mistake in my ORA profile?

HPPSC provides a limited window for profile correction. However, once an application for a specific advertisement is submitted and the fee is paid, modifications are generally not allowed. It is advised to fill the OTR profile with extreme care.

Q5: Is there any interview for the Medical Officer post?

Yes. The selection process includes a 30-mark Personality Test (Interview). Candidates are shortlisted for the interview based on their performance in the Subject Aptitude Test (SAT).

Q6: When will the exam be conducted?

HPPSC usually conducts the screening tests within 3-4 months after the application deadline. Candidates are advised to keep checking the "Examination" section of the official website for updates on the exam schedule and admit card release.

entry.title: UPSC NDA & NA (1) Recruitment 2026: 394 Vacancies for 12th Pass, A Golden Opportunity to Become an Officer!
entry.content:
UPSC NDA & NA (1) Recruitment 2026: 394 Vacancies for 12th Pass, A Golden Opportunity to Become an Officer!

The Union Public Service Commission has released the notification for the National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination (I) 2026. Unmarried male and female candidates can apply.

Post Name: National Defence Academy (Army, Navy, Air Force), Naval Academy (10+2 Cadet Entry)
Last Date: 30 December 2025
Total Posts: 394

Great news for the youth who dream of serving the nation by joining the Indian Armed Forces as an officer. The Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) has released the detailed notification (Examination Notice No.- 3/2026-NDA-I) for one of its most prestigious examinations, the National Defence Academy and Naval Academy Examination (I), 2026. This examination is a golden opportunity for unmarried male and female candidates who are currently in or have passed the 12th grade and wish to build a career as an officer in the Indian Army, Navy, or Air Force.

Through this recruitment drive, a total of 394 vacancies will be filled for the 157th Course of the NDA and the 119th Course of the Indian Naval Academy (INAC). Selected candidates will receive world-class training and will be appointed as Commissioned Officers in the Indian Armed Forces. This is not just a job, but an opportunity to live a life of honor, pride, and adventure. The application process is entirely online and will be conducted on the official UPSC website from December 10, 2025, to December 30, 2025. In this detailed article, we will provide you with complete information about the wing-wise vacancies, eligibility, selection process, exam pattern, preparation strategy, and the application process.

Important Points

Details Information
Online Application Start Date December 10, 2025
Last Date for Online Application December 30, 2025 (until 6:00 PM)
Date of Written Examination April 12, 2026
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Defence Service)
Job Location PAN India

Vacancy Details

A total of 394 posts will be recruited through the UPSC NDA & NA (1) 2026 examination, the details of which are as follows:

Academy and Wing Male Female Total Posts
National Defence Academy (NDA)
Army 198 10 208
Navy 37 05 42
Air Force - Flying 90 02 92
Air Force - Ground Duty (Tech) 16 02 18
Air Force - Ground Duty (Non-Tech) 08 02 10
Naval Academy (10+2 Cadet Entry)
Executive Branch 21 03 24
Grand Total 370 24 394
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may be changed based on the training capacity.

Application Fee

  • For General and OBC candidates: ₹100/- (One Hundred Rupees only).
  • For SC, ST, female candidates, and wards of JCOs/NCOs/ORs: No fee.

The fee can be paid online (Visa/Master/RuPay Credit/Debit Card/UPI) or by depositing cash at any branch of SBI.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet the following eligibility criteria:

Criteria Required Qualification
Nationality The candidate must be a citizen of India, or a subject of Nepal/Bhutan, or a Tibetan refugee (who came to India before January 1, 1962, with the intention of permanently settling).
Age Limit and Marital Status Only unmarried male and female candidates born not earlier than July 01, 2007, and not later than July 01, 2010, are eligible.
Educational Qualification
  • For Army Wing: Passed 12th Class (10+2 pattern) or an equivalent examination from a recognized board.
  • For Air Force and Naval Wings and for the Naval Academy: Passed 12th Class (10+2 pattern) or an equivalent examination with Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics as subjects from a recognized board.
(Candidates appearing in the 12th Class examination can also apply.)
Physical Standards Candidates must meet the physical and medical standards prescribed for the Indian Armed Forces. Detailed information is provided in Appendix-III of the notification.

Selection Process

Selection in the NDA is a two-stage process that tests both the intellectual and personality traits of the candidate:

  1. Written Examination: An objective-type written examination conducted by UPSC, carrying 900 marks. Candidates who qualify in this exam will be called for the SSB interview.
  2. SSB Interview: This is a detailed 5-day personality and intelligence test, carrying 900 marks. It consists of two stages:
    • Stage-I: Officer Intelligence Rating (OIR) test and Picture Perception & Description Test (PP&DT). Only candidates who clear this stage proceed to Stage-II.
    • Stage-II: Interview, Group Testing Officer Tasks (GTO), Psychology Tests, and the Conference.
  3. Medical Examination: Candidates recommended by the SSB will have to undergo a detailed medical examination.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the total marks obtained in the written examination and the SSB interview.

Exam Pattern & Syllabus

The written examination consists of two papers, and there is a negative marking (0.33 marks) for wrong answers.

PaperSubjectMaximum MarksDuration
Paper-I Mathematics 300 2.5 Hours
Paper-II General Ability Test (GAT) 600 2.5 Hours
Total Written Exam 900
SSB Interview 900

Brief Syllabus:

  • Mathematics: Algebra, Matrices and Determinants, Trigonometry, Analytical Geometry of two and three dimensions, Differential Calculus, Integral Calculus and Differential Equations, Vector Algebra, Statistics and Probability.
  • General Ability Test (GAT):
    • Part A - English (200 marks): Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension, and usage of words.
    • Part B - General Knowledge (400 marks): Physics, Chemistry, General Science, History and Freedom Movement, Geography, and Current Affairs.

How to Prepare for the Exam

Success in the NDA exam requires consistency and smart preparation:

  1. Prioritize NCERTs: Make the NCERT books of classes 11th and 12th the foundation of your preparation for Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, and other subjects.
  2. Practice Mathematics Regularly: The mathematics paper is decisive. Memorize the formulas for all topics and practice questions daily.
  3. Improve English: Read an English newspaper daily. This will improve both your vocabulary and comprehension. Practice grammar rules.
  4. Give Time to General Knowledge: NCERT books are sufficient for History, Geography, and Science. For current affairs, read newspapers regularly and follow a monthly magazine.
  5. Solve Previous Years' Papers: Download the last 10 years' NDA question papers from the UPSC website and try to solve them within the time limit. This will help you understand the exam pattern and your weaknesses.
  6. Focus on Physical Fitness: Along with the written exam, it is also important to be physically fit for the SSB. Run regularly and do exercises.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right study material is crucial for good preparation. Here are some recommended books by subject:

Subject Recommended Books
Mathematics
  • NCERT 11th and 12th Mathematics textbooks.
  • "Mathematics for NDA and NA" - R.S. Aggarwal.
General Ability Test (GAT)
  • "Pathfinder for NDA & NA" - Arihant Publications (a comprehensive guide).
  • "Objective General English" - S.P. Bakshi (for English).
  • "Lucent's General Knowledge" (for General Knowledge).
  • NCERT 9th and 10th Science and Social Science textbooks.

How to Apply

The application process is only through the UPSC's online portal:

  1. Go to the official UPSC website: https://upsconline.nic.in.
  2. Create your OTR profile by clicking on "One-time Registration (OTR) for Examinations" (if not already created).
  3. After logging in, click on the "Apply" link for "NDA & NA Examination (I), 2026".
  4. Fill the application form carefully, which will include your personal, educational information, and preference of examination center.
  5. Capture your Live Photograph and upload your signature.
  6. Pay the online application fee (if applicable).
  7. Review your application before final submission and then submit it.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep the following information and scanned copies of documents ready while applying online:

  1. A valid Photo ID (such as Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN Card).
  2. Scanned copy of your recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  3. 10th and 12th class certificates (for proof of date of birth and educational qualification).
  4. Debit/Credit Card or UPI details for payment of the application fee.

Important Links

Link Type Anchor Text
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Download Here
Official Website www.upsc.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Can students appearing in the 12th class apply?

Yes, candidates who are appearing in the 12th class examination can apply for this exam. However, they will have to submit the 12th pass certificate at the time of the SSB interview.

Question 2: Can female candidates apply for all wings?

Yes, female candidates can now apply for all three wings - Army, Navy, and Air Force. Seats are reserved for them in each wing.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there is negative marking for wrong answers in the written examination. One-third (0.33) of the marks allotted to that question will be deducted for each wrong answer.

Question 4: Where is the SSB interview conducted?

The SSB interview is conducted at various Selection Centres across India, such as Allahabad (Prayagraj), Bhopal, Bengaluru, and Kapurthala. You will be allotted a center after you pass the written examination.

entry.title: Indian Statistical Institute (ISI) Recruitment for 16 Non-Teaching Posts
entry.content:
Indian Statistical Institute (ISI) Recruitment for 16 Non-Teaching Posts

Advertisement No. REC/12/2025-26/KOL: The Indian Statistical Institute (ISI) has invited online applications for the direct recruitment of 16 permanent non-teaching posts, including Engineer (Electrical), Electrician, Operator-cum-Mechanic (Lift), and Assistant (Library). The online application process is scheduled from December 29, 2025, to January 28, 2026.

Post Name: Engineer (Electrical), Electrician, Operator-cum-Mechanic (Lift), Assistant (Library)
Last Date: 28 January 2026
Total Posts: 16

The Indian Statistical Institute (ISI), an Institution of National Importance, has announced a recruitment drive to fill various Group 'B' and Group 'C' non-teaching positions on a permanent basis. This recruitment aims to induct skilled technical and administrative personnel to support the institute's operations at its headquarters in Kolkata and other centres across India. The drive includes one post of Engineer (Electrical) and multiple vacancies for Electrician, Operator-cum-Mechanic (Lift), and Assistant (Library).

The selection process is competitive and tailored to each post. For the Group 'B' Engineer post, the selection will be through a two-part written test followed by an interview. For the Group 'C' positions, selection will be based on a single written test followed by a qualifying skill test. The application process is entirely online through the official ISI website. This is an excellent opportunity for candidates with relevant technical and academic qualifications to secure a permanent central government job in a premier institution.

ISI Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. All applications must be submitted online.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Indian Statistical Institute (ISI)
Advertisement Number REC/12/2025-26/KOL
Post Names Engineer, Electrician, Operator-cum-Mechanic, Assistant (Library)
Total Vacancies 16
Application Start Date 29 December 2025
Application Last Date 28 January 2026
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Central Government Job
Job Location Kolkata and other centres of ISI
Official Website www.isical.ac.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive is distributed across four different posts with the following vacancy and pay details:

Post Name Vacancies Category Breakup Pay Level (7th CPC)
Engineer (Electrical) 'A' 1 UR-1 Level 7 (₹44,900 - ₹1,42,400)
Electrician 'A' 5 UR-3, OBC-1, SC-1 Level 3 (₹21,700 - ₹69,100)
Operator-cum-Mechanic (Lift) 'A' 6 UR-4, OBC-1, ST-1 Level 3 (₹21,700 - ₹69,100)
Assistant (Library) 'A' 4 UR-2, OBC-1, EWS-1 Level 5 (₹29,200 - ₹92,300)
Total 16

Application Fee (Non-Refundable)

  • For UR, EWS, and OBC candidates: ₹500/-
  • No fee for SC, ST, PwBD, and all Female candidates.

The fee must be paid online. Candidates applying for multiple posts must pay the fee for each post separately.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet the specific age and qualification requirements for the post they are applying for.

Post NameAge LimitEssential Qualifications & Experience
Engineer (Electrical) 'A' Below 35 years B.E./B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering or equivalent.
Electrician 'A' Below 35 years School Final (Class 10) or equivalent AND a relevant ITI Certificate (Electrician/Wireman trade). Must possess a National Apprenticeship Certificate and an Electrical Workman's Permit.
Operator-cum-Mechanic (Lift) 'A' Below 35 years School Final (Class 10) or equivalent AND a relevant ITI Certificate. Experience in lift operation and maintenance is desirable.
Assistant (Library) 'A' Below 35 years Higher Secondary (10+2) or equivalent AND a Bachelor's degree in Library Science. Requires a typing speed of 30 w.p.m. and knowledge of computer operations.

Selection Process

The selection process is tailored to each post.

  1. For Engineer (Electrical) 'A': Selection will be through a two-part Written Test (MCQ) followed by a Personal Interview. The final merit list will be based on the combined marks of the written test and the interview.
  2. For Electrician 'A', Operator-cum-Mechanic 'A', & Assistant (Library) 'A': The selection will be through a single Written Test (MCQ). Candidates shortlisted from the written test will have to appear for a Skill Test, which will be qualifying in nature. The final merit list will be prepared based only on the marks obtained in the written test.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The structure of the written test varies by post. There is no negative marking mentioned for any test.

Pattern for Engineer (Electrical) 'A':

  • Total Duration: 2 Hours | Total Marks: 200
  • Test-I (General Aptitude): 50 Questions, 100 Marks. (General Intelligence, General English, Quantitative Aptitude, General Awareness).
  • Test-II (Domain Knowledge): 50 Questions, 100 Marks. (Syllabus based on B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering).

Pattern for Electrician 'A' & Operator-cum-Mechanic (Lift) 'A':

  • Total Questions: 100 | Total Marks: 100 | Duration: 2 Hours
  • Domain Knowledge (Relevant Trade): 60 Questions
  • General English: 20 Questions
  • General Knowledge: 20 Questions

Pattern for Assistant (Library) 'A':

  • Total Questions: 100 | Total Marks: 100 | Duration: 2 Hours
  • Domain Knowledge (Library Science): 60 Questions
  • General English: 20 Questions
  • General Knowledge: 20 Questions

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) applicable for any of the posts in this recruitment drive.

How to Prepare for the Exam

A focused preparation strategy is essential, with a strong emphasis on the domain knowledge section.

  • Master the Domain Syllabus: For all posts, the domain/technical section carries the highest weightage. Thoroughly revise your entire B.E./B.Tech or ITI/Diploma curriculum as applicable.
  • General Sections: For the general aptitude/GK/English sections, practice from standard competitive exam books. Focus on improving speed and accuracy.
  • Skill Test Preparation: For Group 'C' posts, the skill test is a crucial qualifying stage. For Assistant (Library), practice typing to achieve the required speed. For technical posts like Electrician, be prepared for a practical trade test.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books and resources.

  • Domain (Engineer/Electrician): "Objective Electrical Technology" by V.K. Mehta & Rohit Mehta; standard textbooks from your B.E./ITI curriculum.
  • Domain (Library Assistant): "Objective Question Bank in Library and Information Science" by Dr. S.P. Sood.
  • General Aptitude & English: Books by R.S. Aggarwal for Quantitative Aptitude and Reasoning, and "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge".

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online through the official ISI website.

  1. Visit the official ISI website: www.isical.ac.in and navigate to the "Jobs" section.
  2. Find the link for "Recruitment of non-teaching posts (Advt. No. REC/12/2025-26/KOL)".
  3. Read the detailed instructions carefully and click on the "Apply Online" link.
  4. Register on the portal and fill out the application form with your personal and educational details.
  5. Upload clear scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other required documents.

  6. Pay the application fee online (if applicable).
  7. Review the application form before final submission. If applying for multiple posts, you must submit a separate application and fee for each.
  8. Download and take a printout of the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

Candidates must have scanned copies of the following documents ready before starting the online application:

  1. Recent passport-size photograph.
  2. Scanned signature.
  3. Proof of Date of Birth (e.g., 10th Certificate).
  4. All educational qualification certificates and mark sheets (10th, 12th, ITI, Degree, etc.).
  5. Electrical Workman's Permit and NAC (for Electrician post).
  6. Experience certificates, if any.
  7. Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD) in the prescribed format.
  8. No Objection Certificate (NOC) if currently employed in a government organization.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there an application fee for female candidates?

No, the notification clearly states that all female candidates, along with SC, ST, and PwBD candidates, are exempted from paying the application fee.

Q2: Is there any negative marking in the written test?

The detailed notification does not mention any negative marking for wrong answers in the written tests for any of the posts.

Q3: What is the selection process for the Engineer (Electrical) 'A' post?

The selection for the Engineer post will be based on the combined marks of a 200-mark written test (comprising General Aptitude and Domain Knowledge) and a 50-mark personal interview.

Q4: I am a final year student. Can I apply?

No, candidates must possess all the required essential qualifications as on the last date of the online application, which is January 28, 2026. Final year students awaiting results are not eligible.

entry.title: CSIR-CMERI Technician Recruitment 2025: 20 Posts Open for Various Trades
entry.content:
CSIR-CMERI Technician Recruitment 2025: 20 Posts Open for Various Trades

A premier research institute invites applications for Technician-I posts in Fitter, Electrician, Electronics Mechanic, and Digital Photography trades. Apply online from December 22, 2025.

Post Name: Technician-I [Group - II (1)]
Last Date: 21 January 2026
Total Posts: 20

The CSIR-Central Mechanical Engineering Research Institute (CSIR-CMERI), a premier R&D institute under the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR), has released a significant recruitment notification (Advertisement No. 11/2025) for technical positions. The institute is looking for dynamic, creative, and dedicated Indian citizens to join as Technicians in various trades for its main campus in Durgapur (West Bengal) and its Extension Centre, the Centre of Excellence for Farm Machinery, in Ludhiana (Punjab).

This is a fantastic opportunity for candidates with an SSC/10th standard qualification along with an ITI certificate or relevant experience to secure a stable government job in a prestigious research organization. CSIR-CMERI is the only national laboratory dedicated to mechanical engineering, playing a crucial role in developing technologies for various sectors. This recruitment drive aims to strengthen its technical workforce, which is vital for its ongoing and future research projects in areas like Advanced Design, Electric Mobility, Robotics, Farm Machinery, and more. A total of 20 vacancies for the post of Technician-I [Group - II (1)] have been announced. The online application process will commence on December 22, 2025, and will close on January 21, 2026.

Important Points

Here is a quick glance at the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Aspirants are advised to take note of these key points to stay informed about the schedule and job specifics. This is a Central Government job opportunity.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization CSIR-Central Mechanical Engineering Research Institute (CSIR-CMERI)
Post Name Technician-I [Group - II (1)]
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Durgapur (West Bengal) and Ludhiana (Punjab)
Online Application Start Date December 22, 2025, at 10:00 AM IST
Online Application Last Date January 21, 2026, up to 5:00 PM IST

Vacancy Details
