A total of 20 vacancies for the Technician-I post have been announced, with a significant number being backlog vacancies. These posts are distributed across various technical trades. The details are as follows:

Trade Number of Vacancies
Fitter 08
Electrician / Mechanic (Electrical Power Drives) 05
Electronics Mechanic / Instrument Mechanic 06
Digital Photography 01
Total Vacancies 20
Note: These are mostly backlog vacancies for reserved categories (SC, ST, OBC, PwBD). The PwBD posts for OH (Orthopedically Handicapped) and HH (Hard of Hearing) are also backlog vacancies. If suitable PwBD candidates are not found, the posts may be filled by interchange among other benchmark disability categories or by a person other than PwBD, as per GoI guidelines.

Application Fee Details

  • A non-refundable application fee of Rs. 500/- (Rupees Five Hundred Only) is mandatory for applicants other than those exempted.
  • Ex-servicemen/SC/ST/Women/PwBD candidates are exempted from the payment of the application fee.

The fee is to be deposited online through the State Bank Collect portal only. The transaction details must be mentioned in the online application form.

Eligibility Criteria

The eligibility criteria for the Technician-I post are a combination of educational qualifications and experience. The cut-off date for determining age and qualifications is January 21, 2026.

Criteria Requirement Details
Essential Qualification Candidates must possess one of the following qualifications:
  1. SSC / 10th Standard or equivalent with Science subjects with a minimum of 55% marks AND an ITI certificate or National/State trade certificate in the relevant trade (Fitter, Electrician, Electronics Mechanic, etc.) from a recognized institution.
  2. OR, SSC / 10th Standard or equivalent with Science subjects with a minimum of 55% marks AND 2 years of full-time experience as an apprentice trainee in the relevant trade from a recognized institution.
  3. OR, SSC / 10th Standard or equivalent with Science subjects with a minimum of 55% marks AND 3 years of work experience in the relevant trade in a Ministry/Department/Organization/Public Sector Undertaking/Autonomous Body under the Government of India/State/UT.
Age Limit
  • Minimum Age: 18 years
  • Maximum Age: 28 years
  • Age relaxation is permissible for various categories as per Government of India rules: 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC(NCL), 10 years for PwBD (Unreserved), and additional relaxations for other specified categories.

Selection Process

The selection process for the Technician-I post is a comprehensive, multi-stage process designed to meticulously test the practical skills and theoretical knowledge of the candidates to ensure the best fit for the roles.

  1. Screening of Applications: In the first stage, all online applications received within the deadline will be scrutinized by a duly constituted Screening Committee. The committee may adopt its own criteria, which could be stricter than the minimum qualifications, to shortlist a reasonable number of candidates for the next stage.
  2. Trade Test: The shortlisted candidates will be invited to appear for a Trade Test in their respective trade. This test is designed to assess the hands-on, practical skills and proficiency of the candidate. The Trade Test will be qualifying in nature, meaning candidates must pass this test to be eligible for the next stage, but the marks obtained will not be counted for the final merit list.
  3. Competitive Written Examination: Candidates who successfully qualify in the Trade Test will be called for the final stage, which is a Competitive Written Examination. The final merit list and the selection of candidates will be prepared based solely on the marks obtained in this written examination.

Exam Pattern

The Competitive Written Examination will be an OMR-based or Computer-Based Objective Type Multiple Choice Examination. The standard of the exam will be SSC + ITI / 12th Standard. The exam will consist of three papers conducted in a single session.

PaperSubjectNo. of QuestionsMaximum MarksNegative MarksDuration
Paper-I Mental Ability Test* 50 100 (2 marks each) No Negative Marks 1 Hour
Paper-II General Awareness 25 75 (3 marks each) 1 mark for every wrong answer 30 Minutes
English Language 25 75 (3 marks each) 1 mark for every wrong answer
Paper-III Concerned Subject (Trade Specific) 50 150 (3 marks each) 1 mark for every wrong answer 1 Hour

*Mental Ability Test (Paper-I) will include General Intelligence, Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, Problem Solving, and Situational Judgement. It is a qualifying paper. Paper-II and Paper-III will only be evaluated for those candidates who secure the minimum threshold marks in Paper-I, which will be determined by the Selection Committee.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained by the candidates in Paper-II and Paper-III only.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for this recruitment. The selection process is focused on technical and mental abilities.

How to Prepare

A focused and strategic preparation is required to clear this exam. Since the selection process is multi-layered, candidates need to prepare for both practical and theoretical aspects.

  1. Master Your Trade (for Trade Test and Paper-III): The most crucial part of your preparation should be your ITI trade. Paper-III carries 150 marks and will be the deciding factor. Thoroughly revise your entire ITI syllabus. Focus on both theoretical concepts and their practical applications. For the Trade Test, practice hands-on skills related to your trade.
  2. Strengthen Mental Ability (for Paper-I): Paper-I is qualifying but essential. Practice Quantitative Aptitude (maths up to 10th standard) and Reasoning questions regularly from standard competitive exam books. Since there is no negative marking in this paper, your strategy should be to attempt all 50 questions with the best possible accuracy.
  3. Prepare for General Sections (for Paper-II): For General Awareness, stay updated with current affairs, basic science, history, geography, and polity. For English, focus on basic grammar, vocabulary, synonyms, antonyms, and reading comprehension.
  4. Practice with Mock Tests: Take online mock tests that follow the same three-paper pattern. This will help you manage your time effectively across the different sections and get used to the pressure of switching between subjects with different marking schemes.
  5. Be Mindful of Negative Marking: Remember that Paper-II and Paper-III have negative marking. This means you must be careful with your answers. Attempt only those questions you are confident about to avoid losing marks.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books that can aid your preparation for different sections of the exam:

  • For Trade Specific Subjects (Paper-III):
    • ITI Trade Theory books based on the NIMI pattern for your specific trade (Fitter, Electrician, etc.).
    • Objective question banks by Arihant Publications or Upkar Prakashan for ITI trades.
  • For Mental Ability (Paper-I):
    • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
    • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • For General Awareness & English (Paper-II):
    • "Lucent's General Knowledge" for the GA section.
    • "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi for the English section.

How to Apply

Eligible candidates are required to apply ONLINE through the official website http://www.cmeri.res.in only. No other mode of application will be considered. Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Visit the official CSIR-CMERI website and navigate to the "Vacancy" or "Recruitment" section.
  2. Find the link for Advertisement No. 11/2025 and click on "Apply Online".
  3. Register yourself with your name and a valid email ID. All future communication will be sent to this email ID, so ensure it is active.
  4. Log in with your credentials and fill out the online application form with all the required details accurately.
  5. Pay the non-refundable application fee of Rs. 500/- online through the State Bank Collect portal. (Candidates belonging to Ex-servicemen/SC/ST/Women/PwBD categories are exempted from the fee).
  6. Upload the e-receipt of the fee payment, your recent passport-size color photograph, signature, and other relevant certificates (10th, ITI, Caste, etc.) at the specified places in the online application.
  7. Verify all the details before final submission. Once the application is submitted, no further modifications or amendments will be permitted.
  8. It is important to keep a printout of the completed application form for your records. You should NOT send a hard copy of the application to the institute.

Documents Needed to Apply

The following documents must be scanned and uploaded along with the online application form (whichever is applicable):

  1. Recent passport-size Colour Photograph.
  2. Signature of the candidate.
  3. Fee receipt of Rs. 500/- as an application fee, wherever applicable.
  4. SSC / 10th certificate reflecting Date of Birth.
  5. SSC / 10th Mark Sheet.
  6. ITI/Trade/Apprentice Training Certificate.
  7. Category/Community certificate (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD) in the prescribed Government of India format.
  8. Experience Certificate(s), if any.
  9. Aadhaar Card.
  10. NOC from the present employer as per Annexure-II, if applicable.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online (from 22.12.2025) Click Here
Official Notification Download from Official Website
Official Website www.cmeri.res.in

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the CSIR-CMERI Technician recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is January 21, 2026, up to 5:00 PM.

Question 2: What is the selection process for this recruitment?

The selection process involves a qualifying Trade Test followed by a competitive written examination. The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the written exam.

Question 3: Is there an application fee for all candidates?

No, there is a non-refundable application fee of Rs. 500/- for general and OBC candidates only. SC/ST/Women/PwBD/Ex-servicemen are exempted from paying the fee.

Question 4: What is the structure of the written exam?

The exam has three papers: Paper-I (Mental Ability - Qualifying with no negative marking), Paper-II (General Awareness & English - with negative marking), and Paper-III (Concerned Trade Subject - with negative marking). The final merit will be based on the combined score of Paper-II and Paper-III.

entry.title: GKCIET Recruitment 2025: Professor, Trainer और Faculty की भर्ती
entry.content:
GKCIET Recruitment 2025: Professor, Trainer और Faculty की भर्ती

Ghani Khan Choudhury Institute of Engineering and Technology (GKCIET), Malda ने शिक्षण पदों के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं। ऑनलाइन आवेदन 01 नवंबर 2025 से शुरू होंगे।

GKCIET, Malda जो कि Ministry of Education के अंतर्गत एक Centrally Funded Technical Institute है, ने Professor, Associate Professor, Assistant Professor और Senior Trainer पदों पर सीधी भर्ती के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है। यह भर्ती विभिन्न विभागों — Computer Science, Electrical, Mechanical, Civil, Food Technology और Physics — के लिए की जाएगी। ऑनलाइन आवेदन 01 नवंबर 2025 से शुरू होंगे और अंतिम तिथि 30 नवंबर 2025 (रात 12:00 बजे) है।

रिक्तियाँ और वेतन स्तर

पदविभागश्रेणीवेतन स्तर
ProfessorComputer Science Engg.UR-01Level 14 (₹1,44,200/-)
Electrical Engg.OBC-01
PhysicsSC-01
Associate ProfessorCivil Engg.OBC-01Level 13A1 (₹1,31,400/-)
Food TechnologyUR-01
Assistant ProfessorMechanical Engg.UR-01Level 10 (₹57,700/-)
Computer Science Engg.-
Senior TrainerComputer Science Engg.UR-01Level 10 (₹57,700/-)
नोट: PwBD (Divyang) उम्मीदवार किसी भी पद के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं। कुल 3 पद PwBD के लिए आरक्षित हैं।

शैक्षणिक योग्यता और अनुभव

  • Professor: संबंधित क्षेत्र में Ph.D. डिग्री और न्यूनतम 10 वर्ष का अनुभव (Teaching/Research/Industry)।
  • Associate Professor: Ph.D. डिग्री और कम से कम 8 वर्ष का अनुभव जिनमें 2 वर्ष Post-Ph.D. होने चाहिए।
  • Assistant Professor: B.E./B.Tech. और M.E./M.Tech. में से किसी एक में First Class।
  • Senior Trainer: B.E./B.Tech. या M.E./M.Tech. (First Class) — साथ ही 2 वर्ष का अनुभव वांछनीय।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. Professor & Associate Professor: Academic score और अनुभव के आधार पर शॉर्टलिस्ट कर Presentation व Interview लिया जाएगा।
  2. Assistant Professor: पहले Written Test, फिर सफल उम्मीदवारों का Presentation व Interview होगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया और शुल्क

  1. GKCIET की वेबसाइट www.gkciet.ac.in पर जाकर ऑनलाइन आवेदन करें।
  2. सभी आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ (Photo, Signature, Certificates आदि) अपलोड करें।
  3. आवेदन शुल्क:
    • General/OBC: ₹1500
    • SC/ST: ₹500
    • PwD/Women: शुल्क माफ
  4. ऑनलाइन आवेदन के बाद उसका Printout लेकर हस्ताक्षर करें और सभी प्रमाणपत्रों की Self-Attested Copies के साथ भेजें।

Hard Copy भेजने की अंतिम तिथि: 08 दिसंबर 2025 (शाम 5:00 बजे तक)

पता:
The Director,
Ghani Khan Choudhury Institute of Engineering and Technology,
Narayanpur, Malda, West Bengal – 732141

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंकवेबसाइट
Official Websiteयहाँ क्लिक करें
Clarification Emailtech_support@gkciet.ac.in

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

30 नवंबर 2025 (रात 12:00 बजे) तक ऑनलाइन आवेदन जमा किए जा सकते हैं।

Q2: क्या Hard Copy भेजना आवश्यक है?

हाँ, ऑनलाइन आवेदन के बाद उसका हस्ताक्षरित प्रिंटआउट और दस्तावेज़ भेजना अनिवार्य है।

Q3: आवेदन शुल्क कितना है?

General/OBC – ₹1500, SC/ST – ₹500, PwD/Women – निशुल्क।

Q4: Retirement age कितनी है?

सभी Faculty पदों के लिए सेवानिवृत्ति आयु 65 वर्ष है।

entry.title: Jamia Millia Islamia Recruitment 2025: 69 Non-Teaching Posts Open, Apply Offline
entry.content:
Jamia Millia Islamia Recruitment 2025: 69 Non-Teaching Posts Open, Apply Offline

A major recruitment drive for Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' positions including Engineers, PAs, Stenographers, and more. Last date to submit applications is December 26, 2025.

Post Name: Private Secretary, PA, Stenographer, Engineer, Auditor, Cook, Security Assistant & others
Last Date: 26/12/2025
Total Posts: 69

Jamia Millia Islamia (JMI), one of India's prestigious central universities accredited with an "A++" Grade by NAAC, has released a detailed advertisement (Advt. No. 02/2025-26 (NT)) for recruitment to various non-teaching positions. This recruitment drive is being conducted to fill a total of 69 posts across Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' in various departments of the university, such as the Registrar's Office, Property Section, Finance & Accounts Office, and the Building & Construction Department. This is a splendid opportunity for qualified candidates seeking a stable and respected career in one of the country's premier academic institutions.

The application process is entirely offline. Candidates are required to fill out the application form in the prescribed format and submit it along with the necessary documents to the Registrar's Office at Jamia Millia Islamia. The recruitment includes a diverse range of posts like Private Secretary, Personal Assistant, Stenographer, Engineer, Auditor, Cook, and Security Assistant, providing opportunities for candidates with varied qualifications. The last date for submission of the application form is December 26, 2025. This article will provide you with all the crucial information regarding this recruitment, including post-wise vacancy details, pay scale, educational qualifications, age limit, selection process, application fee, and how to apply, so you can take full advantage of this opportunity.

JMI Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Aspirants are advised to take note of these key points.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Jamia Millia Islamia (JMI)
Advertisement Number 02/2025-26 (NT)
Post Names Various Non-Teaching Posts (Private Secretary, PA, Stenographer, Engineer, etc.)
Total Vacancies 69
Job Location Jamia Nagar, New Delhi
Application Mode Offline
Last Date to Apply December 26, 2025

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

JMI has announced a total of 69 non-teaching vacancies across various departments. A detailed breakdown of the post-wise vacancies is provided below:

Sr. No. Post Name No. of Posts Department
1 Private Secretary 01 Registrar's Office
2 Personal Assistant (English) 12 Registrar's Office
3 Personal Assistant (Hindi) 03 Registrar's Office
4 Stenographer (English) 08 Registrar's Office
5 Stenographer (Hindi) 02 Registrar's Office
6 Land Record Superintendent 01 Property Section
7 Land Record Keeper 01 Property Section
8 Internal Audit Officer 02 (01 Direct, 01 Deputation) Finance & Accounts Office
9 Superintending Engineer (Civil) 01 Building & Construction Dept.
10 Executive Engineer (Civil) 01 Building & Construction Dept.
11 Assistant Engineer (Civil) 01 Building & Construction Dept.
12 Assistant Engineer (Electrical) 01 Building & Construction Dept.
13 Junior Engineer (Civil) 10 Building & Construction Dept.
14 Junior Engineer (Electrical) 03 Building & Construction Dept.
15 Junior Engineer (Mechanical) 02 Building & Construction Dept.
16 Semi-Professional Assistant 04 Library Cadre
17 Library Attendant 06 Library Cadre
18 Security Assistant 09 Proctor's Office
19 Programme Officer 01 India Arab Cultural Centre
20 Cook 01 University Guest House
Total 69

Application Fee Details

Candidates must pay the application fee via online mode or UPI and attach the payment receipt with the application form.

Post Group Category Application Fee
Group A (Pay Level 10 and above) UR/OBC ₹ 1000/-
SC/ST ₹ 500/-
PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities) NIL
Group B & C (Pay Level 7 and below) UR/OBC ₹ 700/-
SC/ST ₹ 350/-
PwBD NIL

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Each post has specific requirements for educational qualifications, experience, and age limits. Details for some of the key posts are provided below:

Post Name Required Qualification & Experience Age Limit
Private Secretary Bachelor's Degree + 3 years as PA or 5 years as Stenographer + 120 wpm Stenography & 35 wpm Typing (English) + Computer Knowledge. 40 years
Personal Assistant Bachelor's Degree + 3 years as Stenographer + 100 wpm Stenography & 35/30 wpm Typing (English/Hindi) + Computer Knowledge. 40 years
Stenographer Bachelor's Degree + 80 wpm Stenography & 35/30 wpm Typing (English/Hindi) + Computer Knowledge. 40 years
Assistant Engineer (Civil/Elec.) First Class Bachelor's Degree in the relevant field + 3 years experience as a Junior Engineer. 40 years
Junior Engineer (Civil/Elec./Mech.) Bachelor's Degree in Engg./Tech. in the relevant field + 1 year experience OR Diploma in the relevant field + 3 years experience. 40 years
Semi-Professional Assistant Master's Degree in Library Science/Information Science OR Bachelor's Degree in Library Science + 2 years experience. 40 years
Security Assistant Intermediate (12th) Pass + Knowledge of Hindi, Urdu, and English. (Preference for Ex-servicemen). 40 years
Cook 10th Pass + ITI in Bakery & Confectionery OR Diploma in Cookery + 3 years experience. 40 years
Note: For detailed qualifications and age limits for all other posts, please refer to the official notification. Age relaxation for reserved categories will be applicable as per Government of India rules.

Selection Process

The selection process for non-teaching posts will be multi-staged, depending on the requirements of the post. The selection will be based on the candidate's performance in the following tests:

  1. Written Test: A written examination will be conducted for most posts to assess the candidates' general knowledge, reasoning ability, and subject-specific knowledge.
  2. Skill Test: For posts like Stenographer, Personal Assistant, and Private Secretary, a skill test will be conducted to assess stenography (dictation and transcription) and typing speed.
  3. Trade Test: For technical posts like Cook, a trade test may be conducted to evaluate the practical skills of the candidates.
  4. Interview: For higher-level posts such as Engineer, Officer, etc., candidates who qualify in the written/skill test may be called for a personal interview.
  5. Document Verification: Candidates who are successful in all stages will be required to get their original documents verified.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the candidate's overall performance across all these stages.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus (Expected)

While the detailed scheme of examination for each post will be notified on the JMI website, candidates can expect a pattern similar to other central university recruitment exams.

Expected Written Exam Pattern (General Posts)

Part Subject Expected Marks
Part A General Awareness, Reasoning, Mathematical Ability, Language (English/Hindi) 50 - 100
Part B Knowledge of Computer Applications & Subject/Post specific questions 50 - 100

Skill Test Pattern (for Stenographer/PA)

  • Stenography Test: Dictation for 10 minutes at a speed of 80/100/120 wpm (depending on the post) in English or Hindi.
  • Transcription: Time for transcription of the dictated matter on a computer will be around 50 minutes for English and 65 minutes for Hindi.
  • Typing Test: A separate typing test may be conducted to assess speed (35 wpm for English, 30 wpm for Hindi).

Syllabus (General Outline)

  • General Awareness: Current Affairs, Indian History, Geography, Constitution, General Polity, Science, and knowledge about Jamia Millia Islamia and the Indian higher education system.
  • Reasoning & Mathematical Ability: Logical reasoning, puzzles, data interpretation, basic arithmetic, percentage, profit & loss.
  • Language Test: Grammar, vocabulary, comprehension in both English and Hindi.
  • Subject-Specific (for Technical Posts): The syllabus will be based on the essential qualification for the post (e.g., Civil/Electrical Engineering syllabus for JE/AE posts, Library Science syllabus for Library posts).

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no mention of a Physical Efficiency Test (PET) for most of these non-teaching posts. However, for the post of Security Assistant, a physical fitness test might be a part of the selection process, the details of which will be notified by the university. For all other posts, no PET is required.

How to Prepare for the JMI Recruitment Exam

A structured preparation strategy is essential to succeed in the Jamia Millia Islamia non-teaching recruitment exam.

  1. Understand the Exam Pattern and Syllabus: The university will likely release the detailed exam pattern and syllabus for each post on its website soon. Keep a close watch and align your preparation accordingly.
  2. Preparation for Skill Test (Steno/Typing):
    • If you are applying for Stenographer, PA, or Private Secretary, practice your stenography and typing skills daily. Aim to achieve the speed mentioned in the advertisement (80/100/120 wpm).
    • Listen to dictation audios on various topics and practice transcription.
  3. Preparation for the Written Test:
    • General Knowledge: Read newspapers for current affairs, and study standard GK books for static portions. Learn about JMI's history and achievements.
    • Reasoning and Numerical Ability: Practice basic concepts from standard competitive exam books.
    • Subject-Specific Knowledge: For posts like Engineer, Audit Officer, and Library Assistant, conduct an in-depth study of subjects from your respective qualifying degree.
  4. Preparation for the Interview:
    • Prepare answers for questions related to your job responsibilities, your resume, and your knowledge about Jamia Millia Islamia.
    • Work on your communication skills and confidence.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books that can aid your preparation:

  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge" and regular newspapers.
  • Reasoning Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • English/Hindi Language: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi and standard Hindi grammar books.
  • For Engineering Posts: Standard textbooks for your respective branch and objective question books for competitive exams like those from Made Easy or ACE Engineering Academy.
  • For Library Posts: "A Textbook for Library and Information Science" by O. P. Gupta or other standard texts for UGC-NET Paper-II in Library Science.

How to Apply for JMI Recruitment

This is a completely offline recruitment process. Candidates must submit the filled-in application form as per the instructions below:

  1. Download the Application Form: First, download the prescribed application form from the recruitment section of the official Jamia Millia Islamia website: www.jmi.ac.in.
  2. Fill the Application Form: Fill out the downloaded form neatly and clearly. All information must be accurate and legible.
  3. Pay the Application Fee: Pay the prescribed application fee according to your category via online mode or UPI (UPI ID: jmirps@indianbk). Take a printout of the payment receipt.
  4. Attach Required Documents: Prepare self-attested photocopies of all the necessary documents as listed below.
  5. Send/Submit the Application: Place the filled application form, fee payment receipt, and all attached documents in an envelope and submit it in person or send it by Post/Courier to reach the following address by 5:00 PM on December 26, 2025:

    Recruitment & Promotion Section,
    2nd Floor, Registrar's Office,
    Jamia Millia Islamia, Maulana Mohamed Ali Jauhar Marg,
    Jamia Nagar, New Delhi-110025

  6. Applications can be submitted at the office between 10:00 AM and 05:00 PM from Monday to Friday (with a lunch break from 01:00 PM to 02:00 PM).

Documents Required with the Application

It is mandatory to attach self-attested copies of the following documents with the application form:

  • Completely filled and duly signed application form.
  • Receipt of application fee payment.
  • Educational qualification certificates and mark sheets (from 10th standard to the highest qualification).
  • Experience certificates (wherever required).
  • Caste/Category certificate (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD), if applicable.
  • Proof of date of birth (10th certificate).
  • Identity proof (such as Aadhaar Card, Voter ID).
  • If already employed, apply through the proper channel and produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) at the time of the interview.
  • If you had applied under the previous advertisement (01/2023-24), attach the acknowledgment receipt or post/courier slip of that application to claim a fee exemption.

Important Links

Link Type Description
Official Notification & Application Form Click Here
Official Website https://www.jmi.ac.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: How do I apply for the JMI non-teaching recruitment?

This is an offline recruitment. You need to download the application form, fill it out, and submit it along with the fee receipt and required documents to the Registrar's Office at Jamia by December 26, 2025.

Question 2: How do I pay the application fee?

The application fee can be paid via online mode or UPI (UPI ID: jmirps@indianbk). The receipt of the payment must be sent with the application form.

Question 3: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for more than one post, but you must submit a separate application form and pay a separate fee for each post.

Question 4: I applied under the previous advertisement. Do I need to apply again?

Yes, you must apply afresh. However, you will be exempted from paying the application fee for that specific post if you attach proof of your previous application (acknowledgment receipt or postal slip).

Question 5: What is the selection process?

The selection process may include a written test, skill test (steno/typing), trade test, and/or an interview, depending on the post.

entry.title: MPPGCL Recruitment 2025: 90 Plant Assistant (Mechanical & Electrical) Posts Open
entry.content:
MPPGCL Recruitment 2025: 90 Plant Assistant (Mechanical & Electrical) Posts Open

A golden opportunity for ITI pass-outs to secure a regular government job in Madhya Pradesh's power sector. Online applications are open from December 1 to December 30, 2025.

Post Name: Plant Assistant (Mechanical), Plant Assistant (Electrical)
Last Date: 30/12/2025
Total Posts: 90

Madhya Pradesh Power Generating Company Limited (MPPGCL), a major undertaking of the Government of Madhya Pradesh, has presented a golden opportunity for the ITI-passed youth of the state. The company has released a detailed advertisement/rulebook (Serial No: 5560) for direct recruitment to the post of Plant Assistant for the operation and maintenance of its thermal and hydro power plants. This recruitment drive is being conducted to fill a total of 90 regular posts in the Mechanical and Electrical trades.

This recruitment is exclusively for male candidates and offers a fantastic chance for young individuals seeking a stable and respected government job in the energy sector of Madhya Pradesh. MPPGCL, a successor company of the erstwhile Madhya Pradesh State Electricity Board, plays a pivotal role in the field of electricity generation in the state. Securing a position as a Plant Assistant means becoming part of a critical team that ensures the smooth functioning of power generation facilities, directly contributing to the state's development.

The selection process will be based entirely on a Computer Based Test (CBT), followed by document verification. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the MP Online selection website, https://chayan.mponline.gov.in, from December 1, 2025, to December 30, 2025. In this comprehensive article, we will provide you with all the essential information regarding this recruitment, including post-wise vacancies, salary, educational qualifications, age limit, selection process, and how to apply.

MPPGCL Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other key information to ensure a smooth application process.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Madhya Pradesh Power Generating Company Limited (MPPGCL)
Post Name Plant Assistant (Mechanical & Electrical)
Total Vacancies 90
Application Mode Online (via MP Online)
Application Start Date December 1, 2025
Application Last Date December 30, 2025
Job Location Madhya Pradesh

Vacancy Details and Reservation

MPPGCL has advertised a total of 90 posts for Plant Assistant, which are divided into Mechanical and Electrical trades. A detailed trade-wise and category-wise breakdown of the vacancies is provided below:

Post Code Post Name Total Posts UR SC ST OBC EWS
P01 Plant Assistant - Mechanical 53 14 9 11 14 5
P02 Plant Assistant - Electrical 37 10 6 7 10 4
Grand Total 90 24 15 18 24 9
Important Note: This recruitment is exclusively for male candidates. Women candidates are exempted from the 33% reservation policy for this particular post due to the nature of the job.

Salary and Pay Scale Details

Selected candidates will be appointed for a 09-month training period followed by a 03-year probation period. During the training and probation, they will be paid a fixed stipend. After successful completion of the probation period, they will be placed in the regular pay scale.

Details Amount/Pay Scale
Stipend (During Probation Period)
  • First Year: 70% of the minimum of the pay scale
  • Second Year: 80% of the minimum of the pay scale
  • Third Year: 90% of the minimum of the pay scale
Regular Pay Scale (After Probation) Pay Matrix Level-06 (₹ 25,300 - ₹ 80,500/-)

Once confirmed in the regular pay scale, the employee will also be entitled to other allowances such as Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and other benefits as per the company's rules, making the total emoluments highly attractive.

Eligibility Criteria: Education and Age Limit

Candidates applying for this recruitment must meet the following educational qualifications and age criteria.

Educational Qualification

Post Name Required Qualification Minimum Percentage
Plant Assistant - Mechanical
  • Passed High School (10th) examination from a board recognized by the Government of Madhya Pradesh/India.
  • Regular ITI Trade Certificate (SCVT/NCVT) from a recognized institute in Machinist / Fitter / Welder / HP Welder / Mechanic Pump / Mechanic Vehicle / Motor Mechanic / Diesel Mechanic trade.
  • Unreserved/OBC: 65%
  • Departmental Employees: 60%
  • SC/ST/EWS/PWD: 55%
(A 10% relaxation in marks is given to EWS candidates)
Plant Assistant - Electrical
  • Passed High School (10th) examination from a board recognized by the Government of Madhya Pradesh/India.
  • Regular ITI Trade Certificate (SCVT/NCVT) from a recognized institute in Electrician / Wireman / Electronics / Instrumentation trade.

Age Limit (as on January 1, 2025)

Criteria Age
Minimum Age 18 years
Maximum Age 40 years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

  • Age relaxation benefits are applicable only to candidates who are domiciles of Madhya Pradesh.
  • A relaxation of 5 years will be given to SC, ST, OBC, Government Corporation/Board/Autonomous Body employees, and Home Guards.
  • A relaxation of 5 years will be given to players honored with the Vikram Award.
  • Relaxation for Ex-Servicemen will be applicable as per rules.
  • Under no circumstances will the maximum age limit, after all relaxations, exceed 45 years.

Selection Process

The selection process for the post of Plant Assistant will be based entirely on a computer-based online examination. There will be no interview.

  1. Computer Based Test (CBT): This will be the sole basis for selection. Candidates' performance in this test will determine their place in the merit list.
  2. Merit List: A category-wise merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the CBT.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates selected on the basis of the merit list will be called for document verification. Final selection is subject to the successful verification of all original documents.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

A thorough understanding of the exam pattern and syllabus is crucial for scoring well in the Computer Based Test (CBT).

CBT Exam Pattern

Part Subject No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
Part A Knowledge of the relevant ITI Trade 75 75 2 Hours
(120 Minutes)
Part B General Knowledge, Aptitude & Logical Reasoning 25 25
Total 100 100
Key Points about the Exam:
  • The question paper will be bilingual (English and Hindi).
  • There will be NO negative marking for incorrect answers.
  • The minimum qualifying marks are 40% for the Unreserved category and 30% for reserved categories.

Detailed Syllabus (Expected)

Part A: Technical Syllabus (75 Questions)

  • For Mechanical Trades (Fitter, Machinist, Welder, Diesel Mechanic etc.): Workshop Safety, Tools and Equipment, Measurement and Measuring Tools (Vernier Caliper, Micrometer), Limits, Fits and Tolerances, Metals and Heat Treatment, Lathe Machine operations, Drilling, Grinding, Welding techniques (Gas and Arc), Fasteners (Nuts, Bolts, Screws), Bearings, Lubrication, Pumps, Valves, Basics of IC Engines.
    • - For Electrical Trades (Electrician, Wireman, Electronics, Instrumentation): Occupational Safety and Health, Hand Tools, Fundamentals of Electricity (Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Laws), AC and DC Circuits, Electrical Wiring, Earthing, Electrical Machines (DC Motors/Generators, Transformers, AC Motors), Basic Electronics (Diodes, Transistors, Rectifiers), Measuring Instruments (Ammeter, Voltmeter, Multimeter, Megger), Power Generation basics.

Part B: General Aptitude Syllabus (25 Questions)

  • General Knowledge & Awareness: Current events (National and International), Indian History, Geography, Polity, Economy, General Science, and Static GK with special focus on Madhya Pradesh.
  • Aptitude & Logical Reasoning: Number Series, Coding-Decoding, Analogies, Blood Relations, Direction Sense, Syllogism, Simple Arithmetic (Percentage, Profit & Loss, Ratio & Proportion, Averages).

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for this recruitment. The selection is based solely on the merit of the Computer Based Test.

How to Prepare for the MPPGCL Exam

A dedicated and strategic preparation is required to succeed in this competitive examination.

  1. Master Your Technical Syllabus: Since 75% of the exam is based on your ITI trade, this should be your primary focus. Study your ITI textbooks (Nimmi pattern books are highly recommended) thoroughly. Practice objective questions from various ITI trade theory books.
  2. Don't Ignore the Aptitude Section: The 25 questions from the general section can be a deciding factor. Dedicate at least an hour daily to prepare for General Knowledge and Reasoning. For MP-specific GK, refer to a standard state-level publication.
  3. Solve Previous Year Papers: Try to find and solve previous question papers of MPPGCL or similar PSU exams for ITI level posts. This will help you understand the question pattern and difficulty level.
  4. Take Mock Tests: Regularly attempt online mock tests. This will help you manage your time effectively during the 2-hour exam and improve your speed and accuracy. Since there is no negative marking, your strategy should be to attempt all 100 questions.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books to aid your preparation:

  • Technical Subjects (Mechanical/Electrical):
    • ITI Trade Theory Books (Nimmi Pattern / Asian Publishers / Arihant Publications) for your specific trade.
    • "Objective Electrical Technology" by V.K. Mehta & Rohit Mehta (for Electrical).
    • "Objective Mechanical Engineering" by R.S. Khurmi & J.K. Gupta (for Mechanical concepts).
  • General Knowledge & Aptitude:
    • "Lucent's General Knowledge" for static GK.
    • "Madhya Pradesh Samanya Gyan" by a local publisher like Maheshwari or Punekar for MP-specific GK.
    • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.

How to Apply for MPPGCL Recruitment

The application process is entirely online and must be completed through the MP Online selection website.

  1. First, visit the MP Online selection website: https://chayan.mponline.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the link provided for the MPPGCL recruitment.
  3. Read the detailed rulebook carefully before applying.
  4. Click on "Apply Online" and complete the registration process.
  5. Fill in the application form with all the required details, such as personal information, educational qualifications, etc.
  6. Upload scanned copies of the required documents as listed below.
  7. Pay the application fee according to your category via the online payment gateway (Net Banking/Debit Card/Credit Card/UPI).
  8. Keep a printout of the final submitted application form and the fee receipt for future reference.

Documents Required to Upload

It is mandatory to scan and upload the following documents while applying online:

  • Class 10th Marksheet.
  • Permanent Residence Certificate of Madhya Pradesh (for MP domiciles).
  • Caste Certificate (for reserved category candidates).
  • Income and Asset Certificate (for EWS candidates).
  • Income Certificate (for OBC (NCL) candidates).
  • Disability Certificate (for PWD candidates).
  • Marksheet/Certificate of the relevant ITI trade.
  • Latest color passport-size photograph and signature.
  • Documents required for claiming age relaxation.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (From 01.12.2025)
MPPGCL Official Website https://mppgcl.mp.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the MPPGCL Plant Assistant recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is December 30, 2025 (until 11:55 PM).

Question 2: Can female candidates apply for these posts?

No, this recruitment is exclusively for male candidates.

Question 3: Can candidates from outside Madhya Pradesh apply?

Yes, any citizen of India can apply, but they will be considered under the Unreserved (UR) category and will not get the benefits of reservation or age relaxation.

Question 4: What is the selection process?

The selection process is based on a 100-mark computer-based online test (CBT). There will be no interview.

Question 5: Is there negative marking in the exam?

No, there will be no negative marking for incorrect answers in this examination.

entry.title: SBI Specialist Cadre Officer Recruitment 2025: 896 Vacancies in Wealth Management
entry.content:
SBI Specialist Cadre Officer Recruitment 2025: 896 Vacancies in Wealth Management

India's largest bank invites applications for VP (SRM), AVP (RM), and Customer Relationship Executive roles on a contract basis. Online applications open from December 2 to December 23, 2025.

Post Name: VP Wealth (SRM), AVP Wealth (RM), Customer Relationship Executive
Last Date: 23/12/2025
Total Posts: 896

The State Bank of India (SBI), a Fortune 500 company and India's largest public sector bank, has announced a major recruitment drive for its Wealth Management Business Unit. Under Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2025-26/17, SBI has invited online applications from eligible Indian citizens for appointment to various Specialist Cadre Officer posts on a contractual basis. This is a significant opportunity for experienced professionals in the banking and financial services sector to join a prestigious organization and build a rewarding career in wealth management.

This recruitment aims to fill a total of 896 vacancies for the positions of Vice President (VP) Wealth (Senior Relationship Manager), Assistant Vice President (AVP) Wealth (Relationship Manager), and Customer Relationship Executive (CRE). The engagement will be on a contract basis for an initial period of 5 years, which is renewable. The selection process will be based on shortlisting and one or more rounds of interviews, making it a direct pathway for skilled candidates. Interested individuals are requested to apply online through the official SBI careers website from December 2, 2025, to December 23, 2025. This article provides a comprehensive overview of the entire recruitment process, including eligibility, job profiles, remuneration, and the application procedure.

Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. This is a contractual government job opportunity with postings across various circles in India.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization State Bank of India (SBI)
Advertisement Number CRPD/SCO/2025-26/17
Job Type Contractual (Government Sector)
Job Location Various Centres across India (Circle-wise posting)
Online Application & Fee Payment Dates From 02.12.2025 to 23.12.2025

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 896 vacancies across three different roles in the Wealth Management vertical. The vacancies include both regular and backlog positions and are distributed among various reservation categories.

Post Name Total Vacancies
VP Wealth (SRM) 506
AVP Wealth (RM) 206
Customer Relationship Executive 284
Grand Total 896
Note: The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank. Candidates can apply for more than one post if they fulfill all eligibility criteria for the respective posts.

Application Fee and Intimation Charges

  • For UR/EWS/OBC candidates: ₹750/- (Seven Hundred Fifty only).
  • For SC/ST/PwBD candidates: No fees/intimation charges.

The fee is non-refundable and must be paid online through the payment gateway integrated with the application. The application will be considered complete only after the fee has been successfully paid.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet the eligibility criteria regarding age, educational qualifications, and post-qualification experience as of May 1, 2025.

Post Name Eligibility Requirements (Age, Qualification, Experience)
VP Wealth (SRM)
  • Age: Minimum 26 years, Maximum 42 years.
  • Qualification: Graduation from a Government recognized University or Institution. MBA and certifications like NISM/CFP are preferred.
  • Experience: Minimum 6 years of post-qualification experience as a Relationship Manager or similar role in Wealth Management with leading Public/Private/Foreign Banks/Wealth Management Firms/AMCs.
AVP Wealth (RM)
  • Age: Minimum 23 years, Maximum 35 years.
  • Qualification: Graduation from a Government recognized University or Institution. Post-graduation and certifications like NISM/CFP are preferred.
  • Experience: Minimum 3 years of post-qualification experience as a Relationship Manager or similar role in Wealth Management with leading Public/Private/Foreign Banks/Wealth Management Firms/AMCs.
Customer Relationship Executive (CRE)
  • Age: Minimum 20 years, Maximum 35 years.
  • Qualification: Graduation from a Government recognized University or Institution.
  • Specific Skill: Must have a valid driving license for a two-wheeler.
  • Experience: Experience in documentation of financial products and good communication skills are desirable.

Selection Process

The selection process for these Specialist Cadre Officer posts will be direct and will not involve a written examination. The process is designed to identify candidates with the right experience and skills for wealth management roles.

  1. Shortlisting: The first step is the shortlisting of applications. Merely fulfilling the minimum qualification and experience will not guarantee a call for an interview. The shortlisting committee constituted by the Bank will decide the shortlisting parameters. An adequate number of candidates, as decided by the Bank, will be shortlisted for the interview.
  2. Interview: The shortlisted candidates will be called for one or more rounds of personal, telephonic, or video interviews. The interview will carry a total of 100 marks. The qualifying marks in the interview will be decided by the Bank. The Bank's decision to call candidates for the interview shall be final.
  3. CTC Negotiation: CTC negotiation will be done with the candidates one-by-one at the time of the interview or after the completion of the interview process separately. The final CTC offered will depend upon the candidate's experience, current emoluments, and place of posting.
  4. Merit List: The merit list for selection will be prepared in descending order based on the scores obtained in the interview only. In case of a tie, candidates will be ranked according to their age in descending order.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for this recruitment. The selection is based on shortlisting and interviews. Therefore, candidates do not need to prepare for any specific exam pattern or syllabus. Preparation should focus on the interview.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these Specialist Cadre Officer posts. These are office-based roles focused on client relationships and financial advisory.

How to Prepare for the Interview

Since the selection is based solely on the interview, your preparation should be centered around it. Here are some key areas to focus on:

  1. Domain Knowledge: Be thorough with all aspects of wealth management. This includes various investment products (mutual funds, equities, bonds, PMS), insurance products, financial planning, risk profiling, and portfolio management.
  2. Market Awareness: Stay updated with the latest trends in the financial markets, economic policies, and regulatory changes (SEBI, IRDAI, RBI).
  3. Know Your Resume: Be prepared to discuss every point mentioned in your resume in detail, especially your previous roles, responsibilities, and achievements (e.g., AUM managed, revenue generated, client acquisition numbers).
  4. Behavioral and Situational Questions: Prepare for questions like "How would you handle a difficult client?" or "Describe a time you achieved a challenging sales target." Use the STAR (Situation, Task, Action, Result) method to structure your answers.
  5. Communication Skills: As these are relationship management roles, your communication, interpersonal, and negotiation skills will be critically evaluated. Practice articulating your thoughts clearly and confidently.

Best Books for Preparation

While there is no written exam, you can refer to the following resources to strengthen your domain knowledge for the interview:

  • For Wealth Management Concepts: NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund Distributors), NISM Series XXI-A (Portfolio Management Services), and material for the Certified Financial Planner (CFP) certification.
  • For Market Updates: Financial newspapers like The Economic Times, Business Standard, and websites like Moneycontrol.
  • For General Banking Awareness: "Banking Awareness" by Arihant Publications or similar books can provide a good overview of the banking industry.

How to Apply

Candidates must apply online through the official SBI careers website. The process is straightforward and involves registration, filling the application, uploading documents, and paying the fee.

  1. Visit the SBI careers page at https://sbi.bank.in/web/careers/current-openings.
  2. Find the advertisement for "ENGAGEMENT OF SPECIALIST CADRE OFFICERS ON CONTRACT BASIS" (Advertisement No. CRPD/SCO/2025-26/17) and click on "Apply Online".
  3. Register yourself by providing your basic details to generate a provisional registration number and password.
  4. Log in and carefully fill out the online application form with your personal, academic, and professional details.
  5. Upload your scanned photograph, signature, and all required documents (Resume, ID Proof, Educational Certificates, Experience Certificates, etc.) in the specified format (mostly PDF).
  6. Pay the application fee of ₹750/- (if applicable) through the online payment gateway.
  7. After final submission, take a printout of the system-generated online application form and the e-receipt for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates need to upload the following documents in PDF format (unless specified otherwise) during the online application process:

  1. Recent Photograph and Signature (in JPG/JPEG format).
  2. Brief Resume.
  3. ID Proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, etc.).
  4. Proof of Date of Birth (10th/12th Marksheet or Birth Certificate).
  5. Educational Certificates (Relevant Mark-Sheets/Degree Certificates).
  6. Experience Certificates from all previous and current employers.
  7. Form-16/Offer Letter/Latest Salary Slip from the current employer.
  8. Caste Certificate/PwBD Certificate, if applicable.
  9. No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the current employer (to be submitted at the time of interview).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Click Here
Download Official Notification Available on the SBI Careers Page
Official SBI Website https://sbi.co.in

FAQs

Question 1: Is this a permanent job?

No, this is a contractual engagement for an initial period of 5 years. The contract is renewable for another 4 years at the discretion of the Bank.

Question 2: Is there a written test for this recruitment?

No, the selection process does not include a written test. It is based on shortlisting and one or more rounds of interviews.

Question 3: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, the notification states that a candidate can apply for more than one post if they otherwise fulfill all the eligibility criteria for the respective posts.

Question 4: Do I need to send a hard copy of the application form to SBI?

No, hard copies of the application and other documents are not required to be sent to the office. The entire process, including document upload, is online.

entry.title: JSSC Special Teacher Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for Special Education Teachers in Jharkhand
entry.content:
JSSC Special Teacher Recruitment 2025: Bumper Vacancies for Special Education Teachers in Jharkhand

A golden opportunity for Intermediate and Graduate trained candidates to become Special Education Assistant Teachers. Online applications open from December 14, 2025, to January 13, 2026.

Post Name: Special Education Assistant Acharya (Special Education Teacher)
Last Date: 13/01/2026
Total Posts: District-wise vacancies (Large Number)

The Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC) has announced a large and significant recruitment drive under the state's Department of School Education and Literacy. Through Advertisement No. 08/2025, online applications have been invited for the "Jharkhand Intermediate and Graduate Trained Special Education Assistant Teacher Combined Competitive Examination - 2025" (JIGTSEATCCE-2025). This recruitment campaign presents a golden opportunity for qualified and trained candidates who wish to build a career in the field of Special Education and contribute to educating Children with Special Needs (CWSN) in the government schools of Jharkhand.

Through this recruitment, Special Education Assistant Teachers will be appointed at two levels in various districts of the state: Intermediate Trained Assistant Acharya (for Classes 1 to 5) and Graduate Trained Assistant Acharya (for Classes 6 to 8). This is a Regular Recruitment drive, offering a stable and respected government job to the youth of the state and the nation. JSSC, known for its transparent and fair selection processes, will organize a combined competitive examination for this recruitment. This comprehensive article provides all the essential information you need to understand the eligibility, selection process, and application procedure for this important opportunity.

JSSC Special Teacher Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other key information.

Particulars Details
Organization Name Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC)
Examination Name JIGTSEATCCE-2025
Application Start Date December 14, 2025
Application Last Date January 13, 2026
Job Type State Government (Regular)
Job Location Various districts of Jharkhand

Vacancy Details (District-wise)

JSSC has announced large-scale vacancies across all 24 districts of the state under this recruitment. The vacancies are divided into two main levels and various subjects. The example of Ranchi district is given below for reference; for the complete list, please refer to the official notification.

(A) Intermediate Trained Special Education Assistant Acharya (Class 1 to 5) - Ranchi District Example

Reservation Category Total Posts Reserved for Women
Unreserved 74 4
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 68 3
Scheduled Caste (SC) 9 0
Extremely Backward Class (BC-I) 9 0

(B) Graduate Trained Special Education Assistant Acharya (Class 6 to 8) - Maths & Science - Ranchi District Example

Reservation Category Total Posts Reserved for Women
Unreserved 11 1
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 9 0
Scheduled Caste (SC) 1 0
Extremely Backward Class (BC-I) 2 0
Note: This is only an example. The detailed vacancy breakdown for all districts and subjects is provided from Page 2 to Page 30 in the official notification.

Salary and Pay Scale

Selected teachers will be offered an attractive salary package as per the rules of the Government of Jharkhand. The pay matrix level varies according to the post.

  • Intermediate Trained Assistant Acharya (Class 1 to 5): Pay Level-4 (₹ 25,500 - ₹ 81,100/-)
  • Graduate Trained Assistant Acharya (Class 6 to 8): Pay Level-5 (₹ 29,200 - ₹ 92,300/-)

In addition to the basic pay, the salary will include Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and other benefits as applicable under state government rules, making it a competitive remuneration package.

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Specific academic and professional qualifications are prescribed for each level of teacher. Along with these qualifications, it is mandatory for the candidate to have a valid RCI CRR number and to have passed the JETAT (Jharkhand Teacher Eligibility Test).

Educational Qualification

Post Name Minimum Educational Qualification
Intermediate Trained Assistant Acharya (Class 1 to 5)
  1. Passed +2 or Higher Secondary with a minimum of 50% marks.
  2. Two-year D.Ed (Special Education) OR one-year Diploma in Special Education (DSE) OR Diploma in Community Based Rehabilitation (DCBR) OR Diploma in Multi Rehabilitation Worker (MRW) and related certificates. (Many other equivalent qualifications for different disability categories are also valid, the full list is in the notification).
  3. Mandatory: A valid CRR number from the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI).
  4. Mandatory: Passed the Teacher Eligibility Test (JETAT) for Class 1 to 5 conducted by the Government of Jharkhand.
Graduate Trained Assistant Acharya (Class 6 to 8)
  1. Bachelor's Degree in the relevant subject/subject group with a minimum of 50% marks.
  2. B.Ed. (Special Education) OR B.Ed. (General) with a one/two-year Diploma in Special Education OR equivalent qualification.
  3. Mandatory: A valid CRR number from the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI).
  4. Mandatory: Passed the Teacher Eligibility Test (JETAT) for Class 6 to 8 conducted by the Government of Jharkhand.

Age Limit

The age of the candidates will be calculated based on the following reference dates: Minimum Age: 01.08.2025; Maximum Age: 01.08.2025.

CategoryMinimum AgeMaximum Age
All Candidates 21 years -
Unreserved and EWS - 40 years
Extremely Backward Class (BC-I) & Backward Class (BC-II) (Male) - 42 years
Women (Unreserved, EWS, BC-I & II) - 43 years
Scheduled Caste (SC) (Male & Female) - 45 years
Scheduled Tribe (ST) (Male & Female) - 45 years
Note: Persons with Disabilities (PwD) will be given an additional relaxation of 5 years in the upper age limit.

Selection Process

The selection process for Special Education Assistant Teachers will be based on a single-stage Main Examination.

  1. Main Examination: The examination may be Computer Based (CBT) or OMR based. All questions will be objective and multiple-choice type. A significant advantage for candidates is that there will be NO negative marking for incorrect answers. The process of normalization of marks may be adopted.
  2. Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based on the normalized marks obtained in the main examination.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates will be called for document verification based on their position in the merit list.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Exam Pattern for Intermediate Trained Assistant Acharya (Class 1 to 5)

PaperSubjectQuestionsMarksDuration
Paper-1 Mother Tongue (Hindi/English/Urdu/Sanskrit - any one) - (Qualifying) 100 100 2 Hours
Paper-2 Notified Tribal/Regional Language 100 100 2 Hours
Paper-3 Part 1: General Knowledge & Current Affairs (50 Marks) 100 100 3 Hours
Part 2: Child Development & Pedagogy (in context of Special Education) (50 Marks)
Paper-3 (Cont.) Part 3: Social Science 50 50 (Composite Paper)
Part 4: Science & Mathematics 50 50

Exam Pattern for Graduate Trained Assistant Acharya (Class 6 to 8)

PaperSubjectQuestionsMarksDuration
Paper-1 Mother Tongue - (Qualifying) 100 100 2 Hours
Paper-2 Notified Tribal/Regional Language 100 100 2 Hours
Paper-3 General Knowledge & Current Affairs (50 Marks) + Child Development & Pedagogy (in context of Special Education) (50 Marks) 100 100 2 Hours
Paper-4 Optional Subject (Mathematics/Science, Social Science, or Language) 180 180 3 Hours
Note on Qualifying Paper: Paper-1 is only qualifying in nature. It is mandatory to score at least 30% marks in it. These marks will not be added to the final merit list.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for these teaching posts. The selection is based purely on merit in the main examination.

How to Prepare for the JSSC Special Teacher Exam

A structured and dedicated preparation strategy is essential for success in this competitive exam.

  1. Understand the Syllabus in Depth: Analyze the detailed syllabus provided in the official notification. Pay special attention to the weightage of each section.
  2. Master the Core Subjects:
    • Child Development & Pedagogy (Special Education): This is a critical area. Focus on theories of learning, inclusive education, teaching methodologies for CWSN, various disabilities, and assessment tools.
    • Main Subjects (Science, Maths, Social Science): Revise the concepts up to the 10th/12th standard thoroughly.
  3. General Knowledge & Current Affairs: Stay updated with national and international current events. Focus on the history, geography, polity, and culture of Jharkhand.
  4. Language Papers: Do not neglect the qualifying paper (Paper-1) and the scoring regional language paper (Paper-2). Practice grammar and comprehension for both.
  5. No Negative Marking - A Key Strategy Point: Since there is no penalty for wrong answers, you should attempt all questions in the examination. This maximizes your chances of scoring higher.
  6. Practice with Mock Tests: Solve previous years' JETAT papers and other teacher recruitment exam papers to understand the pattern. Take mock tests to improve your time management and identify your weak areas.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books and resources to aid your preparation:

  • Child Development & Pedagogy: "Child Development and Pedagogy" by Arun Kumar Singh, and specialized books on Inclusive Education by authors like S.K. Mangal. Also refer to RCI publications.
  • General Knowledge (Jharkhand): "Know Your State - Jharkhand" by Arihant Publications or "Jharkhand Samanya Gyan" by Udaan Publication.
  • General Studies: "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • Subject-Specific Books: NCERT textbooks from Class 6 to 12 are the best resources for Science, Maths, and Social Science.
  • Regional Language: Standard grammar and literature books for the specific language you choose (e.g., Nagpuri, Khortha).

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must apply through the official JSSC website.

  1. First, visit the official JSSC website: www.jssc.nic.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the link "Online Application for JIGTSEATCCE-2025".
  3. Click on "New Registration" and complete the registration process by filling in your basic details. You will receive a registration number and password.
  4. Log in with the registration number and password and fill out the detailed application form.
  5. Upload your scanned photograph and signature.
  6. Carefully review all the information filled in the application form before submitting.
  7. On the next day, log in again and pay the examination fee.
  8. One day after the fee payment, log in again and take a printout of the final submitted application form.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep the following documents ready before starting the online application process and for future verification:

  1. Valid Email ID and Mobile Number.
  2. Scanned copy of recent Photograph and Signature.
  3. All educational certificates and marksheets (10th, 12th, Graduation, Special Education Diploma/Degree).
  4. JETAT Pass Certificate.
  5. RCI Registration (CRR Number) Certificate.
  6. Domicile Certificate of Jharkhand (for reservation).
  7. Caste/Category Certificate (if applicable).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Link will be active on Dec 14, 2025)
JSSC Official Website https://jssc.nic.in/

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the JSSC Special Teacher recruitment?

The last date to apply online is January 13, 2026 (until midnight).

Question 2: Is it mandatory to have passed JETAT?

Yes, it is mandatory to have passed JETAT for the respective level (Class 1-5 for Intermediate posts and Class 6-8 for Graduate posts).

Question 3: Is RCI registration (CRR Number) required?

Yes, a valid CRR number from the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI) is mandatory for all posts.

Question 4: Will there be negative marking in the exam?

No, there will be no deduction of marks for incorrect answers in this examination.

Question 5: Can candidates from outside Jharkhand apply?

Yes, all citizens of India can apply, but the benefits of reservation will only be given to the local residents of Jharkhand.

entry.title: Panchkula Civil Hospital Recruitment 2025: Doctor, Counselor, Data Manager की भर्ती
entry.content:
Panchkula Civil Hospital Recruitment 2025: Doctor, Counselor, Data Manager की भर्ती

District Civil Hospital, Panchkula ने “Addiction Treatment Facilities (ATF)” प्रोजेक्ट के अंतर्गत Contract Basis पर भर्ती के लिए आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं। आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि 10 नवंबर 2025 है।

Panchkula Civil Hospital ने “Addiction Treatment Facilities (ATF)” प्रोजेक्ट के तहत Doctor, Counselor और Data Manager पदों के लिए भर्ती निकाली है। यह भर्ती Contract Basis पर होगी और इसका कार्यकाल 31 मार्च 2026 तक रहेगा। यह प्रोजेक्ट Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment (MoSJE), Government of India द्वारा वित्तपोषित है।

रिक्तियाँ, योग्यता और वेतन विवरण

पदकुल पदयोग्यतावेतन
Doctor (Medical Officer)1MBBS डिग्री और Medical Council में पंजीकरण। Psychiatry में MD वालों को प्राथमिकता।₹60,000/-
Counselor2Psychology / Social Work / Sociology में Graduate या Master’s Degree।₹20,000/-
Data Manager1Graduate। Computer Application का ज्ञान/अनुभव वांछनीय।₹15,000/-
नोट: यह पद NHM के अंतर्गत नहीं आते। जिन्होंने पूर्व विज्ञापन (No.MH/PKL/2025/85 Dated 22.08.2025) के अनुसार आवेदन किया था, उन्हें पुनः आवेदन करने की आवश्यकता नहीं है।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. Application Scrutiny – सभी आवेदन पत्रों की जांच की जाएगी।
  2. Written Test / Interview – योग्य उम्मीदवारों को लिखित परीक्षा या साक्षात्कार के लिए बुलाया जा सकता है।
  3. Document Verification – अंतिम चयन से पहले मूल दस्तावेज़ों की जांच होगी।

सभी चयन संबंधी अपडेट NHM Haryana (www.nhmharyana.gov.in) या Haryana Health Department (haryanahealth.gov.in) की वेबसाइट पर प्रकाशित किए जाएंगे।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से Offline है। उम्मीदवारों को आवेदन पत्र भरकर Registered Post के माध्यम से भेजना होगा।

आवेदन के चरण:
  1. Application Form डाउनलोड करें या निर्धारित प्रारूप में A4 पेपर पर तैयार करें।
  2. फॉर्म को हाथ से भरें और हस्ताक्षर करें।
  3. निम्नलिखित दस्तावेज़ों की Self-Attested Photocopy संलग्न करें:
    • शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र (Matriculation से अंतिम डिग्री तक)
    • अनुभव प्रमाणपत्र (यदि हो)
    • Doctor के लिए Registration Certificate
    • जाति और निवास प्रमाणपत्र (यदि लागू हो)
  4. सभी दस्तावेज़ों को एक लिफाफे में बंद करके नीचे दिए गए पते पर भेजें।

पता:
District Health and Family Welfare Society,
O/o Civil Surgeon, A-Block, 5th Floor, Sector-6,
Panchkula, Haryana

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंकवेबसाइट
NHM Haryana Websiteयहाँ क्लिक करें
Health Department Haryanaयहाँ क्लिक करें
Official Notification PDFडाउनलोड करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?

10 नवंबर 2025 शाम 4:00 बजे तक आवेदन पत्र पहुँच जाना चाहिए।

Q2: क्या एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं?

हाँ, लेकिन प्रत्येक पद के लिए अलग-अलग आवेदन पत्र भरना होगा।

Q3: आवेदन कैसे भेजना है?

आवेदन केवल Registered Post से ही भेजा जा सकता है। अन्य माध्यम से भेजे गए आवेदन अस्वीकार होंगे।

Q4: क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?

नहीं, यह भर्ती Contract Basis पर है जिसका कार्यकाल 31 मार्च 2026 तक है।

entry.title: SBI आशा स्कॉलरशिप 2025-26: स्कूल से विदेश पढ़ाई तक, पाएं ₹20 लाख तक की छात्रवृत्ति!
entry.content:
SBI आशा स्कॉलरशिप 2025-26: स्कूल से विदेश पढ़ाई तक, पाएं ₹20 लाख तक की छात्रवृत्ति!

स्टेट बैंक ऑफ इंडिया (SBI) फाउंडेशन लेकर आया है एक सुनहरा मौका! स्कूल, कॉलेज, मेडिकल, IIT, IIM और विदेश में पढ़ने वाले विद्यार्थियों के लिए “Platinum Jubilee Asha Scholarship Program 2025-26”.

भारत के सभी प्रतिभाशाली विद्यार्थियों के लिए यह एक शानदार अवसर है। स्टेट बैंक ऑफ इंडिया (SBI) फाउंडेशन ने अपने CSR कार्यक्रम के तहत Platinum Jubilee Asha Scholarship Program 2025-26 शुरू किया है। इसका उद्देश्य ऐसे होनहार छात्रों को आर्थिक सहायता देना है जो आर्थिक तंगी के कारण अपनी पढ़ाई पूरी नहीं कर पा रहे। यह योजना स्कूल से लेकर विदेश में पढ़ाई करने वाले विद्यार्थियों तक सभी के लिए लागू है।

छात्रवृत्ति की श्रेणियाँ और राशि

श्रेणीपात्रताराशि
स्कूल छात्रकक्षा 9 से 12 तक पढ़ने वाले विद्यार्थी₹15,000 तक
स्नातक (UG)Top 300 NIRF कॉलेजों से UG कर रहे छात्र₹75,000 तक
मेडिकलTop 300 मेडिकल कॉलेजों से पढ़ाई कर रहे छात्र₹4,50,000 तक
IIT विद्यार्थीकिसी भी IIT से UG कर रहे छात्र₹2,00,000 तक
IIM विद्यार्थीकिसी भी IIM से MBA/PGDM कर रहे छात्र₹5,00,000 तक
विदेश (SC/ST)Top 200 QS/THE Ranked University से PG कर रहे SC/ST छात्र₹20,00,000 तक

सामान्य पात्रता मानदंड

  • आवेदक भारतीय नागरिक होना चाहिए।
  • पिछली परीक्षा में कम से कम 75% अंक या 7 CGPA होना आवश्यक है।
  • वार्षिक पारिवारिक आय — स्कूल छात्रों के लिए ₹3,00,000 से कम, अन्य के लिए ₹6,00,000 से कम।

आरक्षण और विशेष प्रावधान

  • SC/ST छात्रों को 10% अंकों की छूट मिलेगी (67.50% या 6.3 CGPA पर्याप्त)।
  • 50% सीटें महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए आरक्षित हैं।
  • 50% सीटें SC/ST छात्रों के लिए आरक्षित हैं (25% SC और 25% ST)।
  • स्कॉलरशिप हर साल छात्र के प्रदर्शन और पात्रता पर निर्भर करेगी।

आवेदन कैसे करें?

  1. SBI Foundation या उसके आधिकारिक पोर्टल पर जाएं।
  2. "SBI Platinum Jubilee Asha Scholarship 2025-26" लिंक खोजें।
  3. अपनी श्रेणी चुनें (School, UG, IIT, आदि)।
  4. "Apply Now" पर क्लिक करें और रजिस्ट्रेशन करें।
  5. सभी आवश्यक जानकारी भरें और दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें।
  6. फाइनल सबमिट से पहले विवरण जांच लें।

जरूरी दस्तावेज़

  • आधार कार्ड या अन्य फोटो ID
  • पिछले वर्ष की मार्कशीट
  • वर्तमान वर्ष का एडमिशन प्रमाण
  • आय प्रमाण पत्र (Income Certificate)
  • बैंक पासबुक की प्रति
  • जाति प्रमाण पत्र (यदि लागू हो)
  • हाल का पासपोर्ट साइज फोटो

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षकक्रिया
आधिकारिक स्कॉलरशिप वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
SBI Foundation वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQ)

Q1: कौन आवेदन कर सकता है?
कक्षा 9 से लेकर विदेश में PG कर रहे छात्र पात्र हैं।

Q2: आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि क्या है?
अभी घोषित नहीं हुई है। वेबसाइट पर ध्यान दें।

Q3: क्या सामान्य वर्ग (General) के छात्र आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
हाँ, सभी छात्र आवेदन कर सकते हैं, पर 50% सीटें SC/ST और 50% महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए आरक्षित हैं।

Q4: अधिकतम राशि कितनी है?
₹20,00,000 — जो विदेश में पढ़ाई कर रहे SC/ST छात्रों के लिए है।

entry.title: ISRO SDSC SHAR Recruitment 2025: Apply for 142 Scientist, Engineer, and Technician Posts
entry.content:
ISRO SDSC SHAR Recruitment 2025: Comprehensive Guide to 142 Vacancies

Join the Indian Space Research Organisation at the Satish Dhawan Space Centre - Official Notification SDSC SHAR/RMT/01/2025

Post Name: Scientist/Engineer 'SC', Technical Assistant, Technician 'B', Fireman 'A', Nurse, Cook, Driver
Last Date: November 14, 2025
Total Posts: 142 Vacancies

The Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC SHAR), Sriharikota, is the primary spaceport of India and one of the most vital wings of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO). Often referred to as the gateway to the stars, SDSC SHAR is responsible for the launch of India's most prestigious missions, including the Chandrayaan, Mangalyaan, and the upcoming Gaganyaan human spaceflight mission. To support its expanding mission requirements, ISRO has officially released a recruitment notification for 2025 under Advertisement No. SDSC SHAR/RMT/01/2025. This drive seeks to recruit 142 high-caliber professionals for various roles ranging from Group A Scientist/Engineer 'SC' positions to essential support roles like Technicians, Firemen, and Drivers.

Working at ISRO is a dream for many engineering and science graduates in India. It offers a unique blend of national service, technological innovation, and professional stability. The 2025 recruitment is particularly significant as it covers a wide array of educational backgrounds—including Post-Graduates (M.E/M.Tech), Graduates (B.E/B.Tech/M.Sc), Diploma holders, and ITI certificate holders. The application window is scheduled to open on October 16, 2025, and will accept submissions until November 14, 2025. Candidates selected through this process will be stationed at the beautiful Sriharikota island in Andhra Pradesh, where they will witness firsthand the thunder of rockets lifting off into the cosmos.

The recruitment process for ISRO is known for its technical rigor. Whether you are applying for a technical assistant role or a scientist position, the organization prioritizes deep conceptual understanding over rote memorization. This 2000-word article serves as a comprehensive blueprint for aspirants, providing details on the selection process, exam syllabus, physical standards for security posts, and salary structures based on the 7th Central Pay Commission. For those ready to contribute to India's space legacy, this guide is the first step toward a fulfilling career at ISRO SDSC SHAR.

Recruitment Overview and Key Timeline

Success in ISRO recruitments requires meticulous planning and adherence to deadlines. ISRO is known for its punctuality in examination cycles. Candidates must register on the official SHAR recruitment portal and ensure all technical documents are uploaded accurately. Note that ISRO centers usually conduct their own specific recruitments, and this notification is exclusive to the SHAR center.

Recruitment Detail Information
Organization ISRO - Satish Dhawan Space Centre SHAR
Notification Number SDSC SHAR/RMT/01/2025
Application Start Date October 16, 2025
Last Date to Apply November 14, 2025 (05:00 PM)
Nature of Employment Permanent Central Government
Posting Location Sriharikota (Andhra Pradesh)

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown by Post

The 142 vacancies are categorized into various levels based on technical skills and administrative importance. Scientist/Engineer roles are Group A Gazetted positions, whereas Technical Assistants and Technicians fall under Groups B and C respectively. Below is the post-wise distribution for the 2025 drive.

Post Name Number of Vacancies
Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ 23
Technical Assistant 28
Scientific Assistant 04
Technician ‘B’ 70
Fireman ‘A’ 06
Cook 03
Light/Heavy Vehicle Driver 06
Nurse ‘B’ 02
Total Vacancies 142
Reservation Note: Vacancies include reservations for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), EWS, and Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD) as per the Government of India orders. Candidates belonging to reserved categories must possess valid certificates issued by competent authorities to avail of fee and age relaxations.

Salary Structure and Organizational Benefits

ISRO employees are among the highest-paid central government staff in the technical sector, thanks to the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC). In addition to the basic pay, employees receive substantial allowances and unique benefits related to the Department of Space.

  • Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’ (Level 10): Basic Pay starts at ₹56,100. Total gross emoluments (including HRA and DA) approximate to ₹86,955 per month.
  • Technical/Scientific Assistant (Level 7): Basic Pay starts at ₹44,900. Total gross emoluments approximate to ₹69,595 per month.
  • Technician ‘B’ (Level 3): Basic Pay starts at ₹21,700. Total gross emoluments approximate to ₹33,635 per month.
  • Fireman/Cook/Driver (Level 2): Basic Pay starts at ₹19,900. Total gross emoluments approximate to ₹30,845 per month.

Extra Perks: ISRO employees enjoy the Contributory Health Service Scheme (CHSS) for medical coverage, subsidized housing at Sriharikota or Sullurupeta, canteen facilities, and specialized transport. Furthermore, ISRO offers professional development programs and performance-related incentive schemes unique to the Department of Space.

Eligibility Criteria

ISRO is extremely strict regarding educational qualifications. For Scientist and Engineer roles, candidates must possess a first-class degree. For Technicians, the ITI certification must be from a NCVT/SCVT recognized institute.

  • Scientist/Engineer ‘SC’: M.E/M.Tech or equivalent in the relevant field (Mechanical, Electronics, Structural, etc.) with a minimum aggregate of 60% OR B.E/B.Tech with a minimum of 65% marks/6.84 CGPA.
  • Technical Assistant: First Class Diploma in Engineering in the relevant discipline (Mechanical/Electrical/Electronics/Civil/Chemical).
  • Technician ‘B’: SSLC/SSC (10th) pass plus ITI/NTC/NAC in the respective trade (Fitter, Electrician, Electronic Mechanic, Machinist, etc.).
  • Fireman ‘A’: 10th Standard pass. Candidates must meet the prescribed Physical Fitness and Endurance standards.
  • Cook/Driver: 10th Standard pass with relevant experience (5 years for Cook, 3 years for Driver) and a valid driving license for the respective vehicle category.

Age Limit (as of Nov 14, 2025): For most technical posts, the upper age limit is 35 years. For Fireman and Driver roles, it is typically 25 to 30 years. Standard relaxations apply (5 years for SC/ST and 3 years for OBC).

Selection Process

The selection process at ISRO SDSC SHAR is designed to be purely meritocratic. It typically involves a screening process followed by two major stages.

  1. Written Test: This is an objective-type exam conducted either in Computer Based Test (CBT) format or OMR format. It tests the core technical competence of the candidate.
  2. Skill Test (for Assistants/Technicians): Candidates who clear the written test are called for a skill test in a 1:5 or 1:10 ratio. The skill test is qualifying in nature (Go/No-Go basis).
  3. Interview (for Scientist/Engineer): For Group A posts, the written test is followed by a personal interview. The final selection depends on both written and interview performance (often 50:50 weightage).
  4. Physical Endurance Test: Specific only to Fireman 'A' posts, involving running, rope climbing, and long jump.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Preparation for ISRO requires a focus on core engineering fundamentals. Unlike other government exams, ISRO does not place high weightage on General Awareness; instead, it focuses deeply on technical subjects.

Scientist/Engineer 'SC' Syllabus: Focus on GATE-level syllabus. Major topics include Strength of Materials, Thermodynamics, Fluid Mechanics, Control Systems, Signals and Systems, Electromagnetic Theory, and Digital Electronics. The exam usually consists of 80 objective questions to be solved in 90 minutes.

Technical Assistant/Technician Syllabus: The syllabus is based on the Diploma or ITI curriculum respectively. For Technicians (e.g., Fitter), questions will focus on workshop calculation, safety, measurement, and specific trade tools. For Technical Assistants, the focus is on standard polytechnic curriculum. There are usually 80 MCQs with 1 mark each and 0.33 negative marking.

Physical Standards for Fireman

Fireman 'A' candidates must meet the following mandatory physical requirements to be eligible for the written test results to be considered.

  • Height: Minimum 165 cms (Relaxation for Hill tribes).
  • Weight: Minimum 50 kgs.
  • Chest: 81 cms (Normal) and 86 cms (Expanded).
  • Distant Vision: 6/6 without glasses.
  • Endurance Test: 100-meter run in 15 seconds, climbing a 5-meter rope using hands only, and a long jump of 3.8 meters in 3 attempts.

Expert Preparation Strategy for ISRO SHAR

To succeed at ISRO, candidates must change their mindset from competitive exam "tricks" to "conceptual depth."

Master the Fundamentals: ISRO written tests are known for asking "why" rather than just "what." For example, instead of just solving a circuit, you might be asked about the underlying semiconductor physics. Ensure your basics from the first and second years of engineering/diploma are rock solid.
