Previous Year Papers: ISRO center exams (VSSC, LPSC, SDSC, URSC) often have a similar level of difficulty. Solve at least the last 10 years of papers from all ISRO centers. This will give you a clear idea of the "favorite" topics of ISRO scientists who set the paper.

Skill Test Practice: For Technicians and Technical Assistants, the skill test is crucial. If you are an Electrician, practice wiring circuits and using multi-meters. If you are a Mechanical technician, ensure you can operate lathes and milling machines with precision. Many candidates score full marks in the written test but fail the skill test because they lack hands-on experience.

Best Books for Preparation

Choosing the right books is half the battle. Below are the recommended titles for different levels of the ISRO SDSC SHAR exam.

Scientist/Engineer: GATE previous year solved papers (Made Easy/ACE Academy) and standard textbooks like Morris Mano (Digital) or B.C. Punmia (Civil).
Technical Assistant: Objective Mechanical Engineering by R.S. Khurmi or Diploma-specific guides by G.K. Publications.
Technician 'B': ITI Trade theory books by Arihant Publications or Asian Publishers (Trade specific).
Mathematics: Higher Engineering Mathematics by B.S. Grewal (Essential for Scientists).

How to Apply Online

The application process for ISRO SDSC SHAR is conducted via their integrated recruitment portal. Follow these steps to ensure a valid submission.

  1. Visit the official SHAR website: www.shar.gov.in or apps.shar.gov.in.
  2. Click on the "Careers" link and locate Advertisement No. SDSC SHAR/RMT/01/2025.
  3. Click on "Apply Online." You will be required to register with a valid email and mobile number.
  4. After registration, log in and fill in your educational details, percentage of marks, and experience.
  5. Photograph & Signature: Upload a recent color photograph and signature in the prescribed dimensions (JPEG format).
  6. Fee Payment: Pay the application fee of ₹750 online using Net Banking, Debit/Credit Card, or UPI.
  7. After payment, a "Personalized Payment Confirmation" and Application Number will be generated. Save these carefully.

Application Fee and Refund Policy

ISRO follows a transparent fee structure. All candidates are required to pay an initial fee of ₹750 at the time of application.

  • General/OBC/EWS Male Candidates: Out of ₹750, a refund of ₹500 will be credited to the bank account after the candidate appears for the written test.
  • SC/ST/PwBD/Women/Ex-Servicemen: The full amount of ₹750 will be refunded after the candidate appears for the written test.

Note: No refund will be provided if the candidate fails to attend the written examination. Ensure that the bank account details provided during the application are correct and belong to the candidate or a close relative.

Documents Needed to Apply

Have the following documents ready in digital format (Scanned) before starting the application to avoid session timeouts.

  1. Aadhaar Card: For identity verification.
  2. Educational Certificates: Final degree/diploma/ITI certificate and all-semester mark sheets.
  3. Caste Certificate: For OBC-NCL, SC, or ST candidates (Central Government format).
  4. EWS Certificate: For Economically Weaker Section candidates.
  5. Experience Certificates: Mandatory for Cook and Driver roles.
  6. NOC: No Objection Certificate if you are currently employed in a Government/PSU organization.

Important Links

Resource Description URL Link
Official SDSC SHAR Website www.shar.gov.in
Recruitment Portal (Apply Link) Click Here to Apply
ISRO Main Website www.isro.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: What is the last date to apply for ISRO SDSC SHAR 2025?

The last date for online registration and submission of applications is November 14, 2025, until 05:00 PM IST.

Q2: Can final year students apply for the Scientist/Engineer post?

Generally, ISRO requires candidates to have their final degree and marksheet at the time of application or by the date of document verification. If your results are expected after the closing date, you may not be eligible unless specified otherwise in the detailed advertisement.

Q3: Is there any interview for the Technician 'B' post?

No. For Technician 'B' and Technical Assistant roles, selection is based on the Written Test score and qualifying in the Skill Test. There is no personal interview for these Group C and Group B technical posts.

Q4: What is the vision standard for ISRO technical roles?

For most technical roles, normal vision is required. For the Fireman 'A' post, the standard is very strict: 6/6 distant vision without glasses, and no color blindness is allowed.

Q5: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for multiple posts if you meet the eligibility criteria for each. However, you must pay the application fee for each post separately. Also, ensure that the exam dates do not overlap.

Q6: What is the "Skill Test" like at ISRO?

The Skill Test is practical. For a Fitter, you might be asked to file a metal piece to a specific dimension. For an Electronics TA, you might be asked to design and solder a circuit on a PCB. It is meant to test your actual hands-on ability.

entry.title: PSSSB Stenotypist Recruitment 2025: 109 Posts Open in Various Punjab Govt Departments
entry.content:
PSSSB Stenotypist Recruitment 2025: 109 Posts Open in Various Punjab Govt Departments

A golden opportunity for graduates with Punjabi stenography skills. Online applications are open from December 4 to December 26, 2025.

Post Name: Stenotypist
Last Date: 26/12/2025
Total Posts: 109

The Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB) has released a detailed advertisement (Advertisement No. 14/2025) for the direct recruitment of Group-C Stenotypist posts in various departments of the Punjab Government. This is a golden opportunity for candidates who are graduates, possess skills in Punjabi stenography and computers, and are seeking a stable government job in Punjab. A total of 109 vacancies will be filled through this recruitment drive.

This recruitment is being conducted to fill vacancies in several key departments of the Punjab Government, including the General Administration Department, Revenue Department, Local Audit Wing, Sports Department, Technical Education Department, and various Deputy Commissioner offices. PSSSB, known for its transparent and fair selection processes, will conduct a competitive written examination and a stenography test for this recruitment. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the official PSSSB website, https://sssb.punjab.gov.in, from December 4, 2025, to December 26, 2025. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all the important details you need to know.

PSSSB Recruitment 2025 Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. Candidates are advised to take note of the important dates and other key information to ensure a smooth application process.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB)
Application Start Date December 4, 2025
Last Date to Apply Online December 26, 2025 (up to 5:00 PM)
Last Date to Pay Fee December 30, 2025
Job Type State Government (Group-C)
Job Location Punjab

Vacancy Details Department-wise

PSSSB has announced a total of 109 Stenotypist posts across various departments. These vacancies are classified into different departments and categories. A detailed department-wise breakdown of vacancies is provided below:

Department Name Number of Posts
Chief Local Commissioner, Punjab Bhawan, New Delhi 01
Deputy Commissioner, S.A.S. Nagar 03
Deputy Commissioner, Amritsar 03
Department of Revenue, Rehabilitation and Disaster Management 05
General Administration Department 41
Local Audit Wing, Finance Department 03
Deputy Commissioner, Fatehgarh Sahib 01
Chief Engineer, Panchayati Raj Public Works 04
Deputy Commissioner, Barnala 01
Directorate, Sports Department 11
Deputy Commissioner, Hoshiarpur 04
Department of Local Government 05
Department of Technical Education and Industrial Training 09
Director, Defence Welfare 06
Directorate, Department of Tourism 01
Chief Auditor, Co-operative Societies 06
Additional Director General of Police, Jails (HQ) 01
Additional Director General of Police, Jails (Field Offices) 04
Grand Total 109
Note: The detailed category-wise classification of these vacancies (General, SC, BC, ESM, PwD, EWS, etc.) is available in the official notification. Candidates are advised to check the official notification for the exact number of posts in their respective categories.

Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the application fee online as per their category. The fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

  • General Category: ₹ 1000/-
  • Scheduled Caste (SC) / Backward Class (BC) of Punjab: ₹ 250/-
  • Ex-Servicemen (Self & Dependent): ₹ 200/-
  • Handicapped (PwD): ₹ 500/-

Note: Candidates who had applied and paid the fee for the previous Advertisement No. 07/2023 are exempted from paying the fee again for this recruitment.

Eligibility Criteria: Education and Age Limit

Candidates applying for the post of Stenotypist must possess the following educational and technical qualifications. The cut-off date for calculating age is January 1, 2025.

Educational Qualification

Qualification Type Details
Essential Academic Qualification A candidate must possess a Bachelor's Degree in any discipline from a recognized University or Institution.
Essential Technical Qualification
  1. The candidate must qualify a test in Punjabi Stenography, to be held by the Board, at a speed of 80 words per minute (w.p.m).
  2. The candidate must possess a certificate for completing a course of at least 120 hours in the use of a Personal Computer or Information Technology from a Government recognized institution or a reputed institution which is ISO 9001 certified. OR
  3. The candidate must possess a certificate of 'O' Level from the Department of Electronics Accreditation of Computer Course (DOEACC).

Age Limit (as on 01.01.2025)

Category Maximum Age Limit
General Category 37 years
SC and BC of Punjab 42 years
State and Central Government Employees 45 years
Persons with Disability (PwD) of Punjab 47 years
Widows and Divorced Women 40 years
Ex-Servicemen of Punjab As per Punjab Recruitment Rules, 1982 (after deducting the period of military service from the actual age, the resultant age should not exceed the upper age limit by more than 3 years).
Note for Old Applicants: For candidates who had previously applied under Advertisement No. 07/2023, the age will be calculated as of 01.01.2023, providing them age relaxation.

Selection Process: A Skill-Based Merit System

The selection process for the post of Stenotypist is unique and multi-staged, with a heavy emphasis on practical stenography skills rather than written exam marks for the final merit.

  1. Written Examination (Qualifying Only):
    • All eligible candidates will have to appear for an objective type (MCQ) written examination.
    • This exam will have two parts: Part-A (Punjabi Qualifying Test) and Part-B (Main Examination).
    • Part-A (Punjabi Language Qualifying Test): This test will be of 10th standard level. It is mandatory to score a minimum of 50% marks to qualify. This part is purely qualifying in nature, and its marks will not be considered for the merit list.
    • Part-B (Main Examination): Candidates who clear Part-A will have their Part-B evaluated. It is also mandatory to score a minimum of 50% marks in Part-B to qualify for the next stage. However, the marks of Part-B will also not be considered for the final merit list.
  2. Punjabi Stenography Test (Merit Determining Stage):
    • Candidates who qualify in both parts of the written examination will be called for the Punjabi Stenography Test.
    • This test will be conducted at a speed of 80 words per minute. It will consist of two paragraphs of 200 words each.
    • Candidates will have to transcribe the dictated passage on a computer at a speed of 15 words per minute.
    • A maximum of 8% mistakes will be permissible. Candidates making more than 8% mistakes will be disqualified.
  3. Final Merit List Preparation:
    • The final merit list will be prepared solely on the basis of the number of mistakes made in the Stenography Test.
    • The candidate with the fewest mistakes will be placed at the top of the merit list. The written exam scores are completely discarded for merit preparation.
    • In case of a tie (i.e., two or more candidates have the same number of mistakes), the older candidate will be given preference.
  4. Document Verification (Counseling): Candidates will be called for document verification (counseling) based on their position in the final merit list.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

Written Examination Pattern (Qualifying)

Part Subject No. of Questions Marks Qualifying Marks
Part-A Punjabi Language (10th Standard) 50 50 25 (50%)
Part-B General Knowledge, Current Affairs, English, Punjabi, Logical Reasoning & Mental Ability, ICT 100 100 50 (50%)
Important Note: Both parts are mandatory to qualify but are not used for final merit. There will be negative marking in Part-B (0.25 marks for each wrong answer).

Expected Syllabus

  • Part-A (Punjabi): Grammar, Vocabulary, Synonyms, Antonyms, Sentence Correction, Understanding of standard Punjabi prose (up to 10th level).
  • Part-B (Main Exam):
    • General Knowledge & Current Affairs: History of Punjab & India, Geography, Culture, Polity, Economy, and recent national/international events.
    • English Language: Grammar, Vocabulary, Comprehension, Error Detection.
    • Punjabi Language: Advanced grammar, idioms, and literature.
    • Logical Reasoning & Mental Ability: Series, Coding-Decoding, Puzzles, Analytical Reasoning.
    • Information & Communication Technology (ICT): Basics of Computers, MS Office, Internet, Email, Fundamentals of operating systems.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for the post of Stenotypist, as it is a clerical position.

How to Prepare for the PSSSB Stenotypist Exam

A dual-focus preparation strategy is required: one to qualify the written test and another, more intensive one, to excel in the stenography test.

  1. For the Written Examination (Qualifying):
    • Part-A (Punjabi): Do not take this lightly. Revise your 10th standard Punjabi grammar and practice comprehension. Scoring 50% is mandatory.
    • Part-B: Cover the basics of all subjects mentioned in the syllabus. Since it is only qualifying, focus on your strong areas to ensure you clear the 50% cut-off. Practice with previous PSSSB question papers.
  2. For the Stenography Test (Merit-Making):
    • Daily Practice is Key: Dedicate several hours daily to stenography practice. Your goal is not just speed, but extreme accuracy.
    • Build Speed Gradually: Start with lower speeds and gradually increase to 80 wpm and beyond. Use dictation audios from various sources (news, editorials, etc.).
    • Focus on Accuracy: Every mistake counts towards your final rank. Pay close attention to grammar, punctuation, and spelling.
    • Transcription Practice: Practice transcribing on a computer. Improve your Punjabi typing speed to comfortably meet the 15 wpm transcription requirement.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended resources for your preparation:

  • Punjabi Stenography: Standard Punjabi shorthand practice books and magazines.
  • General Knowledge (Punjab): "Know Your State - Punjab" by Arihant Publications.
  • General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Logical Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • ICT/Computer: "Computer Knowledge" by Lucent Publications.
  • Previous Year Papers: Solve question papers from previous PSSSB exams.

How to Apply for PSSSB Recruitment

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must follow the steps below to apply through the PSSSB's official website:

  1. First, visit the official PSSSB website: https://sssb.punjab.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the "Online Applications" link.
  3. Click on the relevant link for Advertisement No. 14/2025 and then click on "New Registration".
  4. Fill in your basic details to register. You will receive a username and password.
  5. Log in with your username and password and fill out the application form carefully.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, and other required documents (such as graduation degree, computer certificate, category certificate).
  7. After submitting the application form, pay the application fee according to your category via the online payment gateway.
  8. Keep a printout of the final submitted application form and the fee receipt for future reference.

Note for Old Applicants: Candidates who applied under Advertisement No. 07/2023 do not need to register again. They can log in with their old User ID and Password to revalidate and submit their application without any fee.

Documents Needed to Apply

The following documents will be required during the online application and at the time of counseling:

  1. Scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Matriculation (10th) certificate for proof of date of birth and passing Punjabi subject.
  3. Graduation Degree and marksheets of all semesters.
  4. Certificate for the 120-hour computer course or 'O' Level certificate.
  5. Domicile Certificate of Punjab (not older than 5 years).
  6. Reservation category certificate (SC/BC/EWS/ESM/PwD, etc.).
  7. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (From 04.12.2025)
Official Website https://sssb.punjab.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the PSSSB Stenotypist recruitment?

The last date to apply online is December 26, 2025 (up to 5:00 PM), and the last date for fee payment is December 30, 2025.

Question 2: What is the minimum educational qualification for these posts?

The candidate must be a graduate in any discipline, have knowledge of Punjabi stenography, and possess a certificate for a prescribed computer course.

Question 3: What is the selection process?

The selection process includes two written tests (Part-A and Part-B) and a Punjabi Stenography Test. The final merit list will be prepared based *only* on the performance in the Stenography Test.

Question 4: I applied for the previous Advertisement No. 07/2023. Do I need to apply again?

Yes, you need to log in with your old credentials to revalidate and submit your application, but you will not have to pay the fee again.

Question 5: Can candidates from outside Punjab apply?

Yes, any citizen of India can apply, provided they can pass the Punjabi Stenography test. However, reservation benefits are only available to residents (Domicile) of Punjab.

entry.title: STPI Recruitment 2025: Scientist 'B', Officer, Assistant, and Other Posts Open
entry.content:
STPI Recruitment 2025: Scientist 'B', Officer, Assistant, and Other Posts Open

A major recruitment drive by Software Technology Parks of India for 24 Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' posts. Online applications open from November 29, 2025, to January 12, 2026.

Post Name: Scientist 'B', Administrative Officer, Assistant, MTSS, and Others
Last Date: 12/01/2026
Total Posts: 24

Software Technology Parks of India (STPI), a premier science and technology autonomous body under the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), Government of India, has released a major recruitment notification (Employment Notice No. 2(2)/I/STPI-HQ/2025-26) for its various centers across the country. This recruitment drive is being conducted to fill various posts in Group 'A' (S&T), below Group 'A' (S&T and Non-S&T), and Group 'C', with a total of 24 vacancies announced. This presents a fantastic opportunity for talented and qualified candidates who wish to contribute to the growth, innovation, and startup ecosystem of India's IT/ITES industry.

STPI plays a pivotal role in promoting the IT industry, encouraging exports, and fostering research and development in emerging technologies such as IoT, Blockchain, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Machine Learning (ML), Cybersecurity, and Drones. With 68 centers currently spread across the country, STPI offers a dynamic and challenging career. This recruitment will be conducted through both Direct Recruitment and Absorption methods, providing opportunities for fresh talented candidates as well as experienced professionals already working in the government sector. The online application window is open from November 29, 2025, to January 12, 2026. This comprehensive article provides all the essential information you need to apply for these coveted positions.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise summary of the key details regarding this recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameSoftware Technology Parks of India (STPI)
Advertisement Number2(2)/I/STPI-HQ/2025-26
Job LocationAll India, including Bengaluru, Delhi, Hyderabad, Pune, Noida, Guwahati, Jaipur, etc.
Application ModeOnline
Application Start DateNovember 29, 2025
Application Last DateJanuary 12, 2026

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

STPI has announced a total of 24 posts under this recruitment drive. These posts are in various technical (S&T) and non-technical (Non-S&T) categories and fall under Group 'A', 'B', and 'C'. A detailed breakdown of all the posts is given below:

Sr. No. Post Name Post Code Pay Matrix Level No. of Posts Method of Recruitment
1 Member Technical Staff - E-I (Scientist 'B') E-1 (S&T) Level 10 05 Direct Recruitment
2 Member Technical Support Staff (MTSS) ES-V ES-5 (S&T) Level 6 02 Absorption / Direct Recruitment
3 Administrative Officer (A-V) A-5 (Non-S&T) Level 7 03 Absorption / Direct Recruitment
4 Assistant (A-IV) A-4 (Non-S&T) Level 6 03 Absorption / Direct Recruitment
5 Member Technical Support Staff (MTSS) ES-IV ES-4 (S&T) Level 5 04 Absorption / Direct Recruitment
6 Member Technical Support Staff (MTSS) ES-III ES-3 (S&T) Level 4 01 Direct Recruitment
7 Member Technical Support Staff (MTSS) ES-II ES-2 (S&T) Level 2 01 Direct Recruitment
8 Assistant (A-III) A-3 (Non-S&T) Level 5 01 Absorption / Direct Recruitment
9 Assistant (A-II) A-2 (Non-S&T) Level 4 02 Absorption / Direct Recruitment
10 Assistant (A-I) A-1 (Non-S&T) Level 2 01 Direct Recruitment
11 Office Attendant (S-I) S-1 (Non-S&T) Level 1 01 Direct Recruitment

Salary Details and Pay Scale

STPI follows the Central Government pay scales and offers an attractive salary structure. The Pay Matrix Level and corresponding pay scale for each post are given below:

Pay Matrix Level Pay Scale Corresponding Post (Example)
Level 10 ₹ 56,100 - ₹ 1,77,500 Scientist 'B'
Level 7 ₹ 44,900 - ₹ 1,42,400 Administrative Officer (A-V)
Level 6 ₹ 35,400 - ₹ 1,12,400 MTSS ES-V, Assistant (A-IV)
Level 5 ₹ 29,200 - ₹ 92,300 MTSS ES-IV, Assistant (A-III)
Level 4 ₹ 25,500 - ₹ 81,100 MTSS ES-III, Assistant (A-II)
Level 2 ₹ 19,900 - ₹ 63,200 MTSS ES-II, Assistant (A-I)
Level 1 ₹ 18,000 - ₹ 56,900 Office Attendant (S-I)

In addition to the basic pay, the gross salary will include Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance (TA), and other benefits as per Central Government rules, making the total compensation package highly competitive.

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Experience

Each post has specific requirements for educational qualifications and experience. The details for Direct Recruitment are provided below:

Post Name Educational Qualification and Experience
Scientist 'B' Bachelor's Degree in Engineering/Technology with First Class in Electronics/Electricals/Electronics & Communications/Computer Science/IT OR Master's Degree in Computer Applications (MCA) with First Class OR M.Sc. with First Class in Physics/Maths/Statistics/Computer Science/Electronics/IT OR PhD in a relevant subject. (No experience required).
MTSS ES-V Three-year Diploma in Electronics/Computer Science/IT/Telecommunication OR Bachelor's Degree in a relevant subject OR DOEACC 'A' Level Certificate, along with two years of experience.
Administrative Officer (A-V) Graduate in any discipline with 6 years of experience OR Post Graduate in any discipline with 4 years of experience OR MBA with 1 year of experience (in Personnel/Admin/Vigilance).
Assistant (A-IV) Graduate in any discipline with 4 years of experience OR Post Graduate in any discipline with 2 years of experience (in Personnel/Admin/Finance/Vigilance).
MTSS ES-IV Three-year Diploma in Electronics/Computer Science/IT/Telecommunication OR Bachelor's Degree in a relevant subject OR DOEACC 'A' Level Certificate, along with one year of experience.
MTSS ES-III Three-year Diploma in Electronics/Computer Science/IT/Telecommunication OR Bachelor's Degree in a relevant subject OR DOEACC 'A' Level Certificate. (No experience required).
MTSS ES-II Matriculation with ITI/NTC/NAC in Electrical/Electronics/Mechanical/IT OR Intermediate (10+2) with Science OR DOEACC 'O' Level Certificate.
Assistant (A-III) Graduate in any discipline with 2 years of experience OR Post Graduate in any discipline with 1 year of experience (in Personnel/Admin/Finance/Vigilance).
Assistant (A-II) Graduate in any discipline from a recognized university. Desirable: Six months certificate course in Computer Operations.
Assistant (A-I) Intermediate (10+2) with a typing speed of 35 w.p.m in English or 30 w.p.m in Hindi on a computer.
Office Attendant (S-I) Matriculation or equivalent. Desirable: Knowledge of typing, computer, and operating photocopy/fax machines.
Note: The eligibility conditions for Absorption are different and are detailed in the official notification. Candidates applying through absorption must be existing employees of Central/State Govt/PSUs.

Age Limit (for Direct Recruitment)

The age will be calculated as of the closing date of the application, i.e., January 12, 2026. The maximum age limit for direct recruitment is as follows:

Post NameMaximum Age Limit
Scientist 'B'30 years
MTSS ES-V36 years
Administrative Officer (A-V)40 years
Assistant (A-IV)36 years
MTSS ES-IV34 years
Assistant (A-III)34 years
MTSS ES-III32 years
Assistant (A-II)32 years
MTSS ES-II30 years
Assistant (A-I)30 years
Office Attendant (S-I)30 years
Note: Age relaxation for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PWD) and government employees will be applicable as per the rules of the Government of India.

Selection Process

The selection process varies for different posts, primarily involving a written examination, and for some posts, an interview or trade test.

  • For Scientist 'B' (MTS E-I): The selection process will include a written examination followed by a personal interview. The weightage for the final merit list will be 70% for the written test and 30% for the interview.
  • For MTSS ES-II and Office Attendant (S-I): The selection process will involve a written examination. Candidates who pass the written test will also have to appear for a trade test/skill test of a qualifying nature for final selection.
  • For All Other Posts: For all other posts (such as Administrative Officer, Assistant, MTSS ES-V, IV, III), the selection will be based solely on the marks obtained in the written examination.

Written Examination Pattern

The written examination will have different patterns for various groups of posts:

Post Group Exam Pattern and Subjects Total Questions/Marks Duration
MTSS (ES-V, IV, III) A. General Intelligence & Reasoning (20 Marks)
B. General Awareness (20 Marks)
C. Numerical Aptitude (20 Marks)
D. English Comprehension (20 Marks)
E. Technical Area (STPI Specific) (70 Marks)		 150 Questions 2.5 Hours
Administrative Officer (A-V), Assistant (A-IV) A. General Intelligence & Reasoning (25 Marks)
B. General Awareness (25 Marks)
C. Numerical Aptitude (25 Marks)
D. English Comprehension (25 Marks)
E. Central Govt. Rules & Procedures (50 Marks)		 150 Questions 2.5 Hours
Assistant (A-III, A-II) A. General Intelligence & Reasoning (40 Marks)
B. General Awareness (35 Marks)
C. Numerical Aptitude (35 Marks)
D. English Comprehension (40 Marks)		 150 Questions 2.5 Hours
MTSS (ES-II) and Office Attendant (S-I) An MCQ paper with questions based on General Intelligence & Reasoning, General Awareness, Numerical Aptitude, and English Comprehension. 50 Questions (2 Marks each) 1.5 Hours

How to Prepare for the STPI Exam

Success in the STPI written examination requires a well-structured preparation strategy, as the syllabus is divided into various technical and general sections.

  1. Understand the Syllabus: Before starting your preparation, thoroughly read the prescribed syllabus (Annexure-I and II of the notification) for your post.
  2. General Section (for all posts):
    • General Intelligence & Reasoning: Focus on topics like analogies, series, coding-decoding, Venn diagrams, and problem-solving. Practice puzzles regularly.
    • General Awareness: Concentrate on current events, Indian history, geography, culture, economic scenario, and scientific research.
    • Numerical Aptitude: Practice basic mathematical topics like number systems, percentages, profit & loss, time & work, and geometry.
    • English Comprehension: Work on improving your vocabulary, grammar, and reading comprehension skills.
  3. Technical Section:
    • For Scientist 'B': Study the topics mentioned in the syllabus in-depth, such as Digital Circuits, Computer Architecture, Programming, Data Structures, Algorithms, Operating Systems, Databases, and Computer Networks. Preparing at the GATE (CS/IT) level will be beneficial.
    • For MTSS Posts: Focus on diploma-level subjects of Electronics, Computer Science, and IT. This includes electronic circuits, digital electronics, networking, programming basics, and hardware.
  4. Administrative Section:
    • For Administrative Officer and Assistant (A-IV): Pay special attention to Central Government rules and procedures like GFR, FR & SR, CCS Rules, Pension Rules, RTI Act, etc. Standard government publications and handbooks are the best resources.
  5. Mock Tests and Practice: Regularly solve mock tests and previous years' question papers to improve your speed and accuracy.

How to Apply Online

Candidates must apply online only through the official STPI website. Follow the steps below to apply:

  1. Visit the official STPI website: www.stpi.in.
  2. Go to the "Careers" section and click on the relevant "Employment Notice No. 2(2)/I/STPI-HQ/2025-26".
  3. Click on the "Apply Online" link and fill out the application form carefully.
  4. Pay the application fee via NEFT/RTGS or UPI and note down the UTR/Transaction ID.
  5. Enter the UTR/Transaction ID in the application form.
  6. Ensure all required information and documents are correct, and submit the application form.
  7. Candidates already working in Govt./PSU/Autonomous bodies may submit a copy of the NOC received from their organization at the time of application.
  8. Save a printout of the filled application form for future reference.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Link will be active from 29.11.2025)
Official Website https://www.stpi.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for STPI Recruitment 2025?

The last date for receipt of online applications is January 12, 2026.

Question 2: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for more than one post, but you will have to fill out a separate application form and pay the requisite fee for each post.

Question 3: What is the selection process for the Scientist 'B' post?

The selection process for the Scientist 'B' post includes a written examination followed by a personal interview. The final selection will have a weightage of 70:30 (Written:Interview).

Question 4: What is the application fee for SC/ST and women candidates?

SC, ST, PH, and women candidates are exempted from the payment of the application fee.

Question 5: Can government employees apply?

Yes, employees working in Govt./PSU/Autonomous bodies can apply. They may need to submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer at the time of application or document verification.

entry.title: MKBU Recruitment 2025: Apply Online for 18 Non-Teaching Posts
entry.content:
MKBU Recruitment 2025: Apply Online for 18 Non-Teaching Posts

Maharaja Krishnakumarsinhji Bhavnagar University invites applications for Junior Engineer, Lab Technician, Assistant, and other roles. Apply online before January 22, 2026.

Post Name: Junior Engineer, Field Collector, Lab Technician, Lab Assistant, Assistant, Typist, and more
Last Date: 22 January 2026
Total Posts: 18+ (across two advertisements)

Maharaja Krishnakumarsinhji Bhavnagar University (MKBU), a prominent public university in Gujarat, has released two significant employment notifications for a wide range of non-teaching posts. The university invites online applications from eligible Indian citizens to fill up these positions in accordance with the provisions under the "Gujarat Public Universities Act-2023" and "The Gujarat Public Universities Uniform Statue-2024". The first notification (Advertisement No. NT03-12/2025) is for various technical and scientific roles, while the second (Advertisement No. NT02-12/2025) is for clerical positions.

This is a fantastic opportunity for candidates with qualifications ranging from Diplomas and Bachelor's degrees to secure a stable government job within the esteemed environment of a state university. The recruitment drive includes a diverse array of posts such as Junior Engineer (Civil), Field Collector, Laboratory Technician, Laboratory Assistant, Assistant, and Typist. The selection process will be based on a competitive OMR-based MCQ examination designed to test both general knowledge and subject-specific expertise. The application process is entirely online, and candidates can apply through the university's official website. The last date to apply is January 22, 2026. This article provides a comprehensive guide to all essential details, including post-wise eligibility, selection process, and the application procedure.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government positions in a State Public University located in Bhavnagar, Gujarat. Key dates and details are as follows:

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Maharaja Krishnakumarsinhji Bhavnagar University (MKBU)
Job Type Permanent Government Job (State University)
Job Location Bhavnagar, Gujarat
Online Application Start Date 29 December 2025
Last Date for Online Application 22 January 2026 (up to 11:59:59 PM)

Vacancy Details

The university has announced vacancies across two main advertisements for various non-teaching posts. The details are as follows:

Advertisement No. NT03-12/2025

Job Code Post Name No. of Posts Pay Level
NT-03-01 Junior Engineer (CIVIL) 01 Level-7
NT-03-02 Field Collector 02 Level-5
NT-03-03 Lab Technician 04 Level-5
NT-03-04 Lab Assistant 11 Level-2

Advertisement No. NT02-12/2025

Job Code Post Name No. of Posts Pay Level
NT-02-01 Assistant 25 Level-2
NT-02-02 Typist 01 Level-2
Note: Many of these posts will be on a fixed pay for the first five years as per the Government of Gujarat's policy, after which the full pay scale will be applicable. For example, a Junior Engineer will receive a fixed salary of Rs. 49,600/-, and a Lab Assistant will receive Rs. 26,000/- for the first five years.

Application Fee

  • For General category candidates: Rs. 1000/- (Non-refundable)
  • For other category candidates (SC/ST/SEBC/EWS/PwD): Rs. 500/- (Non-refundable)

The fee must be paid through the online payment gateway. The application will be considered incomplete without the payment of the prescribed fee.

Eligibility Criteria

Each post has specific requirements for educational qualifications and age limits. The upper age limit for all direct recruitment posts is generally 35 years, with relaxations applicable as per Gujarat government rules.

Post Name Essential Qualification and Experience Age Limit
Junior Engineer (CIVIL) Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering with 55% marks OR Diploma in Civil Engineering with 55% marks and at least 2 years of experience as a Junior Engineer or Works Assistant in Building Construction. Must have CCC certificate and adequate knowledge of Gujarati, Hindi, and English. 35 Years
Field Collector Bachelor's degree in Botany as a main subject. Must have CCC certificate and adequate knowledge of Gujarati, Hindi, and English. 35 Years
Lab Technician Bachelor's degree with Microbiology, Physics, or Chemistry as a main subject, depending on the department. Must have CCC certificate and adequate knowledge of Gujarati, Hindi, and English. 35 Years
Lab Assistant Bachelor's degree in the relevant subject (Zoology, Physics, Chemistry, Statistics, Microbiology, Botany). Must have CCC certificate and adequate knowledge of Gujarati, Hindi, and English. 35 Years
Assistant / Typist Bachelor's degree in any discipline. Must have a computer typing speed of 40 wpm in English and 25 wpm in Gujarati. Must have CCC certificate and adequate knowledge of Gujarati, Hindi, and English. 35 Years

Selection Process

The selection process for all these Group 'C' posts will be based on a competitive written examination. The process is designed to be fair and transparent, ensuring that selection is based on merit.

  1. Written Examination (MCQ): An objective-type Multiple Choice Question (MCQ) examination will be conducted. The exam will be for a total of 200 marks.
  2. Document Verification: Candidates who clear the written examination will be shortlisted and called for document verification. The number of candidates called will be approximately twice the number of vacancies.
  3. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the MCQ examination, subject to successful document verification.
Important Note for Assistant/Typist: For the posts of Assistant and Typist, a typing test (40 wpm English, 25 wpm Gujarati) is also mandatory. This will be a qualifying test, and its marks will not be added to the merit list. Only those who pass the typing test will be considered for the final merit based on the MCQ exam scores.

Exam Pattern

The written examination for all posts will consist of two parts, both conducted in a single paper. The total duration will be 120 minutes (2 hours).

  • Total Questions: 200 (100 for Part-I, 100 for Part-II)
  • Total Marks: 200 (1 mark per question)
  • Negative Marking: 0.25 marks will be deducted for each wrong answer.
  • Qualifying Marks: 40% for General category, 35% for all other categories.

Part-I: General Knowledge (100 Marks, Common for all posts)

Subject Questions / Marks
Reasoning & Data Interpretation 30
Quantitative Aptitude 20
English 15
Gujarati 15
Computer Fundamentals 20

Part-II: Subjective/Post-Specific Syllabus (100 Marks)

This part will contain 100 MCQs based on the specific technical/academic syllabus related to the post's essential qualification. The university has provided a detailed syllabus for each post, including Junior Engineer (Civil), Field Collector (Botany), Lab Technician (Microbiology, Chemistry, Physics), and Lab Assistant (Zoology, Physics, Chemistry, Statistics, Microbiology, Botany).

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these non-teaching posts.

How to Prepare

A smart and structured preparation strategy is crucial to succeed in this competitive exam.

  1. Understand the Syllabus in Depth: The university has provided a detailed syllabus for both Part-I and Part-II. Analyze it thoroughly to understand the weightage of each topic.
  2. Focus on Part-II (Subject Specific): This section is the key to a high score. Revise your graduation-level syllabus in detail. For technical posts, focus on core concepts and numerical problems. For science posts, focus on fundamental principles and laboratory practices.
  3. Prepare for Part-I (General Knowledge):
    • Reasoning and Aptitude: These are high-scoring sections that require regular practice.
    • Languages (English & Gujarati): Focus on grammar, vocabulary, and reading comprehension.
    • Computer Fundamentals: Prepare basic topics like Operating Systems, MS Office, and the Internet.
  4. Time Management & Mock Tests: Practice with timed mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy. The exam has 200 questions to be answered in 120 minutes, which requires excellent time management. Be mindful of the negative marking.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended resources for your preparation:

  • For Part-I (General Knowledge): Standard competitive exam books for Reasoning and Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal. For English, "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi. For Computer Fundamentals, a book like "Computer Awareness" by Arihant.
  • For Part-II (Subject Specific):
    • Junior Engineer (Civil): Standard diploma/degree level textbooks on Civil Engineering subjects (e.g., books by R.S. Khurmi, B.C. Punmia).
    • Science Posts (Botany, Zoology, Chemistry, etc.): Standard B.Sc. level textbooks for your respective subject.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online through the university's website.

  1. Visit the official MKBU website: https://mkbhavuni.edu.in.
  2. Navigate to the "Recruitment" or "Careers" section and find the relevant advertisement (NT01-12/2025, NT02-12/2025, or NT03-12/2025).
  3. Read the general instructions carefully before proceeding. Click on the "Apply Online" link.
  4. Fill out the application form with your personal, educational, and contact details accurately.
  5. Upload scanned copies of your recent photograph, signature, and other required documents.
  6. Pay the application fee online via the payment gateway.
  7. Review the application form before final submission and take a printout for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should keep the following documents ready for uploading and for verification later:

  1. Recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Proof of Date of Birth (10th marksheet or School Leaving Certificate).
  3. All educational marksheets and certificates (10th, 12th, Diploma, Degree).
  4. CCC certificate for computer knowledge.
  5. Caste/Category Certificate and Non-Creamy Layer Certificate (if applicable).
  6. Experience certificates (if required for the post).
  7. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, etc.).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online / Official Website Apply Link
Download Official Notification Adv 1 | Adv 2 | Adv 3

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the MKBU recruitment?

The last date to submit the online application is January 22, 2026.

Question 2: Can candidates from outside Gujarat apply?

Yes, any Indian citizen can apply. However, they must have adequate knowledge of Gujarati, Hindi, and English. Reservation benefits are only for domiciles of Gujarat.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the MCQ test.

entry.title: Central University of Rajasthan Recruitment 2025: Various Non-Teaching Posts Open
entry.content:
Central University of Rajasthan Recruitment 2025: Various Non-Teaching Posts Open

A major recruitment drive by CURAJ for Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' positions. Apply online before January 20, 2026.

Post Name: Medical Officer, Private Secretary, Technical Assistant, Laboratory Assistant, UDC, MTS, and others
Last Date: 20 January 2026
Total Posts: 12

The Central University of Rajasthan (CURAJ), an institution of national importance with an 'A++' NAAC accreditation, has released a significant employment notification (Advt.: CURAJ/R/F171/Rectt./2025/3155) for various non-teaching positions. The university has invited online applications from eligible Indian citizens to fill up several key administrative and technical support roles under direct recruitment. This is a golden opportunity for candidates seeking a stable and prestigious career within the framework of a Central University.

This recruitment drive aims to fill a total of 12 vacancies across Group 'A', 'B', and 'C', including posts like Medical Officer, Private Secretary, Technical Assistant, Laboratory Assistant, Upper Division Clerk (UDC), and Multi-Tasking Staff (MTS). The selection process will be governed by the University/UGC/GoI Rules and Regulations, ensuring a merit-based and transparent procedure. The application process is a two-step procedure involving online submission followed by the dispatch of a hard copy. The last date for submitting the online application is January 20, 2026, and the hard copy must reach the university by January 27, 2026.

Important Points

This recruitment is for permanent government positions in a Central University located in Rajasthan. Key dates and details are outlined below.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Central University of Rajasthan (CURAJ)
Advertisement Number CURAJ/R/F171/Rectt./2025/3155
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Central University)
Job Location Rajasthan
Last date for online application 20 January 2026 (up to 11:59 PM)
Last date for hardcopy submission 27 January 2026 (up to 05:00 PM)

Vacancy Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 12 non-teaching posts across various groups. The post-wise details, including pay level and reservation, are as follows:

Post Code Name of Post Number of Posts Reservation Pay Level
Group – A (01 Post)
A-01 Medical Officer (Female) 01 01-UR-PWD-OH 10
Group – B (02 Posts)
B-01 Private Secretary 02 01-OBC (B.L.), 01-SC (B.L.) 07
Group - C (09 Posts)
C-01 Technical Assistant 02 01-SC & 01 EWS 05
C-02 Laboratory Assistant 02 01-ST & 01 UR-PWD-HI 04
C-03 Upper Division Clerk 01 01-UR 04
C-04 Laboratory Attendant 03 02-UR, 01-OBC-PWD-VH 01
C-05 Multi-Tasking Staff 01 01-UR 01
Total Posts 12
Note: B.L. stands for Backlog vacancy. PwBD stands for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (OH-Orthopedically Handicapped, HI-Hearing Impairment, VH-Visually Handicapped).

Application Fee

  • Candidates belonging to General/EWS/OBC categories: Rs. 1500/-
  • Candidates belonging to SC/ST/PwBD categories: Rs. 750/-

The application fee is to be paid using Credit Card, Debit Card, Net Banking, or UPI as per the details provided in the online application form. Regular employees of the Central University of Rajasthan are exempt from the application fee.

Eligibility Criteria

The minimum eligibility for each post is governed by the University/UGC/GoI Rules and Regulations. The essential qualifications and age limits are as follows (age limit is as on the last date of application):

Post Code Name of Post Essential Qualification and Experience Age Limit
A-01 Medical Officer (Female) MBBS recognized by M.C.I. Desirable: PG Medical Qualification, experience in a hospital attached with a Medical College/Corporate Hospital. 40 years
B-01 Private Secretary Bachelor's Degree. 3 years of experience as Stenographer/Personal Assistant at Pay Level 06. Proficiency in Stenography (100 w.p.m.) and Typing (35/30 w.p.m.). Knowledge of computer applications. 35 years
C-01 Technical Assistant B.Tech./B.E. in relevant engineering fields with at least 60% marks and two years of experience. OR Bachelor's degree in relevant science fields with at least 60% marks and two years of experience. 32 years
C-02 Laboratory Assistant Bachelor's degree in Engineering/Computer Science/Information Technology or Computer Application. Desirable: Experience in academic/research labs. 30 years
C-03 Upper Division Clerk Bachelor's Degree with at least 50% marks. Two (02) years of experience as a Lower Division Clerk. Knowledge of Computer Applications. 32 years
C-04 Laboratory Attendant 10+2 or its equivalent with Science subjects from a recognized Board. Desirable: Experience of working in a Science Laboratory. 30 years
C-05 Multi-Tasking Staff Matriculation or equivalent pass OR ITI pass. 30 years

Selection Process

The selection process for these non-teaching posts will be as per the Cadre Recruitment Rules (Non-Teaching), 2018 of the University. The process will be multi-staged and is designed to ensure a fair and comprehensive assessment of candidates.

  1. Shortlisting: Based on the number of applications received, the University may restrict the number of candidates to be called for the selection process. This shortlisting will be done on the basis of qualifications and experience higher than the minimum prescribed for the post.
  2. Skill Test / Trade Test (Qualifying): For Group 'B' and 'C' posts requiring specific skills (e.g., Private Secretary, UDC, Technical Assistant), a Skill Test or Trade Test will be conducted first. This test is mandatory and is of a qualifying nature. Marks obtained in this test will not be counted for the final merit list.
  3. Written Examination: Candidates who qualify in the Skill/Trade Test (where applicable) will be called for a competitive Written Test. The final merit for Group 'B' and 'C' posts will be based solely on the marks obtained in this test.
  4. Interview (for Group 'A' Posts Only): For the Group 'A' post (Medical Officer), candidates shortlisted from the written test will be called for a personal interview. The final merit will have a weightage of 80% for the shortlisting process (written test, academics) and 20% for the interview.

Exam Pattern

The University has defined a clear scheme of examination for Group 'A', 'B', and 'C' posts. The language of the test paper will be English only, and there will be negative marking of 0.25 for each wrong answer in MCQ tests.

Pattern for Group 'B' & 'C' Posts

  • Skill Test (Qualifying): This is the first stage. It includes tests like MS Word (noting/drafting), MS Excel (data analysis), PowerPoint presentation, Typing Test, or Hands-on Laboratory Practices, depending on the post. Candidates must secure the minimum qualifying marks (e.g., 35/100 or 18/50) to proceed.
  • Written Test (MCQ - 100 Marks):
    • Duration: 1.5 hours
    • Total Marks: 100
    • Part 1 (50 Marks): Language proficiency in English, General Knowledge, Analytical Ability, University System, Central Universities Act 2009, Statutes, Ordinances, Office Procedures, Filing, Noting, Drafting, Basic GoI Rules.
    • Part 2 (50 Marks): Questions relevant to the subject/work profile of the Post.
    • Qualifying Marks: 35

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for these non-teaching posts.

How to Prepare

A structured preparation strategy is essential for success in this competitive recruitment.

  1. Understand the Syllabus and Rules: Thoroughly read the Cadre Recruitment Rules (2018) and the specific syllabus for your post (Appendix-03) to understand the weightage and topics.
  2. Master the Skill Test: Since the skill test is the first stage for Group B/C posts, it is crucial to qualify. Practice typing, stenography, MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint), or your specific laboratory skills daily.
  3. Prepare General Subjects: For the written test, prepare General English, General Knowledge, Analytical Ability (Reasoning), and basic mathematics.
  4. Focus on Administrative Knowledge: A significant part of the syllabus for administrative posts covers the University System, Central Universities Act, and GoI rules. These are high-scoring areas if prepared well.
  5. Domain Knowledge is Key: For technical posts, revise your diploma/degree level subjects in detail. This will be tested in both the skill test and the written exam.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended resources for your preparation:

  • For General Awareness, Reasoning, and Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" and "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • For General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • For Administrative Procedures & Rules: "Swamy's Handbook" for Central Government staff is an excellent resource.
  • For Domain-Specific Subjects: Standard textbooks from your respective degree/diploma curriculum.

How to Apply

The application process involves both online submission and sending a hard copy:

  1. Visit the official university website www.curaj.ac.in and navigate to the recruitment section.
  2. Find the relevant advertisement (CURAJ/R/F171/Rectt./2025/3155) and click on the link for the Online Application System.
  3. Fill out the online application form with all your details and pay the requisite application fee online.
  4. After submitting the online application, take a printout of the completed form.
  5. Send the hard copy of the application form, along with the online payment receipt and all necessary self-attested enclosures, in an envelope superscribed “Application for the post of ____________ (Post category)” to the following address:
    Registrar (Recruitment Cell),
    Central University of Rajasthan,
    NH-8, Bandarsindri, Kishangarh,
    District - Ajmer, 305817 (Rajasthan)

Documents Needed to Apply

While submitting the hard copy of the application, you must attach self-attested copies of all certificates relating to your qualifications, experience, age, and caste.

  1. Printout of the online application form.
  2. Online payment receipt of the prescribed application fee.
  3. Certificate in support of Date of Birth (10th certificate).
  4. All educational marksheets and certificates.
  5. Experience certificates from all previous employers.
  6. Caste/Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PWD).
  7. Any other document in support of your qualifications and experience.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Online Apply Online
Official Website Click Here
Official Notification Click Here

FAQs

Question 1: What is the last date for submitting the online application?

The last date for submission of the online application is January 20, 2026, up to 11:59 PM.

Question 2: Do I need to send a hard copy after applying online?

Yes, it is mandatory to send a hard copy of the submitted online application form along with all enclosures to the university. The last date to receive the hard copy is January 27, 2026.

Question 3: Is there any interview for Group 'C' posts like UDC and MTS?

No, as per the University's Cadre Recruitment Rules, interviews have been dispensed with for Group 'B' and 'C' posts. Selection will be based on the Written Test after qualifying the Skill Test.

entry.title: JKSSB Driver Recruitment 2025: 107 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती, ऑनलाइन करें आवेदन!
entry.content:
JKSSB Driver Recruitment 2025: 107 पदों पर बंपर भर्ती, ऑनलाइन करें आवेदन!

Jammu & Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) ne vibhinn sarkaari vibhagon mein Driver ke pado ke liye aavedan aamantrit kiye hain. Sirf J&K ke Domicile ummeedwar hi patra hain.

Post Name: Driver
Last Date: 11/11/2025
Total Posts: 107

Jammu aur Kashmir ke yuvaon ke liye sarkaari naukri paane ka ek sunhera avsar hai. Jammu & Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) ne Advertisement Notification No. 09 of 2025 ke tehat, vibhinn vibhagon mein Driver ke kul 107 पदों par sidhi bharti ke liye online aavedan aamantrit kiye hain. Yeh bharti Jammu & Kashmir ke alag-alag zilon ke liye hai aur sirf UT ke Domicile ummeedwar hi iske liye aavedan kar sakte hain.

Mahatvapurna Tithiyan (Important Dates)

Ghatna (Event) Tithi (Date)
Online Application Shuru Hone ki Tithi 13 October 2025
Online Application ki Antim Tithi 11 November 2025

Vacancy aur Vibhago ki Jaankari

Kul 107 pado ko teen mukhya vibhagon mein baanta gaya hai:

Vibhag (Department) Pado ki Sankhya (No. of Posts)
Agriculture Production Department 18
Public Works (R&B) Department 27
Transport Department 62
Kul (Total) 107

Aavedan Shulk (Application Fee)

  • General Category ke liye: ₹ 600/-
  • SC, ST-1, ST-2, aur EWS Category ke liye: ₹ 500/-

Fee ka bhugtan sirf online mode (Net Banking, Credit/Debit Card) se hi kiya ja sakta hai.

Patrata ki Shartein (Eligibility Criteria as on 01.01.2025)

Aavedan karne se pehle ummeedwaron ko nimnlikhit yogyata maapdandon ko poora karna anivarya hai:

Criteria Zaroorat (Requirement)
Nivas (Domicile) Ummeedwar Jammu & Kashmir (UT) ka sthayi niwasi hona chahiye aur uske paas antim tithi tak jaari kiya gaya valid Domicile Certificate hona chahiye.
Aayu Seema (Age Limit) Open Merit (OM) ke liye adhiktam aayu seema 40 varsh hai. SC/ST/RBA/EWS jaise aarakshit vargon ke liye 43 varsh aur Ex-Servicemen ke liye 48 varsh hai.
Shaikshanik Yogyata (Educational Qualification)
  • Minimum Matric (10th pass) aur Maximum 10+2.
  • Ummeedwar ke paas ek valid HGV/PSV Driving License hona anivarya hai.

Chayan Prakriya (Selection Process)

Chayan prakriya do charanon mein sampann hogi:

  1. Driving Test: Yeh ek qualifying test hoga. Is test ko pass karne wale ummeedwar hi agle charan ke liye patra honge.
  2. Likhit Pariksha (Written/OMR Examination): Driving test mein safal ummeedwaron ko likhit pariksha ke liye bulaya jayega. Antim merit list likhit pariksha mein prapt ankon ke aadhar par banayi jayegi.
Pariksha ka Pattern: Pariksha Objective Type (MCQ) hogi aur prashn patra sirf Angrezi bhasha mein hoga. Har galat uttar ke liye 0.25 anko ki negative marking hogi.

Aavedan Kaise Karein (How to Apply)

Application process poori tarah se online hai:

  1. Sabse pehle JKSSB ki official website www.jkssb.nic.in par jaayein.
  2. Advertisement Notification No. 09 of 2025 se sambandhit link par click karein.
  3. "Apply Online" par click karein aur registration process poora karein.
  4. Application form mein sabhi jaankariyan dhyan se bharein aur zaroori documents upload karein.
  5. Online application fee ka bhugtan karein aur form submit karein.
  6. Antim roop se jama kiye gaye application form ka printout apne paas surakshit rakhein.

Mahatvapurna Links (Important Links)

Link ka Title Link Action
Official JKSSB Website Yahan Click Karein

Aksar Puche Jane Wale Sawal (FAQs)

Q1: Kaun aavedan kar sakta hai?

Sirf Jammu & Kashmir ke Domicile certificate dhaarak ummeedwar hi is bharti ke liye aavedan kar sakte hain.

Q2: Chayan prakriya kya hai?

Chayan do charanon mein hoga: pehle ek qualifying Driving Test aur uske baad ek likhit pariksha. Antim merit likhit pariksha ke ankon par aadharit hogi.

Q3: Kya driving license zaroori hai?

Haan, ummeedwar ke paas ek valid HGV/PSV (Heavy Goods Vehicle/Public Service Vehicle) Driving License hona anivarya hai.

Q4: Aavedan kaise karna hai?

Aavedan sirf JKSSB ki official website www.jkssb.nic.in par online madhyam se hi sweekar kiye jayenge.

entry.title: Jagiroad Semiconductor Company Recruitment 2025: असम की महिलाओं के लिए सुनहरा अवसर!
entry.content:
Jagiroad Semiconductor Company Recruitment 2025: असम की महिलाओं के लिए सुनहरा अवसर!

असम सेमीकंडक्टर भर्ती 2025 - नरसापुरा, बैंगलोर में 500-1000 महिला तकनीशियन की भर्ती। Free खाना, रहने की सुविधा, ट्रांसपोर्टेशन और ₹12,000 मासिक स्टाइपेंड!

असम की युवा महिलाओं के लिए यह एक अद्भुत मौका है जो उनके करियर को नई दिशा दे सकता है। जगीरोड सेमीकंडक्टर कंपनी भर्ती 2025 के तहत Tata और Assam Skill Development Mission (ASDM) मिलकर 500 से 1000 महिला तकनीशियनों की भर्ती कर रहे हैं जिन्हें नरसापुरा (बैंगलोर) में Final Assembly Testing and Packaging (FATP) यूनिट में नियुक्त किया जाएगा।

यह भर्ती Apprenticeship Act, 1965 के तहत होगी। चयनित उम्मीदवारों को सुरक्षित कार्य वातावरण, फ्री खाना, रहने की सुविधा, ट्रांसपोर्टेशन और हर महीने ₹12,000 का स्टाइपेंड मिलेगा। यह असम की महिलाओं के लिए सेमीकंडक्टर उद्योग में करियर शुरू करने का सुनहरा अवसर है।

भर्ती का सारांश - मुख्य जानकारी

विषयजानकारी
भर्ती का नामजगीरोड सेमीकंडक्टर कंपनी भर्ती 2025
आयोजक संस्थाTata + Assam Skill Development Mission (ASDM)
पद का नामFemale Technician (FATP)
कुल पद500-1000
कार्य स्थाननरसापुरा, बैंगलोर (कर्नाटक)
रोजगार का प्रकारApprenticeship (अस्थायी प्रशिक्षण)
मासिक स्टाइपेंड₹12,000/-
अतिरिक्त सुविधाएँफ्री खाना, रहना और ट्रांसपोर्टेशन
जॉइनिंग तिथि15 January 2024 तक

यह भर्ती क्यों खास है?

  • केवल महिलाओं के लिए अवसर
  • फूड, स्टे और ट्रांसपोर्ट सब मुफ्त
  • भारत की तेजी से बढ़ती सेमीकंडक्टर इंडस्ट्री में करियर
  • पूरी तरह सुरक्षित वर्क एनवायरनमेंट
  • समान दिन चयन प्रक्रिया और रिजल्ट

पात्रता मानदंड (Eligibility Criteria)

मानदंडआवश्यकता
लिंगकेवल महिला उम्मीदवार
शैक्षणिक योग्यताकक्षा 12वीं (HSC) उत्तीर्ण
पासिंग वर्ष2022 या 2023
आयु सीमा18 से 21 वर्ष (30 November 2023 तक)
स्थान वरीयताअसम राज्य की महिला उम्मीदवार
शारीरिक योग्यताशिफ्ट में कार्य के लिए शारीरिक रूप से फिट
नोट: नाइट शिफ्ट शामिल है, लेकिन सुरक्षा की पूरी व्यवस्था रहती है।

चयन प्रक्रिया

चयन प्रक्रिया 3 चरणों में होती है —

  • Section A: English व Basic Maths टेस्ट (30 मिनट)
  • Section B: गणितीय परीक्षण (20 मिनट)
  • Section C: Practical Beads टेस्ट (30 सेकंड)

परिणाम:

  • Section A Fail = बाहर
  • Section A Pass + B Fail = साधारण भूमिका
  • All Pass = उच्च पदों पर चयन

चयन के बाद प्रक्रिया

  • Step 1: चयन के बाद उम्मीदवारों को बैंगलोर भेजा जाएगा।
  • Step 2: 6 दिन की ट्रेनिंग दी जाएगी।
  • Step 3: Exit टेस्ट पास करने के बाद Apprenticeship letter जारी होगा।
Special Policy: फेल होने पर दोबारा प्रयास का मौका मिलेगा।

वेतन और लाभ

लाभविवरण
मासिक स्टाइपेंड₹12,000
भोजनफ्री – दिन में 3 बार
रहने की सुविधाफ्री हॉस्टल
ट्रांसपोर्टेशनफ्री कंपनी बस
मेडिकलकंपनी में हेल्थ सेंटर

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

संसाधनलिंक
NAPS पोर्टलयहाँ क्लिक करें
ASDM वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

FAQs - सामान्य प्रश्न

Q: क्या केवल महिलाएं आवेदन कर सकती हैं?
हाँ, यह भर्ती केवल महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए है।

Q: क्या शुल्क देना होगा?
नहीं, आवेदन पूर्णतः निशुल्क है।

Q: स्टाइपेंड कितना है?
₹12,000 प्रतिमाह।

Q: क्या रहने और खाने की सुविधा मिलेगी?
हाँ, दोनों मुफ्त उपलब्ध हैं।

Q: क्या प्रशिक्षण अवधि में भी वेतन मिलेगा?
हाँ, प्रशिक्षण के दौरान भी स्टाइपेंड और सभी सुविधाएँ मिलेंगी।

entry.title: Territorial Army भर्ती 2025: Officer और Soldier GD के पदों पर भर्ती!
entry.content:
Territorial Army भर्ती 2025: Officer और Soldier GD के पदों पर भर्ती!

अपनी मौजूदा नौकरी के साथ देश सेवा करने का अनोखा अवसर — महिलाओं और पुरुषों दोनों के लिए।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! Territorial Army (प्रादेशिक सेना) ने वर्ष 2025 के लिए दो अलग-अलग भर्ती अभियानों की घोषणा की है — एक Officer पदों के लिए और दूसरा Soldier (General Duty) पदों के लिए। यह उन सभी देशभक्त युवाओं के लिए सुनहरा मौका है जो अपनी मौजूदा नौकरी या व्यवसाय को छोड़े बिना सेना की वर्दी पहनकर देश सेवा करना चाहते हैं।

Territorial Army Officer भर्ती 2025

यह भर्ती उन “Gainfully Employed” युवाओं के लिए है जो Officer के रूप में Territorial Army में शामिल होना चाहते हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन शुरू12 मई 2025
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि10 जून 2025
ऑनलाइन प्रवेश परीक्षा (संभावित)20 जुलाई 2025

कुल पद

  • पुरुष: 18
  • महिला: 01
  • कुल: 19

पात्रता मानदंड

  • नागरिकता: केवल भारतीय नागरिक (महिला व पुरुष)।
  • आयु सीमा: 18 से 42 वर्ष (10 जून 2025 तक)।
  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता: किसी मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक (Graduate)।
  • रोजगार स्थिति: उम्मीदवार “Gainfully Employed” होना चाहिए (सरकारी, निजी या स्वरोजगार)।
  • नोट: Regular Army, Navy, Air Force, Police, या Paramilitary बलों के सदस्य पात्र नहीं हैं।

आवेदन शुल्क

सभी उम्मीदवारों को ₹500/- का नॉन-रिफंडेबल आवेदन शुल्क ऑनलाइन जमा करना होगा।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. ऑनलाइन प्रवेश परीक्षा (CBT)
  2. SSB साक्षात्कार
  3. चिकित्सा परीक्षण (Medical Examination)

SSB साक्षात्कार के लिए आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

  • Application Form IAF (TA)-9 (Revised) Part-2 — हस्ताक्षरित।
  • सभी शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र।
  • Physical Fitness Certificate (Registered MBBS Doctor से)।
  • Identity Proof (Voter ID, PAN Card, Passport आदि)।
  • Domicile प्रमाणपत्र।
  • Age Proof (10वीं/12वीं की अंक तालिका)।
  • Service Certificate और NOC (यदि किसी संस्था में कार्यरत हैं)।
  • Self-employed के लिए Affidavit एवं Character Certificate।
  • Income Proof और ITR की प्रति।
  • PAN Card एवं Aadhar Card की प्रति।

Territorial Army Soldier (GD) Rally भर्ती 2025

105 Infantry Battalion (TA) RAJRIF द्वारा हरियाणा (Panchkula को छोड़कर) और NCT दिल्ली के निवासियों के लिए भर्ती रैली आयोजित की जाएगी।

रैली का कार्यक्रम (जिला-वार)

तिथिजिलास्थान
28 नवम्बर 2025Rohtak, Kurukshetra105 Inf Bn (TA) RAJRIF, Delhi
29 नवम्बर 2025Jhajjar, Palwal, Nuh
01 दिसम्बर 2025Sonipat, Ambala
02 दिसम्बर 2025Gurugram, Rewari
03 दिसम्बर 2025Bhiwani, Yamuna Nagar
04 दिसम्बर 2025Charkhi Dadri, Sirsa
05 दिसम्बर 2025Hisar, Fatehabad
06 दिसम्बर 2025Jind, Karnal
08 दिसम्बर 2025Mahendergarh, Kaithal
09 दिसम्बर 2025Panipat, Faridabad
10 दिसम्बर 2025NCT Delhi
रिपोर्टिंग समय: सभी उम्मीदवारों को सुबह 02:00 बजे तक रैली स्थल पर पहुँचना होगा। सुबह 05:00 बजे के बाद किसी को प्रवेश नहीं मिलेगा।

पात्रता मानदंड

  • आयु सीमा: 18 से 42 वर्ष।
  • शैक्षणिक योग्यता: 10वीं कक्षा में कुल 45% अंक तथा प्रत्येक विषय में न्यूनतम 33% अंक।
  • शारीरिक मापदंड:
    • ऊंचाई: 160 से.मी.
    • वज़न: न्यूनतम 50 किग्रा
    • छाती: 77 से.मी. (5 से.मी. फुलाव आवश्यक)

आवश्यक दस्तावेज़

  • निवास प्रमाणपत्र (Domicile Certificate)
  • चरित्र प्रमाणपत्र (Sarpanch/SHO/Principal द्वारा हस्ताक्षरित)
  • जाति प्रमाणपत्र
  • 20 रंगीन पासपोर्ट आकार के फोटो (कंप्यूटर प्रिंट मान्य नहीं)
  • मूल अंकतालिका
  • PAN और Aadhaar कार्ड की प्रति
  • विवाहित/अविवाहित प्रमाणपत्र
  • Outstanding खेल प्रमाणपत्र (राज्य/राष्ट्रीय/अंतरराष्ट्रीय स्तर)

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  • Officer Post: उम्मीदवार www.jointerritorialarmy.gov.in या www.indianarmy.nic.in पर 12 मई से 10 जून 2025 तक ऑनलाइन आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
  • Soldier GD Post: उम्मीदवार अपने जिले की निर्धारित तिथि पर सुबह 02:00 बजे सभी दस्तावेज़ों के साथ रैली स्थल पर उपस्थित हों। इसके लिए कोई ऑनलाइन आवेदन नहीं है।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन (Officer)यहाँ क्लिक करें (लिंक 12 मई 2025 से सक्रिय होगा)
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: Territorial Army क्या है? क्या यह पूर्णकालिक नौकरी है?
नहीं, Territorial Army एक अंशकालिक (Part-Time) सेवा है। इसमें आप अपनी नियमित नौकरी के साथ-साथ देश सेवा कर सकते हैं।
प्रश्न 2: Officer भर्ती के लिए कौन आवेदन कर सकता है?
कोई भी भारतीय नागरिक (महिला या पुरुष) जिसकी आयु 18–42 वर्ष है, जो स्नातक है और किसी कार्य/व्यवसाय में संलग्न है, आवेदन कर सकता है।
प्रश्न 3: Soldier GD Rally के लिए कौन पात्र है?
हरियाणा (Panchkula को छोड़कर) और NCT दिल्ली के निवासी जो 10वीं पास हैं और जिनकी आयु 18–42 वर्ष है, आवेदन कर सकते हैं।
प्रश्न 4: Officer का वेतन कितना होता है?
Training या Service के दौरान Territorial Army Officers को Regular Army Officers के समान वेतन, भत्ते और सुविधाएँ मिलती हैं।

entry.title: AISSEE 2026: सैनिक स्कूल प्रवेश परीक्षा — कक्षा VI एवं IX हेतु अधिसूचन
entry.content:
AISSEE 2026 — सैनिक स्कूल प्रवेश परीक्षा: कक्षा VI एवं IX हेतु अधिसूचना

NTA द्वारा जारी — ऑनलाइन पंजीकरण प्रारंभ: कक्षा 6 एवं 9 के लिए अखिल भारतीय सैनिक स्कूल प्रवेश परीक्षा (AISSEE) 2026

नमस्ते! यदि आपके बच्चे का लक्ष्य सैनिक स्कूल में प्रवेश पाना है, तो यह जानकारी आपके लिए उपयोगी है। National Testing Agency (NTA) ने AISSEE 2026 की अधिसूचना जारी कर दी है, जिसके अंतर्गत कक्षा VI और कक्षा IX में प्रवेश हेतु परीक्षा आयोजित की जाएगी। यह परीक्षा सैनिक स्कूलों एवं अनुमोदित नए सैनिक स्कूलों (NSS) — दोनों के लिए समान रूप से मान्य है।

त्वरित सारांश: ऑनलाइन आवेदन: 10 अक्टूबर 2025 से 30 अक्टूबर 2025 तक। परीक्षा: जनवरी 2026 (सटीक तिथि वेबसाइट पर घोषित होगी)। आवेदन हेतु लिंक: exams.nta.nic.in/sainik-school-society.

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ — AISSEE 2026

कार्यक्रमतिथि / विवरण
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ10 अक्टूबर 2025
आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि30 अक्टूबर 2025 (शाम 05:00 बजे तक)
शुल्क भुगतान की अंतिम तिथि31 अक्टूबर 2025 (रात 11:50 बजे तक)
संशोधन विंडो02 नवम्बर से 04 नवम्बर 2025
एडमिट कार्ड डाउनलोडवेबसाइट पर बाद में घोषित किया जाएगा
परीक्षा की तिथिजनवरी 2026
परिणाम घोषणापरीक्षा के लगभग 6 सप्ताह बाद

नोट: नवीनतम अपडेट हेतु NTA की वेबसाइट नियमित रूप से देखें।

शुल्क संरचना (AISSEE 2026)

श्रेणीशुल्क (₹)
सामान्य / रक्षा कर्मियों के वार्ड / OBC (NCL)₹850
SC / ST₹700

शुल्क वापसी योग्य नहीं है। बैंक/गेटवे शुल्क अलग से हो सकता है।

पात्रता मानदंड (Eligibility)

कक्षाआयु सीमा (31.03.2026 तक)अन्य आवश्यकताएँ
कक्षा VI10–12 वर्ष (01.04.2014 से 31.03.2016 के बीच जन्म)कक्षा V उत्तीर्ण या अध्ययनरत (लड़कियाँ भी पात्र हैं)
कक्षा IX13–15 वर्ष (01.04.2011 से 31.03.2013 के बीच जन्म)मान्यता प्राप्त विद्यालय से कक्षा VIII उत्तीर्ण (लड़कियाँ भी पात्र हैं)
न्यूनतम अंक: सैनिक स्कूलों में प्रत्येक विषय में न्यूनतम 25% एवं कुल 40% अंक आवश्यक हैं। SC/ST हेतु अलग प्रावधान हैं।

परीक्षा पैटर्न (Exam Pattern)

यह परीक्षा ऑफलाइन (OMR शीट) पर ली जाएगी। सभी प्रश्न बहुविकल्पीय (MCQ) होंगे। कोई नकारात्मक अंकन नहीं है।

कक्षाविषयप्रश्नकुल अंक
कक्षा VIभाषा (25), गणित (50), बुद्धिमत्ता (25), सामान्य ज्ञान (25)125300
कक्षा IXगणित (50), बुद्धिमत्ता (25), अंग्रेज़ी (25), विज्ञान (25), सामाजिक विज्ञान (25)150400

अवधि: कक्षा VI — 150 मिनट, कक्षा IX — 180 मिनट।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया (How to Apply)

  1. पंजीकरण: NTA वेबसाइट पर नया खाता बनाएँ।
  2. फॉर्म भरें: व्यक्तिगत विवरण, विद्यालय व परीक्षा शहर का चयन करें।
  3. दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें: फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, अंगूठा निशान, DOB प्रमाणपत्र, जाति/डोमिसाइल PDF के रूप में अपलोड करें।
  4. शुल्क भुगतान: ऑनलाइन माध्यम से करें (Debit/Credit/UPI)।
  5. संशोधन: 02–04 नवम्बर 2025 के बीच सीमित फील्ड्स में सुधार संभव।

हार्ड कॉपी भेजने की आवश्यकता नहीं है।

परीक्षा केंद्र व माध्यम

परीक्षा केंद्र Appendix IV में सूचीबद्ध हैं। आवेदन के समय 4 शहर प्राथमिकता से चुन सकते हैं।

  • कक्षा VI — अंग्रेज़ी, हिन्दी सहित 13 भाषाओं में।
  • कक्षा IX — केवल अंग्रेज़ी में।

आरक्षण एवं प्रवेश प्रक्रिया

मौजूदा सैनिक स्कूलों में 67% सीटें राज्य निवासियों के लिए और 33% अन्य राज्यों के लिए आरक्षित हैं। SC/ST/OBC/Defence के लिए भी आरक्षण नियम लागू हैं।

अंतिम प्रवेश e-counselling, मेडिकल फिटनेस और दस्तावेज़ सत्यापन के बाद ही सुनिश्चित होता है।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

संसाधनलिंक
NTA — AISSEE आधिकारिक पृष्ठयहाँ क्लिक करें
NTA मुख्य वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
NCBC (OBC Central List)यहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: एडमिट कार्ड कब जारी होगा?
परीक्षा से कुछ दिन पहले एडमिट कार्ड डाउनलोड लिंक NTA वेबसाइट पर सक्रिय किया जाएगा।
प्रश्न 2: क्या नकारात्मक अंकन है?
नहीं, AISSEE 2026 में नकारात्मक अंकन नहीं है।
प्रश्न 3: क्या लड़कियाँ आवेदन कर सकती हैं?
हाँ, कक्षा VI और IX दोनों के लिए लड़कियाँ पात्र हैं — लेकिन स्कूलवार सीटें अलग-अलग होती हैं।
प्रश्न 4: क्या एक से अधिक आवेदन किए जा सकते हैं?
नहीं, एक ही उम्मीदवार द्वारा एक से अधिक आवेदन करने पर सभी आवेदन रद्द किए जा सकते हैं।
प्रश्न 5: क्या शुल्क वापसी योग्य है?
नहीं, परीक्षा शुल्क किसी भी परिस्थिति में वापसी योग्य नहीं है।

entry.title: NIT Puducherry Recruitment 2025: Group B & C Non-Teaching Posts Open
entry.content:
NIT Puducherry Recruitment 2025: Group B & C Non-Teaching Posts Open

National Institute of Technology Puducherry invites applications for Technical Assistant, Junior Engineer, Superintendent, Pharmacist, Technician, and Junior Assistant roles.

Post Name: Technical Assistant, Junior Engineer, Superintendent, Pharmacist, Stenographer, Technician, Junior Assistant
Last Date: (Refer to original Advt. No. NITPY/01/2025/NT/01072025)
Total Posts: Multiple Vacancies

The National Institute of Technology (NIT) Puducherry, an Institution of National Importance under the Ministry of Education, Government of India, has announced the detailed pattern and modalities for its selection process for various Group 'B' and 'C' non-teaching positions. This follows the initial recruitment advertisement (Ref: Advt. No. NITPY/01/2025/NT/01072025) and provides crucial information for candidates who have applied or are preparing for these coveted government jobs.

This recruitment drive aims to fill key administrative and technical support roles that are essential for the smooth functioning of the institute. The selection process, scheduled to be held tentatively in August/September 2025, will be a multi-stage evaluation including a Screening Test, a Skill Test, and a Final Written Test. This comprehensive approach is designed to select the most competent and skilled individuals for posts like Technical Assistant, Junior Engineer, Superintendent, Pharmacist, Stenographer, Technician, and Junior Assistant. This article provides a detailed breakdown of the selection process, exam patterns, and syllabus to help candidates prepare effectively.

Important Points

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment's selection process. This is a permanent Central Government autonomous body job opportunity located in Karaikal, Puducherry.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization National Institute of Technology (NIT) Puducherry
Job Type Permanent Government Job (Institution of National Importance)
Job Location Thiruvettakudy, Karaikal, Puducherry
Selection Process Dates Tentatively scheduled for August/September 2025
Official Website www.nitpy.ac.in

Vacancy Details

The recruitment is for various Group 'B' and 'C' non-teaching positions. The specific number of vacancies for each post was detailed in the original advertisement (Advt. No. NITPY/01/2025/NT/01072025). The posts for which the selection process modalities have been announced are:

Group Post Name
Group 'B' Posts Technical Assistant
Junior Engineer
Superintendent
Group 'C' Posts Pharmacist
Stenographer
Technician
Junior Assistant
Note: Candidates are advised to refer to the original recruitment advertisement for the exact number of vacancies, reservation details, and specific eligibility criteria for each post.

Application Fee Details

  • Application fee details were provided in the original advertisement (Ref: Advt. No. NITPY/01/2025/NT/01072025).
  • Candidates should refer to that notification for the exact fee amount for their category.

This document primarily details the selection process, assuming candidates have already applied.

Eligibility Criteria

The eligibility criteria, including educational qualifications, experience, and age limit for each post, were detailed in the initial recruitment notification. Candidates appearing for the selection process must fully satisfy themselves about their eligibility as prescribed. Mere appearance in the tests does not entitle a candidate to be considered for selection.

Post Group General Eligibility Outline
Group 'B' (e.g., Technical Assistant, JE, Superintendent) Typically requires a Bachelor's Degree, Diploma in Engineering, or a Master's Degree depending on the post, along with relevant experience. For detailed post-wise qualifications, the original advertisement must be consulted.
Group 'C' (e.g., Technician, Junior Assistant, Stenographer) Typically requires Senior Secondary (10+2) or ITI in a relevant trade. Some posts may require a Bachelor's Degree. Typing/Shorthand skills are mandatory for clerical/steno posts. For detailed post-wise qualifications, the original advertisement must be consulted.

Selection Process

Due to the large number of applications, the selection process for all Group 'B' and 'C' posts will be conducted in a multi-stage format. The process is designed to thoroughly evaluate the aptitude, skills, and knowledge of the candidates. The stages are as follows:

  1. Stage I: Screening Test (Qualifying): For posts like Junior Engineer, Superintendent, and Junior Assistant, an initial Screening Test will be conducted to shortlist candidates for the Skill Test. This test is purely qualifying, and its marks will not be counted for the final merit list.
  2. Stage II: Skill Test (Qualifying): All candidates for all posts must appear for a Skill Test. This test is mandatory and qualifying in nature. Its purpose is to assess the practical expertise and hands-on skills required for the respective post. The marks from the Skill Test will not be counted for the final ranking.
  3. Stage III: Final Written Test (100% Weightage): Candidates who qualify in the Screening Test (where applicable) and the mandatory Skill Test will be allowed to appear for the Final Written Test. The final merit list and selection will be based solely on the marks scored by the candidates in this Final Written Test.
Important: Appearing for both the Screening Test (if applicable for the post) and the Skill Test is compulsory to be considered for the Final Written Test. There will be no negative marking in the Screening Test and Skill Test.

Exam Pattern

The examination will be conducted in stages, with the final merit based on the Written Test. All question papers will be in English.

Stage I: Screening Test (Qualifying)

  • Applicable for: Junior Engineer, Superintendent, Junior Assistant.
  • Nature: Objective Type (MCQs) / Short Answer Questions.
  • Negative Marking: No negative marks.
  • Purpose: To shortlist candidates for the Skill Test/Written Test.

Stage II: Skill Test (Qualifying)

  • Applicable for: All Posts (Technical Assistant, JE, Superintendent, Pharmacist, Stenographer, Technician, Junior Assistant).
  • Nature: Short/Long/Descriptive Answer Type Questions to assess the required expertise for the post. For Stenographer/Junior Assistant, this will include Shorthand/Typing tests.
  • Negative Marking: No negative marks.
  • Purpose: To qualify candidates for the Final Written Test.

Stage III: Final Written Test (100% Weightage)

  • Applicable for: All candidates who qualify in the preceding stages.
  • Nature: Will consist of both objective and descriptive type questions.
  • Syllabus: The questions will be based on the detailed syllabus provided in Annexure-B of the notification.
  • Purpose: The marks scored in this test will be the sole criteria for preparing the final merit list.

PET

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for any of these non-teaching posts. The selection is based on written tests and skill evaluation.

How to Prepare

A comprehensive and structured preparation strategy is essential to clear the multi-stage selection process of NIT Puducherry.

  1. Understand the Syllabus Thoroughly: The first step is to download Annexure-B and carefully analyze the syllabus for your respective post. The syllabus is vast, covering general aptitude, administration, and domain-specific knowledge.
  2. Prepare for General Subjects: For posts like Superintendent and Junior Assistant, the syllabus includes General Awareness, Reasoning, Aptitude, English, and Computer Awareness. Prepare these topics from standard competitive exam books.
  3. Master Domain Knowledge: For technical posts like Technical Assistant and Technician, the core of your preparation should be your specific trade/discipline (e.g., Computer Science, Electronics, Mechanical). Study your diploma/10+2 science syllabus in depth.
  4. Focus on Administrative Rules: For administrative posts (Superintendent, Junior Assistant), a significant portion of the syllabus is dedicated to General Administration, including NITSER Act, FR & SR, CCS Rules, GFR-2017, GeM, etc. These are crucial and scoring areas.
  5. Practice for Skill Test: Do not neglect the skill test as it is mandatory. For clerical/steno posts, practice typing/shorthand daily. For technical posts, revise your practical knowledge and be prepared for descriptive questions related to your field.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books and resources to aid your preparation:

  • For General Awareness, Reasoning, and Aptitude:
    • "Lucent's General Knowledge" for GA.
    • "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
    • "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • For General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • For Computer Awareness: "Objective Computer Knowledge & Literacy" by Kiran Prakashan or a similar book.
