  • For General Administration: "Swamy's Handbook" for Central Government staff is an excellent resource for various rules (CCS, FR & SR, etc.). GFR-2017 can be downloaded from the Department of Expenditure website.
  • For Technical Subjects: Standard diploma or B.Sc. level textbooks for your specific branch are the best resources. For objective practice, refer to books for JE/AE level exams.

How to Apply

The application process for this recruitment was conducted online via the Samarth Portal as mentioned in the original advertisement. This notification primarily deals with the modalities of the selection process for candidates who have already applied.

  1. Candidates were required to visit the official NIT Puducherry website www.nitpy.ac.in and follow the link to the recruitment portal.
  2. The application involved filling out the online form, uploading necessary documents, and paying the application fee.
  3. Candidates who have successfully applied are advised to regularly visit the institute's website for the list of provisionally eligible candidates and the schedule for the Screening/Skill/Written tests.

Documents Needed for Verification

All shortlisted candidates are required to bring the following documents in original, along with a self-attested photocopy, for verification at the time of the selection process:

  1. Original Valid Photo Identity Card (as mandated by the Government).
  2. All Original certificates (from 10th standard onwards).
  3. All Original Semester/Year Wise, Consolidated Marksheets & Degree/Diploma/ITI Certificate.
  4. Original supporting documents to substantiate all claims made in the application (e.g., experience certificates).
  5. Original certificate for PwBD candidates (UD ID).
  6. No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the present/parent employer, for all employed candidates.
  7. Original caste (SC/ST) or valid OBC/EWS certificate (OBC/EWS certificate must be issued on or after 01.04.2025).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Apply Here Click here
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website for Updates www.nitpy.ac.in

FAQs

Question 1: What is the selection process for the Technician post?

The selection process for the Technician post involves a qualifying Skill Test, followed by a Final Written Test. The final merit will be based on the marks of the Written Test only.

Question 2: Is there any negative marking in the examinations?

No, the notification clearly states that no negative marks will be given for attempting wrong answers in the Screening Test, Skill Test, or the Final Written Test (for MCQ parts).

Question 3: Where will the selection process be held?

The entire selection process (Screening Test, Skill Test, and Written Test) will be held at the NIT Puducherry premises in Karaikal.

Question 4: What is the syllabus for the Final Written Test?

The detailed syllabus for various posts is provided in Annexure-B of the official notification. It includes General Awareness, Reasoning, Aptitude, English, Computer Awareness, General Administration, and Subject/Domain-specific knowledge depending on the post.

entry.title: Golden Job Opportunity at Medanta: Recruitment for OP Billing Executive Posts, Freshers Can Apply
entry.content:
Golden Job Opportunity at Medanta: Recruitment for OP Billing Executive Posts, Freshers Can Apply

Join one of India's top healthcare institutions as an Assistant Executive/Executive in OP Billing. A fantastic career start for graduates with strong communication skills.

Post Name: Assistant Executive / Executive – OP Billing
Last Date: Ongoing
Total Posts: Multiple Vacancies

Medanta The Medicity, a prestigious and leading name in India's healthcare sector, has announced a significant recruitment drive to strengthen its Patient Care Services department. This recruitment is for the post of Assistant Executive / Executive – OP Billing, which offers a brilliant opportunity for energetic and talented young individuals who wish to build a career in one of the best medical institutions in the country. Known for its world-class medical facilities and patient-centric approach, Medanta is now ready to welcome new members to its administrative and customer service team.

This recruitment is particularly significant for graduates from any stream who possess excellent communication skills. The most attractive aspect of this post is that it does not require prior experience; freshers can apply with full confidence. This role provides an opportunity to play a crucial part in the smooth operation of the hospital's Out-Patient Department (OPD). The OP Billing Executive directly interacts with patients and their attendants, making them the first and most important link in the patient's experience at the hospital. This role requires a unique blend of administrative efficiency, outstanding customer service, and a deep understanding of hospital policies.

At a time when the healthcare industry is growing rapidly, securing a stable and respected job in an institution like Medanta can be a significant achievement for any young professional. This is not just a job, but a career path that offers you an opportunity to serve society and provides countless avenues for personal and professional growth. In this article, we will discuss in detail all the important information related to this recruitment, such as eligibility criteria, job responsibilities, selection process, and the application method, so that interested candidates can take full advantage of this opportunity.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive. This is a private sector job opportunity with one of India's top hospital chains, offering a chance to work in a dynamic and professional environment.

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameMedanta The Medicity
Advertisement NumberHR/JD/ADM/OPB01/25/VER.1.0
Post NameAssistant Executive / Executive - OP Billing
Total PostsMultiple Vacancies (as per requirement)
Job LocationVarious Medanta Hospitals (depending on the candidate's profile)
Application ModeOnline
Application StatusOngoing, do not wait for a last date.

Why Choose a Career at Medanta?

Starting or advancing your career at Medanta is a decision that promises significant professional growth and personal fulfillment. Medanta is not just a hospital; it is a world-class institution built on the pillars of excellence, innovation, and compassionate care. For an OP Billing Executive, this translates into a work environment that is both challenging and supportive. You will be part of an organization that is at the forefront of medical technology and patient care, allowing you to learn from the best in the industry. The role places you at the heart of patient interaction, giving you the opportunity to make a real difference in their healthcare journey. Medanta's commitment to employee development means you will have access to continuous training and opportunities to grow within the organization, potentially moving into supervisory or managerial roles in the future.

Salary and Remuneration

As this is a recruitment in a prestigious private sector institution, the salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, skills, and experience. Medanta is known for offering a highly competitive salary package that aligns with industry standards. Freshers can expect an attractive starting salary, while experienced candidates may receive a better package based on their previous salary and experience. In addition to the salary, selected candidates may also be eligible for other benefits such as health insurance, provident fund (PF), and performance-based incentives, as per the company's policy.

Post Name Estimated Salary
Assistant Executive / Executive - OP Billing To be decided based on interview and experience (Best in the industry)

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Skills

Medanta has kept the eligibility criteria for this role broad to allow talented candidates from various backgrounds to apply. The detailed requirements for qualification and skills are given below:

Criteria Details
Minimum Educational Qualification The candidate must be a Graduate in any discipline (e.g., B.A., B.Com, B.Sc., BBA, etc.) from a recognized university or institution.
Language Skills The candidate must be proficient in both English and Hindi with excellent oral and written communication skills. This is mandatory as the role involves effective communication with patients and staff.
Work Experience 0 to 3 years of work experience is required for this post. This means that freshers, who have recently completed their graduation, are fully eligible to apply. Candidates with some experience in customer service or billing may have an added advantage.

Age Limit

The official job description document does not mention a specific age limit. However, the minimum age for such corporate roles is typically 18 years. Candidates who are physically and mentally capable of performing the duties of the post can apply. The maximum age limit may be applicable as per the company's internal policies.

Selection Process

The selection process at Medanta is professional and entirely based on merit. Candidates for this post will be selected based on an assessment of their skills, knowledge, and personality. The selection process is likely to include the following stages:

  1. Resume Screening: First, the recruitment team will review all the applications and resumes received. They will shortlist candidates whose profiles (educational qualifications, skills) best match the job requirements. Therefore, it is important that your resume is well-written and clearly highlights your abilities.
  2. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be invited for a personal interview. This interview may be conducted in one or more rounds:
    • HR Round: In this round, the interviewer will assess your personality, communication skills, career goals, and your ability to fit culturally with the company. You may be asked general questions about your background and expectations.
    • Departmental/Operational Round: This round will be conducted by the Manager or Head of Department (HOD) of Patient Care Services. It will test your problem-solving abilities and your capacity to handle the job responsibilities through situational questions. For example, "How would you handle an angry patient who is dissatisfied with their bill?"
  3. Final Selection and Document Verification: Candidates who perform successfully in the interview will be offered the job. Subsequently, they will have to present all their original documents (educational certificates, identity proof, address proof, etc.) for verification.

Job Description and Key Responsibilities

The primary role of an OP Billing Executive is to ensure a smooth and efficient billing process for out-patients, providing excellent customer service. Key responsibilities include:

  • Billing and Cash Handling: Preparing and issuing bills for all OPD services, including consultations, diagnostics, and procedures. This involves accurately handling cash, credit card transactions, and other modes of payment, and ensuring proper reconciliation at the end of the shift.
  • Patient Interaction and Customer Service: Acting as the first point of contact for patients regarding billing queries. Professionally and empathetically answering patient questions about charges, payment options, and hospital policies.
  • Coordination with Other Departments: Liaising with doctors, diagnostic departments, and other hospital staff to ensure that all services are billed correctly. This requires strong interpersonal and coordination skills.
  • System Management: Operating the Hospital Information System (HIS) to generate bills, update patient information, and maintain accurate records.
  • Report Generation: Preparing daily, weekly, or monthly collection reports as required by the management.

How to Prepare for the Interview

Acing the interview for this role requires good preparation. Here are some tips that can help you succeed:

  • Research the Company: Read about Medanta's mission, vision, values, and achievements. This will show that you are serious about working for the company.
  • Understand the Job Role: Understand each responsibility mentioned in the job description. Think about how your skills and experiences align with these responsibilities.
  • Practice Common Interview Questions: Prepare answers for common questions like "Tell me about yourself," "Why do you want to work at Medanta?," and "What are your strengths and weaknesses?".
  • Prepare for Situational Questions: Think about situations related to customer service. For example, how you work under pressure, how you collaborate in a team, or how you handle a difficult customer.
  • Showcase Your Communication Skills: Speak clearly, confidently, and politely during the interview. Remember, this role relies heavily on communication, so this is your chance to demonstrate your abilities.
  • Dress Professionally: Dress in clean and professional attire for the interview. This creates a positive first impression.
  • Ask Questions: At the end of the interview, ask some thoughtful questions about the role, the team, or the company. This shows your interest and curiosity.

How to Apply Online

The application process for this post at Medanta is simple and online. Interested candidates can easily apply by following the steps below:

  1. Visit the Official Portal: First, click on the "Apply Online" link provided in the "Important Links" section below. This will take you to the job portal.
  2. Search for the Job: On the portal, search for this job vacancy using keywords like "Medanta" or "Assistant Executive - Billing".
  3. Register/Login: If you are new to the portal, you will need to register using your email ID and mobile number. If you already have an account, simply log in.
  4. Fill the Application Form: Open the application form for the job. Fill in all the required information such as personal details, contact information, educational qualifications, and work experience (if any) accurately.
  5. Upload Resume: Upload your latest and well-formatted resume (CV). Ensure that it contains all your relevant information.
  6. Review and Submit: Before submitting the application form, carefully review all the filled information to ensure there are no errors. Once everything is correct, submit the application.
  7. Confirmation: After a successful application submission, you may receive a confirmation email or message. Save a printout or screenshot of the application form for future reference.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Official Notification / Job Description Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
Medanta's Official Website Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Who can apply for this post?

Any candidate who has completed a bachelor's degree in any discipline and has a good command of both Hindi and English can apply for this post.

Question 2: Is experience mandatory for this job?

No, this post is also open for freshers. Candidates with 0 to 3 years of experience can apply.

Question 3: What will be the main responsibilities of this job?

The main responsibilities include preparing and issuing bills for out-patients, handling payments, professionally and empathetically answering patient queries, and coordinating with doctors and other hospital staff for smooth operations.

Question 4: What is the selection process?

The selection process will primarily involve resume screening followed by one or more rounds of personal interviews.

Question 5: Is there any application fee?

No, there is no fee to apply for this post. The application process is completely free.

Disclaimer: The information provided in this article is based on the official job description document (HR/JD/ADM/OPB01/25/VER.1.0) issued by Medanta. While every effort has been made to ensure accuracy, candidates are advised to visit the company's official website or contact the recruitment department for confirmation of all details before applying. The "Apply Online" link provided is of an external job portal; candidates should verify the availability of the job on it themselves before applying.

entry.title: Indian Coast Guard Recruitment 2025: Apply for 14 Group C Vacancies in the East Region
entry.content:
Indian Coast Guard Recruitment 2025: Apply for 14 Group C Vacancies in the East Region

Direct Civilian Recruitment for Store Keeper, Driver, Engine Driver, and Peon Posts - Comprehensive Guide on Offline Application Process

Post Name: Store Keeper Grade-II, Engine Driver, Lascar, Multi-Tasking Staff (Peon), Civilian Motor Transport Driver, Welder
Last Date: December 29, 2025
Total Posts: 14 Vacancies

The Indian Coast Guard (ICG), a multi-mission maritime organization under the Ministry of Defence, has officially launched its recruitment drive for the year 2025. Functioning as the primary agency for maritime search and rescue, law enforcement in territorial waters, and environmental protection, the ICG offers a unique and disciplined work environment even for its civilian staff. The latest notification is released specifically for the Coast Guard Region (East), with its headquarters in Chennai, Tamil Nadu. This recruitment seeks to fill 14 Group C Non-Gazetted civilian vacancies. These positions are highly coveted as they offer the security of a central government career while allowing employees to contribute to the nation's maritime defense infrastructure.

The 2025 recruitment cycle targets a wide array of technical and administrative roles, making it accessible for candidates who have completed their 10th or 12th standard. The positions available include Store Keeper Grade-II, Engine Driver, Lascar (First Class), Civilian Motor Transport Driver (Ordinary Grade), Multi-Tasking Staff (Peon), and Welder. For those who prefer technical field work, roles like Engine Driver and Welder are available, while those looking for administrative support can apply for Peon or Store Keeper posts. A critical point for all aspirants is that this is an Offline Recruitment. Candidates must download the prescribed application form, fill it manually, and dispatch it via Ordinary Post to the Chennai headquarters. The final date for the receipt of applications is December 29, 2025.

Working in the Indian Coast Guard as a civilian employee provides more than just a monthly paycheck. It offers a life of discipline, exposure to advanced maritime technology, and a comprehensive benefits package based on the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC). Whether you are an experienced driver with a heavy vehicle license or a matriculate seeking an entry-level peon role in a defense environment, this notification provides a transparent merit-based pathway. This exhaustive 2000-word article serves as your ultimate manual, providing deep insights into the salary structure, discipline-wise eligibility, the intricacies of the selection process, and a step-by-step tutorial on ensuring your offline application is not rejected by the authorities.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Success in offline recruitments depends heavily on punctuality and adherence to posting rules. Candidates must be mindful that the Indian Coast Guard will only accept applications sent through Ordinary Post. Any application sent via Speed Post, Registered Post, or private courier services will likely be returned or rejected at the gate of Fort St. George. Ensure your application is dispatched well in advance of the December deadline.

Recruitment Feature Dates and Key Information
Official Notification Status Released (Chennai East Region)
Application Start Date November 2025 (Active)
Last Date to Receive Application December 29, 2025
Application Mode Offline (Ordinary Post Only)
Job Category Group C (Civilian / Non-Gazetted)
Job Location Chennai and East Coast Stations
Selection Body Indian Coast Guard Headquarters (East)

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Salary Matrix

The 14 vacancies are categorized into specific technical and non-technical cadres. The salary structure follows the latest 7th Pay Commission levels, ensuring a stable financial career with regular increments and central government allowances such as Dearness Allowance (DA) and House Rent Allowance (HRA).

Post Designation Total Vacancies Pay Level (7th CPC) Max Age Limit
Engine Driver 03 (UR-1, OBC-1, SC-1) Level 4 (₹25,500 - ₹81,100) 30 Years
Civilian Motor Transport Driver 03 (UR-1, SC-1, EWS-1) Level 2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200) 27 Years
Store Keeper Grade-II 01 (OBC) Level 2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200) 25 Years
Multi-Tasking Staff (Peon) 04 (UR-2, SC-1, ST-1) Level 1 (₹18,000 - ₹56,900) 27 Years
Lascar (First Class) 02 (UR) Level 1 (₹18,000 - ₹56,900) 30 Years
Welder (Skilled) 01 (UR) Level 2 (₹19,900 - ₹63,200) 27 Years
💰 Estimated Monthly Gross Salary (In-Hand):
  • Engine Driver (Level 4): Approximately ₹42,000 to ₹45,000 per month.
  • Driver / Welder / Store Keeper (Level 2): Approximately ₹32,000 to ₹35,000 per month.
  • Peon / Lascar (Level 1): Approximately ₹28,000 to ₹31,000 per month.

Selected candidates will also be eligible for the New Pension Scheme (NPS), medical facilities for self and family, and Leave Travel Concession (LTC) as per the central government rules for civilian defense employees.

Eligibility Criteria and Educational Qualifications

The Indian Coast Guard maintains strict eligibility standards. Unlike other departments, technical roles in the ICG often require specialized certificates of competency alongside basic education. All qualifications must be completed before the cut-off date of December 29, 2025.

  • Store Keeper Grade-II: Minimum 12th Standard pass from a recognized board. Mandatory: At least one year of experience in handling stores in a government or reputed private organization.
  • Engine Driver: 10th Standard (Matriculation) pass. Mandatory: Certificate of Competency as Engine Driver from a recognized government institute.
  • Lascar (First Class): 10th Standard pass with at least 03 years of experience in service on a boat or sea-going vessel.
  • Civilian Motor Transport Driver (CMTD): 10th Standard pass. Mandatory: Must possess a valid driving license for both Heavy and Light Motor Vehicles. Experience: 02 years in motor vehicle driving.
  • MTS (Peon): 10th Standard pass from a recognized board. Experience: 02 years as an Office Attendant or similar role is highly desirable.
  • Welder (Skilled): 10th Standard pass plus ITI Certificate in the respective trade OR 03 years of practical experience in the trade.

Age Relaxation: Upper age relaxation is provided to various categories: SC/ST (5 years), OBC-NCL (3 years), and PwBD (10 years). The age will be calculated as of the last date of application receipt.

Selection Process and Evaluation roadmap

The selection process for ICG Group C posts is designed to test both the mental caliber and technical proficiency of the candidates. The competition is expected to be high, with the Written Examination being the primary merit decider.

  1. Screening of Applications: Since this is an offline process, the ICG staff will first scrutinize the applications. Only those who have correctly filled the form and attached all valid documents will be issued an Admit Card.
  2. Written Examination: Candidates will be called to Chennai for an objective-type test. The paper usually consists of 80 to 100 questions covering General Knowledge, Mathematics, Reasoning, and English.
  3. Trade / Skill Test: Mandatory for posts like Driver, Engine Driver, and Welder. This is qualifying in nature. Drivers will be tested on their maneuvering skills, and Welders on their technical precision.
  4. Document Verification (DV): Successful candidates must produce their original marksheets, caste certificates, and experience letters for physical verification.
  5. Medical Examination: A basic fitness test to ensure the candidate is fit for a defense-related environment. Vision and hearing must meet the specified standards.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

Preparing for an Indian Coast Guard civilian exam requires a strategy focusing on 10th-standard level basic concepts. The duration of the exam is typically 90 to 120 minutes.

  • General Awareness (25%): Current Affairs, Indian History, Geography (Maritime focus), and Sports.
  • Mathematics (25%): Numerical ability, percentages, ratio, time and work, and basic geometry.
  • Reasoning (25%): Logic puzzles, coding-decoding, series completion, and analogies.
  • General English (25%): Grammar, synonyms, antonyms, and sentence correction.

Negative Marking: Candidates should note that there is usually no negative marking in ICG civilian exams, but they should verify the instructions on the question paper on the day of the exam. Normalization of scores is not required as the exam is conducted in a single shift.

Expert Preparation Strategy for Coast Guard Exams

Success in the ICG Group C drive requires a balanced approach. Since the number of vacancies is limited (14), scoring high in the written test is the only way to ensure a final selection.

Focus on General Awareness: A significant portion of the paper revolves around basic general knowledge. Focus on the role of the Indian Coast Guard and the Ministry of Defence. Learn the names of the various coastal regions of India and the basics of maritime laws. Reading a monthly current affairs magazine will give you the edge.

Trade Proficiency: If you are applying for the Driver or Engine Driver post, the skill test is the ultimate hurdle. Practice driving in complex tracks to prepare for the Chennai field test. Welders should revisit their ITI modules, focusing on safety protocols and different types of welding joints used in maritime repairs.

Accuracy in Mathematics: The mathematics section is based on the 10th-standard level. Use R.S. Aggarwal level books to solve at least 50 numerical problems daily. Focus on Time and Distance and Simple Interest, as these are common topics in ICG question papers. Maintaining a high accuracy rate will help you clear the cut-off comfortably.

How to Apply Offline: Step-by-Step Guide

The offline application process is the most critical stage. Any error in the envelope or the form can lead to automatic rejection. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure your form reaches the destination safely.

  1. Download Form: Download the official Application Format from the Indian Coast Guard website or the link provided below. Print it on a high-quality A4 sheet.
  2. Fill the Data: Fill the form in BLOCK LETTERS using a blue or black ballpoint pen. Ensure your name and date of birth match your 10th-standard marksheet.
  3. Paste Photograph: Paste one recent passport-size color photograph in the designated box. Do not staple it. Cross-sign (Self-attest) the photo carefully without hiding your face.
  4. Compile Documents: Attach self-attested photocopies of your marksheets, caste certificate, experience certificates, and Aadhaar card.
  5. The Envelope: Use a large sturdy envelope (size 28 cm x 13 cm). On the front side, write in BOLD CAPITAL LETTERS:
    "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF [POST NAME] & CATEGORY [YOUR CASTE]"
  6. Postal Mode: Go to your nearest Post Office and send the envelope via ORDINARY POST ONLY. Do not use Speed Post or Registered Post.
  7. Destination Address: Ensure the address mentioned in the links section is written correctly with the proper PIN code.

Best Books for Preparation

Using standard study materials will keep your preparation aligned with the ICG selection pattern. Below are the top recommendations for the 2025 exam.

Subject Area Recommended Book List
General Aptitude Quantitative Aptitude and Logical Reasoning by R.S. Aggarwal.
General English Objective General English by S.P. Bakshi (Arihant Publications).
General Knowledge Lucent’s General Knowledge and Manorama Yearbook.
Driver Trade RTO Traffic Rules and Signs Handbooks (English/Hindi).
Welder Trade ITI Welder Trade Theory by Arihant or Asian Publishers.

Documents Needed to Apply

Documentation is the primary basis for your admit card issuance. Ensure all copies are Self-Attested. Original documents are only required at the time of final verification.

  1. Matriculation (10th) Certificate: Essential as proof of age and educational qualification.
  2. Higher Secondary (12th) Certificate: Mandatory for the Store Keeper post.
  3. Technical Competency Certificate: Engine Driver certificate or ITI certificate (for Welder).
  4. Experience Proof: Experience letters on official letterheads from previous employers.
  5. Caste Certificate: Valid SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS certificate in the Central Government format.
  6. Identity Proof: Clear photocopy of Aadhaar Card or Voter ID.
  7. Extra Photographs: Include two additional passport-size photographs without signature inside the envelope.

Important Links and Address

Resource Description Official Link / Postal Details
Postal Address to Send Form The Commander, Coast Guard Region (East), Near Napier Bridge, Fort St George (PO), Chennai – 600 009
Download Application Form PDF Download Here
Official Indian Coast Guard Site Visit Site

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I send the application via Speed Post if I am running out of time?

No. The Indian Coast Guard explicitly specifies the use of Ordinary Post only. Applications sent through Speed Post, Registered Post, or private couriers are often not accepted at the delivery desk of Fort St. George. It is safer to send it early via ordinary mail.

Q2: Is there an application fee for this recruitment?

No. According to the official notification for the East Region (Chennai), there is no application fee for any category. The recruitment process is completely free of charge.

Q3: What if I don't have the experience certificate for the Peon post?

While the educational qualification is 10th pass, the notification mentions 02 years of experience as desirable. Candidates with experience will be given preference during the initial screening stage.

Q4: What vision standard is required for the Driver post?

For civilian driver posts in the ICG, candidates must have 6/6 distant vision without glasses in both eyes. Color blindness is usually a disqualification for any defense-related driving role.

Q5: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, if you meet the eligibility criteria for multiple posts, you can apply. However, you must send separate applications in separate envelopes for each post category. Do not put multiple forms in a single envelope.

Q6: When will the written exam be conducted?

The specific date has not been announced yet. Generally, ICG conducts exams within 2-3 months after the application deadline. Shortlisted candidates will receive their admit cards through the post.

entry.title: Telangana High Court Office Subordinate Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
Telangana High Court Office Subordinate Recruitment 2026

Notification No. 09/2026: The High Court for the State of Telangana is inviting online applications for direct recruitment to the post of Office Subordinate in the Telangana Judicial Ministerial and Subordinate Service. This is a significant opportunity for candidates who have passed 7th to 10th class to secure a government job in the judicial districts across the state. The online application portal will be open from January 24, 2026, to February 13, 2026. The posts carry a pay scale of ₹19,000-58,850.

Post Name: Office Subordinate
Last Date: 13 February 2026
Total Posts: (District-wise vacancies annexed)

The High Court for the State of Telangana has officially announced the recruitment for the position of Office Subordinate through Notification No. 09/2026, dated January 19, 2026. This recruitment drive aims to fill numerous vacancies in the Judicial Districts of Telangana, offering a stable career path within the state's judicial system. The role of an Office Subordinate is crucial for the smooth functioning of the courts, and this drive provides a valuable chance for individuals with basic education to enter government service.

The selection process is streamlined and consists of a 45-mark OMR-based written examination followed by a 5-mark oral interview (viva-voce). A unique aspect of this recruitment is the educational qualification criteria, which specifically targets candidates who have passed any examination between the 7th and 10th standard. Importantly, candidates with qualifications higher than the 10th class are not eligible, making this an exclusive opportunity for a specific academic segment. The online application window is short, and interested candidates are urged to apply promptly through the official High Court website.

Telangana District Court Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Aspirants must keep track of the key dates to avoid missing any deadlines. The entire process, from application to result declaration, will be managed through the official High Court website.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization High Court for the State of Telangana
Notification Number 09/2026
Post Name Office Subordinate
Application Start Date 24 January 2026
Application Last Date 13 February 2026 (up to 11:59 PM)
Application Mode Online
Job Type Government Job (Judicial Service)
Job Location Various Judicial Districts in Telangana
Official Website https://tshc.gov.in

District-wise Vacancy Details

The vacancies for the post of Office Subordinate are distributed across various judicial districts and units in Telangana. The detailed breakdown is as follows:

District / Unit Total Vacancies
ADILABAD2
BHADRADRI KOTHAGUDEM14
CBI, HYDERABAD9
CITY SMALL CAUSES COURT, HYDERABAD2
HANUMAKONDA2
JANGAON2
JAYASHANKAR-BHUPALAPALLY7
JOGULAMBA GADWAL7
KAMAREDDY14
KARIMNAGAR3
KHAMMAM33
KUMURAM BHEEM ASIFABAD3
MAHABUBABAD10
MAHABUBNAGAR11
MANCHERIAL1
MEDAK2
MEDCHAL-MALKAJGIRI106
SESSIONS JUDGE, HYDERABAD5
NALGONDA3
NARAYANPET6
NIZAMABAD12
PEDDAPALLI11
RANGAREDDY23
SANGAREDDY17
SURYAPET6
YADADRI-BHONGIR8
Total319+ (Please refer to official notification for exact total)
Note: The number of vacancies is subject to change. Candidates are advised to check the detailed category-wise abstract in the official notification.

Examination Fee

  • For OC and BC category candidates: ₹600/- (plus applicable service charges)
  • For SC, ST, EWS, and Persons with Disabilities (PWD) candidates: ₹400/- (plus applicable service charges)

The fee must be paid online. Fees once paid will not be refunded.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet all the eligibility requirements regarding education, age, and language proficiency to be considered for the post.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification
  • Candidates must have passed any examination between the 7th class and 10th class or its equivalent.
  • Crucial Note: Candidates possessing qualifications higher than 10th class (e.g., Intermediate, Diploma, Degree) are NOT ELIGIBLE to apply.
  • Applicants are also encouraged to mention any work experience they have in skills like cooking, carpentry, plumbing, electrical work, etc., in their online application.
Age Limit (as on 01.07.2026)
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 46 Years

Upper Age Limit Relaxation:

  • 5 years for SC, ST, BC, and EWS candidates.
  • 10 years for Physically Handicapped persons.
  • Age relaxation for Ex-servicemen as per rules.
  • Relaxation is also available for candidates working on a contract/outsourcing basis in the specified judicial services (must have at least 2 years of service).
Linguistic Qualification Candidates must have adequate knowledge of the language(s) of the district they are applying for. The list of specified languages for each district will be available in the User Guide on the High Court website.

Selection Process

The selection method for the Office Subordinate post is a two-stage process:

  1. OMR Based Examination (Written Test): This is an objective-type test with Multiple Choice Questions (MCQs). It will be for a total of 45 marks and will have a duration of 60 minutes.
  2. Oral Interview (Viva-Voce): Candidates who qualify in the written examination will be shortlisted in a 1:3 ratio for the oral interview. The interview will be for 5 marks.
  3. Final Merit List: The final selection list will be prepared based on the combined marks obtained in the OMR-based examination and the oral interview (Total 50 marks).

Minimum Qualifying Marks in Written Test:

  • OC and EWS candidates: 40%
  • BC candidates: 35%
  • SC, ST & PH candidates: 30%

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The OMR-based examination will be a single-paper test focusing on General Knowledge. The standard of the questions will be consistent with the minimum educational qualification (7th to 10th class).

Subject No. of Questions Max. Marks Duration Question Language
General Knowledge 45 45 60 Minutes English and Telugu

Syllabus for General Knowledge:

The syllabus will cover topics appropriate for the 7th-10th class level. Candidates should focus on:

  • Current Affairs (State, National, and International).
  • General Science in everyday life.
  • History, Geography, and Polity of India, with a special focus on Telangana.
  • Indian Constitution and its salient features.
  • Telangana State's history, culture, and social movements.
  • Basic arithmetic and reasoning ability.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for the post of Office Subordinate in this recruitment drive.

How to Prepare for the Exam

Preparation should be targeted and consistent, focusing on the basics.

  • Study School Textbooks: The best source for preparation is the Telangana State Board school textbooks from 7th to 10th class for subjects like Social Studies, General Science, and Mathematics.
  • Stay Updated with Current Affairs: Read a local Telugu newspaper daily to stay updated on current events, especially those related to Telangana.
  • Practice Mock Tests: Solve previous years' question papers of similar level exams (like Group-D or other subordinate services) to get familiar with the pattern and manage time effectively.
  • Prepare for the Interview: For the viva-voce, be prepared to answer questions about your background, the district you belong to, and your general awareness. Your communication skills and confidence will be assessed.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard publications and school textbooks for their preparation.

  • Telangana State Board Textbooks (Class 7 to 10): For General Knowledge, Social Studies, and General Science.
  • Lucent's General Knowledge: A good reference book for static GK.
  • A reputed Telugu Daily Newspaper: For staying updated with current affairs.
  • Basic Arithmetic & Reasoning Books: Any standard book like R.S. Aggarwal for practicing basic level problems.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online and consists of two parts: One Time Profile Registration (OTPR) and Application Submission.

  1. Visit the official Telangana High Court website: https://tshc.gov.in.
  2. Find the recruitment notification (No. 09/2026) and click on the "Apply Online" link.
  3. Part A (OTPR): If you are a new user, complete the One Time Profile Registration form. After submission, you will receive an OTPR ID and Password. Keep these credentials safe.
  4. Part B (Application Form): Log in using your OTPR ID and Password. Fill out the application form for the Office Subordinate post for the desired district.
  5. Upload Documents: Upload scanned copies of all required certificates (educational, caste, residence, etc.) in PDF format as instructed in the User Guide.
  6. Pay the examination fee through the online payment gateway.
  7. Review your application carefully and submit it. You will receive a unique application number for each district you apply for.
  8. Download and print the final submitted application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply (for Uploading)

Candidates must scan and keep the following documents ready in PDF format before starting the application process:

  1. Certificates of academic qualifications (7th to 10th class pass certificates, marks lists).
  2. Certificate evidencing date of birth (SSC or equivalent).
  3. Community Certificate (SC/ST/BC/EWS) issued by the competent authority.
  4. Person with Disability (PWD) certificate, if applicable.
  5. Local status/residence certificate as per G.O.Ms.No.124.
  6. Discharge certificate for Ex-servicemen.
  7. No-objection Certificate (NOC) if currently employed.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (Link will be active from 24.01.2026)

(FAQs)

Q1: I have passed Intermediate (12th class). Am I eligible to apply?

No, candidates who have a qualification higher than the 10th class are not eligible for the post of Office Subordinate in this recruitment.

Q2: What is the selection process?

The selection process consists of a 45-mark OMR-based written test on General Knowledge, followed by a 5-mark oral interview (viva-voce).

Q3: Can I apply for more than one district?

Yes, by using the same One Time Profile Registration (OTPR) ID, you can apply for multiple districts where vacancies are available. You will receive a unique application number for each district you apply for.

Q4: In which language will the question paper be?

The question paper for the OMR-based examination will be available in two languages: English and Telugu.

entry.title: BSF Constable (GD) Sports Quota भर्ती 2025 — 391 पदों के लिए आवेदन करें!
entry.content:
BSF Constable (GD) Sports Quota भर्ती 2025 — 391 पदों पर आवेदन करें

पदक विजेता महिला और पुरुष खिलाड़ियों के लिए BSF में शामिल होने का सुनहरा अवसर।

नमस्कार खिलाड़ियों! Border Security Force (BSF), जो Ministry of Home Affairs के अंतर्गत आती है, ने Meritorious Sportspersons के लिए Constable (General Duty) पद पर भर्ती हेतु अधिसूचना जारी की है। यह भर्ती वर्ष 2025 के लिए Sports Quota के अंतर्गत की जा रही है, जिसमें कुल 391 पद (महिला और पुरुष दोनों) शामिल हैं।

यह उन खिलाड़ियों के लिए एक शानदार अवसर है जो देश की सेवा के साथ-साथ अपने खेल के जुनून को भी आगे बढ़ाना चाहते हैं। आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह से ऑनलाइन है। नीचे इस भर्ती से जुड़ी सभी आवश्यक जानकारियाँ दी गई हैं।

BSF Sports Quota भर्ती 2025 — पद विवरण

इस भर्ती में कुल 391 पद 29 विभिन्न खेल विधाओं के लिए हैं। पदों का वितरण इस प्रकार है:

श्रेणीपदों की संख्या
पुरुष खिलाड़ी197
महिला खिलाड़ी194
कुल391

ये पद Archery, Athletics, Boxing, Basketball, Football, Gymnastics, Hockey, Judo, Swimming, Volleyball, Wrestling आदि खेलों में वितरित किए गए हैं। प्रत्येक खेल में कितने पद हैं इसकी विस्तृत जानकारी आधिकारिक अधिसूचना में दी गई है।

नोट: पदों की संख्या अनुमानित है और इनमें परिवर्तन संभव है। चयनित उम्मीदवारों की पोस्टिंग भारत में कहीं भी की जा सकती है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि / समय
ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रारंभ16 अक्टूबर 2025 (रात्रि 00:01 बजे से)
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि04 नवम्बर 2025 (रात्रि 11:59 बजे तक)

वेतनमान एवं आवेदन शुल्क

वेतनमान: चयनित उम्मीदवारों को 7th CPC के अनुसार Level-3 (₹21,700 - ₹69,100) वेतनमान में नियुक्त किया जाएगा। इसके साथ अन्य भत्ते भी दिए जाएंगे।

आवेदन शुल्क: केवल General (UR) और OBC श्रेणी के पुरुष उम्मीदवारों के लिए ₹159/-। महिला, SC, ST श्रेणी के उम्मीदवारों को कोई शुल्क नहीं देना होगा।

पात्रता मानदंड

मानदंडविवरण
आयु सीमा1 अगस्त 2025 तक 18 से 23 वर्ष।
आयु में छूटSC/ST को 5 वर्ष, OBC को 3 वर्ष की छूट। अन्य छूट सरकारी नियमों के अनुसार।
शैक्षणिक योग्यताकिसी मान्यता प्राप्त बोर्ड से 10वीं कक्षा उत्तीर्ण।
खेल योग्यतावे खिलाड़ी जिन्होंने 04/11/2023 से 04/11/2025 के बीच राष्ट्रीय या अंतरराष्ट्रीय स्तर की प्रतियोगिता में भाग लिया या पदक जीता है, पात्र हैं।

चयन प्रक्रिया

BSF Constable (GD) Sports Quota भर्ती में कोई लिखित परीक्षा नहीं होगी। चयन निम्न चरणों पर आधारित होगा:

  1. ऑनलाइन आवेदन एवं स्पोर्ट्स प्रमाणपत्रों की जांच।
  2. दस्तावेज़ सत्यापन।
  3. Physical Standard Test (PST)।
  4. Medical Examination।
  5. खेल उपलब्धियों के आधार पर अंतिम मेरिट सूची।

शारीरिक मापदंड (PST)

पैरामीटरपुरुषमहिला
लंबाई170 सेमी157 सेमी
छाती80 सेमी (सामान्य)
85 सेमी (फुलाने पर)		लागू नहीं

छूट: ST, Gorkha, Kumaoni, Dogra, Maratha एवं उत्तर-पूर्वी राज्यों के उम्मीदवारों को ऊँचाई/छाती में छूट मिलेगी।

ऑनलाइन आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. BSF की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट पर जाएँ: https://rectt.bsf.gov.in
  2. ‘One Time Registration (OTR)’ करें या लॉगिन करें।
  3. “Recruitment of Meritorious Sportspersons to the post of Constable (GD) 2025” लिंक चुनें।
  4. सभी विवरण भरें और आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ अपलोड करें।
  5. यदि लागू हो तो आवेदन शुल्क का भुगतान करें।
  6. फाइनल सबमिशन के बाद प्रिंट आउट निकालें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें
अधिसूचना PDFडाउनलोड करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या इसमें लिखित परीक्षा होगी?
नहीं, इसमें कोई लिखित परीक्षा नहीं होगी। चयन खेल उपलब्धियों, दस्तावेज़ सत्यापन, PST और मेडिकल परीक्षण पर आधारित होगा।
प्रश्न 2: क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?
प्रारंभ में नियुक्ति अस्थायी होगी, परन्तु बाद में स्थायी होने की संभावना है।
प्रश्न 3: क्या राज्य स्तरीय खिलाड़ी पात्र हैं?
मुख्य रूप से राष्ट्रीय या अंतरराष्ट्रीय स्तर के खिलाड़ी पात्र हैं।
प्रश्न 4: क्या महिला उम्मीदवारों को शुल्क देना होगा?
नहीं, सभी श्रेणियों की महिला उम्मीदवारों को आवेदन शुल्क से छूट है।

entry.title: Bombay High Court (Nagpur Bench) Recruitment 2025: Vacancy for Junior Translator and Interpreter, Salary up to ₹1.5 Lakh
entry.content:
Bombay High Court (Nagpur Bench) Recruitment 2025: Vacancy for Junior Translator and Interpreter, Salary up to ₹1.5 Lakh

The Nagpur Bench of the Bombay High Court has issued Notification No. N. Estt. /2025/6314 for the recruitment of 'Junior Translator and Interpreter' (Marathi). A golden opportunity for domiciled candidates of Maharashtra.

An excellent opportunity has emerged for candidates with a strong command of languages who aspire to build a career in the judiciary. The High Court of Judicature at Bombay, Nagpur Bench, has released its official recruitment notification for the year 2025. The Court has invited online applications from eligible and interested candidates for the post of 'Junior Translator and Interpreter' on its establishment. This recruitment is specifically for translators proficient in the 'Marathi' language.

Through this recruitment process, a Select List and a Wait List will be prepared. Selected candidates will be offered an attractive pay scale under Pay Matrix S-18 as per the 7th Pay Commission, starting from ₹ 49,100 and going up to ₹ 1,55,800. The application process has commenced on November 27, 2025, and candidates can apply online until December 12, 2025. This comprehensive article will provide you with every important detail regarding this recruitment, including eligibility criteria, age limit, educational qualifications, selection process, exam pattern, syllabus, and a step-by-step guide to applying.

Recruitment Overview

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameBombay High Court, Nagpur Bench
Advertisement NumberN. Estt. /2025/6314
Post NameJunior Translator and Interpreter
LanguageMarathi
Total PanelsSelect List (06) + Wait List (02)
Pay ScalePay Matrix S-18 (₹ 49,100 - ₹ 1,55,800)
Job LocationNagpur, Maharashtra
Application ModeOnline
Last Date to ApplyDecember 12, 2025 (up to 05:00 PM)
Official Websitebombayhighcourt.nic.in

Important Dates

Candidates should note that the application window is very short. The link will only be active for the specified period.

Event Date and Time
Date of AdvertisementNovember 26, 2025
Online Application CommencesNovember 27, 2025 (from 11:00 AM)
Last Date for Online ApplicationDecember 12, 2025 (up to 05:00 PM)
Admit Card Release DateTo be notified on the website
Date of Screening TestTo be announced soon

Vacancy Details (Panel Preparation)

This recruitment process is being conducted to prepare a panel rather than filling a specific number of vacancies immediately. As per the advertisement, the selection committee will prepare the following two types of lists:

  • Select List: 06 candidates (These are the candidates who will be offered appointment directly).
  • Wait List: 02 candidates (These candidates will be given an opportunity if a candidate from the select list does not join or resigns).

Note: This list will be valid for a period of two years from the date of its publication. The number of vacancies is subject to change due to administrative reasons.

Salary, Allowances, and Probation

The post of Junior Translator in the Bombay High Court is a respectable and high-paying position.

  • Pay Matrix Level: S-18
  • Pay Scale: ₹ 49,100 - ₹ 1,55,800/-
  • Other Allowances: In addition to the basic pay, candidates will receive Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), Transport Allowance, and other admissible allowances as per the rules.
  • Probation Period: Selected candidates will initially be placed on a probation period of 02 years. They will be made permanent only after satisfactory service.

Eligibility Criteria

It is mandatory for the candidate to fulfill the following eligibility conditions as of the date of publication of the advertisement (26/11/2025).

1. Domicile

The candidate must possess a Domicile Certificate of the State of Maharashtra. Candidates from other states are not eligible for this post.

2. Educational Qualification

  • Essential Degree: The candidate must possess a Degree in Languages from a recognized University, with English and Marathi as subjects.
    (Note: Candidates who did not have English and Marathi as subjects at the degree level are not eligible to apply.)
  • Preference: Preference will be given to candidates who possess a Degree in Law from a recognized University.
  • Language Proficiency: The candidate must be proficient in English and Marathi. Additionally, they should have knowledge of either Hindi or Konkani.

3. Computer Qualification

The candidate must possess a certificate of proficiency in operating a Word Processor in Windows and Linux. Knowledge of M.S. Office, M.S. Word, Open Office, etc., is essential. Certificates obtained from the following institutions will be valid:

  • MS-CIT (MKCL)
  • DOEACC / NIELIT
  • C-DAC, NIC, CEDIT, DATAPRO
  • Goa/Maharashtra State Board of Technical Education
  • Computer certificates issued by Universities
  • Qualifications specified by the Government of Maharashtra G.R. (dated 04/02/2013, 08/01/2018, 16/07/2018).

4. Age Limit (as on the date of advertisement)

Category Minimum Age Maximum Age
General (Open)18 years38 years
SC, ST, OBC, SBC (as specified by Govt. of Maharashtra)18 years43 years
High Court / Government Employees (applying through proper channel)18 yearsNo Limit
Other Conditions: The candidate must not have more than two living children born after 28/03/2006 (Small Family Rule).

Selection Process: A Two-Stage Evaluation

The selection process will be conducted in two stages: a Screening Test and a Viva-voce (Interview).

Stage 1: Screening Test (Written Examination)

Eligible candidates will have to appear for a written test of 100 marks.

  • Total Marks: 100
  • Time Duration: 3 Hours (Three Hours)
  • Minimum Qualifying Marks: 50 Marks

Exam Pattern and Syllabus:

Subject Marks
English Grammar30 Marks
Essay20 Marks
Letter Writing10 Marks
Translation
  • English to Marathi
  • English to Hindi or Konkani
40 Marks
Total100 Marks

Stage 2: Viva-voce (Interview)

  • Only those candidates who secure at least 50 marks in the screening test will be called for the viva-voce.
  • Total Marks: 20 Marks

The final merit list will be prepared based on the total marks obtained in the screening test and the viva-voce.

How to Apply Online

The application process is exclusively online. Applications sent through any other mode will not be accepted.

  1. Visit the Website: First, go to the official website of the Bombay High Court at https://bombayhighcourt.nic.in and select "Recruitment" in the "Nagpur Bench" section.
  2. Read Instructions: Click on the "Junior Translator & Interpreter" recruitment link and read the detailed advertisement carefully.
  3. Fee Payment: Click on the "SBI Collect" link and pay the fee of ₹300. Note down the Reference Number (DU Reference No.) received after payment.
  4. Fill the Form: Click on the "Apply Online" link. Enter your personal details, educational qualifications (SSC, HSC, Graduation, PG), and computer qualification.
  5. Enter Reference Number: Enter the fee payment reference number in the correct column.
  6. Upload Photo and Signature: Upload your latest scanned passport-size photo and signature (file size not more than 40 KB, .jpg/.jpeg format).
  7. Submit: Tick the "Declaration" and click on "I Agree" to submit the form. No changes will be possible after submission.
  8. Print Application: After successful submission, note down the Registration ID and take a printout of the form from the "Print Application" tab. Keep it safe for future reference.

Documents Required for Verification

At the time of the viva-voce (interview), candidates will have to produce self-attested copies and original documents of the following:

  • Proof of Date of Birth: Birth Certificate, School Leaving Certificate (LC), or 10th Board Certificate.
  • Educational Certificates: Marksheets and Passing Certificates of SSC, HSC, Graduation, Law Degree, and Post-Graduation. (Marks conversion certificate is mandatory if grades are awarded).
  • Computer Certificate: MS-CIT, DOEACC, or other valid certificates.
  • Experience Certificate: If you have experience in translation work, a certificate from the employer.
  • Caste Certificate: For reserved categories, a certificate issued by the competent authority.
  • Domicile Certificate: Domicile certificate of the State of Maharashtra.
  • Small Family Declaration: As per 'Form-A' given in the advertisement.
  • Character Certificate: Original character certificates issued by two respectable persons (issued after the date of advertisement, 26/11/2025) - 'Form-B'.
  • NOC: No Objection Certificate for government employees.
  • Identity Proof: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, or Driving License.

Preparation Tips

A strong command of languages is essential to succeed in this examination.

  • Grammar: English grammar carries 30 marks. Focus on Tenses, Voice, Narration, Articles, and Vocabulary.
  • Translation: This is the most important part (40 marks). Learn the translation of Legal Terminology from English to Marathi. Practice translating editorials from English newspapers into Marathi daily.
  • Essay and Letter: Practice writing essays on current social and legal issues. Understand the format of formal letter writing.
  • Time Management: 3 hours is sufficient time, but translation can be time-consuming. Therefore, improve your writing speed.

Important Links

Details Link
Official Notification (PDF) Click Here
Apply Online Click Here
SBI Collect (Fee Payment) Click Here

FAQs – Frequently Asked Questions

Question 1: Can candidates from other states apply?
No, the advertisement clearly mentions that the candidate must be a domicile of Maharashtra.
Question 2: Is a Law Degree mandatory?
No, a Law Degree is not mandatory, but candidates who possess a law degree will be given preference in the selection. The essential qualification is a Bachelor's Degree in Languages (English and Marathi).
Question 3: Can final-year students apply?
No, the candidate must fulfill all eligibility conditions and possess the degree as of the date of publication of the advertisement (26/11/2025).
Question 4: What will be the medium of the examination?
Since it is a post for a translator, the examination will be in both English and Marathi.
Question 5: When should the Character Certificate be issued?
The character certificate should be issued after the date of publication of the advertisement (26/11/2025) and must be presented at the time of the interview.

entry.title: GSSSB Royalty Inspector Recruitment 2025: 29 Posts in Industries & Mines Department, Apply Online
entry.content:
GSSSB Royalty Inspector Recruitment 2025: 29 Posts in Industries & Mines Department, Apply Online

A golden opportunity for Geology and Mining Engineering graduates to apply for the post of Royalty Inspector, Class-III in Gujarat. Online applications from November 22 to December 9, 2025.

Post Name: Royalty Inspector, Class-III
Total Posts: 29
Last Date: 09/12/2025
Application Mode: Online

The Gujarat Subordinate Services Selection Board (GSSSB), Gandhinagar, has issued a comprehensive advertisement (Advt. No. 369/202526) for direct recruitment to the prestigious posts of Royalty Inspector, Class-III, in the Directorate of Geology and Mining, under the Industries and Mines Department of the Government of Gujarat. This recruitment drive aims to fill a total of 29 vacancies. This presents a golden opportunity for qualified and talented candidates holding higher educational qualifications in Geology or Mining Engineering who are seeking a stable and respected government job in Gujarat.

The Royalty Inspector plays a vital role in the management of the state's mineral resources and ensuring the proper collection of royalties for the government. GSSSB, known for its transparent and fair selection processes, will conduct a competitive examination for this recruitment. The selection process will involve a written examination followed by document verification. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the GSSSB Online Job Application System (OJAS) portal from November 25, 2025, to December 9, 2025. This article provides detailed information on all essential aspects of this recruitment, including post-wise vacancies, pay scale, eligibility criteria, age limit, selection process, and how to apply.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise overview of the key details regarding this recruitment drive.

Particulars Information
Organization Name Gujarat Subordinate Services Selection Board (GSSSB)
Advertisement Number 369/202526
Post Name Royalty Inspector, Class-III
Total Posts 29
Job Location Gujarat
Application Mode Online (through OJAS Portal)
Application Dates November 25, 2025, to December 9, 2025

Vacancy Details and Category Distribution

GSSSB has announced a total of 29 vacancies for the post of Royalty Inspector, Class-3. These vacancies are distributed across various categories as per the reservation rules of the Government of Gujarat. It is important to note that no vacancies are reserved for Scheduled Caste (SC) candidates in this particular advertisement. For Persons with Disabilities (PwD), a separate advertisement (No: 353/202526) has been issued, hence no posts are reserved for them here.

Reservation Category Number of Posts Reserved for Women Candidates
Unreserved (UR) 12 04
Economically Weaker Section (EWS) 02 00
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 08 02
Socially and Educationally Backward Class (SEBC) 07 02
Scheduled Caste (SC) 00 00
Total 29 08
Reserved for Ex-Servicemen (ESM) 02

Salary and Pay Scale

Selected candidates will be appointed on a fixed pay basis for the first five years, as per the rules of the Government of Gujarat, Finance Department. After completing five years of satisfactory service, they will be placed in the regular pay scale as per the 7th Pay Commission.

Post Name Fixed Pay for First 5 Years (per month) Regular Pay Scale (after 5 years)
Royalty Inspector, Class-III ₹ 49,600/- ₹ 39,900 - ₹ 1,26,600/- (Pay Matrix Level-7 as per 7th CPC)

In addition to the fixed pay, candidates may be eligible for admissible allowances as per government rules. After regularization, the salary will be as per the revised pay matrix, including DA, HRA, etc.

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Candidates applying for the post of Royalty Inspector must meet the specific educational and age criteria. The eligibility will be determined as of the last date of application, i.e., December 9, 2025.

Educational Qualification

Sr. No. Type of Qualification Details
1 Essential Qualification
  1. A postgraduate degree with a minimum of 55% marks in Geology or Applied Geology; OR
  2. A graduate degree with a minimum of 55% marks in Mining Engineering.
  3. The degree must be obtained from a university established or incorporated by or under a Central or State Act or an institution of higher learning recognized as a university under section 3 of UGC Act, 1956.
2 Computer Knowledge Basic knowledge of computer applications as per Gujarat Civil Services Classification and Recruitment (General) Rules, 1967.
3 Language Proficiency Adequate knowledge of Gujarati or Hindi or both languages.

Age Limit (as on 01.01.2025)

The age of candidates will be calculated as of January 1, 2025. The upper age limit for the General category is 37 years. Candidates from reserved categories will receive relaxation in the upper age limit as per Government of Gujarat rules.

Category Minimum Age Maximum Age Age Relaxation
Unreserved Male 18 years 37 years -
Unreserved Female 18 years 42 years 5 years
SEBC, ST, EWS Male 18 years 42 years 5 years
SEBC, ST, EWS Female 18 years 45 years 10 years (5 years general + 5 years category relaxation, up to the maximum limit of 45 years)
Ex-Servicemen 18 years - Service period in defence + 3 years
Note: In any case, the maximum age limit after all relaxations will not exceed 45 years.

Application Fee

Candidates are required to pay the application fee through online mode. In a significant move benefiting candidates, GSSSB will refund the examination fee to all candidates who appear for the examination.

Category Application Fee
General / EWS / SEBC ₹ 500/- + applicable service charges
SC, ST, all Female candidates, and Ex-Servicemen ₹ 400/- + applicable service charges

Note: The fee paid is non-refundable. Candidates are advised to verify their eligibility before making the payment.

Selection Process

The selection process for the Royalty Inspector post will be based on a single-stage competitive written examination. The final selection will be made based on the merit obtained in this examination.

  1. Competitive Written Examination:
    • The examination will be offline (OMR based) and objective type.
    • The exam will consist of 210 questions, with a total of 210 marks.
    • The duration of the examination will be 3 hours (180 minutes).
    • The question paper will be bilingual (Gujarati and English).
    • Negative Marking: There will be a negative marking of 0.25 marks for each incorrect answer.
    • Minimum Qualifying Marks: Candidates must secure at least 40% marks in Part-A and 40% marks in Part-B separately to be considered for the final merit list. For reserved categories (SC/ST/SEBC/EWS/PH/ESM), the qualifying marks are 35% in each part.
  2. Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates (approximately twice the number of vacancies) will be called for document verification based on their performance in the written examination.
  3. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the written examination, after qualifying the minimum passing marks in each section.

Examination Pattern

Part Subject Questions Marks
Part-A Reasoning and Data Interpretation 30 30
Quantitative Aptitude 30 30
Part-A Total 60 60
Part-B General Knowledge and Current Affairs 30 30
Gujarati and English Comprehension 30 30
Subject related to Educational Qualification (Geology/Mining Engineering) 120 120
Part-B Total 180 180
Grand Total 240 240
Note: The total duration for the exam is 3 hours (180 minutes).

How to Prepare for the GSSSB Royalty Inspector Exam

A strategic preparation plan is crucial for success in the GSSSB Royalty Inspector exam. The syllabus has two parts, so balancing your preparation is key.

  1. Understand the Syllabus: The first step is to thoroughly grasp the syllabus for both Part-A (Aptitude) and Part-B (Technical and General Knowledge).
  2. Part-A Preparation:
    • Reasoning & Quantitative Aptitude: Practice regularly from standard books. Focus on topics like analogies, series, coding-decoding, data interpretation, percentages, and basic arithmetic.
    • General Knowledge & Current Affairs: Stay updated with national and international current events, especially those related to Gujarat's economy, mining, and environmental regulations.
    • Gujarati and English Comprehension: Practice reading comprehension passages and focus on improving vocabulary and grammar.
  3. Part-B Preparation:
    • Technical Subject (Geology/Mining): This section carries the highest weightage (120 marks). Focus on core subjects like Mineralogy, Petrology, Structural Geology, Economic Geology, Mining Engineering principles, Geology of Gujarat, Mineral Resources of India, relevant acts and rules (like Mines and Minerals (Development and Regulation) Act, Mineral Conservation Rules, etc.).
    • General Knowledge (Gujarat Specific): Study the geography, history, economy, and administrative structure of Gujarat.
  4. Mock Tests and Previous Papers: Solve mock tests and previous years' GSSSB question papers to understand the exam pattern, difficulty level, and time management.

Best Books for Preparation

Here are some recommended books to aid your preparation:

  • General Aptitude & Reasoning: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal, "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General Knowledge (India): "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • General Knowledge (Gujarat): "Know Your State - Gujarat" by Arihant Publications or a similar book focused on Gujarat.
  • Technical Subjects (Geology/Mining): Standard textbooks for B.Sc. Geology and Mining Engineering, relevant government reports and acts (MMDR Act, 1957; Mineral Conservation Rules).

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online. Candidates must apply through the OJAS portal by following the steps below:

  1. First, visit the official OJAS website: https://ojas.gujarat.gov.in.
  2. Click on "Online Application" and then select "Apply".
  3. Choose GSSSB from the department list and find the advertisement number 369/202526 for Royalty Inspector. Click "Apply".
  4. If you are a new user, complete the "New Registration" process. Existing users can log in directly.
  5. Fill out the application form with all required personal and educational details.
  6. Upload your recent passport-size photograph and signature in the prescribed format.
  7. Review all the entered details carefully before confirming your application.
  8. Pay the application fee online through the available payment gateway.
  9. Take a printout of the final submitted application form and the fee payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Needed for Application and Verification

Candidates should keep the following documents scanned and ready for uploading during the online application and for verification at a later stage:

  1. Recent passport-size photograph.
  2. Signature.
  3. 10th Class marksheet (for date of birth proof).
  4. All semesters/years marksheets and degree certificates for Graduation/Post-Graduation in Geology or Mining Engineering.
  5. Category certificate (ST/SEBC/EWS) if applicable, issued by the competent authority.
  6. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, PAN Card, etc.).

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (From 25.11.2025)
GSSSB Official Website Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the GSSSB Royalty Inspector recruitment?

The last date to apply online is December 9, 2025.

Question 2: What is the minimum educational qualification required?

Candidates must have a postgraduate degree with at least 55% marks in Geology or Applied Geology, or a graduate degree in Mining Engineering with at least 55% marks.

Question 3: Is there negative marking in the exam?

Yes, there will be negative marking of 0.25 marks for each incorrect answer.

Question 4: Will the application fee be refunded?

Yes, the application fee paid by candidates who appear for the examination will be refunded.

Question 5: Are candidates from outside Gujarat eligible to apply?

Yes, candidates from outside Gujarat can apply, but they will be considered under the Unreserved (General) category and will not be eligible for reservation benefits.

Disclaimer: The information provided in this article is based on the advertisement issued by the Gujarat Subordinate Services Selection Board (GSSSB) No. 369/202526. Candidates are advised to refer to the official notification for complete and accurate details before applying. In case of any discrepancy, the information in the official notification shall prevail.

entry.title: DMRC भर्ती 2025: मुंबई मेट्रो के लिए सुपरवाइजर और टेक्नीशियन की बंपर भर्ती!
entry.content:
DMRC भर्ती 2025: मुंबई मेट्रो के लिए सुपरवाइजर और टेक्नीशियन की बंपर भर्ती!

दिल्ली मेट्रो रेल कॉर्पोरेशन (DMRC) ने अनुभवी प्रोफेशनल्स के लिए मुंबई मेट्रो लाइन-3 में नॉन-एक्जीक्यूटिव पदों पर फिक्स्ड टर्म कॉन्ट्रैक्ट आधार पर आवेदन आमंत्रित किए हैं।

दिल्ली मेट्रो रेल कॉर्पोरेशन (DMRC), जिसे “दिल्ली की लाइफलाइन” कहा जाता है, ने अनुभवी उम्मीदवारों के लिए शानदार अवसर प्रदान किया है। DMRC ने मुंबई मेट्रो लाइन-3 (Aqua Line) के संचालन और रखरखाव (O&M) कार्यों के लिए नॉन-एक्जीक्यूटिव पदों पर भर्ती हेतु अधिसूचना (ADVT No. DMRC/PERS/22/HR/2025 (212)) जारी की है।

यह नियुक्ति Fixed Term Contractual Engagement (FTCE) के आधार पर 3 वर्षों के लिए होगी। आवेदन केवल ऑफलाइन माध्यम से Speed Post द्वारा भेजे जाने हैं।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
विज्ञापन जारी होने की तिथि17 अक्टूबर 2025
आवेदन प्राप्त करने की अंतिम तिथि31 अक्टूबर 2025

रिक्ति और वेतन विवरण

इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत कुल 18 पदों पर भर्ती की जाएगी।

पद का नामविभागकुल पदमासिक वेतन (CTC)
सिस्टम सुपरवाइजर / सीनियर सुपरवाइजरE&M, PST, Telecom, Civil, Finance16₹65,000/-
सिस्टम टेक्नीशियनE&M02₹46,000/-
नोट: पदों की संख्या अस्थायी है और इनमें बदलाव संभव है। आरक्षण भारत सरकार के नियमों के अनुसार लागू होगा।

आयु सीमा (01.10.2025 तक)

  • सिस्टम सुपरवाइजर / सीनियर सुपरवाइजर: 18 से 40 वर्ष।
  • सिस्टम टेक्नीशियन: 18 से 35 वर्ष।
  • आरक्षित वर्ग को नियमानुसार आयु में छूट दी जाएगी।

पात्रता मानदंड

पदशैक्षणिक योग्यता एवं अनुभव
सीनियर सुपरवाइजर (Finance)CA Inter / ICWAI Inter के साथ Accounts या Finance में कम से कम 4 वर्ष का अनुभव। न्यूनतम वार्षिक CTC ₹5 लाख। SAP एवं GST का ज्ञान वांछनीय।
सिस्टम सुपरवाइजरसंबंधित ट्रेड में 3 वर्षीय इंजीनियरिंग डिप्लोमा या 4 वर्षीय B.E./B.Tech. डिग्री। कुल 4 वर्ष का अनुभव, जिसमें 2 वर्ष मेट्रो या रेलवे में होना चाहिए। न्यूनतम वार्षिक CTC ₹5 लाख।
सिस्टम टेक्नीशियनमैट्रिक/12वीं पास के साथ Electrician ट्रेड में ITI (NCVT/SCVT)। कुल 4 वर्ष का अनुभव, जिसमें 2 वर्ष मेट्रो या रेलवे में आवश्यक। न्यूनतम वार्षिक CTC ₹3 लाख।
प्राथमिकता: मुंबई या महाराष्ट्र क्षेत्र में कार्य का अनुभव रखने वाले उम्मीदवारों को वरीयता दी जाएगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  1. स्क्रीनिंग: प्राप्त आवेदनों की जांच की जाएगी और योग्य उम्मीदवारों को शॉर्टलिस्ट किया जाएगा।
  2. मेडिकल फिटनेस परीक्षा: चयनित उम्मीदवारों को DMRC द्वारा निर्धारित मेडिकल मानकों को पूरा करना होगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

यह भर्ती प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह ऑफलाइन है। आवेदन करने के चरण नीचे दिए गए हैं:

  1. DMRC की आधिकारिक वेबसाइट से Annexure-I Application Format डाउनलोड करें।
  2. फॉर्म को कैपिटल लेटर्स में साफ-सुथरे तरीके से भरें।
  3. हाल ही का पासपोर्ट साइज फोटो चिपकाएँ और सभी आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ों (शिक्षा, अनुभव, NOC, आदि) की स्वयं-सत्यापित प्रतियाँ संलग्न करें।
  4. आवेदन पत्र को एक लिफाफे में डालें और ऊपर बड़े अक्षरों में “Name of Post…” लिखें।
  5. Speed Post के माध्यम से नीचे दिए पते पर भेजें:

Executive Director (HR),
Delhi Metro Rail Corporation Ltd.,
Metro Bhawan, Fire Brigade Lane,
Barakhamba Road, New Delhi – 110001

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
आधिकारिक DMRC वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
विज्ञापन और आवेदन फॉर्म“Careers” सेक्शन देखें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्र.1: क्या फ्रेशर आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
नहीं, यह भर्ती केवल अनुभवी उम्मीदवारों के लिए है।
प्र.2: आवेदन प्रक्रिया क्या है?
आवेदन केवल ऑफलाइन माध्यम से Speed Post द्वारा भेजा जाएगा।
प्र.3: जॉब लोकेशन क्या होगी?
प्रारंभिक नियुक्ति मुंबई में होगी, परंतु DMRC के किसी भी प्रोजेक्ट (भारत या विदेश) में स्थानांतरण संभव है।
प्र.4: क्या यह स्थायी नौकरी है?
नहीं, यह 3 वर्ष की Fixed Term Contract (FTCE) नौकरी है जिसे प्रदर्शन और आवश्यकता के अनुसार बढ़ाया जा सकता है।

entry.title: PSSSB Group D Recruitment 2025: Bumper Recruitment for 331 Posts of Peon, Chowkidar, Sweeper & More
entry.content:
PSSSB Group D Recruitment 2025: Bumper Recruitment for 331 Posts of Peon, Chowkidar, Sweeper & More

A golden opportunity for 10th-pass candidates to secure a stable government job in Punjab. Online applications are open from November 21 to December 15, 2025.

Post Name: Peon, Chowkidar, Sweeper, Fisherman, Mali, Lab Attendant, and other Group-D Posts
Last Date: 15/12/2025
Total Posts: 331

The Punjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB) has released a large and significant recruitment notification (Advertisement No. 08/2025) for the direct recruitment of Group-D posts in various departments of the Punjab Government. This is a golden opportunity for candidates who have passed the 10th standard and are seeking a stable government job in Punjab. Through this recruitment drive, a total of 331 vacancies will be filled, including diverse roles such as Peon, Chowkidar, Sweeper, Mali, Fisherman, and Lab Attendant.

This recruitment is being conducted to fill vacancies in several key departments of the Punjab Government, such as the General Administration Department, Dairy Development Department, Fisheries Department, and the Deputy Commissioner's office in Mansa. PSSSB, known for its transparent and fair selection processes, will conduct a competitive written examination for this recruitment. This is an excellent pathway for the youth of Punjab to make a significant contribution to the state's public service. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the official PSSSB website, https://sssb.punjab.gov.in, from November 21, 2025, to December 15, 2025.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise summary of the key details regarding this Group-D recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Organization NamePunjab Subordinate Services Selection Board (PSSSB)
Advertisement Number08/2025
Post NamePeon, Chowkidar, Sweeper, Fisherman, Mali, and other Group-D posts
Total Posts331
Job LocationPunjab
Application ModeOnline
Application DatesNovember 21, 2025, to December 15, 2025

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

PSSSB has advertised a total of 331 Group-D posts across various departments. These vacancies are classified into different posts and departments. A detailed post-wise breakdown of vacancies is provided below:

Sr. No. Post Name Department Number of Posts
1 Peon General Administration Department 150
2 Peon Dairy Development Department 22
3 Peon Deputy Commissioner's Office, Mansa 18
4 Peon Chief Auditor 15
5 Peon Director, Fisheries 11
6 Chowkidar General Administration Department 22
7 Chowkidar Director, Fisheries 05
8 Sweeper-cum-Chowkidar Dairy Development Department 04
9 Sweeper-cum-Chowkidar Director, Fisheries 06
10 Safai Sewak Deputy Commissioner's Office, Mansa 05
11 Sweeper-cum-Mali Deputy Commissioner's Office, Mansa 01
12 Fisherman Director, Fisheries 68
13 Mali-cum-Chowkidar Director, Fisheries 02
14 Boatman Director, Fisheries 01
15 Lab Attendant Director, Fisheries 01
Grand Total 331
Note: The detailed category-wise classification of these vacancies (General, SC, BC, ESM, PwD, EWS, etc.) is available in the official notification. Candidates are advised to check the notification for the exact number of posts in their respective categories.

Salary and Pay Scale

Candidates selected for these Group-D posts will be paid in Level-1 of the Pay Matrix as per the recommendations of the 7th Central Pay Commission (7th CPC). This is an entry-level pay scale which will also include Dearness Allowance (DA) and other allowances as applicable by the government from time to time.

Post Name Pay Matrix Level Pay Scale
All Group-D Posts (Peon, Chowkidar, etc.) Level-1 (7th CPC) ₹ 18,000 - ₹ 56,900/-

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

A uniform minimum educational qualification has been set for all these posts, in accordance with the Punjab State (Group-D) Service Rules, 1963.

Educational Qualification

Post Name Minimum Educational Qualification
All Group-D Posts
  • The candidate must have passed the Matriculation (10th Class) examination with Punjabi as one of the subjects from a recognized educational institution.
  • Additionally, for direct recruitment, the candidate will also have to pass a qualifying test of Punjabi language, equivalent to the Matriculation standard, with at least 50% marks, which will be conducted by PSSSB.
Note: The certificate of educational qualification must have been issued on or before the last date of application.

Age Limit (as on January 1, 2025)

The minimum age for candidates is 18 years and the maximum age is 37 years for the General category. Candidates from reserved categories will be given relaxation in the upper age limit as per the rules of the Government of Punjab.

Category Maximum Age Limit
General Category 37 years
Scheduled Caste (SC) and Backward Class (BC) of Punjab 42 years
State and Central Government Employees 45 years
Persons with Disability (PwD) of Punjab 47 years
Widows and Divorced Women 40 years
Ex-Servicemen of Punjab As per Punjab Recruitment Rules, 1982 (after deducting the period of military service from the actual age, the resultant age should not exceed the upper age limit by more than 3 years).

Selection Process

The selection process for these Group-D posts is straightforward and transparent, based on an objective type written examination. The main stages of selection are as follows:

  1. Written Examination: An objective type (Multiple Choice Questions) written examination will be conducted for all eligible candidates. The detailed syllabus and pattern of this examination will be uploaded on the Board's website later.
  2. Qualifying Test of Punjabi Language: Along with the written exam, a mandatory Punjabi language test will also be held. This test will be of Matriculation standard, and it is compulsory to score a minimum of 50% marks to qualify. Candidates who fail this test will be disqualified from the recruitment process, irrespective of their score in the main written exam.
  3. Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based only on the marks obtained in the main written examination. In case of a tie where two or more candidates have the same marks, the older candidate will be given preference. If the age is also the same, preference will be decided based on the percentage of marks obtained in Matriculation.
  4. Document Verification (Counseling): Candidates will be called for document verification (counseling) based on their position in the merit list. In this stage, candidates will have to present all their original academic, reservation, and other certificates.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus (Expected)

While the detailed syllabus will be released later by PSSSB, the exam pattern for Group-D posts generally follows a standard structure. The exam will consist of two parts, both of which are mandatory.

Written Examination Pattern

Part Subject Expected Questions Nature
Part-A Punjabi Language (10th Standard) 50 Qualifying (50% marks needed)
Part-B General Knowledge, Current Affairs, English, Punjabi, Logical Reasoning & Mental Ability 100 Merit-Based

Expected Syllabus
