  • Part-A (Punjabi Qualifying Test): This will test basic proficiency in the Punjabi language up to the 10th-grade level. The syllabus will include Grammar (ਵਿਆਕਰਨ), Vocabulary (ਸ਼ਬਦਾਵਲੀ), Sentence Correction (ਵਾਕ ਸੁਧਾਈ), Synonyms/Antonyms (ਸਮਾਨਾਰਥਕ/ਵਿਰੋਧਾਰਥਕ ਸ਼ਬਦ), and Reading Comprehension.
  • Part-B (Main Written Test):
    • General Knowledge & Current Affairs: This section will focus on the History, Geography, Culture, and Polity of Punjab and India. Questions on national and international current events, sports, awards, and important dates will also be included.
    • English Language: Basic grammar, vocabulary, synonyms, antonyms, sentence completion, and error detection.
    • Punjabi Language: This section will test knowledge beyond the qualifying paper, including idioms, proverbs, and literature.
    • Logical Reasoning & Mental Ability: This section will include questions on topics like number and alphabet series, coding-decoding, blood relations, direction sense, and basic puzzles.
    • Basic Arithmetic: Questions will be from topics like Percentage, Profit & Loss, Simple Interest, Average, and Ratio & Proportion, all at the 10th-grade level.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for these Group-D posts. The selection is based entirely on merit in the written examination.

How to Prepare for the PSSSB Group-D Exam

Although the detailed syllabus will be released later, a general preparation strategy for Group-D level exams is as follows:

  1. Focus on the Punjabi Language: The qualifying test is mandatory, so practice Punjabi grammar, vocabulary, and reading comprehension thoroughly. Read 10th-grade level Punjabi books.
  2. General Knowledge: Pay attention to current affairs related to Punjab and India, as well as the history, geography, and culture of Punjab.
  3. Basic Arithmetic: Practice math topics from the 10th-grade level, such as percentage, profit and loss, average, and time and work.
  4. Mental Ability (Reasoning): Practice topics like logical reasoning, series completion, and coding-decoding.
  5. Previous Year Papers: Solve question papers from previous Group-D or Clerk level exams conducted by PSSSB to get an idea of the exam's pattern and difficulty level.
  6. Regular Practice: Take regular mock tests to improve your speed and accuracy.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books to aid your preparation:

  • Punjab GK: "Know Your State - Punjab" by Arihant Publications or "Sadda Punjab" by Munish Jindal.
  • General Knowledge (India): "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • Logical Reasoning: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Punjabi Language: Standard Punjab School Education Board (PSEB) grammar books for Class 10.

How to Apply Online

The application process is completely online. Candidates must apply through the official PSSSB website by following the steps below:

  1. First, visit the official PSSSB website: https://sssb.punjab.gov.in.
  2. On the homepage, click on the "Online Applications" link.
  3. Click on the relevant link for Advertisement No. 08/2025.
  4. If you are applying for the first time, click on "New Registration" and complete the registration by filling in your basic details. You will receive a username and password.
  5. Log in with your username and password.
  6. Fill in the application form carefully with your personal details, educational qualifications, and other information.
  7. Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, and other required documents (like the matriculation certificate).
  8. After submitting the application form, pay the application fee as per your category through the online payment gateway. The fee payment link will be activated one day after submitting the form.
  9. Keep a printout of the final submitted application form and the fee receipt for future reference.

Note: If you wish to apply for more than one post, you must fill out a separate online form and pay a separate fee for each post.

Documents Needed to Apply

The following documents will be required during the online application and at the time of counseling:

  1. Scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Matriculation (10th) certificate for proof of date of birth.
  3. Proof of passing Matriculation with Punjabi as a subject.
  4. Domicile Certificate of Punjab (not older than 5 years) to claim reservation benefits.
  5. Reservation category certificate (SC/BC/EWS/ESM/PwD, etc.) issued by the competent authority.
  6. Identity Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).
  7. No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the employer for government employees.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Download Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online Click Here (From 21.11.2025)
Official Website https://sssb.punjab.gov.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the PSSSB Group D recruitment?

The last date to apply online is December 15, 2025 (up to 5:00 PM), and the last date for fee payment is December 17, 2025.

Question 2: What is the minimum educational qualification for these posts?

The candidate must have passed Matriculation (10th Class) with Punjabi as a subject.

Question 3: Can candidates from outside Punjab apply?

Yes, any citizen of India can apply, but the benefits of reservation are only available to residents (Domicile) of Punjab.

Question 4: What is the selection process?

The selection process is based on a written competitive examination, which also includes a mandatory qualifying test in the Punjabi language.

Question 5: Can I apply for more than one post?

Yes, you can apply for more than one post, but you will have to fill out a separate online form and pay a separate fee for each post.

entry.title: DSHM Delhi Recruitment 2026 for 200 Pharmacist Posts
entry.content:
DSHM Delhi Recruitment 2026 for 200 Pharmacist Posts

Advertisement No. 02-2026: The Delhi State Health Mission (DSHM) has invited online registrations for the interview-based engagement of 200 Pharmacists on a purely contractual basis. These positions are under the Pradhan Mantri Ayushman Bharat Health Infrastructure Mission (PM-ABHIM). The online registration window is open from January 24, 2026, to February 07, 2026.

Post Name: Pharmacist
Last Date: 07 February 2026
Total Posts: 200

The Delhi State Health Mission (DSHM) has released a significant notification for the recruitment of 200 Pharmacists to be engaged on a contractual basis. This drive is part of the Pradhan Mantri Ayushman Bharat Health Infrastructure Mission (PM-ABHIM), aimed at strengthening public health infrastructure in the national capital. The selected candidates will play a crucial role in the functioning of Polyclinics and Aam Aadmi Mohalla Clinics.

Unlike a traditional written exam process, the selection for these posts will be made directly through an interview. However, all interested and eligible candidates must first register themselves online through the official DSHM portal. This is a major opportunity for individuals with a pharmacy qualification to serve in the public health sector of Delhi. The online registration is mandatory to be considered for the interview process.

DSHM Pharmacist Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must take note of the online registration dates. Only candidates who register online will be considered for the interview.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Delhi State Health Mission (DSHM), Govt. of NCT of Delhi
Advertisement Number 02-2026
Post Name Pharmacist
Total Vacancies 200
Online Registration Start Date 24 January 2026
Online Registration Last Date 07 February 2026 (up to 5:00 PM)
Application Mode Online Registration for Interview
Job Type Contractual Government Job
Job Location Delhi
Official Website dshm.delhi.gov.in

Vacancy and Remuneration Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 200 vacancies for the post of Pharmacist with a fixed monthly remuneration.

Post Name UR OBC SC ST EWS Grand Total Monthly Remuneration (Expected)
Pharmacist 38 69 41 32 20 200 ₹25,000/- (Consolidated)
Note: The remuneration is based on a similar previous notification and is subject to confirmation in the detailed advertisement for this post. The engagement is purely contractual.

Application Fee

  • The summary notification does not mention any application fee. Candidates are advised to check the official portal while registering. The process is expected to be free of cost.

Eligibility Criteria (Expected)

The summary notification does not detail the eligibility criteria. Based on the standard requirements for this post in DSHM, the following qualifications are expected. Candidates must verify these from the official website.

Criteria Expected Eligibility Details
Educational Qualification A candidate must possess one of the following qualifications:
  1. B. Pharma from a recognized institute.
    OR
  2. Diploma in Pharmacy (D. Pharma) from a recognized institute.
Registration: Must be registered as a Pharmacist with the Delhi Pharmacy Council.
Age Limit
  • Maximum Age: 30 Years (Expected).
Age relaxation for reserved categories will be applicable as per government rules.

Selection Process

The selection process is straightforward and does not involve a written examination.

  1. Online Registration: All interested and eligible candidates must first register themselves on the official DSHM portal by the deadline of February 07, 2026.
  2. Document Verification: Candidates will be called for document verification based on their online applications. The schedule and venue for this will be communicated later.
  3. Interview: Candidates whose documents are successfully verified will appear for a personal interview.
  4. Final Selection: The final selection will be based on the candidate's performance in the interview.

Exam Pattern

There is no written examination for this recruitment. The selection is based on document verification and a personal interview. The interview will likely assess the candidate's domain knowledge in pharmacy, communication skills, and general suitability for working in a public health setting.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) required for the post of Pharmacist.

How to Prepare for the Interview

Since the interview is the main selection stage, thorough preparation is crucial.

  • Revise Core Pharmacy Subjects: Brush up on key topics from your D.Pharma/B.Pharma curriculum, such as pharmacology, pharmaceutics, and dispensing practices.
  • Know About Public Health Schemes: Be aware of flagship health programs, especially PM-ABHIM and the Aam Aadmi Mohalla Clinic model in Delhi.
  • Prepare for Common Questions: Be ready to answer questions about your qualifications, your interest in public health, and situational questions.
  • Organize Your Documents: Keep all your original documents and their self-attested photocopies ready for the document verification stage.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no written test, the focus should be on interview preparation. Candidates can refer to:

  • Standard D.Pharma and B.Pharma textbooks for revising core concepts.
  • "A Textbook of Pharmacology" by P. Jagadish Prasad.
  • Websites of the National Health Mission (NHM) and Delhi State Health Mission (DSHM) to understand their functioning.

How to Apply Online

The application process is online registration for the interview. Follow these steps:

  1. Visit the official DSHM registration link provided in the notification.
  2. Fill out the online registration form with all your correct personal, educational, and contact details.
  3. Upload any required documents (like photograph or signature) as per the instructions on the portal.
  4. Review the filled-in details carefully before final submission.
  5. Submit the form before the deadline of 05:00 PM on February 07, 2026.
  6. Save a copy or take a printout of the final submitted registration form for your records.

Documents Needed for Registration & Verification

While registering online, you will need your personal and academic details. For the document verification stage, you should keep the following ready:

  1. Printout of the Online Registration Form.
  2. Proof of Date of Birth (10th Class Certificate).
  3. B.Pharma/D.Pharma Degree/Diploma and all Mark Sheets (Originals and Photocopies).
  4. Valid Registration Certificate from the Delhi Pharmacy Council.
  5. Caste/Category Certificate (OBC/SC/ST/EWS), if applicable. The OBC certificate must be issued in Delhi.
  6. Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhaar Card, Voter ID, etc.).
  7. Recent passport-size photographs.

Important Links

Action Link
Online Registration for Interview Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there a written exam for the DSHM Pharmacist post?

No, the selection is based on document verification and a personal interview. There is no written examination.

Q2: What is the last date to register online?

The last date for online registration is February 07, 2026, up to 5:00 PM.

Q3: I am registered with the pharmacy council of another state. Am I eligible?

The eligibility criteria typically require registration with the Delhi Pharmacy Council. You must confirm this from the detailed advertisement on the official website.

Q4: Is this a permanent government job?

No, this is a purely contractual engagement under the PM-ABHIM scheme.

entry.title: Bihar Police Havildar Clerk Recruitment 2026
entry.content:
Bihar Police Havildar Clerk Recruitment 2026

Advertisement No. 01/2026: Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission (BPSSC) invites online applications for 64 vacancies of Adhinayak Lipik (Clerk) in the Bihar Home Guards service. A great opportunity for 12th pass candidates. The application window is open from January 2, 2026, to February 2, 2026. Get all details on eligibility, selection process, physical standards, and how to apply here.

Post Name: Adhinayak Lipik (अधिनायक लिपिक) / Clerk
Last Date: 02 February 2026
Total Posts: 64

The Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission (BPSSC) has released a significant recruitment notification for the post of Adhinayak Lipik (Clerk) within the Bihar Home Guards service cadre. This recruitment, announced under Advertisement No. 01/2026, aims to fill 64 vacancies through a competitive selection process. This presents a valuable opportunity for eligible Indian citizens, including men, women, and third-gender individuals, who are seeking a stable and respectable government job in Bihar.

The position of Adhinayak Lipik is a crucial administrative role in the Home Department (Special Branch) and offers a Pay Level-4 salary. The selection procedure is a two-tier process involving a written examination and a Physical Efficiency Test (PET), both of which are designed to select the most capable candidates. The online application portal will be active from January 2, 2026, and will close on February 2, 2026. Aspiring candidates who have passed the 12th (Intermediate) examination are encouraged to apply. This article provides a comprehensive overview of the entire recruitment process, including eligibility criteria, selection methodology, exam patterns, physical standards, and a step-by-step guide to filling the online application.

BPSSC Adhinayak Lipik Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully note the key details and important dates to ensure they do not miss this opportunity. This is a state government job in Bihar.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Body Bihar Police Subordinate Services Commission (BPSSC)
Department Bihar Home Guards (Home Department, Special Branch)
Post Name Adhinayak Lipik (Clerk)
Advertisement Number 01/2026
Total Vacancies 64
Application Start Date 02 January 2026
Last Date to Apply Online 02 February 2026
Pay Scale Level-4
Mode of Application Online
Job Type State Government Job
Job Location Bihar
Official Website https://bpssc.bihar.gov.in

Vacancy Details

A total of 64 posts are available for the position of Adhinayak Lipik. The category-wise distribution, including the 35% horizontal reservation for women, is as follows:

Category Total Vacancies Vacancies Reserved for Women (35%)
Unreserved (UR) 26 09
Scheduled Caste (SC) 10 04
Scheduled Tribe (ST) 01 -
Extremely Backward Class (EBC) 11 04
Backward Class (BC) 08 03
Economically Weaker Section (EWS) 06 02
Backward Class Women (BC-W) 02 -
Total 64 22
Note: In addition to the above, 2% of the total posts (i.e., 1 post) are horizontally reserved for the grandchildren of Freedom Fighters.

Application Fee

  • For all categories of candidates (Male, Female, Third Gender): ₹100/- (One Hundred Rupees only).

The application fee must be paid online. It is important to note that if a payment is made and the registration is not completed successfully due to any technical issue, the fee will be refunded by the payment gateway within 7 working days. The commission will not accept any other mode of payment.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must meet all the eligibility criteria regarding nationality, education, and age as of the cut-off date, August 1, 2025.

Criteria Requirement
Nationality The candidate must be an Indian citizen.
Educational Qualification The candidate must have passed the Intermediate (10+2) examination or an equivalent qualification from a recognized board by August 1, 2025.
Age Limit (as on 01.08.2025)
  • Unreserved (General) Male: Minimum 18 years, Maximum 37 years.
  • Unreserved (General) Female: Minimum 18 years, Maximum 40 years.
  • Backward Class (BC) & Extremely Backward Class (EBC) Male/Female/Third Gender: Minimum 18 years, Maximum 40 years.
  • Scheduled Caste (SC) & Scheduled Tribe (ST) Male/Female: Minimum 18 years, Maximum 42 years.
  • Government Servants of Bihar: 5 years relaxation in the upper age limit, provided they have completed a minimum of three years of regular service.

Selection Process

The selection for the post of Adhinayak Lipik will be conducted in a two-stage process. The final merit list will be prepared based on the performance in the Physical Efficiency Test.

  1. Written Examination: This is the first stage and is qualifying in nature. Candidates will have to appear for an OMR-based objective-type written test.
  2. Physical Efficiency Test (PET): Candidates who qualify the written exam (a minimum of 30% marks is required) will be shortlisted for the PET. The number of candidates called for PET will be five times the number of vacancies in each category. The PET is the main competitive stage, and the final merit list will be based on the total marks obtained in the three events of the PET.
  3. Document Verification: After the PET, candidates will undergo document verification.
  4. Medical Examination: Final selected candidates will be required to clear a medical examination.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

Candidates must be well-prepared for both the written test and the physical tests. The details of both are provided below.

Written Examination Pattern (Qualifying)

  • Mode: Offline (OMR based)
  • Level: The standard of the exam will be equivalent to the 10th (Matric) level of the Bihar School Examination Board.
  • Total Questions: 100 Objective Type Questions.
  • Total Marks: 100.
  • Duration: 2 Hours (120 Minutes).
  • Marking Scheme: +1 mark for each correct answer. No negative marking is specified.
  • Minimum Qualifying Marks: 30%.

Syllabus for Written Examination

The question paper will cover the following subjects:
  1. Hindi
  2. English
  3. Mathematics
  4. Social Science (History, Geography, Civics, Economics)
  5. Science (Physics, Chemistry, Zoology, Botany)
  6. General Knowledge and Current Affairs

Physical Efficiency Test (PET) Pattern (Total Marks: 100)

The PET is the most crucial stage, as the final merit will be based on the marks obtained here. It consists of three events:

  1. Race (Maximum 50 Marks)
    • For Males: 1.6 km (1 Mile) to be completed within 6 minutes.
      • Up to 5 min: 50 Marks
      • 5 min to 5 min 20 sec: 40 Marks
      • 5 min 20 sec to 5 min 40 sec: 30 Marks
      • 5 min 40 sec to 6 min: 20 Marks
      • More than 6 min: Disqualified
    • For Females: 1 km to be completed within 5 minutes.
      • Up to 4 min: 50 Marks
      • 4 min to 4 min 20 sec: 40 Marks
      • 4 min 20 sec to 4 min 40 sec: 30 Marks
      • 4 min 40 sec to 5 min: 20 Marks
      • More than 5 min: Disqualified
  2. Shot Put (Gola Fek) (Maximum 25 Marks)
    • For Males: 16-pound shot put, minimum 16 feet throw.
      • 16-17 ft: 09 Marks
      • 17-18 ft: 13 Marks
      • 18-19 ft: 17 Marks
      • 19-20 ft: 21 Marks
      • > 20 ft: 25 Marks
      • < 16 ft: Disqualified
    • For Females: 12-pound shot put, minimum 12 feet throw.
      • 12-13 ft: 09 Marks
      • 13-14 ft: 13 Marks
      • 14-15 ft: 17 Marks
      • 15-16 ft: 21 Marks
      • > 16 ft: 25 Marks
      • < 12 ft: Disqualified
  3. High Jump (Maximum 25 Marks)
    • For Males: Minimum 4 feet.
      • 4 ft: 13 Marks
      • 4 ft 4 inches: 17 Marks
      • 4 ft 8 inches: 21 Marks
      • 5 ft: 25 Marks
      • < 4 ft: Disqualified
    • For Females: Minimum 3 feet.
      • 3 ft: 13 Marks
      • 3 ft 4 inches: 17 Marks
      • 3 ft 8 inches: 21 Marks
      • 4 ft: 25 Marks
      • < 3 ft: Disqualified

Physical Standard Test (PST)

Candidates must meet the following minimum physical standards. No marks are awarded for this, but failing to meet these standards will lead to disqualification.

Category Gender Height Chest (for Males only)
Unreserved (UR) & Backward Class (BC) Male 165 cm 81 cm (un-expanded) to 86 cm (expanded)
Female 155 cm Not Applicable
Extremely Backward Class (EBC) Male 160 cm 81 cm (un-expanded) to 86 cm (expanded)
SC & ST Male 160 cm 79 cm (un-expanded) to 84 cm (expanded)

Note: A minimum chest expansion of 5 cm is mandatory for all male candidates. The minimum weight for all female candidates is 48 kg.

How to Prepare

A dual preparation strategy is required, focusing on both the written exam and the physical efficiency test.

  • For the Written Exam: Since it is qualifying, the goal is to secure at least 30%. Focus on building a strong foundation in all subjects up to the 10th standard level. Use textbooks from the Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) for targeted preparation.
  • For the Physical Efficiency Test (PET): This is the most critical part. Start your physical training immediately.
    • Running: Practice running daily to build stamina and speed. Aim to complete the race in the top time bracket to maximize your score.
    • Shot Put: Learn the correct technique for throwing the shot put. Practice regularly to improve your distance.
    • High Jump: Practice different high jump techniques (like the scissor jump or Fosbury flop) to find what works best for you and clear the maximum height.

Best Books for Preparation

For the qualifying written test, standard books for Class 10th level are sufficient.

  • Bihar School Examination Board (BSEB) Textbooks: For classes 9th and 10th for all subjects (Hindi, English, Maths, Science, Social Science).
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's Samanya Gyan" is a comprehensive book for GK and current affairs.
  • Practice Sets: Solve practice sets for Bihar Police Constable exams, as the written test pattern is similar.

How to Apply

The application process is entirely online. Follow these steps to apply successfully:

  1. Visit the official BPSSC website: https://bpssc.bihar.gov.in.
  2. Go to the "Home Guard" tab on the homepage.
  3. Click on the link for "Advt. No.-01/2026 for the post of Adhinayak Lipik".
  4. The application process is in two parts. First, complete the registration by providing your name, nationality, mobile number, email, and other basic details.
  5. Proceed to make the online payment of the application fee (₹100).
  6. After successful payment, you will receive a registration ID and password on your mobile number and email.
  7. Log in again using these credentials to fill the second part of the application form.
  8. Provide your father's/husband's name, mother's name, address, educational qualifications, etc.
  9. Upload a recent color photograph (background should be white, size 15-25 KB) and signatures in both English and Hindi (black or blue ink, size 15-25 KB).
  10. Review your application carefully using the "Edit Details" option before the final submission.
  11. After confirming all details, submit the application form and take a printout of the final acknowledgment slip for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these documents ready before starting the online application:

  1. A valid and active mobile number and email ID.
  2. Scanned copy of a recent color passport-size photograph (15-25 KB, .jpg/.jpeg/.gif format).
  3. Scanned copy of signature in English (15-25 KB).
  4. Scanned copy of signature in Hindi (15-25 KB).
  5. Educational qualification certificates (for filling details).
  6. Domicile certificate and Caste/Category certificate (for candidates from Bihar claiming reservation).

Important Links

Action Link
Apply Online Click Here
Official Notification Click Here
Official Website Click Here

FAQs

Q1: What is the selection process for the Adhinayak Lipik post?

The selection process includes a qualifying written examination, followed by a competitive Physical Efficiency Test (PET). The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the PET.

Q2: What is the last date to apply?

The last date to submit the online application is February 2, 2026.

Q3: Is the written exam score used for the final merit list?

No, the written exam is only for qualifying purposes. The final merit list will be based entirely on the marks scored in the three events of the Physical Efficiency Test (Race, Shot Put, and High Jump).

Q4: Can candidates from other states apply?

Yes, candidates from other states can apply, but they will be treated as Unreserved (General) category candidates and will not be eligible for any reservation benefits.

entry.title: Indian Air Force Medical Assistant Recruitment Rally 2026
entry.content:
Indian Air Force Medical Assistant Recruitment Rally 2026

The Indian Air Force (IAF) is conducting a recruitment rally for unmarried male candidates from select states and UTs to join as Airmen in the Group ‘Y’ (Non-Technical) Medical Assistant trade. The rally will be held from March 28, 2026, to April 05, 2026, at Lal Parade Ground, Bhopal. This is a golden opportunity for candidates with 10+2 (Science with Biology) or a Diploma/B.Sc in Pharmacy to join the IAF.

Post Name: Airmen (Group ‘Y’ Medical Assistant)
Last Date: 05 April 2026
Total Posts: Not specified

The Indian Air Force offers a unique opportunity for young and dynamic male citizens of India and Nepal to serve the nation by joining as Airmen in the highly respected Medical Assistant trade. A recruitment rally is being organized at Lal Parade Ground, Bhopal (Madhya Pradesh) to select candidates from the states of Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Goa, and the UTs of Daman & Diu, Lakshadweep, and Dadra & Nagar Haveli.

Unlike regular online applications, this is a direct recruitment rally where candidates are required to appear at the venue on scheduled dates for a series of tests. The selection process is comprehensive, starting with a Physical Fitness Test (PFT), followed by a Written Test, Adaptability Test-II, and a detailed Medical Examination. The process is designed to select physically and medically fit individuals with the right aptitude to serve in the Indian Air Force.

IAF Recruitment Rally 2026 (Important Points)

This is a direct rally recruitment. Candidates must appear at the venue on the date specified for their respective districts.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Organization Indian Air Force (IAF)
Post Name Airmen (Group ‘Y’ Medical Assistant)
Rally Dates 28 March 2026 to 05 April 2026
Rally Venue Lal Parade Ground, Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh
Application Mode Direct Walk-in at Rally Venue
Job Type Permanent Govt. Job
Job Location Anywhere in India and Abroad
Official Website airmenselection.cdac.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The number of vacancies is not specified and will be filled based on the requirements of the IAF. Selected candidates will be offered an attractive pay and allowances package.

Stage Pay / Stipend
During Training A stipend of ₹14,600/- per month.
On Completion of Training Pay Scale Level-3 (₹26,900/-) + Military Service Pay + Dearness Allowance and other allowances. (Approximate total emoluments ₹40,000/- per month).
Note: In addition to pay, Airmen are entitled to benefits like ration, accommodation, medical facilities, LTC, and more as per service rules.

Application Fee

  • There is NO application fee for participating in the recruitment rally.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility conditions regarding education, age, and marital status.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification A candidate must have one of the following qualifications:
  1. Option A (10+2): Passed 10+2 / Intermediate or equivalent examination with Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and English from a recognized board, with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate and 50% marks in English.
    OR
  2. Option B (Pharmacy): Passed a Diploma or B.Sc. in Pharmacy with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate. Must also have a minimum of 50% marks in English in Diploma, or in 12th/10th if English was not a subject in Diploma. Must be registered with the State Pharmacy Council.
Age Limit
  • For 10+2 Candidates: Must be born between 24 June 2006 and 24 June 2010 (both days inclusive).
  • For Pharmacy (Diploma/B.Sc.) Candidates:
    • Unmarried: Must be born between 24 June 2003 and 24 June 2007.
    • Married: Must be born between 24 June 2003 and 24 June 2005.
Marital Status Unmarried Indian/Nepalese male citizens. (Exception for married Pharmacy candidates within the specified age bracket).

Selection Process

The selection is a rigorous, multi-stage process conducted on the same day or subsequent days at the rally site.

  1. Document Verification: All candidates must first produce original documents for verification to prove their eligibility.
  2. Physical Fitness Test (PFT): Candidates who clear document verification will undergo a PFT, which is qualifying in nature.
  3. Written Test: Candidates who qualify in the PFT will appear for an objective-type written test.
  4. Adaptability Test - II: Those who pass the written test will undergo this test to assess their suitability for the Air Force way of life. This is also qualifying.
  5. Medical Examination: Candidates who clear all the above stages will undergo a detailed medical examination by the IAF Medical Team.

The final merit list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Written Test, subject to qualifying all other stages.

Exam Pattern (Written Test)

The written test is an objective-type paper designed to test general aptitude and English language skills.

Subject No. of Questions Total Marks Duration
English 20 50 45 Minutes
Reasoning & General Awareness (RAGA) 30
  • Marking Scheme: 1 mark for every correct answer.
  • Negative Marking: 0.25 marks will be deducted for each wrong answer.
  • Syllabus: The syllabus will be based on the 10+2 CBSE curriculum.

Physical Fitness Test (PFT) and Medical Standards

Physical fitness is a mandatory and non-negotiable part of the selection process.

A. Physical Fitness Test (PFT - Qualifying):

Event Qualifying Standard
1.6 Km Run Within 7 minutes (for candidates up to 21 years of age)
Within 7 minutes 30 seconds (for candidates above 21 years)
Additional Tests Candidates must also complete 10 Push-ups, 10 Sit-ups, and 20 Squats within the stipulated time.

B. Medical and Physical Standards:

  • Minimum Height: 152.5 cm.
  • Chest: Minimum range of expansion of 5 cm.
  • Weight: Proportionate to height and age.
  • Hearing: Must have normal hearing.
  • Dental: Should have healthy gums, a good set of teeth, and a minimum of 14 dental points.
  • Vision: Visual standards are specific and detailed in the official notification.

How to Prepare

A dual preparation strategy focusing on both physical and written tests is crucial from day one.

  • Physical Fitness: Start a consistent running and exercise regimen immediately. Aim to complete the 1.6 km run well within the qualifying time. Practice push-ups, sit-ups, and squats correctly.
  • Written Test: Revise your 10+2 level English grammar and vocabulary. For the RAGA section, practice basic reasoning puzzles and stay updated with current affairs of the last 6 months.
  • Document Preparation: This is the first step at the rally. Ensure you have all original documents and their photocopies neatly arranged as per the list in the notification.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to standard books for their written test preparation.

  • English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi or "High School English Grammar and Composition" by Wren & Martin.
  • Reasoning & General Awareness (RAGA): "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal and "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • Practice Sets: A dedicated book for Air Force Group 'Y' exam preparation (e.g., from Arihant or S.K. Publications).

How to Apply

This is a direct recruitment rally. No online application is required before the rally dates.

  1. Carefully read the official notification to check the date your specific district is scheduled for.
  2. Download and fill out the application form if one is provided with the notification (usually it is filled at the venue).
  3. Gather all the original documents and at least 4-5 self-attested photocopies of each.
  4. Arrive at the rally venue, Lal Parade Ground, Bhopal, Madhya Pradesh, on the scheduled date for your district. Reporting time is usually early in the morning (e.g., 6:00 AM).
  5. Participate in the sequence of tests as conducted by the Air Force officials.

Documents Needed for the Rally

Candidates must bring all the following original documents along with multiple self-attested photocopies:

  1. HB Pencil, Eraser, Sharpener, Glue Stick, Stapler, and a Black/Blue Ball Point Pen.
  2. Seven recent, un-attested passport-size colour photographs.
  3. Original and four self-attested photocopies of the Matriculation (10th) Passing Certificate.
  4. Original and four self-attested photocopies of the 10+2/Intermediate/Equivalent Examination Passing Certificate and Mark Sheets.
  5. Original and four self-attested photocopies of the Diploma/B.Sc in Pharmacy Certificate and Mark Sheets and the Pharmacy Council Registration Certificate (for Pharmacy candidates).
  6. Original and four self-attested photocopies of the Domicile Certificate.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Official Airmen Selection Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is this a permanent job in the Indian Air Force?

Yes, candidates who are finally selected will be enrolled as Airmen and will be part of the permanent service of the Indian Air Force, subject to service conditions.

Q2: Can female candidates participate in this rally?

No, this specific recruitment rally is only for unmarried male Indian/Nepalese citizens (with a small exception for married pharmacy candidates).

Q3: What if I miss the rally date for my district?

The rally is conducted district-wise on specific dates. If you miss the scheduled date for your district, you will not be allowed to participate on another day.

Q4: Is there any negative marking in the written test?

Yes, there is a negative marking of 0.25 marks for every incorrect answer in the written test.

entry.title: ISRO SDSC SHAR भर्ती 2025: Scientist, Engineer और Technician के अनेक पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!
entry.content:
ISRO SDSC SHAR भर्ती 2025: Scientist, Engineer और Technician के अनेक पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!

भारत के Spaceport में 10वीं पास, Diploma, Graduate और Postgraduate उम्मीदवारों के लिए सुनहरा अवसर।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! अगर आपका सपना Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) में काम करने का है, तो आपके लिए यह एक बेहतरीन अवसर है। Satish Dhawan Space Centre SHAR (SDSC SHAR) — जिसे "Spaceport of India" कहा जाता है — ने Scientist/Engineer, Technical Assistant, Scientific Assistant, Technician और अन्य अनेक पदों पर भर्ती हेतु अधिसूचना (Advt. No. SDSC SHAR/RMT/01/2025) जारी की है।

यह भर्ती देश के युवाओं को ISRO के प्रतिष्ठित मिशनों का हिस्सा बनने का अवसर देती है। आवेदन प्रक्रिया पूरी तरह ऑनलाइन है। नीचे इस भर्ती से जुड़ी सभी आवश्यक जानकारियाँ दी गई हैं — जैसे कि पद विवरण, योग्यता, आयु सीमा, चयन प्रक्रिया, वेतनमान और आवेदन प्रक्रिया।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि / समय
ऑनलाइन पंजीकरण प्रारंभ16 अक्टूबर 2025 (सुबह 10:00 बजे)
ऑनलाइन पंजीकरण की अंतिम तिथि14 नवम्बर 2025 (शाम 5:00 बजे)

पदों का विवरण

इस भर्ती अभियान में विभिन्न स्तरों और योग्यताओं के अनुसार कई पद शामिल हैं:

Scientist/Engineer 'SC' - Level 10 (₹56,100 - ₹1,77,500)

शाखारिक्तियाँस्थान
Machine Design01Sriharikota
Industrial Engineering03Sriharikota, Dehradun
Electrical & Electronics02Sriharikota
Atmospheric Science/Meteorology01Sriharikota
Analytical Chemistry01Sriharikota
Chemical Engineering15Sriharikota, Thiruvananthapuram

Technical/Scientific/Library Assistant - Level 7 (₹44,900 - ₹1,42,400)

पद नामरिक्तियाँ
Technical Assistant (विभिन्न विषय)कई पद
Scientific Assistant (Chemistry, Computer Science)02
Library Assistant 'A'01
Nurse 'B'01

Technician 'B' और अन्य पद - Level 2, 3, 4

पद नामवेतन स्तररिक्तियाँ
Radiographer 'A'Level 401
Technician 'B' (विभिन्न ट्रेड)Level 3कई पद
Draughtsman 'B' (Civil)Level 301
CookLevel 203
Fireman 'A'Level 206
Light Vehicle Driver 'A'Level 203

आवश्यक योग्यता

  • Scientist/Engineer 'SC': संबंधित विषय में M.E./M.Tech/M.Sc (Engg) या B.E./B.Tech/M.Sc डिग्री।
  • Technical Assistant: संबंधित विषय में फर्स्ट क्लास डिप्लोमा।
  • Scientific Assistant: संबंधित विषय में फर्स्ट क्लास B.Sc।
  • Library Assistant 'A': फर्स्ट क्लास ग्रेजुएशन और लाइब्रेरी साइंस में फर्स्ट क्लास मास्टर डिग्री।
  • Technician 'B' / Draughtsman 'B': 10वीं पास तथा संबंधित ट्रेड में ITI/NTC/NAC प्रमाणपत्र।
  • Cook: 10वीं पास और 5 वर्ष का अनुभव।
  • Light Vehicle Driver 'A': 10वीं पास, 3 वर्ष का अनुभव तथा वैध LVD लाइसेंस।
नोट: प्रत्येक पद की विस्तृत पात्रता और अंकों का प्रतिशत जानने के लिए आधिकारिक अधिसूचना देखें।

आयु सीमा (14.11.2025 तक)

  • Scientist/Engineer 'SC': 18 से 30 वर्ष (कुछ पदों के लिए 28 वर्ष)।
  • Technical/Scientific/Library Assistant: 18 से 35 वर्ष।
  • Technician/Draughtsman/Cook: 18 से 35 वर्ष।
  • Fireman 'A': 18 से 25 वर्ष।
  • Driver 'A': 18 से 35 वर्ष।

आयु में छूट: SC/ST/OBC/PwBD/Ex-Servicemen उम्मीदवारों को सरकारी नियमों के अनुसार छूट दी जाएगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया

  • Scientist/Engineer 'SC': चयन लिखित परीक्षा और साक्षात्कार के आधार पर होगा। दोनों को 50-50% वेटेज दिया जाएगा।
  • अन्य सभी पद: चयन लिखित परीक्षा और स्किल टेस्ट के आधार पर होगा। स्किल टेस्ट केवल क्वालिफाइंग नेचर का होगा।

आवेदन शुल्क

  • Post Code 01–20 & 40: ₹750 (लिखित परीक्षा में शामिल होने पर ₹500 वापस)।
  • Post Code 21–39, 41 & 42: ₹500 (लिखित परीक्षा में शामिल होने पर ₹400 वापस)।
  • महिला, SC/ST, PwBD, ESM उम्मीदवारों को पूर्ण शुल्क वापस किया जाएगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया

  1. आधिकारिक वेबसाइट https://www.shar.gov.in या https://apps.shar.gov.in पर जाएँ।
  2. 16 अक्टूबर से 14 नवम्बर 2025 के बीच आवेदन करें।
  3. स्कैन दस्तावेज़ तैयार रखें – फोटो, हस्ताक्षर, प्रमाणपत्र आदि।
  4. ऑनलाइन शुल्क जमा करें।
  5. आवेदन सबमिट कर उसका प्रिंट सुरक्षित रखें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन / आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें
अधिसूचना (PDF)यहाँ देखें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या ये नौकरियाँ स्थायी हैं?
हाँ, प्रारंभ में अस्थायी होंगी पर भविष्य में स्थायी बनने की संभावना है।
प्रश्न 2: क्या आवेदन शुल्क वापस मिलेगा?
जो उम्मीदवार लिखित परीक्षा में शामिल होंगे, उन्हें प्रोसेसिंग शुल्क का कुछ भाग वापस किया जाएगा।
प्रश्न 3: क्या एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन किया जा सकता है?
हाँ, लेकिन प्रत्येक पद के लिए अलग आवेदन फॉर्म और शुल्क देना होगा।
प्रश्न 4: इंटरव्यू किसके लिए होगा?
केवल Scientist/Engineer 'SC' पदों के लिए इंटरव्यू होगा।

entry.title: HP PWD Recruitment for 50 Junior Draughtsman (Civil) Posts
entry.content:
HP PWD Recruitment for 50 Junior Draughtsman (Civil) Posts

Advertisement No. 129-02/2025-PWD-B&A: The Himachal Pradesh Public Works Department (HPPWD) has announced the recruitment of 50 Junior Draughtsman (Civil) posts through the Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA). Online applications are invited from eligible candidates with an ITI/Diploma in Draughtsmanship (Civil). The online application window is open from January 22, 2026, to February 22, 2026.

Post Name: Junior Draughtsman (Civil)
Last Date: 22 February 2026
Total Posts: 50

The Himachal Pradesh Public Works Department (HPPWD), a key government department responsible for the state's infrastructure, has initiated a recruitment process to fill 50 vacancies for the post of Junior Draughtsman (Civil). The selection process for these technical positions has been entrusted to the newly formed Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA), Hamirpur. This is a significant opportunity for candidates with technical qualifications in civil draughtsmanship to secure a government job in Himachal Pradesh.

The selection will be based on a single-stage Objective Type Screening Test designed to assess the candidates' technical knowledge and general aptitude. The positions offer a stable career path in Pay Band Level-6. The entire application process is online and must be completed through the official HPRCA portal. Candidates are required to have a One Time Registration (OTR) profile on the portal before applying for the post.

HP PWD Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details. All applications must be submitted online through the HPRCA portal.

Particulars Details
Recruiting Department Himachal Pradesh Public Works Department (HPPWD)
Recruitment Agency Himachal Pradesh Rajya Chayan Aayog (HPRCA)
Post Name Junior Draughtsman (Civil)
Total Vacancies 50
Application Start Date 22 January 2026
Application Last Date 22 February 2026 (up to 11:59 PM)
Application Mode Online Only
Job Type Permanent Government Job
Job Location Himachal Pradesh
Official Website hprca.hp.gov.in

Vacancy and Pay Scale Details

The recruitment drive aims to fill 50 vacancies for the post of Junior Draughtsman (Civil). The category-wise distribution is as follows:

Category Vacancies Pay Scale
General (Unreserved)19Pay Band Level-6 (₹25,600 - ₹81,100)
EWS06
SC (Unreserved)10
SC (BPL)02
ST (Unreserved)02
ST (BPL)01
OBC (Unreserved)08
OBC (BPL)02
Total 50
Note: Reservation benefits are applicable only to candidates who are bonafide residents of Himachal Pradesh.

Application Fee

  • For General Category candidates: ₹360/-
  • For SC/ST/OBC/EWS/BPL candidates of Himachal Pradesh: ₹120/-
  • For Female candidates and Ex-Servicemen of Himachal Pradesh: No Fee

The fee must be paid online. Candidates from other states will be treated as General category and will have to pay ₹360.

Eligibility Criteria (as on 22.02.2026)

Candidates must ensure they meet all eligibility conditions regarding education, age, and domicile as of the closing date of the application.

Criteria Details
Educational Qualification A candidate must possess both of the following qualifications:
  1. 10+2 or its equivalent from a recognized Board/University.
  2. A duly recognized ITI Certificate or Diploma in Draughtsmanship (Civil) from an institution recognized by the Central/HP Government.
Domicile Condition The candidate must have passed Matriculation (10th) and 10+2 from any School/Institution located within Himachal Pradesh. This condition is not applicable to Bonafide Himachalis.
Age Limit (as on 01.01.2026)
  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 45 Years
Age relaxation for reserved categories of Himachal Pradesh is applicable as per government rules.
Desirable Qualification Knowledge of customs, manners, and dialects of Himachal Pradesh and suitability for appointment in the peculiar conditions prevailing in the state.

Selection Process

The selection will be based on a single-stage competitive examination.

  1. Objective Type Screening Test: All eligible candidates will appear for a written screening test. The test will consist of 100 multiple-choice questions for a total of 100 marks.
  2. Document Verification: Candidates will be shortlisted for document verification based on their merit in the written test.
  3. Final Merit List: The final merit list will be prepared based solely on the marks obtained in the Objective Type Screening Test.

Exam Pattern and Syllabus

The screening test is a 100-mark objective paper with a duration of 2 hours. There will be negative marking for wrong answers.

Component No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration Negative Marking
Objective Type Screening Test 100 100 2 Hours Yes (1/4th or 0.25 marks per wrong answer)

Syllabus Breakdown:

  • Part A (Technical Subject): 70 questions will be from the syllabus of ITI Diploma/Certificate in Draughtsmanship (Civil).
  • Part B (General Knowledge): 30 questions will cover General Knowledge including GK of Himachal Pradesh, Current Affairs, Everyday Science, Logic, Social Science, General English, and General Hindi.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for the post of Junior Draughtsman (Civil).

How to Prepare for the Exam

A focused preparation strategy is essential, with a strong emphasis on the technical subject.

  • Master the Draughtsmanship Syllabus: Since 70% of the paper is from your technical trade, this should be your top priority. Thoroughly revise all subjects from your ITI/Diploma curriculum, including engineering drawing, building materials, surveying, and CAD software concepts.
  • Prepare for the General Section: Dedicate time to study Himachal Pradesh GK, national current affairs, and the basics of other general subjects listed in the syllabus.
  • Practice with Negative Marking: Solve mock tests and previous year papers of similar technical exams conducted by HPRCA/HPSSC. Practicing with a negative marking scheme is crucial to improve accuracy and avoid losing marks.

Best Books for Preparation

Candidates can refer to these standard books for building a strong foundation.

  • Draughtsmanship (Civil): "Draughtsman Civil Theory" books by NIMI publications or Arihant are excellent resources. Also, refer to your ITI/Diploma textbooks.
  • Himachal Pradesh GK: "The Wonderland Himachal Pradesh" by Jag Mohan Balokhra is a comprehensive guide.
  • General Knowledge: "Lucent's General Knowledge" for the static part.
  • Objective Questions: Books that compile previous year papers of HPSSC/HPRCA exams.

How to Apply Online

The application process is entirely online via the HPRCA's One Time Registration (OTR) portal.

  1. Step 1 (OTR): If you are a new user, you must first register on the HPRCA portal at hprca.hp.gov.in by completing the One Time Registration.
  2. Step 2 (Login & Apply): Log in to the portal using your credentials. Find the advertisement for "Junior Draughtsman (Civil) in HPPWD" and click "Apply".
  3. Step 3 (Fill Form): Your personal details will be auto-filled from your OTR profile. Fill in any additional required information.
  4. Step 4 (Upload Documents): Upload your photograph, signature, and any other required documents if not already done during OTR.
  5. Step 5 (Pay Fee): Pay the application fee online through the payment gateway.
  6. Step 6 (Final Submission): Review your application and submit it. Download a copy of the final application form for your records.

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates should have the following documents ready for the OTR and application process:

  1. Scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. Matriculation (10th) certificate for age proof.
  3. 10+2 certificate and mark sheet.
  4. ITI Certificate or Diploma in Draughtsmanship (Civil) and mark sheet(s).
  5. Bonafide Himachali certificate (if applicable).
  6. Valid Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC), EWS, or BPL certificate as per the prescribed format for HP residents.
  7. Valid Mobile Number and Email ID.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Apply Online (HPRCA Portal) Click Here
Official HPPWD Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: What is the last date to apply for the HP PWD Junior Draughtsman post?

The last date for submitting the online application is February 22, 2026, up to 11:59 PM.

Q2: Is there a fee for female candidates from Himachal Pradesh?

No, as per the notification, female candidates who are residents of Himachal Pradesh are exempted from paying the application fee.

Q3: I have a Diploma in Civil Engineering, not Draughtsmanship. Am I eligible?

The notification specifically requires a Certificate or Diploma in "Draughtsmanship (Civil)". A Diploma in Civil Engineering may not be considered equivalent unless specified by the board. Candidates should refer to the official rules or contact HPRCA for clarification.

Q4: Is there an interview for this post?

No, the selection is based solely on the merit of the Objective Type Screening Test. There is no interview for this post.

entry.title: Satyawati College Recruitment 2025: Permanent Non-Teaching Posts at Delhi University
entry.content:
Satyawati College Recruitment 2025: Permanent Non-Teaching Posts at Delhi University

A fantastic opportunity to secure a stable career in a premier DU college. Online applications are invited for 18 posts including SPA, Assistant, Junior Assistant, and Library Attendant.

Post Name: Senior Personal Assistant, Senior Assistant, Assistant, Junior Assistant, Library Attendant & others
Last Date: 22/12/2025
Total Posts: 18

Satyawati College, a prestigious constituent college of the University of Delhi accredited with an 'A+' Grade by NAAC, has released a detailed advertisement (Advt.No.SC/NTS/2025/01) for recruitment to various non-teaching positions on a permanent basis. This presents a magnificent opportunity for qualified and talented candidates who aspire to build a stable and respected career in one of the country's top academic institutions. Through this recruitment drive, a total of 18 vacancies will be filled, including diverse roles such as Senior Personal Assistant, Senior Assistant, Junior Assistant, and Library Attendant.

This recruitment will be conducted according to the rules and regulations of the University of Delhi, offering a golden chance for individuals looking to build a career in administrative and library services. All posts are permanent, which ensures job security and an excellent work environment within a premier educational setup. The selection process will involve a written examination and/or a skill test, depending on the nature of the posts, ensuring a merit-based and transparent selection.

Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the University of Delhi's recruitment portal, https://dunt.uod.ac.in. The last date for application is set for December 22, 2025, or within two weeks from the date of publication of the advertisement in the Employment News, whichever is later. Candidates are strongly advised to carefully read all the information provided in this article, such as post-wise vacancies, pay scale, educational qualifications, age limit, selection process, and application fee, before applying.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a concise summary of the key details regarding this recruitment drive.

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameSatyawati College (University of Delhi)
Advertisement NumberSC/NTS/2025/01
Job TypePermanent
Job LocationSatyawati College, Ashok Vihar, Delhi
Application ModeOnline
Last Date to ApplyDecember 22, 2025 (or 2 weeks from publication in Employment News)

Vacancy Details and Post-wise Breakdown

Satyawati College has announced a total of 18 vacancies for various non-teaching posts. These vacancies are distributed across different reservation categories. A detailed post-wise and category-wise breakdown is provided below:

Post Name Pay Level Total Posts UR ST OBC EWS PwBD
Senior Personal Assistant 7 1 1 - - - -
Senior Assistant 6 1 - - - - 1 (LD)
Assistant 4 1 1 - - - -
Junior Assistant 2 5 2 - 2 1 -
Semi Professional Assistant 5 1 1 - - - -
Library Assistant 4 1 1 - - - -
Library Attendant 1 8 6 1 - - 1 (LD)
Grand Total 18 12 1 2 1 2

Application Fee Details

Candidates are required to pay a non-refundable application fee online as per their category.

Category Application Fee
General/Unreserved (UR) ₹ 1000/-
OBC (NCL), EWS, Women ₹ 800/-
SC, ST, PwBD ₹ 600/-

Eligibility Criteria: Educational Qualification & Age Limit

Specific educational qualifications and upper age limits have been prescribed for each post. Age relaxation for reserved category candidates will be provided as per the rules of the University of Delhi/UGC/Government of India.

Post Name Educational Qualification and Skills Maximum Age
Senior Personal Assistant
  • A Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University.
  • 3 years of experience as a Personal Assistant in Pay Level 6 OR 5 years of experience as a Stenographer in Pay Level 4.
  • Skill Test: Stenography speed of 120 w.p.m in English or 100 w.p.m in Hindi.
 35 years
Senior Assistant
  • A Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University.
  • 3 years of experience as an Assistant or equivalent in Pay Level 4.
  • Proficiency in computer applications, noting, and drafting.
 35 years
Assistant
  • A Bachelor's Degree from a recognized Institute/University.
  • 2 years of experience as a Junior Assistant or equivalent.
  • Typing speed of 35 w.p.m in English or 30 w.p.m in Hindi.
  • Proficiency in computer operations.
 32 years
Junior Assistant
  • A Bachelor's Degree from a recognized Institute/University.
  • Typing speed of 35 w.p.m in English or 30 w.p.m in Hindi.
  • Proficiency in computer operations.
 32 years
Semi Professional Assistant
  • Master's Degree in Library Science & Information Science OR
  • Bachelor's Degree in Library Science/Library & Information Science with 2 years of relevant experience.
 32 years
Library Assistant
  • Bachelor's Degree in Library & Information Science or equivalent.
  • Typing speed of 30 w.p.m in English/Hindi and knowledge of computer applications.
 32 years
Library Attendant
  • 10+2 or equivalent examination from a recognized Board.
  • Certificate course in Library Science from a recognized institution.
  • Knowledge of computer applications.
 32 years

Selection Process

The selection process for non-teaching posts at Satyawati College will be conducted as per the guidelines of the University of Delhi. The process will primarily consist of the following stages:

  1. Written Examination: An objective-type written examination will be conducted for all posts. This test will include questions based on general awareness, reasoning ability, mathematical ability, language (English/Hindi), and knowledge relevant to the post. The detailed syllabus and scheme of the examination will be made available on the college website.
  2. Skill Test: Candidates who qualify in the written examination will be called for a skill test as per the requirement of the post.
    • For Senior Personal Assistant: Stenography (shorthand) and typing test.
    • For Assistant and Junior Assistant: Typing test on a computer.
    • For Library Assistant: Typing test and knowledge of computer applications.
    This test will be qualifying in nature, but it is mandatory to pass it.
  3. Document Verification: Candidates successful in all stages will be called for the verification of their original documents. In this stage, candidates will have to verify all their original academic, experience, caste, and other certificates.
  4. Final Merit List: The final selection list will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the written examination, provided the candidate qualifies in the skill test (wherever applicable).

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus (Expected)

The examination will be conducted as per the scheme prescribed by the University of Delhi. While the exact pattern will be notified on the college website, candidates can expect the following structure.

Written Test Pattern

The written test will likely consist of two papers: Paper-I (MCQ) and Paper-II (Descriptive, for some posts).

Paper Subject / Section Expected Questions/Marks
Paper-I (MCQ Type) General Awareness ~25-30 questions
Reasoning Ability ~25-30 questions
Mathematical Ability ~25-30 questions
Language (English or Hindi) ~25-30 questions
Paper-II (For Assistant/Sr. Assistant) Descriptive type (Essay, Letter Writing, Noting & Drafting) ~100 Marks

Skill Test Pattern

  • Typing Test (Junior/Assistant): 10-minute test. Required speed: 35 w.p.m in English or 30 w.p.m in Hindi.
  • Stenography Test (SPA): 10-minute dictation at 120/100 w.p.m. Transcription time will be provided as per norms.

Detailed Syllabus

  • General Awareness: Indian History, Polity, Constitution, Art & Culture, Geography, Economics, General Science, Scientific Research, National/International Organizations, and Current Affairs.
  • Reasoning Ability: Analogies, Similarities and Differences, Problem Solving, Analysis, Judgment, Decision Making, Visual Memory, Coding and Decoding, Number Series.
  • Mathematical Ability: Number Systems, Simplification, Decimals, Data Interpretation, Fractions, L.C.M., H.C.F., Ratio & Proportion, Percentage, Average, Profit & Loss, Discount, Simple & Compound Interest.
  • Language (English/Hindi): Vocabulary, Grammar, Sentence Structure, Synonyms, Antonyms, Reading Comprehension, Error Detection.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or any physical standard requirement for these administrative and library posts. The selection is based purely on performance in the written examination and skill tests.

How to Prepare for the Satyawati College Exam

A strategic approach is required to succeed in the Delhi University non-teaching examinations.

  1. Understand the Exam Pattern and Syllabus: The first step is to download the detailed exam pattern and syllabus for your post from the college website once it is released.
  2. General Section Preparation:
    • General Awareness: Focus on current affairs, Indian history, geography, polity, and general science.
    • Reasoning Ability: Practice topics like analogies, series, coding-decoding, and logical reasoning from a standard book.
    • Mathematical Ability: Practice basic arithmetic topics like percentage, profit-loss, average, and time & work.
    • Language (English/Hindi): Focus on grammar, vocabulary, and reading comprehension.
  3. Skill Test Preparation:
    • Typing: If typing is required for your post, practice regularly on a computer to achieve the prescribed speed and accuracy.
    • Stenography: Candidates for the Senior Personal Assistant post should work on their shorthand speed and accuracy daily.
  4. Computer Knowledge: Basic knowledge of computers is essential for all posts. Practice using MS-Office (Word, Excel) and the internet.
  5. Previous Year Papers: Solve question papers from previous non-teaching exams conducted by the University of Delhi or NTA to get an idea of the exam's pattern and difficulty level.

Best Books for Preparation

Here is a list of recommended books to aid your preparation:

  • General Awareness: "Lucent's General Knowledge".
  • Reasoning Ability: "A Modern Approach to Verbal & Non-Verbal Reasoning" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • Quantitative Aptitude: "Quantitative Aptitude for Competitive Examinations" by R.S. Aggarwal.
  • General English: "Objective General English" by S.P. Bakshi.
  • Computer Knowledge: "Objective Computer Awareness" by Arihant Publications.
  • For Library Posts: "A Textbook for Library and Information Science" by O. P. Gupta.

How to Apply Online

The application process is completely online. Candidates must apply through the common non-teaching recruitment portal of the University of Delhi. Follow the steps below to apply:

  1. First, visit the University of Delhi's recruitment portal: https://dunt.uod.ac.in.
  2. If you are a new user, register yourself using your email ID and mobile number.
  3. After registration, log in to your account and search for the advertisement "Advt.No.SC/NTS/2025/01" for "Satyawati College".
  4. Click on "Apply Now" against the post you wish to apply for.
  5. Fill in the application form with all your personal details, educational qualifications, and experience details accurately.
  6. Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, and other necessary documents (like certificates) in the prescribed format.
  7. Before submitting the application form, carefully review all the information you have filled in.
  8. Pay the application fee as per your category through the online payment gateway (Credit Card/Debit Card/Net Banking).
  9. Take a printout of the final submitted application form and the fee receipt for future reference.
Note: Candidates already in government service must apply through the proper channel and will need to produce a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer at the time of document verification.

Documents Needed to Apply and for Verification

The following documents will be required during the online application and at the time of document verification:

  1. Scanned copy of a recent passport-size photograph and signature.
  2. 10th class certificate for proof of date of birth.
  3. All educational qualification certificates and marksheets (10+2, Graduation, Post-graduation, etc.).
  4. Experience certificates, clearly mentioning the post, pay level, and period of service.
  5. Reservation category certificate (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS/PwBD) in the prescribed format and issued by the competent authority. (OBC-NCL and EWS certificates must be issued after 01.04.2025).
  6. Identity proof (such as Aadhaar Card, Voter ID).
  7. No Objection Certificate (NOC) for government employees.

Important Links

Link Type Link
Official Notification and Instructions Click Here (in "Jobs & Opportunities" section)
Apply Online https://dunt.uod.ac.in
Satyawati College Official Website https://www.satyawati.du.ac.in

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: What is the last date to apply for the Satyawati College recruitment?

The last date to apply online is December 22, 2025 (or two weeks from the date of publication of the advertisement in Employment News, whichever is later).

Question 2: Are these jobs permanent?

Yes, as per the notification, all the advertised posts are on a permanent basis.

Question 3: What will be the selection process?

The selection process will include a written examination and a skill test (like typing/stenography test) as per the requirement of the post.

Question 4: Can candidates from outside Delhi apply?

Yes, any eligible citizen of India can apply for these posts. However, the benefit of OBC reservation will only be available to castes included in the Central List.

Question 5: What is the application fee?

The application fee is ₹1000/- for the General category, ₹800/- for OBC/EWS/Women, and ₹600/- for SC/ST/PwBD candidates.

entry.title: Gurugram Medanta Staff Nurse Recruitment 2025: A Premier Opportunity for GNM and B.Sc Nursing Graduates
entry.content:
Gurugram Medanta Staff Nurse Recruitment 2025: A Premier Opportunity for GNM and B.Sc Nursing Graduates

Join one of India's leading healthcare institutions. Freshers are welcome to apply for this rewarding nursing career.

Post Name: Staff Nurse
Last Date: Applications are ongoing
Total Posts: Multiple Vacancies

In a significant move to strengthen its nursing department, Medanta, one of India's most prestigious and leading healthcare institutions, has released a detailed job description (JD) for the recruitment of Staff Nurses. This recruitment drive presents a magnificent opportunity for qualified and dedicated nursing professionals who wish to steer their careers in a new direction by working in a hospital equipped with the best medical facilities in the country. Known for its world-class patient care, state-of-the-art technology, and a team of expert doctors, Medanta is now ready to welcome new members into its esteemed nursing team.

This recruitment is particularly attractive for candidates who have completed their GNM (General Nursing and Midwifery) or B.Sc Nursing and are Registered Nurses. A standout feature of this role is that it does not require prior experience; recent graduates and freshers can apply with full confidence. This position offers candidates the chance to work in a comprehensive and collaborative environment where they can develop their nursing capabilities and gain experience in caring for a diverse range of patients. The role of a Staff Nurse is the backbone of patient care at Medanta, and this is more than just a job—it's a career path that offers immense personal and professional growth.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

Here is a quick overview of the essential details regarding this recruitment drive. This is a private sector job opportunity with one of India's top hospital chains.

Particulars Details
Organization Name Medanta - The Medicity
Post Name Staff Nurse
Total Posts Various (Multiple vacancies are open)
Job Location Gurugram and other Medanta centers
Application Mode Online (Tentative)
Application Status Applications are currently ongoing
Job Type Private Sector

Why a Nursing Career at Medanta?

Choosing to begin or advance your nursing career at Medanta is a decision that promises substantial professional rewards and personal satisfaction. Medanta is not just a hospital; it is an ecosystem of excellence, innovation, and compassionate care. For a nursing professional, this translates into an unparalleled work environment. You will be part of an institution that is accredited by national and international bodies like NABH and JCI, which means you will be practicing nursing at the highest global standards. This commitment to quality ensures that nurses are trained in the latest evidence-based practices and are integral to maintaining a culture of patient safety.

Furthermore, Medanta fosters a culture of continuous learning and development. The organization invests heavily in training its staff, offering regular workshops, seminars, and Continuing Nursing Education (CNE) programs. This allows nurses to stay updated with the latest advancements in medical science and nursing care, specialize in areas of interest such as critical care, oncology, or cardiology, and climb the professional ladder. Working alongside some of the most renowned doctors and specialists in the country provides an invaluable learning experience, promoting a collaborative approach to patient care where nurses are respected members of the multidisciplinary team. The exposure to complex and diverse medical cases sharpens clinical skills and critical thinking, making you a more competent and confident healthcare professional.

Salary and Benefits

  • Competitive Salary: Medanta is known for offering a highly competitive and attractive salary package that aligns with industry standards. The final salary will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, experience (if any), and performance in the interview.
  • Benefits Package: In addition to the salary, selected employees may be eligible for other benefits such as health insurance, provident fund (PF), and performance-based incentives as per the company's policy.

Freshers can expect an attractive starting salary that recognizes their academic qualifications and potential. This comprehensive remuneration package makes Medanta an employer of choice in the healthcare sector.

Eligibility Criteria for Staff Nurse

Candidates wishing to apply for the Staff Nurse role at Medanta must meet the following essential criteria regarding educational qualifications, registration, and experience.

Criteria Requirement Details
Educational Qualification The candidate must possess a minimum qualification in GNM (General Nursing and Midwifery) or a B.Sc in Nursing from a recognized nursing college or institution. This forms the foundational knowledge base required for the role.
Registration It is mandatory for the candidate to be a Registered Nurse (RN) with a respective State Nursing Council. A valid and current registration is a prerequisite for practicing as a nurse in India and is a non-negotiable requirement.
Language Skills Proficiency in both English and Hindi is essential. Effective communication with a diverse patient population, their families, and the multidisciplinary healthcare team is a critical aspect of the job.
Work Experience The job description specifies an experience range of 0 to 3 years. This is a significant advantage as it explicitly opens the door for freshers (candidates with zero experience) to apply and start their career at a top-tier hospital.
Note on Age Limit & Application Fee: The official job description does not specify any age limit for this position. Typically, the minimum age is 18 years. Furthermore, Medanta will not charge any application fee from any candidate for this recruitment process. The application is completely free of cost.

Selection Process

The selection process for the Staff Nurse position at Medanta is designed to be professional and skill-based, ensuring that the best talent is recruited. The selection will likely be based on the following stages:

  1. Application Screening: All applications received will be scrutinized by the HR department. Candidates whose profiles match the eligibility criteria will be shortlisted for the next round. A well-crafted resume highlighting your qualifications and skills is crucial at this stage.
  2. Written Test (Potential): Medanta may conduct a written examination to assess the fundamental and technical knowledge of nursing. This test would likely cover subjects like Fundamentals of Nursing, Medical-Surgical Nursing, Pharmacology, and Community Health Nursing.
  3. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be invited for a personal interview with a panel comprising senior officials from the Human Resources (HR) and Nursing departments. The interview will evaluate the candidate's technical knowledge, soft skills, communication abilities, situational judgment, and overall suitability for the role.
  4. Document Verification: Candidates who are successful in the interview will be required to undergo document verification. They must produce all their original documents, including educational certificates, nursing registration, and identity proof, for verification.

Job Description and Key Responsibilities

As a Staff Nurse at Medanta, the selected candidate will be responsible for all aspects of patient care. The job description is divided into four main areas:

1. Management of Nursing Care

This is the core of the nursing role. Responsibilities include being aware of and implementing the department's vision, mission, and objectives. You will perform duties according to the expectations of a nurse's role in providing comprehensive patient care. This involves knowing the unit's layout, the number and types of patients, and their specific needs. A key duty is providing patients with appropriate information to make informed decisions about their treatment. You will be responsible for maintaining patient records that are current, accurate, and timely, ensuring confidentiality within legal and ethical frameworks. Crucially, you will be expected to take appropriate nursing action in emergency situations, demonstrating clinical competence and calm decision-making.

2. Soft Skills and Critical Thinking

Modern nursing is not just about clinical skills; it's also about patient experience. Medanta emphasizes the use of communication models like AIDET (Acknowledge, Introduce, Duration, Explanation, Thank) and GST (Greet, Smile, Thank). You will use effective communication skills to convey relevant information in a timely manner. Maintaining professional etiquette in English/Hindi with colleagues is expected. You will need to communicate patient status and interventions accurately and effectively using shift reports and other handover tools, ensuring continuity of care.

3. Professional Responsibility

Practicing nursing safely and ethically is paramount. You will be expected to practice based on professional, ethical, and legal standards. This includes maintaining infection control principles to prevent hospital-acquired infections. You will promote an environment that fosters patient safety, independence, quality of life, and health. As a professional, you will participate in regular performance reviews and contribute to peer reviews, fostering a culture of feedback and continuous improvement. This also involves taking ownership of your professional development by attending training and staying updated with best practices.

4. Nursing Admission Assessment

The first point of contact is crucial for setting the tone of care. You will conduct a comprehensive nursing assessment for all patients, considering their clinical status, pain levels, activities of daily living (ADLs), allergies, and safety needs. This includes assessing vital signs, recording critical information, and promptly reporting any abnormalities to the consultant and the team. A vital part of this role is to involve, communicate with, and educate the patient's family members in the future plan of care, ensuring they are partners in the patient's recovery journey.

How to Prepare for the Interview

Success in the Medanta interview requires a combination of strong technical knowledge and excellent soft skills. Here are some tips to help you prepare:

  • Revise Your Nursing Fundamentals: Brush up on core nursing subjects. Be prepared to answer questions on anatomy, physiology, pharmacology, and the principles of nursing care for common medical conditions.
  • Understand the Role: Read the job description carefully. Be prepared to give examples of how your skills and experiences align with the responsibilities mentioned.
  • Research Medanta: Learn about Medanta's history, its values, its key specialties, and any recent achievements. This shows your genuine interest in the organization.
  • Prepare for Behavioral Questions: Be ready to answer questions like "Tell me about a time you had to deal with a difficult patient," or "How do you handle stress?" Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) to structure your answers.
  • Practice Communication: Your communication skills will be assessed. Practice speaking clearly and confidently. Remember the importance of empathy and active listening.
  • Dress Professionally: Your appearance matters. Dress in formal or professional attire for the interview.

How to Apply for Medanta Staff Nurse Recruitment

Interested and eligible candidates can apply for the Staff Nurse position at Medanta through the company's official careers website. Follow these general steps to apply:

  1. First, visit the official Medanta website: www.medanta.org.
  2. Navigate to the "Careers" or "Work with Us" section on the website.
  3. Look for the list of current open vacancies and search for the "Staff Nurse" position.
  4. Click on "Apply Now" and fill out the online application form.
  5. Accurately fill in your personal information, educational qualifications, nursing registration details, and experience (if any) in the application form.
  6. Upload your latest resume (CV) and other necessary documents such as your nursing degree/diploma and registration certificate.
  7. Review all the information filled in the application form before final submission and click the "Submit" button.

Important Links

Link Type Link
View Official Job Description Click Here
Apply on Medanta's Official Careers Page Click Here

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Question 1: Who can apply for this post?

Any candidate who has completed GNM or B.Sc Nursing and is a registered nurse can apply.

Question 2: Can fresher candidates apply?

Yes, as per the job description, fresher candidates are fully eligible to apply. Candidates with 0 to 3 years of experience can apply.

Question 3: Is registration with a nursing council mandatory?

Yes, being a registered nurse with a nursing council is mandatory to apply for this post.

Question 4: What is the selection process?

The selection process will primarily include a personal interview. A written test may also be conducted.

Question 5: Where will the job location be?

The job location is likely to be at Medanta, Gurugram, although postings may also be at other locations as per the company's requirements.

entry.title: BECIL Recruitment for Central Govt Hospital, Delhi NCR
entry.content:
BECIL Recruitment for Central Govt Hospital, Delhi NCR

Vacancy Advertisement No. 534: Broadcast Engineering Consultants India Limited (BECIL) is recruiting for 72 various manpower positions on a contract basis for deployment in a Central Government Hospital in Delhi NCR. The application mode is offline, and the last date to submit applications is January 2, 2026. Get all the details on vacancies, eligibility, salary, and how to apply.

Post Name: DEO, MTS, PCM, PCC, Technician, Radiographer, and more
Last Date: 02 January 2026
Total Posts: 72

Broadcast Engineering Consultants India Limited (BECIL), a Mini Ratna company under the Ministry of Information & Broadcasting, has released a significant recruitment notification (Vacancy Advertisement No. 534) for various positions. BECIL is seeking applications in offline mode to fill 72 posts for manpower deployment in a Central Government Hospital located in the Delhi NCR region. All positions are purely on a contract basis.

The recruitment drive offers a wide range of opportunities in the healthcare and administrative sectors, including roles like Patient Care Manager, Patient Care Coordinator, Data Entry Operator (Medical Transcriptionist), Medical Lab Technician, Radiographer, MTS, and more. This is an excellent chance for qualified individuals to gain experience working in a government hospital setting. The application process is entirely offline, requiring candidates to submit their forms via Speed Post or Registered Post. The last date for the receipt of applications is January 2, 2026.

Important Points

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details to ensure a successful application. This is a contract-based job opportunity for deployment in a government hospital in Delhi NCR.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Broadcast Engineering Consultants India Limited (BECIL)
Advertisement Number 534
Post Names Various (Technician, DEO, MTS, Manager, Coordinator, etc.)
Total Vacancies 72
Application Mode Offline (Speed Post/Registered Post ONLY)
Last Date for Receipt of Application 02 January 2026 (18:00 HRS)
Job Type Contract Basis
Job Location Central Government Hospital, Delhi NCR
Official Website www.becil.com

Vacancy Details

A total of 72 posts are being filled through this recruitment drive, distributed across 16 different positions. The detailed vacancy breakdown is as follows:

Post Name Number of Vacancies Consolidated Pay (per month)
Data Entry Operator (DEO)- Medical Transcriptionists 30 ₹25,506/-
Data Entry Operator (DEO) 10 ₹25,506/-
Multi-tasking Staff (MTS) - Male 10 ₹20,930/-
Patient Care Manager (PCM) 05 ₹30,000/-
