Medical Lab Technician (MLT) 05 ₹40,710/-
Ophthalmic Technician 03 ₹40,710/-
Medical Record Technician (MRT) 03 ₹25,506/-
Assistant Dietician 02 ₹40,710/-
Dental Technician 02 ₹29,325/-
Food Bearer 02 ₹20,930/-
Technical Assistant ENT 01 ₹40,710/-
Patient care Coordinator (PCC) 01 ₹27,690/-
Tailor 01 ₹25,000/-
Lab Attendant 01 ₹25,506/-
PTI- Female 01 ₹40,710/-
Radiographer 01 ₹40,710/-
Total 72
Note: The number of posts mentioned is subject to increase or decrease as per the requirement.

Application Processing Fee

  • For General, OBC, and other unreserved categories: ₹295/-
  • For SC/ST and PwD candidates: NIL (No Fee)

The fee must be paid in the form of a Demand Draft (DD) drawn in favor of "Broadcast Engineering Consultants India Ltd, Noida".

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must ensure they meet the specific age and qualification requirements for the post they are applying for. The age limit will be applicable as on the date of publication of the advertisement.

Post Name Age Limit & Eligibility Details
Technical Assistant ENT Age Limit: Up to 40 years.
Qualification: B.Sc. Degree in Speech and Hearing from a recognized Institution/University. Must be registered with the Rehabilitation Council of India (RCI).
Ophthalmic Technician Age Limit: No Bar.
Qualification: B.Sc. in Ophthalmic Techniques or equivalent from a recognized University.
Patient Care Manager (PCM) Age Limit: Not more than 40 Years.
Qualification: Bachelor's Degree in Life Sciences with a full-time Post Graduate qualification in Hospital (or Healthcare) Management.
Experience: At least one year of experience in a hospital after the Post-Graduation degree.
Patient Care Coordinator (PCC) Age Limit: Not more than 35 years.
Qualification: Full-time Bachelor's Degree in Life Sciences (preferred) or Bachelor's degree in any field.
Experience: At least one year of experience in a hospital after graduation.
Assistant Dietician Age Limit: Not exceeding 35 Years.
Qualification: M.Sc. (Food & Nutrition) from a recognized University.
Experience: 2 years' experience, preferably in a large teaching hospital.
Medical Record Technician (MRT) Age Limit: Between 20 to 40 years.
Qualification: 12th passed with a certificate course (min. 6 months) in Medical Record Technology.
Experience: At least one year of work experience in MS Word & Excel.
Data Entry Operator (DEO)- Medical Transcriptionists Age Limit: 18 to 40 years.
Qualification: 12th Passed from Science Stream (B.Sc. desirable).
Skills: Typing Speed up to 35 wpm.
Experience: 6 months.
Tailor Age Limit: Between 18 to 30 years.
Qualification: 8th passed with a certificate from ITI or any other recognized institution in the trade of tailoring.
Medical Lab Technician (MLT) Age Limit: 21 to 35 years.
Qualification: Bachelor's Degree in Medical Laboratory Technologists / Medical Laboratory Science.
Experience: Two years of experience after the bachelor's degree in the relevant field.
Lab Attendant Age Limit: 21 to 35 years.
Qualification: 12th pass (Science).
Experience: Two years' post-qualification experience as a Lab Attendant.
Dental Technician Age Limit: No Bar.
Qualification: 12th passed (Intermediate) with science subjects and a minimum 2-year diploma from a DCI recognized institution.
Experience: Minimum two years of post-qualification experience as a dental mechanic/technician.
PTI- Female Age Limit: Up to 30 years.
Qualification: Bachelor of Physical Education from a recognized University.
Experience: 2 years minimum in the field of Physical Training.
Radiographer Age Limit: 21 to 35 years.
Qualification: B.Sc. (Hons.) in Radiography or a 3-year B.Sc. in Radiography course from a recognized university.
Data Entry Operator (DEO) Age Limit: 18 to 40 years.
Qualification: 12th Passed from Science Stream (B.Sc. desirable).
Skills: Typing Speed up to 35 wpm.
Experience: 6 months.
Multi-tasking Staff (MTS) - Male Age Limit: 18 to 35 years.
Qualification: Matriculation from a recognized Board/Institution. Preference will be given to experienced candidates.
Food Bearer Age Limit: Between 18 to 40 years.
Qualification: 10th Passed/Matriculate with a certificate of training in food hygiene and safety.
Experience: At least one year as a Bearer/waiter/utility worker in a catering establishment.

Selection Process

The selection of candidates will be based on a multi-stage process. Possession of minimum qualifications does not guarantee a call for the next stage.

  1. Shortlisting of Applications: Applications will be shortlisted based on the eligibility criteria mentioned for each post.
  2. Merit-Based Preference: Preference may be given to candidates with higher marks in the prescribed qualifications or greater relevant experience.
  3. Interview / Assessment / Skill Test: Shortlisted candidates will be informed via Email or phone call for an interview, assessment, or skill test (if applicable for the post).
  4. Final Selection: The final list of selected candidates will be published on the official BECIL website after the conclusion of the final interview/selection process conducted by the client (Central Government Hospital).

Exam Pattern

The advertisement does not specify a written examination for all posts. The selection will primarily be through shortlisting followed by an interview, assessment, or skill test. The nature of the assessment will depend on the post applied for.

  • For posts like Data Entry Operator (DEO), a typing skill test is expected to be part of the selection process.
  • For technical and managerial roles, an interview will likely be conducted to assess domain knowledge and suitability.
  • Shortlisting itself may be done on the basis of marks obtained in the qualifying academic examination.

Candidates are advised to prepare based on the job requirements and responsibilities mentioned for their respective posts.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) mentioned for any of the posts in this recruitment advertisement. However, for posts like Food Bearer, a medical examination is mandatory to ensure health and hygiene standards, including tests like Hemoglobin, LFT, Widal, and Hepatitis A, along with required vaccinations.

How to Prepare for the Selection

Since the selection is primarily based on shortlisting and interviews/skill tests, your preparation should focus on highlighting your qualifications and experience.

  • Craft a Strong Application: Ensure your application form is filled out neatly and accurately, clearly highlighting your educational qualifications and relevant work experience.
  • Prepare for Interviews: For technical and managerial roles, revise the core concepts of your field. Be prepared to answer questions related to your previous work experience and the roles and responsibilities mentioned in the advertisement.
  • Brush Up on Skills: For posts like DEO, practice your typing to meet the required speed of 35 wpm. For technical posts, be prepared for practical questions or assessments related to your trade.
  • Highlight Your Academic Merit: As shortlisting is based on academic qualifications and experience, having a strong academic record is a significant advantage.

Best Books for Preparation

As there is no standardized written exam, preparation should be specific to the post. Candidates should refer to their academic textbooks and professional reference books.

  • For Technical Posts (MLT, Radiographer, etc.): Refer to your degree/diploma textbooks to refresh your knowledge.
  • For Managerial Posts (PCM, PCC): Books on Hospital Management and Healthcare Administration will be useful.
  • For DEO: Practice typing and refer to books on basic computer operations and MS Office.

How to Apply

The application process is strictly offline. No other mode of application will be accepted.

  1. Download the prescribed application form attached to the official advertisement from the BECIL website.
  2. Fill out the application form completely and correctly.
  3. Prepare a Demand Draft (DD) for the application processing fee (₹295/- for general categories, NIL for SC/ST/PwD) in favor of "Broadcast Engineering Consultants India Ltd, Noida".
  4. Gather self-attested photocopies of all required educational and experience certificates.
  5. Place the duly filled-in application form, the Demand Draft, and all the document photocopies in a sealed envelope.
  6. Superscribe the envelope with "Advertisement No: 534" and "Post Applied For: [Your Post Name]".
  7. Send the envelope via SPEED POST or REGISTERED POST only to the following address:

    Broadcast Engineering Consultants India Limited (BECIL),
    BECIL BHAWAN, C-56/A-17, Sector-62,
    Noida-201307 (U.P)

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must attach self-attested photocopies of the following documents along with the application form:

  1. Educational / Professional Certificates.
  2. 10th and 12th certificates (if applicable).
  3. Birth Certificate.
  4. Caste Certificate (if applicable).
  5. Work Experience Certificate(s).
  6. PAN Card copy.
  7. Aadhaar Card copy.
  8. Copy of EPF/ESIC Card from a previous employer (if applicable).
  9. Bank passbook copy showing account details.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification Click Here
Application Form Click Here
Official Website Click Here

FAQs

Q1: What is the mode of application for this recruitment?

The application mode is strictly offline. Applications must be sent via Speed Post or Registered Post only.

Q2: What is the last date to submit the application?

The last date for the receipt of applications at the BECIL office is January 2, 2026, by 18:00 HRS.

Q3: Is there a written examination for all posts?

No, the notification does not specify a mandatory written exam for all posts. The selection will be through shortlisting followed by an interview, assessment, or skill test as deemed fit for the post.

Q4: Is this a permanent government job?

No, all positions are purely on a contract basis for deployment in a Central Government Hospital in Delhi NCR.

entry.title: Jharkhand State Pollution Control Board Recruitment 2026 for 39 JRF, MTS & Other Posts
entry.content:
Jharkhand State Pollution Control Board Recruitment 2026 for 39 JRF, MTS & Other Posts

Advertisement No. 01/2026: The Jharkhand State Pollution Control Board (JSPCB), Ranchi, has announced a recruitment drive for 39 contractual positions to support the "Namami Gange" programme. Applications are invited for posts such as Junior Research Fellow (JRF), Junior Lab Assistant (JLA), Lab Attendant, Field Attendant, and MTS. The application process is entirely offline, with no application fee. The last date to submit the hard copy of the application is February 06, 2026.

Post Name: Junior Research Fellow, Junior Lab Assistant, Lab Attendant, Field Attendant, MTS
Last Date: 06 February 2026
Total Posts: 39

The Jharkhand State Pollution Control Board (JSPCB) is seeking qualified and dedicated individuals to join its team for the prestigious "Namami Gange" project, a flagship initiative of the Government of India. This recruitment aims to fill 39 vacancies on a purely contractual basis to strengthen the board's laboratories and fieldwork capabilities. The engagement is temporary and project-driven, initially for a period of 12 months, with the possibility of extension based on performance and project duration.

This is an excellent opportunity for candidates with qualifications ranging from 10th Standard to M.Sc. with NET/GATE to gain valuable experience in the environmental sector. The selection process will be based on the shortlisting of candidates from their educational qualifications and work experience, followed by an interview. A significant advantage for applicants is that there is no application fee for any of the posts. Interested candidates must submit their applications in the prescribed format via post or by hand before the deadline.

JSPCB Recruitment 2026 (Important Points)

Candidates must carefully review the key dates and details to ensure a successful application. This is a contractual opportunity with no application fee.

Particulars Details
Recruitment Organization Jharkhand State Pollution Control Board (JSPCB)
Project Name Namami Gange Programme
Advertisement Number 01/2026
Post Names Junior Research Fellow, Lab Assistant, MTS, Attendant etc.
Total Vacancies 39
Application Start Date 15 January 2026
Application Last Date 06 February 2026
Application Mode Offline (Post or Hand Delivery)
Application Fee NIL (No Fee)
Job Type Contractual (Government Project)
Job Location Anywhere in Jharkhand
Official Website www.jspcb.org.in
Submission Address The Member Secretary, Jharkhand State Pollution Control Board, T.A. Division Building, Dhurwa, Ranchi – 834004

Vacancy and Emoluments Details

A total of 39 posts are available across four key activity areas. The detailed breakdown is as follows:

Activity / Post Name Vacancies Qualification Monthly Emoluments (+ HRA as per Govt. rules) Age Limit
1. Microbiological and Biological Monitoring and Analysis
Junior Research Fellow (JRF) 04 M.Sc. (Zoology/Microbiology/Env. Science) + NET/GATE ₹37,000/- 32 years
Junior Lab Assistant (JLA) 04 B.Sc. ₹27,000/- 32 years
Lab Attendant (LA) 04 10th Standard ₹24,000/- 30 years
2. Physico-chemical Analysis
Junior Research Fellow (JRF) 10 M.Sc. (Chemistry/Other allied Sciences) + NET/GATE ₹37,000/- 32 years
Junior Lab Assistant (JLA) 06 B.Sc. ₹27,000/- 32 years
Lab Attendant (LA) 05 10th / 12th ₹24,000/- 30 years
3. Fieldwork
Field Attendant 03 10th / 12th ₹24,000/- 30 years
4. Data Management
MTS 03 B.Sc. / Computer ₹27,000/- 30 years
Total 39
Note: The age limit will be reckoned as on the last date of application submission (06/02/2026). Age relaxation for reserved categories will be applicable as per government norms.

Application Fee

  • There is NO APPLICATION FEE for any of the posts. The application process is completely free for all candidates.

Eligibility Criteria

Apart from the educational qualifications and age limits mentioned above, candidates must fulfill the following criteria:

Criteria Details
Nationality Must be a citizen of India.
Language Proficiency Must be able to speak, read, and write Hindi/English or both.
Computer Knowledge Basic computer knowledge is essential for all posts.
Physical Fitness Candidates must be in good physical and mental condition, free from any physical disorder.

Selection Process

The selection process for this recruitment is straightforward and does not involve a written examination. The stages are as follows:

  1. Application Scrutiny and Shortlisting: Applications received will be scrutinized. Candidates will be shortlisted based on their educational qualifications and relevant work experience.
  2. Interview / Further Process: Only the shortlisted candidates will be intimated and called for the next stage of the selection process, which will likely be a personal interview.
  3. Document Verification: Original documents of the candidates will be verified at the time of the interview.

Exam Pattern

The selection process for this recruitment does not involve a written examination. The selection will be based on shortlisting from applications followed by a personal interview for the eligible candidates. The interview will likely focus on assessing the candidate's:

  • Domain knowledge relevant to the post (e.g., questions on microbiology for JRF, lab procedures for JLA).
  • Practical skills and understanding of their field.
  • Suitability for the project and the specific job description.
  • Basic computer proficiency.

PET (Physical Efficiency Test)

There is no Physical Efficiency Test (PET) or Physical Standard Test (PST) for any of the posts in this recruitment drive. However, candidates must be in good physical and mental condition as per the general instructions.

How to Prepare for the Selection

As the selection is based on shortlisting and interview, preparation should be focused on presenting your credentials effectively.

  • Craft a Strong Application: Fill out the application form (Annexure-I) neatly and accurately in capital letters. Ensure your educational and experience details are highlighted clearly.
  • Revise Core Subjects: Brush up on the key concepts from your M.Sc., B.Sc., or other qualifying examinations. Be prepared to answer technical questions during the interview.
  • Prepare for the Interview: Research the "Namami Gange" project and the role of the Pollution Control Board. Practice answering common interview questions related to your skills, experience, and career goals.
  • Organize Your Documents: Keep all original certificates and their self-attested photocopies ready. Ensure your photo ID proof is available for verification.

Best Books for Preparation

Since there is no written test, candidates should refer to their academic textbooks and standard reference books in their respective fields to prepare for the interview.

  • For JRF (Microbiology/Env. Science): Refer to standard postgraduate textbooks like "Prescott's Microbiology" or books on Environmental Science by Gilbert M. Masters.
  • For JRF (Chemistry): Standard M.Sc. level books on Analytical Chemistry and Instrumental Methods of Analysis.
  • For JLA/MTS: Revise concepts from your B.Sc. curriculum and practice basic computer skills (MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint).

How to Apply

The application process is completely offline. Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Visit the official JSPCB website at www.jspcb.org.in to download the detailed advertisement and the application format (Annexure-I).
  2. Fill out the application form completely and correctly in Capital/Block Letters.
  3. Gather self-attested photocopies of all required certificates (educational, experience, caste, etc.).
  4. Place the duly filled-in application form and all the document photocopies in a sealed envelope.
  5. Superscribe the envelope with "Application for the Post of [Your Post Name]" and the relevant activity.
  6. Send the application via Post or submit it by hand to the following address, ensuring it reaches on or before 06/02/2026.

Submission Address:
The Member Secretary,
Jharkhand State Pollution Control Board,
T.A. Division Building, Dhurwa,
Ranchi – 834004, Jharkhand

Documents Needed to Apply

Candidates must attach self-attested photocopies of the following documents along with the application form:

  1. All educational certificates and mark sheets (10th onwards).
  2. NET/GATE qualification certificate (for JRF posts).
  3. Work experience certificates (if any).
  4. Caste Certificate (if claiming reservation).
  5. A valid Photo ID Proof.
  6. Any other relevant supporting documents.

Important Links

Action Link
Official Notification & Application Form Click Here
Official Website Click Here

(FAQs)

Q1: Is there any application fee for this recruitment?

No, there is no application fee for any of the posts. The application is free for all candidates.

Q2: Is this a permanent government job?

No, this is a purely temporary and contractual appointment for the "Namami Gange" project. It does not guarantee any claim for future regularization or permanent employment.

Q3: What is the mode of application?

The application mode is strictly offline. You must send the hard copy of the filled-in application form and documents by post or deliver it by hand to the JSPCB office in Ranchi.

Q4: I want to apply for two different posts. Should I submit one application or two?

You must fill and submit separate application forms for each post you wish to apply for.

entry.title: CGPSC State Service Exam 2025: 238 Posts Open, Applications Started
entry.content:
CGPSC State Service Exam 2025: 238 Posts Open, Applications Started

The Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission (CGPSC) has issued Advertisement No. 06/2025/Exam. A golden opportunity for graduates to secure government jobs in various services including State Administrative Service and State Police Service.

The Chhattisgarh Public Service Commission (CGPSC) has initiated the recruitment process for the State Service Examination for the year 2025. The commission has invited online applications from eligible Indian citizens to fill a total of 238 vacant posts in various services, including Deputy Collector, Deputy Superintendent of Police, and Naib Tehsildar.

This recruitment is a fantastic opportunity for graduate candidates who wish to build a career in the prestigious services of the state of Chhattisgarh. The online registration process has commenced on December 1, 2025, and candidates can apply until December 30, 2025. The preliminary examination is scheduled to be held on February 22, 2026. The selection will be conducted in three stages - Preliminary Examination, Main Examination, and Interview. This comprehensive guide will walk you through every detail of this major recruitment drive.

Recruitment Overview (Important Points)

ParticularsInformation
Organization NameChhattisgarh Public Service Commission (CGPSC)
Advertisement Number06/2025/Exam
Post NameVarious Posts of State Service
Total Posts238
Job LocationChhattisgarh
Application ModeOnline
Application Dates01.12.2025 to 30.12.2025
Official Websitewww.psc.cg.gov.in

Important Dates

Event Date and Time
Online Application StartsDecember 1, 2025 (from 12:00 Noon)
Last Date for Online ApplicationDecember 30, 2025 (till 11:59 PM)
Error Correction (Free)December 31, 2025 to January 2, 2026
Error Correction (Paid)January 3, 2026 to January 5, 2026 (Fee ₹500/-)
Preliminary Examination DateFebruary 22, 2026
Tentative Main Examination DateMay 16, 17, 18 & 19, 2026

Vacancy Details and Pay Scale

The department-wise breakdown of the total 238 posts is as follows:

Sr. No. Post and Department Name Total Vacancies Pay Matrix Level
1State Administrative Service - Deputy Collector14₹56,100 (Level-12)
2State Police Service - Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP)28₹56,100 (Level-12)
3Chhattisgarh State Finance Service Officer02₹56,100 (Level-12)
4State Tax Assistant Commissioner10₹56,100 (Level-12)
5Labour Officer02₹56,100 (Level-12)
6Employment Officer03 + 01 (Backlog)₹56,100 (Level-12)
7Assistant Director/District Women & Child Development Officer04₹56,100 (Level-12)
8District Registrar03₹56,100 (Level-12)
9Chief Municipal Officer 'B' Class18₹56,100 (Level-12)
10Chief Municipal Officer 'C' Class29 + 04 (Backlog)₹38,100 (Level-9)
11Child Development Project Officer05₹38,100 (Level-9)
12Chhattisgarh Subordinate Accounts Service Officer15 + 01 (Backlog)₹38,100 (Level-9)
13Naib Tehsildar51 + 02 (Backlog)₹35,400 (Level-8)
14State Tax Inspector16 + 01 (Backlog)₹28,700 (Level-7)
15Excise Sub Inspector11₹28,700 (Level-7)
16Deputy Registrar12₹28,700 (Level-7)
17Assistant Jail Superintendent06₹25,300 (Level-6)
Grand Total238

Eligibility Criteria: Education, Age, and Physical Standards

Educational Qualification

The candidate must possess a Bachelor's Degree (Graduate) or an equivalent qualification from any of the universities incorporated by an Act of the Central or State Legislature in India or other educational institutions established by an Act of Parliament or declared to be deemed as a University under Section 3 of the University Grants Commission Act, 1956.

Important Tip:
  • Candidates who have appeared or will be appearing in an examination, the passing of which would render them educationally qualified for the Commission's examination but have not been informed of the results, will also be eligible for admission to the Preliminary Examination.
  • It will be mandatory for such candidates who are declared qualified by the Commission for the State Service Main Examination to submit proof of passing the requisite examination along with their application for the Main Examination.

Age Limit (as on 01.01.2025)

For Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP):

  • Minimum Age: 21 years
  • Maximum Age: 28 years

For Other Posts:

  • Minimum Age: 21 years
  • Maximum Age: 30 years (35 years for domiciles of Chhattisgarh)
  • For Chhattisgarh Government Employees: 38 years

Age Relaxation (for local residents of Chhattisgarh):

  • SC/ST/OBC: 5 years
  • Female Candidates: 10 years
  • Widows/Divorcees: 5 years
  • Persons with Disabilities: 5 years
  • Ex-Servicemen: 5 years
  • Award-winning Sportspersons: 5 years
Note: The maximum age limit after combining all relaxations shall not exceed 45 years.

Physical Standards for DSP

Physical measurements are mandatory for the post of Deputy Superintendent of Police:

Standard Male Female
Height168 cm or more155 cm or more
Chest (Unexpanded)84 cmNot Applicable
Chest (Expanded)89 cmNot Applicable
Minimum Expansion5 cmNot Applicable

Selection Process: A Three-Tiered Examination

The selection process for the CGPSC State Service Exam is a rigorous three-stage process designed to select the best candidates for the state's administrative machinery.

1. Preliminary Examination (Objective Type)

  • Purpose: Screening Test to select candidates for the Main Examination.
  • Total Papers: 2 (Both Multiple Choice Questions)
  • Each Paper: 100 questions, 200 marks, 2 hours duration.
  • Negative Marking: 1/3rd of the marks assigned to a question will be deducted for each wrong answer.
Paper Subject Questions Marks Nature
Paper-IGeneral Studies100200Merit-Based
Paper-IIAptitude Test100200Qualifying (33% for UR, 23% for Reserved)

2. Main Examination (Conventional/Written)

Total Papers: 7 (All are descriptive type)

Paper Subject Marks Duration
Paper-ILanguage (Hindi, English, Chhattisgarhi)2003 Hours
Paper-IIEssay2003 Hours
Paper-IIIGeneral Studies-I (History, Constitution, Public Admin)2003 Hours
Paper-IVGeneral Studies-II (General Science, Aptitude, Reasoning)2003 Hours
Paper-VGeneral Studies-III (Economy, Geography of India & CG)2003 Hours
Paper-VIGeneral Studies-IV (Philosophy, Sociology)2003 Hours
Paper-VIIGeneral Studies-V (Welfare, Laws, Sports, Organizations)2003 Hours
Total Written Examination1400

3. Interview / Personality Test

  • Marks: 100
  • Total Marks (Mains + Interview): 1500

How to Prepare for the CGPSC State Service Exam

Success in the CGPSC State Service Examination requires a well-planned and disciplined strategy. Here are some crucial tips:

For the Preliminary Examination:

  • General Studies: Focus on NCERT books (Class 6-12), current affairs, and specific information about Chhattisgarh. Keep an eye on both national and state-level events.
  • Aptitude Test: Practice logical reasoning, mathematics (Class 10 level), Hindi grammar, and the Chhattisgarhi language.
  • Negative Marking: Attempt only those questions you are sure about. Avoid guesswork.
  • Mock Tests: Regularly take mock tests and practice time management.

For the Main Examination:

  • Language Paper: Prepare thoroughly for Hindi grammar, essay writing, and the English & Chhattisgarhi languages.
  • Essay: Practice writing on a variety of topics. Focus on problem-solving and providing a balanced view.
  • General Studies: Make separate notes for each subject. Prepare with a special focus on the context of Chhattisgarh.
  • Answer Writing: Write clear, concise, and factual answers. Use data, diagrams, and examples to support your points.
  • Current Affairs: Pay special attention to current events in Chhattisgarh along with national and international news.

How to Apply Online

  1. Visit the Website: Go to www.psc.cg.gov.in.
  2. Registration: New candidates should click on "Candidate's Registration". Fill in your name, father's name, date of birth, mobile number, and email ID.
  3. OTP Verification: Enter the OTPs received on your mobile and email.
  4. Live Photo: Capture your live photo using a mobile camera.
  5. Upload Signature: Upload your scanned signature (.JPG, max 100KB).
  6. Fill the Form: Log in and fill in personal details, educational qualifications, post preferences, etc.
  7. Upload Documents: Upload the required certificates by scanning them.
  8. Fee Payment: Pay the fee through online modes (UPI, Card, Net Banking).
  9. Print Application: After submitting the form, take a printout of the receipt for your records.
Important: After submission, the Application Status should show "Submitted" and the Payment Status should show "Paid".

Important Links

Resource Link
Apply Online (Direct Link) Click Here
Official Advertisement (PDF) Download Here
Exam Scheme and Syllabus Download Here
Official Website Click Here

FAQs - CGPSC State Service Exam 2025

Question 1: Can final-year students apply?
Yes, final-year students can appear for the preliminary examination, but they must provide proof of passing the degree along with the application for the Main Examination.
Question 2: Can candidates from other states apply?
Yes, but they will be considered only under the unreserved category and will not get any age relaxation. Reservation benefits are only for the domiciles of Chhattisgarh.
Question 3: Is there negative marking in the preliminary exam?
Yes, 1/3rd of the marks (0.33) will be deducted for each wrong answer.
Question 4: How many candidates will be selected for the Main Examination?
Candidates equal to 15 times the number of advertised posts will be selected for the Main Examination (approximately 3570 candidates).
Question 5: Will the examination fee be refunded?
Yes, for local residents of Chhattisgarh who appear in the examination/interview, the examination fee will be refunded. The portal fee will not be refunded.
Disclaimer: This information is based on Advertisement No. 06/2025/Exam. Candidates are advised to read the full advertisement and rules on the official website www.psc.cg.gov.in before applying. In case of any dispute, the decision of the Commission will be final.

entry.title: Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2025: Direct Entry for Outstanding Sportspersons
entry.content:
Indian Army Sports Quota Recruitment 2025: Direct Entry for Outstanding Sportspersons

Elite Career Opportunities for International and National Level Athletes in the Ranks of Havildar and Naib Subedar

Post Name: Direct Havildar and Naib Subedar (Sports Quota)
Last Date: Refer to Official ZRO/Aizawl Notification
Total Posts: Merit-Based (Across Multiple Sports Disciplines)

The Indian Army has always been a staunch supporter of sporting talent in India. Through its "Mission Olympic" program and various regimental sports nodes, the Army has produced world-class athletes who have brought home medals from the Olympics, Commonwealth Games, and Asian Games. To continue this legacy of excellence, the Indian Army has officially launched the Sports Quota Recruitment 2025. This specialized recruitment drive is designed for unmarried male candidates who have demonstrated exceptional skills and achieved significant milestones at the national or international levels. Unlike regular recruitment, this drive offers a direct pathway to the senior non-commissioned officer (NCO) and junior commissioned officer (JCO) ranks of Havildar and Naib Subedar.

Working in the Indian Army as a sportsperson is a matter of immense prestige. It allows athletes to maintain their rigorous training schedules with world-class facilities while wearing the coveted olive green uniform. The 2025 recruitment cycle targets athletes from a wide variety of disciplines, including Athletics, Boxing, Wrestling, Hockey, Shooting, and Archery, among others. The selection process is strictly merit-oriented, focusing on Sports Skill Trials and physical fitness rather than a traditional written examination. Successful candidates are provided with permanent service, attractive pay scales as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC), and dedicated time for their sporting pursuits. The application process is primarily offline, requiring candidates to submit their credentials directly to the designated sports centers or regimental nodes.

The Indian Army's commitment to its athletes includes providing expert coaching, specialized nutrition, and medical support to ensure they reach their peak performance. For an athlete, joining the Army means job security for life and the honor of representing the nation both on the battlefield and in the sporting arena. This 2000-word comprehensive guide provides an exhaustive look at the regional physical standards, the specific sporting achievements required for different ranks, the intricate selection process, and strategic advice for the upcoming trials. For any young man who excels in sports and dreams of serving the nation, this guide is the first step toward a fulfilling military career.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Information

Candidates must be highly attentive to the timeline and regional specifics of the Indian Army recruitment. Since sports quota entries are managed by specialized boards, the dates may vary slightly depending on the specific sport and the recruiting zone. It is vital to keep all original sports certificates ready, as they are the primary basis for shortlisting.

Recruitment Feature Dates and Details
Organization Name Indian Army (Ministry of Defence)
Entry Type Direct Entry Sports Quota
Rank Offered Havildar (NCO) and Naib Subedar (JCO)
Gender Eligibility Unmarried Male Candidates Only
Application Mode Offline (By Post to Specified Nodes)
Selection Method Trials, PFT, PMT, and Medical Board
Official Website joinindianarmy.nic.in

Discipline Wise Vacancies and Pay Scale

The number of vacancies in the Indian Army Sports Quota is dynamic and depends on the requirements of the various sports teams of the Army. Candidates are selected based on their potential to win medals for the Army and the country. The pay and allowances are among the best in the public sector.

Post Designation Rank Category Pay Scale (7th CPC)
Naib Subedar Junior Commissioned Officer (JCO) Level 6 (₹35,400 - ₹1,12,400)
Havildar Non-Commissioned Officer (NCO) Level 5 (₹29,200 - ₹92,300)

Additional Benefits: Apart from the basic pay, Army personnel receive Military Service Pay (MSP), Dearness Allowance (DA), specialized sports allowances, free rations, free medical facilities for family, Canteen (CSD) facilities, and highly concessional travel through the Indian Railways. Permanent personnel are also eligible for the pension benefits as per current government policy.

Eligibility Criteria and Sports Achievements

The eligibility for direct entry as Havildar or Naib Subedar is very specific. The Indian Army looks for high-achievers who have already proven their mettle in competitive arenas. Academic qualification is a secondary requirement compared to sporting excellence.

  • Educational Qualification: Minimum Matriculation (10th Standard) pass from a recognized education board.
  • Age Limit: Candidates must be between 17.5 to 25 years of age. The exact cut-off date will be mentioned in the regional notification.
  • Marital Status: Only Unmarried Male candidates are eligible to apply.

Achievements for Naib Subedar Entry

Direct entry into the Naib Subedar rank is reserved for elite athletes who meet at least one of the following criteria:

  • Represented India in Olympic Games (Senior Category).
  • Winner of a medal in World Championships, Asian Games, or Commonwealth Games.
  • Represented India in World Cups or Asian Championships (Senior Category) and achieved a top-eight finish.

Achievements for Havildar Entry

Candidates with the following achievements are eligible for the rank of Havildar:

  • Represented India in Asian or Commonwealth Championships (Junior/Senior).
  • Winner of a Gold, Silver, or Bronze medal in a recognized National Championship (Senior Category).
  • Individual medal winner in All India Inter-University Tournaments.
  • Medal winner in the National School Games conducted by the School Games Federation of India (SGFI).

Selection Process and Evaluation Stages

The selection process for the Army Sports Quota is distinct from the regular Agniveer recruitment. It is designed to evaluate technical sporting prowess and physical robustness over multiple phases.

  1. Shortlisting of Applications: Applications are screened based on the level of sports achievement. Only those with high-priority certificates (International/National medals) are called for trials.
  2. Sports Skill Trials: This is the most important part of the selection. Candidates are tested on their technique, tactical awareness, and match-playing ability by a board of specialist sports officers and coaches.
  3. Physical Fitness Test (PFT): Candidates who clear the trials must pass the standard Army physical test, which includes a 1.6km run, pull-ups, and balance tests.
  4. Physical Measurement Test (PMT): Verification of height, chest, and weight as per the regional standards.
  5. Medical Examination: A comprehensive check-up by a Military Medical Board to ensure the candidate meets the medical standards of the Indian Army.

Physical Standards and Regional Height Requirements

The Indian Army maintains different height standards for different regions of India to account for the diverse physical builds of the population. Candidates must meet the minimum requirement of their respective home state.

Region Name States and Areas Included Min. Height (cm)
Western Himalayan J&K, Himachal Pradesh, Punjab Hills, Garhwal & Kumaon (UK) 163
Eastern Himalayan Sikkim, Nagaland, Arunachal, Manipur, Tripura, Mizoram, Meghalaya, Assam, Darjeeling 160
Western Plains Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, Delhi, Rajasthan, Western UP 170
Eastern Plains Eastern UP, Bihar, West Bengal, Jharkhand, Odisha 169
Central Plains Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, Gujarat, Maharashtra, D&N Haveli 168
Southern Plains Andhra, Telangana, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Goa, Puducherry 166
Gorkhas & Ladakhis Indian and Nepalese Gorkhas and Ladakhi candidates 157

Chest and Weight: The minimum chest measurement for all candidates is 77 cm with an expansion of 5 cm. Weight must be in proportion to height and age as per the standard Army medical charts. Special concessions may be given to outstanding sportspersons as per the prevailing policy.

Physical Fitness Test (PFT) Details

The PFT is qualifying in nature but mandatory for all ranks. Even for elite athletes, there is no compromise on the basic fitness required to serve in the Army.

  • 1.6 KM Run: To be completed within a specified time (usually within 5 minutes 45 seconds for Group I).
  • Pull Ups (Beams): Minimum 6 repetitions are required to pass, while 10 repetitions provide maximum marks.
  • 9 Feet Ditch: A jump across a 9-foot trench, which must be cleared in one attempt.
  • Zig-Zag Balance: Walking across a narrow zig-zag rail to test balance and coordination.

Expert Preparation Strategy for Sports Trials

Success in the Indian Army sports trials requires more than just raw talent; it requires professional discipline and awareness of the Army’s testing standards.

Maintain Peak Conditioning: The Army trials are often held over multiple days. You must be in your best physical shape. Focus on your specific sport technique while also building the cardiovascular endurance required for the 1.6km run. Since there is no written exam, your performance in the trials is 100% of your merit.

Documentation Accuracy: The Army is very strict about sports certificates. Ensure your certificates are issued by recognized federations (National Federation or SGFI). If your certificates are not verifiable, you will be disqualified instantly. Keep clear, self-attested copies of all your achievements in a sequential folder.

Psychological Readiness: During the personal interaction or interview phase of the trials, the board looks for a disciplined military temperament. Practice answering questions about your sporting career with confidence and clarity. Be prepared to explain your training routine and your goals for representing the Indian Army in future competitions.

Best Books and Resources for Preparation

While there is no academic book for trials, being aware of the Army’s structure and current sports affairs is helpful for the final board interview.

General Awareness: Manorama Yearbook (Sports Section) and daily sports news updates.
Army Knowledge: Basic history of the Indian Army and the "Mission Olympic" program details.
Fitness Guidelines: Standard Army physical training manuals available on official websites.
Diet & Nutrition: High-performance sports nutrition guides to maintain weight and energy during trials.

How to Apply Offline: Step-by-Step Guide

The application for the sports quota is typically conducted through an offline process. Follow these steps meticulously to ensure your application reaches the correct sports node.

  1. Download Advertisement: Visit joinindianarmy.nic.in and look for the "Sports Quota Recruitment" notification for the current year.
  2. Fill the Form: Download the prescribed application format. Fill it in BLOCK LETTERS using a blue ballpoint pen.
  3. Attach Photographs: Paste one recent passport-size photograph on the form and attach two extra photos with the application. Photos should be clear and taken without a cap or sunglasses.
  4. Compile Documents: Attach self-attested copies of your 10th-standard marksheet, sports certificates, domicile certificate, and character certificate.
  5. Address the Envelope: Use a sturdy envelope and write on top: "APPLICATION FOR SPORTS QUOTA RECRUITMENT - [RANK] - [SPORT DISCIPLINE]".
  6. Post the Application: Send the envelope via Registered Post or Speed Post to the address mentioned in the specific regional notification (e.g., Army Sports Institute, Pune or the specific Regimental Centre).
Important Advice: Ensure your application is dispatched early. The Army does not take responsibility for postal delays. Applications received after the deadline will be strictly rejected.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keep these self-attested documents ready. Original documents should be carried only during the physical trial and verification stage.

  1. Matriculation (10th) Certificate: For proof of age and educational qualification.
  2. Sports Certificates: Original and photocopies of all National and International level achievements.
  3. Domicile Certificate: Issued by the competent authority (CO/SDO/Tehsildar) of your state.
  4. Character Certificate: Issued by the School Principal or Village Sarpanch within the last six months.
  5. Aadhaar Card: Essential for identity and biometric verification.
  6. Unmarried Certificate: Signed by the village head or district official.

Important Links

Resource Description Official Link
Join Indian Army Official Portal joinindianarmy.nic.in
Sports Recruitment Node (Pune) Army Sports Institute
Notification Download Link Download Here

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can women athletes apply for this sports quota recruitment?

As per the current 2025 notification for direct Havildar and Naib Subedar entry, the vacancies are open for Male candidates only. Female athletes should check for separate notifications under the Army’s "Corps of Military Police" or Agniveer (Women) schemes.

Q2: Is there any written examination for Havildar and Naib Subedar sports entry?

No. One of the major benefits of this entry is that there is no written competitive exam. The selection is based entirely on your sporting achievements, technical trials, and physical/medical fitness.

Q3: What is the age relaxation for outstanding athletes?

The age limit is strictly 17.5 to 25 years. However, in extremely rare cases of exceptional international talent, the Army may provide a special age waiver through a high-level committee, but this is at the total discretion of the headquarters.

Q4: What happens if I am injured during the trials?

The Indian Army provides basic first aid during the trials. However, if an athlete suffers a significant injury that prevents them from completing the trials or PFT, they may be disqualified for that cycle. Athletes are advised to arrive at the trials in peak injury-free condition.

Q5: Is this a permanent job or like the 4-year Agniveer scheme?

The Direct Entry for Havildar and Naib Subedar under the Sports Quota is a Regular/Permanent commission. It is not part of the short-term Agniveer scheme, providing a full-term career until the retirement age of the respective rank.

Q6: Are medals in State level championships sufficient for Havildar entry?

No. Typically, the Indian Army requires at least a medal in a National Level Championship or representation in an International event. State-level achievements are good for your profile but are usually not enough for direct NCO/JCO entry.

entry.title: EMRS भर्ती 2025: 7200+ टीचिंग एवं नॉन-टीचिंग पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!
entry.content:
EMRS भर्ती 2025: 7200+ टीचिंग एवं नॉन-टीचिंग पदों पर बंपर भर्ती!

देशभर के एकलव्य मॉडल रेज़िडेंशियल स्कूलों (EMRS) में Principal, PGT, TGT एवं अन्य पदों पर भर्ती का सुनहरा अवसर।

नमस्कार दोस्तों! National Education Society for Tribal Students (NESTS), जो कि Ministry of Tribal Affairs के अंतर्गत आती है, ने EMRS Staff Selection Exam (ESSE)-2025 के लिए एक बड़ी भर्ती अधिसूचना जारी की है। इस भर्ती अभियान के तहत देशभर के एकलव्य मॉडल रेज़िडेंशियल स्कूलों में लगभग 7,267 पदों पर नियुक्तियाँ की जाएँगी।

यह भर्ती अनुसूचित जनजाति (ST) वर्ग के छात्रों को गुणवत्तापूर्ण शिक्षा प्रदान करने की दिशा में एक महत्वपूर्ण कदम है। यहाँ हम आपको सभी आवश्यक जानकारी जैसे — पदों का विवरण, पात्रता, परीक्षा योजना, एवं आवेदन प्रक्रिया विस्तार से बताएँगे।

EMRS भर्ती 2025: पदों का विवरण

ESSE-2025 के अंतर्गत एकलव्य मॉडल स्कूलों में निम्नलिखित पदों पर भर्ती की जाएगी:

पद का नामपदों की संख्या
Principal (Group-A)225
Post Graduate Teachers (PGTs)1460
Trained Graduate Teachers (TGTs)3962
Female Staff Nurse550
Hostel Warden635
Accountant61
Junior Secretariat Assistant (JSA)228
Lab Attendant146
कुल7267
नोट: ये पद अनुमानित हैं और इनमें बदलाव संभव है। NESTS आवश्यकतानुसार पद बढ़ा या घटा सकता है।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ

घटनातिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि23 अक्टूबर 2025 (रात 11:50 बजे तक)
पात्रता के लिए कट-ऑफ तिथि23 अक्टूबर 2025
एडमिट कार्ड डाउनलोडजल्द अधिसूचित किया जाएगा
परीक्षा तिथि (Tier-I & II)जल्द अधिसूचित किया जाएगा

आवेदन एवं प्रोसेसिंग शुल्क

महिला, SC, ST और PwBD उम्मीदवारों को आवेदन शुल्क में छूट है, लेकिन प्रोसेसिंग शुल्क देना अनिवार्य है।

पदGen/OBC/EWSमहिला/SC/ST/PwBD
Principal₹2500 (2000 + 500)₹500
PGT & TGT₹2000 (1500 + 500)₹500
Non-Teaching Staff₹1500 (1000 + 500)₹500

पात्रता मानदंड — आयु सीमा एवं योग्यता

पदवेतन स्तरआयु सीमाअनिवार्य योग्यता
PrincipalLevel 1250 वर्ष तकमास्टर डिग्री (50% अंक) + B.Ed + 12 वर्ष का अनुभव।
PGTLevel 840 वर्ष तकसंबंधित विषय में मास्टर डिग्री (50%) + B.Ed (Computer Science को छोड़कर अनिवार्य)।
TGTLevel 735 वर्ष तकस्नातक (50%) + B.Ed + CTET Paper-II पास।
Hostel WardenLevel 535 वर्ष तकNCERT से 4-वर्षीय इंटीग्रेटेड कोर्स या मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक।
JSALevel 230 वर्ष तक12वीं उत्तीर्ण + इंग्लिश में 35 wpm या हिंदी में 30 wpm टाइपिंग स्पीड।
Lab AttendantLevel 130 वर्ष तक10वीं पास + लैब तकनीक प्रमाणपत्र या 12वीं विज्ञान विषय से।
आयु में छूट: SC/ST – 5 वर्ष, OBC-NCL – 3 वर्ष, PwBD – 15 वर्ष, महिला उम्मीदवारों को विशेष छूट।

चयन प्रक्रिया एवं परीक्षा योजना

  1. Tier-I (प्रारंभिक परीक्षा): OMR आधारित ऑब्जेक्टिव टेस्ट।
  2. Tier-II (विषय ज्ञान परीक्षा): ऑब्जेक्टिव + डिस्क्रिप्टिव प्रश्न।
  3. Tier-III (साक्षात्कार/स्किल टेस्ट): Principal हेतु 40 अंक का इंटरव्यू, JSA हेतु टाइपिंग टेस्ट।

मार्किंग: सही उत्तर = 1 अंक, गलत उत्तर पर 1/3 अंक कटेगा।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया (Online Apply)

  1. Step 1: NESTS वेबसाइट https://nests.tribal.gov.in पर जाएँ।
  2. Step 2: ‘Recruitment’ सेक्शन में ESSE-2025 लिंक चुनें।
  3. Step 3: सूचना बुलेटिन ध्यानपूर्वक पढ़ें।
  4. Step 4: रजिस्ट्रेशन करें और लॉगिन आईडी प्राप्त करें।
  5. Step 5: ऑनलाइन फॉर्म भरें और फोटो/सिग्नेचर अपलोड करें।
  6. Step 6: शुल्क का भुगतान ऑनलाइन करें।
  7. Step 7: आवेदन सबमिट करने से पहले जाँचें और प्रिंट लें।

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक

लिंक शीर्षककार्यवाही
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें
अधिसूचना PDFडाउनलोड करें
आधिकारिक वेबसाइटदेखें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

प्रश्न 1: क्या यह स्थायी सरकारी नौकरी है?
हाँ, चयनित कर्मचारी नियमित वेतनमान पर नियुक्त होंगे और 2 वर्ष की प्रोबेशन अवधि के बाद स्थायी माने जाएँगे।
प्रश्न 2: क्या एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं?
हाँ, पात्र उम्मीदवार एक से अधिक पदों के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं, पर प्रत्येक के लिए अलग शुल्क देना होगा।
प्रश्न 3: क्या सभी टीचिंग पदों के लिए B.Ed आवश्यक है?
Principal, PGT (Computer Science को छोड़कर) एवं TGT पदों के लिए B.Ed आवश्यक है।
प्रश्न 4: क्या पसंदीदा लोकेशन मिलेगी?
नहीं, चयनित उम्मीदवारों की पोस्टिंग भारत में कहीं भी की जा सकती है।

entry.title: Aliah University Recruitment 2025: Apply for Technical Assistant, Instructor, and Group-D Posts
entry.content:
Aliah University Non-Teaching Recruitment 2025: Notification for Direct Vacancies Out

Join Aliah University, Kolkata - A Premier Autonomous Institution under the Government of West Bengal - Apply for Group A, B, and C Posts

Post Name: Technical Assistant, Clinical Instructor, Physical Training Instructor, Compositor, Group-D
Last Date: December 01, 2025 (Online), December 04, 2025 (Hard Copy)
Total Posts: 08 Vacancies (Advt AU/Apptt.-2/2025)

Aliah University, located in the heart of Kolkata, West Bengal, is a prestigious state-aided autonomous university under the Department of Minority Affairs and Madrasah Education, Government of West Bengal. Known for its rich heritage and modern approach to professional education, the university has three campuses across New Town and Park Circus. To support its expanding technical and administrative requirements, Aliah University has officially released its latest recruitment notification for the year 2025 under Advertisement Number AU/Apptt.-2/2025. This drive seeks to recruit dedicated and qualified Indian citizens for various Non-Teaching positions including Technical Assistant (Grade-I), Clinical Instructor, Physical Training Instructor, Compositor, and Group-D staff.

Working at Aliah University represents a significant opportunity for those looking for a stable Government of West Bengal autonomous career. The roles advertised are essential for the smooth functioning of specialized departments such as Electrical Engineering and Nursing. The university is looking for candidates who possess not only the required academic qualifications but also relevant industrial or clinical experience to contribute effectively to its academic mission. The recruitment process is unique and requires a two-step commitment: candidates must first complete an online application by December 1, 2025, followed by the submission of a physical hard copy of the application by December 4, 2025. Failure to complete both steps will lead to the rejection of the candidature.

The 2025 notification emphasizes merit and transparency, with pay scales governed by the West Bengal state government’s pay levels (ROPA 2019). Whether you are a technical expert in electrical systems, a nursing professional, or an administrative aspirant, Aliah University provides an environment of professional growth and job security. This exhaustive 2000-word guide is designed to provide you with an in-depth analysis of the eligibility criteria, the intricate details of the application process, salary matrix structures, and strategic advice to help you navigate this competitive selection process successfully.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must be highly attentive to the timeline of this recruitment. Since Aliah University follows a hybrid application model (Online + Offline), ignoring the hard copy deadline is a common reason for application disqualification. Candidates currently employed in government sectors must also account for the time required to obtain a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their current employers.

Recruitment Feature Key Dates and Details
Official Notification Number AU/Apptt.-2/2025
Online Registration Opens November 14, 2025
Last Date for Online Application December 01, 2025 (11:59 PM)
Last Date for Receipt of Hard Copy December 04, 2025 (05:00 PM)
Age Calculation Cut-off Date December 01, 2025
Job Nature Permanent Basis (Autonomous State Govt)
Job Location Kolkata (New Town / Park Circus)

Detailed Vacancy Breakdown and Salary Matrix

The 8 vacancies are distributed across various pay levels based on the technicality and responsibility of the roles. The salary is structured as per the latest West Bengal state pay levels, ensuring that employees receive competitive entry pay along with various allowances such as HRA, DA, and medical benefits.

Post Designation Total Vacancies Pay Level (WB) Entry Basic Pay
Technical Assistant (Grade-I) 01 Level 9 ₹37,100 + Allowances
Clinical Instructor (Nursing) 03 Level 8 ₹35,800 + Allowances
Physical Training Instructor 01 Level 8 ₹35,800 + Allowances
Compositor (Bengali/English) 01 Level 7 ₹32,800 + Allowances
Group-D (Service Staff) 02 Level 1 ₹18,500 + Allowances
Grand Total 08 - -
Allowances Note: In addition to the entry basic pay, selected candidates will be eligible for Dearness Allowance (DA), House Rent Allowance (HRA), and Medical Allowance as per the rules of the Government of West Bengal applicable to autonomous institutions. Employees are also covered under the New Pension Scheme (NPS).

Eligibility Criteria and Educational Qualifications

Aliah University maintains high standards for its technical and clinical staff. It is mandatory for candidates to possess post-qualification experience for the higher-level technical roles. Below is the detailed breakdown of the requirements for each post category.

  • Technical Assistant (Grade-I): Specifically for the Electrical Engineering department. Candidates must have a B.E./B.Tech in Electrical Engineering with 2 years of experience OR a First Class Diploma in Electrical Engineering with 3 years of experience in a reputed laboratory or firm.
  • Clinical Instructor: Specifically for the Nursing department. Requirements: M.Sc Nursing OR B.Sc Nursing with 1 year of experience OR Basic B.Sc Nursing with a Post Basic Diploma in clinical specialty. Candidates must be registered with the State Nursing Council.
  • Physical Training Instructor: Master’s degree in Physical Education (M.P.Ed) with 1 year of experience OR a Bachelor’s degree in Physical Education (B.P.Ed) with 3 years of experience.
  • Compositor: Honors Graduate in English with Bengali as a subject. Candidates must have 5 years of experience as a Junior Assistant or equivalent, a typing speed of 35 WPM, proficiency in MS Office, and experience with Bengali software typing.
  • Group-D: Minimum qualification is a pass in Class VIII from a recognized school. No prior experience is mandatory for this level.

Age Limit: The maximum age limit for all posts is typically below 40 years as of December 1, 2025. Relaxations in the upper age limit are applicable for SC, ST, OBC, and PwD candidates as per the prevailing orders of the Government of West Bengal.

Selection Process and General Instructions

The selection process at Aliah University is designed to filter candidates based on academic merit and technical expertise. The university reserves the right to adopt a multi-tier selection model depending on the number of applications received.

  1. Shortlisting: Based on the data provided in the online form, the university will shortlist candidates whose qualifications and experience best match the requirements. If the application count is high, higher academic cut-offs may be applied.
  2. Personal Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be invited for a personal interview at the New Town campus in Kolkata. For technical posts, a trade test or skill test may precede the interview.
  3. Probation Period: All permanent appointments are subject to a probation period of 02 years, which can be extended by one more year based on performance evaluation.
  4. Document Verification: Candidates must produce all original certificates, experience letters, and category proofs during the interview/document verification stage.

General Instructions: If a candidate wishes to apply for multiple posts, they must fill out a separate online form, pay the separate application fee, and send a separate hard copy envelope for each post. Incomplete applications (e.g., online done but hard copy not sent) will be rejected.

Application Fee and Payment Portal

The application fee is non-refundable and must be paid through the university’s integrated online payment gateway during the online form submission process.

Post Category General Category Fee SC/ST/OBC/PD Fee
Technical Assistant, Clinical Instructor, PTI, Compositor ₹300/- ₹150/-
Group-D Positions ₹200/- ₹100/-

Candidates must ensure that the transaction is successful and save the payment receipt, as the Transaction ID must be mentioned on the hard copy of the application form.

Expert Preparation Strategy for Aliah University

Securing a position in a state university requires a blend of professional knowledge and institutional awareness. Since the interview is a key component, candidates should focus on the following roadmap.

Subject Core Competency: For Technical Assistants and Clinical Instructors, the interview panel will deep-dive into practical scenarios. If you are an Electrical TA, be prepared for questions on power systems, circuit protection, and laboratory equipment maintenance. Clinical Instructors should be thorough with nursing protocols, patient care management, and the latest healthcare standards in West Bengal.

Awareness of Institutional Context: Research the history of Aliah University (formerly Mohammedan College) and its role as a premier minority-focused institution. Familiarize yourself with the "Aliah University Act" and the basic hierarchy of university administration (Registrar, Vice-Chancellor, etc.). This knowledge often creates a positive impression during the personal interview.

Linguistic and Typing Skills: For the Compositor post, speed and accuracy in both Bengali and English typing are non-negotiable. Practice using specialized Bengali typing software like Avro or Bijoy, as the university requires proficiency in regional language documentation and publishing tasks.

How to Apply: Step-by-Step Guide

Follow these steps meticulously to ensure that your application is valid for both the online and offline stages.

  1. Online Registration: Visit the official recruitment portal at www.aliah.ac.in/recruitment. Register as a new user with a valid email and mobile number.
  2. Fill Online Form: Enter all academic scores, percentage of marks, and detailed work experience. Ensure the spelling of your name matches your 10th-standard certificate.
  3. Fee Payment: Pay the prescribed application fee through the online portal and download the transaction receipt.
  4. Print and Sign: After final online submission, download the generated PDF application form. Print it out and sign at the designated place.
  5. Document Compilation: Attach self-attested copies of all marksheets (10th to highest degree), experience certificates, age proof (Birth certificate/10th admit card), and category certificate.
  6. Envelope Packing: Place the signed form and documents in an envelope. Clearly write on the envelope: "APPLICATION FOR THE POST OF [NAME OF POST]".
  7. Final Submission: Send the envelope via Speed Post, Courier, or deliver it in person to the university registrar’s office by December 4, 2025.

Documents Needed to Apply

Keeping these documents ready in digital format (for online) and physical format (for offline) is essential. All copies sent via post must be Self-Attested.

  1. Identity and Age Proof: Aadhar Card and 10th Standard Admit Card/Certificate.
  2. Educational Marksheets: All semesters/years of Graduation, Diploma, or Post-Graduation as applicable.
  3. Professional Proof: Registration certificate with the Nursing Council (for Clinical Instructor).
  4. Experience Certificates: Formal letters from previous employers mentioning dates of service and nature of work.
  5. Category Certificate: SC/ST/OBC (A or B)/PwD certificate issued by the competent authority of the Government of West Bengal.
  6. NOC: "No Objection Certificate" from the current employer (if currently in government service).

Important Links

Resource Name Direct Official Link
Aliah University Official Website www.aliah.ac.in
Online Application Portal Apply Online Here
Postal Address The Registrar, Aliah University, IIA/27, New Town, Kolkata-700 160, WB

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: Can I only apply online and not send the hard copy?

No. The recruitment notification clearly states that both online application and the submission of the physical hard copy are mandatory. If you fail to send the hard copy by December 4, 2025, your online application will not be considered.

Q2: Is the experience mandatory for Technical Assistant Grade-I?

Yes. For Technical Assistant (Grade-I), you must have at least 2 years of experience if you have a B.E./B.Tech, or 3 years of experience if you have a Diploma. This must be post-qualification experience.

Q3: What if my original certificates are not in English or Bengali?

If your certificates are in any other language, you must provide an officially notarized translation in English or Bengali along with the original copies during the document verification stage.

Q4: I am an OBC candidate from outside West Bengal. Am I eligible for fee concession?

Generally, reservation benefits and fee concessions in state-aided universities are reserved for candidates who possess a certificate issued by the Government of West Bengal. Candidates from other states are usually treated as General/UR category candidates.

Q5: I applied for the Junior Peon post earlier in 2024. Do I need to apply again for Group-D?

As per the FAQs provided in the official notification, candidates who applied for the post of Junior Peon against Advt AU/Apptt.-4/2024 do not need to apply again for the current Group-D vacancies.

Q6: When will the interview be conducted?

The university has not announced a specific date yet. Candidates are advised to keep checking the "Recruitment" section of the official website for updates on the list of shortlisted candidates and interview schedules.

entry.title: VNSGU Recruitment 2025: Surat में Clerk और Stenographer के पदों पर भर्ती
entry.content:
VNSGU Recruitment 2025: Surat में Clerk और Stenographer के पदों पर भर्ती

Veer Narmad South Gujarat University, Surat ने 37 पदों पर भर्ती के लिए अधिसूचना जारी की है। ₹40,800 तक वेतन पाने का यह सुनहरा अवसर है। आवेदन प्रक्रिया शुरू हो चुकी है।

Gujarat के योग्य उम्मीदवारों के लिए Veer Narmad South Gujarat University (VNSGU) ने एक नई भर्ती अधिसूचना जारी की है। यह भर्ती Junior Clerk/Typist और Junior Stenographer पदों के लिए है। कुल 37 पद भरे जाएंगे। यह अवसर स्थिर करियर और बेहतर वेतन चाहने वाले युवाओं के लिए उपयुक्त है। आवेदन प्रक्रिया ऑनलाइन और ऑफलाइन दोनों माध्यमों से की जाएगी।

महत्वपूर्ण तिथियाँ (Important Dates)

कार्यक्रमतिथि
ऑनलाइन आवेदन की अंतिम तिथि21 नवंबर 2025 (शाम 6:00 बजे तक)
हार्ड कॉपी विश्वविद्यालय में पहुँचने की अंतिम तिथि28 नवंबर 2025 (शाम 6:10 बजे तक)

पदों का विवरण एवं वेतन

पद का नामकुल पदप्रारंभिक वेतननियमित वेतनमान
Junior Clerk/Typist35₹26,000 प्रतिमाहLevel-02 (₹19,900 – ₹63,200)
Junior Stenographer02₹40,800 प्रतिमाहLevel-06 (₹35,400 – ₹1,12,400)

पात्रता मानदंड (Eligibility Criteria)

Junior Clerk/Typist के लिए:

  • UGC मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक (Graduation)।
  • Basic Computer Knowledge प्रमाणपत्र आवश्यक।
  • Gujarati और English टाइपिंग का ज्ञान वांछनीय।

Junior Stenographer के लिए:

  • UGC मान्यता प्राप्त विश्वविद्यालय से स्नातक (Graduation)।
  • English Stenography में 90 wpm या Gujarati Stenography में 60 wpm की गति आवश्यक।
  • Basic Computer Knowledge प्रमाणपत्र अनिवार्य।
ध्यान दें: यदि किसी चयनित उम्मीदवार ने C.C.C परीक्षा उत्तीर्ण नहीं की है तो उसे नियुक्ति के दौरान यह परीक्षा उत्तीर्ण करनी होगी।

चयन प्रक्रिया (Selection Process)

VNSGU उम्मीदवारों का चयन निम्न चरणों के माध्यम से करेगी:

  1. आवेदन पत्रों की जांच (Scrutiny)।
  2. लिखित परीक्षा (Written Test)।
  3. कौशल परीक्षा (Typing या Stenography Test)।
  4. साक्षात्कार (Interview)।
  5. अंतिम मेरिट सूची प्रदर्शन के आधार पर तैयार की जाएगी।

आवेदन प्रक्रिया (How to Apply)

Step 1: ऑनलाइन आवेदन

  1. https://vnsgunt.samarth.edu.in वेबसाइट पर जाएँ।
  2. "Employment Notice" सेक्शन में सही पद चुनें और "Apply Now" पर क्लिक करें।
  3. सभी जानकारी भरें और शुल्क जमा करें (General: ₹500, Reserved: ₹200)।
  4. फॉर्म सबमिट करने के बाद उसका प्रिंटआउट निकाल लें।

Step 2: ऑफलाइन सबमिशन

  1. ऑनलाइन फॉर्म की 2 प्रिंट कॉपी निकालें।
  2. आवश्यक दस्तावेज़ (शैक्षणिक प्रमाणपत्र, पहचान पत्र आदि) संलग्न करें।
  3. लिफाफे पर “Application for the post of …” लिखकर नीचे दिए पते पर भेजें:

पता:
The Registrar, Veer Narmad South Gujarat University, University Campus, Udhna-Magdalla Road, Surat - 395007, Gujarat.

महत्वपूर्ण लिंक (Important Links)

लिंक शीर्षकलिंक
ऑनलाइन आवेदन करेंयहाँ क्लिक करें
VNSGU आधिकारिक वेबसाइटयहाँ क्लिक करें

अक्सर पूछे जाने वाले प्रश्न (FAQs)

Q1: आवेदन शुल्क कैसे जमा करें?

आवेदन शुल्क केवल ऑनलाइन माध्यम से (Debit/Credit Card या Net Banking) जमा किया जा सकता है।

Q2: क्या अनुभव आवश्यक है?

नहीं, किसी भी पद के लिए अनुभव आवश्यक नहीं है। नए स्नातक (Fresh Graduates) भी आवेदन कर सकते हैं।

Q3: यदि हार्ड कॉपी समय पर नहीं पहुँची तो?

यदि आपकी हार्ड कॉपी 28 नवंबर 2025 तक विश्वविद्यालय नहीं पहुँचती है, तो आपका आवेदन रद्द कर दिया जाएगा।

entry.title: Jharkhand Police Recruitment 2025: Notification for 23,673
entry.content:
Jharkhand Police Recruitment 2025: Notification for 23,673

State Government Approves New Joint Recruitment Rules 2025 for Constable, Sub-Inspector, and Equivalent Posts

Post Name: Constable (Arakshi), Sub-Inspector (Daroga), Excise Constable, Jail Warder, Forest Guard
Last Date: To be announced (Upcoming 2025)
Total Posts: 23,673 Vacancies

The Government of Jharkhand has officially paved the way for one of the largest recruitment drives in the state's history. In a landmark cabinet decision, the state government has approved the Jharkhand State Police Joint Recruitment Rules 2025, aimed at filling a massive backlog of 23,673 vacant positions within the police department. This recruitment is a strategic move to bolster the internal security of Jharkhand, which has been grappling with a shortage of personnel for several years. The vacancies range from entry-level Constable (Arakshi) positions to supervisory roles such as Sub-Inspector (Daroga) and Inspector. This announcement has brought a ray of hope to millions of aspirants who have been awaiting a stable career in the state's uniformed services.

The 2025 recruitment drive is unique because it introduces a unified selection process. Previously, departments like the Excise Wing, Prison Services, and Forest Department conducted separate recruitment cycles, which often led to administrative delays and legal challenges. Under the new Joint Recruitment Rules, a single combined examination will be conducted for Constables and their equivalent ranks across different state departments. This streamlined approach ensures that vacancies are filled faster and more transparently. Furthermore, the government has decided to cancel all previous stalled advertisements for Constable posts, offering one-time fee and age relaxations to previous applicants to ensure they are not unfairly disadvantaged. This guide provides an exhaustive 2000-word analysis of the upcoming notification, eligibility criteria, and preparation roadmap.

According to the 2023 report by the Bureau of Police Research and Development (BPR&D), Jharkhand currently has one of the lowest police-to-population ratios in India. The state needs more boots on the ground to manage law and order, VIP security, and anti-insurgency operations effectively. By inducting over 23,000 new personnel, the government intends to improve public safety and provide the youth of Jharkhand with a prestigious opportunity to serve their motherland. Whether you are a 10th-pass student looking for your first job or a graduate aiming for the rank of Daroga, the Jharkhand Police Recruitment 2025 is a life-changing opportunity. In the following sections, we will detail every technical requirement and academic syllabus needed to succeed.

Important Highlights and Recruitment Timeline

Candidates must stay informed about the critical phases of the recruitment. While the cabinet has approved the rules, the formal requisition (Adhyachana) is being sent to the Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC). The official detailed notification is expected to be released in the first quarter of 2025. It is essential to maintain all previous application records if you wish to claim the special relaxations offered for this cycle.

Recruitment Feature Dates and Information
Organization Name Jharkhand Police Department
Total Number of Vacancies 23,673 Posts
Combined Posts Included Constable, SI, Excise Constable, Jail Warder, Forest Guard
Application Start Date Upcoming (Early 2025)
Job Type Permanent State Government Job
Job Location Jharkhand (Across 24 Districts)
Selection Body Jharkhand Staff Selection Commission (JSSC)

Detailed Vacancy Analysis and Recruitment Mode

The total 23,673 vacancies represent the currently unoccupied sanctioned posts in the state police. These will be filled through two primary channels: Direct Recruitment for freshers and Promotions for serving personnel. The new rules specifically emphasize the 100% direct entry for Constables and a 50-50 split for the Sub-Inspector rank.

Post Designation Recruitment Channel Status
Constable (Arakshi) & Equivalent 100% Direct Entry (Competitive Exam) Majority of Vacancies
Sub-Inspector (Daroga) 50% Direct Entry / 50% Promotion Technical & Supervisory
Assistant Sub-Inspector (ASI) 100% Promotion from Constables Internal Progression
Excise/Jail/Forest Guards Direct Entry (Joint Exam 2025) Unified Process
Total Vacancies to be Filled - 23,673
Special Note: For the first joint recruitment held under these new rules, all candidates who had applied for previous Constable advertisements that were recently cancelled will be eligible for a Full Fee Waiver and a Significant Age Relaxation. This is a one-time gesture by the Jharkhand Government to compensate for the lost time due to policy revisions.

Application Fee and Payment Process

  • New General/OBC/EWS Candidates: ₹100/- (Tentative).
  • SC/ST Candidates of Jharkhand: ₹50/- (Tentative).
  • Previous Applicants of Cancelled Ads: **EXEMPTED** (No Fee).

The fee must be paid through the official JSSC portal using online payment gateways. Previous applicants will be required to provide their old registration numbers to activate the zero-fee option. Fees once paid for the new cycle are non-refundable.

Eligibility Criteria and Requirements

Eligibility for the Jharkhand Police depends on the rank you are applying for. The 2025 rules have standardized the requirements for all equivalent ranks like Excise Constable and Jail Warder to ensure a level playing field.

Post Level Educational Qualification Expected Age Limit
Constable / Forest Guard 10th Standard (Matriculation) from a recognized board. 18 to 25 Years
Sub-Inspector (Daroga) Bachelor's Degree (Graduation) in any discipline. 20 to 27 Years
Excise Constable 10th Standard (Matriculation) pass. 18 to 25 Years

Age Relaxation: Jharkhand state provides age relaxation to reserved categories: 2 years for BC-I/BC-II (Male), 3 years for Female (All categories), and 5 years for SC/ST (Male & Female). The cut-off date for age calculation will be announced in the official JSSC notification.

Selection Process

The selection process for Jharkhand Police is a multi-tier assessment focusing on both physical grit and mental aptitude. Under the new Joint Rules, the sequence of the exams is designed to filter out only the most fit candidates before the written stage.

  1. Physical Efficiency Test (PET): This is usually the first stage. It is a qualifying round consisting of a long-distance run. No marks are added to the merit list from this stage.
  2. Physical Measurement Test (PMT): Verification of height and chest (Chest for males only). This is also qualifying.
  3. Written Examination: The most critical stage. It is usually OMR-based and consists of two papers testing language skills and general knowledge.
  4. Medical Examination: Conducted by a board of government doctors to check for vision, hearing, flat feet, knock knees, and overall health.
  5. Document Verification: The final verification of domicile, caste, and academic certificates.

Exam Pattern and Detailed Syllabus

The written exam is divided into two distinct papers. Each correct answer provides 3 marks, while each incorrect answer results in a 1-mark deduction.

Paper 1: Regional and Tribal Language (100 Questions): Candidates must choose one language from the official list (e.g., Nagpuri, Khortha, Santhali, Mundari, Urdu, etc.). This paper is essential as it tests your ability to interact with the local populace. Scoring at least 30% is mandatory to qualify for Paper 2 evaluation.

Paper 2: General Studies and Aptitude (100 Questions):

  • Part A - General Awareness (50 Qs): Focuses on Current Affairs, Indian History, Indian Constitution, and heavily on Jharkhand General Knowledge (History of Jharkhand movement, Tribes, Rivers, and Mineral resources).
  • Part B - Numerical Ability & Science (50 Qs): Basic Mathematics (10th standard level) and General Science.

Physical Endurance Test (PET) Standards

The PET in Jharkhand is a test of stamina. Unlike other state police exams that focus on 1.6km sprints, Jharkhand requires long-distance endurance running.

  • Male Candidates (All Categories): 10 Kilometers run to be completed within 60 minutes (1 Hour).
  • Female Candidates (All Categories): 05 Kilometers run to be completed within 40 minutes.
  • Male Height: 160 cm (General/OBC), 155 cm (SC/ST).
  • Male Chest: 81 cm (General/OBC), 79 cm (SC/ST).
  • Female Height: 148 cm (All Categories).

Expert Preparation Strategy for Jharkhand Police

Success in a mega-recruitment of 23,673 posts requires a balanced preparation of both physical training and academic study. You cannot neglect one for the other.

Building Stamina: Running 10 kilometers is not about speed; it is about consistent pacing. Start your training by walking/jogging 2-3 km and gradually increase it to 10 km over a period of 2 months. Focus on your breathing technique and ensure you are using proper running shoes to avoid shin splints. Morning training sessions are best to build the discipline required for police life.

Mastering Jharkhand GK: This section is the tie-breaker. You must be thorough with the "Jharkhand Movement" (Birsa Munda, Sidho-Kanho), the geography of the Chota Nagpur plateau, and the current administrative structure of the state. Read the Jharkhand Economic Survey and Budget to answer questions in the current affairs section effectively.

Regional Language Advantage: Choose a language you are most comfortable with. If you are from the Ranchi/Khunti belt, Nagpuri is a great choice. If you are from the Dhanbad/Bokaro/Giridih region, Khortha is very popular. Practice previous year JSSC language papers to understand the depth of grammar and folk literature required.

Best Books for Preparation

Using the right study material for JSSC exams is vital. Below are the top recommendations from previous successful Jharkhand police candidates.

Jharkhand State GK: "Jharkhand Samanya Gyan" by Dr. Manish Ranjan (IAS) or Udaan Publications.
Numerical Ability: Quantitative Aptitude by R.S. Aggarwal (10th Level).
General Science: Lucent's General Science.
Reasoning: Verbal and Non-Verbal Reasoning by Arihant.
Language Paper: Standard Grammar and Folk literature books from local Jharkhand publishers for Khortha/Nagpuri.

How to Apply Online

The application process will be hosted on the official JSSC portal. Follow these steps meticulously once the notification is live.

  1. Visit the official website of JSSC: https://jssc.nic.in.
  2. Look for the link "Apply Online for Joint Jharkhand Police Exam 2025" on the homepage.
  3. New Registration: Enter your primary details (Name, DOB, Mobile, Email).
  4. Previous Applicants: If you applied for the cancelled advertisements, enter your old registration number to claim age and fee waivers.
  5. Login with your new credentials and fill in your academic scores and District Preference.
  6. Upload a high-quality scanned photograph and your signature (Ensure the size is between 10kb to 50kb).
  7. Choose your Regional Language (Paper 1) carefully as it cannot be changed later.
  8. Pay the application fee (if applicable) through the online payment gateway.
  9. Download the confirmation page and keep a printout safe for the physical test admit card.

Documents Needed to Apply

Since this is a state-level job with reservation benefits, your domicile and category certificates must be issued by the competent Jharkhand authorities.

  1. Matriculation (10th) Certificate: For DOB and Name verification.
  2. Graduation Degree: Mandatory if applying for the Daroga (Sub-Inspector) post.
  3. Residential Certificate: Valid Local Resident Certificate of Jharkhand (issued by CO/SDO).
  4. Caste Certificate: Valid SC/ST/BC-I/BC-II certificate for reservation seekers.
  5. Income Certificate: For candidates applying under the EWS category.
  6. Identity Proof: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Voter ID for biometric verification.

Important Links

Resource Description Direct Official Link
JSSC Official Portal jssc.nic.in
Jharkhand Police Official Site jhpolice.gov.in
Cabinet Decision Reference Download Approval PDF

Frequently Asked Questions

Q1: When will the Jharkhand Police 23,673 recruitment notification be released?

The cabinet has already approved the rules. Requisitions are being sent to JSSC. Expect the official notification in early 2025.

Q2: Will there be separate exams for Excise Constable and District Police?

No. Under the Joint Recruitment Rules 2025, there will be one unified exam for all equivalent Constable ranks. Candidates will give their preferences during application.

Q3: What is the age relaxation for candidates who applied for cancelled exams?

The government has promised a one-time age relaxation to all candidates who were eligible for the cancelled ads, ensuring they do not lose their chance due to state delays.

Q4: What is the physical running requirement for male candidates?

Male candidates must complete a 10-kilometer run within 60 minutes. This is a qualifying test and does not carry marks toward the final merit.

Q5: Is it mandatory to choose a regional language in the written exam?

Yes. Paper 1 is entirely based on a Regional or Tribal language of your choice from the approved state list. Proficiency in this is mandatory to pass the exam.

Q6: Can candidates from other states apply?

Yes, candidates from other states can apply for General category seats. however, they will not be eligible for category-based reservations or the special fee/age waivers provided to Jharkhand residents.

entry.title: JKSSB Recruitment 2026 for 239 Health & Medical Education Posts
entry.content:
JKSSB Recruitment 2026 for 239 Health & Medical Education Posts

Advertisement Notification No. 01 of 2026: The Jammu & Kashmir Services Selection Board (JKSSB) has announced its intention to recruit for 239 UT Cadre posts belonging to the Health and Medical Education Department. JKSSB will be inviting Online Application Forms from eligible candidates soon. This is a major upcoming opportunity for individuals seeking a government job in the health sector of Jammu & Kashmir.

Post Name: Various UT Cadre Health & Medical Posts
Last Date: To Be Announced
Total Posts: 239
